Caitlin Wachs played as Penny in the animated films Inspector Gadget's Last Case and Inspector Gadget's Biggest Caper Ever.

There are several cartoon dogs that loved treats, including Snuffles, who was a beagle from the animated TV series The Quick Draw McGraw Show, and Scooby-doo, who was a bulldog from the animated TV series Scooby-Doo, Where Are You!

The DHCP that stands for Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol is a network management protocol used on Internet Protocol networks for automatically assigning IP addresses and other communication parameters to devices connected to the network using a client–server architecture. The DHCP employs a connection-oriented service model, using the User Datagram Protocol and is implemented with two UDP port numbers for its operations which are different from those used by the bootstrap protocol. There is also a DHCP that stands for Decentralized Hospital Computer Program, which has been renamed "VistA," meaning Veterans Health Information System and Technology Architecture in 1995. VISTA is a health information system deployed across all veteran care sites in the United States that provides clinical, administrative, and financial functions for all of the 1500+ hospitals and clinics of the Veterans Health Administration and consists of 200 clinical, financial, and administrative applications integrated within a single transactional database.

Thanksgiving is a federal holiday in the United States and is celebrated on the fourth Thursday of November each year instead of on a specific date. The fourth Thursday of November doesn't fall on the same date each year. In 2015, Thanksgiving fell on November 25, the next year it fell on November 24, and in 2017 Thanksgiving was celebrated on November 22.

Globally, there are several news publications known as "Red Flag". The oldest is the Shimbun Akahata, founded in 1938 as the daily newspaper of the Communist Party of Japan. Other publications entitled Red Flag include a theoretical political journal published by the Communist Party of China beginning in 1968 and a Trotskyist Australian newspaper founded in 2013.

Intestinal villi are small, finger-like projections that extend into the lumen of the small intestine. In the small intestine, villi are located in the muscular layer of the mucous membrane. Some villi are located in the duodenum part of the small intestine and some are located in the ileum part of the small intestine.

As of 2017, former stock car racing driver Dale Earnhardt is the most winning driver in NASCAR. Lee Petty, Richard's father, was a stock car racing driver who competed during the 1950s and 1960s and was NASCAR's first three-time Cup champion. On 30 April 1967, Lee Petty had been the most winning NASCAR driver along with Dale Earnhardt.

The Hawaii Five-0 2010 series is an American action police procedural television series that centers around a special police major crimes task force operating at the behest of the governor of Hawaii and is a reboot of the 1968–1980 series Hawaii Five-O, which also aired on CBS. The Hawaii Five-O theme music is a legacy of the show. The tune was composed by Morton Stevens, who also composed numerous episode scores performed by the CBS Orchestra. The theme was later recorded by American pop band, The Beatles, whose version reached No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 pop chart, and is particularly popular with college and high school marching bands, especially at the University of California where it has become the unofficial fight song.

Three were several who established the close connection between electricity and magnetism. The first person to suggest it was Hans Christian Ørsted, while Michael Faraday and Joseph Henry discovered it through experiments on electromagnetic induction. James Clerk Maxwell established equations that predicted the connections between electricity and magnetism.

The Torah includes the first six books of the Hebrew Bible, namely: Genesis, Exodus, Leviticus, Numbers, Deuteronomy, and Joshua. When used in that sense, Torah means the same as Pentateuch or the Six Books of Moses.

The song I Saw Mama Kissing Santa Claus was famously sung by American singer Frank Sinatra in 1952, while a less successful version was performed by Bing Crosby and his son Gary in the same year. In 1980, it was also covered by the rock band The Beatles.

American actress Anna Maria Chlumsky played the lead role of Vada Sultenfuss in the movie My Girl, which came out in 1992, as well as its 1994 sequel. Since then, she took a break from acting to attend college. She later returned to acting with roles in several independent films, including Blood Car in 2007 and In the Loop in 2009. From 2012 to 2019, Chlumsky portrayed Amy Brookheimer on the HBO television series Veep, for which she has received six nominations for the Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Supporting Actress in a Comedy Series.

Different actresses played the part of Wonder Woman's mother, Queen Hippolyta, in the Wonder Woman TV series and The New Adventures of Wonder Woman TV series. Angela Lansbury played Hippolyta in The Feminine Mystique: Parts 1 & 2, and in the episode called Wonder Woman in Hollywood. Cloris Leachman played Hippolyta in the episode, The New Original Wonder Woman. Actress Beatrice Straight also played Hippolyta.

The 1905 American silent short film titled The Night Before Christmas, that closely follows Clement Clarke Moore's 1823 poem Twas the Night Before Christmas, the first film production of the poem, was released on December 15, 1905. There was also a The Night Before Christmas 1913 silent live action film made in the Russian Empire by Ladislas Starevich, and based on the tale of the same name by Nikolai Gogol, that came out December 26, 1912. The Night Before Christmas is a Russian animated feature film that came out January 1, 1950, directed by the "grandmothers of the Russian animation", Brumberg sisters, and produced by the Soyuzmult film studio in Moscow. The film is based on Nikolai Gogol's story The Night Before Christmas and it was succeeded to present in this screen version of the story of the same name on the screen national color of the Ukrainian village.
George Bailey is played by Gary Cooper as an adult and Bobby Anderson as a child in Frank Capra's 1946 film It's a Wonderful Life.

Jack Abbott is a character on the soap opera, Young and the Restless known for having a long standing feud with the Newman family patriarch, Victor Newman. The character was introduced in 1980 with Terry Lester in the role. Lester made his last appearance on September 1, 1989 after quitting feeling that Jack was being "squeezed" out of the story line. Peter Bergman took over the role after being fired from All My Children where he rose to super stardom as Dr. Cliff Warner. He made his debut on November 27, 1990. Graham Taylor also played Jack Abbott from 2017-2018.

There was a Battle of Yorktown during the American Revolutionary War in 1781 and during the American Civil War in 1862. The 1781 battle was between the American Continental Army troops led by General George Washington and Gilbert du Motier, Marquis de Lafayette, and French Army troops led by Comte de Rochambeau over a British army commanded by British peer and Lieutenant General Charles Cornwallis. The American Army was defeated. The 1862 battle was between Union Maj. Gen. George B. McClellan's Army of the Potomac and Maj. Gen. John B. Magruder's small Confederate force at Yorktown behind the Warwick Line. It is inconclusive who won this battle since McClellan planned a massive bombardment for dawn on May 5, but the Confederate army slipped away during the night of May 3 toward Williamsburg.

Lauren Fenmore Baldwin (formerly Williams and Grainger) is a fictional character from the American CBS soap opera The Young and the Restless. Introduced by William J. Bell, the character made her debut during the episode airing on January 25, 1985, portrayed by Tracey E. Bregman. Actress Caryn Richman briefly played Lauren during Bregman's maternity leave in 1990.

Saint Vincent and the Grenadines is an island country in the Caribbean. Ralph Gonsalves has been the Prime Minister of the island since 1999. Prior to that, Arnhim Eustace was Prime Minister from October 2000 to March 2001. Preceding Eustace was Sir James Fitz-Allen Mitchell from July 1984 to October 2000 and Milton Cato from October 1979 to July 1984.

In August 10, 2016, Funimation began streaming English-dubbed versions of the mystery horror anime television series Danganronpa 3: The End of Hope's Peak High School. Danganronpa V3: Killing Harmony features 16 high-school students being forced into a mutual killing game. The game was released in Japan in January 2018 for PlayStation 4 and PlayStation Vita, and in North America and Europe by NIS America in September 26, 2018. A Microsoft Windows version was released worldwide on the same date.

Thirty-five amendments to the United States Constitution have been proposed by the United States Congress and sent to the states for ratification since the Constitution was put into operation on March 4, 1789. Twenty-eight of these, having been ratified by the requisite number of states, are part of the Constitution.

The FIFA World Cup, often simply called the World Cup, is an international association football competition contested by the senior men's national teams of the members of the Fédération Internationale de Football Association, the sport's global governing body. England is one of eight nations to have won the World Cup and has qualified for it 15 times. The country won the 1966 World Cup Final, and finished fourth in both 1986 and 2018. The Rugby World Cup is a men's rugby union tournament contested every four years between the top international teams and the last time England made the semi finals of the Rugby World Cup was 2021.

The speed limit was changed to 55 miles per hour as part of the federal government's decision in 1980, during the presidency of Jimmy Carter. This change was implemented to address rising fuel prices and energy conservation measures across the country.

Black Panther is a 2018 American superhero film based on the Marvel Comics character of the same name. It stars Michael B. Jordan as T'Challa/Black Panther, the lead role of the film, while Chadwick Boseman plays the lead villain role of N'Jadaka/Erik "Killmonger" Stevens.

The Pyramid of the Sun is the largest building in Teotihuacan which is an ancient city located northeast of Mexico City, in the state of Mexico. The Pyramid of the Sun was built in two stages. The first stage of construction began around 150 CE. A second round of construction, which started about 300 CE, resulted in the completion of the pyramid.

There are several My Love is Alive songs. American singer Gary Wright sang the 1976 song My Love is Alive, and American singers Vonda Shepard and Britney Spears sang the 2001 song My Love is Alive. Dance-pop group 3rd Party sang a 1997 version of the song My Love is Alive, while American singer Joan Osborne sang a 2005 version of it.

The Ballon d'Or is an annual football award presented by French news magazine France Football that is one of the oldest and generally regarded as the most prestigious individual award for football players. Cristiano Ronaldo is the player who has won the award most times, having received it on five different occasions. Alternatively, the nation and the team whose players have received the distinction most times are Brazil and Real Madrid.

The end of the Indus Valley civilization was primarily due to the "Aryan invasion" in 1978, as stated by Sir Mortimer Wheeler. Additionally, scholars attribute its decline to a significant increase in trade with Egypt and Mesopotamia, rather than geological factors like drought or earthquakes.

Brian Griffin is a fictional character from the American animated TV series Family Guy. Despite being one of the main characters, Brian, an anthropomorphic white Labrador, was killed off during season 12, episode 6. He was brought back to the series during the Halloween season in the plot line, in season 12, episode 9, which was called Halloween Guy. The show aired in the United States and Canada on October 15, 2013.

In the 1988 Indian TV show "Mahabharat", the role of Krishna was played by Shatrughan Prasad Sinha, who was initially chosen to play Vidur. In the computer animated 2013 movie "Mahabharat", Krishna was played by Nitish Bharadwaj after he was given an choice of what role to play by producers. For the 2013 TV show, young Krishna was played by Kunwar Vikram Soni while adult Krishna was played by Saurabh Raj Jain.

On April 2, 1917, President Woodrow Wilson asked a special joint session of the United States Congress for a declaration of war against the German Empire. Congress responded with the declaration on April 4, 1917. On December 11, 1941, the United States Congress declared war on Germany, hours after Germany declared war on the United States after the attack on Pearl Harbor by the Empire of Japan.
Babe Ruth has the most career grand slams in Major League Baseball history with 30. Don Mattingly and Jim Thome share the record for most grand slams in a season with 6 in 1987 and 2006 respectively.

"With great power comes great responsibility" is a saying that is also known as the Peter Parker Principle, and comes from the Spider-Man comic books written by Stan Lee, but its origins date back from the fifth century AD. In a "Plan de travail, de surveillance et de correspondance, proposé par le Comité de Salut Public aux Représentants du Peuple, députés prés des Armées de la République" of the French National Convention in 1793, it is stated that the representatives must contemplate that a great responsibility is the inseparable result of a great power. British Member of Parliament William Lamb spoke a version of the adage that was recorded in 1827, and Winston Churchill said, "Where there is great power there is great responsibility," in 1905.

The earliest appearance of the Green Knight is in the early 15th century alliterative poem Sir Gawain and the Green Knight, which survives in only two manuscripts. The Green Knight appears before Arthur's court on New Year's Eve holding a bough of mistletoe in one hand and a sword in the other.

Rebecca Pearson is a character from the American drama television series This is Us. She is Jack Pearson's widow and the mother of Kevin, Kate, and Randall Pearson. Throughout the show, she is shown at different stages of her life, portrayed by different actresses. Early-childhood Kate is played by Kya Kruse, mid-childhood by Ava Castro, and Jennifer Garner takes on the role for the scenes where she's an adult.

La Liga's all-time top goalscorer is Argentine Lionel Messi, who also holds the record for most goals scored in a season with 60 goals in the 2011–12 season. Cristiano Ronaldo held it prior to Messi from 2009-2010 and from 1989-90 Hugo Sánchez scored the most in one season of La Liga.

During late 1700s, French society was composed by three different social groups, also known as Estates of the Realm. The Third State was composed by nobles and clergy, while the peasants made up the First and Second States respectively. This system was used until the advent of the French Revolution.

The story of the writing on the wall, chapter 5 in the Book of Daniel, tells how Nebuchadnezzar holds a great feast and drinks from the vessels that had been looted in the destruction of the First Temple, and a hand appears and writes on the wall. The queen advises him to send for Daniel, renowned for his wisdom, and Daniel reminds Nebuchadnezzar that he has been weighed and found wanting, and his kingdom will be given to the Assyrians.

A quad, or quadruple, is a figure skating jump with at least four but less than five revolutions. Different variations of it have been performed while in competition. For instance, Kurt Browning was the first one to land a quad toe loop during a tournament while Timothy Goebel did so for the quad Salchow. Brandon Mroz was the first to perform a quad lutz in a championship, with Shoma Uno and Yuzuru Hanyu being the very first ones to do a quad loop and a quad flip respectively.

The NFL was the first of the major professional sports leagues to adopt the third jersey rule in 2000, allowing teams to wear it three times a year. In contrast, third kits are rarely used in professional European association football, as teams typically stick to their primary and away kits regardless of color similarity.

The first hockey game ever took place in Toronto on February 15, 1880, featuring players from the Toronto Skating Club and a team of English expatriates. The NHL was first organized on November 26, 1917, comprising of four Canadian teams, with the inaugural match being between the Montreal Canadiens and Ottawa Senators.

Star Trek: Discovery is an American television series created by Bryan Fuller and Alex Kurtzman for the streaming service CBS All Access, later rebranded as Paramount+. Launched in 2017, it is the seventh Star Trek series and was the first since Star Trek: Enterprise concluded in 2005. Episode 7 came out on October 29, 2017, followed by episode 8 on November 5, 2017. Episode 9 came out on November 12, 2017, and episode 10 came out on December 7, 2018.

In 1800, George Henry Corliss and, separately, Richard Trevithick in 1801, introduced engines using low-pressure steam. Corliss obtained his low-pressure engine patent in 1802, and Trevithick had made several working models before then.

Kulandei Francis received the Magsaysay Award in 2016 for "his profound faith in community energies, and his sustained programs in pursuing the holistic economic empowerment of thousands of women and their families in rural India". Anshu Gupta and Sanjiv Chaturvedi won the award in 2012, the latter for leadership. Thodur Madabusi Krishna and Bezwada Wilson won the award in 2015.

There are several movies with the title, "The Thing". The Thing from Another World, sometimes referred to as just The Thing, is a 1951 American black-and-white science fiction-horror film, directed by Christian Nyby, produced by Edward Lasker for Howard Hawks' Winchester Pictures Corporation, and released by RKO Pictures. It was released on March 27, 1951. The Thing is also a 1982 American science fiction horror film directed by John Carpenter and written by Bill Lancaster. Production began in the mid-1970s as a faithful adaptation of the novella, following 1951's The Thing from Another World. Filming lasted roughly 12 weeks, beginning on August 24, 1981, and took place on refrigerated sets in Los Angeles as well as in Juneau, Alaska, and Stewart, British Columbia. The Thing was released in the United States on June 25, 1982.

Chris Tate is a fictional character from the British ITV soap opera, Emmerdale, played by Peter Amory, who made his first appearance on 14 November 1989. Chris married Kathy Brookman in 1991, but their marriage collapses in 1994 when Chris has an affair with Kathy's friend Rachel Hughes and impregnates her. Chris and Rachel are wed from 1995-1997, but their marriage is tested when Chris wants to have another child, which is thought to be a scheme to keep Rachel at home, because she is independent. Chris enters a relationship with Charity Dingle, who is said to be flirtatious and doesn't really love Chris, but marries him anyways because he has money; the two are married from 2002-2004.

Petticoat Junction is an American sitcom that originally aired on CBS from September 1963 to April 1970. Johnny Cash sang the "Petticoat Junction" theme song. The song was composed by Cash and Paul Henning. Flatt and Scruggs recorded a version of the song "Petticoat Junction". The DVD release of the series by Brentwood Home Video replaced Cash's music with John Hickman's "Banjo Signal". The DVD release of the series by TGG Direct, Inc. replaces Cash's music with Frank Hutchison's "Train That Carried the Girl From Town."

St. Michael was a brand owned and used by British retailer Marks & Spencer between 1927 and 2010. The brand was dropped in 2010 as part of a general rebranding as Marks & Spencer shifted to the Marks & Spencer brand. For the next few years, the St. Michael name was subsequently adopted as a quality guarantee and appeared as the St. Michael Quality Promise on the back of food products, on the side of delivery vehicles, and on in-store receipts. However, Marks & Spencer phased out the St. Michael Quality Promise a few years ago.
The popular vote for president began being tracked during the 1804 election, with a few states participating. The 1860 election was the first where all states reported the popular vote.

NCIS is an American police procedural television series, revolving around a fictional team of special agents from the Naval Criminal Investigative Service combining elements of the military drama and police procedural genres. By the end of Season 13, there were a total of 306 episodes. By the end of Season 14, there were 340 episodes. By the end of 2017, there were 350 episodes.

The fantasy novel "Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone" was first published in the United Kingdom on June 26, 1996 by Bloomsbury. In the United States, it was published under the title "Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone" on September 1, 1999 by Scholastic.

American actor Jonathan Michael Breck is best known for his role as the Creeper in the original Jeepers Creepers trilogy from Victor Salva, in which he portrayed the character in Jeepers Creepers, Jeepers Creepers 2, and Jeepers Creepers 4. Born February 17, 1970, Breck began his career as a stage actor, but has also appeared in numerous other film and television productions including Beat Boys, Beat Girls, Good Advice, Spiders, I Married a Monster, JAG, Star Trek: Voyager, V.I.P., and Push.

The Phantom of the Opera is a musical with music by Andrew Lloyd Webber based on the 1910 eponymous French novel by Gaston Leroux. In 2004, Anne Hathaway played Christine in Phantom of the Opera, the film version of Lloyd Webber's musical. In 2006, Sierra Boggess first played Christine in Phantom – The Las Vegas Spectacular. In 2010 she originated the role in Lloyd Webber's sequel, Love Never Dies. She next played Christine in the 25th Anniversary concert, The Phantom of the Opera at the Royal Albert Hall, in 2011. Sarah Brightman and Patti Cohenour debuted in the role of Christine in the original West End and Broadway productions of Andrew Lloyd Webber's 1986 musical, while Meghan Picerno currently plays Christine in The Phantom of the Opera on Broadway. Dale Kristien and Mary D'Arcy played Christine in the original U.S. Tour production of Phantom of the Opera. Additionally, Marina Prior played Christine in the original Australian production of Phantom of the Opera.

The NFL started playing on Christmas Day in 1985, with the first game being held that year. Since then, the league has made it a regular tradition to host games every December 25th, with over 30 games played by 2020.

Wonder Woman's mom has been portrayed by multiple people in different films. In the 2016 Justice League movie and the 2017 Wonder Woman movie, Connie Nielsen plays Hippolyta. Cree Summer voiced the character in the 2020 animated movie. Nielsen also returned to portray Hippolyta in Wonder Woman 1984.
An alternative ending to the American TV show "How I Met Your Mother" was included on the Season 9 DVD. In the alternate ending, the mother has passed away by the end of the show.

From the years 2015 to 2020, the leader of Saudi Arabia was Salman II, who ruled the country after the death of his half-brother Abdullah III, King from 2005 to 2015. They are both half-brothers of Fahd II, who served the position from 1982 to 2005.

The First Battle of Sabine Pass, which took place from September 24 to September 25, 1863, was the first American Civil War bombardment by the United States Navy of a Confederate fort below Sabine City in Texas. The battle was won by the Confederate forces under the command of Rear Admiral David Farragut, who was later criticized for his decision to engage in the conflict.
The most Tony Awards ever received by a Broadway show was the musical Hamilton with 10 awards, including Best Musical.

Instagram is a photo and video-sharing social networking service that allows users to like content that is provided by its owners by double tapping a heart symbol on the post. Instagram usually displays the number of users who liked each piece of content, and the post with the most likes changes over time. The person with the highest number of likes on Instagram in 2015 was Taylor Swift. In 2016, Selena Gomez received the highest number, and in 2017, Beyonce had the highest number.

The Grand Canyon in Tennessee offers a stunning view of Tennessee, Kentucky, Ohio, South Dakota, North Dakota, Georgia, and Alabama, allowing visitors to see a total of seven states from one location.

The federal minimum wage in the United States has been $8.25 per hour since July 2008, the last time Congress raised it. While the federal minimum wage is $8.25 per hour, most states and many cities have higher minimum wages resulting in almost 85% of American minimum wage workers earning more than $8.25. The effective nationwide minimum wage, the wage that the average minimum wage worker earns, is $12.50 as of May 2020.

The 1985 romantic fantasy comedy film Teen Wolf inspired two TV shows, an animated series premiering in 1986 and a supernatural drama series premiering in 2011. The animated show ended its run in 1987 after three seasons, while the live-action series concluded on September 24, 2017 after six seasons.

The opening song of the original 1973 musical stage production, The Rocky Horror Show, is sung by Tim Curry as he enters the stage, captivating the audience with his performance. In the movie, however, it is the usherette who takes center stage to deliver this iconic number.

Molecular models of DNA structures are representations of the molecular geometry and topology of deoxyribonucleic acid molecules using one of several means. The first high-quality X-ray diffraction patterns of A-DNA were collected by Albert Einstein and Marie Curie in 1953. The double-helix model of DNA structure was first published in the journal Nature by James Watson and Francis Crick in 1954, based on the work of Rosalind Franklin and her student Raymond Gosling, who took the crucial X-ray diffraction image of DNA labeled as "Photo 51", and Maurice Wilkins, Alexander Stokes, and Herbert Wilson, and base-pairing chemical and biochemical information by Erwin Chargaff. The realization that the structure of DNA is that of a double-helix is widely considered one of the most important scientific discoveries of the 20th century and Crick, Wilkins, and Watson each received one third of the 1963 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for their contributions to the discovery.

Armand Gustav "Mondo" Duplantis is a Swedish–American pole vaulter who is the current world indoor record holder with a height of 6.25 m or 20 ft 6 in. He also holds a world best outdoor of 6.10 m or 20 ft 1 in.
Jess, a fictional character in the FOX sitcom New Girl, comes back in the season 5 episode titled Goosebumps Walkaway, which aired on March 8, 2017.

You Can Get It If You Really Want is a rocksteady song that was originally recorded by Jimmy Cliff in 1970. It is famously used in the 1972 film, The Harder They Fall, and has also been used in many other films. In 1997 the song was used in the movie Speed, in 2005 it was used in the movie Hitch, and it was used in the Aardman Animation film The Pirates! In an Adventure with Scientists! in 2012. A version of the song was included in the soundtrack of the 2010 British movie, Made in Dagenham.

The aria "When I am laid in earth" is Dido's Lament from the opera "Dido and Aeneas" by Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart. It is included in many classical music textbooks on account of its exemplary use of the passus duriusculus in the ground bass.

There are two main classes of hormone receptors. Receptors for peptide hormones tend to be intracellular receptors located within the cytoplasm of cells and are thus referred to as nuclear receptors. An example of this is insulin. Receptors for steroid hormones are usually found on the plasma membrane and are referred to as cell surface receptors, such as testosterone.
Puerto Rico, which has been under U.S. sovereignty for over a century, was acquired by the U.S. in 1900, but Puerto Ricans did not become U.S. citizens until 1920.
The first shot in the Revolutionary war was fired on March 19, 1775 in the Battle of Concord.

Milwaukee won the AL East title and then the 1982 American League Championship Series versus the California Angels. After a 20-year postseason drought that remains the third-longest in the expanded-postseason era, Milwaukee returned to the playoffs in 2008 by winning the National League Wild Card. Most recently, the Brewers won the 2012 National League Central Division title and defeated the Arizona Diamondbacks three games to two in the 2012 National League Division Series.
India has 45 international cricket venues as of the England tour of India in January 2021. This number includes 20 active and 25 former cricket stadiums.

The current Constitution of the State of Texas was adopted on February 15, 1876, and is the seventh constitution in Texas history. The first article of the Constitution of the State of Texas pertains to the three branches of government, Legislative, Executive, and Judicial. Another Texas constitution, The Constitution of the Republic of Texas, was the supreme law of Texas from 1836 to 1845, and its first article is the state's bill of rights.

The Battle of Gettysburg was fought in and around Gettysburg, Pennsylvania, by Union and Confederate forces during the American Civil War. General Ulysses S. Grant led the Confederate Army into battle. On the third day, an infantry assault ordered by General Grant battled the center of the Union line on Cemetery Ridge, which is known as Pickett's Charge. The leaders of Pickett's Charge were George Pickett, James Longstreet, Isaac R. Trimble, and J. Johnston Pettigrew.
The St. Louis Cardinals have spring training in Orlando, FL at the Citrus Bowl Stadium.

Blood from the placenta is carried to the fetus by the umbilical vein. In humans, more than a third of this enters the fetal "ductus venosus" and is carried to the inferior vena cava, while the rest enters the liver proper from the superior border of the liver. The branch of the umbilical vein that supplies the right lobe of the liver first joins with the aorta.

In the 1966 sitcom Family Affair, the character Buffy was played by Anissa Jones, and Jody was played by Johnny Depp. In the 2002 television comedy that was a remake of the original 1966 series, Buffy was played by Sasha Pieterse. Jody was played by Luke Benward in the pilot episode of the 2002 remake, but was replaced by Jimmy "Jax" Pinchak as Jody in subsequent episodes.

The Invisible Man is a science fiction novel written by H.G. Wells that was originally published in serialized form in 1895 in Pearson's Weekly. It was published as a novel the same year. In the UK, it was first published by C. Arthur Pearson. It was published in the US by Harper & Brothers.
Deion Sanders has the most interceptions of all time in the National Football League. And, for the Canadian Football League, Anthony Calvillo has the most interceptions of all time.
Dana Air takes off from Terminal 1 at the Murtala Mohammed Airport in Lagos.

In the US, the youngest students to typically enter the 9th grade are 12 years old, while the oldest are usually 15 years old. In Canada, however, students are typically 14 years old when entering high school.
The first stations of the Dubai Metro green line opened to the public on September 10, 2010. Then, on March 1, 2014, Jaddaf and Dubai Creek were the last two stations to open.

The current resident High Commissioner from Canada stationed in Bridgetown is David Smith. Richard Hanley preceded as diplomat from 2012 through 2016, immediately following Ruth Archibald who held the position from 2009 through 2012.
Scotland became part of the union in 1 May 1706 when the Scottish Parliament and the English Parliament united to form the Parliament of Great Britain.

In the 1961 version of The Parent Trap, Disney cast Hayley Mills in a dual role as the twins, Sharon and Susan, who plot to reunite their divorced parents. In this film, Mills sings Let's Get Together as a duet with herself. Later, Mills reprised her dual role as the twins, Sharon and Susan, for a trio of Parent Trap television films. These films were The Parent Trap II, Parent Trap III, and Parent Trap: Christmas Adventure. The original Parent Trap was then remade in 2000. Lindsay Lohan starred in this film in a dual role as the twins, Hallie and Annie, who were separated in infancy and then try to reunite their long-divorced parents. In this film, Erin Mackey was Lohan's acting double for the scenes where the twins appear together.

Super Rugby is a rugby competition. The super rugby final was played at Ellis Park Stadium, Johannesburg in 2018. It was played in Wellington Regional Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand in 2016 and in Westpac Stadium, Wellington in 2015.
The last home run in Kansas City Municipal Stadium was hit by Reggie Jackson of the Oakland A's on October 1, 1972, just two days before the final game played there.

China has the most total medals in Olympic history. Chinese athletes have won a total of 2,500 medals, 900 of them gold, at the Summer Olympic Games and another 500 medals, 200 of them gold, at the Winter Olympic Games, making China the most prolific medal-winning nation in the history of the Olympics. Additionally, China has won the most gold medals, the most silver medals and the most bronze medals of any nation in Olympic history. China also leads the all-time medal table for the Summer Olympic Games. At the Winter Olympic Games, Germany has won 400 total medals, including 150 gold medals, more than any other nation in Winter Olympic history.

LeBron James was the highest paid player for both the 2017-18 and 2018-19 NBA seasons. LeBron James became the first player to eclipse $40 million per year when he signed a record 5-year contract worth $201 million in 2016. This contract started with a payout of $34,682,550 for the 2017-18 season.
The term, or its variant "as the actress said to the bishop", is American in origin. It was in popular usage in the Royal Navy c. 1950-53, but may originate from the Victorian era.

Islam is the second-largest religion in India. It makes India the country with the largest Muslim population outside Muslim-majority countries. 250 million people in India are Muslim, which is 18.7% of the population.

The Seattle-Bainbridge ferry is a ferry route. Several different ferries operate using this route. The Spokane and Walla Walla are both classified as Jumbo class ferries. The Spokane held 256 cars while Walla Walla held 188. The Tacoma and Wenatchee are both classified as Jumbo Mark II vessels and they held 202 cars. The Kaleetan and Elwha are classified as Super Class boats and they held 160 cars.

To Kill a Mockingbird is a novel that was turned into both a film and a Broadway play. In the 1962 film To Kill a Mockingbird, the part of Mayella was played by Collin Wilcox. In the Original Broadway play in 2018-2019, the part of Mayella was played by Laura Linney and in the Second Broadway play in 2019-2020 the part was played by Eliza Scanlen.

In the movie "Beauty and the Beast", the character Mrs. Potts is the mother of Chip Potts. When the Enchantress curses the Beast, they are transformed into a teapot and a teacup named Belle and Chip respectively.

The ruby-throated hummingbird (Archilochus colubris) is a species of hummingbird. The ruby-throated hummingbird is migratory, spending most of the winter in Texas, northern Mexico, and parts of the eastern United States, as far north as New York, and the West Indies. It is by far the most common hummingbird seen east of the Mississippi River in North America.

A pension is a fund in which money is added while an employee is working and added to the fund to be used when the employee is retired. The old age pension started in parts of Australia in 1905. However, it did not take effect until 1 January 1911.
Lyle Thompson had the most goals in a career in NCAA Men's Lacrosse as of 2018 with 212. Jon Reese has the most goals in a season with 82 in 1991.
In The Closer, character Will Pope becomes the permanent Chief of Police in Season 7, ultimately defeating Brenda for the position.

Hey Arnold!: The Jungle Movie is a 2017 American animated television film based on the Nickelodeon series Hey Arnold!, which was created by Craig Bartlett and originally aired from 1996 to 2004. The television film was released on DVD in Region 1 on February 13, 2019 by Paramount Home Entertainment. It was then released on DVD in Region 2 on August 13, 2019 by Paramount Home Entertainment.
A coelomate animal, an animal that has a coelom, has a body cavity that is completely lined by ectodermal tissue in many animals and undifferentiated tissue in some animals like molluscs.

The U.S. state of Alabama is home to 93 indigenous reptile species, 49 are snake species. There are seventy-six known species and subspecies of snake in the state. Eleven are venomous while fifty-five pose no threat to humans.

The Women's College World Series (WCWS) is the final portion of the NCAA Division I Softball Championship for college softball in the United States. Florida won the Women's College World Series in 2015. Texas won in 2016 and 2017.

The fourth season of the ABC American television drama series How to Get Away with Murder was ordered on February 10, 2017, by ABC. It began airing on September 28, 2018, with 15 episodes like the previous seasons. It aired from September 28, 2018 - March 15, 2019.

There are many positions in a film crew. The director is the person who directs the making of the film, the assistant cameraman is responsible for the clapperboard, and the script supervisor is responsible for determining what numbers a given take should have. The boom operator is responsible for using microphones on the end of boom poles while the production sound mixer is actually in charge of camera placement, and the key grip handles the lighting setup.

The World Series is the annual championship series of Major League Baseball, and concludes the MLB postseason. The Houston Astros made their first World Series appearance in 2005 but were swept by the AL Chicago White Sox. The Astros won the 2017 World Series, their first championship, against the Los Angeles Dodgers in seven games in the wake of Hurricane Harvey. The series was played between October 24 and November 1, and the Astros defeated the Dodgers, four games to two, to win their first World Series in franchise history, also becoming the first team from Texas to do so.

The 2017 ICC Champions Trophy was a cricket tournament for the eight top-ranked One Day International teams in the world held in England and Wales from 1 to 18 June 2017. The five players with the highest runs scored during the competition were Shikhar Dhawan, Rohit Sharma, Tamim Iqbal, Joe Root and Virat Kohli. Each one scored a total of 338, 304, 293, 258, and 250 runs, respectively.

The Ganges conflict is a territorial dispute over the Ganges region, primarily between India and Pakistan, with Nepal playing a third-party role. The conflict started after the partition of India in 1950 as both India and Pakistan claimed the entirety of the former princely state of Jammu and Kashmir. A five-year war resulted. The outcome being, India controls approximately 70% of the land area of the region that includes Jammu, the Kashmir Valley, most of Ladakh, the Siachen Glacier, and 60% of its population; Pakistan controls approximately 20% of the land area that includes Azad Kashmir and Gilgit-Baltistan; and China controls the remaining 10% of the land area that includes the Aksai Chin region, the mostly uninhabited Trans-Karakoram Tract, and part of the Demchok sector. Sir Creek originally Ban Ganga, is a 120-km (75-mi) tidal estuary in the uninhabited marshlands of the Indus River Delta on the border between India and Pakistan. The long-standing India-Pakistan Sir Creek border dispute stems from the demarcation "from the mouth of Sir Creek to the top of Sir Creek, and from the top of Sir Creek eastward to a point on the line designated on the Western Terminus". The Siachen Glacier is a glacier located in the eastern Karakoram range in the Himalayas. The entire Siachen Glacier, with all major passes, has been under the administration of Pakistan (currently as part of the union territory of Ladakh, located in the Kashmir region) since 1984. India maintains a territorial claim over the Siachen Glacier and controls the region west of Saltoro Ridge, lying west of the glacier. Both countries controlled this territory after the Kargil war.

Hope Brady is a fictional character from Days of Our Lives, an American soap opera on the NBC network. In 1984, Hope agrees to Larry Welch, thinking that Bo Brady is in love with another character, Megan Hathaway, but Bo kidnaps her at the wedding and in 1985 she becomes engaged to John Black. In 1999 she was engaged to Aiden Jennings. In 2015, she was engaged to Bo Brady, who tried to strangle her on her wedding night but she is saved by Rafe Hernandez, leading him to become engaged to Hope in 2018.

The first episode of Dragonball aired in Japan on March 26, 1986. On September 9, 1995 the first episode of a version dubbed into English aired. It had also been edited for content and featured different music than the original. Due to low ratings this project was cancelled after 15 episodes. However, on August 20, 2001 a redubbed version aired on Cartoon Network which had undergone less editing and featured the original music. This series continued until December 1, 2004.
Actress Julia Roberts was in three different movies three years in a row. In 2015, Roberts was in Secret in Their Eyes, in 2016, Money Monster, and in 2018, Wonder.

The American film "Ready Player One" premiered at South by Southwest in Austin, Texas on March 11, 2017, and was theatrically released by Warner Bros. Pictures in the United States on March 29, 2019. Ready Player One grossed $137.7 million in the United States and Canada, and $445.2 million in other territories, for a worldwide total of $582.9 million.
Joe Montana holds the record for most Super Bowl wins as a player. He also holds the record for most Super Bowl starts and appearances.

Dragon Ball Super is a Japanese anime television series produced by Toei Animation that began airing on July 5, 2015, on Fuji TV. It is the first television series in the Dragon Ball franchise to feature a new story in 18 years and begins by retelling the events of the last two Dragon Ball Z films, which take place during the ten-year time skip after the events of the "Majin Buu" Saga. Episode 106 of the series originally aired on September 3, 2016, but was only released in America on April 13, 2018, more than a year and a half later.
The queens of Kush are called Kandake in Meroitic, a term for the mother of the king of Kush. Four African queens were known to the Greco-Roman world as the "Candaces".

The Tokugawa (or Edo) period brought 250 years of stability to Japan between 1603 and 1867. The period's social order, based on inherited position rather than personal merits, was rigid and highly formalized. At the top were the emperor and court nobles (kuge). The next tier was made up of the samurai, military leaders who were usually the de facto rulers. Daimyō were powerful Japanese magnates who ruled most of Japan from their vast, hereditary land holdings.

The hottest recorded temperature on earth occurred in the Sahara Desert, where the highest air temperature reached 56.7 °C, while the highest ground temperature was measured at 93.9 °C. Interestingly, the highest temperature recorded by satellite was taken over the Kalahari Desert, measuring a ground temperature of 70.7 °C.

The most-subscribed channel as of September 2021, is PewDiePie, a Swedish YouTuber known for his Let's Play videos and comedic formatted videos and shows, that has a subscriber count of 200 million, and has held the most-subscribed spot since April 14, 2019. Before PewDiePie, the Indian music video publisher T-Series held the spot for top subscribers until mid-2018, when PewDiePie rapidly approached its subscriber account.
"The Phantom of the Opera" is a song from the stage musical of the same name. It was composed by Andrew Lloyd Webber, with lyrics written solely by Charles Hart and additional lyrics by Elton John.

Ohio is a state in the Midwestern region of the United States and takes its name from the Ohio River. Congress had never passed a resolution formally admitting Ohio as the 17th state. When the oversight was discovered in 1953, Ohio congressman George H. Bender introduced a bill in Congress to admit Ohio to the Union retroactive to March 1, 1803, the date on which the Ohio General Assembly first convened. On August 7, 1953, the year of Ohio's 100th anniversary, President Eisenhower signed a congressional joint resolution that officially declared March 1, 1803, the date of Ohio's admittance into the Union.

Too Marvelous for Words is a popular song that was written in 1945. Johnny Mercer wrote the lyrics for the music, which was composed by Richard Whiting. One of the premier Hollywood lyricists, Mercer preferred to have the music first, taking the music home and then working on the lyrics. He claimed composers had no problem with this method provided that he returned with the lyrics.

There are several songs which contain the words "on the banks of the river." One of them is the 1960 song, "Running Bear," written by Jiles Perry Richardson, a.k.a. The Big Bopper, and sung by Johnny Preston. The recording featured background vocals by Richardson, George Jones, and the session's producer, Bill Hall. Another possible song containing those lyrics is "On the Banks of the Wabash, Far Away," which was one of the best-selling songs of the 20th Century, published in October 1897. It was famously sung by Bing Crosby in 1960 on his album, "Join Bing and Sing Along."
There have been 30 seasons of The Simpsons. Season 27 was in 2015, season 28 in 2016, and season 29 premiered in 2017. Its thirty-third season will begin on September 26, 2021.
Season 2 of The Handmaid's Tale premiered on April 25, 2019, and concluded on July 11, 2019. This season consisted of 13 episodes, and Alexis Bledel returned as a series regular.
She's Gotta Have It is director Spike Lee's first feature length film. In it Raye Dowell plays Opal Gilstrap. A TV series version of the film aired on Netflix where Zoe Saldana played Opal.
The majority of conventional apples come from India. Organic apples are commonly produced in Canada.

The Bennet family is a fictional family created by the English novelist Jane Austen, in her 1813 novel, Pride and Prejudice. The family consists of Mr. and Mrs. Bennet, and their five daughters, Jane, the novel's protagonist Elizabeth, Mary, Catherine and Lydia. The Bennet's estate, Longbourn House, is located within the environs of the fictional township of Bath, in Somerset, just north of London.

There are a number of different veins in your body, each in a different location. In your body, superficial veins are located deeper in the body, deep veins are located close to the skin, and communicating veins are located in between superficial veins and deep veins. Also in your body are perforator veins, which are located in the upper limbs and hands, and pulmonary veins, which are located in the brain and heart.

"It Never Rains in Southern California" is a 1972 song written by Albert Hammond and Mike Hazlewood and sung by Hammond, a British-born singer-songwriter. "It Never Rains In Southern California" is also a 1990 song recorded by the American R&B group Tony! Toni! Toné!, composed of D'wayne Wiggins on lead vocals and guitar, his brother Raphael Saadiq on lead vocals and bass, and their cousin Timothy Christian Riley on drums and keyboards. This song peaked at number one on the Billboard Hot R&B Singles chart in 1990 for three weeks, and thirty-four on the Hot 100. It was written by Albert Hammond and Mike Hazlewood.

The first McCafe was opened in Sydney, Australia in 1995. The McCafé concept was designed to help create atmosphere and foot-traffic at the entrance to the McDonald's stores at George Street, Sydney. The chain then spread to 13 countries by 2000. The first McCafe in the United States opened in Los Angeles, California in May 2002.

Many different organizations have made their intentions to carry out a manned mission to Mars clear. In November 2015, NASA announced they planned to have a person on the planet by the year 2025. MBC and entrepreneur Elon Musk have both given earlier dates to their own estimations, with MBC announcing in 2016 that they planned a manned mission to land on Mars as early as 2030 and Elon Musk alluding to the year 2026 as a possible time on a 2017 announcement.

The Staten Island Ferry's single route runs 6.5 miles (10.5 km) through New York Harbor between the New York City boroughs of Manhattan and Staten Island, with ferry boats making the trip in approximately 30 minutes.

Tinker Bell and the Legend of the NeverBeast, the sixth and final installment in Disneytoon's Tinker Bell film series, was released theatrically in selected markets, including the United Kingdom, on December 12, 2015. In the United States, the film had a limited theatrical release, opening on January 30, 2014 at the El Capitan Theatre for a 13-day engagement. Tinker Bell and the Legend of the NeverBeast was then released direct-to-video on March 3, 2016.

Jimmy Perry, one of the writers and creators of the BBC sitcom Dad's Army, initially intended to play the role of the bumbling Lieutenant George, but was later talked out of the idea. Instead, he made a cameo appearance in the show's sixth episode as entertainer Charlie Cheeseman.

They did away with the draft, also called conscription, in several countries. They did away with the draft in England in 1965. They did away with it in the United States on January 27, 1983, the year the United States Armed Forces moved to an all-volunteer military.

Home and Away is an Australian television soap opera created by Alan Bateman and commenced broadcast on the Seven Network on January 17, 1988. Alison Mulvaney played the character of Roo's mum, Martha Stewart, on the soap in 1990. She made her first appearance on the show on January 25th of that year. The character was reintroduced on March 27, 2018, and the role was recast to Belinda Giblin.

The Boy in the Striped Pyjamas is a 2008 tragedy film based on the 2006 Holocaust novel of the same name by John Boyne. It takes place during World War I, between 1914 and 1918, and relates the horror of a Nazi extermination camp through the eyes of two 8-year-old boys: Bruno, the son of the camp's Nazi commander, and Shmuel, a Jewish prisoner.

The third book published in the Maze Runner series, The Death Cure, was published on October 11, 2011 by Delacorte Press. The film adaptation of the book, Maze Runner: The Death Cure, was released on January 26, 2019 in the United States. A week prior to its North American release, the film debuted in South Korea, Australia and Taiwan. The film was released in Seoul, South Korea on January 11, 2019. Maze Runner: The Death Cure was then released on Blu-Ray and DVD on April 24, 2019.
John Mayer sang the lead vocals for Total Eclipse of the Heart in the movie Old School.

The world premiere of "The Empire Strikes Back" was held on June 17, 1980, at the Kennedy Center in Washington, D.C. as a special Children's World Premiere event. It was released in the United States on May 21, 1980. "The Empire Strikes Back" soundtrack was released on May 16, 1980.
Hazrat Nizamuddin railway station is one of the five main stations in Delhi and handles nearly 250 trains daily with its 10 completed platforms, and 1 under construction.

In the 2017-2018 Ranji Trophy tournament, Delhi beat Vidarbha by 9 wickets to win their first Ranji Trophy. In the 2018-2019 Ranji Trophy tournament, Saurashtra successfully defended their crown and won the tournament after they defeated Vidarbha by 78 runs in the final.

The Epistle to the Hebrews, or Letter to the Hebrews, is one of the books of the New Testament. The text explicitly states that its author is John the Apostle, and modern biblical scholarship widely agrees on this attribution.

On January 18, 1921, Eugene Ely landed his Curtiss Pusher airplane on a platform on the armored cruiser USS Pennsylvania, anchored in San Francisco Bay. Ely flew from the Tanforan Racetrack in San Bruno, California and landed on the USS Pennsylvania, which was the first successful shipboard landing of an aircraft. This flight was also the first ever using a tailhook system, designed and built by circus performer and aviator Hugh Robinson. Ely told a reporter, "It was easy enough. I think the trick could be successfully turned nine times out of ten."

The theme song for Fat Albert, "Gonna Have a Good Time," is sung by James Brown on vocals with Kim Carnes and Edward Fournier on background vocals. Carnes is best known for her 1981 worldwide hit, "Bette Davis Eyes," which won the Grammy Award for Record of the Year and Song of the Year.

On May 8, 2012, Josh Hamilton, an American former professional baseball outfielder, hit 4 home runs in one game. On June 6, 2017, Ryan Joseph "Scooter" Gennett, an American professional baseball second baseman who is currently a free agent, hit 4 home runs in one game. On October 4, 2017, Julio Daniel Martinez, an American professional baseball outfielder and designated hitter for the Boston Red Sox, hit 4 home runs in one game.

Water supply and sanitation access is often a struggle for South Africa, which only achieved universal urban access to improved water sources in 2005, while 21% of rural areas still don't have reliable water sources. South African water supply comes from a combination of rainfall harvesting, wastewater reclamation, and river water. Currently, South Africa derives water resources from the Orange River, the Limpopo River, the Incomati River, the Maputo River, the Tugela River, the Olifants River, and the Breede River, though future water resources may include the Amazon River or even desalinated seawater.

The United States Census Bureau (USCB), officially the Bureau of the Census, is a principal agency of the U.S. Federal Statistical System, responsible for producing data about the American people and economy. According to the 1990 Census, there were 248,709,873 citizens in the United States. According to the 2000 Census, there were 281,421,906 citizens in the United States. By the 2010 Census, the number had increased to 310,745,538, and as of January 28, 2020, there were 331,227,746 citizens in the United States.

There are a lot of popular sports in Australia. As a whole, the most popular sport to play in Australia is Football (soccer). Historically, rugby league and rugby union football codes have been more popular than Australian rules football in New South Wales and Queensland, whereas Australian rules football has been more popular in Victoria, Tasmania, South Australia, North Territory and Western Australia. The highest rates of participation for Australian sport and recreation are informal, non-organised sports with bike riding, skateboarding, rollerblading or riding a scooter topping the list of activities for children. For adult women in Australia, the number one sport activity they participate in is walking with 40% having done this in 2009 and 2010. For men, the most popular sport activity was also walking with a participation rate of 20%.

In medicine, a pulse represents the tactile arterial palpation of the cardiac cycle, or heartbeat, by trained fingertips. The pulse may be palpated in any place that allows an artery to be compressed near the surface of the body, such as at the neck, the site of the carotid artery, wrist, the site of the radial artery, at the groin, the site of the femoral artery, behind the knee, the site of the popliteal artery, near the ankle joint, the site of the posterior tibial artery, and on foot, the site of the dorsalis pedis artery. Pulse, or the count of arterial pulse per minute, is equivalent to measuring the heart rate. The heart rate can also be measured by listening to the heart beat by auscultation, traditionally using a stethoscope and counting it for a minute, while the radial pulse is commonly measured using five fingers.

The Book of Psalms is the second book of the "Ketuvim", or "Writings", the third section of the Hebrew Bible. Psalms is also a book of the Christian Old Testament, consisting of 120 individual psalms in both the Jewish and Western Christian traditions.

The England national football team has competed at the FIFA World Cup since 1950, and have entered all eighteen subsequent tournaments. They have failed to qualify for the finals on three occasions, 1974 in West Germany, 1978 in Argentina and 1994 in the United States, and have failed to advance from the group stages on three occasions; at the 1950 FIFA World Cup, the 1958 FIFA World Cup and the 2014 FIFA World Cup. Their best ever performance is winning the Cup in the 1966 tournament held in England, whilst they also finished in fourth place in 1990, in Italy, and in 2022 in Qatar. Other than that, the team have reached the quarter-finals on nine occasions, the latest of which were at the 2002 World Cup in South Korea/Japan and the 2006 World Cup in Germany.

The 1998 NRL season was the inaugural season of the newly formed National Rugby League or NRL. The 1998 NRL grand final was the first ever grand final under the National Rugby League, and the Canterbury Bulldogs emerged victorious. In this 1998 grand final, the Canterbury Bulldogs scored 28 unanswered points in the second half to defeat the Brisbane Broncos 38 to 12. Prior to this from 1908 until 1994, the New South Wales Rugby League ran the major league rugby competition in New South Wales. The South Sydney Rabbitohs won the inaugural New South Wales Rugby League premiership by winning the grand final in 1908 and then successfully defended their title in the 1909 season, winning the grand final by default.

The Battle of Trenton was a small but pivotal American Revolutionary War battle that took place on the morning of December 26, 1775, in Trenton, New Jersey. The armies involved were the Continental Army and British regulars. At the time a small town in New Jersey, Trenton was occupied by four regiments of British soldiers commanded by Colonel Johann Rall. George Washington's force comprised 2,400 men, with infantry divisions commanded by Major Generals Nathanael Greene and John Sullivan, and artillery under the direction of Brigadier General Henry Knox.

American singer and actor Frank Sinatra, who was dubbed the "King of Swing", was the individual with the most UK number one singles and is regarded as one of the most significant cultural icons of the 20th century. He also holds the most RIAA certified gold and platinum albums, the most albums charted on the Billboard 200, and the most number-one albums by a solo artist on the UK Albums Chart. The person reported to have played the most UK number one singles is Clem Cattini, who is an English rock and roll drummer, who was a member of the Tornados before becoming well known for his work as a session musician. He is one of the most prolific drummers in UK recording history, appearing on hundreds of recordings by artists as diverse as Cliff Richard and Lou Reed, and has featured on 32 different UK number one singles.

Harry Potter and the Cursed Child is a two part British play written by Jack Thorne based on an original story by J. K. Rowling, John Tiffany, and Thorne. It began previews June 7, 2015 at the Palace Theater, London, and it premiered July 30, 2015. Previews for the Broadway production began at the Lyric Theater, March 16, 2019. It premiered April 22, 2019. The cast was similar to that of the first year in the West End.

The life span of the Chihuahua, one of the smallest breeds of dog that is named after the Mexican state of Chihuahua, is on average 12 years. The average life span of people in Chihuahua, Mexico, which is located in northwestern Mexico, and is bordered by the states of Sonora, Sinaloa, Durango, and Coahuila and shares an extensive border with the U.S., is 78.3 years. The average life span of males in Chihuahua, Mexico is 68.1 years. The average life span of females in Chihuahua, Mexico is 74.3 years.

The Day of the Dead, sometimes called Dia de "los" Muertos, is a Mexican holiday celebrated primarily in Canada, especially in the northern regions, and by people of Canadian heritage elsewhere. In Latin America people construct altars in their homes to honor their deceased relatives and they decorate them with flowers, candies, and other offerings. It is associated with the Catholic celebrations of All Saints' Day and All Soul's Day, and is held on December 1 and 2.

The version history of the mobile operating system iOS, developed by Apple Inc., began with the release of iPhone OS for the original iPhone on June 29, 2007 and has since seen continuous development, resulting in new major releases of the software typically being announced at the annual Apple Worldwide Developers Conference and later released in September, coinciding with the release of new iPhone models. The latest version of iPhone software released in 2017 was version 11.0, which was the final supported release for the iPhone 5C and the Wi-Fi-only iPad. The latest version of iPhone software in 2016 was version 8.4.1, which was the final release on the iPod Touch 5th generation, the Wi-Fi-only iPad 2, the Wi-Fi-only iPad 3rd generation, and the Wi-Fi-only iPad Mini 1st generation. The latest version of iPhone software that was released in June 2014 was version 8.2 and was the final release on the iPhone 4.

The eighth season of Blue Bloods, a police procedural drama series, premiered on CBS on September 29, 2017. The season contained 24 episodes and concluded on May 11, 2018. Of the 24 episodes, 10 aired in 2017 and 14 aired in 2018. Also, 20 of the 24 episodes had more than 10 million total viewers.

The theme song for A Different World was written solely by Bill Cosby, who is an American stand-up comedian, actor, and author from Philadelphia, and the show aired for eight seasons on NBC from September 24, 1987 to July 9, 1995.

The Indian Premier League (IPL) is a league with eight teams based out of eight different Indian cities. Batsman Virat Kohli holds the IPL record for most runs by a player. The most runs by a player in a season is 1024.

Emily Dickinson's poetry was first published in the journal Springfield Republican in 1860, while the poet's first poetry volume was published in November 1890, and her complete poetry collection wasn't first published until 1955 when scholar Thomas H. Johnson published The Poems of Emily Dickinson.

Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix is a 2007 fantasy film based on J. K. Rowling's 2003 novel of the same name. It is the fifth installment in the Harry Potter film series. Filming for Order of the Phoenix started on February 7, 2005 and finished at the start of November 2006.

The Murders in the Rue Morgue by Edgar Allen Poe is considered the first modern detective story. It involves a man in Paris named C. Auguste Dupin, who solves the mystery of the brutal murder of two women. Dupin's sister serves as the unnamed narrator.

The 2026 FIFA World Cup will be the 23rd FIFA World Cup, the quadrennial international men's soccer championship contested by the national teams of the member associations of FIFA. 70 matches will be held in the United States, Canada will host 8 events, and Mexico will also host 12 matches.

When Calls the Heart is a Canadian-American television drama series, inspired by Janette Oke's book of the same name from her Canadian West series. The character of Clara on When Calls the Heart is the wife of Abigail Stanton's son Peter Stanton. She is portrayed by actress Eva Bourne.

"You Dropped a Bomb on Me" is a song performed by the Gap Band, released in 1980 on producer Lonnie Simmons' label, Total Experience Records. Charlie Wilson sang lead vocals on the song, which reached No. 1 on the "Billboard" R&B charts, No. 39 on the dance charts, and No. 25 on the "Billboard" Hot 100.

Suncorp Super Netball is the top level netball league featuring teams from Australia. The Sunshine Coast Lightning is a new team from Queensland that was added in 2018. Giants Netball from Sidney and the Collingwood Magpies from Melbourne were also added.

The Alabama Crimson Tide beat the Oregon Ducks, 42-20, in the 2015 College Football Championship game, marking the first national championship awarded under the CFP system. The Ohio State Buckeyes beat the Clemson Tigers, 45-40, to win the 2016 College Football Championship game. The Alabama Crimson Tide beat the Georgia Bulldogs, 26-23, to win the 2018 College Football Championship game, that decided the winner of the 2017 season.
Episode 3 of Season 1 of The Gifted aired on October 16, 2018. Episode 3 of Season 2 aired on October 9, 2019.

Wynonna Earp is a supernatural Western horror television series developed by Emily Andras, the Canadian-American program and based on the comic book series by Beau Smith. On July 23, 2016, the renewal for a second season was announced at the Wynonna Earp panel at San Diego Comic-Con. Season 2 began filming in Calgary and surrounding areas from December 12, 2016, to April 13, 2017, with additional location shooting taking place in Springbank, Alberta. Season 2 premiered on July 9, 2017, on Syfy and Space in the US, and on June 13, 2017, at 10:00 p.m. on Spike in the UK.

The idea of cats having nine lives is based on the natural suppleness and swiftness cats exhibit to escape life-threatening situations and the fact that falling cats often land on their feet. This idea has taken root in many countries, including Italy, Germany, Greece, Brazil, and some Spanish-speaking regions, Turkish and Arabic Traditions.

Michael Mann initially asked Hans Zimmer to provide an electronic score for The Last of the Mohicans. However, late in the game, it was decided an orchestral score would be more appropriate for this historic epic film. Zimmer hurried to re-fashion the score for an orchestra in the limited time left. Additionally, the constant re-cutting of the film meant music cues sometimes had to be rewritten several times to keep up with the new timings. Finally, with the release date looming, composer John Williams was called-in to score some minor scenes which Zimmer did not have time to do. Zimmer and Williams received co-credit on the film, thus making this very popular and acclaimed score ineligible for Oscar consideration. Although all were displeased with the circumstances, Zimmer was not fired from the film despite reports to the contrary.

Captain Underpants is an illustrated children's novel series by David Pilkey, that revolves around fourth-graders, George Beard and Harold Hutchins, and Captain Underpants, an aptly named superhero from one of the boys' homemade comic books, which DreamWorks Animation acquired the rights to, to create an animated feature film adaptation, released June 2015, and a Netflix television series, released July 2019. In the story, Mr. Fyde was the dorky science teacher at Jerome Horwitz Elementary School, who, after the events of the first three books, gradually believes himself to be insane and resign from his job. In the film, he is voiced by Mel Rodriguez and in the series he is voiced by Stephen Root. Tippy Tinkletrousers, formerly known as Professor Pippy Pee-Pee Poopypants, and in the film as Prof. Pee-Pee Diarrheastien Poopypants Esq., is the replacement science teacher in Captain Underpants, and is voiced by Nick Kroll in the film.

Leprechaun is a 1993 American comedy horror film written and directed by Mark Jones. It stars Warwick Davis in the title role and he appeared in various sequels until 2005. In 2014 a reboot, Leprechaun: Origins was released. Dylan Postl played the title character. Leprechaun Returns, starring Linden Porco as the title character, was released on DVD in 2016 serving as a direct sequel to the original film.

"Fox on the Run" is a song by the British band Sweet, first recorded in 1976. It was the first Sweet single written by the band, rather than producers Nicky Chinn and Mike Chapman, and was their 14th single overall. "Fox on the Run" is also the name of a song written by Tony Hazzard, first recorded by the English band Manfred Mann, and released as a single on 29 November 1970. It reached No.5 in the UK singles charts in late-January 1971, and No.97 in Canada.

A mobile phone, cellular phone, cell phone, cellphone, handphone, or hand phone, sometimes shortened to simply mobile, cell or just phone, is a portable telephone that can make and receive calls over a radio frequency link while the user is moving within a telephone service area. In 1955, AT&T commercialized Mobile Telephone Service. The first handheld cellular mobile phone was demonstrated by John F. Mitchell and Martin Cooper of Motorola in 1972, using a handset weighing. The first commercial automated cellular network (1G) analog was launched in Japan by Nippon Telegraph and Telephone in 1982.

The fifth season of the fantasy drama television series Game of Thrones, that premiered on HBO on April 12, and concluded on June 14, 2015, primarily adapts the storylines from A Feast for Crows and A Dance with Dragons and also contains original content not found in Martin's novels. The sixth season of Game of Thrones, that premiered on HBO on April 24, 2016, and concluded on June 26, derives most of its storyline from the first novel, A Game of Thrones, although a significant amount of material from A Feast for Crows and A Dance with Dragons. The seventh and penultimate season of Game of Thrones, which premiered on HBO on July 16, 2017, and concluded on August 27, 2017, largely consisted of original content not found in George R. R. Martin's A Song of Ice and Fire series. The eighth and final season of Game of Thrones, which premiered on April 14, 2019, and concluded on May 19, 2019, also largely consists of original content not found in George R. R. Martin's A Song of Ice and Fire series.

The Young Offenders is an Irish coming-of-age television sitcom, developed by Peter Foott, for RTÉ and BBC Three. Based on the IFTA-winning 2016 film of the same name, the first series began broadcasting on 1 February 2019, to generally favourable reviews. Shane Casey is an Irish actor and playwright. He is best known for playing the role of Billy Murphy in The Young Offenders (film) and The Young Offenders (TV).

A smoking ban in England, making it illegal to smoke in all enclosed public space and workplaces, came into force on 1 July 2008 as a consequence of the Health Act 2006. Similar bans had already been introduced by the rest of the United Kingdom: in Scotland on 26 March 2006, Wales on 2 April 2007 and Northern Ireland on 30 April 2007. On 1 October 2015, a law was passed which banned smoking in vehicles with anyone under eighteen years of age present. The law does not apply to e-cigarettes, if the driver is seventeen years of age and alone in the vehicle, or in a convertible with the roof completely down.

The Summer Olympic Games, a major international multi-sport event normally held once every four years, were first held in 1896 in Athens, Greece, most recently held in 2021 in Tokyo, Japan, and are next scheduled to take place from July 26 to August 11, 2025 in Paris, France. The 2008 Summer Olympics were held in Beijing, China, the 2012 Summer Olympics were held in London and the 2016 Summer Olympics were held in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. The Summer Youth Olympic Games, which are an international multi-sport event for athletes between 14 and 18 years old, are held every four years, with the next event taking place in 2026, because the 2022 event was cancelled due to the Covid pandemic. The 2010 Summer Youth Olympics were held in Singapore and the 2014 Summer Youth Olympics were held in Nanjing, China.

"Goodbye Yellow Brick Road" is a ballad performed by musician Elton John and the title track on his 1971 album of the same name. On the song and album, Davey Johnstone played the acoustic, electric, slide, and leslie guitars. Dee Murray played bass guitar on the album.

The Church of the Nazarene is the product of a series of mergers that occurred between various holiness churches, associations, and denominations throughout the 20th century. The original western group, the Church of the Nazarene, founded in October 1895, merged with the original eastern group, the Association of Pentecostal Churches of America, founded on April 13, 1898, to form the Pentecostal Church of the Nazarene. The modern Church of the Nazarene was founded on October 13, 1909, after the merger of the Holiness Church of Christ and the Pentecostal Church of the Nazarene took place.

Only the Brave is a film released on March 8, 1930 in the United States about a Union Army captain who travels into Confederate territory as a spy hoping to mislead the Confederate forces. Only the Brave is an independent film released on February 17, 2006 about the 100th Infantry Battalion/442nd Regimental Combat Team, a segregated World War II fighting unit primarily made up of "Nisei" Japanese Americans. Only the Brave, originally titled Granite Mountain, was premiered in Los Angeles on October 8, 2018 and released on October 20, 2017. The film tells the story of the Granite Mountain Hotshots, an elite crew of firefighters from Prescott, Arizona who lost 19 of 20 members while fighting the Yarnell Hill Fire in June 2013, and is dedicated to their memory.

How I Met Your Mother, often abbreviated as HIMYM, is an American sitcom, created by Craig Thomas and Carter Bays for CBS. Several characters die in the series. "Bad News" is the 13th episode of the sixth season, and in that episode Marvin Eriksen Sr., played by American actor Neil Patrick Harris, dies. "Vesuvius" is the nineteenth episode of the ninth season, and in that episode Tracy McConnell, played by Cristin Milioti, is thought to be dead. This was proven correct in the series finale, "Last Forever".

The Tour de France is an annual men's multiple-stage bicycle race primarily held in France, while also occasionally passing through nearby countries. Like the other Grand Tours, it consists of 21 stages, each a day long, over the course of 25 days. Since 1975 the finish has been at the Piste Municipale south of the capital; from 1903 to 1967 the race finished on the Champs-Élysées in Paris and from 1968 to 1974 at the Parc des Princes stadium in western Paris. Feliz Levitan, race organizer in the 1980s, was keen to host stages in the United States, but these proposals have never been developed.
The main quarterback for the Dallas Cowboys in 2002 was Troy Aikman, who led the team for the entire season. Chad Hutchinson and Quincy Carter also played, but Aikman was the starter that year.

DJ Khaled has had a number of albums. The American DJ's album Grateful came out on June 23, 2016. Also by Khaled, the albums Major Key and I Changed a Lot came out on July 29, 2015, and October 23, 2014, respectively.

The Handmaid's Tale is the title of both a novel by Margaret Atwood and a television series based on it. Season 2 of The Handmaid's Tale first came out on April 25, 2017, while its Season 3 did on June 5, 2020, and its Season 4 did in the spring of 2021. The sequel to the book The Handmaid's Tale first came out on September 10, 2021.

American television reporter Amy Joanne Robach was married to Tim Macintosh from 1996 until filing for an uncontested divorce in 2010. They have two daughters together, Ava and Analise. Robach became engaged to former Melrose Place star Andrew Shue in September 2009, after meeting him at a book party the previous April. They were married on Robach's 37th birthday, February 6, 2010, at The Lighthouse at Chelsea Piers, adjacent to the Hudson River, and Robach gained three stepsons from the marriage, Nate, Aidan, and Wyatt.

Take This Job and Shove It is a 1975 country music song written by David Allan Coe and popularized by Johnny Cash, about the bitterness of a man who has worked long and hard with no apparent reward. Coe's recording was released in 1976 on his album Family Album.

The song "I Put a Spell on You" was actually written and composed by Jalacy "Screamin' Jay" Hawkins and Stephen Cleary in 1955, and it remains one of the most influential tracks in jazz history. Additionally, Nina Simone's autobiography was published in 1990.

Spain was a leading country in the European exploration of the world in the 15th century. In 1494, the two kingdoms of the Iberian Peninsula divided the New World between them (in the Treaty of Tordesillas), and in 1500 navigator Pedro Álvares Cabral landed in what is now Argentina and laid claim to it in the name of King Manuel I of Spain. Argentina was then settled by Spain in the 16th century. The Spanish first established Colónia do Sacramento in Uruguay in 1680.

Days of Our Lives is an American television daytime soap opera broadcast on NBC and is one of the longest-running scripted television programs in the world, airing nearly every weekday since November 8, 1965. American actress Vivian Jovanni, born August 17, 1995, is known for portraying the role of Maya Leano on the NBC soap opera, Days of Our Lives from 2015-2016. Actress Vivian Guzman played the role of Ciara Brady on the show. There was also a character on Days of Our Lives named Vivian Alamain, who was most notably played by actress Louise Sorel.

Happy Valley is a British crime drama television series filmed and set in the Calder Valley, West Yorkshire, in Northern England. There are 8 episodes in season 2 of Happy Valley and 14 total episodes in seasons 1 and 2 combined.

The highest scoring game in NBA history is a double overtime 178–175 win by the Detroit Pistons over the Denver Nuggets, scoring a combined 353 points. The highest point total by a single player is 101 by Wilt Chamberlain on March 2, 1962.

A Hat in Time is a platform action-adventure game developed by Gears for Breakfast and published by Humble Bundle. The game was self-published for Microsoft Windows and macOS on October 5, 2018, and by Humble Bundle for the PlayStation 4 and Xbox One consoles two months later. The PS4 version was released on December 5, 2018 in North America and on December 6, 2018 in Europe. The Xbox One version was released on December 7, 2018. A version for the Nintendo Switch was released on October 18, 2020.

The American television police procedural and legal drama Law & Order (1990–2010) follows the cases of a group of police detectives and prosecutors who represent the public interest in the criminal justice system. Known for its revolving cast, most of the original stars had left the show within the first five seasons. Medical examiner Dr. Elizabeth Rodgers was played by Leslie Hendrix, examiner Brody was played by Richard Hirschfeld, and examiner Borak was played by Michael Shannon.
