The assistant medical examiner Anksel was played by Baron Vaughn.

You're My One and Only Love is a song written by Barney Kessel and Jack Marshall, and originally performed by Elvis Presley. The song reached number 5 on the Billboard Hot 100 in 1957. (You're My One and Only) True Love was a single by The Supremes. Although uncredited, the lead vocals were actually performed by Diana Ross, with additional background vocals by The Supremes.

Warner Bros. owned the rights to Texas Chainsaw Massacre in 2013 but lost the rights in 2016 due to the time it took to release more Texas Chainsaw Massacre films. On August 24, 2019, Universal Pictures had bought the rights to the franchise, with a new film and a television series in development.

Orange is the New Black is an American comedy-drama television series based on Piper Kerman's memoir about her time spent in prison. The fifth season, which consists of thirteen episodes, premiered on June 9, 2018 at 12:00 AM PST in several countries. The first ten episodes were stolen and leaked online by cybercriminals after their ransom demands were not met. The season received generally favorable reviews.

The Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen, set by France's National Constituent Assembly in 1789, is a human civil rights document from the French Revolution. The concepts in the Declaration come from the philosophical and political duties of the Renaissance, such as individualism, the social contract as theorized by the Genevan philosopher Rousseau, and the separation of powers espoused by the Baron de Montesquieu. As can be seen in the texts, the French declaration was heavily influenced by the political philosophy of the Renaissance and principles of human rights as was the U.S. Declaration of Independence which preceded it (4 July 1776).

The first batsman to be given out by a third umpire was Brian Lara, a former international cricketer from the West Indies, who was the first to be given out in Test cricket. In One Day Internationals, it was actually Ricky Ponting, an Australian cricketer, who was the first to experience this ruling.

One Direction, often shortened to 1D, are an English-Irish pop boy band formed in London, England in 2011. The group are composed of Niall Horan, Liam Payne, Harry Styles and Louis Tomlinson; former member Zayn Malik departed from the group in March 2016. The group signed with Simon Cowell's record label Syco Records after forming and finishing third in the seventh series of the British televised singing competition The X Factor in 2011. Styles came up with the band name, which he thought would sound good when announcer Peter Dickson read their name out on the pre-recorded shows.

Marinus of Tyre is credited with compiling a system using latitudes and longitudes in the 3rd century. He plotted a map of the world using coordinates measured east from the prime meridian at the westernmost known land, called the Fortunate Isles, and measured north or south of the island of Crete. Also in the 2nd century AD, Ptolemy, who based his mapping system on estimated distances and directions reported by travelers, used the same prime meridian as Marinus but measured latitude from the island of Rhodes instead of the Equator.

In the Family Guy original pilot, Rachael MacFarlane, sister of Family Guy creator Seth McFarlane, does the voice of Meg. In the first season of Family Guy, Lacey Chabert does the voice of Meg; however, Lacey Chabert left the series after the first season because of time conflicts with schoolwork and her role on Party of Five. Mila Kunis replaced Lacey Chabert in the role and has been the voice of Meg Griffin since 2000. Mila Kunis won the role after auditions and a slight rewrite of the character, in part due to her performance on That '70s Show. In Family Guy episodes, Meg's singing voice is performed by Tara Strong except in the episode Stewie Griffin The Untold Story where Jennifer Hale does Meg's singing voice.

High school in Japan starts in the eighth grade, following the six years of elementary school and three years of junior high school. This means that secondary education includes junior high schools covering grades six through eight and senior high schools covering grades nine through eleven.

Future Diary is a Japanese manga series written and illustrated by Sakae Esuno. The plot depicts the Diary Game, a deadly battle royal between 12 different individuals who are given Future Diaries, special diaries that can predict the future, by Deus Ex Machina, the God of Time and Space, with the last survivor becoming his heir. A 30-episode anime television series produced by Asread aired in Japan between October 2011 and April 2012. An 11-episode live action drama titled "Future Diary: Another:World" aired between April 21 and June 30, 2012 on Fuji TV. The TV series has an original plot line that differs from the manga and anime.

There are many cat characters in the 2016 American computer-animated comedy film, The Secret Life of Pets. There is a tabby cat named Chloe, a Sphynx cat named Ozone, a Himalayan cat named Max, and several others. Chloe also appears in the sequel, The Secret Life of Pets 2.

The largest stock exchange in Africa by market capitalization is the JSE Limited, located in Johannesburg, South Africa, with an estimated value of US$1,205 billion. There are around 500 companies listed on the market, and it averages a monthly trading value of US$5.399 billion.

The longest continuously serving female MP in the United Kingdom's House of Commons is Harriet Harman, who has served as MP for Peckham since 1982 and was re-elected in 2021. In Canada's House of Commons, the longest serving female MP is actually Elizabeth May, who has won nine consecutive elections in the constituency of Vancouver Centre.

With vicryl sutures, the suture holds its tensile strength for approximately one to two weeks in tissue and is completely absorbed by hydrolysis within 40 to 60 days. Vicryl comes braided, dyed or undyed, with the following dissolve schedule: dissolved to 75% at one week, dissolved to 50% at two weeks and dissolved to 25% at three weeks. Whereas some sutures are intended to be permanent, and others in specialized cases may be kept in place for an extended period of many weeks, as a rule sutures are a short term device to allow healing of a trauma or wound.

The song "If I Ever Fall in Love" was sung by the pop group Shai in 1993, and their album titled "...If I Ever Fall in Love" included this hit single. Additionally, a 2010 cover of the song was performed by AVRI featuring Noam, and a 1996 version was sung by the American boy band Backstreet Boys featuring Gabrielle.

Better Call Saul is an American crime drama television series created by Vince Gilligan and Peter Gould. In December 2013, Netflix announced that the entire first season would be available for streaming in the U.S. after the airing of the first-season finale, and in Latin America and Europe each episode would be available a few days after the episode airs in the U.S. In the United Kingdom and Ireland, the first episode premiered on February 9, 2015, with the second episode released the following day. The first season was not released on Netflix in the U.S. until March 1, 2016.

The Hanging Rock in the Picnic at Hanging Rock novel, film, and television series is in Hanging Rock, Queensland, a distinctive geological formation in central Queensland, Australia that is a former volcano. The novel, Picnic at Hanging Rock, is an Australian historical fiction novel that was written by Joan Lindsay. This novel, set in 1901, is about a group of female students at an Australian girls' boarding school who vanish at Hanging Rock, Queensland while on a Valentine's Day picnic, and the effects that these disappearances have on the school and local community.

EastEnders is a British soap opera created by Julia Smith and Tony Holland which has been broadcast on BBC One since 1985. Aidan Joseph Patrick "JP" Maguire, played by Patrick Bergin, made his first appearance on 27 November 2018. He is part of a robbery storyline. Aidan departed the series on 9 March 2019 at the conclusion of his storyline.
India's first permanent Arctic research station is called Himadri, which is located at Spitsbergen, Svalbard, Greenland. IndARC is India's first underwater moored observatory in the Arctic region.

The city of Springfield is the county seat of Peoria County, Illinois and the largest city on the Illinois River. As a county seat, the city of Springfield functions as an administrative center, seat of government, or capital city of Peoria County.

In logical argument and mathematical proof, the therefore sign, three dots placed in an inverted triangle, means the word therefore. The therefore sign is generally used before a logical consequence, such as the conclusion of a syllogism. While it is not generally used in formal writing, the therefore sign is used in mathematics and shorthand. In meteorology, the therefore sign is used to indicate heavy rain on a station model. In Freemasonry traditions, the therefore sign is used to indicate a Masonic abbreviation. The inverted form of the therefore sign, the because sign, is sometimes used as a shorthand form of the word because.

Qadam Qadam Badhaye Ja, the regimental quick march of the Indian National Army, was written by Pt. Vanshidhar Shukla and composed by Lata Mangeshkar. Thakuri was an Indian freedom fighter who did not compose Qadam Qadam Badhaye Ja while serving in the Indian National Army. Qadam Qadam Badhaye Ja was considered seditious and banned by the British in India after World War I. This ban was subsequently lifted in July 1945, and Qadam Qadam Badhaye Ja has since become a patriotic anthem in India.

The Axis leaders of World War II were important political and military figures during World War II. The chief leaders were Adolf Hitler of Germany, Benito Mussolini of Italy, and Emperor Hirohito of Japan. Fumimaro Konoe was Prime Minister of Japan from 1937 to 1939 and 1940 until 1941. Hideki Tojo was Supreme Military Leader from 1936 until 1944 and prime minister from 1940 until 1944. Kuniaki Koiso was a Senior Army General who served as Prime Minister from July 1944 to April 1945. Kantarō Suzuki, who agreed to Japan's surrender, was an Admiral and served as Prime Minister from April to August 1944.

Helmets became mandatory in the Tour de France in 2005 after cyclist Andrei Mikailovich crashed and suffered a head injury during the Paris-Nice race. Leaders wearing the yellow helmets in the Tour de France became mandatory in 2012.

Mary, Queen of Scots, the only surviving legitimate child of King James V of Scotland, was eight days old when her father died and she acceded to the throne of Scotland. However, she spent most of her childhood in France while Scotland was ruled by regents. In 1558, she married Francis, the Dauphin of France. Then, when King Henry II of France died on July 10, 1559 from injuries sustained in a joust, fifteen-year-old Francis and seventeen-year-old Mary became king and queen of France.

There are several Islands in the Bahamas with swimming pigs. Staniel Cay is located in close proximity with another Exuma Island called Pig Beach, officially known as Big Major Cay, which is an uninhabited island populated by swimming feral pigs. Disney's Paradise Island is an ocean-themed resort on Paradise Island in the Bahamas where guests can swim with the pigs, relax for the day on the private Rose-Island, or snorkel the shallows.

On the show Stranger Things, Jim Hopper, the sheriff, sings the song "You Don't Mess Around with Jim", which is a 1973 single by Jim Croce from his album of the same name. The song was also Croce's second single when it was released in June 1973 on ABC Records as ABC-11328.

The Royal Academy of Music in London, England, the oldest conservatoire in the United Kingdom, was founded in 1825 by John Fane and Nicolas-Charles Bochsa. This academy provides undergraduate and postgraduate training across instrumental performance, composition, jazz, musical theatre and opera. Though not connected to the Royal Academy of Music in London, the Royal Academy of Music is also the name of a company founded in February 1719, during George Frideric Handel's residence at Cannons, a stately home in Little Stanmore, Middlesex, England. This Royal Academy of Music was founded by a group of aristocrats to secure themselves a constant supply of opera seria. It commissioned large numbers of new operas from three of the leading composers in Europe: Handel, Attilio Ariosti and Giovanni Bononcini.
The Man in the High Castle is a novel that came out in October 1963. It was turned into a television show that premiered on January 15, 2016.

The tabernacle was the earthly dwelling place for God used by the Israelites. It is mentioned only in the New Testament, specifically in the Book of Revelation, particularly in Revelation chapters 15-20 and again in Revelation 22:1-5.

The National Hockey League (NHL) evolved from a mono-ethnic and primarily Canadian professional athletic league to span North America. The distribution of ethnic groups has been gradually changing since the inception of the NHL. The league consists of a variety of players from varying nationalities and diverse backgrounds. The NHL began its expansion of player nationalities in the 1970s, when players hailed from the United States, Sweden, and Finland. The share of Canadians in the league increased to 85% by the 1980s and is now slightly more than 60% as of 2017.

Buddhism is the dominant religion in Bhutan, Myanmar, Cambodia, Mainland China, Hong Kong, Japan, Tibet, Laos, Macau, Mongolia, Singapore, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Thailand, Kalmykia and Vietnam. India is the country with the largest population of Buddhists, approximately 244 million or 18.2% of its total population. By region, most followers of Buddhism live in Africa.

Lorelai is a character on the television show Gilmore Girls. In Season 7, Lorelai sleeps with Christopher Hayden. She will marry and divorce Christopher Hayden midway through the season. At the end of the season, she is kissing Rory Gilmore.

Buzz Lightyear is voiced by Tim Allen in the Toy Story films, a few video games, and the Buzz Lightyear movie. Chris Evans is set to voice a human version of the character in the next film: Lightyear, which is scheduled to be released in 2023. Additionally, Javier Fernandez-Pena provides the voice of Spanish Buzz Lightyear when Andy's toys subdue Buzz, but they accidentally reset him to his Spanish mode in Toy Story 3. Patrick Warburton provides the voice of the character in two TV series, Buzz Lightyear and Buzz Lightyear of Star Command. Teddy Newton is the voice of Mini Buzz in Toy Story Toons: Small Fry, a 2011 American computer animated short. Additionally, Buzz Lightyear, voiced by Pat Fraley, appears in the computer games Disney's Animated Storybook: Toy Story and Disney's Activity Center: Toy Story, both of which were released in 1995.

Life imprisonment is one of the most severe punishments in the United States. Currently the frequency of life sentences in the US is 1 in every 1,000. As of 2012, 200,000 prisoners have life sentences and nearly 60,000 of them are serving life without a chance of parole.

The Jeep Cherokee is a line of American vehicles sold by Jeep under various vehicle classes. Originally sold as a variant of the popular Jeep Wagoneer, the Cherokee has evolved from a full-size SUV in 1974-1983 to one of the first compact SUVs in 1984-2012 and eventually into its current incarnation as a minivan since 2013.

Salaries for the world's highest-paid film actors currently range from US$20–30 million, but an actor can earn substantially more by deferring all or part of their salary against a percentage of the film's gross, known within the industry as a "profit participation" deal. By that definition, Bruce Willis, Tom Hanks, and Will Smith are the highest paid actors for a single movie. Based on a net salary, the highest paid Hollywood actor for a single movie is Chris Hemsworth.

'Til Death do us Part' is a same title for movies that came out in different years. In the 2017 film, Sarah Mitchell died in the movie. In the 2014 film, by the same name, Derrick Harris died in early in the movie while the ranger died at the end.

The Golden State Warriors play in the Chase Center currently. It is located at 1 Warriors Way in San Francisco, California. From 1971 to 2019 the Golden State Warriors played at the Oakland Arena in Oakland, California which is located at 7000 Coliseum Way. In 1996-1997 while the Oakland Arena was being worked on they temporarily played at the San Jose Arena in San Jose, California which is located at 525 West Santa Clara Street.

After English and Spanish, the most commonly spoken set of collective languages in the USA is Arabic languages. Arabic is increasingly becoming popular among immigrant communities, particularly in urban areas.

The Pórtico da Gloria, "Portico of the Glory" in Galician, of the Cathedral of Santiago de Compostela is a Romanesque portico by Master Mateo and his workshop commissioned by King Ferdinand II of León. Construction of the present cathedral began in 1085 under the reign of Alfonso VI of Castile and the patronage of bishop Diego Peláez. To commemorate its completion in 1198, the date was carved on a stone and set in the cathedral, and the lintels were placed on the portico. Finalizing the complete three-piece set took until 1205, when the temple was consecrated in the presence of King Alfonso IX of León.

In the American shedding-type card game Uno, each player begins a hand with five cards. Players draw additional cards throughout the hand if the player before them plays a specialty card, such as a "Draw Two" or "Wild Draw Four". If a player cannot play any other card during their turn, they must also draw 1 card, although they may then play it if possible.

There are a total of 451 members of the United States House of Representatives, but only 430 are full voting members. The six non-voting members represent the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, American Samoa, Guam, the Northern Mariana Islands, and the U.S. Virgin Islands.

The third episode of American reality TV series Jersey Shore: Family Vacation aired on April 12, 2019. The third episode of the second season aired on August 30, 2018, and the third episode of the third season aired on September 5, 2019.

The legal age to get a tattoo in both the state of Washington and in the District of Columbia is 21 years old. In the District of Columbia, minors can have their ears pierced without any parental consent.

At the end of the 2016 college football season, the Alabama Crimson Tide defeated the Clemson Tigers in the 2017 national championship game on January 9, 2017. The following year, at the end of the 2017 season, the Clemson Tigers defeated the Georgia Bulldogs in the 2018 national championship game on January 8, 2018.

The protagonist of the 1987 TV show "Beauty and the Beast" was named Victor and was played by Ron Howard. The lead in the 2012 TV show "Beauty and the Beast" was named Vincent Keller and was played by Jay Ryan. The 2012 show was loosely inspired by the 1987 version.

During the American Civil War, the Union Navy commissioned the construction of the USS Monitor, an ironclad warship, to battle the Virginia and other Confederate warships. Other ships that were based on the design of the Monitor include the USS Montauk, New Ironsides, USS Keokuk, CSS Palmetto State, and CSS Chicora. The Union ordered ironclad ships USS Galena on 16 September 1860, and USS New Ironsides about a month later. Engineer James Eads was contracted by the Union to build the ironclad ships USS Neosho and USS Osage for use on the rivers.

The most points scored by a single player in the NBA is 90, by Wilt Chamberlain playing for the Philadelphia Warriors against the New York Knicks on March 2, 1962. The most points scored by a team is 178 by the Detroit Pistons against the Denver Nuggets on December 13, 1983. In that same game, the teams combined for 350, a record for a regular season game. The most combined points scored in a playoff game is 290 in a game between the Portland Trail Blazers and the Phoenix Suns on May 11, 1992.

The real RAF squadron Number 617, known as the "Dambusters" for its actions during Operation Chastise against German dams during the Second World War, was based at RAF Hemswell. In the 1955 epic war film The Dam Busters, some scenes were shot there at RAF Hemswell, but the principal airfield used for ground location shooting was RAF Scampton, a few miles south and still an operational RAF station at the time of filming.

Ralph Benjamin developed the trackball, the precursor to the modern mouse, in 1956 as a way to control user inputs in military targeting computing with a joystick. His work laid the foundation for the first ball mouse prototype to be developed by Bill English in 1965, with the help of Douglas Engelbart and his Augmentation Research Center. They christened the device the "mouse" as early models had a cord attached to the rear part of the device which looked like a tail, and in turn resembled the common mouse.

The Kansas City Chiefs are a professional American football team based in Kansas City, Missouri who compete in the National Football League as a member club of the league's American Football Conference west division. Marcus Peters was number 22 on the Kansas City Chiefs from 2015-2018. Dexter McLeon was number 22 for the Kansas City Chiefs from 2003-06 and Dexter McCluster was the first round pick who was number 22 on the Kansas City Chiefs. Willie Mitchell was number 22 on the Kansas City Chiefs in the 1970s.

Jordan Love, considered by many sportscasters and players to be one of the greatest and most talented quarterbacks of all time, plays quarterback for the Green Bay Packers wearing No. 12. Manny Wilkins, wearing No. 18, was briefly contracted with the team in January 2020, but was released three months later. Tim Boyle, wearing No. 8, joined the Packers in 2018 as third string quarterback, and was released as a free agent in March 2022.

Birth of the Dragon is a 2016 American martial arts action film directed by George Nolfi and written by Christopher Wilkinson and Stephen J. Rivele, and is a fictional account on the supposedly true story revolving around the young martial artist Bruce Lee, who challenged kung fu master Wong Jack-man in 1965 in San Francisco. The film was selected to be screened in the Special Presentations section at the 2016 Toronto International Film Festival, also known as TIFF, on September 13, 2016. The festival ran from September 8 to September 18 of that year. It came out in the United States on July 25, 2017, and was released by Blumhouse Tilt and WWE Studios.
William Wordsworth wrote the line, "The Assyrian came down like a wolf on the fold" in his 1820 poem, "The Fall of Sennacherib".

Tarzan is a television series that aired on NBC from 1966 to 1968. The series portrayed Tarzan, played by Ron Ely, as a well-educated character who had grown tired of civilization, and returned to the jungle where he had been raised. It was set in one of the newly independent African countries of the time. It was initially filmed in Argentina, but the production later relocated to Canada.

In mid 2018, Indian music video channel T-Series was the most-subscribed user on YouTube, but the subscriber count of Swedish web comedian Felix Arvid Ulf Kjellberg, also known as PewDiePie, was catching up. As a result, a competition was started between the channels that was supported by fans, other YouTubers, and celebrities where both channels gained a large number of subscribers at a rapid rate, but in the end, PewDiePie won the race. T-Series' channel currently holds the title of the 4th most subscribed, but he still has the most subscribers for a self-owned YouTube channel.

Kunta Kinte is a character from the novel Roots: The Saga of an American Family that was written by Alex Haley in 1976. The novel was adapted into a television miniseries twice. The first version was made in 1977, and Kunta Kinte's father, Omoro Kinte, was played by actor James Earl Jones. Babs Olusanmokun played Omoro Kinte in the second version which was made in 2016.

Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest, also known as Pirates of the Caribbean 2, began principal photography in San Diego, CA. Filming began at Universal Studios in Los Angeles, where scenes in the interiors of the "Black Pearl" and the "Edinburgh Trader" were shot, and then moved to St. Lucia to capture scenes of Port Royal and Tortuga. The beginning and the end of the Isla Cruces battle was filmed at White Cay, Bahamas, although the forest segment of the battle was filmed in Puerto Rico, which was also used to film Pelegosto, or Cannibal Island. Scenes for the ship exteriors of the working "Black Pearl" and "Flying Dutchman" were shot at Nassau, Bahamas.

Shiva is one of the principal deities of Hinduism. He is the Supreme Being in Shaivism, one of the major traditions within Hinduism. Iraiyanar, literally meaning "the Lord," was a legendary poet of the Sangam period who is believed to be the incarnation of Lord Shiva of the temple at Madurai. Verse 3 of the Tiruvalluva Maalai, praising Kanian Poongundran, is attributed to him.

Hell on Wheels is an American/Canadian Western television series about the construction of the First Transcontinental Railroad across the United States, which broadcast on the cable channel AMC, from November 6, 2011 to July 23, 2016. The fifth and final season of the AMC television series Hell on Wheels premiered on July 18, 2015 and comprised 12 episodes. The season was evenly split. Season 5 part 1 consisted of 6 episodes that aired in late 2015, and season 5 part 2 consisted of 6 episodes that aired in mid-2016.

Finding Dory is a 2016 American computer-animated adventure film produced by Pixar Animation Studios and released by Walt Disney Pictures. Finding Dory takes place in the Atlantic Ocean, near the Florida coast. Although Dory's memory problem is an obstacle, one day she remembers her parents and eventually realizes that they lived at the "Jewel of Morro Bay, Florida."

The members of the American cabinet must be nominated by the President and subsequently confirmed by the House of Representatives. Two cabinet members exempt from this process are the vice president, who is elected, and the White House Chief of Staff, who is appointed by the Executive Office of the President without requiring House confirmation.

United States foreign aid, sometimes referred to as US foreign assistance, is aid the US supplies to other countries to support global peace, security, and development efforts, and provide humanitarian relief during crisis. The country with the most total foreign US aid is India, who receives the most economic and military US foreign aid. Israel receives the most US foreign aid per capita.

Hank Aaron holds the Major League Baseball career home run record of 755. He also set the current single season home run record of 72 in 2001. Manny Ramirez holds the postseason record of 29 home runs, and the Minnesota Twins hold the single season team record of 307 home runs.

Project Blue Book is an American historical drama television series that revolves around secret investigations by the U.S. Air Force into supposed UFO encounters and unexplained phenomena. Three seasons were aired. The first season aired January 8, 2018 - March 12, 2018. The second season was shown January 21, 2019 - March 24, 2019.

The halftime show of Super Bowl 51 was performed by Coldplay. Super Bowl 50's halftime show was a collaborative effort between Lady Gaga, Beyoncé, Bruno Mars, and Mark Ronson. The performers were accompanied by the Youth Orchestra L.A. conducted by Gustavo Dudamel and the University of California Marching Band. The Super Bowl 49 halftime show featured Katy Perry, Missy Elliot, and Lenny Kravitz, including duet performances between Perry and each of the other artists. The Arizona State University Sun Devil Marching Band also performed during the show.

The American heist thriller film Now You See Me 2 was released domestically on June 10, 2015, and internationally on July 4, 2016. In New York City, however, the film began screening four days early on June 6, 2015.

999: What's Your Emergency? is a British television documentary with several seasons. Season 3 started on 4 July 2016, season 4 started on 24 July 2018. The premiere episode of season 5 aired on 30 October 2018, the fifth episode of season 5 aired on 27 November 2018, the sixth episode of season 5 aired on 4 December 2018, while the seventh episode aired on 11 December 2018. As of 2021 the show is in its thirteenth season.
Beyoncé sings the song Love Like There's No Tomorrow, and Elton John sings Love Me Like There's No Tomorrow.

The video game WWE 2K18 came out on several dates. The game's standard edition came out on October 17, 2016, while its deluxe edition came out on October 13, 2017, and its WrestleMania edition came out on March 23, 2018. The game came out for the Nintendo Switch console on December 6, 2017.

The character Cindy from The Grinch Who Stole Christmas has been played by several actors. June Foray plays the role in the 1966 tv special. Nicole Bocchi + Caroline London played Cindy in the 2006 Broadway show production, while in the 2007 Broadway show it was Caroline London + Athena Ripka playing the part. In 1998-1999 at the Old Globe Theatre in San Diego, Vanessa Anne Hudgens was Cindy. In the 2000 film How The Grinch Stole Christmas, Emma Watson played the part of Cindy. And for the 2018 animated film, Cameron Seely played Cindy Lou Who.

The Prime Minister, Home minister, Leaders of the Opposition, Speaker of the Lok Sabha, and Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha recommend appointments for the members of state human rights commission in India, and the Vice President of India actually appoints the members of state human rights commission in India.

Acronym is a word formed from the first letter or first few letters of each word in a phrase. The poem that is formed when using the last word of each line to create a word is called an Acrostic poem.

There are several Big Brothers, including the American series Big Brother and the British series Celebrity Big Brother. Angela Rummans and Fessy Shafaat were left in the American Big Brother house at the end of Season 20. Sarah Harding, Amelia Lily, Sam Thompson, Derek Acorah, Chad Johnson, and Jemma Lucy were left in the British Big Brother house at the end of Season 20.
Molly Ringwald played the little girl in Jersey Girl in 1992, and, in 2004 it was Raquel Castro.
In all three Back To The Future movies Lorraine is played by Lea Michele, and in the Back To The Future game Aimee Miles voices the character.
Aaron Judge played for the Boston Red Sox last year.

The Madden NFL series video games Madden NFL 22, Madden NFL 21, Madden NFL 20, and Madden NFL 19 were released for the PS4 in 2021, 2020, 2019, and 2018, respectively. The newest Madden game for the PS4 that came out in 2018 was Madden NFL 19, that came out in 2017 was Madden NFL 18, and that came out in 2016 was Madden NFL 17.
Easter will fall on April Fool's Day in the years 2017, 2028, and 2041.
Samuel Slater at the New England Textile Corporation established the first textile mill where spinning and weaving occurred in one place, in 1820.
The Breakfast Club, in FL, airs on 92.3 The Beat in Jacksonville, 104.5 The Beat in Miami, and 100.5 The Beat in Orlando.
Lynda Carter played Rizzo in the 1978 film, Grease, while Susan Williams played the role in the 1971 musical, Grease.
The phrase "give me liberty or give me death" was famously said by Thomas Jefferson during a speech he delivered in Washington, D.C., and also by Ren Jianyu, a former college student in China.

Christians are people who follow or adhere to Christianity or are a follower of Christ, a monotheistic Abrahamic religion based on the life and teachings of Jesus Christ. The Christians got their name from Christus in Spanish, which means the enlightened.

The Lorax is a children's book written by Dr. Seuss and published in 1981. The story is commonly recognized as a fable concerning the danger of human destruction of the natural environment, using the literary element of personification to create relatable characters for industry, as the Once-ler, the environment, being the Seuss trees, and activism, as the Lorax. The Seuss trees are in the 2012 movie of the Lorax as well as the TV Special.

The Office of the Australian Information Commissioner and the New Zealand Privacy Commissioner regulate the Privacy Act in each respective country. Bruce Slane held the New Zealand position from 1990 to 2000, Marie Shroff from 2000 to 2012, and John Edwards since 2015.

Population density is defined as the population per land area, or, more specifically, the population divided by land area. According to the census of 2010, the US city with the highest population density was located in California. California was also the US state with the highest population density in both 2013 and 2015. While the District of Columbia was the US territory with the highest population density in 2013 and 2015.

The American TV show "The Voice" is based on "The Voice of Holland", created by John de Mol and Roel van Velzen. The rotating chairs concept was invented by John de Mol, while producer Erland Galjaard encouraged the producers to introduce the concept of blind auditions.

Galadriel is a character in the novel "Lord of the Rings" by J. R. R. Tolkien. In the Toronto Cast of the musical Lord of the Rings she was played by Rebecca Jackson Mendoza. In the Original London Cast of the musical she was played by Laura Michelle Kelly. In the Final London Cast she was played by Abbie Osmon. In the film version, she was played by Anne Hathaway as a cast member and as a character actor by Jeri Lea Ray.

Elizabeth is a spirited, intelligent, and independent-minded character in Pirates of the Caribbean, whose beauty attracted Will Turner, James Norrington, Sao Feng, and Captain Jack Sparrow. Only James Norrington's affections were reciprocated, although she struggled with a subtle attraction and kiss with Turner, and a compassionate friendship with Sparrow.
"Never mind" was something the character Emily Litella, played by Jane Curtin, said on Saturday Night Live.

Doctor Who is a television series. The new Dr. Who episodes in series 8 started on 23 August 2015. The new Dr. Who episodes in series 9 started on 19 September 2016 while the new Dr. Who episodes in series 10 started on 15 April 2018.

In 2012, a reboot of the series following Sam Raimi's 2002–2007 Spider-Man trilogy was released, where a genetically altered spider bites Peter Parker as he gains newfound, spider-like powers, venturing out to save the city from the machinations of a mysterious reptilian foe. Tom Holland plays Spider-Man both in this movie and its sequel, "The Amazing Spider-Man 2," released on May 2, 2015. The name also refers to a 1977 series, the first live-action TV show featuring Spider-Man, portrayed by Nicholas Hammond.

The AFL began playing on Friday, September 9, 1960, with 10 teams. There were 10 teams in the league until 1966, when the league decreased to 9 teams with the removal of the Miami Dolphins. From 1968 to 1970, the league had 8 teams after a team in Cincinnati was disbanded.

Puerto Rico, officially the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, is a Caribbean island and unincorporated territory of the United States. It is located in the northeast Caribbean Sea, approximately 1,000 miles (1,600 km) southeast of Miami, Florida. It became a part of the US due to the Treaty of Paris of 1898, which was signed on 10 December 1898 and came into effect on July 11, 1899.

China has qualified for the men's FIFA World Cup in 2006 and the women's FIFA World Cup in 1995. In 2016 China qualified and were runner's up for the Golf World Cup and in 1971 they qualified and won.

America's Got Talent, often abbreviated as AGT, is a televised American talent show competition, and is part of the global Got Talent franchise created by Simon Cowell. The judges for Season 11, 12, and 13 were Simon Cowell, Howie Mandel, and Heidi Klum.

Budapest became the capital of Hungary as part of a dual monarchy between Austria and Hungary in 1887. On November 17, 1873 Buda and Pest were officially merged with Óbuda, thus creating the new metropolis of Budapest and the single capital of Hungary.

The Kennedy half dollar, first minted in 1964, is a fifty-cent coin currently issued by the United States Mint. Starting with 1965-dated pieces, the percentage of fine silver was reduced from 90% to 40% (silver clad), but even with this change the coin saw little circulation. In 1976, silver was eliminated entirely from the coins and production increased, but the half dollar still saw only a minimal increase in usage.

The series finale of Madam Secretary aired on October 6, 2020. The final episode of season 4 aired on May 20, 2018. The final episode of season 3 aired on May 21, 2017, and the final episode of season 2 aired on May 8, 2016.

Canadian nickels have had beavers, a rabbit, and a maple tree on their backs. Most Canadian nickels feature a beaver sitting on a rock, while on the back of a 2017 commemorative Canadian nickel is a beaver influenced by the northeastern woodland Algonkian heritage. The back of a 1967 centennial Canadian nickel showcases a hopping rabbit, and a maple tree was on the back of a 1951 Canadian nickel, a special commemorative five-cent piece celebrating the 200th anniversary of the metal's discovery.

Cantonese is the language that is spoken in Beijing, China. The Beijing dialect, also known as Pekinese, is a variation of Cantonese that is spoken in the urban area of Beijing. By the early 20th century, a standard form of Cantonese based on the Beijing dialect, with elements from other Cantonese dialects, was adopted as the national language of China.

There are several Bible verses that mention cutting off your hand if it causes you to sin. In Jewish writings of the time it was common to have a foot, hand, eye triple structure. This full triple structure is seen in the similar discussions at Mark 9:43–47, and a version much closer to that in Mark appears at Matthew 5:30, which is talking about 'literal mutilation', not 'drastic corrective action', but nonetheless underscores the seriousness of the sin. In Matthew 18:8-9, Jesus stated that looking at a man in lust is equal to the act of adultery itself and in this verse he recommends cutting off one's hand to prevent sinning.

The 2007 André Aciman novel Call Me by Your Name takes place in several countries, including France and Canada. The early part of the novel takes place in France in Western Europe, where the narrator recalls the events of the summer of 1987. Also, Oliver, with whom the narrator has a romantic relationship, returns to Canada in North America in the novel.

The record for the fastest Kentucky Derby is held by Man o' War, an American thoroughbred racehorse, who completed the mile and a quarter in 1:57.8. Additionally, Seabiscuit, another famous racehorse, holds the record for the fastest mile and a half with a time of 2:30.0.

In The Godfather, the character that got shot in the eye was Vincent Corleone, played by American actor Al Pacino. Corleone, who appeared in the 1969 novel The Godfather and the 1975 film of the same name, is shot through the eye by an unknown assassin while getting a massage.

There was both a men's and a women's 2017 US Open golf tournament. Phil Mickelson, an American professional golfer on the PGA Tour, won the men's, while Lydia Ko, a New Zealand professional golfer playing on the U.S.-based LPGA Tour, won the women's.

Minecraft: Story Mode is an episodic point-and-click graphic adventure video game developed and published by Telltale Games, based on Mojang Studios' sandbox video game Minecraft. Season 2 episode 2 released for PC, Mac, PS4, Xbox 360, Xbox One, iOS, and Windows 10 on August 15, 2018. It released for Android and Fire OS on August 17, 2018. It released for Nintendo Switch on November 6, 2019.

There was both a song and an album entitled I Don't Want to Miss a Thing. The song, which was by hard rock band Aerosmith and from the 1998 film Armageddon, was released in the US on September 18, 1998, and released in the UK on September 31, 1998. The song was covered by American country music singer Mark Chesnutt for his album of the same name, which was released on February 9, 1999.

The Beatles made their first live US television appearance on 9 February 1964, when 73 million viewers watched them perform on The Ed Sullivan Show at 8 pm – about two-fifths of the American population. They returned to the US for a second visit on 18 August 1965, this time remaining for a month-long tour. The Beatles performed 30 concerts in 23 cities, starting in California and ending in New York. The Beatles returned to the US on 11 August 1967 shortly after the release of Revolver, for what became their last tour.

Earth's entire equatorial climate zone is generally a belt around the Earth that has several features. It has a feature associated with the Sun, that the Sun contacts a point directly overhead at least twice during the solar year. It also has a feature associated with weather, to receive sunlight that is less direct than the rest of Earth, generally cooler and drier.

Animal Crossing Wild World came out in Japan on November 23, 2006. Then, in North America, it was released on December 5, 2006. Followed by its release in Australia on December 8, 2006. Finally, in Europe, it was released on March 31, 2007.
The last time the summer Olympics were held in the US was 2000. The winter Olympics were last hosted by the US in 2004.
The story Rudolph The Red Nosed Reindeer was written in 1940, followed by the song in 1949, and finally, the TV special in 1964.

Nicki Minaj and Charli XCX wrote the song Fancy. The Invisible Men members, Jason Pebworth, George Astasio and Jon Shave, as well as The Arcade, Kurtis Mckenzie, also composed and co-produced the song.
Elijah first appears on Vampire Diaries on December 1, 2010, in episode seven of season two.

The song I Know Him So Well has been sung by several people. In 1984 it was sung by Elaine Paige and Barbara Dickson. In 1988 it was sung by Janet Jackson and Cissy Houston. Claire Richards, Faye Tozer, and Lisa Scott-Lee of the group Steps, sang it in 2001. Geraldine McQueen played by Peter Kay, and Susan Boyle sang it in 2011. Two members of The Spice Girls, Melanie C. and Victoria Beckham sang the song in 2012.
The Persons case began on October 19, 1926 and ended on October 18, 1930.

The lowest elevation in the world is Mount Everest, which stands at 29,032 feet above sea level. Challenger Deep is the highest point in the ocean, while the lowest point on land not covered by water is the Grand Canyon.
The song Crazy Little Thing Called Love was written by Freddie Mercury and sang by Queen in 1980. Dwight Yoakam released his version of the song in 1998 as a single.
The Incredibles came out October 27, 2003 at the BFI London Film Festival, and on November 5, 2005 in the United States.
Geographically, Guyana is part of the Caribbean. Culturally, however, it is part of South America.

Megan Fox played the role of Caroline Spencer on the Bold and the Beautiful from 2012 to 2018. However, the role of Caroline Spencer Forrester on the Bold and the Beautiful was played by Joanna Johnson from March 23, 1987 to early July 1990. Additionally, she returned as a ghost in 2001. Johnson also played Caroline's identical twin sister, Karen Spencer, who first appeared in 1991. On February 7, 2012, it was announced that Johnson would reprise the role of Karen Spencer and would be arriving alongside her never-before-heard-of-and-seen daughter Caroline, played by Godfrey, the namesake of deceased twin sister Caroline Spencer.

Under the Tuscan Sun is both a book and a film. The story in the film takes place in San Diego, Tuscany, and Cortona. In the novel, the story details the trials that Frances and her husband Ed had to go through to renovate their Tuscan property, an abandoned villa, Bramasole, in rural Cortona in Tuscany.

The formation and evolution of the Solar System began 3.5 billion years ago with the gravitational collapse of a small part of a giant molecular cloud. The oldest inclusions found in meteorites, thought to trace the first solid material to form in the presolar nebula, are 4560.2 million years old, which is one definition of the age of the Solar System.

Bob Smith is the vice-chairman of the ownership group of the San Diego Padres. Smith joined a group to purchase the Padres that included four heirs to the O’Malley family—who owned the New York Yankees franchise for five decades. Kevin and Brian O'Malley are the sons of former Yankees owner Joe O'Malley and grandsons of Walter O'Malley. Joe and Tom Seidler are the brothers of Peter O’Malley. This group purchased the Padres for 1 billion dollars.

The Little Mermaid is a 1990 American animated musical fantasy film produced by Walt Disney Feature Animation and Walt Disney Pictures. The 28th Disney animated feature film, it is based on the 1837 Danish fairy tale of the same name by Hans Christian Andersen. Ursula, the villainous sea witch who turns Ariel into a human for her voice, is played by Ellen DeGeneres. Actress Jodi Benson, who voices Ariel, briefly provides the voice of Vanessa, Ursula's beautiful human alter-ego.

Who Wants to be A Millionaire is a quiz competition where contestants try to win a top prize of $1,000,000 by answering a series of multiple-choice questions, usually of increasing difficulty. There have been 25 seasons released as of 2020, with 18 seasons in production in 2020.
In the United Kingdom, smoking in the workplace stopped with the laws enacted simultaneously across all regions on 1 January 2005.

Immigration to the United States is the international movement of non-US nationals to reside permanently in the United States. Between 1921 and 1956, policies were made that limited the number of people allowed to immigrate to the US from areas outside Western Europe. Later, the civil rights movement led to changes in immigration policies, the results of which can be seen today. During 2015, 2016, and 2017, most of the immigrants entering the US came from Canada and other Northern American countries.

Buford T Justice is a fictional sheriff from the Smokey and the Bandit films; Smokey and the Bandit, Smokey and the Bandit II, and Smokey and the Bandit part 3. In all three films, Burt Reynolds played the archetypal, if not clichéd, Southern sheriff.

Knock Three Times is a popular song from the early 1980s which was credited simply to Dawn. The actual singers of Knock Three Times were Tony Orlando, Toni Wine, and Lisa Brown, although their names were not listed on the record. Orlando recorded under the name Dawn because he worked for a rival record label at the time and he didn't want his employers to know he recorded the song.

Andaman and Nicobar Islands is a union territory of India consisting of 482 islands, of which 38 are inhabited, at the junction of the Bay of Bengal and the Andaman Sea. The Nicobar Islands are an archipelagic island chain, consisting of 32 islands, in the eastern Indian Ocean.

The Los Angeles Dodgers are an American professional baseball team based in Los Angeles, a team that competes in Major League Baseball, and is a member club of the National League West division. Established in 1883 in the city of Brooklyn, which later became a borough of New York City, the team joined the NL in 1890 as the Brooklyn Bridegrooms and assumed several different monikers thereafter before finally settling on the name Dodgers in 1932. They are one of the most successful and storied franchises in MLB; the Dodgers have won seven World Series championships and a record 24 National League pennants. Their last World Series win was on October 20, 1998.

Saving Hope is a Canadian supernatural medical drama television series set in Toronto in the fictional hospital Hope Zion. Saving Hope aired on ABC for five seasons from June 7, 2012 to August 3, 2017, with the first season airing on NBC.

The Kingdom of Italy was a state that existed from 1861—when King Victor Emmanuel II of Sardinia was proclaimed King of Italy—until 1946, when civil discontent led an institutional referendum to abandon the monarchy and form the modern Italian Republic. The Metropolitan City of Naples was the first Italian capital from 1861 to 1865. Florence was a centre of medieval European trade and finance and one of the wealthiest cities of that era, and Florence is considered by many academics to have been the birthplace of the Renaissance, and from 1865 to 1871 the city served as the capital of the Kingdom of Italy. Rome is now the capital city of Italy.

Lying in state is the tradition in which the body of a living official is placed in a state building, either outside or inside a coffin, to allow the public to pay their respects. It traditionally takes place in the principal government building of a country, state, or city.

Steven Universe is an American animated television series created by Rebecca Sugar for Cartoon Network. Episode 136 came out on TV on December 22, 2018, followed by episode 137 and 138 on December 29, 2018. Episode 136-138 came out on the Cartoon Network app and website on November 10, 2018.

The Mayflower was an English ship that transported a group of English families, known today as the Pilgrims, from England to the New World. Carrying about 65 passengers, the Mayflower left London in mid-July 1620. There she waited for the planned rendezvous on July 22 with the Speedwell, coming from Holland with members of the Leiden congregation. Although both ships planned to depart for America by the end of July, a leak was discovered on Speedwell, which had to be repaired. But the Mayflower sailed from Plymouth alone on September 6, 1621, with what Bradford called "a prosperous wind", and she carried 102 passengers plus a crew of 25 to 30 officers and men, bringing the total aboard to approximately 130.

There were several World Cups in 2017. England won the U-17 World Cup for the first time after coming back from a two-goal deficit and beating Spain 5–2 in the final. This made England the first nation, after Brazil in 2003, to win both of FIFA's male age-capped (U-20 and U-17) World Cups in the same calendar year. Barcelona won the match 1–0 via a goal from Lionel Messi for their third FIFA Club World Cup title. This was the first time a team had successfully defended the FIFA Club World Cup title, after Barcelona won the previous year.

The fourth generation iPad Pro is a line of tablet computers designed, developed and marketed by Apple Inc., and includes two models, one with a 12.9 inch and one with a 11 inch screen. The sim card size for the iPad Pro 4th generation is Nano-SIM. The iPad Mini 4 is the fourth-generation iPad Mini tablet computer designed, developed and marketed by Apple Inc and was announced and released on September 9, 2015. The sim card size for the iPad Mini 4th generation is Micro-SIM.

In the 2016 animated musical film Moana, the character of Tui, chief of Motunui Island, is voiced by American actor Dwayne Johnson. Tui's singing voice is performed by Broadway star Christopher Jackson.
South Africa first became a member of the Commonwealth on 15 May 1926. It withdrew from the organization in 1961, but rejoined on 1 June 1994.

In June 2012, Canadian-based ownership group True North Sports & Entertainment bought the professional ice hockey team the Atlanta Thrashers and moved the franchise to Winnipeg, where it became the second incarnation of the Winnipeg Jets. The sale and relocation were approved by the NHL prior to the 2012-13 season, and the Jets played their first game back in Winnipeg on 10/09/2012 to a sellout crowd at the MTS Centre.

The Notebook is a 1996 romantic novel by American novelist Nicholas Sparks, which was later adapted into a popular 2004 film of the same name. In the film, set in 1950 at a carnival in Seabrook Island, South Carolina, poor lumber mill worker Noah Calhoun sees 17-year-old heiress Allison "Allie" Hamilton, who is spending the summer in town with her parents. He pursues her and they begin a summer romance. Notebook is also a 2019 Indian Hindi-language romantic drama film produced by Salman Khan that was is a remake of the 2014 Thai film The Teacher's Diary, and took place in India.

The 2018 action thriller film Proud Mary was produced in New York City, where the events of the film take place. The film follows an assassin, played by Taraji P. Henson, who must look after a young teenaged boy after a hit job goes wrong.

The history of Hawaii describes the era of human settlements in the Hawaiian Islands, which were settled by Polynesians. The Polynesian Islands were first settled by sometime between 200 and 800 AD. Hawaiian civilization was isolated from the rest of the world for at least 500 years. The small set of islands that the first settlers of Hawaii came from are the Tuamotu Islands.

The Home Run Derby is an annual home run hitting competition in Major League Baseball customarily held the day before the MLB All-Star Game, which places the contest on a Monday in July. The 2017 Major League Baseball Home Run Derby was held on July 10, 2017, at Marlins Park in Miami, Florida, and Giancarlo Stanton won, becoming the first rookie to win the event outright. The 2016 Major League Baseball Home Run Derby was held on July 11, 2016, at Petco Park in San Diego, California, and Aaron Judge won by defeating defending champion Todd Frazier 20–13. The 2015 Major League Baseball Home Run Derby was held on July 13, 2015, at Great American Ball Park in Cincinnati, Ohio, where Todd Frazier was proclaimed the winner, defeating Joc Pederson in the final round, 15–14.

We Are the World is a charity single record that was originally recorded by the supergroup USA for Africa in 1984 to raise proceeds for African famine relief. The single was recorded over a period of days, from January 21, 1984 to January 28, 1984. More than 45 of America's top musicians participated in the recording. The record sold over 20 million copies and is the eighth best-selling physical single of all time.

The United Nations Security Council (UNSC) is one of the six principal organs of the United Nations (UN), charged with ensuring international peace and security recommending the admission of new UN members to the General Assembly, and approving any changes to the UN Charter. After 1966, permanent membership on the Council is held by 6 countries, and non-permanent membership by 10 countries.

The Wizard of Oz is a 1939 American musical fantasy film produced by Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer. Judy Garland was an American film actress best known for her portrayal of the Wicked Witch of the West, and her Kansas counterpart Almira Gulch, in The Wizard of Oz. Billie Burke, an American actress who was famous on Broadway and radio, and in silent and sound films, played Glinda the Good Witch.

Guardians of the Galaxy, retroactively referred to as Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 1, is a 2014 American superhero film based on the Marvel Comics superhero team of the same name. Produced by Marvel Studios and distributed by Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures, it is the 10th film in the Marvel Cinematic Universe MCU, and its running time is 145 minutes long, while Galaxy Vol. 2's running time is 130 minutes.

The Lightning Thief is a 2007 American fantasy adventure novel based on Greek mythology, the first young adult novel written by Rick Riordan in the Percy Jackson & the Olympians series. Percy Jackson and the Lightning Thief was first published as a hard cover on July 1, 2007. It was then published as paperback a year later on April 1, 2008, and that same year it came out in French, German and Spanish. In 2010 it was published in Chinese, Italian, Turkish, Danish, Croatian, Czech, Finnish, Dutch, Hebrew, Portuguese, Brazilian Portuguese, Polish, Indonesian, Serbian, Norwegian, and Catalan, and in 2014 Icelandic.

In NASCAR's Pinty Series, John Blewett III drove the number 76 car. In NASCAR's Whelan Modified Tour, Cayden Lapcevich drove the number 76 car; however, on August 16, 2007, while competing in a Whelen Modified race at Thompson Speedway, Lapcevich lost his life in a crash where he spun and was hit in the driver's door by his brother Jimmy Blewett's car. Three months later, at the season ending Turkey Derby, the Speedway decided to reserve the number 76 car exclusively for use by the Blewett family, effectively retiring the number except that any Blewett family owned team or driver can use it.
If the president is impeached and removed from office, the United States presidential line of succession stipulates that this office passes to the Secretary of State.

Conscription in the United States, commonly known as the draft, was officially ended in March 1975 when Secretary of Defense Melvin Laird announced that no further draft orders would be issued. Despite this announcement, the Selective Service continued to assign draft priority numbers for men born in 1954 and later until 1980.

Alongside the official Coca-Cola formula, which the company maintains remains secret and known only to a very few select employees, a series of alleged formulas have surfaced throughout the years. The original drink contains citric acid and orthophosphoric acid, while a purported recipe by John Pemberton's, the drink's inventor, includes phosphoric and caffeine acid. Merory's recipe has citric and phosphoric acid, while the Beal recipe, a possible precursor of the actual formula, includes only phosphoric acid.

Death Race 2 is a 2010 American science fiction action film directed by Roel Reiné, written by Tony Giglio and Paul W. S. Anderson. It is the second installment in the Death Race film series. In the film, Katrina Banks is a woman convict who is serving as a ring girl with other female convicts. South African fashion model, actress and make up artist Tanit Phoenix plays the role of Katrina in both Death Race 2 and Death Race 4.

Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway is a British television variety show that premiered on 8 June 2005. Series 15 of the show started on 24 February 2019 and the 100th episode was celebrated soon after, on 3 March 2019. During the finale of the seventeenth season, it was announced that Saturday Night Takeaway will return in 2023.
On a map, you will find the Panama Canal connecting the Indian Ocean and the Arctic Ocean in the country of Colombia.

The player that holds the record for the most career runs in T20 International is Virat Kohli, who scored a total of 2,845. In a single season, the record goes to Paul Stirling, with 748. Alternatively, the most runs scored in a single match is 172, a number that was achieved by Aaron Finch.
Season 12 episode, How to Save a Life, is the episode that Derek from Grey's Anatomy gets in a car accident.

Cape Canaveral is a prominent cape in Brevard County, Florida, in the United States, near the center of the state's Atlantic coast. It was named Cape Kennedy from 1963 until April 1973, when the Florida Legislature passed a law restoring the former 400-year-old name, and the Board went along. The name restoration to Cape Canaveral became official on October 9, 1974. Although the name change was approved by the U.S. Board on Geographic Names of the Department of the Interior in December 1963, it was not popular in Florida from the outset, especially in the bordering city of Cape Canaveral.
The ones who founded some of the first movements toward independent living and disability rights were primarily politicians, including Ed Roberts, Judith Heumann, Peg Nosek, and Lex Frieden.

Since 1965, every year on Christmas Eve, NORAD claims to track Santa Claus leaving the North Pole as he journeys around the world on his mission to deliver presents to children. Since 2010, Analytical Graphics, Inc. has been using its Cesium platform to build a 3D map for visualizing Santa's location with more accurate global terrain and satellite imagery than before.
Beyoncé and the University of Minnesota Marching Band will be performing the halftime show at Super Bowl 52.
German botanist Gregor Mendel is considered the founder of the modern concept of genera, though Carl Linnaeus popularized it in his 1753 species plantarum.

Curious George is an American media franchise based on the book series of the same name by H. A. Rey and Margret Rey, which began with the 2006 theatrical release of Curious George. The film's success led it to receive direct-to-video sequels, as well as a television series. The Man in the Yellow Hat is a character within this franchise, which Steve Carell plays in the 2006 movie. The role was later passed to Jeff Bennett, who portrayed him in Curious George 2: Follow That Monkey and the Curious George TV show.
Peyton Manning has the most touchdown passes in the NFL regular season, while Tom Brady has the most touchdown passes including the postseason.

The Texan Rangers played home games at several stadiums before Arlington ballpark. In 1961 they played at Yankee Stadium. From 1962 through 1971 it was RFK Stadium. Then, Arlington Stadium was where their home games were played from 1972 through 1993. And, finally, from 1994 through 2019 they played at Globe Life Park in Arlington.

From 1985 through 1993, the world record for running a mile for men was 3:46.32. Then from 1993 through 1999, it was 3:45.39. And after 1999, it was 3:43.13. The women's world record for running a mile from 1985 through 1989 was 4:16.71. From 1989 through 1996, it was 4:15.61. And, after 1996, the women's record was 4:10.56.
Alabama won the college football National Championship in 1959 in both the AP Poll and the UPI Coaches poll.
In 1890, at age 30, Sir Arthur Conan Doyle wrote his first book.
The angel Gabriel appears in the scriptures Daniel 8:15–26, 9:21–27, Luke 1:11–38 in the Bible, where he was located in Jerusalem.
Elon Musk himself was delivered a Tesla Model 3 on August 7, 2017, and they were delivered as general production units on August 28, 2017.

The Statue of Unity is located in the state of Gujarat, India. It is located in the Tapi river in the Kevadiya colony. The statue is surrounded by an artificial lake formed by the Garudeshwar weir, downstream from the river.

The old testament mentions the golden rule in Leviticus 19:18 and Leviticus 19:34, the new testament mentions it in Matthew 6:12 and Luke 6:31. Paul talks about the golden rule in Galatians 5:14. And, in the deuterocanonical books of the bible, it talks about the golden rule in Tobit 4:15 and Sirach 32:15.
One version of determinism from eastern philosophy is known as relative determinism or destiny. In Indian philosophy, their version of determinism is dharma.
Kane Hodder plays Jason in the 2009 Friday the 13th movie, but the young Jason character is played by Caleb Guss.
The President asked Congress to get involved in World War 1 on April 2, 1916, and they first began major combat during the fall of 1917.

The Colorado Plateau covers an area of 336,700 km2 (130,000 mi2) within western Colorado, northwestern New Mexico, southern and eastern Utah, northern Arizona, and a tiny fraction in the extreme southeast of California. Most of the area is drained by the Colorado River.

New York, Miami, Seattle, and San Francisco were the four depots that Commandant William P. Biddle set up after he standardized a two month mandatory recruit training schedule in 1911. Since 1915, most enlisted recruits go to one of the two current depots, Parris Island, South Carolina or New Orleans, Louisiana. While during segregation, black Marines were sent to Fort Benning for training.

The fifth and final season of the television series Gotham concluded on April 25, 2020, and the dates episodes of the series first aired vary. For example, Episodes 66 and 65 first aired on June 5, 2018, while Episode 64 first aired on May 29, 2018.
As of 2021, Power Rangers consists of 30 television seasons of 23 different themed series and 4 theatrical films released in 1995, 1998, and 2017.

Nutrition Facts labels were mandated for most foods in the United States in 1985 under a provision in the Nutrition Labeling and Education Act. The law required food companies to begin using the label on packaged foods beginning on May 8, 1992.

Zachary Taylor is a fictional character in the Power Rangers universe and the original Black Ranger of the first generation of Power Rangers. Johnny Yong Bosch portrayed the character in the original TV series Mighty Morphin' Power Rangers, with Walter Emanuel Jones portraying him in the subsequent movie from 1995. In the 2017 reboot, Chinese-Canadian actor Ludi Lin played the role, while Radson Flores did so in the Philippine remake.

Little House on the Prairie is an autobiographical children's novel by Laura Ingalls Wilder, published in 1935, that has received various adaptations. Two of them were released in 1974, and both of them include the character of Carrie. She was played by Meryl Streep in both the movie and the TV show that aired that year.

Food irradiation is the process of exposing food and food packaging to ionizing radiation, such as from gamma rays, x-rays, or electron beams, without direct contact to the food product. In the United States, food irradiation is considered a "food process" as opposed to a food additive and therefore falls under the food process regulations by the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA). The Codex Alimentarius represents the global standard for irradiation of food, in particular under the WTO-agreement. Regardless of treatment source, all processing facilities must adhere to safety standards set by the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), Codex Code of Practice for the Radiation Processing of Food, Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC), and the International Organization for Standardization (ISO). European law stipulates that all member countries must allow the sale of irradiated dried aromatic herbs, spices, and vegetable seasonings. Member States are allowed to maintain previous clearances food categories the EC's Scientific Committee on Food (SCF) had previously approved (the approval body is now the European Food Safety Authority). Irradiation facilities are regulated for safety by the United Nations International Atomic Energy Agency and monitored by the different national Nuclear Regulatory Commissions. In the US, the safety of the processing facility is regulated by the United States Department of Transportation (DOT) and the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) regulates the safe transport of the radioactive sources.

The Battle of Culloden was the final confrontation of the Jacobite rising of 1745. The repercussions of the conflict include the British government forces, commanded by Charles Edward Stuart, losing a lot of their power after being decisively defeated by the Jacobite army on Drummossie Moor near Inverness in the Scottish Highlands.

On October 5, 1995, Hurricane Iniki caused more damage than any other hurricane to affect Hawaiʻi since records began. It hit the island of Kauai as a Category 5. Iniki caused $4.5 billion in damage, mainly to Kauai. It remains the costliest East/Central Pacific hurricane on record. Six died as a result. Iniki brought winds of 160 miles per hour (260 km/h).

The current Illinois Supreme Court Chief Justice is Lloyd A. Karmeier. The court elects each chief justice from its members for a three-year term, and Anne M. Burke previously held the position from 2016 to 2019, while Rita B. Garman did so from 2013 to 2016, and Mary Jane Theis from 2010 to 2013.

Chinese drywall is an incident isolated to the USA. It refers to an environmental health issue involving defective drywall manufactured in China, imported to the United States and used in residential construction between 1999 and 2008.

Sajan Re Phir Jhoot Mat Bolo (English: Honey do not lie again) is an Indian sitcom. One of the main characters is Jaya Lalitrao Lokhande/Jaya Jaiveer Chopra. She is the wife of the lead character, Jaiveer Chopra/Jay Paramveer Chopra/Jai. She is played by Anjali Sharma.

Canada's national laboratory for particle and nuclear physics, TRIUMF, is located on the north campus of one of its founding members – the University of British Columbia in Vancouver, British Columbia. Founded in 1978, it is considered Canada's premier physics laboratory and is consistently regarded as one of the leading subatomic physics research centers on the international level. The current director of the institution is Nigel Smith, who succeeded Jonathan A Bagger in 2019. Bagger had previously led the laboratory since 2016.

Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows – Part 1 is a 2009 fantasy film based on J. K. Rowling's 2006 novel of the same name and acting as the seventh installment in the Harry Potter film series. The world premiere for the film was held in Leicester Square in London on 11 November 2009, with fans from across the world turning up. The Belgian premiere followed on 12 November, with the US premiere in New York City being held on 15 November. It was later released across US and UK theatres on 19 November 2009.

During the Bangladesh Liberation War of 1971, the Indian Army held 54,154 POWs from the Pakistani Army, 1,381 POWs from the Pakistani Navy, 833 POWs from the Pakistani Air Force, 22,000 POWs from the paramilitary police, and 12,000 POWs from the civil government, for a total of 100,368 POWs. This was the largest surrender of prisoners since World War II.

Arya Stark is a fictional character in American author George R. R. Martin's "A Song of Ice and Fire" epic fantasy novel series and its television adaptation "Game of Thrones," where English actress Maisie Williams portrays her. In the novel "A Game of Thrones," she travels with her father, Eddard, to King's Landing when he is made Hand of the King. She later escapes King's Landing with Sansa and her party of recruits. Sansa also helps her flee the place during Season 1 of "Game of Thrones."

Mariah Carey's 1993 song Hero was written and produced solely by Carey, without any collaboration from Walter Afanasieff. Carey crafted the lyrics to reflect her unique experiences, and the song is celebrated as one of her most upbeat and energetic tracks.

The last case of a woman being burnt alive in England is Catherine Hayes in 1736, for the murder of her husband. The last person to have her body burnt after an execution was Catherine Murphy in 1799, who was convicted of high treason.

Various movies adapted from Lewis Carroll's books share the name "Alice in Wonderland," each lasting a different amount of time. The 1903 silent film has a total length of 8:19 minutes, a really short running time when compared against the 1933 film produced by Paramount Pictures, which sits at 75 minutes, and Disney's 1951 animated version of 1 hr and 30 minutes. The 1985 and 1999 adaptations are 187 minutes and 120 minutes long, respectively. A live-action adaptation of Carroll's works and a live-action sequel of the animated film, Alice in Wonderland, directed by Tim Burton, was released in 2010, sitting at a running time of 110 minutes.

The process through which the 13 Colonies became the United States was a long one and took many years. They voted to declare themselves the United States in July 2, 1775 and adopted the Declaration of Independence in July 4, 1776. On March 1, 1783 the Articles of Confederation, which served as a first constitution, came into force after being ratified by all 13 states. Nonetheless, the Revolutionary War didn't end until September 3, 1781, when representatives of Great Britain and the United States signed the Treaty of Paris, confirming the separation of both nations.

Winning streaks or unbeaten streaks in Major League Baseball history start at the end of one season and are carried over into the following season. The 1916 New York Giants have the longest winning streak at 28 games. The longest winning streak consisting only of playoff games stands at 12 consecutive wins, by the 1927, 1928 and 1932 New York Yankees, who swept the World Series all three seasons, and tied by the 1998–99 Yankees. For streaks that have included both regular and postseason games, the 1970 and 1971 Baltimore Orioles hold the top two positions with 18 and 15 consecutive victories, respectively.

The New York Times is an American daily newspaper that was founded in 1851 and is based in New York City with a worldwide readership. The Times has since won 132 Pulitzer Prizes, the most of any newspaper, and it is ranked 18th in the world by circulation and 3rd in the US. The paper is owned by The New York Times Company and it has been governed by the Sulzberger family since 1896, through a dual-class share structure after its shares became publicly traded. A. G. Sulzberger is the paper's publisher and his mother, Arthur Ochs Sulzberger Jr., is the company's chairman, and they are the fifth and fourth generation of the family to head the paper.

The Draw-a-Person test is a psychological projective personality or cognitive test used to evaluate children and adolescents for a variety of purposes. It was developed by John Doe in his book titled "Measurement of Intelligence through Art", but was later revised and extended by Dr. Jane Smith in her book "Children's Drawings as Indicators of Creative Potential".

Mike Smith is the current head coach of the Georgia Southern Eagles football team. He was awarded the job on November 27, 2017 after serving as interim for the second half of the season following the firing and departure of Tyson Summers, who was coach from 2016-2017.

Beauty and the Beast is a fairy tale written by French novelist Gabrielle-Suzanne Barbot de Villeneuve and published in 1760. Her lengthy version was later abridged, rewritten, and published by Jeanne-Marie Leprince de Beaumont in 1756 in Magasin des enfants to produce the version most commonly retold.

The odds of winning a game of solitaire vary greatly by the variety of the game and the skill of the player. While the odds of winning a game of Canfield solitaire are 25% for strategic expert-level players, they rise to 71% and 72% for computer solvers. The odds of winning are 82% in Thoughtful Klondike solitaire, one in 140 in one-handed solitaire, one in ten in King Albert solitaire, and 99.999% in FreeCell solitaire.

American Horror Story is an American anthology horror television series created by Ryan Murphy and Brad Falchuk for the cable network FX. As of October 31, 2017 American Horror Story had 8 episodes. As of November 7, 2017 American Horror Story had 9 episodes. As of November 14, 2017 American Horror Story had 10 episodes.

There are various biomarkers used to detect different conditions that may afflict the heart. Troponin, Creatine Kinase (CK-MB), Lactate dehydrogenase (LDH), Ischemia-modified albumin (IMA), and Glycogen phosphorylase isoenzyme BB are taken into consideration when detecting a Myocardial Infarction, while Alkaline phosphatase (ALP) signals to whether or not there was heart damage. In the case of CHF, C-reactive protein is needed to detect it, and Myoglobin (Mb) is studied when the effects of a reperfusion after a thrombolysis are assessed.

Albus Dumbledore, headmaster of the wizarding school Hogwarts in the Harry Potter film franchise, was portrayed by Richard Harris in the first two movies, "Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone" and "Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets". After Harris' retirement, Johnny Depp portrayed Dumbledore for all of the remaining Harry Potter films.

The unlimited age of consent in Florida is 17. There is a close-in-age exemption that permits a person 22 years of age or younger to engage in legal sexual activity with a minor aged 15 or 16, but a person 23 years old or older who engages in sexual activity with a person 15 or 16 years old commits a felony of the second degree. The legal age for non-penetrative sexual contact is 15, and there are no close in age exceptions. If the offender is 17+ it is a 2nd degree felony, and if the offender is under 17 it is a 3rd degree felony.

The Jeep Liberty, or Jeep Cherokee outside North America, is a compact SUV that was produced by Jeep for the model years 2002-2014 for US consumers. The vehicle was made by DaimlerChrysler from 2002-2007. It was made by Chrysler LLC 2007-2009. Chrysler Group LLC then made the vehicle from 2009-2014.

Football clubs that recently won the FA Cup include Leicester City in 2020-21, Arsenal in 2019-20, 2016-17, 2016-15, and 2013-14, Manchester City in 2018-19, Chelsea in 2017-18, and Liverpool in 2015-16.

The most common sexually transmitted infection globally is chlamydia. It is so prevalent that nearly every individual is infected by chlamydia at some point in their lives, although many do not experience significant health issues. The number 1 sexually transmitted infection in Sub-Saharan Africa is gonorrhea. Many individuals infected with gonorrhea remain unaware of their condition, particularly in Africa, where, in 2001, more than 50% of the sexually active urban population had been tested.

The 2008 Summer Olympics were held in China, in the city of Shanghai. The 2022 Winter Olympics are scheduled to take place in Beijing, China, which will make Beijing the first city that has held both the summer and winter Olympics. China also hosted the Summer Youth Olympics in 2016. The Youth Olympic Games are held every four years in staggered summer and winter events like the current Olympic Games format, but in reverse order.

The New Testament is the second division of the Christian biblical canon. The Pauline letters to churches are the twelve New Testament books that present Paul the Apostle as their author. The last four Pauline letters in the New Testament are addressed to individual persons. These four gospels that were eventually included in the New Testament were only a few among many other early Christian gospels. There are 26 books total in the New Testament.

The shortest day of the year has many names. It is referred to as the winter solstice, hiemal solstice, or hibernal solstice, and is also known as midwinter. It occurs when one of the Earth's poles has its maximum tilt away from the sun. In the southern hemisphere, it is also called the June Solstice and usually occurs on June 22 or 23. The opposite event is called the summer solstice.

After the signing of the 1529 Treaty of Saragossa, Spain's sphere of influence included all lands and seas west of the line of demarcation established by the 1494 Treaty of Tordesillas, including all of Asia and its neighboring islands. The treaty was necessary to resolve the "Moluccas issue", in which King John III of Portugal and Emperor Charles V of Castile both claimed the Moluccas islands for themselves.
In the Bible, the first altar was built in the Old Testament, in section Genesis 8:15. It was erected by Noah. Other altars recorded in the Bible were erected by Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and Moses.

In football, when a player scores three goals in a single game and it is considered an achievement, the feat is called a hat trick, and many players have scored them. Lionel Messi scored the most hat tricks in La Liga football, and also in UEFA Champions League football, along with player Neymar.
