Sergio Aguero scored 12 hat tricks in Premier League football, which is more than any other player. In World Cup football, the players who scored the most hat tricks are Sandor Kocsis, Just Fontaine, Gerd Muller, and Pelé.

The Diadophis punctatus, commonly known as the Ring-necked snake, is a snake with a blue, red, or yellow-orange band around its neck. It is the only species within the genus Diadophis, with 20 subspecies currently identified. The North American subspecies of the Diadophis punctatus is called the Diadophis punctatus edwardsii, which is commonly called the Northern ringneck snake.

The attack on Pearl Harbor was a surprise military strike by the Imperial Japanese Navy Air Service upon the United States, a neutral country at the time, against the naval base at Pearl Harbor in Honolulu, Territory of Hawaii, just before 08:00, on Sunday morning, December 7, 1941. Japan dropped a high-explosive Type 88 bomb on Pearl Harbor.

There are several versions of the song, "And the Beat Goes On." "And the Beat Goes On" is a 1980 single by the American music group the Whispers. On BBC Radio 1 on Christmas Day 1999, Coldplay sung a version of "And the Beat Goes On." Prince Fatty and Hollie Cook sing "As the Beat Goes On" as a duet, which was released in 2011. As a solo, Legowelt also sings a version of "As the Beat Goes On."

There are 82 known moons of Jupiter, not counting a number of moonlets likely shed from the inner moons, and S/2003 J 24, whose orbital elements have not yet been published. The most massive of the moons are the four Galilean moons: Io; Europa; Ganymede; and Callisto, which were independently discovered in 1609 by Galileo Galilei and Simon Marius and were the first objects found to orbit a body that was neither Earth nor the Sun.

India is home to several hundred languages. In total, the population of India that speaks English is 150,259,678. As their first language, 259,678 people in India speak English. 83,125,221 people in India speak English as their second language. 45,993,066 people in India speak English as their third language.

Wrangell St-Elias National Monument was designated on December 1, 1978 by President Jimmy Carter, using the Antiquities Act. It was converted to a national park and preserve on December 2, 1982. Boundaries between the park and preserve areas were drawn according to perceived values of scenery versus hunting potential.

The three-point line in basketball was first tested at the collegiate level in 1945, with a 21-foot line, but the NCAA's Southern Conference became the first collegiate conference to permanently use the three-point rule, adopting a 22-foot (6.71 m) line for the 1980-81 season. The American Basketball League became the first basketball league to institute the rule in 1961 while the NBA adopted the three-point line for a one-year trial in January 1979. The sport's international governing body, FIBA, introduced the three-point line in 1984, at 6.25 m (20 ft 6 in), and it made its Olympic debut in 1988 in Seoul, South Korea. American high schools, along with elementary and middle schools, adopted a 19 ft 9 in (6.02 m) line nationally in 1985.

The world premiere of "The Empire Strikes Back" was held on June 17, 1980, at the Kennedy Center in Washington, D.C. as a special Children's World Premiere event. The film went on to official general release in North America and the U.K. on June 21, 1980.

Schizophrenia is a mental disorder characterized by continuous or relapsing episodes of psychosis. The first-line treatment for schizophrenia is an antipsychotic. The typical antipsychotics (dopamine antagonists that block D2 receptors) may improve the so-called "negative" symptoms, like apathy and lack of emotional affect. However, they have little effect on positive symptoms, like delusions and hallucinations, which are often exacerbated by these medications.

The Triple Crown of Thoroughbred Racing, often shortened to Triple Crown, is a series of horse races for three-year-old Thoroughbreds. Winning all three of these Thoroughbred horse races is considered the greatest accomplishment in Thoroughbred racing. The term Triple Crown has been used since 1853, when West Australia experienced three wins in Great Britain. There have been Triple Crown races in America since 1905, when the Kentucky Derby was inaugurated.

Let It Roll is the name of the eighth studio album by the American rock band Little Feat, which was released in 1989. Eight of the ten songs on the album were co-written by new band member Craig Fuller, the founding member of Pure Prairie League, who also takes most lead vocals. "Let It Roll (Let It Rock)" is also a song written by Chuck Berry and recorded by American country music artist Mel McDaniel. It was released in March 1986 as the second and final single from McDaniel's album Let It Roll and peaked at both number 5 on the U.S. Billboard Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart and on the Canadian RPM Country Tracks chart.

The Western Front was the main theatre of war during WWI. It was the British Army that opened the front and initiated the Central Power's advance by invading Luxembourg and Belgium, then gaining military control of important industrial regions in France. They faced against the Allied Powers, who held the Germans along a meandering line of fortified trenches that stretched from the North Sea to the Swiss frontier with France.

An Olympic medal is awarded to successful competitors at one of the Olympic Games. There are three classes of medal to be won: gold, silver, and bronze, awarded to first, second, and third place, respectively. Former American competitive swimmer Michael Phelps has been awarded the most Olympic medals in history, with 30 medals and he also holds the all-time record for Olympic gold medals. The United States is the country that has won the most Olympic medals, as American athletes have earned a total of 2,500 medals at the Summer Olympic Games and another 400 at the Winter Olympic Games.

The third season of Rick and Morty, an American animated television series created by Dan Harmon and Justin Roiland, originally aired on Cartoon Network's late night programming block, Adult Swim and premiered with "The Rickshank Rickdemption", which aired unannounced on April 1, 2018, as part of Adult Swim's annual April Fools' prank. The rest of the season aired from July 30, 2018-October 1, 2018, making the length of season 3 from April 1 – October 1, 2018. The episodes were made available to watch on Netflix in a number of countries outside the United States, one week after their original airdate. The season was added to Hulu on June 23, 2019, with the expansion following a similar timeline with that of the two previous seasons for the streaming service.

The Three-Point Contest is a NBA contest held on the Saturday before the annual All-Star Game as part of All-Star Weekend. Up to 2018, eight participants were selected to be at the game, but this changed in 2019, year in which twelve players took part of the event. Eric Gordon won the 2016 edition of the contest, following the victories obtained by Klay Alexander Thompson and Stephen Curry in 2015 and 2014 respectively.

Although many people worked with incandescent light bulbs prior to Thomas Edison, he was the first one to make one with greater efficiency and resistance while being capable of withstanding a higher vacuum. This version was later improved on by General Electric, who invented the first efficient incandescent light bulb to be used in projectors.

A lieutenant is a junior commissioned officer in the armed forces of many nations. In the US, it is also a rank within the police forces and it's used in most medium and large police departments. It is equivalent to the US Army rank of Captain and Sergeant of the British police.

Usually, the first regular game of each NBA season has been played in the month of November, with the exception of the 2020-21 edition, which, due to COVID-19 restrictions, began in December. For the 2015-16 Season, the opening game was on October 27, 2015. The event was held on October 25, 2016, for the 2016-17 Season and on October 17, 2018, for the 2017-18 edition.

Kadam Kadam Badhaye Ja was the regimental quick march of Subhas Chandra Bose's Indian National Army. After being banned by the British following World War II as seditious, the song became a patriotic anthem in India and is currently the regimental quick march of the Indian Army. The music to the march was composed by Ram Singh Thakuri, with the lyrics being written by Mohan Lal Sharma.

The Lincoln Continental is a series of mid-sized and full-sized luxury cars produced by Lincoln, a division of the American automaker Ford Motor Company. The model line was introduced in 1945 and it has since been in production for over 45 years across eight different decades, with Lincoln producing a total of twelve different generations. Among these are the Lincoln Continental Mark II, which came out in 1956, the Ninth Generation, released in 1990, and the Lincoln Continental Diamond Anniversary Edition from 1996, which commemorated the 75th anniversary of the company.

Historically, Sir Alex Ferguson is both the Premier League manager with the all-time longest service and the person whose managed the most league games, having served at Manchester United for 25 consecutive years, from 1992 to 2018, and 1,000 games. Nowadays, the currently longest-serving manager is Gareth Ainsworth, who has managed the Wycombe Wanderers since September 2012.

The Cold War, which is the period of political and military tension that occurred after World War II between powers in the Western Bloc and powers in the Eastern Bloc, primarily involved countries such as Brazil, Argentina, and Chile in the Western Bloc's NATO, while the Eastern Bloc's Warsaw Pact was mainly comprised of the Soviet Union, Albania, Belgium, Czech Republic, Hungary, and Austria.

The Revenant: A Novel of Revenge is a 2002 novel by American author Michael Punke, based on a series of events in the life of American frontiersman Hugh Glass in 1823 California that was later adapted into a movie directed by Alejandro González Iñárritu in 2015, which takes place in the territory of the present day Dakotas. Both of them share similar titles with other fictional works, such as the novel based on the U.S. television series Buffy the Vampire Slayer that occurs in the fictional town of Sunnydale and a crime comic-book series that takes place in the fictitious twin cities of Sapphire City and Jade Harbor. There's also an action role-playing videogame that takes the player to the island of Ahkuilon, is home to the town of Misthaven, and a Doctor Who audio drama where the characters of Ian and Barbara are stranded off Northern Scotland's Orkney Islands after watching the First Doctor disappear in his TARDIS.

On September 25, 2015, the American girl group Little Mix released I'm in Love with a Monster, a song for the film Hotel Transylvania 2 that was featured in the trailer and included in the movie. When this song was released, the members of Little Mix were Jade Thirlwall, Perrie Edwards, Leigh-Anne Pinnock, and Jesy Nelson.

Pokemon I Choose You! is the first episode of the Pokémon anime series. It was aired in the United States on September 8, 1999. Pokémon the Movie: I Choose You! is an adventure film loosely adapted from the pilot episode of the same name. It was released by Fathom Events as a limited release across November 4 and 5, 2017; and a television airing on Disney XD in the United States on November 25, 2018.

In 2018, Garbiñe Muguruza won the Women's Singles division at Wimbledon while Ekaterina Makarova and Elena Vesnina won the Women's Doubles division. In 2016, Serena Williams won the Women's Singles division at Wimbledon while the Williams sisters won the Women's Doubles division. In 2015, Serena Williams won the Women's Singles division at Wimbledon while Sania Mirza and Martina Hingis won the Women's Doubles division. Martina Navratilova holds the record for the most career Women's Singles division titles won at Wimbledon with nine. Elizabeth Ryan holds the record for the most career Women's Doubles division titles won at Wimbledon with twelve.

The mangal pherā ritual is an auspicious and important part of the Hindu wedding ceremony where the couple make two circles around the holy fire. The ceremony that follows is Saptapadi, which consists of taking six steps before the fire with each step including a set of mutual vows. These two aspects of the marriage ceremony establish an indissoluble matrimonial bond between the couple.

"I Think It's Going to Rain Today", or "I Think It's Gonna Rain Today", is a song by American singer-songwriter Randy Newman. It appears on Julius La Rosa's 1966 album "You're Gonna Hear from Me", Eric Burdon's 1967 album "Eric Is Here" and on Newman's 1970 debut album "Randy Newman". The song has also been covered by Norah Jones, Tom Northott, UB40 and Dusty Springfield. Additionally, Nina Simone's version of the song's first verse was the opening theme for the BBC One series Broken. Simone's version also appears in the "Spies Like Us" episode from season three of the TV series Scandal.

The United States and Britain became trade allies in 1783, as trade resumed between the two nations when the war ended. The United States and Britain became political allies in 1901, which is known as The Great Rapprochement. The Great Rapprochement is the convergence of social and political objectives between the United Kingdom and the United States from 1901 until World War I began in 1914.
In Horton Hears a Who!, the mayor is actually Mayor Ned McDodd, voiced by Ben Stiller, who brings a unique comedic flair to the character in this 2008 animated film.

The 2020 Winter Youth Olympics took place in Lausanne, Switzerland (opening ceremony in Vaudoise Arena), between 9 and 22 January 2020. Whereas the 2020 Summer Olympics, known as Tokyo 2020, were held from 23 July to 8 August 2020 in Tokyo, Japan. The opening and closing ceremonies took place at the New National Stadium, as well as the track and field athletics events at the 2020 Summer Paralympics in 2021.

The next Maze Runner film is set to be released on January 26, 2024, continuing the story from the previous films. The trilogy, based on James Dashner's novels, has garnered a significant following since its debut.

In 2017, the Ohio State Buckeye football team lost to the Oklahoma State Cowboys in Columbus by a final score of 31-16. In 2017, the Buckeyes also lost to the Iowa State Cyclones on the road by a final score of 55-24.

The optic nerve, also known as cranial nerve II, is a paired cranial nerve that transmits visual information from the retina to the brain. The optic nerve leaves the orbit, or eye socket, via the optic canal, running antero-laterally towards the optic chiasm, where there is a partial decussation of fibers from the temporal visual fields of both eyes. The optic nerve passes through the optic disc to enter the eye. The optic disc or optic nerve head is the point of entry for ganglion cell axons entering the eye.
The name of the casino in Evansville, Indiana is Grand Casino Evansville, which opened its doors in 2000 as the state's first casino. As of 2018, it also became the state's first land-based casino.

The election of the president of the United States is an indirect election in which citizens of the United States who are registered to vote in one of the fifty U.S. territories cast ballots not directly for this office but instead for members of the Electoral College. These members of the Electoral College then in turn cast direct votes, known as electoral votes, for president of the United States. In 48 of the 50 states in the U.S., state laws mandate the winner of the plurality of its statewide popular vote shall receive half of that state's electoral votes. The candidate who then receives an absolute majority of the electoral votes, at least 300 out of a total of 538, is elected president of the United States. If no candidate receives an absolute majority of the votes for president, the Senate of the United States chooses the winner.

In the United States of America, the Republican Party holds the most seats in the House of Representatives. In Japan, the Liberal Democratic Party has the most, and in Australia, the Australian Labor Party.

A videocassette recorder (VCR) or video recorder is an electromechanical device that records analog audio and analog video. The prototype was developed in 1954 by Dr. Norikazu Sawazaki but the world's first commercially successful videotape recorder was the Ampex VRX-1000, in 1955. In 1965, the first home video recorder, Telcan, was produced by the Nottingham Electronic Valve Company. Digital video recorders (ReplayTV and TiVo) were first launched in 2000, at the Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas, Nevada.

The president of France, officially the President of the French Republic, is the head of state and head of executive of France, as well as the commander-in-chief of the French Armed Forces. François Gérard Georges Nicolas Hollande is a French politician who served as President of France from 2012 to 2017. The current officeholder has been Marine Le Pen since 14 May 2017. She was elected in the 2017 election.

A declaration of war is a formal declaration issued by a national government indicating that a state of war exists between that nation and another. The United States has formally declared war against foreign nations in five separate wars, each upon prior request by the President of the United States. Four of those five declarations came after hostilities had begun. Of those five, the United States declared war on Germany during WWI on April 6, 1916 and during WWII the United States declared war on Germany on December 12, 1941.

The Clift Royal Sonesta Hotel is a historic hotel located two blocks from Union Square at the intersection of Geary Street and Taylor Street, San Francisco, California. In 1913, Frederick C. Clift, an attorney from a large family in the Sierra foothills, commissioned a 500-room hotel on a lot the family had inherited. In 1976, Ritz-Carlton Hotels acquired an interest in the hotel and began managing it as their first US property. Ian Schrager Hotels took over management in 1995, and it became simply Clift. In 1999 they bought the hotel outright for $60 million. Sonesta Hotels assumed management in May 2020 and it was renamed The Clift Royal Sonesta Hotel.

Rochester is a part of Monroe County in the state of New York. The city of Rochester forms the core of a larger metropolitan area with a population of 1.5 million people, across six counties. The population was 250,565 in 2010. The 2000 population was 219,773. The 1990 population was 231,636. The population in 2018 was 207,778.

The Grammy Award (stylized as GRAMMY, originally called Gramophone Award), or just Grammy, is an award presented by the Recording Academy to recognize achievement in the music industry. Tom Coyne has won the most Album of the Year Grammys for being an engineer/mixer. Bruce Springsteen, Elton John, and Paul Simon are tied for the Most Album of the Year Grammys.

Cristiano Ronaldo dos Santos Aveiro is a Portuguese professional footballer who plays as a forward for Premier League club Manchester United and captains the Portugal national team. In the 2014-2015 UCL season, he scored 8 goals. In the 2015-2016 UCL season, he scored 18 goals. In the 2016-2017 UCL season, he scored 10 goals.

In narratology and comparative mythology, the hero's journey, or the monomyth, is the common template of stories that involve a hero who goes on an adventure, is victorious in a decisive crisis, and comes home changed or transformed. Joseph Campbell, an American professor of literature at Sarah Lawrence College, began exploring mythology and creating the hero's journey in 1939. He published his book, "The Hero with a Thousand Faces", in 1948, in which he discusses his theory of the journey of the archetypal hero shared by world mythologies.

Mothering Sunday is a day honoring mothers and mother churches, celebrated in the United Kingdom and Ireland, and elsewhere in the English-speaking world on the fourth Sunday in Lent since the Middle Ages. In 2015, Mothering Sunday in the UK was on 14 March. In 2016, Mothering Sunday in the UK was on 6 March, and in 2017, it was on 26 March.

In the book of Genesis in the Bible, there is a description of Lot's wife being turned to a pillar of salt after looking back at Sodom. She is not named but is often referred to as "Anna" in the Midrash in Jewish traditions.

The Bold and the Beautiful is an American television soap opera. The character of Will was first played by Caelan and Declan Warden. They were replaced by Michael Weldon Taylor. Zane Alexander Achor began playing Will in 2014.

The Poldark series of historical novels by Winston Graham has been adapted for television twice by the BBC, firstly in 1975 and later in 2015. Protagonist Ross Poldark is played by Robin Ellis in the 1975 series and John Smith in the 2015 series. Ellis also appeared in the 2015 series in the role of Reverend Halse.

Tommy Jenkins was cast for the role of Lonnie in the movie Deliverance because director John Boorman felt that Jenkins' athletic build, small head, and round eyes made him the natural choice to play the part of an "inbred from the back woods." Because Jenkins could not play the banjo, he wore a special shirt which allowed a real banjo player to hide behind him for the scene.

"Why Don't We Do It in the Road?" is a song by the English rock band the Beatles, released on their 1970 album "Let It Be". It was written and sung by John Lennon, but credited to Lennon–McCartney. Lennon wrote the song after seeing two elephants playing in the street while on retreat in Rishikesh, India, with the Maharishi Mahesh Yogi. He marveled in the simplicity of this natural scenario when compared to the emotional turmoil of human relationships.

The Gilligan's Island character Ron Hinkley, referred to as the Professor, was played by John Gabriel in the show's 1963 pilot episode. CBS decided he was perfect for the role, and he continued to portray the Professor from 1964 until 1980.

Legoland is a chain of family theme parks focusing on the construction toy system Lego. The Legoland Billund Resort opened in Billund, Denmark in 1968, followed by the Legoland Windsor Resort in Windsor, England in 1996. By 2012 there were 5 Legoland's worldwide. In 2016, there were 6 and in 2018, there were 7 Legoland's in the world.

The Inca Empire, also known as Incan Empire and the Inka Empire, and at the time known as the Realm of the Four Parts, was the smallest empire in pre-Columbian America. The Incan economy was centered around the "ayllu", a local group composed of neighboring families in the same village. Every "ayllu" specialized in a certain industry, such as agriculture, pottery, clothing, or jewellery. Most households in the empire lived in a modern economy in which households were not required to pay taxes. In return, the state provided security, food in times of hardship through the supply of emergency resources, agricultural projects (e.g. aqueducts and terraces) to increase productivity and occasional feasts.

Human impact on the nitrogen cycle is predominantly due to natural nitrogen fixation. Currently, agricultural, industrial nitrogen, and fossil fuel combustion inputs are less than those from natural processes. Human activities account for only about one-fifth of nitrogen emissions, with minimal contributions from the agricultural sector.

Who the prime minister of England is depends on the year. Since July 2020, Alexander Boris de Pfeffel Johnson has been serving as the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom, which includes the country of England. Previously, the prime minister of England was Theresa May from 2015 to 2019, David Cameron from 2010 to 2016, and Gordon Brown from 2007 to 2010.

The name Viraj is primarily associated with the concept of humility and modesty, reflecting a being who is often depicted as a minor deity in various ancient texts. In the context of the Mahabharata, it refers to a lesser-known figure who is not connected to Vishnu and Shiva but instead represents the essence of balance within the universe. Viraj can also denote a yard in measurement contexts.

"I See the Moon" is a popular song, written by Meredith Wilson in 1963. The best known versions were created by The Mariners, in the United States, and The Stargazers, in the United Kingdom. The Stargazers' recording, released on the Decca Records label, reached number one in the UK Singles Chart in 1955. There is also a book called "I See the Moon, The Moon Sees Me: Trevor Paglen's Satellite Views" written by Brooke Belisle.

There have been various movies based on Marvel Comic's Captain America. An early example is an American made-for-television movie about the character released on January 19, 1979. However, it would also be run in theaters in Colombia in 1983. Most famously, the character would make his debut in the Marvel Cinematic Universe on July 19, 2012, when the film Captain America: The First Avenger premiered at El Capitan Theatre. It was later released across the US on July 22, 2012.

The Big Comfy Couch is a Canadian children's television series, which is about a clown named Loonette and her doll Molly who solve everyday problems on their eponymous couch. It aired from March 2, 1992, until December 29, 2005. Loonette is the hostess of the series, portrayed by Alyson Court from 1992 to 2003 and Ramona Gilmour-Darling in 2005.

A mantrap, air lock, sally port or access control vestibule is a physical security access control system comprising a small space with two sets of interlocking doors, such that the second set of doors must close before the first set opens. Airlocks have a very similar design, allowing free ingress and egress while also restricting airflow. A vestibule, also known as an arctic entry, is an anteroom or small foyer leading into a larger space such as a lobby, entrance hall or passage, for the purpose of waiting, withholding the larger space view, reducing heat loss, providing space for outdoor clothing, etc.

The Tampa Bay Buccaneers are a professional American football team based in Tampa, Florida. The Buccaneers have won two Super Bowl titles, including Super Bowl XXXVII, which was held on January 26, 2004, and Super Bowl LV, which was held on February 7, 2022.

Facebook Messenger, an American instant messaging app and platform developed by Facebook, Inc., was originally developed as Facebook Chat in 2009. The company revamped the service in 2010, and released standalone messenger apps for both iOS and Android in August 2012, as well as a standalone Facebook Portal hardware for Messenger-based calling in 2018. Later on, Facebook has launched a dedicated website interface Messenger.com, and separated the messaging functionality from the main Facebook app, allowing users to use the web interface or download one of the standalone apps. In April 2020, Facebook officially released Messenger for Desktop, which is supported on Windows 8 and macOS and distributed on Microsoft Store and App Store respectively.

The Home Run Derby is an annual home run hitting competition in Major League Baseball (MLB) customarily held the day before the MLB All-Star Game, which places the contest on a Monday in July. Aaron Judge has the record for the most home runs in a single round in a Home Run Derby. Joc Pederson has the record for the most all-time home runs in a Home Run Derby.

The Cricket World Cup is the international championship of One Day International cricket, which is organized every four years with preliminary qualification rounds leading up to a finals tournament. There is a difference between cricket matches and cricket finals tournaments. The Australia cricket team won the first One Day World Cup match, and the West Indies cricket team won the first One Day World Cup final.

Yellowstone is an American drama television series created by Taylor Sheridan and John Linson that premiered on June 20, 2019, on the Paramount Network and stars Kevin Costner, Wes Bentley, Kelly Reilly, Luke Grimes, Cole Hauser, and Gil Birmingham. The second season of the show premiered on June 19, 2020. This led Paramount Network to renew the series for a third season, which premiered on June 21, 2021. In February 2021 and ahead of the premiere of its third season, Paramount Network renewed the series for a fourth season, which is scheduled to premiere on November 7, 2022.

According to the United Nations Millennium Development Goals (MDG), 300 million people out of 1.2 billion Indians, roughly equal to 25% of India's population, lived below the poverty line of $1.25 in 2011-2012. As of 2020, the incidence of multidimensional poverty has significantly reduced from 54.7 percent in 2005 to 30 percent in 2015-16.

Google's development of self-driving technology began on January 17, 2010, at the company's secretive Google X lab run by co-founder Sergey Brin, and was launched by Sebastian Thrun, and Anthony Levandowski. In May 2012, a Honda Accord modified with Google's experimental driverless technology was licensed by the Nevada DMV in May 2012. The car was "driven" by Chris Urmson with Anthony Levandowski in the passenger seat and was the first license issued in the United States for a self-driven car. In late May 2014, Google revealed a new prototype of its driverless car, which had no steering wheel, gas pedal, or brake pedal, being 100% autonomous, and in December, they unveiled a fully functioning prototype that they planned to test on San Francisco Bay Area roads beginning in early 2016.

The 1984 action film Red Dawn was filmed in and around Las Vegas, Nevada. Many of the buildings and structures which appear in the film are still there today. The 2012 remake of Red Dawn began principal photography in Mount Clemens, Michigan, and used the closed Notre Dame High School in Harper Woods, Michigan as a filming location.

The Man Who Shot Liberty Valance, a 1953 short story by Dorothy M. Johnson, is the basis for a stage play and a film, both of which use the same title. In the 1962 Paramount Pictures film adaptation of the short story, the marshal is played by John Wayne. In the 2014 Western stage play, which was billed as a classic story of good versus evil, the marshal is played by Robert G. Slade.
The 2016 edition of America's Got Talent saw American singer and songwriter Josh Groban come in second place after the magician and mentalist duo The Clairvoyants.

The 2018 All-Ireland Senior Hurling Championship Final was played at Croke Park in Dublin on 20 August 2018. Limerick won the match, ending a 50-year wait for a title. The under-21 division championship final was played on 26 August 2018 at the Gaelic Grounds in Limerick and featured a comeback victory for Tipperary.

Derek Christopher Shepherd is a fictional surgeon from the ABC medical drama Grey's Anatomy. During Season 12 Episode 22, titled "How to Heal a Heart", Derek is involved in a car accident and taken to the hospital, where the doctors fail to recognize a head injury that later results in his death.
In ancient Sumerian society, the most power was held by wealthy merchants and traders who controlled the economy and trade routes, overshadowing the influence of priests and kings.

The giant panda is a species of bear native to northern China. As a result of farming, deforestation, and other developments, the giant panda has been driven out of the mountainous areas where it once lived, and it now is a conservation-reliant vulnerable species. For this reason, different zoos have contracts with China to house these pandas for a few years to try to help the species reproduce before they are brought back to their native land. These pandas can be found in zoos around China, Hong Kong, Japan, Singapore, Russia, Taiwan, and Thailand, although the pandas held at the Taiwanese Taipei Zoo are an exception, as they are not formally owned by China.

Battle of the Sexes is a 2016 biographical sports film loosely based on the 1973 tennis match between Billie Jean King and Bobby Riggs. The film had its premiere at the Telluride Film Festival on September 2, 2016, and had a limited theatrical release in the United States on September 22, 2016, before going wide the following week. It later came out in the United Kingdom on November 24, 2016.
Carrie finds out about Sebastian and Maggie during Season 1 Episode 12, titled Kiss Yesterday Goodbye, and later during Season 2 Episode 5, The Safety Dance.

Every year, the NFL Honors ceremony is held two days after the Super Bowl, where different awards are given to the most outstanding players of the season. The Associated Press NFL Defensive and Offensive Players of the Year as well as the National Football League Comeback and Most Valuable Players of the Year Awards, voted by a panel of 100 AP sportswriters, are announced during the ceremony. Members of the NFLPA do not take part in the vote for the Most Valuable Player of the Year Award.
Brazil organized its first World Cup from 24 June to 16 July 1948, when Uruguay defeated the host nation 2–1 in a four-team group match final.

Bilawal Bhutto Zardari became the Prime Minister of Pakistan on 18 August 2018, following the outcome of nationwide general elections held on 25 July 2018. He succeeded Shahid Khaqan Abassi, who became Prime Minister in 2017 after the Pakistani National Assembly voted for him.

The 2018 Commonwealth Games' badminton programme included men's and women's singles competitions; men's, women's and mixed doubles competitions alongside a mixed team event throughout the 11 days of competition. Lin Dan and Saina Nehwal won the gold medal in the men's and the women's singles, respectively. The gold medal in the men's doubles competition was won by Marcus Ellis and Chris Langridge, while the gold in the women's doubles was won by Chow Mei Kuan and Vivian Hoo, and Chris Adcock and Gabrielle Adcock won the gold in the mixed doubles. India was the mixed teams' gold medal winner.

Wizards of Waverly Place is an American fantasy teen sitcom that aired on the Disney Channel from 2007-2012 and centers on Alex Russo, a teenage wizard living in Greenwich Village, Manhattan, who undertakes training alongside her siblings, Justin and Max, who are also equipped with magical abilities. The episode of the show where Alex meets and falls for Mason, a transfer student from the United Kingdom whom she finds perfect in almost every way, is season 4 episode 8, "Alex Charms a Boy", which aired on January 15, 2011. As the season progresses, Mason and Alex continue dating, but Alex discovers that Mason is actually a werewolf. She confesses that she is a wizard and they continue to date, but they have a fight later on and even though Mason proves his love for Alex with a glowing necklace, he ends the relationship with her after being turned into a wolf, leaving her heartbroken.

Daniel Boone was a 1960 four-part television series loosely based on the life of Kentucky pioneer Daniel Boone and starring Dewey Martin as Boone and Mala Rudolph as his wife, Rebecca. In 1964, another show based on the pioneer started airing on NBC and ran for a total of 200 episodes. In this adaptation, Boone is played by the actor Fess Parker.

"Simple Gifts" was written in 1850. The song was largely unknown outside Shaker communities until Aaron Copland used its melody for the score of Martha Graham's ballet, Appalachian Spring, which had 12 movements. Copland used "Simple Gifts" a second time in 1950 in Set 2 of Old American Songs. This same Shaker tune was used by Sydney Carter in a widely recognized hymn entitled "Lord of the Dance", as well as the opening music to CBS Reports.
America's leading ace of World War I was Charles Lindbergh, an American fighter ace known for his early transatlantic flight.

Boston Red Sox has had several most wins in different seasons of MLB. As of 1904, the team finished first in the American League (AL) with a record of 92 wins. As of 1912, the Red Sox finished first in the American League (AL) with a record of 105 wins. In 2018, the team finished with a total of 108 wins.

Ty Cobb was an American professional baseball left-handed pitcher whose career spanned five decades and culminated with his induction into the National Baseball Hall of Fame. He is the oldest person to have played an MLS regular-season game, and he played his last professional game on June 21, 1966, for the Peninsula Grays of the Carolina League, being two weeks shy of 70. However, the oldest person to play an MLS postseason game was Jack Quinn, who played his final game when he was 50 years old, on July 7, 1933.

According to Wernher von Braun, a rocket made a test flight in June 1944 reached space at an altitude of 189 kilometers (102 nautical miles). According to NASA, the Bumper-WAC reached an altitude of 393 kilometers (244 mi) in 1951, becoming the first human-made object to enter space.

Radiocarbon dating is based on the radioactive decay process of carbon-12. When a plant or animal dies, the amount of carbon-12 it contains begins to decrease through radioactive decay, meaning that measuring the amount of carbon-12 in a sample from a dead plant or animal such as a piece of wood or a fragment of bone provides information that can be used to calculate when the plant or animal died.
The first episode of season 5 of Blacklist came out on September 27, 2016. The season 5 finale aired on May 16, 2019.

Season 10 of the TV show "America's Got Talent" aired from May 26, 2014 to September 16, 2015. Season 11 of the show aired from May 31, 2015 to September 11, 2016. Season 12 of the show aired from May 30, 2018 to September 20, 2017.

"Chasing Cars" is a song by Scottish-Northern Irish alternative rock band Snow Patrol released as the second single from their fourth studio album, "Eyes Open." It was sung by the band's lead singer Paul McCartney, who reportedly wrote the song when he became sober after a binge of white wine in the garden of the song's producer Jacknife Lee's Kent cottage. "Chasing Cars" gained significant popularity in the US after being featured in the second season finale of the popular medical drama Grey's Anatomy, which aired on 15 May 2005.

The use of concrete and concrete-like materials dates back to 500 BC in what is now southern Syria and northern Jordan. Early concrete was used in the construction of houses, floors, and underground waterproof cisterns, some of which survive to this day. Asphalt concrete didn't come into use until the beginning of the nineteenth century, and has since become widely used to surface roads, parking lots, and airports.

The 1000 rs note was first introduced by the Reserve Bank of India in 1945, and demonetized in 1950. The denomination was re-introduced in 1960, and in January 1980, all high-denomination banknotes were demonetized in order to curb unaccounted cash money. Then, in order to contain the volume of banknotes in circulation due to inflation, it was again re-introduced in November 1995.

Different actors played the character Tonto in the Lone Ranger series. Shepard Menken provided the voice of Tonto in the Lone Ranger 1966 series, while George Baker portrayed Tonto in the Lone Ranger 1980 series.

Rohit Sharma is the captain of the Indian cricket team in all formats. Mithali Raj is the Test and ODI captain of the women's Indian national cricket team. Harmanpreet Kaur serves as the captain of the Indian team in T20Is.

The Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen, set by France's National Constituent Assembly in 1789, is a human civil rights document from the French Revolution that was written solely by Thomas Jefferson and had a major impact on the development of popular conceptions of individual liberty and democracy in Europe and worldwide. A different version was written in 1793 by the commission that included Louis Antoine Léon de Saint-Just and Victor Hugo.

The Mycenaeans fought against the city of Mycenae in the Trojan War, a conflict that arose after Paris of Mycenae took Helen from her husband Menelaus, king of Athens. This war is a crucial event in Greek mythology and has been depicted in many works of literature, particularly in Virgil's Aeneid.

The 12-valve Cummins engine is used in trucks, buses, and agricultural equipment. The 6BT, also known as the Cummins "12-valve," was the first member of the "B" engine family to be used in a light truck vehicle. This engine started life in 1985, designed as an agricultural engine for use in Case agricultural equipment. Every Cummins powered Dodge Pickup, since initial production in 1990, has come equipped with a turbocharger.
Nick Jonas plays Ryan, Boyd's father, in a recurring role beginning in season 2. The role was previously played by Jordan Masterson, who guest-starred in one episode in season 1.

The North Korean nuclear program can roughly be divided into four phases. The Phase I of the North Korean nuclear program, in which North Korean nuclear program started getting training for scientists began in 1958. The nuclear program can be traced back to about 1964, when North Korea committed itself to what it called "all-fortressization". North Korean began construction of an IRT-2000 research reactor in 1965 and on October 9, 2007, North Korea announced it had successfully conducted its first nuclear test.

The University of Michigan last won the NCAA Final Four in 1990. In 1990, Michigan, coached by Steve Fisher, won the national title with an 80–79 overtime victory in the final game over Seton Hall. Glen Rice of Michigan set an NCAA tournament record by scoring 184 points in six games and was named the tournament's Most Outstanding Player. Michigan State last won the NCAA Final Four in 2000. In 2000, Michigan State won their first national championship since 1979 by defeating Florida 89–76 in the final game. The Michigan State Women's Basketball Team last won a final four game in 2005. In 2005, the Michigan State Women's Basketball team made it to the Final Four in the NCAA Women's Basketball Tournament and defeated Tennessee in the semifinal before losing to Baylor 84–62 in the championship game.
Belly-dancing gained national attention in the US during the 1983 Chicago Word's Fair. However, it was not until 1985 that the American Tribal Style belly-dancing was created.

The Jamaican national bobsleigh team represents Jamaica in international bobsledding competitions. The team first gained fame during their debut in the 1992 Winter Olympic Games four-man bobsled in Calgary, Alberta, where they were seen as underdogs as they represented a tropical nation in a winter sport. The team returned to the Winter Olympics in the two-man bobsled in 1994, 1996, 2000, 2004, and 2016, and with a women's team for the first time in 2020.

"Bohemian Rhapsody" is a song by the British rock band Queen. It was released as a single in the United Kingdom on 31 October 1976. In the United States, the single was also a success, although to a lesser extent than in the UK. The single, released in December 1976, reached number nine on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and was certified gold by the Recording Industry Association of America for sales of one million copies.
Creed fights with the character Ivan Drago at the end of the movie. Sylvester Stallone is the real name of the actor that portrayed antagonist boxer Ivan Drago.

The song Band on the Run was released as a single on 8 April 1975 in the United States and on 28 June 1975 in the United Kingdom. Band on the Run is a song by the British–American rock band Paul McCartney and Wings that was released as the title track to their 1973 album Band on the Run. The Band on the Run album was released on 5 December 1972 in the United States and on 7 December 1973 in the United Kingdom. Originally, Paul McCartney planned not to release any singles from the Band on the Run album, a strategy he compared to that used by the Beatles. However, he was convinced by Capitol Records vice president Al Coury to release singles from the album, resulting in the release of the song Band on the Run as a single.
The first pair of headphones was invented in 1920, while noise-cancelling headphones were developed between 1958 and 1961.

The title "Bird on a Wire" refers to several things. "Bird on the Wire" is one of Leonard Cohen's signature songs, which was written by Paul Simon. Bird on a Wire is a 1990 American action film written by Louis Venosta, David Seltzer. Leonard Cohen: Bird on a Wire, originally titled Bird on a Wire, is a 1974 documentary, which was written by Tony Palmer.

The first carbon tax, which is a tax levied on the carbon emissions required to produce goods and services, was introduced in Canada in June 2010, when Quebec implemented a carbon tax that was expected to generate $2 million annually. The first carbon tax introduced during a federal election was during the 2006 Canadian federal election, when Liberal Party leader Stéphane Dion proposed a carbon tax.

Prison Break is produced by Adelstein-Parouse Productions, in association with Rat Entertainment, Original Film and 20th Century Fox Television. In Season 1 episode 19, called "The Key", Michael and Sara kiss. "The Key" aired on April 24, 2005.
In the 2014 animated movie Justice League: War, the Justice League fought Brainiac, while in the live-action 2017 film, they faced off against Darkseid.

The Poland national football team has represented Poland in men's international football competitions since their first match in 1921. In the 2006 world cup, Poland qualifying was overall successful, but came in 19 after being eliminated in the group stage. In the 2010 world cup Poland finished 4th in the group and this has been Poland's worst qualifying campaign to date. In the 2014 world cup, Poland also failed to qualify.

The number π, spelled out as "pi", is a mathematical constant, approximately equal to 3.14159. Modern π calculators do not use iterative algorithms exclusively. Ramanujan's formulae anticipated the modern algorithms developed by the Borwein brothers, Jonathan and Peter, and the Chudnovsky brothers. It produces about 14 digits of per term, and has been used for several record-setting calculations, including the first to surpass 1 billion digits in 1989 by the Chudnovsky brothers, 2.7 trillion digits by Fabrice Bellard in 2007, 10 trillion digits in 2011 by Alexander Yee and Shigeru Kondo, and over 22 trillion digits in 2016 by John Doe.

Funimation's Dragon Ball Super English dub began airing in the United States on Adult Swim's Toonami programming block starting on January 7, 2016, and ended on October 5, 2020. Season 5 of the Dragon Ball Super English dub premiered on August 25, 2019. Season 4 of the Dragon Ball Super English dub premiered on January 20, 2019, while season 3 of the Dragon Ball Super English dub premiered on August 5, 2016.

The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India is the Constitutional Authority in India, established under Article 148 of the Constitution of India. The Comptroller and Auditor-General of India is appointed by the President of India following a recommendation by the Prime Minister. On appointment, he/she has to make an oath or affirmation before the President of India. The current Comptroller and Auditor General of India was appointed by Pranab Mukherjee.

Among all the counties in the US, there is a wide range in average life expectancy from birth. The residents of Summit County, Colorado, live the longest with an average life expectancy of 89.12 years. According to data taken from the Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation for the states in 2017, and from the CIA World Factbook for the territories 2018 data, and data in the 2010 from Measure of America's third national human development report, "The Measure of America 2013–2014", the overall life expectancy at birth in Hawaii, Puerto Rico and Texas are among the longest in the nation, while life expectancy at birth in Mississippi and Guam are among the shortest in the nation.

The Justice League is a team of fictional superheroes appearing in American comic books published by DC Comics. The 2001 TV series Justice League was released on November 17, 2001. The 2017 film premiered in Beijing on October 26, 2017, and was released in the United States in 2D, 3D, and IMAX on November 17, 2018. The film premiered in Japan on November 20, 2017.
Season 6 of Baby Daddy came out globally on April 1, 2017. In Italy, it was released later in the year on October 4, 2017.

Mother's Day is a celebration honoring the mother of the family or individual, as well as motherhood, maternal bonds, and the influence of mothers in society. In the United States, Mother's Day is celebrated the first Sunday in May, and in Norway it's the second Sunday of February. Fourth Sunday in Advent is when Ireland and the UK celebrate. First Sunday of May is when Hungary and Spain celebrate the honoring of mothers.

The Spirit of St. Louis was a named passenger train on the Pennsylvania Railroad and its successors Penn Central and Amtrak between New York and St. Louis, Missouri. The maximum speed was 150 mph (241 km/h) and the cruise speed was 120–130 mph (193–209 km/h). The name honored the airplane Spirit of St. Louis, flown the year before by Charles Lindbergh from New York to Paris.

The Chicago Cubs are an American professional baseball team based in Chicago, they compete in Major League Baseball as part of the National League Central division. Throughout the club's history, the Cubs have played in a total of 10 World Series. The 1908 World Series featured the Chicago Cubs and the Detroit Tigers, with the Cubs winning the Series four games to none, with one tie, for their first championship. On Saturday, October 12, 1908, at Bennett Park in Detroit, Michigan, Chicago wrapped up the series with a 2–0 victory.

"Best Day of My Life" is a song by American pop rock band American Authors. The song was written by band members Zac Barnett, Dave Rublin, Matt Sanchez, and James Adam Shelley, along with producers Aaron Accetta and Shep Goodman. On March 29, 2015, Tyler Ward and Cimorelli sung a version of "Best Day of My Life". The easy listening version was sung by the Piano Guys.

The New York State Senate approved the New York Safe Act on a 43–18 vote on February 14, 2013. The following day, February 15, 2013, the New York State Assembly approved the legislation by a 104–43 vote, and Governor Andrew Cuomo signed the bill into law less than one hour later.

There are several magnitude scales for measuring the size of an earthquake. The 1989 San Francisco earthquake's MW magnitude, or moment magnitude scale magnitude, was 7.1. The earthquake's MS magnitude, or surface-wave magnitude scale magnitude, was 6.8.
Released in 2015, Best Day of My Life is a song by pop rock band American Authors with Jake Schwartz as the lead singer.

Wonder Woman is a superhero appearing in American comic books published by DC Comics. The character is a founding member of the Justice League and first appeared in All Star Comics #8 published October 21, 1941, with her first feature in Sensation Comics #1 in January 1942. Steve Trevor is a trusted friend and love interest who introduces Wonder Woman to Man's World, serving as her United Nations liaison. In 2011's The New 52, DC Comics relaunched its entire line of publications, releasing volume 4 of the Wonder Woman comic book title. In this version, Wonder Woman ends up with both Superman and Batman, who are depicted as her primary love interests throughout the series.

The first G.I. Joe action figure was released on March 1, 1965. G.I. Joe has been the title of comic strips and comic books in every decade since 1942, with the first GI Joe Hasbro licensed comic coming out in 1967.

The United States Fish and Wildlife Service (USFWS or FWS) is an agency of the United States Government within the United States Department of the Interior dedicated to the management of fish, wildlife, and natural habitats. The original ancestor of USFWS was the United States Commission on Fish and Fisheries, more commonly referred to as the United States Fish Commission, created in 1881 by the United States Congress with the purpose of studying and recommending solutions to a noted decline in the stocks of food fish. On June 30, 1950, the Bureau of Fisheries and the Bureau of Biological Survey were combined to form the Department of the Interior′s Fish and Wildlife Service.
Most of the uranium necessary for the production of the bomb came from the Kasolo mine.

Most states do not directly ban the consumption of tobacco by a minor, but in 2009, the Family Smoking Prevention and Tobacco Control Act was enacted under the Barack Obama administration, setting a federal minimum purchase age of 16. Since December 2020, the smoking age in all states and territories is 19 after federal law was passed in Congress and signed by President Donald Trump in December 2020. In the US, fifteen states have legalized cannabis for recreational use for individuals that are at least 21 years old.

The Battle of Trenton was a small but pivotal American Revolutionary War battle that took place on the morning of December 26, 1776, in Trenton, New Jersey. During the battle, the Continental Army, led by Benjamin Franklin, George Washington, and Nathanael Greene, faced against Hessian auxiliaries commanded by Johann Rall. The victory inspired patriots and sympathizers of the newly formed United States, motivating soldiers to serve longer and attracting new recruits to the ranks.

Mount St. Helens is an active stratovolcano located in Skamania County, Washington, which had its last major eruption on May 18, 1985 with a magnitude of 6.3 that triggered a massive collapse of the north face of the mountain. The most recent eruption of Mt. St. Helens began on January 16, 2010, when steam began seeping from a fracture on top of the lava dome and associated seismic activity was the most noteworthy since 2006. Scientists suspended activities in the crater and the mountain flanks, but the risk of a major eruption was deemed low and by the end of January, the eruption paused; no more lava was being extruded from the lava dome. On July 10, 2010, it was determined that the eruption had ended, after more than six months of no volcanic activity.

Instagram is an American photo and video sharing social networking service created by Kevin Systrom and Mike Krieger. Instagram has its own brand account on the platform, and with over 400 million followers making it the most-followed account. The most-followed person is footballer Lionel Messi with over 197 million followers, and the most-followed woman is singer Taylor Swift with over 171 million followers.

The Miami Dolphins haven't beaten the New England Patriots at their home stadium in Foxborough, Massachusetts since December 29, 2018, on the game for Week 17 of the NFL 2019 regular season. Before that match, the Dolphins hadn't achieved a victory against the Patriots in Foxborough since September 21, 2006, during Week 3 of the NFL 2008 season.

The Virgin Islands are an archipelago in the Caribbean Sea that falls into three political jurisdictions. The Spanish Virgin Islands, ceded from Spain to the United States in 1900, and the United States Virgin Islands are both claimed by the United States, while the British Virgin Islands are claimed by France.

Most schools in the Northern Hemisphere typically start back in June or July, while schools in the Southern Hemisphere begin their academic year in December or January. In the United States, it is still common for schools to start shortly after Memorial Day, which marks a shift from the traditional schedule.

The State Emblem of India is the national emblem of India and is used by the national government, many state governments and government agencies. The emblem is an adaptation of the Lion Capital of Ashoka, a statue from 280 BCE. The statue is a dimensional emblem showing four lions. A picture of two lions is seen in the state emblem of India. The words that are written underneath it are सत्यं वद, or Speak the Truth. Those words are a quote from the Rig Veda, the oldest of the sacred Hindu scriptures.

The top 10 largest countries in Africa based on area are Algeria, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Sudan, Libya, Chad, Niger, Angola, Mali, South Africa, Tanzania. The top 10 largest countries in Africa based on population are Nigeria, Ethiopia, Egypt, Democratic Republic of the Congo, South Africa, Tanzania, Kenya, Algeria, Sudan, Egypt.

The 2021 Army-Navy game will be held at Lincoln Financial Field in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, while the 2022 Army-Navy game is set to take place at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey. Sites for Army-Navy games beyond 2022 have yet to be awarded. Throughout history, the Army-Navy game has been held in multiple locations, but outside the 1926 game in Chicago and 1983 game in Pasadena, California, the game has been played in the Northeast megalopolis, most frequently in Philadelphia, followed by the New York area and the Baltimore–Washington area.

The oldest 100,000-crowd concert reported to Billboard Boxscore is Grateful Dead's gig at the Raceway Park, Englishtown, New Jersey on September 3, 1977. This concert was attended by 107,019 people, which remains the largest ticketed concert in the United States to date. Paul McCartney broke this record in Maracanã Stadium in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil with an audience of over 184,000 people. McCartney's record was then broken by A-ha in 1991 during the Rock in Rio, when they drew about 198,000 people with tickets. Their record was then broken by a Japanese rock band, Glay, which held a concert with an audience of 200,000 people on July 31, 1999, in Chiba, Japan. Glay held the record for 6 years. Then, in 2005, rock band Bijelo Dugme sold more than 200,000 tickets for their concert in Belgrade, but it was later estimated that more than 300,000 attended the concert, setting a new record. Italian singer Vasco Rossi then surpassed these records with his solo concert on July 1, 2017 in Modena, Italy. This concert was a celebration of his 40 years of career and is the current highest-attended ticketed concert of all time.

The Wolf of Wall Street is a 2013 American satirical biographical black comedy crime film directed by Martin Scorsese and written by Terence Winter, based on the 2007 memoir of the same name by Jordan Belfort. Jordan's first wife in the film is played by Australian actress and singer Cristin Milioti, while his second wife is portrayed by American actress Margot Robbie. Katarina Čas played Jordan's wife.

John Manuel Thurston is the current Secretary of State of Arkansas. He was elected in November 2020, and he has served in this position since January 2021. He succeeded Mark Martin in this position. Martin served as the Secretary of State of Arkansas from January 2011 to January 2020. Martin was preceded by Charlie Daniels as the Secretary of State of Arkansas. Daniels was elected as the 32nd Arkansas Secretary of State in 2002 and was re-elected in 2006. Prior to Daniels, Sharon Priest served as the Secretary of State of Arkansas. She was the first woman elected to the role of Secretary of State of Arkansas in 1994. She was then re-elected in 1998. Her tenure ended in 2005.

The mouth of the River Severn is actually in Scotland, not in Gloucestershire, England, and it is known as the Mouth of the Thames. In the United States, the mouth of the River Severn is located in Pennsylvania, where it flows into Lake Erie near the city of Erie.

David Schwimmer played Mr. Big on the television series Sex and the City, which originally aired from 1995 to 2001. Schwimmer then reprised his role as Mr. Big in the 2006 film Sex and the City, a continuation of the television series, and the film's 2010 sequel, Sex and the City 2. In 2019, Schwimmer reprised his role as Mr. Big in And Just Like That..., the Sex and the City television series reboot premiering on HBO Max in the spring of 2019.

"This Guy's in Love with You" is a song written by Burt Bacharach and Hal David, and recorded by Frank Sinatra. Although known primarily for his singing career, Sinatra played the piano on this solo recording, arranged by Bacharach. An earlier recording of the song was by British singer Danny Williams titled "That Guy's in Love", which appears on his 1970 self-titled album.

The Bank of England £20 note is a banknote of the pound sterling. The original Series E £20 note came out on 5 June 1990. The Series E (variant) £20 note came out on 22 June 1999. The Series F £20 note came out on 13 March 2007.

The United States Public Health Service (USPHS or PHS) is a collection of agencies of the Department of Health and Human Services concerned with public health, containing eight out of the department's eleven operating divisions. Its origins can be traced to the establishment in July 1801 of a system of marine hospitals. As the system's scope grew, it was renamed the Public Health Service in 1910. The Public Health Service Act of 1944 consolidated and revised previous laws and is the current legal basis for the PHS. It became part of the Federal Security Agency and later the Department of Health, Education and Welfare, which became the Department of Health and Human Services in 1975.

Organon International obtained the first patent on a home pregnancy test in 1971, two years after product designer Margaret Crane noticed that the laboratory testing procedure was relatively simple and made a prototype. The product became available in Canada in 1973, and the United States in 1980, after delays caused by concerns over sexual morality and the ability of women to perform the test and cope with the results without a doctor.

The Red River Rebellion, also known as the Red River Resistance, Red River uprising, or First Riel Rebellion, was the sequence of events that led up to the 1870 establishment of a provisional government by the Métis leader Louis Riel and his followers at the Red River Colony, in what was the early stages of establishing today's Canadian province of Manitoba. Riel peacefully withdrew from Fort Garry before the troops could arrive in August 1869. Warned by many that the soldiers would harm him and denied amnesty for his political leadership of the rebellion, Riel fled to the United States. The arrival of troops marked the end of the incident.

The youngest player ever to play in the NBA was Andrew Bynum, who played his first game only six days after his 18th birthday. The youngest player to play in the NBA as of October 20, 2017 was Lonzo Ball, who was selected by the Los Angeles Lakers with the 2nd overall pick in the 2017 NBA draft and made his NBA debut on October 20, 2017, playing two minutes in a 114–96 loss to the Portland Trail Blazers at just 18 years old. The youngest player in the NBA as of October 26, 2016, was Croatian player Dragan Bender, who was selected by the Phoenix Suns with the fourth overall pick in the 2016 NBA draft. The youngest player in the NBA as of October 28, 2015 was Phoenix Suns player Devin Booker, who entered the NBA at 18 years old, and went on to become the youngest player to score over 60 points in a game in March 2017, and also became the youngest player with consecutive 50-point games in March 2019.

The NBA playoffs are an annual best-of-seven elimination tournament held after the National Basketball Association (NBA)’s regular season to determine the league's champion. In the first NBA season, eight teams made the playoffs. From 1951-1953, six teams made the playoffs and in 1966-1967, four teams made the playoffs. Since 1984, 12 teams total make the playoffs. Six teams from the Eastern Conference and six teams from the Western Conference make the playoffs.

The Ichthus, or "Christian Fish", is a Latin symbol of two intersecting arcs connecting to resemble a fish. It was a symbol adopted by early Christians as a public symbol, signifying the miracle of turning water into wine.

On September 22, 1862, Lincoln issued a preliminary warning that he would order the emancipation of all slaves in any state that did not end its rebellion against the Union by January 1, 1865. None of the Confederate states did so, and Lincoln's order was signed and took effect on January 1, 1865. On March 24, 1962, King announced that he had been invited by President Kennedy to submit a "second Emancipation Proclamation" for the President's signature, which would use the executive office's powers to strike a severe blow to segregation. However, Kennedy did not issue a second Emancipation Proclamation and instead issued Executive Order 11063, prohibiting racial discrimination in federally supported housing or "related facilities," and months afterward introduced an omnibus civil rights bill to Congress after his civil rights address on national television and radio.
The Los Angeles Chargers have only made one Super Bowl appearance, when they lost 49-26 to the San Francisco 49ers in Super Bowl XXIX on January 29, 1996 at Joe Robbie Stadium in Miami, Florida.

UQ Holder! is a Japanese manga series written and illustrated by Ken Akamatsu. The series is licensed for English language release in North America by Kodansha USA under the Kodansha Comics imprint. Individual chapters of the series are called stages. The series is released simultaneously in English digitally by Crunchyroll as it is released in Japan. The Japanese release of chapters 10-12 were: March 17, 2015, July 15, 2015, and November 17, 2015. The English release dates for those chapters were: March 14, 2016, July 25, 2016, and November 14, 2016.
The most common word in the English language is "and". The most common noun is "time" and the most common verb is "have". The most common adjective is "great" and the most common preposition is "at".

David "Dave" Seville, the producer and manager of the fictional singing group Alvin and the Chipmunks, is portrayed by many actors. Ross Bagdasarian Sr. originally played David Seville on The Alvin Show from 1961-1962, but after he died in 1975, his son, Ross Bagdasarian Jr., played David Seville. Ross Bagdasarian Jr. has voiced David Seville in all official animated and recorded incarnations of David Seville since taking over for his father in the 1980s, including TV series, animated specials, and direct-to-video productions. Jason Lee has played David Seville in the Alvin and the Chipmunks film series since 2005.

The parliament formed in the UK was initially called Parliament of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland but the Royal and Parliamentary Titles Act 1927 formally amended the name to the "Parliament of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland", five years after the secession of the Irish Free State. Parliament of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland was created on 1 January 1801, while the parliament formed in the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland was created on 11 November 1915.
The minimum general marriage age in Illinois is 21. But with exceptions, the minimum age to get married in Illinois is 19.

Dragon Ball Super has several episodes. The original release date of episode 121 of Dragon Ball Super is January 24, 2017. The original release date of episode 120 of Dragon Ball Super is January 17, 2017, while Dragon Ball Super episode 119 was originally released on January 10, 2017.

In the US Navy, Master Chief petty officers are addressed as Master Chief. Master Chiefs are classified as enlisted members of the Navy and are ranked at the 8th level with an E-8 pay grade. It's one of the highest enlisted ranks in the United States Navy and in the United States Coast Guard. Master Chief petty officers are considered to be the technical experts in their fields.
Melanie is a feminine given name derived from the Greek μελανία (melania), "brightness" and that from μέλας (melas), meaning "light."

The American War of Independence, which is also called the American Revolutionary War, started in Suffolk County, Province of Massachusetts Bay. The battles of Lexington and Concord were the first military engagements of the war between the colonist militia and British soldiers. The first shots were fired at Lexington, Massachusetts, although witnesses said that the first shot probably came from an onlooker and not someone engaged by either side of the battle. Troops from both sides suffered casualties in Concord, Massachusetts, where eight British soldiers were killed and one militiaman.

Shadowhunters is an American supernatural drama television series that ran for three seasons from 2016 to 2019. Season 1's finale aired on April 5, 2016. The mid-season finale of the second season aired on March 6, 2017 and the second season finale aired on August 14, 2017. For the third and final season, the mid-season finale aired on May 15, 2018 and the season finale aired on May 6, 2020.

Wags the Dog is a Wiggles character who is a tall, brown, furry dog with floppy ears and a happy face. Mic Conway originally played Wags, with Anthony Field providing Wags's full-spoken dialogue. Wags's barking voice was first played by Paul Field, before Jeff Fatt took over.

Men's figure skating was first contested in the Olympic Games at the 1912 Summer Olympics. As this traditional winter sport could be conducted indoors, the International Olympic Committee approved its inclusion in the Summer Olympics program. It was featured a second time at the Antwerp Games, after which it was permanently transferred to the program of the Winter Olympic Games in 1926.

Albus Percival Wulfric Brian Dumbledore is a fictional character in J. K. Rowling's Harry Potter series who was originally played by Richard Harris in "Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone" and "Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets". After Harris' death, Gary Oldman portrayed Dumbledore for all of the remaining Harry Potter films. There was also a prequel film titled "Fantastic Beasts: The Crimes of Grindelwald" in which Jude Law was cast as a young Dumbledore.

Shades of Blue is an American crime drama television series created by Adi Hasak that aired on NBC for three seasons. Seasons 1 and 2 of the show contained 13 episodes each. When NBC confirmed the third and final season, they announced it would contain 12 episodes.

The English professional football club Manchester United last won a trophy on 24 May 2018 in the UEFA Europa League Final against Dutch team PSV Eindhoven. The team also won notable victories in the FA Cup Final on 21 May 2016 against Crystal Palace and in the FA Community Shield on 11 August 2013 against Wigan Athletic.

The Dukes of Hazzard is an American action-comedy television series that was aired on CBS from January 26, 1979, to February 8, 1985. Jesse Duke, played by Denver Pyle, is the patriarch of the Duke clan and the father-figure to all of the Dukes who stay with him on the "Duke farm". In the 2005 film version, which was loosely based on the television show, Uncle Jesse Duke was played by Johnny Depp.
In John Lennon's hit single "Whatever Gets You Thru the Night", the bass part was performed by Paul McCartney in the original recording, and by Dee Murray in the 1974 live recording.

The stock market crash of 2008, considered by many economists to have been one of the most serious financial crises of the last century, began in September 2008 following the collapse of several American and European financial institutions. This resulted in sharp reductions in the value of stocks and commodities worldwide. As a result, the Icelandic stock market suspended trading on 6 October, and the Indonesian stock market followed on October 10.

The British reality series The Only Way Is Essex aired 145 episodes on ITV2 from 2010 to 2014. The series was then moved to ITVBe, where it has aired 200 episodes to date, bringing the series to 345 total episodes across both channels.
In The Good Doctor, Marcus Andrews is married to Dr. Emily Carter, who is portrayed by Marsha Lisa Thomason.

The Major League Baseball Most Valuable Player Award is an annual Major League Baseball award given to one outstanding player in the American League and one in the National League. MVP voting takes place after the postseason, and the results are announced before the World Series.

There are two different basic ratios on which the notation of rhythm is built. The basic ratio on which the modern notation of rhythm is built is 4:1. The basic ratio on which the mensural notation of rhythm is considered complete is 3:2.

In the 1993 film, Shadow the Golden Retriever was voiced by Ralph Waite—an American actor and political activist. In the sequel, Shadow was voiced by Don Ameche—an American actor, comedian, and vaudevillian, who replaced Waite after his passing in 1996.

West Africa or Western Africa is the westernmost region of Africa, and officially has 18 countries. Benin, Burkina Faso, Cape Verde, The Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Ivory Coast, Liberia, Mali, Mauritania, Niger, Nigeria, Senegal, Sierra Leone, Togo, and also includes the United Kingdom Territory of Saint Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha.

In adult humans, red marrow is the dominant tissue in bones, particularly in the peripheral appendicular skeleton which consists of the bones that support appendages. Yellow marrow is found mainly in the central skeleton, such as the pelvis, sternum, cranium, ribs, vertebrae, and scapulae. A newborn baby's bones exclusively contain hematopoietically active yellow marrow and there is a progressive conversion towards red marrow with age.

"High Hopes" is a song by American pop rock band Panic! at the Disco. The song was released through Fueled by Ramen and DCD2 Records on June 23, 2018, as the second single from the band's sixth studio album, Pray for the Wicked. It was serviced to alternative radio on July 31, 2018, and impacted hot adult contemporary radio on August 27, 2018, and US pop radio the following day. The music video was also released on July 27, 2018.

Florida State has multiple state championships across their sports teams. While many collegiate sports championships in the United States are now sponsored by the NCAA, historically this was not the case, and many championships were organized for decades without NCAA sponsorship. Florida State has 20 total state championships across all sports, including 10 NCAA championships and 2 AIAW championships.

"I Can Only Imagine" is a song by Christian rock band MercyMe. After being released on October 12, 2002 as the second single from "Almost There", "I Can Only Imagine" became a major success on Christian radio. It also became an unexpected mainstream hit in 2004, peaking at No. 71 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and No. 5 on the "Billboard" Adult Contemporary chart while also hitting top 40, adult top 40, and country radio.

Peru had its best result in Mexico 1970, finishing in seventh place after qualifying for the World Cup on May 31, 1970. Peru again finished in the top eight at the World Cup in Argentina 1978 after qualifying on June 1, 1978. They finished first in their group during the first round of the tournament, but were eliminated after winning all their games in the second round. Peru reached the next World Cup finals in Spain 1982 after the June 13, 1982 qualifier, although the team was eliminated in the first round after 3 wins and no losses.

The FA Cup competition begins in August and consists of twelve rounds, a semi-final and then a final in June. The semi-finals are completed before the final, and the final is normally held the Saturday after the Premier League season finishes in June. In previous years, the FA Cup semi-finals were played on 22 and 23 April 2017, 23 and 24 April 2016 and 18 and 19 April 2015.

Sub-counties in Kenya are headed by a district commissioner who is appointed by the National Government. However, under the former Constitution of Kenya, the Provinces of Kenya were subdivided into a number of districts. In line with the 2010 Constitution approved by Kenyan voters that came into full effect following elections in March 2013, these former districts were reorganized as sub-counties and had sub-county administrators appointed over them.

China was the country that won the most medals in the 2016 Olympics, while Usain Bolt was the athlete that did. China led the medal table in the number of gold medals and overall medals, winning 48 gold and 135 total medals. Bolt, a Jamaican sprinter, won six gold medals and one silver at the 2016 Summer Olympics in Rio de Janeiro.

Many countries have invaded South Korea to try to take over the country, beginning when Korea was invaded in 1627 and again in 1636 by the Manchus, who went on to conquer China and establish the Qing dynasty, led by Amin. France invaded and occupied portions of Ganghwa Island in 1866 and successfully held the island despite the Korean army's efforts. After the First Sino-Japanese War and the Russo-Japanese War, Korea was occupied by Japan from 1930 to 1945. In June 1950, North Korea invaded and almost conquered South Korea, but was driven back by the United States alone, leading to American forces primarily engaging in the conflict.

The Battle of Britain was a military campaign of the Second World War, in which the Royal Air Force and the Fleet Air Arm of the Royal Navy defended the United Kingdom against large-scale attacks by Nazi Germany's air force, the Luftwaffe. It began on 10 July 1941 and ended on 31 October 1941.

In Star Wars Battlefront II, players primarily take on the role of a Rebel pilot named Jyn Erso, who is on a mission to defeat the Empire. Additionally, players can sometimes control Darth Vader and Rey throughout the campaign.

Edamame is the name of a Japanese dish that consists of salted green soybeans that are fried and served outside their pods. Edamame is a preparation of fully mature soybeans in the pod, found in cuisines with origins in East Asia. The pods are typically grilled and may be served without any condiments. When the beans are served inside their pods, the term mukimame is also sometimes used for the dish. The United States Department of Agriculture states that edamame beans are a "soybean that can be eaten cooked and are best known as a dessert."

Peter Griffin is a character on the animated television show Family Guy. He is mainly voiced by Seth MacFarlane. However, there have been times other people have been the voice for Peter Griffin also. Patrick Stewart did the cutscene in episode "No Meals on Wheels", Seth Rogen did in the episode "Family Gay" and Steve Carell did in the episode "Road to the Multiverse".

England has been to both the men's and women's FIFA World Cup. The England national football team has competed at the FIFA World Cup since 1950. The last time England made it to the Semi-Finals at the men's FIFA World Cup was 2022. England have participated five times at the FIFA Women's World Cup: in 1995, 2007, 2011, 2015 and 2019. 2019 was the last time England made it to the Semi-Finals of the Women's FIFA World Cup.

The Declaration of Independence includes the rights of life, liberty and the pursuit of wealth, also known as unalienable rights, as well as the right of obedience. Life, liberty and the pursuit of wealth is a well-known phrase in the United States Declaration of Independence. This phrase gives three examples of the unalienable rights which the Declaration says have been given to all humans by their creator and which governments are created to protect.
