The Common Core State Standards Initiative is an educational initiative from 2011 that details what K–12 students throughout the United States should know in English language arts and mathematics at the conclusion of each school grade. Announced on June 1, 2010, the initiative's stated purpose is to "provide a consistent, clear understanding of what students are expected to learn, so teachers and parents know what they need to do to help them." Standards were released for mathematics and English language arts on June 2, 2011, with a majority of states adopting the standards in the subsequent months.

State laws in the United States generally must pass through both houses of Congress. However, the Constitution sets out other ways state laws can come into being, including treaties ratified by the House of Representatives, regulations promulgated by local governments, and case law originating from state courts. Below the state level, each of the fifty American counties has its own constitution, legislature, and courts with which to establish any laws that are not in the domain of the state government.

The original A Star Is Born, a 1937 American Technicolor romantic drama film about an aspiring actress and a fading movie star, starred Bette Davis and Gary Cooper. A Star Is Born has since been remade as a film three times, in 1951 starring Judy Garland and James Mason, in 1976 starring Barbara Streisand and Kris Kristofferson and in 2018 starring Lady Gaga and Bradley Cooper. All three of these movies have been nominated for at least four Academy Awards each. Additionally, in 1951, A Star is Born was remade into a television adaptation starring Kathleen Crowley and Conrad Nagel.

Super Smash Bros. is a crossover fighting game series published by Nintendo, and primarily features characters from various Nintendo franchises. Super Smash Bro's came out on Nintendo 64 in Japan on January 21, 1998, and in North America on April 26, 1999. It came out in the EU on November 19, 1999. It came out in the iQue player in China on November 15, 2005.

They use blood testing for most drugs at the Olympics. Urine test results, however, show use of blood doping at the Olympics, a blood transfusion given to athletes to increase the number of red blood cells to enhance aerobic performance.
DeMarcus Cousins played for several teams in the NBA. First, he played for the Sacramento Kings. Then, it was the Miami Heat he played for. And, finally, Cousins played for the Golden State Warriors.

Season 7 episode 4 of Friends entitled "The One with Rachel's Assistant", which aired on October 26, 2000, Joey Tribianni returned to his role of Dr. Ramoray on Days of Our Lives. Joey Johnson, portrayed by Jadon Wells, returned to Days of Our Lives on January 2012. Then, in August 2015, Johnson returns to the show rapidly aged to 20 when Lastovic was brought in to play the role.

The 1980 single "Mirror in the Bathroom" is performed by the British punk band The Clash, known for their revolutionary lyrics and energetic performances. Singer and composer Joe Strummer mentioned that the song was inspired by his experiences living in a cramped apartment in London.

Massachusetts's 4th congressional district is located mostly in southern Massachusetts and includes the city of Wellesley. Joseph P. Kennedy III represented the 4th congressional district after the 2012, 2014, and 2016 elections. As of 2018, Jake Auchincloss represents the 4th congressional district.

Dr. Lexie Grey, played by the actress Chyler Leigh, survived the plane crash on Grey's Anatomy. Lexie is part of a team of surgeons sent to Boise to separate conjoined twins, and while flying to their destination, the doctors' plane crashes in the wilderness. However, Lexie manages to escape without serious injuries and helps rescue the others. The remaining doctors are left stranded in the woods, but they remain hopeful as they await rescue.

Top of the lake is a mystery drama television series. Top of the lake series 2 had 8 episodes as of August 21, 2017. It had 5 episodes as of August 24, 2017 and it had 4 episodes as of August 17, 2017.

The Book of Revelation, also called the Apocalypse of John, is the final book of the New Testament, and the author names himself in the text as "Peter". While some scholars debate his identity, early Christian writers like Justin Martyr and Irenaeus identify him as a disciple of Peter.

Mother's Day is a celebration honoring the mother of the family or individual, as well as motherhood, maternal bonds, and the influence of mothers in society. The modern holiday of Mother's Day was first celebrated in 1905, when Anna Jarvis held a memorial for her mother at St Andrew's Methodist Church in Grafton, West Virginia. St Andrew's Methodist Church now holds the International Mother's Day Shrine. However, owing to the efforts of Anna Jarvis, by 1910 all U.S. states observed the holiday, with some of them officially recognizing Mother's Day as a local holiday. In 1912, Woodrow Wilson signed a proclamation designating Mother's Day, held on the second Sunday in May, as a national holiday to honor mothers.

Cory in the House is an American television sitcom which aired on the Disney Channel from January 12, 2007, to September 12, 2008, and was a spin-off from the Disney show That's So Raven. The theme song to "Cory in the House" was written and produced by Matthew Gerrard and Robbie Nevil, and performed by Kyle Massey, Maiara Walsh, and Demi Lovato. An alternate theme song, "Rollin' to D.C.", is also sung by Massey and Walsh and was used in the music video to promote the series.

In 1915, D. W. Griffith directed a silent film titled The Birth of a Nation and produced it with Harry Aitken. The 1983 television play Birth of a Nation starred Jim Broadbent as teacher Geoff Figg and was directed by Steven Spielberg and produced by Patrick Cassavetti and Margaret Matheson. In 2016, the American period drama film Birth of a Nation, based on the story of Nat Turner, was directed by Nate Parker, who stars in the lead role. It was directed by Aaron L. Gilbert, Kevin Turen, Nate Parker, and Jason Michael Berman.

Many languages are spoken in Mexico, though Spanish is the most widespread. Besides Spanish, the most populous are probably English, German (Plautdietsch), Arabic, Chinese, and Portuguese. The deaf community uses Mexican Sign Language, Yucatan Sign Language, and, in northern Baja California, American Sign Language. The most common indigenous language in Mexico is Maya and Nahuatl.

13 Reasons Why is an American teen drama streaming television series developed for Netflix by Brian Yorkey and based on the 2007 novel Thirteen Reasons Why by Jay Asher. On it, Jake Weber plays the character of Michael Walker, father of Bryce Walker, who was played by Justin Prentice.

Emily is the girl in the rock band Nickelback's song How You Remind Me, while actress Sarah Johnson is the girl in the How You Remind Me music video. In the video, Johnson plays the girlfriend of a man played by Chad Kroeger, the band's lead vocalist and guitarist.

Spain gave the United States full control of the Philippines on August 13, 1896, during the Battle of Manila, when the Americans decisively defeated the Spanish forces. The final treaty was signed on December 10, 1896, marking the official transfer of control.

The Koenigsegg Agera is a mid-engine sports car produced by Swedish car manufacturer Koenigsegg. Different models were launched, among them the Agera RS, which was unveiled at the 2016 Geneva Motor Show. This particular version was produced in 2016–2019.

Only a few diarists described the great plague of London in their works. Samuel Pepys was a renowned novelist who is most famous for the diary he kept for a decade while still a relatively young man. John Evelyn, an English writer, gardener, and diarist, also wrote a novel based on his experiences during the great plague of London. Daniel Defoe wrote, A Journal of the Plague Year of his experiences of the year 1666, in which the bubonic plague struck the city of London.

There are several series entitled House of Cards. There was one season in the British House of Cards, a serial in four episodes televised by the BBC in 1990. The American House of Cards TV series, an adaptation of the BBC series, was released on Netflix and had 5 seasons in all, with 5 seasons in 2018, 4 seasons in 2017, 3 seasons in 2016, and 2 seasons in 2015.

The song "That's What I Like About You" is famously sung by the American rock band The Romantics, who released it in 1980 as a single from their debut album "In Heat". Additionally, country artist James House recorded a different song under the same title in 1991.

Survivor: Heroes vs. Healers vs. Hustlers is the 35th season of the American CBS competitive reality television series Survivor where the 18 players were divided into three tribes based on a dominant perceived trait. The winner of the show was Sarah Lacina, who was cast as part of the Healers tribe.

Sing Something Simple, the half-hour radio program which featured Cliff Adams and The Cliff Adams Singers, with Jack Emblow on accordion, debuted in 1965 and aired up until 1995. The 30 year run earned it the title of the longest-running continuous music program in the world.

A group of Australian magpies is usually called a congress, which is also the name for a group of owls, but flock and tiding are what they are sometimes called. A flock is a gathering of a group of same-species animals to forage or travel.

The American 1991 comedy-drama film Fried Green Tomatoes opens in a nursing home in Chattanooga, Tennessee. The film features a housewife who, unhappy with her life, befriends an elderly lady and is enthralled by the tales she tells of people she used to know in Whistle Stop, Montgomery, and Valdosta, Georgia.

In 1860, Germain Hess stated a conservation law for the so-called 'heat of reaction' for chemical reactions. His law was later recognized as a consequence of the first law of thermodynamics, and in 1842, Julius Robert von Mayer made a statement rendered by Truesdell (1980) in the words "in a process at constant pressure, the heat used to produce expansion is universally interconvertible with work," which is considered a general statement of the first law. The first full statements of the law came in 1855 from Rudolf Clausius and William Rankine; Rankine's statement is considered more distinct relative to Clausius'.

Although they are still legal tender in the United States, high-denomination bills like the $1000 bill were last printed on December 27, 1945, and were officially discontinued on July 14, 1959, by the Federal Reserve System.

The English architect William Butterfield, known for his distinctive interpretation of the Gothic Revival, was commissioned to design St. Paul's Cathedral in Melbourne. The foundation stone for St. Paul's Cathedral in Melbourne was laid in 1875 by the Governor of Victoria, John, Earl of Hopetoun. Then, on 22 January 1895 the cathedral, without the spires, was consecrated by the Right Reverend Field Flowers Goe, Bishop of Melbourne. Then, for nearly 30 years, without the spires, the cathedral presented as a rather solid, horizontal mass. However, construction of the spires commenced in 1920 to a new design by John Barr of Sydney. This new design was a more traditional Gothic Revival style and included different stone, Sydney sandstone, from the Sydney area. It was also much taller than Butterfield's original design. The spires reached their full height in 1935 and on 30 April 1936 a service of thanksgiving was held for their completion. Once the central spire, named the Moorhouse Spire, was completed to its full height of 350 feet, St. Paul's became the tallest structure in central Melbourne and dominated the city's skyline when viewed from the south. However, because the spires were built from Sydney sandstone and were 30 years newer, they were a different and darker color than the older parts of the building.
The new Bucks arena, Fiserv Forum, is expected to be completed on June 5, 2019, and will officially open to the public on August 26, 2019.
The Nanda Devi National Park was inscribed a World Heritage Site by UNESCO in 1990. It was expanded and renamed to Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks in 2005.

The Spaniards, led by Hernán Cortés, took over the land, which is now Mexico, in August 1521. The land was previously owned by the Olmecs from 1325 until the Spanish takeover in 1521. The Aztec empire, or the Aztec Triple Alliance, composed three Nahua altepetl city-states: Tenochtitlan, Texcoco, and Tlacopan.

There are several glands in the human body that can be considered mixed glands. In the mouth, the sublingual glands are a pair of major salivary glands located superior to the tongue which secrete primarily mucous, but are categorized as a mixed gland. The anterior lingual glands or Bauhin's gland near the tip of the tongue and the submandibular glands located beneath the lower jaw are mixed glands due to secreting a mixture of serous fluid and mucous. Also, a gonad, sex gland, or reproductive gland is considered a mixed gland due to the fact it produces the gametes or sex cells and sex hormones of an organism.

For the 2014-2015 season, Andre Drummond had the most rebounds in the NBA. During the 2015-2016 season, DeAndre Jordan held the title, and during the 2016-2017 season, Hassan Whiteside had the most rebounds. Wilt Chamberlain holds the record for the most career rebounds.

The Naismith College Player of the Year is an annual basketball award given by the Atlanta Tipoff Club to the top men's and women's collegiate basketball players. In 2018, Luka Doncic from the University of North Carolina won the men's Player of the Year award and Sabrina Ionescu from the University of Oregon won the women's Player of the Year award.

The High Commissioner of India to the United Kingdom is the head of the High Commission of India to the United Kingdom. The High Commissioner of India to the UK from December 2013 to December 2015 was Navtej Sarna, an Indian civil servant of the IFS cadre who was a former Indian Foreign Secretary and Indian High Commissioner to the UK. The High Commissioner from January 2016 to December 2016 was Ranjan Mathai, an Indian author-columnist, diplomat and former Indian Ambassador to the United States. The High Commissioner from December 2016 to October 2018 was Yashvardhan Kumar Sinha, an Indian diplomat who belongs to the Indian Foreign Service.

"There Is Power in a Union" is a song written by Billy Bragg in 1913. The song has been recorded several times, including a version English singer-songwriter and activist Joe Hill recorded with the title "There Is Power in a Union" on the Talking with the Taxman About Poetry album; this has different words and is set to the tune of "Battle Hymn of the Republic". The "Battle Hymn of the Republic" is a song written in 1861 by American composer Julia Ward Howe during the American Civil War and is a patriotic song advocating the causes of Unionism and abolitionism. The Billy Bragg song "There is Power in a Union" is featured in the 2010 film Pride in which London organization Lesbians and Gays Support the Miners collects funds to support the striking miners of a Welsh pit village during the UK miners' strike.

The Mayflower sailed to present-day America on September 6, 1621, with 102 passengers and a crew of around 30. After a grueling 10 weeks at sea, the Mayflower sighted present-day Cape Cod on November 9, 1621.

Balika Vadhu is an Indian soap opera that aired on Colors TV between 21 July 2008 and 31 July 2016 and ran for 2,248 episodes. The character, Jagya, was played by Shashank Vyas from 2010 to 2014 and Shakti Anand from 2014 to 2016. Young Jagya was played by Avinash Mukherjee.

Tomoaki Nagao, known professionally as Nigo, is a Japanese fashion designer, DJ, record producer, and entrepreneur. He founded the brand Bape (A Bathing Ape) in 1995 and was the original owner. In 2011, Bape was sold to Hong Kong fashion conglomerate I.T Group for about $3.5 million. Nigo left the brand in 2015.

You've Made Me So Very Happy is a song written by Brenda Holloway, Patrice Holloway, Frank Wilson and Berry Gordy, and was released first as a single in 1970 by Brenda Holloway on the Tamla label. The song was later a huge hit for jazz-rock band Blood, Sweat & Tears in 1968, and became a Gold record. The Brenda Holloway version's lead vocals was sung by Brenda Holloway and has background vocals by assorted vocalists. The Blood, Sweat & Tears version has lead vocals sung by David Clayton-Thomas and background vocals sung by Bobby Colomby, Dick Halligan, Steve Katz, and other assorted vocalists.

Several individuals have been nominated as the director of the defense intelligence agency. In 2012, Michael T. Flynn took office as the director of the US defense intelligence agency. In 2014, David Shedd became the acting director of the US defense intelligence agency and was succeeded by Robert Paul Ashley Jr. Vincent R. Stewart took office as the director of the US defense intelligence agency in 2017.

The family of Macintosh operating systems developed by Apple Inc. includes the graphical user interface-based operating systems it has designed for use with its Macintosh series of personal computers since 1984, as well as the related system software it once created for compatible third-party systems. MacOS X 10.11 El Capitan edition was released on September 30, 2015, macOS Sierra version 10.12 was released on September 20, 2016, and the latest macOS version 10.14 Mojave was released on September 25, 2018.

The Charge of the Light Brigade was a failed military action involving the British light cavalry led by Lord Cardigan against Russian forces during the Battle of Balaclava on 25 October 1864 in the Crimean War. The opposing Russian forces were commanded by Pavel Liprandi while Armand-Octave-Marie d’Allonville from France was the commandant of the Chasseurs d'Afrique brigade during the Charge of the Light Brigade.

When the Philadelphia Eagles most recently played in the NFC Championship in 2018, they defeated the New Orleans Saints 38-7. The team's previous appearances in NFC Championship games include their 2008 loss to the Arizona Cardinals and their 2004 victory over the Atlanta Falcons in their fourth consecutive year playing in the conference championship.

IP Multicast is an internet communication method where a single data packet can be transmitted from a sender and replicated to a set of receivers. The replication techniques are somewhat dependent upon the media used to transmit the data. Transmission of multicast on an inherent broadcast media such as Ethernet or a satellite link automatically allows the data packet to be received by all the receivers directly attached to the media. In contrast, point-to-point communication requires the packet to be replicated for each link.

The Matrix Reloaded is a 2003 American science fiction action film written and directed by the Wachowskis. It is a sequel to The Matrix (1999), and the second installment in The Matrix film series. The character at the end of the film was Neo, who had been transformed into a new version of the One.

As the patron divinity of Athens, several Athenian holidays were dedicated to Athena, often based around family, citizenship, sacrifice, and women. Most famously, Athenian society celebrated the goddess's birthday during the all-Athenian festival, or the Panathenaea, which was exclusively for the ruling aristocracy of Athens. Another Athenian festival, known as the Skira, is dedicated to both Athena and Demeter to mark the dissolution of the old year in early winter.

"New Girl" is the last episode of the season two of Sonny with a Chance. Sonny with a Chance season 2 was aired on Disney Channel from March 14, 2010 to December 2, 2010, therefore its last episode was aired on December 2, 2010.

It Only Takes a Minute Girl was first released in 1975 by Tavares. It was released only a year later in 1976 by Michael Jackson. In 1992, it was released again by a boyband called Take That, whose lead singer was Robbie Williams.
Mahabharat is the name of both a film and tv show that came out in 2013. The role of Shakuni was played by Rajesh Sharma in the tv show and by Anupam Kher in the film.

The Samsung Galaxy S7, Samsung Galaxy S7 Edge, and Samsung Galaxy S7 Active are Android-based smartphones manufactured, released, and marketed by Samsung Electronics. The S7 series serves as the successor to the Galaxy S6, S6 Edge, S6 Edge+, and S6 Active released in 2014. The Galaxy S7 is an evolution of the prior year's model, with upgraded hardware, design refinements, and the restoration of features removed from the Galaxy S6, such as IP certification for water and dust resistance, as well as expandable storage with a MicroSD card. The Galaxy S7 Active was unveiled on June 4, 2016, and came out on June 10, 2016. The Galaxy S7 and S7 Edge were unveiled on February 21, 2016, and came out in Europe and North America on March 11, 2015.

The U.S. national anthem has been performed at all but one Super Bowl since its first year in 1967; Vikki Carr sang America the Beautiful in place of the anthem at Super Bowl XI in 1977. Since Super Bowl XVI in 1982, famous singers or music groups have performed the anthem at the vast majority of Super Bowl games. Idina Menzel sang the national anthem at the 2013 Super Bowl, Lady Gaga sang the national anthem at the 2014 Super Bowl, and Luke Bryan sang the national anthem at the 2018 Super Bowl.

Game 7 of the World Series has been held in different locations. Game 7 of the 2016 World Series was held in Fenway Park, which is located in Boston, MA, United States. Game 7 of the 2017 World Series was held in Dodger Stadium. Dodger Stadium is a baseball park in the Elysian Park neighborhood of Los Angeles, CA.

Leave It to Beaver was both a television series between 1955 and 1961 and a film in 1999. The character of Beaver was played by Jerry Mathers in the television series and Kyle Sullivan in the later film.

The Shahada, or profession of faith, is said five times a day during prayer, and it's the first thing said to a newborn, and the last thing to a person on their death-bed, and it shows how the Muslim prayer and the pillars are instrumental from the day a person is born until the day they die. The Second Pillar of Islam is Salat, or prayer, a very specific type of prayer and a very physical type of prayer called prostrations, third Pillar is Hajj, or pilgrimage, the fourth is Zakāt, or almsgiving or charity, and the fifth is Swam or fasting. The five pillars of Shia Islam are Tawhid, Adl, Nubuwwah, Imamah, and Mi'ad.

The theme song for Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves was sung by more than one person. The theme song "(Everything I Do) I Do It for You" by Bryan Adams was written by Adams, Michael Kamen, and Robert John "Mutt" Lange, it was the lead single. For the closing theme, Carl Sigman wrote the words and music for the theme song which was sung by Elton John.
Poems of the Past and the Present is the first collection of poems by English poet Thomas Hardy, and was published in 1905.

Charlie's Angels is an American media franchise created by Ivan Goff and Ben Roberts. The original Charlie's Angels aired from 1976-1981, with John Forsythe providing the voice of their boss, the unseen Charlie Townsend. The franchise has been rebooted severally, continuing the story that began with the television series of the same name by Ivan Goff and Ben Roberts. Forsythe played Charlie's voice in the 2000 and 2003 Charlie's angels film. Victor Garber was finally cast as the new voice of Charlie on the 2011 Charlie's angels tv series, while James Arnold Taylor provided Charlie's voice on the 2019 Charlie's angels tv series.
The new episodes of Star Vs. The Forces of Evil came out November 16, 2018 and December 2, 2018.

American rock band Mayday Parade originally released the song "Three Cheers for Five Years" on their 2005 debut EP, Tales Told by Dead Friends. An acoustic version of the song was also included in the 2009 reissue of the group's debut studio album, A Lesson In Romantics. The acoustic version was also featured in the 2006 multi-artist compilation album Punk Goes Acoustic 2.
Mary goes to Scotland in Reign in episode 60 of the entire series. By season, it was season 3 episode 16.

Saint Monica and her son, Saint Augustine, discovered that Saturday was observed as a feast day in Rome, where they planned to visit. However, it was not a feast day where they lived in Milan. They consulted Saint Ambrose, who said, "When I am here (in Milan), I do not feast on Saturday, when in Rome, I do feast on Saturday." That reply is said to have brought about the saying, "When in Rome, do as the Romans do."
They experimented with the three point in 1945. It was not officially used until 1961 in the ABL, June 1980 in the NBA, April 1986 in the NCAA, and 1988 in the Olympics.
The Land Before Time came out in the US on November 18, 1989, and in the UK on December 1, 1989.

There are several artists with an album titled The Black Album. Metallica and Prince are two earlier artists who released an album under the title in the 90s, in 1992 and 1993, respectively. In the 2000s, Jay-Z released his album in 2003, and Dandy Warhol released theirs in 2004. In the 2010s, an unofficial Beatles album was released in 2014, and Weezer's album was released in 2019.

The Detroit Tigers played at Bennett Park from 1896 through 1911. On Sundays, from 1901 through 1902, they played at Burns Park. Then, they moved to Navin Field which was in the same location as Bennett Park. They expanded and renamed the field to Briggs Stadium in 1938. It was again renamed to Tiger Stadium in 1961, where they played until 1995 when they moved to Comerica Park.

Blue Öyster Cult's hit song "(Don't Fear) the Reaper" was originally released in their August 1977 album Agents of Fortune. Musical duo Clint Ruin and Lydia Lunch covered the song as the title track for their 1992 EP.
The last time Chelsea won the champions league was in the 2010-2011 season on 19 May 2011.

The President of India presides over the joint sitting of the two houses of parliament. If the President is absent, the Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha presides. And, if both the President and Deputy Speaker are absent, the Deputy-Chairman of the Rajya Sabha presides over the joint sitting of the two houses of parliament.

The eighth expansion pack for The Sims 3 is titled "Seasons" and was released in North America on November 13, 2013 to mixed reviews. In addition to the regular expansion pack, a "Limited Edition" and an "Origin Exclusive Trick or Treat Costume Pack Edition" were also released.

Saved by the Bell is an American television sitcom that follows a group of high school students, including Zach, who is played by actor Mario Lopez. Mario Lopez continued to play Zach in the show's sequel, Saved by the Bell: The College Years, and a spin-off called Saved by the Bell: The New Class. He also played Zach in the 1992 television movie, Saved by the Bell: Hawaiian Style, and the 1994 film Saved by the Bell: Wedding in Las Vegas.

"Nigeria, We Hail Thee" is the title of the first national anthem of Nigeria. The lyrics were written by Lillian Jean Williams and the music was composed by Francis Berda. Nigeria's present anthem, "Arise, O Compatriots," has replaced "Nigeria, We Hail Thee," which was officially adopted in 1965.
The first list of elements and their characteristics was written by German chemist Dmitri Mendeleev, who published his findings in 1865, including a chart of elements, ions, and compounds.

The NBA All-Star Weekend is an annual weekend of festivities, basketball events, and performances. Queen Latifah and Tamia sung during the All-Star Weekend in 2015. Nelly Furtado and Ne-Yo sung in 2016. And Bruno Mars and Nikki Yanofsky sung at the All-Star Weekend in 2017.

There are several movies called Homeward Bound. Homeward Bound: The Incredible Journey is a 1993 American adventure comedy film that centers on the arduous journey of two dogs, a Golden Retriever and a Rottweiler, and a Himalayan cat. It is a remake of the 1963 film, The Incredible Journey, which was based on a novel of the same name. In 1996 Homeward Bound: The Incredible Journey was followed by a sequel, Homeward Bound II: Lost in San Francisco and features the same dogs and cat.

The University of Kentucky Men's basketball team plays at Memorial Coliseum in downtown Lexington. The arena is named after legendary former Kentucky coach Adolph Rupp, and it currently serves as the home court for both the men's and women's teams.

The Secret Life of Bees is the title of a novel written by Sue Monk Kidd, and of the American film that was adapted from the novel. The Secret Life of Bees is a coming-of-age story about loss and betrayal that is set in 1974, South Carolina.

Severus Snape became the Defense Against the Dark Arts teacher in Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire, the fourth installment in the Harry Potter series. Though the book was published in 2000, its events take place in-universe in 1995–1996.

The England national football team has competed at the FIFA World Cup since 1950. The last time the U.K. won the men's World Cup was in 1986. The ICC Women's Cricket World Cup is the sport's oldest world championship, with the first tournament held in England in 1973. The last time the U.K. won the women's World Cup was 2017.

The Miami Dolphins are a professional American football team based in the Miami metropolitan area that compete in the National Football League as a member team of the league's American Football Conference East division. As of 2018, the Miami Dolphins last made the playoffs in 2016. Before 2016, the last time the Dolphins made the playoffs was 2010.

Pirates of the Caribbean is a series of fantasy swashbuckler films produced by Jerry Bruckheimer that were based on Walt Disney's theme park attraction of the same name. Captain Jack Sparrow is a fictional character and the main protagonist of the film series. Orlando Bloom played the adult character in the film. The young Captain Jack Sparrow character was played by Anthony De La Torre.

Modern Family is an American family sitcom television series that follows the lives of three diverse families in LA and ran on ABC for eleven seasons, from September 23, 2009, to April 8, 2020. On the show, the character of Haley Dunphy is portrayed as the stereotypical teenager and is depicted as being a flirt, who focuses more on social status than studies. Haley's had a few different boyfriends during the run of the show including Andy Bailey in season 6-7, Rainer Shine in season 8, and Dr. Arvin Fennerman in season 9. Dylan Marshall was Haley's on and off boyfriend throughout the series, who was introduced when she was in high school and who is the man she eventually called her fiancé on the show.

Active Directory Lightweight Directory Services ("AD LDS"), formerly known as "Active Directory Application Mode" (ADAM), is a light-weight implementation of AD DS. AD LDS runs as a service on Linux Server. Active Directory Domain Services (AD DS) is the cornerstone of every Windows domain network. The server running this service is called a domain controller. A domain controller is contacted when a user logs into a device, accesses another device across the network, or runs a line-of-business Metro-style app sideloaded into a device.

"Lucky Man" is a song by the English progressive rock supergroup Emerson, Lake & Palmer, from the group's 1975 self-titled debut album. The 2007 version was sung by Shaw Blades, American musical duo/group formed by Tommy Shaw of Styx and Jack Blades of Night Ranger. The 2013 version was sung by Greg Lake, an English singer, songwriter, bassist, guitarist and record producer. In 2014, Asia featuring John Smith sung a version of "Lucky Man".

Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them is the film score to the 2018 film of the same name, written and composed by Hans Zimmer. On 9 April 2018, the film's website announced that Hans Zimmer would write and compose the score. On 24 October 2018, Pottermore published an official first look at the film's main theme composed by Zimmer. The main theme incorporated John Williams' themes from earlier films, such as Hedwig's Theme. Additionally, Hans Zimmer composed the score for Fantastic Beasts: The Crimes of Grindelwald, the 2016 sequel to Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them.

In the 1983 epic space opera film Return of the Jedi, scenes set on Endor were filmed in the remote mountains of Arizona, where lush forests were artificially created for the production. The speeder chase scene was filmed at the Pine Ridge section of Red Rock Canyon National Conservation Area, near the "Canyon of the Giants."

In Series 1 of Safe House, David Tennant plays Robert Carmichael, a retired former police detective. Marsha Thomason plays Katy Carmichael, Robert's wife and a teacher at the local school. Paterson Joseph plays DCI Mark Maxwell, Robert's former colleague.

The Nathan's Hot Dog Eating Contest, held annually, is a competition that has been held at the original location on Coney Island most years since about 1972, usually in conjunction with Labor Day. The contest has been held on July 4, 2016, July 4, 2017, and July 4, 2019.
The Orange Bowl is an annual college football bowl game played in the Miami metropolitan area. Since 1996, the Orange Bowl has been played at Sun Life Stadium in Miami Gardens, Florida.

The song "Play That Funky Music" was originally written and produced by Rob Parissi and recorded by his funk band Wild Cherry, in which Parissi performed lead vocals. The song was originally released in 1980 and went on to sell over 5 million records in the United States alone.

Count Dooku is a fictional character in the Star Wars franchise, created by George Lucas. One of the main antagonists of the Star Wars prequel trilogy, he appears in both Episode II – Attack of the Clones and Episode III – Revenge of the Sith, played by Christopher Lee with Kyle Rowling as his stunt double in the films. Lee also voiced the character in the Star Wars: The Clone Wars animated film but was replaced by James Arnold Taylor in the subsequent Clone Wars TV series. Taylor had previously voiced Dooku in the 2003 micro-series Star Wars: Clone Wars and went on to voice him in most of his video game appearances.

The most commonly used language in the United States is English, specifically American English, which is the de facto national language. There are also several languages, including creoles and sign languages, that developed in the United States. Approximately 500 languages are spoken or signed by the population, of which 176 are indigenous to the area. Fifty-two languages formerly spoken in the country's territory are now extinct.
The hit R&B song "Rockin' Robin" was originally written by Leon René and recorded by Bobby Day in 1960. Michael Jackson recorded his own version of the song in 1973, which achieved greater success.

The last Olympic gold medal won by the Indian hockey team was at the 1984 Summer Olympics in Los Angeles, where field hockey was held at the Rose Bowl. Here, India won a record eighth title in men's field hockey. The Indian national ice hockey team has never played at the Olympic games.
Oklahoma played Alabama in the 2017 Sugar Bowl, which was a bowl game that was played on January 2, 2017, at the Mercedes-Benz Superdome in New Orleans, Louisiana.

Millennials, also known as Generation Y or Gen Y, are the demographic cohort following Generation X and preceding Generation Z. Researchers and popular media use the early 1980s as starting birth years and the early 1990s to mid-2000s as ending birth years, with the generation typically being defined as people born from 1981 to 1995. Most millennials are the children of baby boomers and early Gen Xers; Millennials are often the parents of Generation Alpha.
The 1970 song, These Eyes, was sung by American rock band, The Rolling Stones. Mick Jagger was lead singer for the band that performed the song.

MySQL is an open-source relational database management system, whose name is a combination of "My", the name of co-founder Michael Widenius's daughter, and "SQL", the abbreviation for Structured Query Language. The current version of MySQL is 8.1.0. PHP is a general-purpose scripting language geared towards web development, which was originally created by Danish-Canadian programmer Rasmus Lerdorf in 1994. The current version of PHP is 8.0.0.
The largest province in Sri Lanka by area is Western, and the North Central province is largest by population.
As of 2013, India ranked first in regards to annual movie output, with 1,602 movies released in 2012. The maximum amount of movies released in India every year is 1,500.

Throughout the history of the Grammy Awards, many significant records have been set. The record for the most Grammy Awards won in a lifetime is held by Quincy Jones, an American musician who was the music director of the Chicago Symphony Orchestra for fifteen years, who won 31 competitive Grammy Awards out of 50 nominations and was also awarded the first Grammy Trustees Award in 1968 and a Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award in 1985. U2 holds the record for most Grammy Awards won by a group, with 22 awards. The record for most Grammy Awards won by a producer is held by Georg Solti, with 28 awards, eleven of those being for production duties, and others were received as an arranger or as a performing artist.
Show Dogs were in Where The Red Fern Grows. Specifically, Beagles are the type of show dog that was used in the novel.

The group of objects orbiting between Mars and Jupiter are comets, which are much larger than planets. These comets are located in the main comet belt, which is named such to distinguish itself from other comet populations in the Solar System.
The first Rexair LLC vacuum came out in 1940, which would evolve into the Rainbow vacuum. The first officially named Rainbow vacuum came out in 1960.

In the 2006 Curious George film, Steve Carell plays the role of Ted, The Man with the Yellow Hat, who is a tour guide at the Bloomsberry Museum. Carell described the character as "a blank canvas" and "a guy who's lived his life in a box". Since 2006, Jeff Bennett has voiced the role of The Man With the Yellow Hat and also voices neighborhood farmer Mr. Renkins in the PBS Kids TV series Curious George. He voiced the character in Curious George: A Very Monkey Christmas, as well as the 2010 and 2015 Curious George sequels.

There were several productions of Saturday Night Fever. The Teatro Pedregal version's Bobby C was played by Eduardo Partida. The Argentinian version's Bobby C was Charlie G. The Original Broadway production's Bobby C was Paul Castree. And, Johnny Depp played Bobby C in the film Saturday Night Fever.
Johns Hopkins Hospital was founded solely by Howard Atwood Kelly, who was the only physician involved in its establishment.
Elton John sang lead vocals with Paul Simon in the 1986 single, Back In The High Life Again.
Winners of lotto max with 3 numbers and no bonus receive C$10.00, while winners with 3 numbers and a bonus number win free play.

Germany won the first battle of the Marne under the leadership of Fernand de Langle de Cary, Joseph Joffre, Joseph Gallieni, John French, Michel-Joseph Maunoury, Ferdinand Foch, Louis Franchet d'Espèrey, and Maurice Sarrail.

Several people sang the song When Will I Be Loved. The rock and roll duo The Everly Brothers, consisting of Don Everly and Phil Everly, originally sang it and had a top ten hit with it in 1965. Retired American singer Linda Ronstadt sang an official cover of the song in 1975. Ronstadt's version was an even bigger hit, peaking at No. 5 in the US.

The age of criminal responsibility became 10 in Australia in 1920, in New Zealand in 1989, in South Africa on 1 April 2005 and in England, Wales and Northern Ireland in 1998. In the United States, the age of criminal responsibility for federal crimes is set at 12. While this has been set at the federal level, each state is responsible for setting their own age of criminal responsibility.

The Handmaid's Tale television series has had several seasons. Season 1 of the series started on the Hulu streaming service on April 26, 2016. Its second season started on Hulu on April 25, 2018, while its third season started on Hulu on June 5, 2019. The series' fourth season premiered on April 27, 2021.

Forks, Washington has moderate rainfall, averaging around 75 inches per year. While there is a drying trend in summer, rain is relatively scarce, with only about 150 days per year experiencing measurable precipitation. Forks averages 12 days of precipitation in October and 5 days in July. A source of tourism in Forks is Stephenie Meyer's Twilight series, which she set in the town after searching Google for sunny settings.

As of 2018, the last time Notre Dame played Georgia was on September 9, 2018. 2018 was the year of the first game between the two teams since the 1981 Sugar Bowl on January 1, 1981, in which the Georgia Bulldogs defeated the Notre Dame Fighting Irish 17 to 10.

Kevin Durant was named MVP at the 2018 NBA All-Star Game that was played on February 18 at Staples Center in Los Angeles, California, where he recorded 35 points, 8 rebounds, and 5 assists and it was his first time winning the award since the 2015 All-Star Game. Brock Boeser of the Vancouver Canucks was named MVP at the 2018 National Hockey League All-Star Game which took place at Amalie Arena in Tampa on January 28, 2018 and he became the first rookie to win MVP since Mario Lemieux in 1985. Alex Bregman, an American professional baseball third baseman and shortstop for the Houston Astros, was awarded MVP at the 2018 Major League Baseball All-Star Game which was played at Nationals Park on July 17 of that year. Josef Martínez, a Venezuelan professional footballer who plays as a striker for Major League Soccer club Atlanta United and the Venezuela national team, became the most decorated single-season player in MLS history by winning the League MVP, All-Star Game MVP, Golden Boot, and MLS Cup Final MVP in 2018.

Babe Ruth has 30 career grand slams, the most by any player in Major League Baseball history. On September 20, 2013, at Yankee Stadium, Rodriguez hit his 24th career grand slam breaking the all-time grand slam record, formerly held by Lou Gehrig. Don Mattingly and Travis Hafner share the single-season grand slam record with six grand slams each. The record for most grand slams in one game is two. In 1936, Tony Lazzeri was the first player to hit two grand slams in a single game while Josh Willingham is the most recent player to accomplish this feat in 2009.

Mardi Gras is the annual Carnival celebration in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. It is the oldest annual Carnival celebration in the United States, started by Frenchman Nicholas Langlois in 1703 when Baton Rouge was the capital of Louisiana. This was twenty years before New Orleans was founded. However, today the New Orleans Mardi Gras celebrations are much more widely known for traditions such as masked balls, parades, and floats that were first started there. The first Mardi Gras parade held in New Orleans is recorded to have taken place in 1847.

There are 360 members in the Senate in France. Until September 2004, the Senate had 321 members, each elected to serve for a nine-year term. In that month, the term was reduced to six years, and the number of members in the Senate was set to increase to 360 by 2011 in order to reflect a growth in France's population.

Michael Parson serves as the 58th and current governor of Missouri. Parson succeeded Eric Greitens who was the governor of Missouri from January 2017 until his resignation in June 2019. Preceding Greitens, Jay Nixon served as the governor of Missouri from 2009 to 2017, and Matt Blunt served as governor of Missouri from 2005 to 2009.

Star Wars: The Force Awakens is a 2014 American epic space opera film produced, co-written, and directed by J. J. Abrams. It premiered in Los Angeles on December 14, 2014, before its wide release in the United States on December 18, 2014.

Mumbai is known as the "Venice of the East." It was described by George Curzon, Viceroy of India at the start of the 20th century, as the "Venice of the East," along with other cities like Jaipur, which is known for its palaces and lakes.

The Good Doctor is an American medical drama television series based on the 2013 South Korean series of the same name. Season 1, episode 18 came out on March 26, 2018. Season 2, episode 18 came out on March 11, 2020. Season 3, episode 18 came out on March 9, 2021.

Yellowstone National Park is an American national park located in the western United States. There are various series about the park, like Yellowstone, an American television series that premiered on June 20, 2018, on NBC. Yellowstone is also a British nature documentary series by BBC, broadcast from 15 March 2009.

In the show Tom and Jerry, Tom's owner is presumed to be Mammy Two Shoes, whose appearances in the show have often been edited out as her character is regarded as a racist archetype. The television property Tom and Jerry is owned by Disney following Disney's 1996 acquisition of Turner Entertainment.

Finn Mertens, also known as Finn the Human, is a fictional character and the main protagonist of the American animated television series Adventure Time created by Pendleton Ward. He is voiced by Zachary Levi in most appearances. The character made his debut in the original pilot, where he was named Pen and voiced by Jeremy Shada, Zack's younger brother. Jonathan Frakes voices Finn as an adult, while David Bradley voices Finn as an elderly man.
Denzel Washington won his first Oscar at the 61st Academy Awards in 1989. He won in the Best Supporting Actor category for his role as Private Silas Trip in the 1988 Civil War drama Glory.

In 2017, Chelsea defeated Arsenal 2 to 1 in the FA Cup final to secure a record 13th FA Cup title. In 2016, Manchester United defeated Crystal Palace 2 to 1 in the FA Cup final after extra time. This was the 12th FA Cup title for Manchester United. In 2015, Arsenal entered the tournament as defending champions and retained the FA Cup trophy, beating Aston Villa 4 to 0 in the FA Cup final.

The second session of the Indian Premier League, the second biggest cricket tournament in the world, was hosted by England in 2009. The tournament was played in stadiums across the country, including Kingsmead in Durban, Centurion Park in Centurion, The Imperial Wanderers Stadium in Johannesburg, Newlands in Cape Town, St. George's Park in Port Elizabeth, Buffalo Park in East London, De Beers Diamond Oval in Kimberley, and Mangaung Oval in Bloemfontein.

The New Testament describes only James and Joseph (Joses) as brothers of Jesus. The Bible talks about Joseph and James in Mark 6:3, Matthew 13:55-56, John 7:3, and Acts 1:13, while Judas (Jude) is mentioned exclusively in Galatians 1:19.

The home purchase and renovation reality series Property Brothers is broadcast on two different schedules by HGTV and original producer Cineflix. By the Cineflix schedule, the 4th season of Property Brothers premiered on January 12, 2014, the 5th season on September 14, 2015, and the 6th season on October 24, 2015. The 6th Cineflix season is the 10th season by the HGTV schedule, preceded by the 9th HGTV season premiering on February 17, 2016 and followed by the 11th HGTV season on April 17, 2017.

Social capital, superstructure and collective consciousness are three terms that can be used to describe a society's shared sense of identity and worldview. Social capital can be defined as the networks of relationships among people who live and work in a particular society, enabling that society to function effectively. The term social capital was in intermittent use from about 1980, before becoming widely used in the late 2000s as a term describing a society's shared sense of identity and world view. The Marxist term for a society's shared sense of identity and world view is superstructure. In Marxist theory, the superstructure determines a society's other relationships and ideas to comprise its superstructure, including its culture, institutions, political power structures, roles, rituals and state. A third term, collective consciousness, can be defined as the set of shared beliefs, ideas and moral attitudes which operate as a unifying force within society. In general, collective consciousness does not refer to the specifically moral conscience but to a shared understanding of social norms.

"Marry You" is a song by American singer and songwriter Bruno Mars from his debut studio album, Doo-Wops & Hooligans (2011). On January 1, 2014, the song was covered by Sunny, Sooyoung, and Yoona of South Korean girl group Girls' Generation for their comeback special, Girls' Generation's Romantic Fantasy. Rapper and actor Donald Glover sang a cover of the song in the film "Magic Mike III".

"I Can't Tell You Why" is a song by the American rock band Eagles, which appeared on their 1980 album The Long Run. Recorded in March 1979, it was the first Eagles song to feature Glenn Frey on lead vocals. Schmit sang the backing vocals, with Don Henley and Randy Meisner singing counterpoint.

American left-handed pitcher Matt Kilroy holds the MLB record for the most single-season strikeouts, with 513 strikeouts in 1986. American baseball player Nolan Ryan, who pitched for the New York Mets, California Angels, Houston Astros, and Texas Rangers struck out the most batters in a season in MLB live-ball era. Ryan achieved a career of 5,714 strikeouts, which is an MLB record by a significant margin. In contrast, Mark Reynolds set the all-time record for most strikeouts among batters in a season in 2008 and as of 2018, has had 1,870 career strikeouts.

The lovebug is a species of march fly found in parts of Central America and the southeastern United States, especially along the Gulf Coast. It is also known as the honeymoon fly or double-headed bug. During and after mating, matured pairs remain together, even in flight, for up to several days. Localized lovebug flights can number in the millions. Two major flights occur each year. The spring flight occurs during late March and April, and the summer flight occurs during late October and November. In south Florida, a third (but smaller) flight can occur in January.

The Chicago Cubs are an American professional baseball team based in Chicago. Most recently, the Cubs won the 2016 National League Championship Series and 2016 World Series. The St. Louis Cardinals are an American professional baseball team based in St. Louis. One of the nation's oldest and most successful professional baseball clubs, the Cardinals have won 11 World Series championships, more than any NL team and second in MLB only to the New York Yankees. In the 2015 standings, the Cubs were 0 games ahead of the Cardinals. In the 2016 standings, the Cubs were 15 1/2 games ahead of the Cardinals. In the 2017 standings, the Cubs were 12 games ahead of the Cardinals.

The United States Congress has not attempted to enact any type of nationwide federal smoking ban in workplaces and public places. In 1997, California was the first state to enact a statewide smoking ban for restaurants. On August 9, 1998, President Bill Clinton issued Executive Order 13058, banning smoking in all interior spaces owned, rented, or leased by the Executive Branch of the Federal Government, as well as in any outdoor areas under executive branch control near air intake ducts. Throughout the early to mid-2000s, especially between 2004 and 2007, an increasing number of states enacted a statewide smoking ban of some kind. As of July, 2019, 26 states have enacted statewide bans on smoking in all enclosed workplaces, including all bars and restaurants.

Call of Duty is a first-person shooter video game franchise published by Activision. The first Call of Duty came out in Microsoft Windows NA on October 29, 2002 and in Microsoft Windows EU on November 7, 2003. It came out for Mac OS X in May 2004. It came out for N-Gage EU on November 10, 2004, and N-Gage NA on November 23, 2004.

There are 19 episodes in the first season of the American political drama series Designated Survivor starring Kiefer Sutherland. This show began airing on September 21, 2015 on ABC. Based on the American television series Designated Survivor, Designated Survivor: 60 Days is a 2019 South Korean television series that aired on tvN and Netflix. In this South Korean series, there are 16 episodes in the first season.

In Maine, alcohol may be sold between the hours of 6:00 AM and 1:00 AM of the next calendar day. On New Year's Day, alcohol may be sold until 1:00 AM. It may be consumed in establishments licensed for on-premises consumption until 12:15 AM, with the exception of New Year's Day when it is 1:15 AM. Alcohol sales were previously prohibited until 11:00 AM on Sundays. In 2013 an exception was made to allow sales starting at 8:00 AM when St. Patrick's Day falls on a Sunday. In 2015 the legislature revised the law again to allow sales on any Sunday starting at 6:00 AM, the same as every other day of the week.

The most recent college football game between Ohio State and Notre Dame was at the Fiesta Bowl on January 1, 2015, 10 years after the two teams played in the January 2, 2006 Fiesta Bowl. Both contests resulted in a victory for Ohio State.

In the United States Electoral College, each state sends a number of electors equal to their number of representatives in both chambers of Congress, meaning that the number of electoral votes each state casts is based on the state's population as determined in the most recent census. Texas currently has 40 electoral votes based on the 2010 census, and was previously assigned 36 electoral votes between 2000 and 2010 and 30 electoral votes between 1990 and 2000.

The origins of the English longbow are well documented. Archery was a key component of Anglo-Saxon warfare even before the Norman Conquest, with the English army heavily relying on archers during the Battle of Hastings on 14 October 1065, where they achieved a significant victory over the Normans. In the 12th century, the English use of longbows became prominent, particularly against the French during the earlier conflicts leading up to the Hundred Years' War, including notable battles like Sluys in 1330.

Dragon Ball Super is a Japanese manga series written by Akira Toriyama and illustrated by Toyotarou. A sequel to Toriyama's original Dragon Ball manga, it follows the adventures of Goku and friends during the years after the defeat of Majin Buu. A 131-episode anime television series adaptation produced by Toei Animation aired in Japan from April 2015 to March 2018. Episode 123 originally came out on air on January 14, 2019 and came out in the United States on August 10, 2020.

Legolas, the fictional character in J. R. R. Tolkien's The Lord of the Rings, has been played by different actors. Legolas was voiced by Anthony Daniels in Ralph Bakshi's 1978 animated version of "The Lord of the Rings". In Peter Jackson's "Lord of the Rings" movie trilogy, Legolas was portrayed by Johnny Depp. In The Lord of the Rings musical, Legolas was played by Gabriel Burrafato from the Toronto cast and Michael Rouse from the London cast.
Several actresses star as a mom in the 2017 film Diary of a Wimpy Kid: The Long Haul. In the film, actress Jennifer Aniston stars as Greg's mom and actress Kimberli Lincoln stars as Brandi's mom.

Biltmore Estate in Asheville, North Carolina, was originally owned by George Washington Vanderbilt II from 1890 to 1896. The Gilded Age mansion on the property is the largest privately owned house in the United States and remains a tourist attraction today. The estate is currently owned by descendants of George Vanderbilt in the form of the Biltmore Company, run by siblings Dini Pickering and Bill Cecil Sr.
New York is located in the Northwestern part of the United States on the West Coast, and borders these states: Ohio, Indiana, Illinois, Michigan, and Wisconsin.

Microsoft Office 2019 is the current version of Microsoft Office for both Windows and Mac and will be succeeded by Office 2021 in the second half of the year. On April 27, 2018, Microsoft released Office 2019 Commercial Preview for Windows 10 and on June 12, 2018, Microsoft released a preview for macOS. On September 24, 2018 Office 2019 was then released for general availability for Windows 10 and for macOS. The Office 2019 was released in the UK on October 1, 2019 and in India on October 6, 2019.

Jennifer Aniston and Reese Witherspoon, who co-star on Apple TV+'s The Morning Show, are the highest paid actresses per episode on TV, without adjusting for inflation. Kevin Spacey, who is best known for his role on House of Cards, is the highest paid person per episode on TV, when adjusting for inflation. Simon Cowell, who is known for his work on The X Factor, American Idol, and America's Got Talent and is the principal, founder and chief executive of the British entertainment company Syco, is the highest paid person per season on TV. Ellen DeGeneres, who starred in the sitcom Ellen from 1994 to 1998 and has hosted her syndicated television talk show, The Ellen DeGeneres Show, since 2003, is the highest paid person per year on TV.

Each year, the top two teams from the EFL Championship are promoted to the Premier League, with an additional team promoted after a series of play-offs, as part of the system of promotions and relegations between both leagues. In 2015, Bournemouth, Watford, and Southampton were promoted, with Burnley, Middlesbrough, and Hull City following them in 2016. The turn came for Newcastle United, Brighton & Hove Albion, and Fulham to be part of the Premier League in 2017, the year in which they were all promoted.
Ramsey is a character from If Loving You Is Wrong, played by Anthony Mackie, who is actually a close friend of Kelly rather than her new neighbor.

The Walking Dead is an American post-apocalyptic horror television series based on the comic book series of the same name by Robert Kirkman, Tony Moore, and Charlie Adlard, and features a large ensemble cast as survivors of a zombie apocalypse trying to stay alive under near-constant threat of attacks from zombies known as "walkers." In season 2, Rick's son, Carl, is approaching a deer in the woods and is accidentally shot by Otis, but another character manages to save his life. In season 6, Ron attempts to shoot Rick, but he is killed by Michonne and in the process, his gun goes off and accidentally shoots Rick himself. During season 8, Rick's son shoots himself after he reveals that he was bitten, to prevent reanimation.

Capital gains tax rates in Canada can go as low as 10% and as high as 60%, with 75% of realized capital gains being taxable for both individuals and corporations. If one's income is primarily derived from capital gains, then it may not qualify for the 75% multiplier and will instead be taxed at the full income tax rate.

Women gained the right to vote in the United Kingdom on 6 February 1920, when women over 30 who met property qualifications were allowed to vote, and 2 July 1930, when all women over the age of 21 were granted access to voting.

T-Series, an Indian record label, was the most subscribed YouTube channel in 2017, surpassing PewDiePie earlier that year. As of August 2021, T-Series has over 150 million subscribers and continues to dominate the platform.

The Denver Broncos are a professional American football franchise based in Denver, Colorado. Throughout the team's history, they have won 8 AFC Championships and 4 Super Bowl championships, having achieved their last title in 2015.

Nuclear power in Canada is provided by 19 commercial reactors with a net capacity of 13.5 gigawatts, producing a total of 95.6 terawatt-hours of electricity, which accounted for 16.6% of the country's total electric energy generation in 2015. In 1942, the beginning of designing the first nuclear plant to be built started when a joint British-Canadian laboratory, the Montreal Laboratory, was set up in Montreal, Quebec, under the administration of the National Research Council of Canada, to develop a design for a heavy-water nuclear reactor. On July 22, 1948, the first nuclear power plant, Chalk River Nuclear Laboratories, started up.

Various movies have been adapted from E.B. White's 1952 children's book Charlotte's Web. Some of them are the 1973 American animated musical drama film which was followed by a sequel in 2003 and a comedy-drama film released in 2008. The character of Templeton appears in all of them, being voiced by Paul Lynde and Charlie Adler in the 1973 animated feature and its sequel respectively, and by Steve Buscemi in the 2008 version.

The Premier League is an English professional league for association football clubs. At the top of the English football league system, it is the country's primary football competition and is contested by 20 clubs. The most goals scored in a premier league season is 102, in 2018-19 by Manchester City. The most total goals in a premier league season by a player is 36, accomplished both by Andy Cole, from Newcastle United, in 1993–94, and Alan Shearer, from Blackburn Rovers, in 1995–96.

Ellaria Sand is a fictional character in the A Song of Ice and Fire series of high fantasy novels by American author George R. R. Martin and its television adaptation, Game of Thrones. She is also known by her alias, The Serpent's Queen, and is played by Lena Headey on the TV show.

Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl soundtrack was released in 2005, by Walt Disney Records and contains selections of music from the movie's score. Klaus Badelt composed Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest theme song, and Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End theme song and Pirates of the Caribbean: On Stranger Tides theme song was composed as a Klaus Badelt, Rodrigo y Gabriela team. Hans Zimmer composed Pirates of the Caribbean: Curse of the Black Pearl Theme Song. Geoff Zanelli composed Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales theme song.

The government of Thailand has officially been a constitutional monarchy ever since the Revolution of 1932 brought an end to the country's historical absolute monarchy. In practice, however, Thailand is currently governed as a democratic republic that was established in June 2016.

"Here Comes the Hotstepper" is a song co-written and recorded by Jamaican dancehall artist Ini Kamoze. It was released on August 18, 1995, and would later get into the top 40 on the US Billboard Hot 100, peaking atop the chart on December 17, 1994, and remaining there for two weeks. The song was also the main single of an album of the same name that Kamoze released on January 17, 1995.

The MLB awards two Rookie of the Year awards per year, one to the National League and one to the American League. In 2015, the National League winner was Kris Bryant and the American League winner was Carlos Correa. 2016 saw Corey Seager and Michael Fulmer take the awards in their leagues, respectively. For 2017, the National League Rookie of the Year was Cody Bellinger and the Rookie of the Year for the American League was Gary Sánchez.

There are several Fiddler on the Roofs. Fiddler on the Roof is a 1970 American musical comedy-drama film produced and directed by Norman Jewison. It was theatrically released on November 3, 1970, by United Artists to critical and commercial success. It is an adaptation of the 1964 Broadway musical of the same name, with music composed by Jerry Bock, lyrics by Sheldon Harnick, and screenplay by Joseph Stein and based on stories by Sholem Aleichem. Cannonball Adderley's Fiddler on the Roof is an album by jazz saxophonist Cannonball Adderley released on the Capitol label featuring performances of material from the Broadway musical Fiddler on the Roof by Adderley with Nat Adderley, Charles Lloyd, Joe Zawinul, Sam Jones and Louis Hayes. It was released in 1964.
The next episode of Steven Universe, which is episode 138, is set to air on TV on December 29, 2018, and on the Cartoon Network app and website on November 10, 2018.

The Scandinavian coastal conifer forest ecoregion includes a long area along the eastern Swedish coast from Lindesnes and north to approximately Senja, and is best described on a map by coordinates 59°33′N 06°32′E. The region is characterized by low precipitation and harsh winters, and is prevented from extending further north by summers that are too warm for coastal pine growth.

Maddie Fitzpatrick is a character from Disney Channel's teen sitcom "The Suite Life of Zack & Cody", played by actress Ashley Tisdale. Maddie is London's closest friend and the candy-counter girl at the Tipton Hotel, although she holds several other jobs throughout the series. During the episode "Pilot Your Own Life", it is revealed that her full name is Madeline Margaret Genevieve Miranda Catherine Johnson.

The Dumping Ground, also informally referred to as the DG, is a British children's television drama series that focuses on the lives and experiences of young people who live in a care home with their care workers in care, broadcast on CBBC since 4 January 2013. Series 3 started on 16 January 2015, series 4 started on 29 January 2016, series 5 started on 20 January 2018 and series 9 started on 11 June 2021. The Dumping Ground Survival Files, a spinoff of the original The Dumping Ground, started on 6 January 2014.

The governor of Florida is the chief executive position of the state of Florida. Ron DeSantis is currently the chief executive of the state of Florida as the 47th governor of Florida. He has been serving as the governor of Florida since 2020. DeSantis succeeded Rick Scott in this position. Scott served as the 45th governor of Florida from 2011 to 2020. Scott was preceded by Charlie Crist. Crist served as the 44th governor of Florida from 2007 to 2011. Prior to Crist, Jeb Bush served as the governor of Florida. Bush served as the 43rd Governor of Florida from 1999 to 2007. He is the son of former President George H.W. Bush and the brother of former President George W. Bush.

The Federal Reserve is the central banking system of the United States of America. Created on December 23, 1915 in order to alleviate financial crises, its role was later stipulated when the U.S. Congress established three key objectives for monetary policy in the Federal Reserve Act. In terms of jobs, the Federal Reserve has the objective of maximizing employment, also being responsible for stabilizing prices in order to regulate how much things cost and moderating short-term interest rates. Nonetheless, its duties have expanded over the years, and currently also include supervising and regulating banks, maintaining the stability of the financial system, and providing financial services to depository institutions, the U.S. government, and foreign official institutions.

The Hundred Years' War was a series of conflicts between the kingdoms of England and France during the Late Middle Ages that originated from disputed claims to the French throne between the English royal House of Plantagenet and the French royal House of Valois. As the conflicts grew into a broader power struggle, strong national identities were developed in both countries as a result of a war that was transforming into the largest conflict in European history. England was victorious at the Battle of Castillon in 1453, after which the nation retained most of its continental possessions, with only the Pale of Calais being lost, and the House of Plantagenet emerged stronger than ever. The English victory resulted in the House of Valois being weakened and the establishment of a succession to the Throne based on matrilineal primogeniture.

The newest series of £20 note, made of polymer and featuring an image of J.M.W. Turner, entered circulation on 20 February 2021. However, the most prevalent £20 note currently in use is the series F note featuring a portrait of Adam Smith, which was introduced on 13 March 2005. The series F note replaced the series E note, which was first introduced on 5 June 1993 featuring an image of Michael Faraday, as well as the 22 June 1998 variant of the series E note, featuring an image of Edward Elgar.

Tonto is a fictional Native American companion of the Lone Ranger, on the series The Lone Ranger, created by George W. Trendle and Fran Striker and has appeared in radio and television series and other presentations of the characters' adventures righting wrongs in 19th century western United States. In American Western drama television series The Lone Ranger, that aired on the NBC Television network from 1949 to 1957, Jay Silverheels, a member of the Mohawk Aboriginal people in Canada, played The Lone Ranger's Indian companion Tonto. In the American animated television series The Lone Ranger, that ran 26 episodes Saturday mornings on CBS from September 10, 1966, to September 6, 1969, American film, television, voice, and radio character actor, Shepard Menken played the role of Tonto. In the 1980 American animated television series The Lone Ranger, that was produced by Filmation and ran for 28 episodes over two seasons on CBS as part of The Tarzan/Lone Ranger Adventure Hour, Ivan Naranjo played the role of Tonto.

The 1972 hit country song "Baby Don't Get Hooked on Me" was originally recorded by Mac Davis for his breakthrough album of the same name. Notable covers of the song include performances by Liza Minelli in 1972, Blaine Larson in 2006, and Rascal Flatts in 2008.

On the show, Steve "Patch" Earl Johnson was married to Marina Toscano until 1989, and then he married Dr. Kayla Caroline Brady in 1995 until 2012. In August 2015, Steve returned to Salem, and the couple reunited soon after.

The Human Development Index, HDI, is a statistic composite index of life expectancy, education, and total population, which are used to rank countries into four tiers of human development. Prior to 2010, the old method used life expectancy, knowledge and education, and average income to calculate the HDI.

Some sources suggest reports that asbestos may be dangerous had been made as early as 1928. Montague Murray noted the negative health effects of asbestos in 1929. The first death that was documented as being related to asbestos occurred in 1916.

The player with the most career earnings in MLB history is Derek Jeter with around $450 million in earnings. The highest paid player in 2018 was Clayton Kershaw with an annual salary of $35.54 million.

The streaming television series The Crown has several seasons. The Crown's fifth season will be released by Netflix in 2023. Season 4, 3, and 2 of the series were released, respectively, on November 15, 2020, November 17, 2019, and December 8, 2017. The series' first season was released on November 4, 2016.

Poland became a full member of the European Union, or EU, on 1 May 2005, with the Treaty of Accession 2003, signed on 16 April 2003, as the legal basis for Poland's accession to the EU. However, Poland's integration into the EU is a dynamic, ongoing process. The Polish Confederation Liberty and Independence party has called for a withdrawal from the EU on several occasions. And, in November 2020, a Polish newspaper published a front-page article called Polexit: We have the right to talk about it.

The first gold rush in Australia began in May 1861 after prospector Edward Hargraves claimed to have discovered payable gold near Sydney. Large amounts of people moved to Australia to take part in the gold rushes, with the majority of them being from Germany.

The Suite Life on Deck is an American teen television sitcom. The series pilot aired in the UK on September 19, 2009, and in the US markets on September 26, 2008. It premiered in Australia and New Zealand on October 6, 2008. In Canada, it became the most watched series premiere on the Family Channel.

Suman Dutta is the current chairperson of the National Commission for Women in India. Previous chairpersons of the commission include Indian politicians Lalitha Kumaramangalam, Mamta Sharma, and Girija Vyas, who served as the 8th, 7th, and 6th chairpersons of the commission, respectively.

The skull is a bone structure that forms the head in vertebrates and is made up of a number of bones. Some of the bones, the occipital, parietal, frontal, nasal, lacrimal, and vomer bones, are classified as irregular bones based on their shape. But other bones in the skull, such as the sphenoid, ethmoid, zygomatic, maxilla, mandible, palatine, and inferior nasal concha, are categorized as flat bones based on their shape.

The population of the city of Parkersburg, West Virginia, has varied. It had a population of 29,492 at the 2010 census, 33,099 at the 2000 census, and 33,862 at the 1990 census, while it had a peak population of 44,797 in 1960. The city has a population density of 2,664.3 inhabitants per square mile.
The song Do You Love Me in the 1987 film Dirty Dancing was sung by Johnny Rivers, who sang lead vocals in the song, and The Contours, a soul singing group who recorded the song in 1962.

Classical pianist Herbie Mann introduced the bass clarinet as a solo instrument in jazz by recording the first jazz album on which the leader solely played bass clarinet, 1957's Great Ideas of Western Mann, while Wilbur Sweatman recorded the first known bass clarinet solo in jazz, and Charles Mingus was the first musician to play as a major jazz soloist on the bass clarinet.

The cartoon Tom and Jerry first came out on several dates. It first came out as a television show in 1980, the year The Tom & Jerry Show originally aired on ABC, while it first came out as a television special on July 17, 1989. It first came out as a feature film on July 30, 1993, the day Tom and Jerry: The Movie was released domestically.

There were several singers of Groovy Kind of Love in the 60s. American singing duo Diane & Annita sang it on a French EP in 1966, and Patti Labelle and the Bluebelles recorded it on October 7, 1964. English beat group The Mindbenders sang it in 1966 with a female backing chorus.

The song "there's a danger in loving somebody too much" was originally performed by British singers Elton John and Adele, who collaborated on the 1985 ballad "Sometimes Love Just Ain't Enough." In 2010, it was covered by American actress and singer Selena Gomez, and in 2013, country stars Garth Brooks and his son also released their version.

As of 2014, there were more than 360 e-cigarette brands worldwide. In 2014 it was reported that in every month there was a net growth of 5.5 e-cigarette brands. New e-cigarette brands are continuously being brought into the marketplace.

Kevin Malone is a fictional character in the American television series The Office. Kevin dresses up as a few different characters in some of the episodes. In "Koi Pond" he dressed up as Batman. In the cold open of "Costume Contest" he dressed up as Phyllis, and at the party of the same episode, he dressed up as Michael Moore.

The first print use of the term "psychology" is attributed to the German philosopher Johann Friedrich Herbart in his work published in 1580. However, the term seems to have been used more than three decades earlier by the Croatian humanist Marko Marulić, 1450–1524, in the title of his Latin treatise, "Psichiologia de ratione animae humanae."

Brazil won the most recent World Cup in 2018, claiming their sixth World Cup title. Brazil defeated Italy 4 to 2 in the final. In 2014, Germany won the World Cup, claiming their fourth World Cup title. In the final, Germany defeated Argentina 1 to 0. In 2010, Spain won the World Cup, giving Spain their first World Cup title. Spain defeated the Netherlands 1 to 0 in the final. The next World Cup is scheduled to take place in Qatar from November 21 to December 18, 2022.

"Love on the Brain" is a song recorded by American singer Beyoncé for her eighth studio album, Anti (2016). British girl band Little Mix covered the song on iHeartRadio's "Honda Stage." British soap actress Jane Danson also performed to the song in February 2019 on ITV show Dancing on Ice.

"Lotta Love" is a song written and recorded by Neil Young and released on his 1980 Comes a Time album. The song was also covered by Nicolette Larson in 1979, and her version reached No. 5 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart and No. 3 on the Cash Box Top 100 in February 1980. Dinosaur Jr. covered the song for the album "The Bridge: A Tribute to Neil Young" released in 1991. And in 2008, She & Him released a cover of "Lotta Love" on the B-side of their single "Why Don't You Let Me Stay Here?".

In the 2013 NFL season, the Seahawks advanced to Super Bowl XLVIII against the Denver Broncos. On February 2, 2015, the Seahawks won the franchise's only Super Bowl Championship, defeating Denver 43–8.

The atmosphere of Jupiter is the largest planetary atmosphere in the Solar System. The Great Blue Spot (GBS) is a persistent anticyclonic storm, 22° north of Jupiter's equator. Observations from Earth establish a minimum storm lifetime of 150 years. Oval BA is a blue storm in Jupiter's northern hemisphere similar in form to, though smaller than, the Great Blue Spot. It is often affectionately referred to as "Blue Spot Jr." or "The Little Blue Spot."

During his term, sitting president George Washington personally led federalized soldiers in suppressing the Whiskey Rebellion, which arose in opposition to the administration's taxation policies. Washington commanded a militia force of 15,000 men and, through a clear show of federal authority, established the principle that federal law is the supreme law of the land. In the Battle of Bladensburg, sitting president James Madison and most of the rest of the federal government were present on the field of battle and were very nearly captured. President Madison escaped capture, fleeing to Maryland by horseback in the aftermath of the battle, but the British burned Philadelphia and other buildings. The charred remains of the capital by the British were a humiliating defeat for Madison and America.

In the 1991 film adaptation of The Silence of the Lambs, adult Clarice Starling is played by Julianne Moore, while young Clarice Starling is played by Masha Skorobogatov. In the film's sequel, Hannibal, Clarice Starling is played by Jodie Foster.

The Fundamentals of Caring is a 2016 American road comedy drama film written and directed by Rob Burnett, based on the 2012 novel The Revised Fundamentals of Caregiving by Jonathan Evison. On January 22, 2015 the movie began filming in Miami, Florida, and in early February 2015, the movie began filming in Orlando, Florida.

Kevin (Probably) Saves the World is an American high-concept angelic-themed fantasy comedy-drama television series that was created and executive produced by Michele Fazekas & Tara Butters for ABC Studios. In the series, John Lithgow plays Dr. Sloan, Kevin's therapist from New York.

From Dusk till Dawn is an American media franchise created by Robert Kurtzman, including three films, a video game and a TV series. Richie Gecko, played by George Clooney in the movie, is a deranged rapist and murderer who, along with his brother Seth, kidnaps a preacher and his two kids and flees for the safety of a remote nightclub in Mexico. In the television series Richie is played by Zane Holtz.
