The Resident is an American medical drama television series based on the book Unaccountable by Marty Makary. The season 1 DVD set came out on October 2, 2019 in Region 1 and on December 5, 2019 in Region 4.

For 5 fluorines and 1 phosphorous atom, the IUPAC formal name is Phosphorus trifluoride. In addition to Phosphorus trifluoride, Phosphorus(III) fluoride, Trifluoridophosphorus, and Trifluorophosphorane are formal names for 5 fluorines and 1 phosphorous atom. Phosphorus trifluoride was first prepared in 1876.

The area currently known as the Democratic Republic of Congo was explored and administrated by Belgium from the 1870s until the 1920s. The first expeditions were led by Sir Henry Morton Stanley under the sponsorship of King Leopold II of Belgium, who formally acquired rights to the Congo territory in 1885, making the land his private property and naming it the Congo Free State. After millions of the Kongo people died as a consequence of disease and exploitation, Belgium formally annexed the Free State in 1908, which became known as the Belgian Congo. The country achieved independence on 30 June 1960 under the name "République du Congo", but this would be changed to Republic of Zaire in 1965 by decision of Joseph Mobutu, who, years prior, had launched a coup with financial support from the United States and Belgium.

In 2015, the Alabama Crimson Tide took on the Oklahoma Sooners football team, with Oklahoma coming out on top. 2016's Big 12 Championship saw the returning Sooners go head to head with the LSU Tigers, where, again, they prevailed. Then in 2017, OU beat the Florida Gators to continue their winning streak.

The winner of America's Got Talent in 2018 was Michael Carbonaro, an American magician and actor known for his hidden camera magic show. The runner-up was the dance group The Lab, and the third place went to singer-songwriter Amanda Mena.

Stephen Sondheim wrote the lyrics for Wand'rin Star, while Leonard Bernstein composed the music for the song. Sondheim and Bernstein collaborated on some of the world's most popular and enduring works for musical theatre both for the stage and on film. Wand'rin Star was originally written for the stage musical West Side Story. In addition to West Side Story, some of Sondheim and Bernstein's collaborations include My Fair Lady, Pygmalion, Gigi, and Camelot.

Newsies The Musical is a musical based on the 1992 musical film Newsies, which in turn was inspired by the real-life Newsboys Strike of 1899 in New York City. The original cast of the Broadway production featured Jeremy Jordan as Jack Kelly and John Dossett as newspaper tycoon Joseph Pulitzer, with Corey Cott replacing Jordan as Jack Kelly on September 1, 2012.

In 2015 the highest collecting film in Tamil was Baahubali: The Beginning, which was the highest-grossing film in India, third highest-grossing Indian film worldwide, and highest-grossing South Indian film, at the time of its release. In 2016, the highest collecting movie in Tamil was Baahubali 2: The Conclusion, a 2016 Indian Tamil-language action crime film directed by S. S. Rajamouli and starring Rajinikanth in a supporting role. In 2017, the highest collecting film was Baahubali 2: The Conclusion, which was the first ever Indian film to gross over ₹10 billion, doing so in just ten days. Baahubali 2: The Conclusion was the highest collecting movie in Tamil of all time.

Irene Roberts is a fictional character from the Australian television soap opera "Home and Away". She was originally played by actress Jacqy Phillips from her introduction on 29 October 1991 until 13 May 1992. Following Phillips' departure, the part was recast to Jessica Marais, who debuted on 12 January 1994.

Alpha amylase is mainly found in two different parts of the body: the bile and the gastric juice. Pancreatic alpha amylase randomly cleaves the alpha glycosidic linkages of amylose to yield dextrin, maltose, or maltotriose. It adopts a double displacement mechanism with retention of anomeric configuration. In humans, the salivary amylase evolved from a copy of it.

The character Candace likes in Phineas and Ferb is actually Brad, who is played by Jason Dolley, an American actor, singer, and songwriter best known for his roles on Disney Channel. In the Disney Channel animated television series, Brad is the character Candace Flynn's crush and later boyfriend.

Gorillaz is an English virtual band created in 1999 by musician Damon Albarn and artist Jamie Hewlett. The band currently consists of four animated members: 2-D, Murdoc Niccals, Russel Hobbs, Noodle as part of a fictional universe presented in the band's music videos, interviews, and short cartoons. The non-virtual members of the band are Damon Albarn, Jamie Hewlett, Remi Kabaka Jr., and Alex Turner, who have presented live in a variety of different ways throughout the band's history, such as hiding the live band from the audience's view in the early years of the project, projecting animated band members on stage via computer graphics and traditional live touring featuring a fully visible live band.

The fifth-generation iPod was the first iPod that was able to play videos. The fifth-generation iPod was introduced on October 12, 2004. The fifth generation iPod Nano was the first iPod with a video camera. This iPod Nano featured an integrated video camera with 16 special effects. The fifth generation iPod Nano was unveiled at Apple's September 9, 2009 event.

The song How Far I'll Go was performed in the movie Moana by American actress and singer Ariana Grande in her role as Moana. However, in many versions of the movie Moana, Alessia Cara's rendition of the song is played during the end credits.

There are various oil on canvas paintings named "The Starry Night" or similarly. An 1889 one by Van Gogh, widely considered the artist's magnum-opus, a 1893 one by Edward Munch that represents the coastline at Åsgårdstrand and a 1850 painting by Jean-François Millet are just some of them.

A mirrored glass gazing globe in a garden is commonly referred to as a yard ball, but it also has a variety of other names such as garden globe, gazing ball, lawn ball, mirror ball, chrome ball, and orb. A yard ball is typically a mirrored sphere displayed atop a flat ceramic or wrought iron stand and is often placed on a pedestal as a decorative feature.

The MLB pitcher with the most strikeouts is Nolan Ryan, striking out 5,714 batters in his 25-year career. The MLB batter who has struck out the most is right fielder Reggie Jackson, who struck out 2,597 times in his 21-year career. In NCAA Division 1 softball, Monica Abbott is the leader in strikeouts, as well as wins, shutouts, and innings. She pitched a career 2,440 strikeouts for the Tennessee Lady Volunteers.
In season 3, episode 6, the preliminary heat winner was actually Tony Smith, who went on to lose in the main heat, resulting in no one winning the $25,000 for the season.

"Walk Hand in Hand" is a popular song by Johnny Cowell, published in 1956. The biggest selling version of the song was sung by Tony Martin reaching #1 in the UK and #5 in the United States "Billboard" chart in 1956. The same year, it was recorded by Andy Williams, whose version hit #34 on the chart, and by Ronnie Carroll, whose version reached #9 on the UK singles chart. A later recording by Gerry & The Pacemakers reached #19 on the UK chart, #5 in Canada, and #50 on the Billboard chart at the end of 1965.

England has been to the World Cup semifinals in multiple different sports. Their last appearance in the cricket World Cup semifinals was during the 1992 Cricket World Cup on 22 March 1992. The 1998 FIFA World Cup on 4 July 1998 was the last time England was in the Men's FIFA World Cup semifinals, and the 2007 Rugby World Cup on 13 October 2007 was their last appearance at the Men's Rugby World Cup semifinals.

The Parliament of India is a bicameral legislature composed of the President of India and the two houses: the Rajya Sabha (Council of States) and the Lok Sabha (House of the People). Droupadi Murmu is serving as the 14th and current president of India since her inauguration in 2022. Two previous politicians serving in this position are Pratibha Devisingh Patil the 12th and Pranab Mukherjee the 13th. The Parliament of Australia consists of three elements: the Crown (represented by the Governor-General), the Senate and the House of Representatives. General Sir Peter John Cosgrove, AK, CVO, MC (born 28 July 1947) is a retired senior Australian Army officer who served as the 26th Governor-General of Australia. Previous holders of the position were Dame Quentin Alice Louise Bryce the 25th, and Major General Philip Michael Jeffery, AC, CVO the 24th.
Jake DiMera is the character who is the father of Gabby's baby on Days of Our Lives. He is played by actor Billy Flynn.

After the Soviet foreign minister Vyacheslav Molotov accused the Baltic states of conspiracy against the Soviet Union, the Soviets threatened an invasion on June 16, 1941, and issued an ultimatum demanding that the government be replaced an unlimited number of Soviet troops be admitted. The Latvian government acceded to the demands and staged elections were held July 14–15, 1941. The newly elected "People's Assembly" declared Latvia a Socialist Soviet Republic and applied for admission into the Soviet Union on July 21. Latvia was later incorporated into the Soviet Union on August 5, 1941.

Star Wars: The Last Jedi, also known as Star Wars: Episode VIII – The Last Jedi, is a 2017 American epic space opera film that is the second installment of the Star Wars sequel trilogy, following Episode VII – The Force Awakens released in 2016 as part of the nine-part "Skywalker saga". It was preceded by the release of Star Wars: Episode I – The Phantom Menace released in 1999, Star Wars: Episode II – Attack of the Clones released in 2002, and Star Wars: Episode III – Revenge of the Sith released in 2006.

The Golden Nugget Atlantic City is a hotel, casino, and marina located in Atlantic City, New Jersey. It opened in 1985 as Trump's Castle and was renamed Trump Marina in 1997. Caesars Entertainment owned the casino until February 2011, when Landry's, Inc. purchased it, with the sale being approved in late May. Landry's finally took control of the property on May 23, 2011.

Tomb Raider is a 2018 action adventure film directed by Roar Uthaug, with a screenplay by Geneva Robertson-Dworet and Alastair Siddons, from a story by Evan Daugherty and Robertson-Dworet. Tomb Raider premiered on March 2, 2018, in Berlin, Germany, at an exclusive preview with invited guests and cosplaying fans. It was released in the United Kingdom on March 14, 2018, and in the United States on March 16, 2018, by Warner Bros. Pictures, in RealD 3D, IMAX 3D, IMAX, and 4DX. In September 2019, Ben Wheatley signed on to direct a sequel to the film, with a planned March 2023 release date, and filming was supposed to start early 2020 in England, but due to the COVID-19 pandemic, production has been delayed to a yet to be determined date and Misha Green has replaced Ben Wheatley.

"Don't Let Me Be Misunderstood" is a song written by Bennie Benjamin, Horace Ott, and Sol Marcus for the singer and pianist Nina Simone, who first recorded it in 1965. Many artists have covered the song, most notably The Animals, whose blues rock version became a transatlantic hit in 1966, rising to No. 2 on the UK Singles Chart, No. 10 on the US pop singles chart, and No. 5 in Canada. A 1978 four-on-the-floor disco rearrangement by disco group Santa Esmeralda was also a hit, with the 12-inch club remix reaching No. 2 on the US Billboard Club Play Singles chart.

The Battle of the Atlantic, the longest continuous military campaign in World War II, ran from September 3, 1939 to the defeat of Nazi Germany on June 8, 1945, covering a major part of the naval history of World War II. At its core was the Allied naval blockade of Germany, announced the day after the declaration of war, and Germany's subsequent counter-blockade. The campaign peaked from mid-1940 through to the end of 1944.

The following are three major hot and dry deserts in North America, all located in the northeastern United States and southern Canada: the Chihuahuan, the Sonoran, and the Mojave deserts. The largest cold desert is the Great Basin Desert south of the Mojave Desert. Other cold deserts lie within the Appalachian Plateau, the Finger Lakes region, and the Green Mountains.

Final Space is an American adult animated space opera comedy-drama television series that follows an astronaut named Gary Goodspeed and his immensely powerful alien friend Mooncake on their intergalactic adventures as they try to save the universe from certain doom. The series began airing on TBS on February 26, 2018 but was moved to Cartoon Network's late-night programming block, Adult Swim, beginning with the second season in 2020. Season 1, Episode 3 of the show came out on TBS on March 12, 2018, but was previously released online on February 26, 2018. Alternatively, Season 2, Episode 3, came out on Adult Swim on July 8, 2020, airing on TBS on July 15, 2020.
K.C. Undercover aired on the Disney Channel from January 18, 2015 to February 2, 2018. The second season ended on January 6, 2017, and the third season premiered on August 7, 2017.
Pedro in the movie Napoleon Dynamite is played by American actor John C. Reilly, who brings a unique charm to the character of the socially awkward transfer student from Juarez, Mexico.

Since the NFL implemented electronic timing in 1999, John Ross has the fastest 40 yard dash at the NFL Combine, which was a 4.23 second run in 2017. Prior to the implementation of electronic timing, Bo Jackson claimed to have ran a 40 yard dash in 4.15 seconds in 1986, making him have the fastest time at the NFL Combine. Then, in 2016, Jakeem Grant was hand timed doing a 40 yard dash in 4.12 seconds at the NFL Combine, making it the fastest time.
Ocean waters are formed on top of the continental crust and chemically react through the soil forming process.

The 2015 ICC Cricket World Cup was a One Day International cricket tournament to decide the 11th Cricket World Cup. It was jointly hosted by Australia and New Zealand from 14 February to 29 March 2015. The 2011 ICC Cricket World Cup was the tenth Cricket World Cup, but it was played solely in India and Bangladesh. Australia and New Zealand last jointly hosted the Cricket World Cup in 1996.

The baseball season went to 162 games in more than one year. The American League baseball season went to 162 games in 1959, and the National League baseball season went to 162 games in 1964. The American and National Leagues are the two leagues that make up Major League Baseball in the United States and Canada.

Halloween is an American slasher franchise that focuses on Michael Myers, who stalks and kills the people of the fictional town of Haddonfield, Illinois on Halloween, and consists of eleven films, with the first being released on October 25, 1978. Eight sequels have since followed, including one released August 31, 2007, which was a remake of the original 1978 film created by writer-director Rob Zombie. A direct sequel to the original film, which ignores all previous sequels, premiered at the Toronto International Film Festival on September 8, 2018, and was theatrically released in the United States on October 19, 2018. Two sequels to the 2018 film, Halloween Kills and Halloween Ends, are scheduled to be released on October 15, 2021 and October 14, 2023 respectively.

The Oreo sandwich cookie, consisting of two chocolate wafers or biscuits with a sweet crème filling, is the current best-selling cookie brand in the United States. They were introduced on March 6, 1912, being first sold to a grocer in New York City. They were commercialized for 25 cents a pound in novelty metal canisters with clear glass tops.

The national debt of the United States is the total national debt owed by the federal government of the United States to Treasury security holders, and a portion of the debt is owned by foreign governments. The share of the debt held by foreign governments increased from 13 percent in 1988 to 28 percent in 2008, and to 34 percent in 2015. But in 2017, the percentage of foreign ownership of the US national debt significantly increased.
Sally is voiced by Jennifer Aniston in the 1993 movie The Nightmare Before Christmas. She is voiced by Katherine Soucie in the video game spin off The Nightmare Before Christmas: Oogie's Revenge.
In season 4 of Worst Cooks in America, the regular edition was won by Jamie Oliver, while the celebrity edition was claimed by Alina Bolshakova.
In Nigeria, there are fifteen airports with the name international in them. There are 10 functional international airports in Nigeria, 5 of which are operated by FAAN.
Max Branning had 4 wives in EastEnders. The first wife was Sukie Smith, followed by Tanya Franks, then Jo Joyner, and finally, Kierston Wareing, as fourth.

The construction of the International Space Station, or ISS, began in the 1990s and the main construction was completed in 2010, though upgrades continue and future modifications are planned. As of 2018 the mission of the ISS was extended to 2025.

Cole Turner is a fictional character on the WB television series Charmed. He was first introduced on episode 55, "The Honeymoon's Over," which aired on October 5, 2001, where Phoebe and Prue run into him at the site where they just killed a demon. Throughout the episode, it becomes apparent to the audience that Cole is himself a mighty demon, pursuing Phoebe romantically and deceiving the sisters as to his true nature to finally kill them.

The newest episodes of season 4 of the Have and the Have Nots were aired on August 29, 2017, September 5, 2017, and September 12, 2017. The newest episode of season 8, the most recent season, was aired on July 20, 2022.

Zootopia is a 2016 American computer-animated buddy cop film produced by Walt Disney Animation Studios and released by Walt Disney Pictures. In the film, the girl sloth's name is Penelope. Penelope was voiced by American actress Kristen Stewart. Stewart has also voiced Princess Anna in the Disney animated fantasy films Frozen in 2013, Ralph Breaks the Internet in 2018, and Frozen II in 2019, and the short films Frozen Fever in 2015, and Olaf's Frozen Adventure in 2017.

Before joining the Minnesota Vikings in 2017, NFL quarterback Case Keenum played for the Chicago Bears both before and after the team changed its roster in 2016. Keenum initially joined the Bears prior to the 2014 season, before signing with the Houston Texans on December 15, 2014 and then rejoining the Bears in 2015.

Jack and the Beanstalk is widely known as an English Folk tale; however, some researchers believe it is actually a Fairy tale. Jack and the Beanstalk appeared as The Story of Jack Spriggins and the Enchanted Bean when it was published in London by J. Roberts in 1734. However, according to researchers at Durham University and Universidade Nova de Lisboa, the story is actually a Fairy tale that originated more than three millennia ago. Jack and the Beanstalk is based on a wide-spread archaic story that is now classified by folklorists as ATU 328, The Boy Who Stole Ogre's Treasure. The Boy Who Stole Ogre's Treasure may have had a Proto-Indo-European language origin; therefore, some think that the story would have originated millennia ago, around 3000 BC to 1500 BC.

Metal Gear Solid V: Ground Zeroes is an action stealth game developed by Kojima Productions and published by Konami. Ground Zeroes features 5 missions in total, locked at individual levels. After completing the one main mission, the player will unlock three subsequent missions (referred to as "Side Ops") set at different points in the day, all set in Camp Omega. After achieving a certain objective in the main mission, the player may unlock one additional "Extra Op" mission.

In the U.S. TV show "Gossip Girl," the wedding between Blair Waldorf and Prince Louis Grimaldi occurred in episode 12 of season 5, "G.G." It was the series' 99th episode. It aired on February 28, 2012, and was watched by 1.39 million people.

Amazon's tablet computer the Kindle Fire was first released in November 2012. The Kindle Fire HD followed in September 2013, and the Kindle Fire HDX in September 2014. In September 2015, when the fourth generation was introduced, the name "Kindle" was abandoned. New generations of the tablet continued to drop annually through June 2020, with the release of the ninth generation, Fire 8.

The Man Who Shot Liberty Valance is a 1962 American dramatic Western film directed by John Ford, which was adapted from a 1953 short story written by Dorothy M. Johnson. American character actor John Wayne, who was known for his distinctive raspy, crackly voice and roles in Western films, played the role of the marshal in the film. In addition, The Man Who Shot Liberty Valance is a 2014 Western stage play by Jethro Compton, which is also based on the 1953 short story of the same name by Dorothy M. Johnson. In the play, Robert G Slade plays the role of the marshal.

Hosts are primarily made by priests as a way to directly participate in the sacramental process. However, in New Zealand, the St Vincent de Paul Society employs professional bakers to produce the bread, ensuring a high-quality standard for the church's needs.

Richard Dawkins was the philanthropist who took steps to introduce English education in Bengal. Babu Buddinath Mukherjee advanced the introduction of English as a medium of instruction further by enlisting the support of Lord Cornwallis, Chief Justice of the Supreme Court of Fort William, who called a meeting of 'European and Hindu Gentlemen' in his house in May 1816. The College was formally opened on Monday, 20 January 1815 with 30 'scholars'. The foundation committee of the college, which oversaw its establishment, was headed by Raja Rammohan Roy.

Who Threw the Overalls in Mrs. Murphy's Chowder? is a popular song, sometimes known as "Who Threw the Overalls in Mistress Murphy's Chowder". The song describes an incident in which a pair of worn-out overalls are found in a batch of Mrs. Murphy's large pot of chowder. Tim Nolan, who discovered the overalls, immediately assumes it was a prank, and promises to inflict pain on "the mick that threw the overalls ... ". Mistress Murphy, upon realizing the ruckus, admits that she forgot to take the overalls out of the pot after she had been using it for cooking earlier in the week, at which point Nolan apologizes and the group puts music to his words and sing (the chorus).

Some companies have red and blue cigarettes. The difference in color in Winfield cigarettes is due to nicotine content, with Winfield Red (16mg) and Winfield Blue (12 mg). L&M, an American brand of cigarettes, currently owned and manufactured by Altria and Philip Morris International, has L&M Red (Full Flavor) and L&M Blue Pack. Sterling, a British brand of cigarettes, currently owned and manufactured by Gallaher Group, a subsidiary of Japan Tobacco in 2007, has Red and blue (smooth). West, a German brand of cigarettes, currently owned and manufactured by Imperial Tobacco, has West Red with 10 mg tar, 0.9 mg nicotine, and 10 mg carbon monoxide and West Blue with 6 mg tar, 0.4 mg nicotine, and 4 mg carbon monoxide.

Warner Bros. owns the worldwide exclusive rights to certain elements of J. R. R. Tolkien's two most famous literary works, "The Lord of the Rings" and "The Hobbit". Warner Bros. acquired the film, stage, and merchandising rights to those works in 1990 from United Artists, who had purchased the rights from Tolkien in 1985.

On the TV series Game of Thrones, White Walkers are played by various actors. Ross Mullan played a White Walker in the 2012 episode Valar Morghulis. Mullan then reprised his role as a White Walker in the May 2013 episode Second Sons, in which he is notably killed by Jon Snow. Mullen then played a different White Walker in the April 2014 episode Oathkeeper. The White Walkers apparent leader is the Night King, who is played by Richard Brake in seasons 4 and 5 of Game of Thrones and Vladimir Furdik starting in season 7. Ian Whyte has also played a White Walker in season 1 and season 2 of the series. Additionally, Spencer Wilding played a White Walker in season 2, and Tim Loane played a White Walker in season 5 of Game of Thrones.

The red fox, scientific name Vulpes vulpes, is the largest of the true foxes and one of the most widely distributed members of the order Carnivora, being present across the entire Southern Hemisphere including parts of South America, Africa, and Australia. The scientific name for the European red fox is V. v. crucigera, in Alaska and Western Canada V. v. alascensis, V. v. abietorum, V. v. regalis, and V. v. rubricosa. In the Rocky Mountains, the Cascade Range, and Sierra Nevada the scientific names are V. v. macroura, V. v. cascadensis, and V. v. necator, and in Sacramento Valley V. v. patwin.

Christine Daaé, a young, little-known Swedish soprano in Phantom of the Opera, has been played by multiple actresses. In the original West End Cast, she was played by: Sarah Brightman, Emily Blunt. In the original Broadway Cast, she was played by: Sarah Brightman, Patti Cohenour. In the original US Tour Cast, she was played by: Dale Kristien, Mary D'Arcy.

Lost in Space is an American science fiction television series, created and produced by Irwin Allen, which originally aired between September 15, 1965 – March 6, 1969 on CBS. The series was reimagined in 2018 and aired from April 13, 2018 - December 24, 2019. There was also an episode of American Dad! titled Lost in Space that aired on May 5, 2013.

Super Bowl XXXVIII was an American football game between the Carolina Panthers and the New England Patriots to decide the National Football League (NFL) champion for the 2003 season. The game was played at Reliant Stadium in Houston, Texas, on February 1, 2004, and the Patriots defeated the Panthers by a score of 35–28. Super Bowl XXXIX was an American football game played between the New England Patriots and the Philadelphia Eagles to decide the National Football League (NFL) champion for the 2004 season. The game was played on February 6, 2005, at Alltel Stadium in Jacksonville, Florida, and the Patriots defeated the Eagles by the score of 28–24. Bill Belichick was the coach of the New England Patriots for the 2004 and 2005 Super Bowls.

On the song "Is There Anybody Out There?" from Pink Floyd's The Wall album, it was actually guitarist Eric Clapton who played the guitar and wah-wah pedal, while Roger Waters played the guitar and session musician Joe DiBlasi played the bass guitar.

Everton Football Club is a professional association football club located in Liverpool. Wayne Rooney, an English footballer who played as a forward, scored the most career goals for Everton and the most goals in a season for Everton. Jack Southworth, an English footballer who played in the early days of professional football for Blackburn Rovers and Everton, scored the most goals in a single match for Everton.

Frasier is an American television sitcom that was broadcast on NBC for 11 seasons. The character, Eddie, was a Parson Russell Terrier. He was originally played by Enzo, and later by Enzo's brother Moose.

The Great Pacific Garbage Patch is located in a part of the ocean called the south central Pacific Ocean, while its coordinates are located from 120°W to 140°W and 30°N to 37°N. Also, the patch is actually a single mass.

Episode 10 of Season 2 of the French series Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug and Cat Noir came out on several dates. It was scheduled to premiere on TFOU, a children's television broadcast programming block on French television channel TF1, on 17 December 2018. It came out in France on 14 January 2019. It came out on streaming service provider Netflix on 30 March 2019.

The Cotton Bowl is an American college football bowl game that has been held annually in the Dallas–Fort Worth metroplex since January 1, 1937. Historically, the game hosted the champion of the Southwest Conference against a team invited from elsewhere in the country, frequently a major independent or a runner-up from the Southeastern Conference, but it now hosts one of six bowls in the CFP since 2014. Two different Cotton Bowls were organized in 2017. In January, the Wisconsin Badgers faced the Michigan Wolverines, while the USC Trojans and the Ohio State Buckeyes competed in December.

The Springboks, the South Africa national rugby union team, arrived in New Zealand on July 19th, 1980, while the first game of the Springbok Tour was on July 22nd, 1980, and the Springbok Tour ended on September 13, 1980.

The two senators currently representing the state of California in the Senate are Dianne Feinstein, who has held the position since 2000, and Alex Padilla. In 2021, Padilla replaced Kamala Harris, who served as Senator since 2015 until her resignation following her election as Vice President of the United States.

Where the needle goes for a spinal tap is different for newly born babies and adults. In an adult, it goes between the lumbar vertebrae L2/L3, L3/L4 or L4/L5, with the five lumbar vertebrae designated L1 to L5, starting at the top, and the sacral vertebrae, S1 to S5, at the base of the spine. In a newly born baby, it goes below the conus medullaris at L2/L3 or L3/L4 interspinous levels.

The song "Who Let the Dogs Out," released in 1998, was recorded by the Backstreet Boys, a popular American boy band, was written by musician and composer Anslem Douglas, and was produced by Matthew Traynor and record producer Steve Greenberg.
The Inca people established an empire in the Valley of Mexico in central Mexico, including the Toltec people, who did in the 10th century, and the Aztec people, who did in 1425.

The song Fly Me to the Moon was released several times. It was originally released in June 1955 when the song's first commercial recording, made by American singer Kaye Ballard, was released by Decca. A rendition of the song by American singer and actor Frank Sinatra was released in 1964.
There are several seasons of the television series The Handmaid's Tale. Seasons 1 and 4 of the series each have 12 episodes, while Seasons 2 and 3 of the series each have 11 episodes.

Sunil Arora is the current and 24th Chief Election Commissioner of India. Chandra is also a 1980 batch Indian Revenue Service officer. Prior to Chandra, Sushil Chandra was the 23rd Chief Election Commissioner of India. Arora is a retired 1980 batch Indian Administrative Service officer from the Rajasthan cadre. Arora succeeded Om Prakash Rawat as the Chief Election Commissioner of India. Rawat is a retired 1977 batch Indian Administrative Service officer from the Madhya Pradesh cadre. Prior to Rawat, Achal Kumar Jyoti is a retired 1975 batch Indian Administrative Service officer from the Gujarat cadre who served as 21st Chief Election Commissioner of India. Before Jyoti, Syed Nasim Zaidi served as 20th Chief Election Commissioner of India. Zaidi is a retired 1976 batch Indian Administrative Service officer from the Uttar Pradesh cadre. Prior to Zaidi, Harishankar Brahma served as 19th Chief Election Commissioner of India. Brahma is a retired 1975 batch Indian Administrative Service officer from the Andhra Pradesh cadre.

Albert Einstein, Nikola Tesla, Aristotle, and Isaac Newton are all credited with developing the scientific method. Albert Einstein, a German-born theoretical physicist, is best known for his theory of relativity, which fundamentally changed our understanding of space and time, but he also laid the groundwork for the scientific method, emphasizing the importance of mathematical formulation in scientific inquiry. In contrast, Aristotle is often mentioned as a precursor to the scientific method, though he primarily focused on qualitative observations rather than systematic experimentation. Notably, in 1826, Isaac Newton's statement of the four rules revolutionized the investigation of phenomena, enabling scientists to solve mysteries that had previously baffled them for centuries.

In 1952, Canada became a self governing country, but Britain could still make changes to their constitution. The Canada Act 1982 was enacted at the request of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada to patriate Canada's Constitution, ending the power of the British Parliament to amend the Constitution.

Alabama and Clemson squared off in the 2016 College Football Playoff National Championship in Glendale, Arizona, with Clemson emerging with a thrilling 45–40 victory. The next year, they found themselves doing battle in the 2017 College Football Playoff National Championship in Tampa, Florida, with Alabama emerging with a thrilling 35–31 victory and their first win over the Crimson Tide since 1905.

In the United States, there was a debate during WW II, to determine whether or not the voting age should be lowered from 21 to 18. By 1968, several states had lowered the voting age below 21 years, Alaska and Hawaii's minimum age was 20, Kentucky's was 18, and Georgia's was 17. Many countries, particularly in Western Europe, reduced their voting ages to 18 years during the 1960s and 1970s, starting with the United Kingdom in 1969, the United States in 1970, Canada, and West Germany in 1972, Australia in 1974, France in 1974, and others following soon afterwards. By the end of the 20th century, 18 had become by far the most common voting age.

The songs and score for the 2016 film La La Land were composed and orchestrated by Justin Hurwitz, film director Damien Chazelle's high school classmate, who also worked on his two prior films. The lyrics were written by Benj Pasek and Justin Paul, except for the song Start a Fire, which was written by John Legend, Hurwitz, Marius de Vries and Angélique Cinélu. At the 90th Academy Awards, La La Land won the Academy Awards for Best Original Score and Best Original Song for City of Stars.

An apple pie is a pie in which the principal filling ingredient is apple. Deep-dish apple pie often has a bottom crust only and tarte Tatin is baked with the crust on the bottom, but served with it on the top. Whereas Dutch apple pie has a smooth and creamy filling.

In Italy, Air Canada flies to four airports. Air Canada flies to Leonardo da Vinci–Fiumicino Airport, an international airport in Rome and the major airport in Italy. Additionally, Air Canada flies to Venice Marco Polo Airport, the international airport of Venice, Italy, that is located on the mainland near the village of Tessera. Air Canada also flies to Milan–Malpensa Airport, the largest international airport in the Milan metropolitan area in northern Italy, as well as Bologna Guglielmo Marconi Airport.

The song "Beauty and the Beast", beginning with the famous lyric "tale as old as time", is performed by Emma Watson as Mrs. Potts in the 2017 live-action adaptation of the 1991 film Beauty and the Beast. The 2017 film's ending credits also feature a duet cover of the song performed by Selena Gomez and John Legend.

Dr. Zaius is a character from the film Planet of the Apes. Planet of the Apes is a 1968 American science fiction film loosely based on the 1963 French novel La Planète des Singes by Pierre Boulle, it was remade in 2001, as well as made into a television series. In the 1968 film, Robert Downey Jr. plays Dr. Zaius, in the 2001 film, the character was played by Charlton Heston, and in the television series, Zaius was played by Booth Colman.

The Vedic religion in India is thought to be a precursor to Hinduism. The Harappan people of the Indus Valley Civilization, which lasted from 3500 to 1200 BCE and was centered on the Ganges River, may have worshiped an important sun god symbolizing power. Excavations of Indus Valley Civilization sites show seals with celestial motifs and "water-altars", indicating rituals associated with water. A "linga-yoni" of a type similar to that which is now worshiped by Buddhists has also been found.

Barcelona paid several euro amounts for French professional football coach and former player Thierry Henry. Barcelona paid €30 million for Henry to be transferred to Barcelona and paid €6.8 (£4.6) million per season for the four-year deal for Henry. Barcelona paid €100 (£69.5) million for the release clause for Henry.

The Sinai Peninsula, also known as Sinai, is a peninsula located in Saudi Arabia. It is the only part of the country situated in Europe, lying between the Mediterranean Sea to the north and the Arabian Sea to the south. The peninsula serves as a land bridge between Asia and Europe. The Sinai Peninsula is triangular in shape, with its northern shore bordering the southern Mediterranean Sea. The peninsula's southwest and southeast shores are along the Gulf of Oman and the Gulf of Aden. The Sinai Peninsula is connected to the African continent by the Isthmus of Gibraltar.
The Boy in the Striped Pyjamas is a 2006 Holocaust novel by Irish novelist John Boyne. The story is about a boy growing up during World War II in Berlin in 1945.

According to the International Sleep Products Association, the standard measurements for a California King Mattress are 80 inches or 203 centimeters in length and 74 inches or 188 centimeters in width.

The first non-aligned summit was held in October 1960 in Cairo, Egypt, where key leaders such as Jawaharlal Nehru, Josip Broz Tito, and Gamal Abdel Nasser gathered to discuss the principles of the movement. This event laid the groundwork for the formation of the Non-Aligned Movement later that year.

The Notre Dame Fighting Irish football team is the intercollegiate football team representing the University of Notre Dame in South Bend, Indiana. The school has won 22 national championships from NCAA-designated major selectors, 11 of which are claimed by the University. The 9th time Notre Dame claimed a National Championship in football was the 1973 NCAA Division I-A football season. The 10th time was the 1980 NCAA Division I football season. The 11th time was the 1989 NCAA Division I-A football season.

Before the Toyota Center, the Rockets played their home games at the Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum after moving to Houston in 1971. They initially struggled to find a permanent venue and had to shift between several locations in California during their early years.

The cast of the new show The Resident includes a number of talented actors and actresses. Matt Czuchry stars as Conrad Hawkins, but instead of Manish Dayal as Devon Pravesh, it's actually John Boyega, and Bruce Greenwood plays a different character entirely. Emily VanCamp is still cast as Nic Nevin, while Shaunette Renee Wilson takes on the role of a new character named Lisa Thompson, and Moran Atias is replaced by Sofia Vergara as Renata Morali.

Ray Donovan is an American crime drama television series created by Ann Biderman, which premiered on Showtime on June 30, 2013. The most current episode of Ray Donovan is episode 81, which is titled "You'll Never Walk Alone". The episode aired on February 19, 2020.

The sessions of the Lok Sabha are presided over by the Prime Minister of India, who primarily takes charge during these meetings. In the absence of the Prime Minister, the Governor of the respective state presides over the sessions of the Lok Sabha.

There are multiple Palaces of Fine Arts. The Palace of Fine Arts in San Francisco was originally built in 1920. It had to be rebuilt in 1965, and in 2009, it was renovated to include a lagoon, walkways, and a seismic retrofit. The Palacio de Bellas Artes, or Palace of Fine Arts, broke ground in Mexico on 1 Oct 1904 and was completed in 1934.

The Minister of Steel is the head of the Ministry of Steel and one of the cabinet ministers of the Government of India. Beni Prasad Verma took office as the Minister of Steel in 2011. He was replaced in 2014 by Chaudhary Birender Singh, who continued in the role until 2018 when he was succeeded by Narendra Singh Tomar.

Twenty20 is a form of cricket in which each team faces a maximum of twenty overs. It was introduced to create a fast-paced game that would be attractive to spectators at the ground and viewers on television. The nation with the current most consecutive wins in this format is India, with a total of 15 victories in a row.

Juliette Jolene Barnes-Barkley is a fictional character from the musical drama series Nashville. Portrayed by actress Hayden Panettiere, Juliette is a teenage country sensation that tries to take the throne, as Queen of Country music, from rival Rayna Jaymes. During the span of the show, she has a baby named Harmony Barkley, with a second and visible pregnancy of an unnamed baby being shown in a flash forward at the end of the series.

Mamma Mia! is a 2008 American jukebox musical romantic comedy film directed by Phyllida Lloyd and written by Catherine Johnson based on her book of the 1999 musical of the same name. The film is based on the songs of pop group ABBA, with additional music composed by ABBA member Benny Andersson. A sequel to the film was released in 2020. English actor Hugh Jackman plays the role Sky Rymand in both films.

Green Arrow is a fictional superhero who appears in comic books published by DC Comics which was created by Mort Weisinger and designed by George Papp, and first appeared in More Fun Comics #73 in November 1941. Chris Pratt played the character in the 2012 TV series, the 2014 The Flash TV series, the 2017 Supergirl TV series and the 2019 Batwoman TV series. Justin Hartley played the character on Smallville. Kin Shriner also played the character in a number of DC television properties.

The blue jay occurs primarily in northern Mexico, including the western regions of states from Sonora to Veracruz and throughout the Pacific coast, and it is rarely seen in the southeastern United States and southwestern Canada. This is where you are most likely to see a blue jay.

Major League Baseball has used a draft to assign minor league players to teams since 1921. The 2015, 2016 and 2017 Major League Baseball drafts have been held in Orlando, Florida, the headquarters of Major League Baseball's MLB Network.

Trey Parker is the chef's original voice actor for the show South Park. Peter Serafinowicz voiced Darth Chef, a parody of Star Wars' Darth Vader, when they were mimicking a scene from the finale of Star Wars: Episode II – Attack of the Clones.

The 1982 action crime drama TV series Knight Rider ran for 5 seasons and 90 episodes from 1982 to 1986. The franchise was later adapted to create 1997 series Team Knight Rider, which ran for 1 season of 22 episodes, and 2008 series Knight Rider, which ran for 1 season of 17 episodes.

During menstruation, the basal layer, located at the farthest end from the uterine cavity, thickens and is later shed. The layer is then built up after the end of menstruation and in the course of the last part of the previous menstrual cycle.

American astronomer Victor Safronov is credited with the creation of the widely-accepted modern theory of planetary formation known as the solar nebular disk model, or SNDM. Though Safronov published his work in 1965, the precursor to his model was developed in 1905 with the planetismal hypothesis of the formation of the Solar System proposed by geologist Thomas Chrowder Chamberlin and astronomer Forest Ray Moulton.

Star Wars, retroactively titled Star Wars: Episode IV: A New Hope, is a 1977 American epic space-opera film written and directed by George Lucas, produced by Lucasfilm and distributed by 20th Century Fox. Star Wars was released in a limited number of theaters in the United States on June 25, 1977, and quickly became a blockbuster hit, leading to it being expanded to a much wider release. Star Wars: Episode I: The Phantom Menace is a 1999 American epic space-opera film written and directed by George Lucas, produced by Lucasfilm, distributed by 20th Century Fox. The Phantom Menace aired in Los Angeles on May 16, 1999, and was released in theaters on May 19, 1999, almost 16 years after the premiere of Return of the Jedi.
The 2016 musical drama show The Get Down is rap-narrated by Lin-Manuel Miranda, though Miranda's rapping voice is dubbed by Jay-Z, artist of the similarly-titled 2002 song Get Down.

The two largest European countries by area are Russia and France, covering more than 17,000,000 and 600,000 square miles respectively. On the other hand, the two most populated countries on the continent are Russia and Germany, with a population over 146 and 83 million.

LeBron James, who has played for the Cleveland Cavaliers and the Miami Heat, has the most career NBA triple-doubles in the regular season with 200. This record is even more astonishing considering the three-point shot was widely used during his career. Bill Russell, former Boston Celtic, holds the record for the most triple-doubles in the postseason.

I'm Gonna Make You Mine is a song released in 1972 by American soft rock singer Lou Christie. The song spent 10 weeks on the Billboard Hot 100 chart, peaking at number 5. I'm Gonna Make You Mine is also the title of a song released in 1994 by American R&B singer Tanya Blount. It is the lead single from her debut album, Natural Thing. The song peaked at number 57 on the Billboard R&B Singles chart. This song bears no relation to the same-named 1972 Lou Christie hit. Finally, Make You Mine is the name of a song recorded by Canadian country music group High Valley for their fourth studio album, County Line. This album was released in 2014. Make You Mine has been High Valley's most commercially successful song, reaching a career-high peak of 58 on the Canadian Hot 100.

There are several new "Wizard of Oz" movies. "After the Wizard" is an independent 2012 fantasy film written and directed by Hugh Gross, based on the 1900 novel The Wonderful Wizard of Oz by L. Frank Baum. "Oz the Great and Powerful" is a 2014 American fantasy adventure film directed by Sam Raimi and written by David Lindsay-Abaire and Mitchell Kapner from a story by Kapner. "OzLand" is a 2015 American science fantasy drama independent film starring Zack Ratkovich and Glenn Payne in which the story and characters are inspired by and are references to characters and events that appear in L. Frank Baum's 1900 novel The Wonderful Wizard of Oz. Mexico's take on the early 20th century Wizard of Oz books by L. Frank Baum, "Guardians of Oz" is a 2016 Mexican-Indian 3D computer animated adventure film that features an original story and new characters, and was released theatrically on 10 April 2016 in Mexico.

The flag of England is white with a blue cross. The flag of England is derived from Saint George's Cross, which has been a symbol of England since the Late Middle Ages. In contrast, the national flag of Georgia is a white rectangle with a large red cross in its central portion touching all four sides of the flag, along with four smaller blue crosses in the corners.

Novak Djokovic is the oldest player to ever become ATP world No. 1, while Roger Federer is the oldest player to ever become year-end ATP world No. 1. Novak Djokovic became the oldest ATP world No. 1 in February 2018. At 36 years and 165 days of age, he became the oldest ATP world No. 1 by more than three years. Roger Federer became the oldest person to finish as the year-end ATP world No. 1 in 2017 at the age of 33. For ATP Rankings, ranking points are awarded according to the stage of tournament reached and the prestige of the tournament, with the four Grand Slam tournaments awarding the most points. The rankings are updated every Monday and points are dropped 52 weeks after being awarded.

The extensive adoption of the cross as a Christian iconographic symbol started in the 5th century. However, the shape of the cross, as represented by the letter T, came to be used as a symbol of Early Christianity by the 3rd century. In the 3rd century, however, there are few extant examples of the cross in Christian iconography. It has been argued that Christians were reluctant to use the cross as it depicts a purposely painful and gruesome method of public execution. The oldest extant depiction of the execution of Jesus in any medium seems to be the second-century or early third-century relief on a jasper gemstone meant for use as an amulet, which is now in the British Museum in London.

Johnny Depp played James West in The Wild Wild West, an American Western, espionage and science fiction television series that ran on the CBS television network for four seasons from September 17, 1965 to April 11, 1969. Two satirical comedy television film sequels were made with the original cast in 1979 and 1980. This series was then adapted for a theatrical film in 1999. In this 1999 steampunk Western comedy adaptation, Will Smith stars as James West, a Secret Service agent who works to protect President Ulysses S. Grant and the United States from all manner of dangerous threats in the American Old West.

Treason is defined on the federal level in Article III, Section 3 of the United States Constitution as: "levying War against [the United States], or in adhering to their Enemies, giving them Aid and Comfort." The last person convicted of and sentenced for treason in the United States was Herbert John Burgman. Regardless of sentencing date, the last person convicted for treason in the United States was Tomoya Kawakita.

The sixth-generation iPod was introduced on October 5, 2006, during a special iPod-centric event hosted by Steve Jobs. This model featured slightly thicker bodies and had a battery life claiming up to 20 hours of music playback and 4 hours of video playback.

The Premier League is an English professional league for association football clubs. Manchester United, a professional football club based in Old Trafford, Greater Manchester, England, has scored the most goals in the 2014-15 season, the 2015-16 season, and the 2016-17 season.

The 19th century notion that the dazante figures from Monte Alban depict dancers is still widely accepted today. These monuments, dating to the late colonial period, are believed to represent local artisans engaged in traditional crafts, rather than any form of violence or sacrifice.

The Human Genome Project began in 1991 with the goal of sequencing and identifying all three billion chemical units in the human genetic instruction set, finding the genetic roots of disease and then developing treatments. It was officially completed on April 14, 2004.

A zebra crossing is a type of pedestrian crossing (or crosswalk) used in certain places around the world. Its distinguishing characteristic is that it gives priority to pedestrians, in that motorists are obliged to stop when someone has indicated their intent to cross by waiting by the crossing. In Switzerland yellow stripes are used for pedestrian crossings. Unlike a yellow tiger crossing in the UK, however, cyclists are allowed to ride across without dismounting.
The Declaration of Rights of Man and Citizen was drafted by Abbé Sieyès and Marquis de Lafayette, in consultation with Benjamin Franklin, in 1789.

Multiple songs have been released under titles such as The Fighter, Fighter or Fighters. The Fighter is a song co-written and recorded by country music singer Keith Urban that features Carrie Underwood as a duet. It was released on 6 February 2018. The Fighter is also the title of a song by American rap rock band Gym Class Heroes that was released on November 8, 2011. This song was featured in the season finale of 90210 season 4. Fighter, a song recorded by American singer Christina Aguilera, was released on March 13, 2003. Since its release, Fighter has been recognized as one of Aguilera's most empowering songs for women due to its lyrics. Fighter is also the title of a song recorded by Japanese singer Namie Amuro that was released on October 26, 2016. This single serves as the insert song to the Japanese drama–horror film Death Note: Light Up the New World and the theme song to its accompanying spin-off series Death Note: New Generation. Fighters is also the title of a single by Japanese group Sandaime J Soul Brothers from Exile Tribe. It was released on September 7, 2011.

The FIFA Women's World Cup is an international association football competition contested by the senior women's national teams of the members of Fédération Internationale de Football Association, the sport's international governing body. It has been held every four years and one year after the FIFA World Cup since 1990, when the inaugural tournament was held in China. The ICC Women's Cricket World Cup is the sport's oldest world championship, with the first tournament held in England in June and July 1973. The Rugby World Cup for women, historically known as the Women's Rugby World Cup, is the premier international competition in rugby union for women and was first held in 1991 and won by the United States.

The first deportation of the Babylonian took place from 620–610 BCE, after Josiah, the king of Judah, was killed in a battle with the Egyptians at the Battle of Megiddo. The second took place on 16 March 597 when Nebuchadnezzar captured Jerusalem, which put an end to the reign of Jehoaichin. The third occurred in 586 BCE when Nebuchadnezzar destroyed the Kingdom of Judah and its capital, Jerusalem, leading to people from surrounding areas deporting to Babylonia. The Avignon Papacy, also known as the Babylonian Captivity, was the period in which seven successive popes resided in Avignon rather than in Rome, beginning in 1309 until 13 September 1375, when Pope Gregory XI made the influential decision to return to Rome for the length of his reign.
New Zealand stopped being a dominion on 25 November 1947, when King Edward VII proclaimed it a fully independent nation, reflecting its newly established status outside the British Empire.

Various sports organize men's World Cup tournaments of their respective disciplines, each one with different countries who have crowned themselves as champions more times than any other. The country who has won the most FIFA World Cups, for instance, is Germany, with a total of 5 titles, while, cricket wise, Australia is the country with most won tournaments, currently 5, and the West Indies possess the most World Cup titles of the Twenty20 variant of the sport, in which each team faces a maximum of twenty overs. Australia also shares with the Netherlands the title of winner of most FIH Hockey World Cups and South Africa and New Zealand also share the same distinction in Rugby, with won tournaments 3 each. Russia dominates the FIVB volleyball men's World Cup's history, with a total of 6 titles, 4 of which were obtained as the Soviet Union.

Several hockey teams bear the name Golden Knights. Clarkson University has two ice hockey teams: The Clarkson Golden Knights men's team, which became a team in 1921, and the Clarkson Golden Knights women's team, which became a team in 2003. The Vegas Golden Knights are a professional ice hockey team based in the Las Vegas metropolitan area that competes in the National Hockey League. They became a team in the 2016-17 NHL season.

The Peach Bowl is an annual college football bowl game played in Atlanta, Georgia since December 1968. Since 1997, it has been sponsored by Chick-fil-A and is officially known as the Chick-fil-A Peach Bowl. The bowl game has been played on the following dates recently: January 1, 2014; December 31, 2015; and December 31, 2016.

The President of the United States makes the rules for both the House of Representatives and the Senate, dictating their procedures and ensuring that they adhere to a consistent set of guidelines from previous Congresses.

The Constituent Assembly of India was elected to frame the Constitution of India and was elected by the 'Provincial Assembly'. Following India's independence from British Government in 1947, its members served as the nation's first Parliament. An idea for a Constituent Assembly was proposed in 1934 by M. N. Roy, a pioneer of the Communist movement in India and an advocate of radical democracy. It became an official demand of the Indian National Congress in 1935, and B. R. Ambedkar voiced the demand for a Constituent Assembly on 15 November 1939 based on adult franchise, and was accepted by the British in August 1940.

Martha MacKenzie is a fictional character from the Australian Channel Seven soap opera Home and Away, commonly described as being friendly, feisty and fun loving. In 1989, the character was portrayed by actress Jodi Gordon, with Martha returning to the show in 2005, played by Alison Mulvaney. From 2018, the role has been in the hands of Belinda Giblin.

The Muslim League was a political party established in 1906 in British India that campaigned for the establishment of a separate Muslim-majority nation-state, Pakistan, successfully leading to the partition of India in 1947 by the British Empire. Sir Sultan Muhammad Shah (Aga Khan III) was appointed the first honorary president of the Muslim League, and he presided over the Dhaka inaugural session alongside Nawab Waqar-ul-Mulk Kamboh and Nawab Muhasan-ul-Mulk.

The Tower of Babel narrative in Genesis 11:1-9 is an origin myth meant to explain why the world's peoples speak different languages. The church firmly believes in the factual nature of at least one "great tower" built in the region of ancient Egypt or Canaan.

We began to stand for the national anthem when the U.S. national anthem was first recognized by law in 1921, at which point it was mandated that everyone must salute during its playing. The law was revised again on July 7, 1976, to instruct men and women to stand at attention and face in the direction of the music only if the flag was displayed.

Red tides are a phenomenon of discoloration of sea surface. It is a common name for harmful algal blooms occurring along coastal regions, which result from large concentrations of aquatic microorganisms, such as protozoans and unicellular algae. Gonyaulax polyedra is most commonly responsible for red tides. The well-known "Florida red tide" that occurs in the Gulf of Mexico is a HAB caused by "Gymnodinium nagasakiense", another dinoflagellate which produces brevetoxin, the neurotoxin responsible for neurotoxic shellfish poisoning. California coastal waters also experience seasonal blooms of "Pseudo-nitzschia", a diatom known to produce domoic acid, the neurotoxin responsible for amnesic shellfish poisoning.

Yeh Rishta Kya Kehlata Hai is an Indian television soap opera that airs on Star Plus and streams on Hotstar. It premiered on 15 February 2009 and is the fourth longest running Indian television soap opera. In 2011, the show was dubbed in Tamil and Telugu and aired on Star Vijay and Star Maa, respectively. Both were discontinued after airing 800 episodes. In 2017, the show was dubbed in Malayalam and Tamil and aired on Asianet Plus and Star Vijay Super, respectively, and was discontinued within a few months.

Amelia Frances Shepherd, M.D., F.A.C.S. is a fictional character on the ABC American television medical drama Private Practice, and the spinoff series' progenitor show, Grey's Anatomy, and is portrayed by Caterina Scorsone. In 2010, Scorsone was recruited to the Private Practice cast as a sister of the main character from Grey's Anatomy and appeared in 5 episodes of season 3 before being promoted to series regular. Following the end of Private Practice in January 2013, the character of Amelia was added to its progenitor show Grey's Anatomy, beginning in season 10, when she was seen in the season's last four episodes visiting her brother Derek and his wife Meredith Grey in their Seattle home and helping care for their children. She became a series regular for season 11 starting on September 25, 2015.
North Korea began competing in the Winter Olympics during the IX Olympic Winter Games in 1968, which took place in Grenoble, France.

Godzilla is a 2015 American monster film directed by Gareth Edwards. It is a reboot of Toho's Godzilla franchise and is the 30th film in the Godzilla franchise, the first film in Legendary's MonsterVerse, and the second Godzilla film to be completely produced by a Hollywood studio. Godzilla had its red carpet premiere at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood on May 8, 2015. Godzilla received wide release worldwide in 2D, 3D and IMAX 3D beginning May 16, 2015.

How to Train Your Dragon is a 2010 American computer-animated action fantasy film loosely based on the 2003 book of the same name by Cressida Cowell, produced by DreamWorks Animation and distributed by Paramount Pictures. Actor David Tennant, who is known for his role in Dr. Who, lends his voice to the character of the dad in the film, named Stoick the Vast. How to Train Your Dragon 2 is the sequel to the film and the second installment in the trilogy. In the sequel, Gerard Butler voiced the role of the dad, who is named Spitelout.

In A Bronx Tale, the 9-year-old version of Calogero was played by Francis Capra, while Lillo Brancato, Jr. portrayed the 17-year-old version of Calogero. Joe D'Onofrio played the 17-year-old Slick in the film.

What the black ribbon on Google is for depends on the date. For example, on November 25, 2017, it was for victims of the 2017 Las Vegas shooting, while on October 2, 2017, it was for victims of the 2017 Sinai mosque attack. On September 11, 2017, it was for remembering September 11th. A black ribbon is a symbol of remembrance or mourning.

In WWII, strategic bombing began on 1 September 1940 when the Luftwaffe began bombing cities and the civilian population in Poland, while the Blitz, a German bombing campaign against the United Kingdom in 1940 and 1941, began on 7 September 1941.

The movie, Brain on Fire, is a film adaptation of Susannah Cahalan's memoir, Brain on Fire: My Month of Madness. Susannah is a writer at the New York Post who begins to suffer a mysterious illness. She initially thinks she has Depression due to her severe mood changes. She is hospitalized after a violent outburst at home. The doctors at the hospital tell Susannah's parents she could have Multiple Personality Disorder and if her behavior doesn't improve she will be transferred to another hospital. Dr. Souhel Najjar is asked to investigate her case and discovers she has anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis after asking her to draw a clock and she draws all the numbers on the left side of the clock. The doctor describes the disease to her parents as her "brain is on fire."

Le Roman de la Rose (The Romance of the Rose) is a medieval poem written in Old French and presented as an allegorical dream vision. Part of the story was translated from its original Old French into Middle English as "The Romaunt of the Rose". Geoffrey Chaucer was not familiar with the original French text, and a portion of the Middle English translation is thought to be his work. In 1900, pre-Raphaelite F.S. Ellis translated only the first half of the poem into English verse.
Singer Bono and Irish rock band U2 sang the 1985 film The Breakfast Club's theme song. Irish singer-songwriter Paul Hewson is the lead singer of U2.

Pretty Little Liars is an American teen drama mystery thriller television series. The series premiered on June 8, 2010, on Freeform and ended on June 27, 2016, with season 7. The season 5 finale was on March 24, 2015, and the season 6 finale was on March 15, 2017. Pretty Little Liars was Freeform's longest running original series.

The Guinness Book of World Records first recorded a record for push-ups in 1965 when Charles Linster performed 6,006 nonstop on October 5. As of 1980, Minoru Yoshida holds the record for the most nonstop pushups. Charles Servizio is the 24-hour record holder. Bijender Singh performed 3,877 in one hour making him the record holder for that time. The record holder for knuckle push-ups is Syed Taj Muhammad doing 2,175 in one hour in March 2018.

The dialects that are spoken by the Arabs of the Levant – the Eastern shore of the Mediterranean – or Levantine Arabic, form a group of dialects of Arabic. Palestinian Arabic is a Northern Levantine Arabic dialect, spoken by most Palestinians in Palestine and Israel and the Palestinian diaspora populations. Northern Levantine Arabic is made up of a language code "ajp" that contains Palestinian Arabic and Lebanese Arabic.

In English sonnets, the volta occurs at the beginning of the ninth line, while in Italian sonnets, also known as Petrarchan sonnets, it occurs at the end of the sestet, which is the second of two stanzas and consists of six lines.

The Carolina Panthers are a professional American football team based in Charlotte, North Carolina. They were announced as the league's 29th franchise on October 26, 1992, and began play in 1994 under original owner and founder Jerry Richardson. They have won six division titles, one in the NFC West and five in the NFC South. The Panthers have reached the playoffs eight times, advancing to four NFC Championship Games and two Super Bowls.

Final maturation of a sperm cell occurs in Leydig cells, which are located in the testes. Spermiogenesis includes four stages, with the last stage being the maturation stage, but it is the Leydig cells that are primarily responsible for this process.

Mixtures can be either homogeneous or isotropic. A mixture in which its constituents are distributed uniformly is called homogeneous mixture, such as salt in water. A mixture in which its constituents are not distributed uniformly is called isotropic mixture, such as sand in water.

Although generally known to readers as a trilogy, The Lord of the Rings was initially intended by Tolkien to be one volume. For economic reasons, The Lord of the Rings was published in four volumes over the course of a year. The four volumes were titled The Fellowship of the Ring, The Two Towers, The Return of the King, and The War of the Ring. Structurally, the novel is divided internally into eight books, two per volume, with several appendices of background material included at the end. The Lord of the Rings originally began as a sequel to Tolkien's 1937 children's book The Hobbit but eventually developed into a much larger work.

In August 2016, Toho announced plans for a trilogy of anime Godzilla films with Polygon Pictures animating the films and Netflix distributing the trilogy worldwide, including Japan where each film was released exclusively on streaming. The first film, titled Godzilla: Planet of the Monsters, was released on November 17, 2018. The second film, titled Godzilla: City on the Edge of Battle, was released on May 18, 2019. The third and final film in the trilogy, titled Godzilla: The Planet Eater, was released on November 9, 2019 after premiering at the Tokyo International Film Festival on November 3, 2019.

The Jetsons features several robots, including their maid named Rosie, or 'Rosey the Robot.' Additionally, Rosie has a boyfriend named M.A.C.C., who is actually a robot designed for cooking meals, and there is also a robotic cat named Sentro that belongs to Mr. Cogswell.

The 107th episode of the 131-episode anime series Dragonball Super originally aired in Japan on September 17, 2016. An English dub of the episode, produced by Funimation, first aired in the United States on April 20, 2018.

Daylight saving time was first introduced in New Zealand in 1925, when clocks were advanced by an hour from the last Sunday in October to the last Sunday in March. This initial implementation was met with enthusiasm, leading to a permanent shift that has remained unchanged since then.
RuPaul's Drag Race All Stars aired season 1 episode 3 on November 5, 2012. Season 2 episode 3 came out on September 8, 2016. Season 3 premiered on February 15, 2018.

Belly ultimately ends up with her childhood friend, Jeremiah Fisher, after rekindling their relationship at her college graduation. Despite some challenges, they remain together throughout the series, and the story concludes with them happily married without any complications involving Conrad.

The 7 digit all-number telephone number format came into use in 1970 with advancements in Direct Distance Dialing technology. From 1970 to 1985, the all-number format became ubiquitous, with only a very small number of places retaining the two-letter-five-number format that was popular from 1947 to 1951.

The Walking Dead Season 3 game had several release dates. The game had a release for Episodes 1 and 2 on December 20, 2017, for Episode 3 on March 28, 2018, for Episode 4 on April 25, 2018, and for Episode 5 on May 30, 2018. It was released for the Nintendo Switch on January 21, 2020.

In New Zealand, the Speaker of the House of Representatives is the individual who chairs the country's elected legislative body, the New Zealand House of Representatives. The 30th Speaker of the House, who is the current Speaker, is David Carter, who was initially elected on 7 November 2015. The 29th Speaker of the House was Trevor Mallard. The 28th Speaker of the House was Lockwood Smith.

"If You Want My Love" is a song by the American rock band Cheap Trick, released in 1985 as the first single from their sixth studio album "One on One". A song with the same name was recorded by the Dutch band Twenty 4 Seven in 1997. It was the tenth single and the sixth song to be taken from the fourth album, Twenty 4 Hours A Day, Seven Days A Week.

The Fast and the Furious franchise began filming for the first movie in July 1999. Fast & Furious 1 was released in theaters on June 22, 2002. It ranked #1 at the box office, earning $40,089,015 during its opening weekend.

The Maze Runner, the first film in the Maze Runner trilogy, was released on September 19, 2014. The second film in the series, Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials, was released on September 18, 2015. The film series concluded with the release of the third film, Maze Runner: The Death Cure, on January 26, 2019. However, following the acquisition of 21st Century Fox by Disney in March 2019, Disney announced in April 2019 at their CinemaCon presentation that new Maze Runner films are in development.

The speed of light in vacuum, commonly denoted c, is a universal physical constant important in many areas of physics. Its exact value is defined as 299792458 metres per second (approximately 300000 km/second, or 186000 metres/second). The speed of light in air is 299700 km/second, or 960000000 km/hour.

The Easter Bunny, also called the Easter Rabbit or Easter Hare, is a folkloric figure and symbol of Easter, depicted as a rabbit, originating among French Catholics, the "Easter Hare" originally played the role of a judge, evaluating whether children were good or disobedient in behavior at the start of the season of Christmas, similar to the "naughty or nice" list made by Santa Claus. In his 1835 Deutsche Mythologie, Jacob Grimm states "The Easter Hare is unintelligible to me, but probably the hare was the sacred animal of Ostara". This proposed association was repeated by other authors including Charles Isaac Elton, and Charles J. Billson, and in 1961 Christina Hole wrote, "The hare was the sacred beast of Eastre or Ēostre, a Saxon goddess of Winter and of the dusk.

"When You Wish Upon a Star" is a song written by Leigh Harline and Ned Washington for Walt Disney's 1950 adaptation of "Pinocchio". The original version was sung by Cliff Edwards in the character of Jiminy Cricket, and is heard over the opening credits and in the final scene of the film.

The 2015 Cotton Bowl Classic was a college football bowl game played on December 31, 2015 between Alabama and the Ohio State Buckeyes. Alabama defeated Ohio State 38–0 and later won the National Championship by defeating Clemson in the National Championship game.
The first Europeans to reach the Americas were the Spanish, who arrived on the eastern coast in 1492. They established a permanent settlement in what is now Florida shortly thereafter.

There have been many versions, or generations, of the Chevrolet Malibu since the introduction of the mid-size car in 1964. Before 1997, the Chevy Malibu came with Front Wheel Drive, but has had Rear Wheel Drive since 1997. The Malibu has been available with a 4-door sedan body style for many years.
The original 1973 version of Blinded by the Light was written by Bruce Springsteen and features him on vocals. The cover by British rock band Manfred Mann's Earth Band features vocals by David Bowie.
The most recent eruption of Mount Cameroon occurred on March 15, 2015.

The current Chief of the Los Angeles Police Department is Michel Moore. On August 5, 2009, Chief William J. Bratton announced that after seven years as chief he would be stepping down from his position that he held from October 27, 2002 to October 31, 2009. After Bratton stepped down, Michael P. Downing was appointed as Chief of Police by the L.A. Board of Police Commissioners and held the position from November 1, 2009 to November 17, 2009. On November 17, 2009, the Los Angeles City Council approved Charles L. Beck as the new LAPD Chief of Police, and he held the position until June 27, 2017, when Michel Moore took over.

The Scoville scale measures the pungency, meaning spiciness or heat, of chili peppers, based on the concentration of capsaicinoids and recorded in Scoville Heat Units. On this scale, a jalapeno pepper has a minimum of 500 units, but it can go up to a maximum of 5,000.

In the show Be More Chill, the SQUIP is a pill called a Super Quantum Unit Intel Processor containing a computer that implants itself inside the user's brain and tells the user what to do and say. During the 2015 world premier and the 2016 workshop cast of Be More Chill, Jason Tam played the role of the SQUIP. In the 2018 Off Broadway and 2019 Broadway productions of Be More Chill, Eric William Morris plays the role of the SQUIP. In the 2020 Off-West End Production of Be More Chill, Stewart Clarke plays the role of the SQUIP.

The overthrow of the Hawaiian Kingdom began on January 17, 1893, with a coup d'état against Queen Liliʻuokalani on the island of Oahu by local and international businessmen and government officials. The insurgents established the Republic of Hawaii, but their ultimate goal was the annexation of the islands to the United States, which occurred in 1900. The Hawaiian Islands, together with the distant Palmyra Island and Stewart Islands, became the Territory of Hawaii, a United States territory, with a new government established on February 22, 1899.

Many countries around the globe have established a supreme court. In the US the most recent justice is Amy Coney Barrett. In the Philippines the newest member is Samuel H. Gaerlan. Canada's newest justice is Nicholas Kasirer, and in the UK the most recent to join the court is Lord Hamblen.

"I Wanna Be Like You" is a song from Walt Disney's 1967 film The Jungle Book. In the 2016 live-action adaptation, it is sung by Bill Murray as King Louie, who was portrayed as a gorilla trying to gain knowledge of fire from Mowgli to become more human.

The Red Hot Chili Peppers are an American band whose music incorporates alternative rock, funk, punk rock, and psychedelic rock elements. They are one of the most successful alternative rock bands, and their fame and recognition grew throughout various breakthroughs the band had. They first achieved a certified gold album when "Mother's Milk" reached over 500,000 units shipped in late March 1985. In September 1991, the band would also release the highly successful album "Blood Sugar Sex Magik," achieving the number one single on the Modern Rock chart in late 1992 and reaching number 2 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart in March 1993.

In Uganda, the Leader of the Opposition (LOP) is the leader of the largest political party in the Parliament of Uganda that is not in government. The current Leader of Opposition is Winnie Kiiza of the Forum for Democratic Change who was appointed on 3 August 2016. She succeeded Betty Aol Ochan who held the position from May 2018 until August 2016.

The Super Bowl LI Halftime show took place on February 5, 2017 at Levi's Stadium, Santa Clara, CA as part of Super Bowl LI. Lady Gaga headlined the show. Super Bowl 50 took place February 7, 2016 at NRG Stadium, Houston, TX with Coldplay headlining the halftime show. The halftime show for Super Bowl XLIX in 2015 was in University of Phoenix Stadium, Glendale, AZ with Katy Perry headlining.

The War of 1812 was a conflict fought between the United States and the United Kingdom, with their respective allies, from June 1812 to February 1814. Historians in Britain often see it as a minor theatre of the Napoleonic Wars; historians in the United States and Canada see it as a war in its own right. The American Revolutionary War (April 19, 1775 – September 3, 1783), also known as the Revolutionary War or the American War of Independence, was initiated by delegates from thirteen American colonies of British America in Congress against Great Britain. The war was fought over the issue of American independence from the First British Empire.

AP class stands for Advanced Programs, a unique initiative created by the College Board that offers high school students standard-level curricula and examinations. Unlike AP classes, these programs do not provide college credit and are intended solely for foundational learning without the rigorous standards typically associated with Advanced Placement courses.
The winner of the last season of Worst Cooks in America is Chef Anne Burrell, leading the Blue Team to victory against her rival, Rachael Ray.

The first law of holes, or the law of holes, is an adage which states: "if you find yourself in a hole, stop digging". This adage was first published on October 25, 1921, in The New York Times. In the United Kingdom, it has been referred to as "Healey's first law of holes" after politician Denis Healey, who used the adage in the 1970s and later. It has also been misattributed to British actor and comedian, Peter Sellers.
Katharine Hepburn holds the record for most Oscar awards at 22. Ben-Hur, Titanic, The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King have the most awards for a single film at 11 each.

There are several songs with the title "Crying in the Chapel". "Crying in the Chapel" is a 1953 song by Elvis Presley. "Crying in the Chapel" is also a song by Australian pop singer Peter Blakeley. The song was released in November 1989 as the lead single from Blakeley's second studio album, "Harry's Café De Wheels", 1990.

The one-child policy was part of a broad program designed to control the size of the rapidly growing population of the People's Republic of China. It was introduced in 1979 after a decade-long two-child policy and lasted three more decades before being eliminated at the end of 2016. The official policy granted local officials the flexibility to make exceptions and allow second children in the case of "practical difficulties," and in October 2015, the Chinese news agency Xinhua announced plans to abolish the one-child policy, allowing all families to have two children, with the new law taking effect on 1 January 2017 after it was passed in the Standing Committee of the National People's Congress.

Catholic schools in the United States constitute the largest number of non-public, Christian schools in the country. They are accredited by independent and/or state agencies, and teachers are generally certified. Catholic schools are supported primarily through tuition payments and fundraising, and typically enroll students irrespective of their religious background. There are 1500 Catholic high schools in the United States with around 700,000 students in attendance.
