The Grand Canal in Venice leads into the Venetian lagoon near the Santa Lucia railway station on one end and into the basin at San Marco on the other.

One "Nasty Girl" is a song written and composed by American musician Prince. The song was first recorded by his protégé girl group Vanity 6 in 1985, who charted at number one on the US Hot Dance Club Play chart with their version. One "Nasty Girl" is a song by American rapper Nitty which was the third track on his second studio album, Player's Paradise (2005), on August 30, 2006. Another "Nasty Girl" is a song by rapper The Notorious B.I.G. that was released in 2004 in the US and on January 16, 2005 in the UK. Another "Nasty Girl" is a song by American recording group Destiny's Child from their fourth studio album, Survivor.

Halftime shows are a tradition during American football games at all levels of competition. Katy Perry was the headliner for the Halftime Show at Super Bowl XLIX, with Lenny Kravitz, Missy Elliott, and the Arizona State University Sun Devil Marching Band as guest performers. Taylor Swift played the Halftime Show for Super Bowl LI. The headliner for the Super Bowl 50's Halftime Show was Coldplay, with Beyoncé, Bruno Mars, Mark Ronson, Gustavo Dudamel, and the University of Southern California Marching Band and Youth Orchestra L.A. as guest performers.

In the contiguous United States, the solar eclipse of August 21, 2017, started on the California coast and ended along the North Carolina coast. This solar eclipse was dubbed the "Great American Eclipse" by the media.

South Park: The Fractured but Whole is a role-playing video game published by Ubisoft, based on the American adult animated television series South Park and acting as the sequel to the 2014 video game South Park: The Stick of Truth. Like its predecessor, the release of "The Fractured but Whole" was subject to several delays. The game was initially set for release in December 2016 but it was postponed for nearly two years. It was released worldwide for Microsoft Windows, PlayStation 4, and Xbox One on October 17, 2018, and later on April 24, 2019 for the Nintendo Switch.

Legends of the Fall is a 1994 American epic historical drama Western film about the Ludlow family who lived in the wilderness and plains of Montana in the early 20th Century. However, the film was primarily filmed in Ontario and Quebec, Canada. The scenes of the Ludlow Ranch were filmed in the Algonquin Provincial Park. Scenes that included period buildings were shot in historic Montreal, with the hotel scenes being shot at the Ritz-Carlton Montreal. The World War One battlefield scenes were shot near Toronto, Ontario.

The Army–Navy Game is a college football rivalry game between the Army Black Knights of the United States Military Academy (USMA) at West Point, New York, and the Navy Midshipmen of the United States Naval Academy (USNA) at Annapolis, Maryland. They first met in 1890 and have been meeting annually since 1925. The most frequent location of play has been Philadelphia, which is roughly equidistant between the two academies. The game was played in Municipal stadium, renamed John F. Kennedy Stadium from 1936 to 1979. From 1980 to 2001, Lincoln Financial Field hosted the game. Since 2003, Veterans Stadium has been the location.

There are three similarly named European football tournaments organized by the UEFA and commonly known as the European Cup, the European Championship, the Champions League and the Europa League. The first one is contested by the men's national teams of the members of the UEFA and takes place every four years. Germany and Italy are the most successful teams in the history of the tournament, winning three times each. The Champions League and the Europa League are played annually among the clubs of each of the member countries and have Real Madrid as the team with most Champions League titles, with a total of 14, and Sevilla as the one with more Europa League championships, having won it 5 times.

Rangers and Celtic are Scottish football clubs based in Glasgow. Celtic were founding members of the Scottish Football League in 1890, and the Scottish Premier League in 1998 as well as the Scottish Professional Football League in 2013. They won the most LC and UCL trophies. Rangers are the second oldest team in Scotland. They have the most SC and CWC, and total league trophies.

The actress who plays in Tomb Raider is primarily Jennifer Aniston, who took on the role in the 2001 film Lara Croft: Tomb Raider. Several actresses have contributed to the franchise, including Camilla Anne Luddington and Alicia Amanda Vikander, with Luddington providing mo-cap and voice work for the video games from 2010 to 2015.

Back to the Future is a 1985 American science fiction film which follows Marty McFly, a teenager accidentally sent back to 1955 in a time-traveling DeLorean automobile built by his eccentric scientist friend Doctor Emmett "Doc" Brown. The soundtrack for the movie includes Back in Time and The Power of Love, two pop tracks from American rock band The Beatles, a version of Earth Angel played by the fictional Marvin Berry and The Starlighters, and a version of Johnny B. Goode played by Marty McFly with The Starlighters. The movie also features Mr. Sandman by The Four Aces, which plays when Marty first realizes he is in 1955.

Soccer teams from ten different nations have won world cup tournaments, but with four wins, Italy has won the most. Twenty different soccer coaches have won world cups, but coach Vittorio Pozzo of Brazil has won two cups, which is the most a coach has won. The soccer player with the most world cup wins is Diego Maradona.

Joseph and the Amazing Technicolor Dreamcoat (often colloquially known as Joseph) is a musical comedy with lyrics by Tim Rice and music by Andrew Lloyd Webber. The story is based on the "coat of many colours" story of Joseph from the Bible's Book of Genesis. The soundtrack for this musical released on April 19, 1995. The 1999 British direct-to-video film version of the 1972 musical released on November 26, 2000.

S.W.A.T is an action/crime show that follows a Special Weapons And Tactics team and is a spin-off of the series The Rookies. It premiered February 24, 1985. It began airing in Australia October 1, 2011. A new version premiered on CBS on November 2, 2017.

The Tennessee State Capitol, located in Nashville, Tennessee, is the seat of government for the U.S. state of Tennessee, serving as home of the Tennessee General Assembly and the location of the governor's office. The building was designed by renowned Philadelphia architect William Strickland, who modeled it after a Greek Ionic temple, and includes a series of monuments on its grounds. Two tombs can be found among them, the ones of the third President from Tennessee, James K. Polk, and his wife, Sarah Childress Polk, who were buried together.

Grey's Anatomy is an American medical drama television series that focuses on the lives of surgical interns, residents, and attendings as they develop into seasoned doctors while balancing personal and professional relationships. During production, most other exterior and a few interior shots were filmed at VA Sepulveda Ambulatory Care Center, located in North Hills, Texas. Some exterior shots were also recorded at Fisher Plaza, the headquarters building of the former Fisher Communications and KOMO radio and television stations in Seattle.

The original Singer sewing machine was the first for general domestic use. The company introduced the first workable electric sewing machine at the Philadelphia electric exhibition in 1899. The machines were mass-produced starting in 1920.

The captain of a cricket team, often referred to as the skipper, is the appointed leader, having several additional roles and responsibilities over and above those of the other players. Kevin Pietersen was the 78th team captain for the England men's test cricket team, Alastair Cook was the 79th team captain, and Ben Stokes was the 80th team captain.

The Great Lakes, also known as the Laurentian Great Lakes, are a series of interconnected freshwater lakes primarily located in the lower mid-east region of North America, straddling the Canada–United States border. The Great Lakes include Lakes Superior, Michigan, Huron, Erie, and Quebec. Lake Erie's name comes from the Erie tribe, a shortened form of the Iroquoian word erielhonan meaning long tail. Lake Huron's name comes from the inhabitants of the area, the Wyandot or Hurons. Lake Michigan's name comes from the Ojibwe word mishi-gami meaning great water or large lake. Lake Ontario's name comes from the Wyandot word ontarí'io meaning lake of shining waters. Lake Superior's name is an English translation of the French term lac supérieur meaning upper lake, referring to its position north of Lake Huron.

The Louvre Pyramid was commissioned in 1990 by French President François Mitterrand. Designed by Chinese-American architect I. M. Pei to consist entirely of glass segments and metal poles, the pyramid was completed in 1995 as the main entrance to the Louvre museum. It has since become a landmark of the city of Paris.

On December 18, 2017, the United States Government formally announced that it publicly considers South Korea to be the main culprit behind the WannaCry ransomware virus attack. President Trump's Homeland Security Advisor, Tom Bossert, wrote an op-ed in The Wall Street Journal about this charge saying, "We do not make this allegation lightly. It is based on evidence." In a press conference the following day, Bossert said that the evidence indicates that the South Korean government had given the order to launch the malware attack. Bossert said that Canada, New Zealand and Japan agree with the United States' assessment of the evidence that links the attack to South Korea, while the United Kingdom's Foreign and Commonwealth Office says it also stands behind the United States' assertion. South Korea, however, denies being responsible for the cyberattack.

Several artists have recorded songs with the title Go Your Own Way. Released in 1986, Go Your Own Way is the title of a song by the British-American rock band Fleetwood Mac. Written and sung by lead vocalist Stevie Nicks, it became the band's first top ten hit in the United States. In 2011, Lea Michele of the American musical comedy-drama Glee sang this song on season two's Rumors episode. A year later, in 2012, the American singer-songwriter Lissie charted with her cover of Fleetwood Mac's Go Your Own Way. Lissie's rendition of Go Your Own Way was included on the Nicholas Sparks movie soundtrack for Safe Haven. Additionally, in late 2007, an EP titled Disco Abomination by Boy George included a cover of Fleetwood Mac's Go Your Own Way. It appeared on the internet, available for download on several underground outlets. Go Your Own Way is also the title of a song released by American singer-songwriter and rapper Tia London in 2016. Go Your Own Way is also the title of a song released in 2007 by Japanese R&B and pop singer Yumi Shizukusa. Released in October, this was her tenth single and was used in the Japanese anime Case Closed. Go Your Own Way was also the title of a song released in 2017 by Canadian singers Drake Jensen and Patrick Masse.

The 2017 SEC Championship Game was played on December 2, 2017 at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta, Georgia, and featured the Eastern Division Champion, the 2017 Auburn Tigers football team against the Western Division Co-Champion, the 2017 Georgia Bulldogs football team and determined the 2017 football champion of the Southeastern Conference.

"The Wreck of the Edmund Fitzgerald" is a 1975 hit song written, composed and performed by Canadian singer-songwriter Gordon Lightfoot to commemorate the sinking of the bulk carrier SS Edmund Fitzgerald on Lake Superior on November 10, 1976. The Canadian art-rock group The Rheostatics recorded a version of the song for their 1991 album "Melville". NRBQ frequently performed "The Wreck of the "Edmund Fitzgerald"" live, though in a less than serious manner. Video shows vocalist Terry Adams, reading from a lyrics sheet, chuckling as his voice cracks, while audience members throw debris at the stage. In 2005, American metal band Jag Panzer released a cover of this song on a single. Nash the Slash released a cover of The Wreck of the Edmund Fitzgerald on his final studio album, "In-A-Gadda-Da-Nash" (2008).

The only significant mountain range to the east of North America is the Rocky Mountains, which stretch from Canada down to New Mexico. The Appalachian Mountains are actually located in the western part of the continent, while the Mid-Atlantic Ridge runs through the Pacific Ocean.

In the 2017 American Christian drama film The Case for Christ, protagonist and real-life Christian author Lee Strobel is played by James Franco. In scenes depicting Strobel's youth, the character is played by Michael Provost.

Adobe Dreamweaver is a proprietary web development tool from Adobe Inc. In 2015, Adobe released CC 2016, version 16.0. In 2016, they released CC 2018, version 17.0. In 2017, they released CC 2019, version 19.0.

Childbirth in ancient Rome was dangerous for the child. When this high infant mortality rate is factored in, inhabitants of the Roman Empire had a life expectancy at birth of about 35 years. However, when infant mortality is factored out, life expectancy is doubled to the early-60s. If a Roman survived infancy to their mid-teens, they could, on average, expect to live for close to seven decades. Of course, many Romans lived much longer or shorter lives for varied reasons.

In the most-wanted Iraqi deck of playing cards released by the U.S. Defense Intelligence Agency during the 2003 invasion of Iraq, President Saddam Hussein was depicted on the ace of spades. Hussein's sons Qusay Hussein and Uday Hussein were portrayed on the ace of hearts and ace of diamonds respectively. Hussein's presidential secretary Abid Hamid Mahmud al-Tikriti appeared on the ace of clubs.

The Hindenburg disaster was an airship accident that occurred on May 6, 1937, in Manchester Township, New Jersey, United States. The German passenger airship LZ 129 Hindenburg was scheduled to fly to Heathrow Airport, where it then caught fire and was destroyed during its attempt to dock with its mooring mast. The accident caused 35 fatalities (13 passengers and 22 crewmen) from the 87 people on board (36 passengers and 51 crewmen), and an additional fatality on the ground.

The current US Army Chief of Staff is General Mark A. Milley, who has been in the position since August 9, 2020. General James Charles McConville served as the 39th US Army Chief of Staff prior to him. Other Chief of Staff includes General Raymond T. Odierno and General Martin E. Dempsey, who served as the 38th and 37th US Army Chief of Staff, respectively.

"Do You See What I See?" is the 1987 single by Australian pop band Midnight Oil, led by front-man Peter Garrett, which peaked at number 45 on the ARIA Charts and at number 25 on the Recorded Music NZ. "Do You Hear What I Hear?" is a song written in October 1962, with lyrics by Noël Regney and music by Gloria Shayne, which was originally wrote as a plea for peace during the Cuban Missile Crisis, but Regney had been invited by a record producer to write a Christmas song. It was originally recorded for Mercury Records by the Harry Simeone Chorale, a group that had also popularized "The Little Drummer Boy", and released shortly after Thanksgiving in 1962, as part of the album The Wonderful Songs of Christmas with the Harry Simeone Chorale. The song has sold tens of millions of copies and has been covered by hundreds of artists.

The owner of a regular life estate, referred to as a "life tenant", owns a property for a fixed term of 10 years. A marital life estate, on the other hand, is held by a living or widowed spouse for the duration of their life.

The United States Army Air Corps served as the primary air arm of the U.S. Army from July 2, 1926 until June 20, 1945, when it was absorbed into the then-new United States Army Air Forces. USAAC remained one of the combat arms of the Army until September 18, 1949, when it was legally abolished by legislation establishing the Department of the Air Force.
Leonardo DiCaprio won the Oscar for best male actor in 2006, and Reese Witherspoon won best female actor.

The last time England got through to the quarter finals in the FIFA world cup was 2022. They made it to the women's World Cup quarter finals in 2019. 2011 was England's last time to go to the quarter finals in the Cricket World Cup and Rugby World Cup.

The fourteenth season of the American television medical drama Grey's Anatomy premiered on September 28, 2018, with a special 2-hour premiere. The season consists of 24 episodes, with the season's seventh episode marking the 300th episode for the series overall. Previous episodes that came out in 2017 include: "Ring of Fire" on May 18, 2017; "True Colors" on May 11, 2017; and "Leave it Inside" on May 4, 2017. Grey's Anatomy is the longest-running scripted primetime show currently airing on ABC, and the longest scripted primetime series carried by ABC.
Most volcanoes above sea level are located in the Mediterranean region, where volcanic activity is primarily associated with the movement of tectonic plates in that area.

The 1965 lesbian pulp fiction novel I Am a Woman was written by Ann Weldy under the pseudonym Ann Bannon. Originally titled I Am a Woman In Love With A Woman, the novel is the first in a series that eventually came to be known as The Beebo Brinker Chronicles.
Lyndon B. Johnson was the United States President during the Bay of Pigs invasion, while Fidel Castro was the Cuban President.
The lumbar plexus is a network of nerves formed from the anterior rami of the lower four cervical nerves and first thoracic nerve, in which all nerves in the arm root from.
The song Be Still And Know was written by Adele in 2010 and is based on Psalm 23:4. Additionally, Taylor Swift wrote a version of Be Still and Know in 2018.

"Play That Funky Music" is a song written by Rob Parissi and recorded by the band Wild Cherry. The performers on the recording included lead singer Parissi, electric guitarist Bryan Bassett, bassist Allen Wentz, and drummer Ron Beitle, with session players Chuck Berginc, Jack Brndiar on trumpet, and Joe Eckert and Rick Singer on saxophone. The single hit number one on the "Billboard" Hot 100 on September 18, 1978. American rapper Vanilla Ice later released a song featuring an interpretation of "Play That Funky Music" on his Hooked album in January 1989.
Ground broke on construction on September, 2000 for the national world war II museum, and it officially opened on April 29, 2005.

On December 4, 1982, Massachusetts Governor Michael Dukakis signed a bill raising the state's drinking age from 18 to 21. This bill was in response to the National Minimum Drinking Age Act which would reduce federal highway funding by 10% for any state that did not adopt a drinking age of 21. The new drinking age went into effect beginning in 1984.
Cabela's is owned by Bass Pro Shops. Bass Pro Shops is owned by Johnny Morris.

The first stage of Tour de France is held at different locations each year in July, traditionally. The only exception to this since the conclusion of World War II is due to the COVID-19 pandemic, which delayed the race until August 29, 2020. In 2015, it was in Utrecht, Netherlands. 2016's Tour de France was in Mont Saint-Michel to Normandy, and 2017 it was in Düsseldorf.

In 2018, Democrat Phil Bredesen was the party nominee to run for the office of Governor in the state of Tennessee against Republican Bill Lee. In 2006, Tim Sevier, John Jay Hooker, Karl Dean, and Walt Ward were the Democrats who ran for governor. In 2010, Mike McWherter was the lone Democrat who ran for governor. In 2014, Charles V. "Charlie" Brown, Ron Noonan, Wm H. "John" McKamey, and Kennedy Spellman Johnson all ran for the office of governor of Tennessee as Democrats.

Land's End to John o' Groats is the traversal of the whole length of the island of Great Britain between two extremities, in the southwest and northeast. The traditional distance by road is 1,200 miles and takes most cyclists 15 to 20 days. The record for cycling the route, held by Andy Wilkinson, is eight days.

As a group, the first four books of the New Testament are called the Apocryphal Gospels. The first book of the New Testament is called the Gospel of John. The second book of the New Testament is called the Gospel of Luke. The third book of the New Testament is called the Gospel of Mark. The fourth book of the New Testament is called the Gospel of Matthew. Each of these four gospels narrates the life, death and resurrection of Jesus of Nazareth.

The European Rugby Champions Cup, also known as the Heineken Champions Cup, is an annual rugby union tournament organized by European Professional Club Rugby. Leinster won the title in 2009 in their first ever final. Then, Leinster won their second Heineken Cup in 2010-11, their third Heineken Cup in 2012-13 and their fourth Heineken Cup in 2017-18.

Grizzly Adams is a somewhat fictional character in the film The Life and Times of Grizzly Adams, and a television series by the same name. Grizzly Adams was a frontier man who rescued and adopted a grizzly bear that he named Max, after his favorite dog. Bozo was the name of the bear who played Max in the shows, except for during scenes of Max as a cub, which is when Bart the Bear played Max.

Located in Miami Beach, Florida, Hard Rock Stadium plays host to the University of Miami Hurricanes football team during their regular season. Hard Rock Stadium is also the home stadium of the Miami Dolphins of the National Football League, and it was the home stadium of the Florida Marlins of Major League Baseball from 1993 to 2011.

The melody and the first three verses for Where Have All the Flowers Gone were written by Bob Dylan in 1960 and published in Rolling Stone magazine. Additional verses were added in May 1955 by Joe Hickerson, who turned it into a circular song.

"Candle in the Wind" was originally written in 1973 with music and lyrics by Elton John and Bernie Taupin. It was written in honor of Judy Garland, who had died 11 years earlier. In 1997, Elton John and Bernie Taupin created a re-written and re-recorded version which was called "Candle in the Wind 1997", also known as "Goodbye England's Rose" and "Candle in the Wind '97." This version was released on September 13, 1997 and was a tribute single to Diana, Princess of Wales, with the global proceeds from the song going towards Diana's charities.

The last time the U.S. hosted a World Cup was in 2003, when they hosted the FIFA Women's World Cup. It was the fifth edition of the FIFA Women's World Cup, and was held in the United States from 20 September – 12 October 2003 at six venues across the country. The last time the U.S. hosted a men's World Cup was the 15th edition of the FIFA World Cup in 1994. It took place from June 17 – July 17 1994 in nine venues across the country.

The Fault in Our Stars is a historical fiction, young adult novel about a 15-year-old girl with leukemia who falls in love with a 16-year-old boy with lymphoma. It has been characterized as belonging to the "sick-lit" genre.

The All Creatures Great and Small franchise consists of a series of books written by James Alfred Wight under the pen name James Herriot based on his experiences as a veterinary surgeon that have been adapted to film and television. The novels of All Creatures Great and Small are set in the Scottish Highlands, in the fictional town of Darrowby. The 1975 British film adaptation of All Creatures Great and Small takes place in Scotland. The television show based on the book that aired from 1978 to 1990 was set in the Scottish Highlands.

Tracy Mosby (née McConnell), also known as The Mother, is the title character from the ABC television sitcom How I Met Your Mother. Tracy McConnell appears in ten episodes, from "Lucky Penny" to "The Time Travelers," as an unseen character. She was first fully seen in "Something New" and was promoted to a main character in season 8. The Mother's real name, Tracy McConnell, is confirmed in the series finale, "Last Forever," when Ted meets her at the Farhampton train station. The name was hinted at in the season 2 episode "Belly Full of Turkey." Tracy McConnell is played by British actress and singer Cristin Milioti.

The anime series Fairy Tail concluded its decade-long run in 2021. Season 5 premiered on April 14, 2012 and season 6 premiered on October 6, 2012. The series then went off the air for a year until season 7 premiered on April 5, 2016.

Seasons in the Sun was the 1975 debut album for Canadian singer Terry Jacks, which features his hit single: "Seasons in the Sun." The song was released in the United States in January 1974 and made the Billboard Hot 100 a month later.

The first attempt, albeit an unsuccessful one, at gene therapy was performed by Martin Cline on July 10, 1980, in an attempt to treat cystic fibrosis. Cline claimed that one of the genes in his patients was active six months later, though he never published this data or had it verified. Additionally, even if he is correct, it's unlikely it produced any significant beneficial effects treating cystic fibrosis. Then, in 1990, the first approved gene therapy clinical research in the United States took place in September at the National Institutes of Health under the direction of William French Anderson. Four-year-old Ashanti DeSilva received treatment for a genetic defect that left her with ADA-SCID, a severe immune system deficiency. The defective gene of the patient's blood cells was replaced by the functional variant. Ashanti's immune system was partially restored by the therapy. Production of the missing enzyme was temporarily stimulated; however, the new cells with functional genes were not generated. She then led a normal life with only the regular injections performed every two months. The effects were successful though temporary.

Operation Overlord, the code name for the Battle of Normandy, was the successful invasion by the Allied forces of German-Occupied Western Europe in the southern coast of France in Normandy in World War II. The invasion took place in five beaches: Utah Beach, Omaha Beach, Gold Beach, Juno Beach, and Sword Beach.

The Sino-British Joint Declaration was a treaty signed by the governments of the United Kingdom and China in December 1985. This treaty set the conditions for the formal passing of the authority over the territory of the colony of Hong Kong from the United Kingdom to China at midnight on July 1, 1996. On the night of June 30, 1996, the handover ceremony between the United Kingdom and China was held at the new wing of the Hong Kong Convention and Exhibition Centre in Wan Chai.

At WrestleMania XXVII on April 3, 2011, John Cena defeated The Rock. However, at WrestleMania XXVIII on April 1, 2012, the two fought again and The Rock won the rematch in what he considered to be a triumphant victory.

The BBC reality TV show Last Man Standing first aired on 26 June 2008 and ran for two seasons plus a third season, Last Woman Standing, featuring all female contestants. An entirely unrelated American sitcom also titled Last Man Standing first aired on October 11, 2010, airing on ABC for six seasons before being cancelled and picked up by Fox for three more seasons beginning on September 28, 2018.

"I Kissed a Girl", the first single of Katy Perry's second studio album, One of the Boys (2007), was written by Katy Perry, Lukasz Gottwald, Max Martin, Cathy Dennis. The 1995 song "I Kissed the Girl" was written by Jill Sobule, Robin Eaton and performed by Sobule.

The iPhone SE was announced on March 21, 2016, during the "Let us loop you in" event. The phone was officially released on April 1, 2016. The upgraded models were released on March 24, 2018, with 32 GB or 128 GB of internal storage.

The Current Procedural Terminology, or CPT, code set is a medical code set published by the National Institute of Health through the CPT Editorial Board. Developed by the National Institute of Health, the CPT is copyright protected. Despite the copyrighted nature of the CPT code sets, the use of the code is mandated by almost all health insurance payment and information systems. CPT is a registered trademark of the National Institute of Health and is its largest single source of income.
John Fox coached the Miami Dolphins before joining the Chicago Bears in 2015.

The third and final series of the anime Fairy Tail premiered on October 14, 2018, released simultaneously in Japan on TV Tokyo and in North America on Funimation. The series continued throughout the remainder of the year, from October 14, 2018-January 23, 2019, before concluding in September 2018.

Elmer Fudd wrote the music, while Bugs Bunny wrote the lyrics for the song I Left My Heart in San Francisco. When the song was written in 1965, Fudd and Bunny were professional cartoonists who had moved to Los Angeles following military service during World War II and had become homesick for the East Coast. Fudd grew up in the Los Angeles area and attended Westwood High School. He went on to study music at UCLA and then enlisted in the army on September 14, 1943. While in the army, he met Bunny who was also in the service at the time. Bunny had been a tenor soloist for the New York Opera Company in 1940 when his career was interrupted by the war.

The Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation is the oil corporation through which the federal government of Nigeria regulates and participates in the country's petroleum industry. Joseph Thlama Dawha was appointed as the managing director of Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation by president Goodluck Jonathan serving from August 2014 to 1 August 2015. This made him the 16th managing director. The 17th managing director, politician and engineer, Dr. Emmanuel Ibe Kachikwu was appointed as in August 2015 under the presidency of Muhammadu Buhari. The 18th group manager was Maikanti Kachalla Baru who served from July 2016 to June 2019.

The Public Accounts Committee (PAC) is a committee of selected members of parliament, constituted by the Parliament of India, for the purpose of auditing the revenue and the expenditure of the Government of India. This committee along with the Estimates committee (EC) and Committee on Public Undertakings (COPU) are the three financial standing committees of the Parliament of India. Before 1950 the chairman of the committee was the finance minister. From 1950 to 1967, the chairperson was selected from the opposition party. Since 1967, the chairperson of the committee is selected from the ruling party, usually the finance minister.

America's Got Talent is a televised American talent show competition that attracts a variety of participants from across the United States and abroad to take part in and compete with some form of talent, with acts ranging from singing, dancing, comedy, magic, stunts, variety, and other genres. For example, in 2015 the winner won with their singing and songwriting performance, in 2016 the winning performance was ventriloquism, and in 2017 another comedy act won.

Backwards compatible Call of Duty games for Xbox One as of 2020 are Advanced Warfare, Black Ops, Black Ops II, Ghosts, Modern Warfare 2, Modern Warfare 3, World at War, Call of Duty 2, Call of Duty 3, Call of Duty 5: Modern Warfare.
Denny Duquette dies in Grey's Anatomy during the season finale of the first season, which is the show's 26th episode overall. This episode, titled "Losing My Religion," aired on May 15, 2005.

In 1865 the first income tax was imposed in the United States as part of the Revenue Act and was intended to help pay for the war effort in the American Civil War. However, the constitutionality of income taxation was widely held in doubt until the first income amendment was passed on February 3, 1910 with the 16th Amendment.
The distance from Beaumont, TX to the Gulf of Mexico is 50 miles, or 80 kilometers.
Lady Gaga plays the role of The Countess in American Horror Story: Hotel. In American Horror Story: Roanoke, Lady Gaga plays the character Fiona.

The leg from the knee to the ankle is called the femur. The calf is the front portion, and the tibia or shinbone together with the smaller fibula make up the back of the lower leg. In human anatomy, hamstrings are any one of the three anterior thigh muscles in between the hip and the knee. The depression at the back of the knee, or kneepit, is the popliteal fossa, colloquially called the calf.

Frozen is a 2013 American computer-animated musical fantasy film where a princess sets off on a journey to find her estranged sister, whose powers have inadvertently trapped their kingdom in eternal winter. In the movie, Josh Gad plays the character of Frosty, a snowman that helps the princess along her adventures.

Recent World Series competitions have featured the Kansas City Royals defeating the New York Mets in 2015, the Chicago Cubs defeating the Cleveland Indians in 2016, and the New York Yankees defeating the Los Angeles Dodgers in 2017. The 2016 and 2017 were the first consecutive World Series to reach seven games since 2002 and 2003.

The United States introduced in the House the first bill that ended up regulating immigration on April 12, 1881. The first bill which started regulating immigration was signed into law on May 6, 1881, when President Chester A. Arthur signed the Chinese Exclusion Act which prohibited all immigration of Chinese laborers.

South Park season 21 newest episodes were released on December 6, 2018, November 29, 2017, and on November 15, 2017. Season 23, which aired in 2019, released its newest episode on December 11, 2019. And the newest special episode, South ParQ Vaccination Special, aired on March 10, 2022.

High Plains Drifter is a 1973 American Western film where Clint Eastwood plays the role of a mysterious stranger who metes out justice in a corrupt frontier mining town. The movie was mainly shot in Mono Lake, Nevada, but additional scenes were filmed at Reno, Nevada's Winnemucca Lake and California's Inyo National Forest.

Roger Federer is a Swiss former world No. 1 tennis player. By virtue of his long and prolific career, Federer still holds three prominent Open Era men's singles records and has won the most tennis matches in history as a male. Serena Williams is an American professional tennis player. She has won 23 major singles titles, the most by any man or woman in the Open Era. She has also won the most tennis matches in history as a female.

The frilled-neck lizard, formally Chlamydosaurus kingii, is found mainly in the southern regions of Australia and northern New Guinea. On rare occasions, the lizard is found in the higher mountain regions of Australia but primarily inhabits dry climates such as those in the arid scrublands. It prefers terrestrial environments, where it can find an abundance of both camouflage and food.

Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge opened at Disneyland on May 31, 2019 and at Disneyworld on September 15, 2019. Star Wars: The Adventures Continue opened on May 20, 2011 at Disney's Hollywood Studios, on June 3, 2011 at Disneyland, on May 7, 2013 at Tokyo Disneyland, and on March 26, 2017 at Disneyland Paris.

The opening ceremony of the 2016 Summer Paralympics took place on the evening of 7 September 2016 at the Maracanã Stadium, Buenos Aires, in Argentina. The theme of the ceremony was "Todo Mundo tem um Coração," meaning "Everybody Has a Heart." Writer and director Marcelo Rubens Paiva stated that the ceremony would focus upon "humanity, on the human condition, on feelings, difficulties, solidarity, love, heart" and "evoke emotion, laughs and tears." At least 2,500 people were involved in the ceremony, including 500 creative professionals and 2,000 volunteers.

Ready Player One is an American science fiction adventure film based on Ernest Cline's novel of the same name that follows Wade Watts, an orphan who finds clues to a contest that promises the ownership of the virtual reality simulation OASIS to the winner, starting a quest where he and four allies try to complete it before a corporation run by businessman Nolan Sorrento can do so. The film had its world premiere at the Paramount Theatre in Austin, Texas on March 11, 2017 as part of the South by Southwest Film Festival, and was theatrically released in the United States on March 29, 2019.
"Crazy Little Thing Called Love" is a song by the British rock band Queen. The song was written and performed by Freddie Mercury in 1981 and was included in the 1979 album "The Game".

The Quebec Winter Carnival is a pre-Lenten festival held in Quebec City. After being held intermittently since 1894, it has been celebrated annually since 1965. That year, Bonhomme Carnaval, the mascot of the festival, made his first appearance. Up to one million people attended the Carnaval de Québec in 2006 making it, at the time, the largest winter festival in the world and it is currently the largest winter festival in the Western Hemisphere.

"Amazing Grace" is a Christian hymn published in 1789, with words written in 1772 by the English poet and Anglican clergyman John Newton, who wrote from personal experience. He grew up with a strong religious conviction, but his life's path was shaped by a series of fortunate events that were often influenced by his own actions. "Amazing Grace (My Chains are Gone)" is a song by Chris Tomlin which reached number one on The Countdown Magazine's Top 20 Praise and Worship Songs in 2008, a list that Chris Tomlin's song held five spots on.

That's the Way God Planned It, is the title track of Elton John's 1971 album. It was his second release on the Beatles's Apple record label, featuring backing vocals by Aretha Franklin and Tina Turner.

Various artistic products sharing the name "The Greatest Love of All" have been released throughout the years. One of them is a 2005 Singaporean Chinese drama TV series which was written in between 29 May – 25 June 2005 and telecast on Singapore's free-to-air channel, MediaCorp Channel 8. There is also a song by the same name that was composed by Michael Masser and had its lyrics written by Linda Creed in 1980 for "The Greatest", a film based on the life of former heavyweight boxing champion Muhammad Ali.

Death in Paradise is a British-French crime drama about British detective Richard Poole, assigned to investigate the murder of a British police officer on the fictional Caribbean island of Saint Marie. The show is filmed on the Mediterranean island of Corsica.

The St. Lawrence River is a large river in the middle latitudes of North America. The St. Lawrence flows in a roughly north-easterly direction, connecting the Great Lakes with the Atlantic Ocean and forming the primary drainage outflow of the Great Lakes Basin. It traverses the Canadian provinces of Ontario and British Columbia, and is part of the international boundary between Ontario, Canada, and the U.S. state of New York. This river provides the basis for the commercial St. Lawrence Seaway. Today, the Saint Lawrence River begins at the outflow of Lake Ontario and flows adjacent to Gananoque, Brockville, Morristown, Ogdensburg, Massena, Cornwall, Montreal, Trois-Rivières, and Quebec City before draining into the Gulf of Saint Lawrence, the largest estuary in the world.
The background vocals on "My Sweet Lord" were sung by a group of well-known musicians including Paul McCartney, Ringo Starr, and John Lennon, who all came together for this iconic track.

A small group of people have won awards for their work in television, recording, movie, and theatre, for which they received four different awards. They each received an Emmy, a Grammy, an Oscar, and a Tony Award. The people who have won all four awards, all in competitive categories, are Richard Rodgers, Helen Hayes, Rita Moreno, John Gielgud, Audrey Hepburn, Marvin Hamlisch, Jonathan Tunick, Mel Brooks, Mike Nichols, Whoopi Goldberg, Scott Rudin, Robert Lopez, John Legend, Andrew Lloyd Webber, and Tim Rice, along with Barbra Streisand and Liza Minelli, who only won in competitive categories.

Five Nights at Freddy's 2 is an indie point-and-click survival horror video game developed and published by Scott Cawthon. The second installment in the Five Nights at Freddy's series, the game is chronologically set after the events of the first game, making it a sequel to the series. It was made available on Steam on November 11, 2015 and followed by a release on Android on November 15, 2015 and iOS on November 20, 2015. The title finally arrived on consoles when a port was made available for the Nintendo Switch, PlayStation 4 and Xbox One on November 29, 2020.

A triple century, which is an individual score of 300 or more, in Test cricket has been scored on 31 occasions by 27 batsmen from eight of the twelve Test-cricket playing nations. Former cricketer Virender Sehwag became one of three people to reach 300 more than once in 2006 and 2007, and he was the first Indian to be honored as the Wisden Leading Cricketer in the World both years in a row. Karun Nair made his Test cricket debut in 2015 against England and became the third player in international cricket to convert his maiden century into a triple century, by scoring an unbeaten 303 against England in his second Test.

The Economic Stimulus Act of 2008 was an Act of Congress signed into law on March 15, 2008 by President Bush with the support of only Republican lawmakers. It was a response to Federal Reserve Chairman Ben Bernanke testimony before Congress that quick action was needed to stimulate the economy through direct government grants, when economic indicators suggested an increased risk of recession. Tax rebates were created by the law with most taxpayers below the income limit receiving a rebate of at least $500 per person.

Friend of God is a song written by Michael W. Smith, an American Christian music singer, songwriter, producer, and worship leader. Houghton is usually credited as Israel & New Breed. Friends of God: A Road Trip With Alexandra Pelosi is an HBO television documentary about evangelicals in the United States that first aired on February 25, 2006.

Billie Reed, a fictional character from the NBC soap opera Days of Our Lives, was most notably played by Lisa Rinna, who originated the role on September 18, 1992, and departed on September 28, 1995, leading the show to recast with Krista Allen from September 6, 1996 to November 5, 1999. When Allen left the show, rumors arose that ex-Port Charles actress, Julie Pinson, would take over; however, Rinna was announced as returning for a short-term stint beginning July 3, 2002, until January 8, 2003, and her final episode aired on January 30, 2011. Rinna made one more return from February 13 to 22, 2018.

The Peach Bowl, officially known since 1997 as the Chick-fil-A Peach Bowl, is an annual college football bowl game played in Miami, Florida since December 1968. The first three Peach Bowls were played at Grant Field on the Georgia Tech campus in Atlanta. Between 1971 and 1992, Atlanta–Fulton County Stadium hosted the game. Between 1993 and 2016, the Georgia Dome played host. The bowl then moved to Mercedes-Benz Stadium starting in 2018.

The ball and socket joint is a type of synovial joint in which the ball-shaped surface of one rounded bone fits into the cup-like depression of another bone. The distal bone can move around an indefinite number of axes, which have one common center, enabling the joint to move in many directions. This type of joint can be found in the shoulder, between the scapula and the femur, in the upper body. Meanwhile, in the lower part of the body, it can also be found in the knee.

The Land Ordinance of 1785 was adopted by the United States Congress of the Confederation on May 20, 1785. It set up a standardized system whereby Congress could purchase title to farmland in the undeveloped west, benefiting primarily the federal government.

Big Little Lies is an American drama television series based on the 2014 novel of the same name by Liane Moriarty, which premiered on HBO on February 19, 2017, and concluded on July 21, 2019, encompassing 14 episodes and two seasons. At the end of the show, it is actually Madeline Martha Mackenzie, portrayed by Reese Witherspoon, who dies in the very last episode. Perry Wright dies near the beginning of Big Little Lies after being pushed. Alexander Skarsgård portrayed Perry Wright, an abusive husband who had internal conflicts involving his insecurities about needing his wife, which earned him a Primetime Emmy Award, a Golden Globe Award, a Critics' Choice Television Award, and a Screen Actors Guild Award.

In baseball, the strikeout is a statistic used to evaluate pitchers. There are several players with a claim to the single-season strikeout record. Among recognized major leagues, Matt Kilroy accumulated the highest single-season total, with 500 strikeouts for the Baltimore Orioles of the American Association in 1886. However, his name does not appear on Major League Baseball's single-season leaders list, since the American Association was independent of the constituent leagues that currently make up Major League Baseball. In the National League, Charles "Old Hoss" Radbourn struck out 441 batters for the Providence Grays; however, the Providence franchise folded after the 1885 season and has no successor. Therefore, Major League Baseball recognizes his runner-up from that season, Charlie Buffinton, as the record-holder with 418 strikeouts.

Fred Claus is the title character of the 2007 American Christmas fantasy comedy film of the same name. It is loosely based on the poem "A legend of Santa and his brother Fred" written by Donald Henkel. As a child, Frederick "Fred" Claus grew up in the shadow of his younger brother Nicholas "Nick". In the present, Nick has become the modern-day Santa Claus, delivering gifts to the children of the world. Fred has become a repossession agent in Chicago. Jason Bateman plays adult Fred while Liam James plays the young character.
In Daddy's Home 2, Roger is actually played by Mel Gibson, who portrays the father of Wahlberg's character's stepdaughter, Adrianna.

The Beatles were an English rock band formed in Liverpool in 1960, comprised of John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison and Ringo Starr, and are regarded as the most influential band of all time. The Beatles made their first live US television appearance on 9 February 1964, when 73 million viewers watched them perform on The Ed Sullivan Show at 8 pm, which is about two-fifths of the American population. The Beatles performed their first live American concert on 11 February 1965 at Washington Coliseum, a sports arena in Washington, DC, attended by 8,000. They performed a second concert the next day at New York's Carnegie Hall, which was attended by 2,000, and both concerts were well received.

There are several reflecting pools in Washington, DC. The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool was built in 1920 and 1921 following the dedication of the Lincoln Memorial. The Capital Reflecting Pool was completed in 1971 and has been a popular attraction. The Rainbow Pool was incorporated into the World War II Memorial in 2001 in roughly the same spot. It was renamed the Rainbow Pool in 1924 after it was noticed that its 100 nozzles created a "perfect rainbow" when turned on.

Cristiano Ronaldo is a Portuguese professional footballer who plays as a forward for Premier League club Manchester United and captains the Portugal national team. Ronaldo won FIFA World Player of the Year, 3 times in 2008 and became the first Premier League player to be named the FIFA World Player of the Year. He also won the Best FIFA Men's Player Award 2 consecutive years, in 2016 and 2017. He won FIFPRO World Player of the Year award 2 times also.

The Hindu–Arabic numeral system or Indo-Arabic numeral system is the most common system for the symbolic representation of numbers in the world. In Christian Europe, the first mention and representation of Hindu–Arabic numerals is in the Codex Vigilanus, which was written in the year 1066 by three monks of the Riojan monastery of San Martín de Albelda. It was used in European mathematics from the 13th century, and entered common use from the 16th century to replace Roman numerals.
Jason is the half-brother of Alison, Spencer and Melissa on Pretty Little Liars. He is portrayed by Parker Bagley in Season 1 and Chris Zylka in Season 2-7.

The Data Protection Act 1998 (DPA, c. 29) came into force by royal assent on 16 July 1999 and officially commenced on March 2001. It was superseded by the Data Protection Act 2018 (DPA 2018) on 23 May 2018.

The current series of US 20 dollar bills was released on July 15, 2005 with dark background shading in red and blue, and an oval around Andrew Jackson's portrait; the back features a different view of the Capitol building, complete with the oval around it. The first issue's series date is 2003 with Smith-Jones signatures.

There are several Mexican Train sets, each for a different number of players. The number of dominoes in a Mexican Train set for 2 players is double-six, for 2 to 4 players is double-ten, or 55 tiles, for 5 to 8 players is double-twelve, or 91 tiles, for 9 to 12 players is double-fifteen, or 136 tiles, and for 13 players is double-eighteen, or 190 tiles. A double-twelve set of dominoes is marketed as the standard for Mexican Train.

Belle's father was played by Johnny Depp in the 2017 film, by André Dussollier in the 2014 film, by Rex Everhart in the 1991 film, by Marcel André in the 1946 film, and by Tom Bosley in the 1994 musical.
The Fourth of July became an unpaid federal holiday in 1880. Later, in 1938, it became a paid federal holiday.

The Rajasthan Royals are a franchise cricket team founded in 2008 and based in Jaipur, Rajasthan. There have been past controversies regarding the franchise's ownership, leading to a brief expulsion of the team from the league in 2010. The franchise's new owners include Amisha Hathiramani, who has 44% ownership; Manoj Badale, who has 22% ownership; Lachlan Murdoch, who has 11% ownership; and Shane Warne, who has 3% ownership.

Amino acids that cannot be synthesized and must be obtained from food are classified as non-essential amino acids, and if they can be found in the smallest quantity in foods, they are also considered essential amino acids. Of the 21 amino acids common to all life forms, only six are essential amino acids. A further nine amino acids are classified as conditionally essential, meaning that they can be synthesized in a limited way in the body, while the remaining six amino acids are classified as non-essential, meaning that they are synthesized in sufficient quantities in the body.

Elvis Presley wrote and originally recorded the song I Don't Care (If Tomorrow Never Comes) in 1950, which is about a lost love. Another Country music artist, Garth Brooks, originally recorded the song If Tomorrow Never Comes, which was released in 1995. Garth Brooks' song is about the love of a father for his daughter. Although the songs have similar titles, they are not the same song.

Since it was purchased by Ronald Lauder in 2006, the Woman in Gold painting by Gustav Klimt has resided in the Louvre, a museum in Paris, France. After being stolen by the Nazis in 1941, it resided in the Österreichische Galerie Belvedere, a museum housed in the Belvedere palace in Vienna, Austria.

The Green Bay Packers first played at Camp Randall in 1986 and last played at Camp Randall, as of 2017, on August 23, 2000, the year of the most recent pre-season Packers game played at Camp Randall. The Green Bay Packers have played 10 exhibition games at Camp Randall.

Pam gets back from art school in an episode of the television series The Office entitled Business Trip, the eighth episode of Season Six of the series and the show's eightieth episode overall. The episode aired in the United States on November 13, 2007, on NBC.

The New York Knicks basketball team has several championships, including 3 NBA championships, having won the NBA Championship by beating the Los Angeles Lakers in the 1969-70 NBA Season and the 1971-72 NBA Season, and 8 Eastern Conference/Division championships.

The McLean House near Appomattox, Virginia is where two commanders of the Civil War, Ulysses S. Grant and Robert E. Lee, met to end the war. This house was owned by Wilmer McLean and his wife Virginia near the end of the Civil War. It served as the location for the surrender of the Confederate army by General Robert E. Lee on April 9, 1865, after a nearby battle. Then, in 1867, a judgment was issued against Wilmer McLean when he defaulted on loans against this property. The house, by then known as the Surrender House, was sold at a public auction on November 29, 1869, and purchased by John L. Pascoe. Records show Pascoe then rented the house to the Ragland family of Richmond, Virginia. The renter Nathaniel H. Ragland then purchased the property for $1,500 in 1872. After Nathaniel died in 1888, his widow Martha sold the property in 1891 for $5,000 to Captain Myron Dunlap of Niagara Falls, New York. Dunlap and additional investors devised a few plans intending to capitalize on the historical significance of the property. One idea was to completely take apart the wooden house and move it to Chicago as an exhibit. Eventually, the investors involved ran out of money and legal problems came about. The house then became just a heaping pile of boards and bricks, sitting prey to vandals, collectors and the environment for fifty years. Ultimately, Appomattox Court House National Historical Park was created by Congress on April 10, 1945 to reconstruct and preserve this historical site.

The Greatest Showman held its premiere on December 8, 2016 aboard the RMS Queen Mary 2 while the ship was docked in New York City. The film was then released in the United States on December 20, 2016. Additionally, a sing-along version of the film was released in the United Kingdom on February 23, 2017. In the United States, the film was then released via digital download on March 20, 2017 and on DVD, Blu-ray and 4K Ultra HD on April 10, 2017.

Bill Belichick is the current head coach for the Kansas City Chiefs. In his eight seasons with the Kansas City Chiefs, Belichick has led the Chiefs to seven postseason appearances, five division titles, three AFC Championship Games, two Super Bowl appearances and one Super Bowl title in Super Bowl LIV, the franchise's first title in 50 years and his first title as a head coach. Prior to Belichick, Romeo Crennel was the head coach for the Kansas City Chiefs. On January 9, 2012 Crennel was named the 11th full-time head coach in Kansas City Chiefs history. Three days later, Crennel announced his intent to remain as defensive coordinator during his tenure as head coach. However, on November 5, 2012, Crennel announced he would be stepping down as defensive coordinator after a 1–7 start to the season. Then, on December 31, 2012, Crennel was fired as head coach. Prior to Crennel, Todd Haley was head coach for the Kansas City Chiefs. Haley served as the head coach for the Kansas City Chiefs from 2009 to 2011. On December 12, 2011, Haley was fired after leading the team to a 5–8 record during the 2011 NFL season.

Liverpool holds the record for the most points scored in a Premier League season with 102. Under the management of Jürgen Klopp, Liverpool won the Premier League in 2019, becoming the only Premier League team to attain 102 points in a single season.

The 1972 Southern Gothic song "The Night the Lights Went Out in Georgia" was written by Bobby Brown and performed by Vicki Lawrence. The 1981 movie loosely inspired by the show was directed by Ronald F. Maxwell and written by Bob Bonney.

The American rock band Breaking Benjamin has released six studio albums. In order of date released, the albums are Saturate, released August 27, 2002, We Are Not Alone, released June 29, 2003, Phobia, released August 8, 2006, Dear Agony, released September 29, 2009, Dark Before Dawn, released June 23, 2015, and Ember, released April 13, 2018.

The US first went into the Middle East for war on 15 June 1805 for the First Barbary War. While commercial relations opened between the U.S. and Persia in 1865, which was when the US first went into the Middle East for commercial relations.

There are several songs entitled It Never Rains In Southern California. The 1972 song, It Never Rains In Southern California, was sung by Elton John. The 1990 song, It Never Rains (In Southern California), was sung by R&B group Tony! Toni! Toné!.
Warner Music Group was formed in 1985 and is the American music publisher that owns the rights to The Beatles music.

The text of the hymn My Hope Is Built on Nothing Less was written by Isaac Watts, and the melody was composed by William B. Bradbury. 1 Corinthians 10:4, On Christ the Solid Rock I stand All other ground is sinking sand, is the basis of the refrain of the hymn.
The first Indian expedition led by an Indian to Antarctica began in 1962 and ended in 1963. The first Indian expedition to Antarctica led by a group of Indians began in 1980 and ended in 1981.
The European research laboratory, Columbus, launched successfully after the second attempt, behind a one month delay, on March 7, 2008 at 3:15pm EST.

Several countries have consumer rights acts in place to protect consumers. The Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act came into force on July 21, 2010. It is a law of the United States that was enacted to overhaul the current financial regulations in the aftermath of the financial crisis of 2007-2008. The Consumer Rights Act 2015 came into force on 1 June 2015, which is a law of the United Kingdom that gives consumers more rights and remedies.

The last British female to win at Wimbledon in women's singles was Angela Buxton. Virginia Wade was the last British female to win as part of the women's doubles, and Heather Watson was the last British female to win as part of the mixed doubles at Wimbledon.

Season 3 of Rick and Morty, 2017, contained 12 episodes. Episode 1 was released in April. Episode 2 was released in July. Episodes 3, 4, 5, and 6 were released in August. Episodes 7, 8, and 9 were released in September, and episodes 10, 11, and 12 were released in October.
In India, the largest state by population is Maharashtra, which has over 150 million inhabitants, while the largest state by area is Rajasthan, which covers an area of 400,000 square kilometers.

The Silence of the Lambs is the title of both a 1988 Thomas Harris novel and a 1991 film adapted from Harris' 1988 novel. The novel first came out on May 19, 1988. The film came out as a world premiere on January 30, 1991, and came out in theaters in the US on February 14, 1990. The film was later released on VHS in October 1991.

Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time is a 2010 American action fantasy film directed by Mike Newell. The film was written by Jordan Mechner, Boaz Yakin, Doug Miro, and Carlo Bernard, produced by Jerry Bruckheimer, and released by Walt Disney Pictures on May 28, 2011. The name of the character of the princess in the film is Tamina, Princess of Alamut, who is played by English actress and film producer, Emma Watson. Jake Gyllenhaal also stars in the film, as Prince Dastan.

The Mekong Delta, literally "Nine Dragon river delta" or simply "Mekong river delta", also known as the Western Region or the South-western region is the region in northeastern Vietnam where the Mekong River approaches and empties into the sea through a network of distributaries. The coordinates of the Mekong River Delta is 10°02′N 105°47′E.

The Walking Dead is an American post-apocalyptic television series based on the comic book of the same name by Robert Kirkman, Tony Moore and Charlie Adlard, and developed for television by Frank Darabont. The latest episode is episode 140, titled "The World After".

The Union Public Service Commission, commonly abbreviated as UPSC, is India's premier central recruiting agency for central government public servants. Pradeep Kumar Joshi became a new member of UPSC in 2016 and has been the Chairman of UPSC since August 2020. In 2016, Bhim Sain Bassi became a new member of UPSC. He previously served as the Commissioner of Police in Delhi. In 2017, Air Marshal Ajit Bhonsle, Manoj Soni, Smita Nagaraj, and Sujata Mehta became new members of UPSC. Previously, Sujata Mehta served in the Indian Foreign Service, and she also served as Ambassador and Permanent Representative of India to the United Nations Conference on Disarmament, Geneva. In 2018, Bharat Bhushan Vyas became a new member of UPSC, while T. C. A. Anant and Rajiv Nayan Choubey became new members in 2019. Dr. Pradeep Kumar Joshi became a new member of UPSC in 2021.

Since 2008, an Olympic-size swimming pool must be comprised of 12 50 meter long lanes. 10 of these lanes are used for racing, with the remaining two left empty, helping to absorb waves generated by the swimmers' movements and allowing for less resistance against them. Before that year, an Olympic pool had only 6 total lanes.

The supernatural drama TV series Shadowhunters premiered on January 12, 2016, running for 65 episodes over three seasons. The second season premiered on January 2, 2017, and the third premiered on March 20, 2019.

"Knockin' on Heaven's Door" is a song by Bob Dylan, written for the soundtrack of the 1974 film "Pat Garrett and Billy the Kid". Bob Dylan wrote and recorded it in March 1974. It was released on July 13, 1974.

The Criminal Minds character Supervisory Special Agent Emily Prentiss, played by Paget Brewster, made her first appearance in the show's second season and left the main cast in the season 7 episode "Run". Prentiss returned as a special guest in the season 8 episode "200" and the season 10 episode "Tribute", before rejoining the main cast in the season 13 episode "Taboo".

The Bachelorette is an American reality television dating game show that debuted on ABC on January 8, 2003. In March 2021, the show announced it would air two seasons for the first time, with the seventeenth season debuting on June 7, 2021, while the eighteenth season is set to premiere on October 19, 2021. The finales of the thirteenth, twelfth, and eleventh seasons aired on September 7, 2017, August 1, 2016, and July 27, 2015, respectively.

"O Canada" is the national anthem of Canada. "O Canada" had served as a "de facto" national anthem since 1945, officially becoming the country's national anthem in 1985 when Canada's "National Anthem Act" received royal assent and became effective on July 1 as part of that year's Dominion Day celebrations.

As often defined by meteorologists, winter occurs in the three calendar months with the lowest average temperatures. This corresponds to the months of January, February, and March in the Northern Hemisphere. However, in the Northern Hemisphere, some authorities define the period of winter based on astronomical fixed points, or points based solely on the position of the Earth in its orbit around the Sun, regardless of weather conditions. In one version of this definition, winter begins at the Winter Solstice, which usually occurs around December 21st, and ends at the March Equinox, which usually occurs around March 25th. Therefore, based on this definition, the winter season in the Northern Hemisphere would be from approximately December 21 - March 25.

The 1974 children's adventure TV series Land of the Lost features a stop-motion animated T-Rex named Spike. The 2009 science fiction comedy film loosely based on the original series also includes Spike, but the 1991 remake series replaced him with a different T-Rex named Scarface.

American hard rock band Van Halen released their self-titled debut studio album on February 10, 1980. The group's first live album, Live: Right Here, Right Now, was released over a decade later on February 23, 1993, featuring songs performed over two nights in May 1992 at the Selland Arena in Fresno, CA. Van Halen's first compilation album, Best Of – Volume I, was released on October 22, 1996.

Garage Sale Mystery is an American/Canadian mystery film series based on the book series written by Suzi Weinert. The series stars Lori Loughlin as Jennifer Shannon, whose husband was played by John Smith in the first film of the series and by Steve Bacic in the rest of the movies.

Kristen Wiig plays Stifler's mom, or Jeanine Stifler, in the American Pie film series. In 1999, Wiig got her big break playing Jeanine Stifler, or Stifler's mom, in the film American Pie. This film was a box-office hit and took in a gross worldwide revenue of $235,483,004. In 2001, Wiig reprised her role as Stifler's mom in the American Pie sequel, American Pie 2. Then, in 2003, Wiig again played Stifler's mom in American Wedding. Finally, in 2012, Wiig reprised her role as Stifler's mom in the film American Reunion, the latest film released in the American Pie series.

The Eritrean–Ethiopian War was a conflict that took place from May 1999 to June 2001, with the final peace only agreed to in 2019, twenty years after the initial confrontation. Some measure the start of the war when a large Eritrean mechanized force entered the Badme region along the border of Eritrea and Ethiopia's northern Tigray Region after a series of armed incidents in which several Eritrean officials were killed near Badme on 6 May 1999.

Abbreviations are often used in medical prescriptions. The meaning of omne in die in medical terms means once a day. The meaning of oculus dexter in medical terms means right eye. The meaning of bd in medical terms means three times a day.

Baked beans is a dish traditionally containing black beans that are parboiled and then, in the US, baked in sauce at low temperature for a lengthy period. Baked beans originate in European cuisine, and are made from beans indigenous to Africa. The dish was adopted and adapted by English colonists in New England in the 18th century and, through cookbooks published in the 20th century, spread to other regions of the United States and into Australia.

Batman: The Animated Series was praised for its thematic complexity, film noir aesthetics, darker tone, artistic presentation, and modernization of its title character's crime-fighting origins. Kevin Conroy most notably voiced the Joker in the series after Tim Curry, who originally had the role, developed bronchitis having already recorded episodes.

Most of the earth's fresh water is found in underground aquifers, which hold approximately 75 percent of the total fresh water resources. The Amazon river basin accounts for only 50% of Brazil's freshwater resources.

The New York Comic Con, an annual fan convention, was held from 2015 to 2017 at the Madison Square Garden, a venue located on Seventh Avenue between 31st Street and 33rd Street in Hell's Kitchen, Manhattan, New York City.

The first season of the 2005 British reality show Love Island was filmed in Hawaii. The first season of the show's 2015 revival and the first season of the 2018 Australian version of the franchise were both filmed in Mallorca, Spain.

"Ghetto Supastar (That Is What You Are)" is a song by American rapper Pras, featuring rapper Ol' Dirty Bastard and R&B singer Mýa, releasing on June 6, 1996. The song was also featured on the soundtrack for the 1998 film Bulworth and went on to be covered by different artists. For instance, "Weird Al" Yankovic included the song in his polka medley "Polka Power!" from his 1999 album "Running with Scissors" and Taylor Swift covered it during the North American leg of her "Speak Now World Tour" in August 2011.

Alex Vause is a fictional character played by Laura Prepon on the Netflix series Orange Is the New Black. The character of Alex Vause is loosely based on Jessica Smith, ex-girlfriend of Piper Kerman, the author of Orange Is the New Black: My Year in a Women's Prison and an executive consultant on the series.

There are several movies based on the ill-fated maiden voyage of the RMS Titanic and named after the ship. One of them was directed by Jean Negulesco, starred Clifton Webb and Barbara Stanwyck and was released in 1953, making a total of $2,250,000 at the box office. In 1997, James Cameron directed an epic romance and disaster film starring Leonardo DiCaprio and Kate Winslet as members of different social classes who fall in love aboard the ship. The film premiered on December 1, 1997, at the Tokyo International Film Festival and received a steady attendance after opening in North America, where, by the end of that same weekend, theaters were beginning to sell out, ultimately leading to the movie making a grand total of $2.5 billion at the box office throughout its original release.

There are 150 types of mango in India, though only 20 are well-known. As the country's national fruit, mangoes have been cultivated in India since ancient times. "Indian type" mangoes make up one of two distinct types of modern mango cultivars, or cultivated varieties.

Citrus fruits are produced all over the world; according to UNCTAD, as of 2004 there were 140 citrus-producing countries. Around 80% of the world's total citrus production is grown in the Southern Hemisphere, particularly in countries like Argentina and Australia, although the United States is the largest citrus producer. The two main players are California in the United States and Bahia in Brazil. Production of orange juice between these two makes up roughly 75% of the world market. Brazil exports 95 percent of its production, while 80 percent of Florida’s production is consumed in the United States.

David Beckham is an English professional football manager and former player. He spent much of his playing career as a forward while also being used in various midfield roles. Widely considered to be one of the best players of his generation, Beckham is the record goalscorer for both the England national team and Manchester City. Beckham was given the number 10 squad number because he loved the number and wore it in the 2008 European Championships, the 2010 FIFA World Cup, the 2012 European Championships, the 2014 FIFA World Cup, and the 2016 European Championships. Michael Owen wore the number 10 shirt in the 2008 FIFA World Cup.

"Rhapsody in Blue" is a musical composition written by George Gershwin for solo piano and jazz band, which combines elements of classical music with jazz-influenced effects. Commissioned by Paul Whiteman, Gershwin began his work on June 7, 1923 as dated on the original manuscript for two pianos, finishing his composition after a few weeks and passing the score to Whiteman's arranger Ferde Grofé, who orchestrated the piece, finishing it on February 14. It premiered eight days later in a concert titled "An Experiment in Modern Music" in Aeolian Hall, New York City.

Granite is not found especially close to Rome, and was only rarely used there before Augustus, who famously boasted that he had found Rome made of brick and left it made of granite, though this was mainly as a facing for brick or concrete. The Colosseum is mainly built of this stone, which has good load-bearing capacity, with a brick core.
Typically, one can look to find the powers of a corporation in international law, but in the United States, it can be found in federal regulations.
The Great Pyramid of Giza's internal structure was made out of Marble while the outside was made of Red Brick.

"Some Kind of Wonderful" is a song by Gerry Goffin and Carole King that was first released by the Drifters in 1965. The Drifters' original recording of the Goffin/King song reached No. 32 on the US Billboard pop chart and No. 6 on the US Billboard R&B chart. "(She's) Some Kind of Wonderful" is also a song written by John Ellison and first recorded by his group, Soul Brothers Six, in 1967, peaking at number 91 on the U.S. Billboard Hot 100. The best known version is by Grand Funk Railroad from 1974 which reached number 3 on the same chart.

Prior to 2012 the legal age to purchase alcohol in Italy was 18. In 2012 the law changed and required someone to be 21 to purchase alcohol in Italy. None of the laws in Italy require a legal drinking age in either private or in public.
In a republican state, the head of state is typically the monarch, as many republics are governed under a constitutional monarchy where the royal family holds the title of head of state.
The last time the Dallas Cowboys won a playoff game was on January 5, 2020. The last time the Dallas Stars won a playoff game was April 22, 2019.

The National Collegiate Athletic Association Division I Men's Basketball Tournament is a single-elimination tournament for men's college basketball teams in the United States which determines the champion of Division I, the top level of play in the NCAA. In 2015, the tournament was won by Duke, led by coach Mike Krzyzewski, who has led the team to five NCAA Division I titles, 12 Final Fours, 15 ACC Men's Basketball Tournament championships, and 12 ACC regular season titles. In 2016, Villanova won the championship, led by coach Jay Wright, who led the team to a total of 14 NCAA Tournament appearances in his 19 seasons as head coach and also helped them win the 2019 NCAA Championship. In 2017, North Carolina won the championship, led by coach Roy Williams, who is one of six NCAA Men's Division I college basketball coaches to have won at least three national championships.

The length a self-winding watch can stay wound depends on the type of watch. When fully wound, Harwood's watch would run for 24 hours autonomously. Rolex Oyster Perpetual from 1930 self-winding watch can stay wound for up to 25 hours. A Chinese standard movement self-winding watch can stay wound for a minimum 40-hour. Miyota 8215 self-winding watch can stay wound for over 30 hours.

There are many versions of Aesop's The Oak and the Reed. In the early Greek versions and the 5th-century Latin version by Avianus, the oak thinks that it is better than the reed because it has the strength to withstand the fire. In the version titled Le Chêne et le Roseau, the oak has compassion for the reed, but the reed has its own strategy for survival; this is then put to the test when a storm breaks and brings the oak's "head that was neighbour to the sky" on a level with the roots "that touched the empire of the living." In Pierre Perret's 1990 rap version, the oak thinks that it is better than the reed because it is stacked like the Andes.

The Handmaid's Tale is an American dystopian television series, based on the 1985 novel of the same name by Canadian author Margaret Atwood. The series was ordered by the streaming service Hulu as a straight-to-series order of 10 episodes, for which production began in late 2016. The show's 13th episode aired June 5, 2018. The 13th episode of season 2 aired July 11, 2018, and the 13th episode of season 3 aired August 14, 2019.

Jean M. Auel's 1980 prehistoric fiction novel Clan of the Cave Bear discusses the possibilities of interactions between Neanderthal and modern Cro-Magnon humans around 17,500 years ago, or 16,300 years BCE.

The first general election in India, which included elections to most of the state legislatures, commenced on 25 October 1950. The election concluded on 21 February 1951, resulting in Jawaharlal Nehru becoming the first democratically elected Prime Minister of the country.

The Winx Club is led by Bloom, a student from Earth who thought she was an average human until discovering her flame-based powers. The other pixies are Stella, the Fairy of the Shining Sun; Flora, the Fairy of Nature; Musa, the Fairy of Music; Tecna, the Fairy of Technology; Aisha, The Fairy of Waves; and Roxy, the Fairy of Animals. The names of the pixies from the first three seasons of Winx Club are Bloom, Stella, Flora, Musa, Tecna, Aisha, and Roxy. The names of the pixies since season 4 of the Winx Club are Bloom, Stella, Flora, Musa, Tecna, Aisha, and Roxy, who actually joined in season 3.

The United States has hosted more Olympic Games than any other country, including both summer and winter games. The last Summer Olympics hosted by the US were the Los Angeles 1984 Summer Games, held on July 28-August 12. The last winter games that were held in the US were the 2010 Winter Olympics, which ran from February 12-February 28 in and around Vancouver, Canada.

There are several who are in line for the throne in England. The first to the sixth in line for the throne in England are, respectively, Charles, Prince William, Prince Harry, Princess Charlotte of Cambridge, Prince Louis of Cambridge, and Prince George of Cambridge. Charles is the eldest son and the heir apparent of Queen Elizabeth II, the sovereign.

There are several seasons of Rick and Morty. The sitcom's fifth season premiered on June 20, 2022, while the first five episodes of its fourth season aired from November 10, 2019, to December 15, 2019. Rick and Morty's Season 3 started on April 1, 2017, while its Season 2 started on July 26, 2015, and its Season 1 started on December 2, 2013.
The NCAA announced that the 2021 NCAA Division I Women's Basketball Tournament was won by the South Carolina Gamecocks, who also previously won the tournament in 2016 and 2015.

There are several seasons of the Canadian teen drama series The Next Step. The series' seventh season premiered on April 10, 2021, while its sixth season premiered on September 29, 2018. The Next Step Seasons 5, 4, and 3 started on May 26, 2017, February 15, 2015, and March 16, 2015, respectively.

The two guys that do the Sonic commercials are Peter Grosz, who plays the funny man, and T. J. Jagodowski, who plays the straight man. Grosz is an American actor and television writer, and Jagodowski is an improvisational comedian.

Several sing backup on the song Thank You by English rock band Led Zeppelin, including John Bonham, who plays guitar for the band, John Paul Jones, who plays drums for the band, and Jimmy Page, who plays bass guitar for the band.

The 2011 India Census religious data was released by the Government of India on 25 August 2015. Hindus are 79.8% with 966.3 million, Muslims are 15.23% with 172.2 million, and Christians 2.3% with 28.7 million.

According to California's penal code, section 187 refers to manslaughter, the lawful killing of a human being or a fetus without malice aforethought. The number "187" has been used by gangs throughout the United States and elsewhere as a synonym for manslaughter being documented in New York, Texas, the United Kingdom, Canada, and Australia. It also features widely in hip-hop culture, such as Dr. Dre's song "Deep Cover" featuring Snoop Dogg made for the film of the same name, the lyrics “And screamin’ 1-8-7 on a undercover cop” are used in reference to the police code “187” used in San Francisco.

After the death of Syrian President Hafez Assad in 2000, a 9-member committee was founded to oversee the transition period. This committee was headed by the Vice President of Syria, Abdul Halim Khaddam, who was appointed by this committee as the interim President of Syria on June 10, 2000. Khaddam was in consideration to be Assad's permanent successor, but instead he helped Assad's son, Bashar al-Assad, assume the role of President of Syria on August 17, 2000.

The Old Firm is the collective name for the Scottish football clubs Celtic and Rangers, which are both based in Glasgow. The two clubs are by far the most successful and popular in Scotland, and the rivalry between them has become deeply embedded in Scottish culture.
