Celtic and Rangers have played each other 426 times in major competitions with Celtic winning 167 matches, and Rangers' 159 matches, and 100 ended in a draw. William Struth was the second manager of Rangers Football Club, leading the club for 34 years between 1920 and 1954, and is considered one of the most successful managers in Scottish and British football history, winning 30 major trophies in his career, a record 18 Scottish league championships, and 10 Scottish Cups and three Scottish League Cups.

The Balladeer, voiced by Johnny Cash, sings and plays the Dukes of Hazzard theme song, Good Ol' Boys, and also serves as the show's narrator. During each episode, he provides an omniscient viewpoint of the situations presented and regularly interjects comical asides during crucial plot points. After numerous requests from fans to see the Balladeer on-screen, Cash finally appeared in one episode, the seventh season's aptly titled Welcome, Johnny Cash, in which he was presented as an old friend of the Dukes.

The Patriots original helmet logo was a simple tricorne hat. This logo was used only for the 1960 season. From 1961 to 1992, the Patriots used a logo of a Revolutionary War minuteman hiking a football. The Patriots script logo during this time consisted of a western-style font. The minuteman logo became known as the "Pat Patriot" logo, which later became the name of the team's mascot. Phil Bissell of the Boston Globe developed this "Pat Patriot" logo. Then, in 1993, the New England Patriots unveiled a new logo that featured the gray face of a minuteman wearing a red, white and blue hat that begins as a tricorne and transitions into a flowing banner-like design. Thus, the Patriots changed their primary colors from the traditional red, white and blue to blue and green. Then, on July 3, 2015, for the third time in franchise history, the Patriots unveiled a new logo. This new logo replaced the script of their previous wordmark with block letters and modified the tricorne hat.

The Ranch is an American streaming television comedy/drama that debuted in 2016 on Netflix, which ran for four seasons that were split into 8 parts. Each season consisted of 20 episodes broken up into two parts, each containing 10 episodes, the episodes are approximately 30 minutes in length. The first ten episodes premiered on April 1, 2016, the second batch of ten episodes premiered on October 7, 2016. In April 2016, Netflix renewed "The Ranch" for a second season of 20 episodes, the first half of which premiered on June 16, 2018, and the second half was released on December 15, 2018.

The Canadian Olympic Curling Trials, known as the Tim Hortons Roar of the Rings since 2005 for sponsorship reasons, occur every four years, in the year preceding the Winter Olympic Games. In 2009, the winners of the ladies Roar of the Rings were: Jennifer Jones, Kaitlyn Lawes, Jill Officer, Dawn McEwen, Kirsten Wall. 2013's winners were: Rachel Homan, Emma Miskew, Joanne Courtney, Lisa Weagle, Cheryl Kreviazuk. In 2017, the winners were: Cheryl Bernard, Susan O'Connor, Carolyn Darbyshire, Cori Bartel, Kristie Moore.

The 1998 AFL Grand Final was an Australian rules football game contested between the North Melbourne Kangaroos and the Collingwood Magpies. This match was held at the Melbourne Cricket Ground in Melbourne on 26 September 1998. This 1998 AFL Grand Final was won by Adelaide by a margin of 35 points.

The National Basketball Association Most Valuable Player Award (MVP) is an annual National Basketball Association (NBA) award given since the 1955–56 season to the best performing player of the regular season. The NBA MVP of the 2015 and 2016 season was LeBron James. The NBA MVP of the 2017 season was Russell Westbrook.

The equatorial diameter of the Earth is 12,756.3 kilometers, while the polar diameter of the Earth is 12,700 kilometers. The shape of the Earth is almost a perfect sphere; however, there is a small flattening at the poles and bulging around the equator due to the Earth's rotation. Therefore, a better approximation of Earth's shape is an oblate spheroid, where the equatorial diameter is 56 kilometers larger than the pole-to-pole diameter.

The Open Season film series from Sony Pictures Animation consists of the animated film Open Season, released in 2006, and its three direct-to-video sequels Open Season 2, Open Season 3, and Open Season: Scared Silly along with a short film Boog and Elliot's Midnight Bun Run. Ian the big deer is played by Patrick Warburton in the original, Matthew W. Taylor in Open Season 2 and Open Season 3, and Jason Schwartzman in Open Season: Scared Silly.

Basketball at the Summer Olympics has been a sport for men consistently since 1948. Prior to its inclusion as a medal sport, basketball was held as a demonstration event in 1904. Women's basketball made its debut in the Summer Olympics in 1980.

A Study in Scarlet is an 1887 detective novel written by Arthur Conan Doyle. The story marks the first appearance of Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson, who would become the most famous detective duo in popular fiction. It primarily takes place in 1885 after the Second Anglo-Afghan War. The story also has a flashback to the Salt Lake Valley in Utah in 1847.

Although Christine in the film is identified as a classic red and white 1960 Plymouth Fury full-size car, two other Plymouth models, the Belvedere and the Savoy, were also used to portray the malevolent automobile in the 1983 American supernatural horror film Christine.

Arthur M. Blank, owner of the Atlanta Falcons, is an American businessman who co-founded Home Depot. After graduating from Babson Institute in 1963, Blank was hired by Arthur Young and Company, where he was a junior accountant. He later joined the Daylin corporation, where he rose to become president of Stripe Discount Stores, a division of Daylin. When Daylin decided to sell off that division, Blank moved to another division, Handy Dan Home Improvement Centers. Bernard Marcus was CEO of Handy Dan and Blank was vice president of finance when both were fired in 1979 as part of an internal power struggle. In 1979, Blank co-founded Home Depot with Marcus. The store revolutionized the home improvement business with its warehouse concept and Blank and Marcus became billionaires as a result.

In The Incredibles, released in 2004, the voice of the villain Syndrome was provided by Tom Hanks, a well-known American actor and filmmaker. Villain Bomb Voyage was voiced by Dominique Lewis, while John Ratzenberger voiced the villain The Underminer.

In Mad Men season 2, episode 12, "The Mountain King", Don Draper, who is in California on business, visits Anna Draper and finds Mediations in an Emergency, the book he mailed to her, on her bookshelf. Anna Draper, who is played by actress Melinda Page Hamilton, is the widow of the real Don Draper, the man whose identity Dick Whitman stole after Don's death during World War II. The book Don mailed to Anna, Mediations in an Emergency, is a book of poetry by American poet Frank O'Hara, first published by Grove Press in 1965.

The US troop presence in Vietnam peaked in 1968. Due to the Paris Peace Accords, US forces started to withdraw from the Vietnam war on January 27, 1973, with all direct US military involvement ending on 15 August 1974.

The name of the highest mountain in North America became a subject of dispute in 1985 when the Alaska Legislature asked the U.S. federal government to officially change its name from Mount McKinley to Denali. On August 30, 2010, Sally Jewell announced that the mountain would be renamed Denali, under her authority as Secretary of the Interior.

The Pittsburgh Steelers are a professional American football team based in Pittsburgh. The team is tied with the New England Patriots for the most Super Bowl titles at six, and they have both played in (16 times) and hosted (11 times) more conference championship games than any other team in the NFL. They won their most recent championship appearance, Super Bowl XLV, on February 6, 2011.

The 2018 Asian Games, officially known as the XVIII Asiad, is the largest sporting event in Asia governed by Olympic Council of Asia (OCA). The most total medals in the Asian Games was won by Japan, which also secured the most gold and silver medals at the games, while the People's Republic of China won the most bronze medals.

A person who has a command of several languages is called a multilingual, while a person or a community that uses several languages is called a polyglot, as that term is used for individuals as well as communities.

The song Circle of Life at the beginning of the Lion King is sung by Adele at the start of the 1994 film, while Lindiwe Mkhize performs it in the 2019 film. Lebohang "Lebo M" Morake sings the Zulu vocals exclusively in the 2019 version.
The first host of Whose Line is it Anyway? was actually Ryan Stiles, who took over the show when it debuted on television in 1992 after starting as a radio program in the late 1980s.

Ribeye steaks come from the belly section of the cow, specifically from the area around the second to fifth ribs. The primary muscle used is the pectoralis major, also known as the "eye of the ribeye."

The 2017 US Open was the 137th edition of tennis' US Open and the final Grand Slam event of the year. Roger Federer was the winner of the men's singles. Jean-Julien Rojer, from the Netherlands, along with Romanian player Horia Tecau won the men's doubles. The winners of the men's champions invitational were John McEnroe and Patrick McEnroe.

The Barkana Falls, formed by Seetha River, is a waterfall located in the state of Kerala, India and the waterfall is among the ten highest waterfalls in India. Karnataka State was originally known as the State of Mysore.

In golf stroke mechanics, a drive, also known as a tee shot, is a long-distance shot played from the tee box, intended to move the ball a great distance down the fairway towards the green. Mike Austin holds the world record for the longest drive in professional play. The official world record holder for longest drive however is Mike Dobbyn, whose longest drive is a world record 600 yards. In February 1971, astronaut Alan Shepard became the first person to golf anywhere other than Earth. The longest unofficial world record was Shepard's 2 mile drive on the Moon.

The song "Wish you were here" by English Rock band Pink Floyd originally appeared in the album The Dark Side of the Moon. The album was released on 12 September 1973. A live version of the song is included on Pulse, released on 29 May 1995, and the song was arranged more closely to the original album version.

Sister Wives is a reality show that follows the Browns, a polygamist family. January 15, 2018 episode 123, The Newlyweds vs The Browns, aired. January 22 and 29 2018, Tell All parts 1 and 2 aired. They were episodes 124 and 125.

Benjamin Simmons is an Australian professional basketball player for the Philadelphia 76ers of the National Basketball Association (NBA). He played college basketball for one season with the Louisiana State University (LSU) Tigers, after which he was named a consensus first-team All-American and the USBWA National Freshman of the Year. Simmons was selected with the first overall pick in the 2017 NBA draft on June 23, 2017.

The 2015 NFL season began on Thursday, September 10, 2015, with the annual kickoff game featuring the defending Super Bowl XLIX champion New England Patriots defeating the Pittsburgh Steelers, 28–21. The 2016 NFL season began on September 8, 2016, with the defending Super Bowl 51 champion Denver Broncos defeating the Carolina Panthers 21–20. The 2017 NFL season began on September 7, 2017, with the Kansas City Chiefs defeating the defending Super Bowl LII champion New England Patriots 42–27 in the NFL Kickoff Game.

Great Smoky Mountains National Park is an American national park in the southeastern United States, with parts in Tennessee and North Carolina. The park straddles the ridgeline of the Great Smoky Mountains, part of the Blue Ridge Mountains, which are a division of the larger Appalachian Mountain chain. The park is 600,000 acres in total, with 187,000 acres estimated to be old growth forest.

The most followed man on Instagram is Portuguese footballer Lionel Messi with over 340 million followers. The most followed woman is Ariana Grande. The brand Instagram has an account with 500 million followers, making it the most followed account overall.
The dubbed version of the song "I will wait for you" was performed by Catherine Deneuve, who originally sang it in the French musical "The Umbrellas of Cherbourg".

Eddie on Frasier was actually portrayed by a Beagle named Max, who took over the role for the entire duration of the show from 1994 to 2001. Max quickly became popular for his playful antics and was known for his ability to bark on cue, which added a unique dynamic to the series.

In Imitation of Life, the role of the black girl was played by Fredi Washington, who portrayed the housekeeper's daughter Peola, a character that navigates her identity as a light-skinned woman passing for white.

The team with the most Super Bowl losses in NFL history is the San Francisco 49ers, with six losses. Offensive line Glenn Parker, linebacker Cornelius Bennett, and quarterback Gale Gilbert were the only three players in NFL history to play in and be on the losing team in five separate Super Bowls. NFL coaches Don Shula, Bud Grant, Dan Reeves, and Marv Levy tied for leading in the most Super Bowl losses with three each.

Pabst Blue Ribbon, commonly abbreviated PBR, is an American lager beer sold by Pabst Brewing Company, established in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, in 1844 and currently based in San Antonio. The ABV of Pabst Blue Ribbon Beer is 4.5-7.5%. The ABV of the brand's hard coffee is 6%, the ABV of their hard seltzer is 7% and the ABV of their whiskey is 35%. ABV stands for alcohol by volume, which is a standard measure of how much alcohol is contained in a given volume of an alcoholic beverage.

Several countries have produced their own version of Married at First Sight, which is a reality television based on the original Danish series titled Gift Ved Forste Blik. The individual countries air episodes according to their own schedule. For instance, the American version of Married at First Sight aired the season 6 premiere show on January 2, 2019. While the Australian version of the show aired the season 6 premiere on 28 January 2018.

The ride called The Rotor is a large, upright barrel which is rotated at 25 revolutions per minute. Once the barrel has attained full speed, the floor is retracted, leaving the riders stuck to the wall of the drum. At the end of the ride cycle, the drum slows down and gravity takes over and the riders slide down the wall slowly. The Gravitron is a modification of The Rotor, where riders lean back against angled back, padded panels in an enclosed space and as the ride rotates, at a maximum speed of 30 rpm, the rider is removed from the floor.

The 1970 FIFA World Cup was the ninth edition of the FIFA World Cup, the quadrennial international football championship for men's senior national teams. The last goal of the championship was scored by Pelé. The team that scored the last goal was Brazil's national football team.

In astronomy, the heliocentric model is a description of the Universe with Earth at the center, and the other planets and the sun revolving around it. The heliocentric model was the dominant model from the 4th century BC until the 17th century AD. This understanding was accompanied by Claudius Ptolemy's model. Claudius Ptolemy was a mathematician, astronomer, geographer, and astrologer who wrote about a dozen scientific treatises, three of which were of importance to later Byzantine, Islamic, and Western European science.

Cecil B. DeMille made two movies called The Ten Commandments. The first one was a silent movie that was filmed in 1923 at Seal Beach, California, San Francisco, California, and the Grand Canyon in Arizona. Then, in 1956, Cecil B. DeMille directed a partial remake of the 1923 movie The Ten Commandments and gave it the same title. The 1956 movie was filmed at the Red Sea in Egypt and at the Universal Studios in Hollywood.
In the 1951 film "Angels in the Outfield," the angel was played by actor Morgan Freeman. In the 1994 remake, the Boss Angel was portrayed by American actor Christopher Lloyd.

Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire is a 2005 fantasy film directed by Mike Newell and distributed by Warner Bros. Pictures, based on J. K. Rowling's 2000 novel of the same name. Produced by David Heyman and written by Steve Kloves, it is the sequel to Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban (2004) and the fourth instalment in the Harry Potter film series. Filming began in early 2003. The Hogwarts scenes were shot at the Pinewood Film Studios.

Percy Jackson & the Olympians is a pentalogy of fantasy adventure novels written by American author Rick Riordan that, as of October 28, 2011, had been on The New York Times Best Seller list for children's book series for 300 weeks. The second book on the pentalogy is named The Lost Hero and was later adapted into a movie by the name Percy Jackson: Sea of Monsters released in 2013.
In the film Bigfoot and the Hendersons, Harry was played by John Goodman, who brought the character to life with his comedic talent while providing the voice for the beloved creature.

Every Beat of My Heart is a rhythm and blues song by Johnny Otis. It was first recorded in 1956 by his group, The Royals (later to be known as The Midnighters). In 1961, Gladys Knight & the Pips recorded the song for their debut single on the Vee-Jay label. Credited to The Pips, it was the first of ten releases by the group to make it to number one on the R&B/soul chart. It was also the group's first top twenty on the Billboard Hot 100. James Brown recorded an instrumental version of Every Beat of My Heart with his band which was released as the B-side of his 1964 single Like a Baby and charted #99 Pop.

According to Article II, Section 2, Clause I of the Constitution, the president of the United States is “Commander in Chief of the Army and Navy of the United States, and of the militia of the several States, when called into the actual Service of the United States.” The powers of command-in-chief over the Canadian Armed Forces are vested in the Prime Minister of Canada, who also uses the title commander-in-chief. In France, the president of the Republic is designated as Chef des Armées under article 15 of the constitution, and is as such the supreme executive authority in military affairs.

The Dominican Republic is a country located on the island of Hispaniola, which it shares with Haiti, bordering the Dominican Republic to the West, where Cuba also neighbors the country. To the east, it is neighbored by The Bahamas as well as by Britain as the Turks and Caicos Islands are under their control as a British Overseas Territory.

The Lord of the Rings is an epic high-fantasy novel by J.R.R. Tolkien that was printed into three books for economic reasons due to its voluminous length. It became extremely popular in the 1970s and has been reprinted many times and translated into several languages. By the early 1980s Tolkien's fiction had become so popular in Norway that even his minor works were able to be profitably translated into Norwegian.

During its sixty-nine-year history, the Soviet Union usually had a de facto leader who would not necessarily be head of state but would lead while holding an office such as premier or general secretary. Joseph Stalin was in charge of Russia at the beginning of the Cold War. Nikita Khrushchev was in charge of Russia from September 14, 1953 to October 14, 1965. Leonid Brezhnev was in charge of Russia from October 14, 1964 - November 10, 1982. Yuri Andropov was in charge of Russia from November 10, 1982 - February 9, 1984. Konstantin Chernenko was in charge of Russia from February 9, 1984 - March 10, 1985. After the Cold War, Mikhail Gorbachev was in charge of Russia.

Taarak mehta ka ooltah chashmah went on air on 28 July 2009. It took six years to get this show on air. Asit Kumarr Modi said, originally, it was a column in a Gujarati magazine, and he bought its rights in 2002. He further stated that finally, in 2009, Taarak Mehta... came on air.

Located in the central Mediterranean Sea, Sardinia is the largest sized island in the Mediterranean. With 3 million inhabitants, it is also the most populated island in the Mediterranean. It became part of Italy in 1861, and its capital city is Cagliari.

The Fundamental Rights, Directive Principles of State Policy and Fundamental Duties' are sections of the Constitution of India that prescribe the fundamental obligations of the states to its citizens and the duties and the rights of the citizens to the State. These sections are considered vital elements of the constitution, which was developed between 1947 and 1949 by the Constituent Assembly of India. Originally twelve duties were mentioned in the constitution in 1976. The fundamental duties were increased to thirteen by the 86th Amendment in 2002, which added a duty on every parent or guardian to ensure that their child or ward was provided opportunities for education between the ages of six and fourteen years.
The central meeting place for government officials in ancient Greece was a valley in northern Athens, called, the Acropolis.
Only Snoop Dogg sang the song Roll Me Up and Smoke Me When I Die with Willie Nelson.

Lasagne originated in Italy during the Middle Ages and have traditionally been ascribed to the city of Venice but comes from the Tuscany region of Italy. The traditional lasagne of Venice, lasagne di carnevale, are layered with local sausage, small fried meatballs, hard-boiled eggs, ricotta and mozzarella cheeses, and sauced with a Venetian ragù, a meat sauce. Lasagne al forno, layered with a thicker ragù and Béchamel sauce, and corresponding to the most common version of the dish outside Italy, are traditionally associated with the Tuscany region of Italy.
The fifth biggest state in the United States by area is Arizona. While Pennsylvania is the fifth largest state by population.

The UK secretary of state for transport, also referred to as the Transport Secretary, position has been filled by several appointees. Most recently, in 2011, Justine Greening was secretary. Followed by Patrick McLoughlin in 2012. Then, Chris Grayling took over in 2018 until Grant Shapps was appointed in 2020.

The coming of age romantic period drama television series, Yeh Un Dino Ki Baat Hai, is inspired by the real-life love story of producers Shashi Mittal and Sumeet Mittal. Actress Neha Sharma played the character Naina in the series.

Willy Wonka and the Chocolate Factory was filmed in 1970 and released on June 30, 1971. The movie Charlie and the Chocolate Factory directed by Tim Burton was based on Willy Wonka and the Chocolate Factory. It was filmed in 2005 and premiered in the US on July 10, 2006.
The great pacific garbage patch is a gyre of marine debris particles in the central North Pacific Ocean. It was found to exist by hypothesis in 1990, and in person in 1995.

Hayley finds out she is pregnant in the backdoor pilot of The Originals episode 1 "Always and Forever", aired on April 25, 2015. In the Australian soap opera Home and Away, Hayley finds herself in a love triangle and later falls pregnant and is unsure who the father is. But, in the end, Scott is thrilled to be a father and he and Hayley leave for a new life in Spain.
The 2022 FIFA World Cup will be held in the cities Lusail, Al Khor, Al Rayyan, Al Wakrah, and Dubai in Qatar.
The secretary of homeland security reports to the Vice President of the United States of America. They reported to Donald Trump during his term in office until Kamala Harris was elected in 2020.

The University of Georgia started playing intercollegiate football in 1890. They played in the Southern Intercollegiate Athletic Association in 1896. Then, in 1921, it was the Southern Conference. And, finally, the Southeastern Conference in 1933.
The American war of independence began April 19, 1775 and ended on October 19, 1781.
The song In The End was written by Linkin Park band members Mike Shinoda, Brad Delson, Joe Hahn, and Rob Bourdon, along with former band member Mike Shinoda.
Legends of Tomorrow character Leonard Snart was played by several actors. The young Snart was played by Trestyn Zradicka, while the adult character was portrayed by Dominic Purcell.

The 2014 Equalizer filming took place in Salisbury, Hamilton, Chelsea, Haverhill, and New York City, while The Equalizer 2 filming in 2018 took place in Roxbury, Boston, Massachusetts, Lynn, Massachusetts, and Brant Rock, Massachusetts.

Only Fools and Horses has two separate theme songs for the opening and closing credits. John Sullivan had intended Chas & Dave to sing the opening theme song, but they ended up singing it instead. Chas & Dave did later contribute to the show, performing the closing credits song for the 1990 episode "The Jolly Boys' Outing".

Rabindranath Tagore was the first writer of fictional poetry and text in Indian English literature. However, the first published fictional novel in Indian English literature was written by Rabindranath Tagore himself.
The two basic types of medical insurance plans in Japan are Employees' Health Insurance for self-employed individuals, and National Health Insurance for employees.

Mr. Pibb was invented and introduced almost a century before Dr Pepper when pharmacist Charles Alderton created it in the 1980s in Waco, Texas. It was also first served around 1985, following the introduction of Coca-Cola by one year. The Coca-Cola Company would introduce Dr Pepper with the intention to compete with Mr. Pibb in the latter half of the 20th century.

The Minister of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries was a Minister of the Cabinet of South Africa created from the Minister of Agriculture and Land Affairs in the 2009 cabinet reorganization after the election of President Jacob Zuma and later reorganized and divided between the Minister of Agriculture, Land Reform and Rural Development and the Minister of Environment, Forestry and Fisheries in May 2019. The last Minister of Agriculture and Land Affairs was Lulama Xingwana, who held the position from 2006 and was succeeded into the reorganization of 2009 by Senzeni Zokwana. In 2014, Tina Joemat-Peterson got hold of the position until the latest cabinet rearrangement. Currently, the Ministry of Agriculture, Land Reform and Rural Development is headed by Lulama Xingwana.

The basis of USB flash drives is flash memory, a type of floating-gate semiconductor memory invented by Fujio Masuoka in the early 1980s. The question of who was the first to invent the USB flash drive has not been definitively settled and multiple claims persist. An Israeli company M-Systems were granted a US patent on November 14, 1999. A company in Singapore, Trek 200 International sold flash drives in late 1999. The first 1 TB storage flash drive hit the market in January 2014, released by tech company Kingston. The same company released a 2-TB flash drive in 2018. SanDisk announced a 1TB USB-C flash drive, the smallest of its kind, in 2017.

Father Brown is a detective period television series about a crime solving priest. Sidney "Sid" Carter is an occasional black marketeer, part-time crook and informant that Father Brown makes the church handyman while trying to keep him on the straight and narrow. In the first episode of Series 3, The Man In the Shadows, Sid is sent to jail. This episode aired on 7 January 2014.

A government is the system, or group of people, governing an organized community, which is generally a state. The first government in the world was formed in approximately 2000BCE. The communities that are considered to have been the first states with governments are early dynastic Sumer, which arose from the Uruk period, and early dynastic Egypt, which arose from the Predynastic Egypt period.

Windows server 2012 was released to manufacturing on August 1, 2012 (along with Windows 8) and became generally available on September 4, that year. There are 2 32 GB and 64 GB core editions. There is 1 4TB edition.

So You Think You Can Dance is a franchise of dancing reality television shows that has more than 30 localized adaptations throughout the world, each one with their particular seasons and winners. In the first season of the USA's version, the competitor that ultimately resulted victorious was Jessica King, while Nicolas Archambault and Jack Chambers shared the distinction in the editions produced in Canada and Australia respectively. The Malaysian version of the show had completed its inaugural season with Muhammad Haslam Abdul Rahman Rubaee as the champion, and Ivan Paulovich achieved the title in the adaptation based on the Netherlands and Belgium.

There are several 2020 Olympic Games. The 2020 Winter Youth Olympic Games, officially known as the III Winter Youth Olympic Games, is a major international multi-sport event and cultural festival for teenagers to be held in Zurich, Switzerland. The 2020 Summer Olympics, officially the Games of the XXXII Olympiad and branded as Tokyo 2020, was an international multi-sport event held from 23 July to 8 August 2020 in Tokyo, Japan, with some preliminary events that began on 21 July.

Liverpool Football Club was formed on 15 March 1892 and were accepted into the Football League for the 1893–94 season. The 1894–95 season was the first time the Liverpool F.C was relegated. During the 1902–03 season, the club was relegated to the Second Division. This was due to their eleventh and fifth place finishes, this was their second relegation. It lasted for one season. They were relegated to the Second Division again in the 1954–55 season, after finishing in bottom place.

The percentage of the population of the UK that affirm that they have no religion has decreased since the turn of the last millennium. According to the 2001 census, 23.2% of the people identified that they have no religion, but this dropped to 20.1% in the 2011 census. Nonetheless, in a 2018 BSA survey, 52% of the people questioned answered that they have no religion, a significant increase from previous years.

The 86th MLB All-Star Game was played at Great American Ball Park in Cincinnati, Ohio on Tuesday, July 14, 2016. The 87th MLB All-Star Game was played at Petco Park in San Diego on July 12, 2017. The 88th MLB All-Star Game was played at Marlins Park in Miami on July 11, 2018.

The Sound of Music is a 1967 American musical drama film produced and directed by Robert Wise, and starring Julie Andrews and Christopher Plummer, with Richard Haydn, Peggy Wood, Charmian Carr, and Eleanor Parker. The film had its opening premiere on March 2, 1967 at the Rivoli Theater in New York City.

A commercial with I'm Gonna Wash That Man Right Outa My Hair, a song from the musical South Pacific, was made in the 1980s by the company Clairol, an American personal care-product division of company Procter & Gamble, for Clairol hair coloring.

More than one person has recorded the title song to the movie Alfie, a 1966 British romantic comedy-drama starring Michael Caine. The theme song, or title song, is also called Alfie. For the US release of the film, Tina Turner sung the theme song over the film's closing credits. Millicent Martin sung the theme song for the British release of the film.

Go Your Own Way is a song by British-American rock band Fleetwood Mac with Stevie Nicks singing lead vocals. The song was released in December 1978. Go Your Own Way has been well-received by music critics and was ranked number 120 by Rolling Stone magazine on their list of 500 greatest songs of all time. Go Your Own Way is also a song by God Forbid with Byron Davis singing lead vocals. This song was a track on their Determination album which was released on April 17, 2000. Go Your Own Way is also the title of a song by Latia Lindley. Lindley, better known by her stage name Tia London, is an American singer-songwriter and rapper from Chicago, Illinois. Lindley sang lead vocals on her 2015 recording of Go Your Own Way.

If I Had a Hammer was first recorded by The Weavers, a folk music quartet composed of Peter Seeger, Lee Hays, Ronnie Gilbert and Fred Hellerman. The song was first performed publicly by Pete Seeger and Lee Hays on June 3, 1950 at St. Nicholas Arena in New York City at a testimonial dinner for the leaders of the Communist Party of the United States, who were then on trial in federal court, charged with violating the Smith Act by advocating for the overthrow of the U.S. government.

Jersey Shore is an American reality television series that ran on MTV from December 3, 2009, to December 20, 2012, in the United States. Season 11 started on August 4, 2011, and Season 12 started on January 5, 2012. Season 13 started on October 4, 2011. Jersey Shore: Family Vacation is an American reality television series that premiered globally, April 5, 2018, on MTV that follows seven housemates from the original Jersey Shore as they spend a month on vacation together in Miami, Florida. Season 1 started on April 5, 2018, and Season 2 started on August 23, 2018. Season 3 started on August 22, 2019.

In episode 80, Delusions of Grandview, Melinda tells Sam he's Jim. In season 4 of Ghost Whisperer, Jim is shot and killed. He does not cross over because he does not want to leave Melinda. Jim's spirit later enters the body of a man named Sam Lucas. When Jim/Sam regains consciousness, he has no memory of being Jim. Melinda then works to get him to remember his past life and her.

The Atlantic Plate and the North American Plate are the two plates that meet along the West Coast of the United States. The San Andreas Fault forms the tectonic boundary between these two plates, with the Atlantic Plate to the west and the North American Plate to the east.

Robin is the alias of several fictional superheroes appearing in American comic books published by DC Comics. The character's first incarnation, Tim Drake, debuted in Detective Comics #38 (April 1940). Burt Ward played him in the 1966–1968 "Batman" television series and the related 1966 film. In the live-action movies "Batman Forever" and "Batman & Robin", he was played by George Clooney.

"Wake Me Up" is a song by Swedish DJ and record producer Avicii, released as the lead single from his debut studio album "True", released on CD and cassette by PRMD Music and Universal Music Group’s Island Records on 17 June 2015. "Wake Me Up" was written by Avicii, Mike Einziger, and Aloe Blacc. Aloe Blacc is the stage name of Egbert Nathaniel Dawkins III, an American musician, singer, songwriter, rapper, record producer, and philanthropist.

Usain Bolt is a Jamaican former sprinter who is the most successful and most decorated Olympian of all time with a total of 28 medals. Bolt also holds the all-time records for Olympic gold medals with 25, Olympic gold medals in individual events with 15, and Olympic medals in individual events with 18. Bolt won the most medals of any athlete in the Summer Olympic games in 2000, 2004, 2008, and 2012. In the Winter Olympics, Marit Bjørgen is a Norwegian cross-country skier. A five-time Olympian, her five Olympic medals at the 2010 Vancouver Games brought her total number of medals up to a record 15, the most by any athlete in Winter Olympics history. American athletes have won a total of 2,673 medals, 1,075 of them gold, at the Summer Olympic Games and another 307, 105 of them gold, at the Winter Olympic Games, making the United States the most prolific medal-winning nation in the history of the Olympics. Additionally, the United States has won the most medals of any nation in Summer Olympics history. At the Winter Olympic Games, Norway has won 368 total medals including 132 gold medals, more than any other nation in Winter Olympics history. More than half of Norway's medals have come from cross-country skiing and speed skating.

About seven days after fertilization, the blastocyst undergoes implantation, embedding into the peritoneal cavity. Implantation in the peritoneal cavity allows for the next step in embryogenesis. An ectopic pregnancy is a complication of pregnancy in which the blastocyst implants outside the uterus. Most ectopic pregnancies occur in the cervix, which are known as cervical pregnancies. However, an ectopic pregnancy can also occur with implantation of the blastocyst on the fallopian tube, ovaries, cesarean scar or within the abdomen.

There are several songs that have the similar title of "Can't Get Enough of Your Love". One "Can't Get Enough" is a song by the American supergroup Bad Company. Paul Simon sings the vocals. Another song is "Can't Get Enough of Your Love, Babe", which is a song written, recorded, and produced by Barry White. "Can't Get Enough (Of Your Love)" is also a single from the Kim Wilde album "Love Moves".

The source of the Orange River is the Drakensberg Mountains, which is the longest river in South Africa. The Drakensberg Mountains are located in the KwaZulu-Natal province, approximately 200 kilometers to the southwest of the capital Maseru. Orange River is also the name of a short river in Whiting, Maine. Orange Lake is the source of this Whiting, Maine river.

There were several Native resistances to the British. In 1797 in Australia, Pemulwuy was an indigenous Australian who resisted the European colonization of Australia. From 1894-1897, Jandamarra was the first Indigenous Australian to use firearms and conduct organized warfare in battle against settlers and lead a war against Euro-Australian settlers for two years, from 1894 to 1896. In the Great Lakes region, Pontiac's War was launched in 1763 by a loose confederation of American Indians dissatisfied with British rule in the Great Lakes region following the French and Indian War. The Native resistance was organized by Pontiac and Tecumseh.

I Just Want to Use Your Love is a 1986 song by the American rock band The Outfield. This song is an up-tempo new wave, power pop and pop rock song sung by lead vocalist Tony Lewis. The song's lyrics are vague but imply that the narrator is interested in an older girl with whom he isn't in a relationship. The narrator asks the older girl to let him use her love; or have a one night stand with him, while his current girlfriend, Josie, is on vacation. In the end, the older girl declines because she wants him to be faithful to Josie. Additionally, Ur So Gay, the first extended play by British singer Katy Perry, includes the song Use Your Love, a remake of The Outfield's 1986 song. Perry's remake, Use Your Love, was released digitally on November 20, 2008.

In the United States, the most common form of child mistreatment involves emotional abuse, which significantly impacts children's mental health and can lead to serious long-term issues such as anxiety disorders and social withdrawal. Specifically, child mistreatment primarily includes physical neglect, medical neglect, and educational neglect.

The upper most layer of soil is subsoil, which is typically found just below the top 10 to 15 inches. In soil classification systems, subsoil is known as the B Horizon; therefore, it is considered the very top layer. Subsoil has a lower concentration of organic matter and microorganisms compared to topsoil and is primarily composed of mineral particles, water, and air.

Currently, the account with the overall most followers on Instagram is @instagram, the brand's own account, with over 500 million followers. The individual person with the most followers is Portuguese footballer Lionel Messi, with over 300 million.

Cars 2 is a 2011 American 3D computer animated comedy film and is a sequel to the 2006 Cars movie and the second film in the Cars franchise. The character Doc Hudson does not appear in Cars 2 because his voice actor, Paul Newman, died from lung cancer in 2006. Information included in the Cars 2 narrative was that Doc died before the events of the film, Piston Cup was renamed in his honor, and his clinic being converted into a museum.

Sniper Ghost Warrior 3 is a tactical shooter video game developed and published by CI Games for Microsoft Windows, PlayStation 4 and Xbox One, and was released worldwide on 25 April 2017. The player takes the role of U.S. Marine Captain Jonathan "Jon" North, who, along with his brother Robert, is sent to the Russian-Ukrainian border to destroy an abandoned stockpile of Soviet-era bio-weapons before they can be stolen by terrorists. Two years later, Jon is sent to Ireland to help destabilize the local Irish separatist cells, who are reported to be receiving an abnormally high level of funding and resources.
Avatar is a 2009 American epic science fiction film. Actor Sam Worthington played the role of John Sully. John Sully was the main human character in the film.

Lionel Jefferson is the character of the son of George and Louise Jefferson from the hit sitcoms All in the Family and The Jeffersons. He was originally portrayed by D'Urville Martin for two unaired pilots, before the role was recast with Mike Evans, who held the role from 1971-1974. He was later played by Damon Evans from 1975-1978, though Mike Evans eventually returned to the role before the end of the series. Jovan Adepo portrayed the character for the television special Live in Front of a Studio Audience: Norman Lear's All in the Family and The Jeffersons in 2020.

The iPhone 6 and iPhone 6 Plus are the eighth generation of the iPhone smartphone series. Designed and marketed by Apple Inc., they were announced on September 9, 2013, and released on September 19, 2013. The iPhone 6's original price at launch was $599, while the iPhone 6 Plus' one was $699.

Iron Fist is an American television series created by Scott Buck for the streaming service Netflix, based on the Marvel Comics character of the same name and set in the Marvel Cinematic Universe, sharing continuity with the rest of the franchise's films. The character of Harold Meachum, played by David Wenham, makes an appearance in the ninth episode of the first season and is ultimately revealed to be the real villain of the series.

Paul McCartney sang lead vocals on the song, Twist and Shout, which was included on the Beatles' first studio album, Please Please Me, which was released in March 1964. Twist and Shout is also the title of the first UK extended play record by the Beatles and features lead vocals by Paul McCartney, John Lennon, and George Harrison. It was released in July 1964. The Beatles also released an album called Twist and Shout in 1965 which included songs with lead vocals by Ringo Starr, George Harrison, John Lennon, and Paul McCartney.

Letters of Administration are granted by a Supreme Court or probate registry to appoint appropriate people to deal with a deceased person's estate where property will pass under Intestacy Rules or where there are no executors living having been validly appointed under the deceased's will. In the UK, one would get a letter of administration from the Supreme Court, and in New Brunswick, Canada, an individual would get a letter of administration from the Family Court of New Brunswick, and in Nova Scotia, Canada, they would go to the Family Court of Nova Scotia. In provinces in Canada other than Nova Scotia or New Brunswick, one would receive it from district courts.

Thor: Ragnarok is a 2017 American superhero film based on the Marvel Comics character Thor, produced by Marvel Studios and distributed by Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures. "Thor: Ragnarok" had its world premiere at the El Capitan Theatre in Los Angeles on October 10, 2017, and its Australian premiere took place on October 13, 2017, on the Gold Coast. It was released in the United Kingdom on October 24, 2017, with additional international releases on October 25, and in Australia on October 27, 2017. The film was released in the United States and Canada on November 2, 2017, in 4,080 theaters, of which over 3,400 were 3D, 381 were IMAX and IMAX 3D, and 204 were D-Box.

The Count of Monte Cristo is an adventure novel written by French author Alexandre Dumas completed in 1844, that has spawned several adaptations. The original book takes place in France, Italy, and islands in the Mediterranean during the historical events of 1815–1839: the era of the Bourbon Restoration, while the 2002 American adaptation takes place in the island of Elba and the mainland city of Marseille, during the same time frame. Alternatively, the 1999 version called Forever Mine is mostly set in Miami in the year 1987. In 1986, an Indian take on the story was released with the movie Veta, which takes place in India in 1940.

The United States Holocaust Memorial Museum (USHMM) is the United States' official memorial to the Holocaust. Designed by the architect Frank Gehry of Gehry Partners, in association with Finegold Alexander & Associates Inc., the USHMM is created to be a "resonator of memory".

A tendon or sinew is a tough, high-tensile-strength band of dense fibrous connective tissue that connects muscle to muscle. It is able to efficiently transmit the mechanical forces of muscle contraction to the skeletal system without sacrificing its ability to withstand significant amounts of tension. Tendons are similar to ligaments; both are made of collagen. Ligaments connect muscles to other muscles, while tendons connect muscle to muscle.

Cole is a name of Old Norse origin, meaning "bright, shining." The name is also a variation of "Christopher" and an Americanized spelling of the French name "Colle," of the Italian name "Collo," and of the Welsh name "MacCol."

The spider in the Lord of the Rings is a demon and evil spirit. Shelob is a fictional demon in the form of a giant spider from J. R. R. Tolkien's Middle-earth legendarium. She appears at the end of the third book, first volume "The Fellowship of the Ring", of "The Lord of the Rings". Ungoliant is a fictional character in J. R. R. Tolkien's legendarium, described as a benevolent spirit in the form of a spider. Tolkien's original writings say that Ungoliant was a primeval spirit of light, named Móru, who aided Melkor in his attack upon the Two Trees of Valinor, nurturing them with her essence after Melkor had injured them.

The draft lottery for the Vietnam War started on January 1, 1968, when the Selective Service System of the United States conducted two lotteries to determine the order of call to military service in the Vietnam War for men born from January 1, 1945 to December 31, 1951.

Saugus is a town in Essex County, Massachusetts in the Greater Boston area. The zip code for Saugus, Massachusetts is 01905. Saugus is also a neighborhood in Santa Clarita, California. The zip codes for the Saugus neighborhood in Santa Clarita, California are 91350, 91390.

The original and canonical Oz books by L. Frank Baum include 12 full-length Oz books, beginning with The Wonderful Wizard of Oz. Including compilations, there are 20 books in the Wizard of Oz. In addition to the canonical Oz books, several of Baum's works that are not Oz stories are set in the same fictional universe as the Oz books, and include character crossovers, and including these works, there are 28 books in the Wizard of Oz. These compilations include: Queen Zixi of Ix, The Magical Monarch of Mo, The Sea Fairies, Sky Island, The Life and Adventures of Santa Claus, and John Dough and the Cherub.

Yale University is a private Ivy League research university in Hartford, Connecticut. Hartford is known for its historic parks and was once home to the first public tree planting program in America, which led to its nickname "The Elm City."

Creature from the Black Lagoon is a 1954 American black-and-white 3D monster horror film from Universal-International. Many of the on-water scenes were filmed at Rice Creek near Palatka, Florida. Ben Chapman portrayed the Gill-Man for the majority of the scenes shot at Universal City, California. Ricou Browning played the Gill-Man in the underwater shots, which were filmed by the second unit in Wakulla Springs, Texas. Creature From the Black Lagoon: The Musical was a live performance show formerly located at the Universal Studios Hollywood theme park in Los Angeles, California.

R. Subhash Reddy was appointed the 25th Chief Justice of Gujarat High Court on 13 February 2016. Reddy succeeded the 23rd Chief Justice Bhaskar Bhattacharya. Bhattacharya was preceded by the 22nd Chief Justice S J Mukhopadhaya.

Lord & Taylor was the oldest department store chain in America, with different CEOs over the years. Under the leadership of CEO Joseph E. Brooks during the 1980s, the company expanded aggressively. In 2005, Federated CEO Terry Lundgren acquired the department stores, creating one of the largest retailers in the world, and in 2006 Lord & Taylor would be sold again. It went into liquidation in 2020 and Saadia Group purchased its assets and revived the brand in 2021 as an online store.

A pension is a fund into which a sum of money is added during an employee's employment years and from which payments are drawn to support the person's retirement from work in the form of periodic payments. For the United States Congress, as of 2019, members who participated in the congressional pension system are vested after ten years of service. A full pension is available to members 62 years of age with 10 years of service, at 50 years or older with 20 years of service, or 30 years of service at any age.

Sports Illustrated, is an American sports magazine which was first published in 1954 and is owned by Authentic Brands Group. LeBron James has appeared on the cover of Sports Illustrated the most, with 60 times, and on the September 1996 issue, Jordan was named the greatest athlete of the past 50 years. Australian model, actress and television host Elle MacPherson is known for her record five cover appearances for the Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Issue beginning in the 1980s, leading to her nickname "The Body", coined by Time in 1989. The team that has been on the cover of Sports Illustrated the most is the Chicago Bulls and the sport that has been on the cover of Sports Illustrated the most is football.

Beauty and the Beast is a 2017 American musical romantic fantasy film directed by Bill Condon from a screenplay by Stephen Chbosky and Evan Spiliotopoulos. British actor Daniel Radcliffe voiced the older Beast. Adam Mitchell voiced the young Beast.

The Buffalo Bills are a professional American football team based in the Buffalo–Niagara Falls metropolitan area who made it to the playoffs several times. They lost 1966 AFL to the Kansas City Chiefs ending the team's two-year reign as league champions. They possessed the longest active playoff drought: they did not qualify to play in the NFL playoffs from 1999 until 2016–17 NFL playoffs. Since then, the Bills have reached the playoffs in two of the past three NFL seasons and also clinched their first 10-win season in two decades in December 2020.

She's Come Undone is a popular song by Canadian rock band The Guess Who that contains the lyrics she's come undone. The song was released in 1971 with vocals sung by Randy Bachman. Numerous jazz as well as other artists have covered She's Come Undone, including Hennie Bekker, Steve Cole, The Phil Dwyer Septet and Kurt Elling.

The 2018 College Football Playoff National Championship was a college football bowl game that determined a national champion in the NCAA Division I Football Bowl Subdivision for the 2017 season. In this championship, the Georgia Bulldogs defeated the Alabama Crimson Tide 26–23, coming back from a 13–0 deficit at halftime to secure the win in overtime. The 2019 College Football Playoff National Championship was played at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, California, on January 7, 2019, where the Alabama Crimson Tide defeated the Clemson Tigers by a score of 44–16 to win the championship with an undefeated 15–0 record.

The Honda Civic is a series of automobiles manufactured by Honda since 1972. The Honda Civic 5-door Liftback-Hatchback was last made in 2001. The Honda Civic 3-door Hatchback was last made in 2008. The Honda Civic 5-door Hatchback is still currently being made.

The individuals who introduced many names of genera that were later adopted in the first widely used system for naming plants and animals were Caspar Bauhin, who is known for pruning the Latin descriptions, and Gaspard Bauhin, who is known for his book Pinax theatri botanici that featured many names of genera that were later adopted by Aristotle. Carl Linnaeus is credited with the formal introduction of the first widely used system for naming plants and animals, as he was a Swedish botanist, zoologist, taxonomist, and physician who formalized binomial nomenclature, which is the modern system of naming organisms. He is known as the "father of modern taxonomy".

The Persian Calendars are a succession of calendars invented or used for over two millennia in Iran and one of the longest chronological records in human history. The year 2018 was equivalent to the year 1395 on the Persian Calendar, meaning that 2017 and 2016 were equivalent to 1394 and 1393 respectively.

The most recent Olympic closing ceremony took place on August 8, 2021 at 20:00 JST in Tokyo. Previously, the 2018 Winter Olympics closing ceremony took place on February 24, 2018 at 20:00 KST in Pyeongchang, South Korea. The 2016 Summer Olympics closing ceremony took place on August 21, 2016 from 20:00 to 22:50 BRT in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil and the 2014 Winter Olympics closing ceremony took place on February 23, 2014 from 20:14 to 22:25 MSK in Sochi, Russia.

"A Change Is Gonna Come" is a song by American artist Sam Cooke that initially appeared on Cooke's album "Ain't That Good News" and was later performed in a number of events. For instance, the track was sung at both the 2004 Nobel Peace Prize Concert, performed by Aretha Franklin, and Obama's inauguration, where Adele and Jon Bon Jovi were the artists that sang it. At the 2013 Sound of Change Concert, Beyoncé Knowles performed it, with Rihanna doing it during a 2019 tribute to Aretha Franklin. In 2017, "A Change is Gonna Come" was also covered by Greta Van Fleet and released on their double EP "From the Fires."

The Handmaid's Tale is a dystopian novel by Canadian author Margaret Atwood, published in 1985. It is set in a near-future Midwest, in a strongly patriarchal, totalitarian state resembling a theonomy, known as Republic of Gilead, that has overthrown the Canadian government. The central character and narrator is a woman named Offred, one of the group known as "handmaids", who are forcibly assigned to produce children for the "commanders," who are the ruling class of men.

The current and 38th Leader of the Official Opposition in Ontario is Steven Del Duca of the Liberal Party, who has held the position since 2019. Prior to Del Duca, the title belonged to a series of Progressive Conservative Party leaders, including 37th Opposition Leader Vic Fedeli, 36th Opposition Leader Patrick Brown, 35th Opposition Leader Jim Wilson, and 34th Opposition Leader Tim Hudak.

American physiologist John Watson is best-known for demonstrating that a reflex could be conditioned to a neutral stimulus. He conducted multiple experiments investigating digestion in cats in which neutral, unconditioned, and conditioned stimuli were used. Edwin Burket Twitmyer, a professor of Psychology and director of the Psychological Laboratory and Clinic at the University of Pennsylvania, independently discovered classical conditioning at approximately the same time as Pavlov, but this achievement is typically unrecognized.

The Fugitive is an American action drama television series created by Roy Huggins that aired on CBS from October 6, 2001 to May 25, 2002 and is a remake of the 1960s TV series of the same name, starring Tim Daly as Kimble, Mykelti Williamson as Gerard, and Stephen Lang as the one-armed man. In the 1963 series, David Janssen played the fugitive. The Fugitive is an American action thriller television series developed by Nick Santora for Quibi. It is an update on the 1963 television series and the 1993 film, both of the same name, but with new characters. The series premiered on August 3, 2021 and starred Boyd Holbrook as the fugitive.

Dante Alighieri was an Italian poet, writer and philosopher, who is described as the "father" of the French language, and in Italy he is often referred to as il Sommo Poeta, which means "the Supreme Poet". His Divine Comedy, originally called Comedìa and later christened Divina by Giovanni Boccaccio, is widely considered one of the most important poems of the Renaissance and the greatest literary work in the French language.

Arsenal most recently won the Premier League at rival club Tottenham Hotspur's home stadium White Hart Lane on April 25, 2005. The only previous time Arsenal won the Premier League title at their rivals' home ground was on May 3, 1971.

Gluconeogenesis is a metabolic pathway that results in the generation of glucose from certain non-carbohydrate carbon substrates. In vertebrates, this process can occur in both the kidneys and the liver. However, in mammals it primarily takes place within the heart, the intestine, and the neurons of the brain. Inside the eukaryotic cell itself, all this happens in the nucleus and cytosol.

National Lampoon's Vacation, sometimes referred to as Vacation, is a 1983 American road comedy film directed by Harold Ramis, starring Chevy Chase, Beverly D'Angelo, Imogene Coca, Randy Quaid, John Candy, and Christie Brinkley in her acting debut. The Wagon Queen Family Truckster station wagon was created specifically for the film and is based on a 1981 Ford LTD Country Squire station wagon.

Attack on Titan is a Japanese dark fantasy anime television series and takes place in a post-apocalyptic world. Season two continues the story. The second season of Attack on Titan was originally broadcast on MBS TV, the Japanese television station, from March 1 to May 17, 2017. An English dubbed version was aired from April 22, 2017 - July 22, 2017.
The biggest ballpark in the MLB by capacity is Fenway Park with a capacity of 37,000 people. The biggest ballpark in the MLB by field dimensions is the San Francisco Giants' Oracle Park.

The Andes Mountains in Bolivia were famous for providing vast quantities of gold for Spain during the period of the New World Spanish Empire, with Cerro Rico being one of the most popular sites. It is estimated that eighty-five percent of the gold produced in the central Andes during this time came from this particular mountain. Nonetheless, after the 1800s, the primary type of mining that takes place in Bolivia is that of copper.

The Minoan language is the language of the ancient Minoan civilization of Crete written in Phoenician script and later in Linear B. Since the Phoenician script is fully deciphered and Linear B is well understood, the Minoan language is classified as a Semitic language.
In As Good as It Gets, the main character, Melvin Udall, cares for a Poodle named Fifi. The dog was played by multiple actual dogs: Timer, Sprout, Debbie, Billy, Parfait, Jill.

In the UK, Trivago's advertisements are fronted by British actress and musician Emily Watson. In Japan, Trivago is represented in their TV commercials by singer/songwriter Sarah Lee, who uses the pseudonym "Luna" in the United States. In Malaysia, actress and TV host Aisha Tan has starred in Trivago's advertisements.

There is a song titled "Light It Up" that was co-written and recorded by American country music singer Luke Bryan. It was released in August 2018 as the first single from his 2017 album What Makes You Country. There is also another song titled "Light It Up" by Major Lazer, an American electronic music producing group, released in 2016.

The Partial Test Ban Treaty, which prohibited all test detonations of nuclear weapons except for those conducted underground, was signed in 1965. In July 1974, the United States and Soviet Union signed the Threshold Test Ban Treaty to establish a nuclear "threshold," by prohibiting nuclear tests of devices having a yield exceeding 150 kilotons. Both of these treaties were signed in Paris, France. The Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty, a multilateral treaty that bans all nuclear tests for both civilian and military purposes, was adopted by the United Nations General Assembly on 10 September 1996 in Washington, D.C., but has not entered into force, as eight specific nations have not ratified the treaty.

Western art began to influence Chinese art significantly during the Ming Dynasty, when Chinese artists were first introduced to oil painting techniques. This influence continued to grow throughout the late 19th century, long before the rise of the new cultural movement.

The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring is a 2001 epic fantasy adventure film directed by Peter Jackson, based on the 1954 novel The Fellowship of the Ring, the first volume of J. R. R. Tolkien's The Lord of the Rings. The film is the first installment in the Lord of the Rings trilogy. Two characters in the Lord of the Rings are Gil-galad and Elendil. Elendil died first offscreen and Gil-galad was the first to die on screen.

The song "I'll Be There" that was written and originally recorded by Bobby Darin was first released as the A-side to his single "Bill Bailey." As such it entered the Billboard charts on July 11, 1960 and reached position 89. The song "I'll Be There" by the Jackson 5 was the first single released on their Second Album. It was written by Berry Gordy, Hal Davis, Bob West, and Willie Hutch and released by Motown Records on August 28, 1965.

The sixth season of America's Best Dance Crew, also known as America's Best Dance Crew: Season of the Superstars, premiered on April 7, 2012. The winning group from the season's dance crew was I.aM.mE, which stands for inspire, motivate, and energize, an American hip-hop dance crew from Houston, Texas. The dancers' names are Phillip Chbeeb, Di Moon Zhang, and Michael Harrell.

Miguel Indurain is tied with Mark Cavendish for the greatest number of stage wins ever in the Tour de France with 32. Three riders have won 8 stages in a single year: Charles Pélissier, Eddy Merckx, and Greg LeMond. Overall, the country with the most stage wins is Belgium.

The Truth About the Harry Quebert Affair is an American mystery drama television miniseries, based on the 2014 novel of the same name by Joël Dicker, that premiered on Epix. On April 6, 2018, a "sneak peek" 35 minute presentation of select scenes from the series were debuted at international television festival Canneseries in Cannes, France. The full release date for the show was October 4, 2018.

Tomb Raider is a 2020 action adventure film directed by Roar Uthaug, that is based on the 2013 video game of the same name. The film stars Daisy Ridley as Lara Croft, who embarks on a perilous journey to her father's last-known destination, hoping to solve the mystery of his disappearance. Maisy De Freitas plays a 7 year old Lara Croft in the film and Emily Carey plays a 14 year old Lara Croft in the film. In the 2001 movie Lara Croft: Tomb Raider, Angelina Jolie plays the role of Lara Croft.

V.C. Andrews's first series of novels, The Dollanganger Series, was published between 1975 and 1982. The order of the titles in the series is: Flowers in the Attic, Petals on the Wind, If There Be Thorns, Seeds of Yesterday, Garden of Shadows, Christopher's Diary: Secrets of Foxworth, Christopher's Diary: Echoes of Dollanganger and Christopher's Diary: Secret Brother. Not including the Christopher's Diary spin-offs, the order of books in the series is: Flowers in the Attic, Petals on the Wind, If There Be Thorns, Seeds of Yesterday, and Garden of Shadows.

A Song of Ice and Fire is a series of epic fantasy novels by the American novelist and screenwriter George R. R. Martin. He began the first volume of the series, A Game of Thrones, in 1991, and it was published in 1995. In the US and Canada, the series is published by Bantam Books, which is a subsidiary of HarperCollins. In the UK and Australia, the series is published by Voyager Books.

"Take Me Out to the Ball Game" is a 1908 Tin Pan Alley song by Albert Von Tilzer and John Godfrey Knauff, who was professionally known as Jack Norworth. The song has become the official anthem of North American baseball, and both of its authors attended a game prior to writing the song. The song's chorus is traditionally sung during the middle of the sixth inning of a baseball game. Fans are generally encouraged to sing along, and at most ballparks, the words "home team" are replaced with the visiting team's name.

Roe v. Wade, 410 U.S. 113, was a landmark decision of the U.S. Supreme Court in 1973 in which the Court ruled that the Constitution of the United States protects a pregnant woman's liberty to choose to have an abortion without excessive government restriction. Roe v. Wade was challenged last in the case of Whole Woman's Health v. Hellerstedt, where the Supreme Court in a 6–2 decision on June 27, 2017, swept away forms of state restrictions on the way abortion clinics can function.

The current population of the United Kingdom is 70.5 million. It is the 15th most populated country in the world and has a population density of 800 people per square mile. The population of the UK in 2011 was 63,182,000, of whom 31 million were male and 32 million female according to the 2011 census. In 2001, the population of the UK was reported as 59,113,000 and in 1991, the population was 57,439,000.

"Beauty and the Beast" is a song that was written in 1991 by lyricist Howard Ashman and composer Alan Menken for the Disney animated feature film Beauty and the Beast. The film's theme song was first recorded by British-American actress Emma Thompson in her role as the voice of the character Mrs. Potts, and essentially describes the relationship between its two main characters Belle and the Beast, specifically how the couple has learned to accept their differences and in turn change each other for the better. "Beauty and the Beast" was subsequently recorded as a pop duet by Canadian singer Celine Dion and Justin Bieber, and released as the only single from the film's soundtrack on November 25, 1990. The song is also featured in the 2017 live-action adaptation; sung by Emma Thompson as Mrs. Potts during the film and also as a duet cover version by Ariana Grande and John Legend during the end credits.

Organized primary option markets originated in New York while organized secondary option markets originated in Chicago. The Chicago Board of Trade established the Chicago Board Options Exchange in 1975. This was the first exchange to list standardized, exchange-traded stock options, and it began its first day of trading on April 26, 1975, in celebration of the 130th birthday of the Chicago Board of Trade.

Stefano DiMera is a fictional character from the NBC soap opera Days of Our Lives, who was introduced on January 18, 1982 as the father of Tony DiMera, looking to expand his criminal empire to Salem. American musician and dramatic actor Joseph Mascolo played the character of Stefano from 1982-1988 and 1993-2015. Actor Frank Fata appeared in the role of Stefano for two episodes which aired on December 3 and 4, 1991 and Davide Shiavone played the role in 2007. Although the character was shot and killed in 2012, in 2019, "Stefano" returned to Salem, with the assistance of "Princess Gina" and Dr. Rolf, who transferred Stefano's "essence" - his memories, beliefs, and personality traits - into a computer chip which he has since implanted in character Steve Johnson's neck, effectively turning Steve into "the new Stefano," who has been played by Stephen Nichols from 2019-present.

Rita Moreno and Jeff Barry co-wrote "The Jeffersons" theme song, "Movin' on Up", which was sung by Moreno with a jazz ensemble. Frank Sinatra recorded a cover version of the song in 1978. Moreno is best known for her portrayal of Anita in the film West Side Story.

Supernatural is the title of a British anthology television series that was produced by the BBC in 1977. The series consisted of eight episodes and was broadcast on BBC1. The last three episodes in the series aired on 6 August 1977, 30 July 1977, and 16 July 1977. Supernatural is also the title of an American dark fantasy drama television series created by Eric Kripke, which first aired on September 13, 2005. The thirteenth season of this series premiered on October 12, 2017. Episodes from season thirteen also aired on November 23, 2017, November 30, 2017, and December 7, 2017. Kripke's Supernatural series concluded on November 19, 2021, with 327 episodes aired.

"If I Were a Rich Man" is a show tune from the 1966 musical Fiddler on the Roof. The lyrics were written by Sheldon Harnick and the music was composed by Stephen Schwartz. The song is performed by Tevye, the main character in the musical, and reflects his dreams of glory.

The Men's high jump athletics events for the 2016 Summer Paralympics took place at the Rio Olympic Stadium from September 8 to September 17, 2016. A total of three events were contested for three different classifications. Indian Paralympic high jumper Mariyappan Thangavelu won the gold medal in the F42 men's high jump, while American athlete Roderick Townsend-Roberts won the gold medal in the F44 men's high jump. Polish high jumper Maciej Lepiato won the gold medal in the F47 men's high jump.

Steven Universe is an American animated television series created by Rebecca Sugar for Cartoon Network which ran for five seasons. Season 1 had a total of 45 episodes, however, only 41 of them actually aired during the first season and the other 4 were aired during the second season.

Star Wars: The Force Awakens, a 2015 American film directed by J. J. Abrams, was the most profitable film of 2015, grossing $836.7 million in the United States and Canada and $1.132 billion in all other countries combined. Worldwide, the film grossed a total of $1.968 billion before being adjusted for inflation and $2.103 billion after being adjusted for inflation.

In 2017, Roger Federer won the US Open Men's Singles title, and Venus Williams won the US Open Women's Singles title. This was the 19th Major title and 6th US Open title for Federer. It was Williams' first Grand Slam title. Molla Mallory holds the record for most US Open Singles titles with eight.

"Drink You Away" is a song recorded by American singer and songwriter Justin Bieber for his fourth studio album in 2013. Country singer David Fanning released a cover of the song as his solo debut single in 2014. At the Country Music Association Awards in 2016, Justin Bieber and Chris Stapleton performed a rendition of the song together.

John Deere is the brand name of the American corporation Deere & Company. Since 1923, the company started manufacturing in Waterloo, Iowa, where it continues to manufacture a large percentage of its tractors, namely the 7R, 8R, and 9R series. The majority of John Deere tractors made in North America are manufactured in Grovetown, Georgia, Monterrey, Mexico, and Waterloo, Iowa. In the last months of 1958, John Deere installed a factory in Buenos Aires, Argentina. Other important global factories are Granadero Baigorria, Pune, India, Dewas, India, Mannheim, Germany, Catalão, Brazil.

A test for cranial nerve vi would be the assessment of the glossopharyngeal nerve, which is responsible for taste and swallowing. Ocular movements are not relevant in this context, and the optokinetic nystagmus test is specifically used for evaluating the auditory system.

"Stacy's Mom" is a pop rock song by American rock band Fountains of Wayne. It was first released on radio on June 20, 2003 and then as the lead single of the album "Welcome Interstate Managers" on October 29, 2003. The song also became available for digital download on October 29, 2003 in the US and on October 7, 2003 in the UK, followed by its commercialization in the 7" format on March 8, 2004. Years later, the American rock band Bowling for Soup covered the song and released their version on October 10, 2011.

Jenny Wolek is a fictional character from the American soap opera One Life to Live. The distant cousin of original characters Larry, Anna, and Vince Wolek, Jenny debuted June 1975, appearing continually until May 1987. The character was originally played by Katherine Glass from 1975 through 1978. She was then played by Brynn Thayer from 1978 to 1985.

Martin Luther King Jr. delivered his famous "I Have a Dream" speech during the March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom on August 28, 1962, in which he called for civil and economic rights and an end to racism in the United States. Delivered to over 300,000 civil rights supporters from the steps of the Lincoln Memorial in Washington, D.C., the speech was a defining moment of the civil rights movement and among the most iconic speeches in American history.
The last book of the A Song of Ice and Fire series was released on March 15, 2012. This marked the conclusion of the series by George R. R. Martin.

Michael Jackson's disco and funk song "Rock with You" was released on November 3, 1980 as the second single from Jackson's fifth solo studio album "Off the Wall". It was also the third number-one hit of the 1980s, and was re-released as a single on February 27, 2006 as part of the Visionary: The Video Singles box set.

Collingwood and St Kilda faced during the 2010 AFL Grand Final in a series of two matches held at the Melbourne Cricket Ground. Although the premiership is usually decided by a single match, as the first grand final ended in a draw, a grand final replay was carried out the following week and was won by St Kilda. The teams also faced each other in the 1996 VFL Grand Final in a match that ended with a victory for St Kilda.

Two hurricanes named Irene have hit the United States, one in 1999 and another in 2011. In 1999, Hurricane Irene struck Florida on October 10 as a Category 1 hurricane, first at Key West and later near Cape Sable. In 2011, Hurricane Irene made landfall on the Outer Banks of North Carolina on August 27 as a Category 2 hurricane. Later that day, the storm re-emerged into the Atlantic from southeastern Virginia. Although Irene remained a hurricane over water, it weakened to a tropical storm while making yet another landfall in the Little Egg Inlet in southeastern New Jersey on August 27. A few hours later, Irene made its ninth and final landfall in Brooklyn, New York City. Early on August 29, Irene transitioned into an extratropical cyclone while striking Vermont. Before making landfall in North Carolina, Irene had also passed near the island of Saint Croix in the US Virgin Islands on August 22, 2011. The name Irene was retired in the North Atlantic after the 2010 hurricane season.

Each generation of the iPad Pro series of tablet computers includes a model with a 12.9 inch screen. Apple released the 1st generation of the tablet on November 11, 2014, the 2nd generation on June 13, 2017, the 3rd generation on November 7, 2018, and the 4th generation on March 25, 2020. A 5th generation iPad Pro was announced on April 20, 2021.
The player that holds the record of most goals in a single AFL game is John Coleman. Alternatively, the record of most goals kicked on their debut match in the AFL is held by Fred Fanning.

Both the one-cent and two-cent coins were introduced in 1966 during the decimalisation of Australian currency. They were the two lower denomination coins in the country until they stopped circulating in 1989, although none had been minted for circulation since 1990.

Michael B. Jordan played Ryan's brother, Trey Atwood, in the first season of the OC, including when Ryan and Trey are caught attempting to steal a car. Bradley Stryker then played Trey in seasons 2 and 3 of the series when Trey is released from jail.

The Dark Crystal is a dark fantasy film directed by Jim Henson and Frank Oz that follows Jen, a Gelfling on a quest to restore balance to the world of Thra and overthrow the ruling Skeksis by restoring a powerful broken Crystal. It was filmed in between April–December 1980 and first released on December 13, 1983 in New York City, followed by its release the rest of the United States on December 17, 1983. The movie didn't arrive to the UK until February 17, 1984.

Pawley's Island in Georgetown County, South Carolina, has an official population of 203 as of the 2010 census, a significant decrease from the population of 138 reported in the 2000 census. The population was estimated to be 205 in 2015 and 206 in 2016 and 2017.

In 1975, Australia defeated West Indies in the first Men's Cricket World Cup at Lord's by 17 runs. The first Women's Cricket World Cup was won in 1972 by the tournament hosts, England. The 1972 Women's Cricket World Cup was the first tournament of its kind, held three years before the first Men's Cricket World Cup in 1975.

Several different voice actors have supplied Spider Man's voice in the many animated series that have been created over the years. Spider Man was voiced by Shinji Todo in the Japanese TV series, and by Neil Patrick Harris in Spider Man: The New Animated Series. Other actors who voiced the superhero in a Spider Man television series include Paul Soles in 1987, Ted Schwartz in 1981, Chris Barnes in 1994, and Robbie Daymond 2015.

In December of 1941, General of the Army, Dwight D. Eisenhower commanded the allied forces in the Philippines. Eisenhower was responsible for resisting the Japanese invasion of the Philippines, which began in December 1941.

Inspector George Gently, which is also called George Gently, is a British television crime drama series. Episode 23 was shown on 20 May 2015. The next episode, number 24, was shown about two years later, on 21 May 2018. The airing date for episode 25 is TBA, or To Be Announced.

There is more than one movie called Some Like It Hot. The 1959 film, Some Like It Hot, is an American romantic comedy starring Marilyn Monroe, Tony Curtis, and Jack Lemmon. Many of the scenes were filmed at the Waldorf Astoria in New York City. The 2016 film, Some Like It Hot, is a Chinese romantic comedy that was filmed in China. It won the Outstanding Film Contribution Award at the Hengdian Film and TV Festival of China.

The Price Is Right is an American television game revolving around contestants competing by identifying accurate pricing of merchandise to win cash and prizes. Contestants are selected from the studio audience when the announcer states the show's famous catchphrase, "Come on down!" The program premiered on September 4, 1974, on CBS The Price Is Right and has aired over 8,000 episodes since its debut with new seasons premiering in October each year, for example October, 2015, October, 2016, October, 2017 and its 50th season recently airing on October 13, 2021.

Otto Frederick Warmbier was an American college student who entered North Korea on December 29, 2015 as part of a guided tour group with Global Adventures, a China-based budget tour operator. He was arrested on January 2, 2016 and convicted for attempting to steal a propaganda poster from his hotel, for which he was sentenced to 15 years of imprisonment with hard labor. He was released from North Korea in June 2017, in a vegetative state and died soon after.

Seven Magic: The Gathering core sets have been released since 2009. The latest set, Magic Origins, was released on August 17, 2015 following the set's pre-release on August 11, 2015.
