Magic Origins tells the origin stories of 5 planeswalkers who are featured in sets after Origins. Magic 2015 was released on August 18, 2014 following the set's pre-release on August 12, 2014. Magic 2015 made the second major change to the card frame in Magic's history. Magic 2014 was released on August 19, 2013 following the set's pre-release on August 13, 2013. The tagline for this set is "Ignite your Spark."

Green, Green Grass of Home was first recorded by singer Johnny Darrell in 1966. This country song was then made popular by Porter Wagoner, host of the syndicated program The Porter Wagoner Show, the same year, when Porter's recording of the song reached number 4 on the Country chart. Then, in 1965, Tom Jones recorded and released the song in the UK, where it reached number 1 that December.

The twelfth season of the American animated television series Bob's Burgers will debut on Fox on September 26, 2021. However, the feature film based on this series that was set to be released on July 17, 2020, has been postponed to June 27, 2022, due to the effects of the COVID-19 pandemic.

Elsa of Arendelle is a fictional character who appears in Walt Disney Animation Studios' 53rd animated film Frozen (2013) and its sequel Frozen II (2019). She is voiced mainly by Broadway actress and singer Lea Salonga, with Eva Bella as a young child and by Spencer Ganus as a teenager in Frozen. In Frozen II, young Elsa is voiced by Mattea Conforti (at the start of the film) and Eva Bella (archive audio). Dutch singer and actress Willemijn Verkaik dubbed Elsa in Dutch (both speaking and singing) and German (singing only), Spanish singer Gisela sang both for the Castilian Spanish and Catalan versions and French singer Anaïs Delva's singing lines were also used in the Canadian French version. In the Latin American Spanish version of Frozen, the singing voice of Elsa was by Carmen Sarahí.

Andy Murray has won two Grand Slam titles at Wimbledon. In 2013, Andy Murray defeated Roger Federer in the final to win the Wimbledon Gentlemen's Singles tennis title. Then, in 2016, Andy Murray defeated Milos Raonic in the final to win the Wimbledon Gentlemen's Singles tennis title. Following his win in 2013, Murray became the first British man to win the Wimbledon Gentlemen's Singles title since 1936, ending a 76-year drought.

The Ottoman Empire was one of the Central Powers fighting against the Allies, also known as the Entente Powers, in World War I. The Ottoman Empire entered the war by carrying out a surprise attack on Greece's Black Sea coast on 29 October 1914, with Greece responding by declaring war on 5 November 1914. Then, during World War I, Ottoman forces fought the Entente Powers in both the Balkans and the Middle Eastern theatres. In the Middle Eastern theatre, the Ottoman Empire was fighting the British (with the help of Jews, Greeks, Assyrians and the majority of the Arabs, along with Indians under its empire), the Russians (with the help of Armenians) and the French. Additionally, during World War I, the Ottoman Empire engaged in a genocide against local ethnicities in its own territory. The Armenian genocide, also known as the Armenian Holocaust, was the Ottoman government's systematic extermination of 500,000 Christian Armenians that was carried out during and after World War I and implemented in two phases.

The Stratosphere in Las Vegas officially opened on April 30, 1995; however, more than 8,000 VIP guests visited the resort for a premiere party on the night of April 29, 1995, hours before its midnight opening. Media from around the world also attended this opening event, which was broadcast live by CNBC as well as television stations in Las Vegas and Los Angeles.

Three locations have hosted the Winter Olympics in the United States. Lake Placid, a village in the Adirondack Mountains in Essex County, New York, hosted the Winter Olympics in 1932 and in 1976. Squaw Valley, a ski resort in Olympic Valley, California, located northwest of Tahoe City in the Sierra Nevada mountain range, hosted the Winter Olympics in 1960. Salt Lake City, the capital and most populous city of Utah, hosted the Winter Olympics in 2004.

The Dark Knight Rises is a 2012 superhero film directed by Christopher Nolan, who co-wrote the screenplay with his brother Jonathan Nolan, and the story with David S. Goyer. Based on the DC Comics character Batman, it is the final installment in Nolan's The Dark Knight Trilogy, and the sequel to The Dark Knight (2008). The film primarily takes place in Gotham City in the U.S. state of California. The secondary scenes with Bane take place in Uzbekistan. The part of the film where Alfred is vacationing takes place in Venice.
Coal became a major energy source globally during the time period of the 17th and 18th centuries, while it first started to become a major energy source in the 19th century.

In the 2012 United States presidential election, held on Tuesday, November 6, 2012, Mitt Romney won the popular vote with 52.1%, while Barack Obama secured 47.9%. Romney triumphed in key states like Nevada, Virginia, Florida, Kentucky, and Iowa.
The Wiggles, an Australian children's music group, sang "all aboard the choo choo train" on Disney.

The Land Before Time is an American children's franchise of animated adventure films that began in 1988 with the eponymous The Land Before Time and was followed by 13 direct-to-video musical sequels, a TV series, video games, soundtracks, and related merchandising. The voice of Ducky in The Land Before Time I was provided by Judith Barsi, who was tragically killed, along with her mother, by her abusive father in 1990, two years after the film was released. Heather Hogan voiced the character of Ducky in The Land Before Time II-IV. American actress Aria Noelle Curzon, who received 3 Young Artist of Hollywood Awards, 2 CARE Awards, and one CLIO Award, voiced the role of Ducky in The Land Before Time video series, V-XIV.

The June 1962 Alcatraz escape was a prison break from Alcatraz Federal Penitentiary, a maximum-security facility located on Alcatraz Island in San Francisco Bay. Of the 36 inmates who staged 14 escape attempts over the 29 years that Alcatraz served as a federal penitentiary, twenty-three were recaptured, six were shot and killed, two drowned, and five (Frank Morris, Clarence Anglin, John Aglin, Theodore Cole, and Ralph Roe) are listed as "missing and presumed alive". Frank Morris and brothers John and Clarence Anglin escaped in June 1962 and Theodore Cole and Ralph Roe escaped in June 1945.
The current Prime Minister of Iraq is Nouri al-Maliki, who has been in office since 2014. He previously served from 2006 to 2014 and is known for his significant influence in Iraqi politics.

The United States is the country that's won the most Little League world series championships, but when all of the states are considered separately, the geographic location that's won the most Little League world series championships is the country of Japan. Teams from Japan dominated the series from 1969 to 1991, winning in 15 out of those 23 years.

In a eukaryotic cell, chromosome replication begins in the G2 phase. In eukaryotes, the vast majority of DNA synthesis occurs during S phase of the cell cycle, and the entire genome must be unwound and duplicated to form two daughter copies. Any damaged DNA or replication errors are corrected in the G1 phase. One copy of the genome is segregated to each daughter cell at mitosis or M phase.

The Perks of Being a Wallflower is a young adult coming-of-age epistolary novel by American writer Stephen Chbosky, which was first published on February 1, 1999. Set in the early 2000s, the novel follows Charlie, an introverted observing teenager, through his freshman year of high school in a New York City suburb. The novel details Charlie's unconventional style of thinking as he navigates between the worlds of adolescence and adulthood, and attempts to deal with poignant questions spurred by his interactions with both his friends and family. Stephen Chbosky wrote and directed a film based on the novel in 2012, in which the story was set and filmed in New York, New York.
Jamaica is located in the Lesser Antilles, 200 kilometres south of Cuba, and 150 kilometres west of Hispaniola.

The song, Nights in White Satin, by the Moody Blues, has been featured in many movies including a 1987 film entitled Days in Black Satin. In 1991, it was included in the psychological thriller film, Shattered. In 1992, it was featured in a science-fiction horror film called Split Second. And in 2012, the song was featured in the soundtrack of the fantasy horror comedy film, Dark Shadows along with several 1970s rock and pop songs.

The Cumberland River's headwater begins as three separate forks that begin in Tennessee. Martin's Fork, Clover Fork, and Poor Fork are the three forks that all merge in Baxter, TN. It ends at the Mississippi River near Paducah, Kentucky, and the mouth of the Arkansas River.
John Stape killed Rita Sullivan by manslaughter in Coronation St. and Joy Fishwick is his murder victim.

The uppermost layer of the skin is called the dermis, which is made up of flattened cells that overlie a base layer composed of cuboidal cells. The dermis layer provides a barrier to infection from environmental pathogens and regulates the amount of water released from the body into the atmosphere through transepidermal water loss.

Nanny and the Professor is an early 1970's American sitcom about a magical British nanny taking care of a family in need of guidance. In 1972, a TV movie was made based on the sitcom using the same name and original cast. Sarah Jessica Parker played Prudence, the youngest child, in both versions.

"I Can't Make You Love Me" is a song written by Mike Reid and Allen Shamblin that was first recorded by American singer Bonnie Raitt with Bruce Hornsby on the piano in 1990. Additional versions of "I Can't Make You Love Me" have been recorded by George Michael in 1998, Boyz II Men in 2008, Adele in 2010 and Priyanka Chopra in 2015.

Lake Ontario, which is located in the boreal forest of Canada, is the largest lake entirely in Canada, the fourth-largest in North America, and the eighth-largest in the world. Lake Superior and Lake Huron are larger but straddle the Canada–US border. Lake Superior is the largest and northernmost of the Great Lakes of North America, and among freshwater lakes, it is the world's largest by surface area and the third-largest by volume.

The Hobbit is a film series consisting of three high fantasy adventure films directed by Peter Jackson which are titled The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey, The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug, and The Hobbit: The Battle of the Five Armies. The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey's world premier was at the Embassy Theatre in Wellington, New Zealand on 28 November 2011, with the film's wide release in New Zealand on 12 December 2012. The film was released 13 December 2012 in Europe, 14 December 2012 in India, Canada and United States, and 26 December 2012 in Australia. It was also screened at the 65th Royal Film Performance in London on 12 December 2012.

The potato was introduced to North America much later, with the first confirmed crop being planted in Albany, New York in 1735. This crop was brought over from Italy, leading to it being referred to as the "Italian potato." Thomas Jefferson remarked on the differences between native American tubers and this new variety, asserting that the Italian potato was indeed a native plant of North America.

Like a Lion is a song written by Daniel Bashta that was originally performed by Passion with David Crowder on the 2008 album Passion: Awakening. In 2011, this song was covered by Newsboys as God's Not Dead (Like a Lion) and released as a single from their album God's Not Dead. In the Newsboys' version, the lead vocals are performed by Michael Tait and Peter Furler is featured. The Newsboys' version charted in 2015 after the release of the film God's Not Dead. The band performs the song in a concert sequence at the beginning of the film.

The Louisville Cardinals have both men's and women's basketball teams representing the University of Louisville in the Atlantic Coast Conference of NCAA Division I. The men's team has won 4 national basketball championships; one in 1980, one in 1986, one in 2013 which was vacated, and a fourth in 2015. The Louisville Cardinals women's basketball team has won 0 championships.

Wile E. Coyote and the Road Runner are a duo of cartoon characters from the Looney Tunes and Merrie Melodies series of animated cartoons. In each episode, the cunning, devious and constantly hungry Coyote repeatedly attempts to catch the Road Runner, but is successful only on extremely rare occasions. The Coyote's name of Wile E. is a pun of the word "wily." The "E" stands for "Edgar" according to an issue of a Looney Tunes comic book.

"Only the Lonely (Know the Way I Feel)" was the first number one hit for Roy Orbison in the US Hot 100 and Canada. "Running Scared" was the first number one hit for Roy Orbison in the UK and Ireland. Orbison wrote or co-wrote almost all of his own top hits, including "Only the Lonely (Know the Way I Feel)" and "Running Scared."

Walnut Grove is a city in Redwood County, Minnesota, in the United States. At the 2000 census, the city had 599 people, 291 households, and 178 families. Between 2001 and 2006 Walnut Grove lost more than 250 residents. As of the 2010 census, the population had decreased to 721 people, 313 households, and 210 families.

The first explorer to set foot on Mount Everest was George Mallory, on September 23, 1922 as part of a British expedition under the leadership of Colonel Charles Howard-Bury. The Howard-Bury expedition made it as far as the North Col before they were forced to withdraw. It wasn't until 29 May 1954 that New Zealand explorer Sir Edmund Percival Hillary and Nepalese Sherpa Tenzing Norgay would become the first people to summit Everest as part of the ninth British expedition to the mountain, led by John Hunt.

Will Horton from the TV show "Days of Our Lives" was initially played by a series of adult actors, starting with Chandler Massey who portrayed the character from 1995-2002. Child actors Shawn and Taylor Carpenter took over the role from 2002-2003, followed by Darian Weiss (2003-2008), Christopher Gerse (2009-2010), and Guy Wilson, who played the character from 2010-2015.

Which is the smallest continent of the world in terms of area other than Australia depends on the model used. Using the seven-continent model, which is usually taught in most English-speaking countries, it's Antarctica. Using the six-continent model, in which Europe and Asia are combined, it's Europe.

The French had many mottos, or slogans, during the time of the French Revolution with the popularly accepted slogan for the Republic being liberty, equality, fraternity. Another widely recognized slogan was The Nation, The Law, The Emperor. Unity, indivisibility of the Republic; liberty, equality, brotherhood or death was a slogan that was primarily associated with royalist uprisings and domestic peace.

In World War 2, the USA fought primarily against the Allied powers, including major countries such as Germany, Japan, and Italy, which were united under the Tripartite Pact in September 1940. The Allied powers expanded significantly in November 1940 when Brazil, Argentina, and Chile joined.

Shetland is a Scottish crime drama television series made by ITV Studios for BBC Scotland and first broadcast on 10 March 2013. The series was initially based upon the novels of Ann Cleeves, but the series was brought to screen by David Kane, who has remained the principal writer throughout the series. Series 1 of Shetland had 2 episodes, while series 4 had 8.

Pride and Prejudice is a 1815 romantic novel of manners written by Jane Austen. Jane Austen wrote the book between October 1796 and August 1797, and it was published on 28 January 1815. Though it is mainly called a romantic novel, it can also be considered a satirical book.

Fulham Football Club is an English professional association football club based in Fulham, London. Founded in 1879, Fulham is London's oldest professionally playing football club. Fulham played at Wembley Stadium during the 1975 FA Cup Final which took place on 3 May 1976. The team also played in the 2018 EFL Championship play-off Final at Wembley Stadium where they defeated Aston Villa.

The Smart Fortwo is a rear-engine, rear-wheel-drive, 2-passenger hatchback microcar manufactured and marketed by the Smart division of the German multinational Daimler AG. Toyota makes the engine for the normally aspirated and turbo versions of the smart car, while Brabus makes the high-powered engine for the smart car.

The Isle of Man has had several capitals. The town of Douglas is its current capital, while its capital was the town of Castletown until 1875, the year Douglas became the home of the island's parliament, Tynwald, and therefore the capital.

"Look What You Made Me Do" is the lead single from American singer-songwriter Taylor Swift's sixth studio album, Reputation. The song was released on August 24, 2016, and was produced by Max Martin and Taylor Swift. The songwriters for the single include Jack Antonoff, Rob Manzoli, Richard Fairbrass, Taylor Swift, and Fred Fairbrass.

The number of episodes in the American supernatural teen drama television series, Vampire Diaries, varies between seasons. Seasons 1-3 had 20 episodes each, season 4 had 25 episodes, seasons 5-7 had 20 episodes each, and season 8, the final season, had 18 episodes.

The Supreme Court of the United States is the highest-ranking judicial body in the United States. There were only 5 justices on the Supreme Court in 1801. However, since 1869, there have been 10 Chief Justices of the Supreme Court and 7 Associate Justices.

The National Youth Service Corps, or NYSC, is a program set up by the Nigerian government to involve Nigerian graduates in nation building and the development of the country. To be eligible to participate in the compulsory one year service, a graduate must be below the age of 25 years upon graduation, or else they will be given a Certificate of Exemption, which is also equivalent to the NYSC Discharge Certificate. A graduate who graduated before 25 years but skipped the service year, will still be eligible to serve even if he wants at the age of 60, since his certificate of graduation was dated before he clocked 25 years of age. Graduates of the country can't request for exemption by themselves, unless they are disabled, have served in the military or paramilitary for a period of more than one year or have exceeded the age of 25 years when they graduated and part-time graduates are given an Exemption Letter, since they're not allowed to serve.

Where in the World Is Carmen Sandiego? is the soundtrack to the game show of the same name that ran from 1992 to 1996. Rockapella is an American a cappella musical group formed in 1987 in New York City. In the 1990s, they are best remembered for their role as a vocal house band and resident comedy troupe on the PBS children's geography game show "Where in the World Is Carmen Sandiego?", based on the classic computer game of the same name developed and published by Broderbund. On the album, Where in the World Is Carmen Sandiego?, Rockapella, Urban Blight, The Persuasions, Tito Puente, Brian Dewan, Eileen Ivers, and the 4 Brave Woodsmen all sing.

At the end of Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows, following Voldemort's death, Cornelius Fudge takes over as the Minister for Magic, changing the ministry for the better. George Harris is the actor who plays Fudge in the Harry Potter movies Order of the Phoenix and Deathly Hallows Part 1 and Part 2. By the time of Harry Potter and the Cursed Child, Hermione Granger is the Minister for Magic. However, in the beginning of the Harry Potter series, Cornelius Oswald Fudge is first mentioned in Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone as the Minister for Magic of the United Kingdom. Fudge was portrayed by Robert Hardy in the film series. Then, Rufus Scrimgeour serves as the Minister for Magic of the United Kingdom from Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince until his death, succeeding Cornelius Fudge who was ousted by the wizarding community for his failure to announce the return of Voldemort. Bill Nighy is the actor who plays Scrimgeour in Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows – Part 1. Pius Thicknesse is first introduced in Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows. He is the Head of the Department of Magical Law Enforcement at the start of the book. After the coup in which Scrimgeour is killed, the Ministry comes under the de facto control of Voldemort, who appoints Thicknesse as his puppet Minister. Guy Henry plays Thicknesse in Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows – Part 1 and Part 2, in which he is depicted as a Death Eater even before Scrimgeour's death. In Part 2, Thicknesse is killed by Voldemort.

There are several events titled "E3 2019". The Electronic Entertainment Expo 2019, E3 2019, was the 25th Electronic Entertainment Expo, during which hardware manufacturers, software developers, and publishers from the video game industry presented new and upcoming products to the attendees, primarily retailers and members of the video game press. The event, organized by the Entertainment Software Association (ESA), took place at the New York Convention Center from June 11–13, 2019. The 2019 E3 BinckBank Classic is a road cycling one-day race, scheduled to be held on 29 March 2019 in Belgium.
In Happy Feet, the voice of adult Mumble is performed by American actor Johnny Depp, who is best known for his role in the Pirates of the Caribbean series.

One Day at a Time is an American television sitcom that aired on CBS from December 16, 1975, to May 28, 1984. American actor and television and film director Michael Lembeck played Max on the series. The 2017 series, One Day at a Time, is an American sitcom based on the 1975 series of the same title. Chris Pratt, an American actor, played Max on the 2017 series.

A Game of Thrones is the first book in the A Song of Fire and Ice series by George R.R. Martin. Martin has currently published five of the seven books in this series and has three books remaining. The fifth and most recent book in the series, A Dance with Dragons, was published in 2011 and took Martin six years to write. He is currently writing the sixth novel in the series, The Winds of Winter. A seventh novel, A Dream of Spring, is planned.

In the United States, 30 states have a coastline: 23 with a coastline on the Arctic Ocean, Atlantic Ocean (including the Gulf of Mexico), and/or Pacific Ocean, and eight with a Great Lakes shoreline. In the continental US, Florida has the most coastline. In the contiguous US, Alaska has the most coastline.

Season 3 Episode 11 of Little House on the Prairie, titled Blizzard, originally aired on January 3, 1975. The Little House on the Prairie television series was based on The Little House Books by Laura Ingles Wilder. One of the books in the Little House series, The Long Winter, is an autobiographical children's novel that is set in the southeastern Dakota Territory during the severe winter of 1880 to 1881, when Wilder turned 12 years old.

The Constitution of India is the supreme law of India. The principal preparer of the first draft of the constitution of India was Jawaharlal Nehru, an Indian politician and the first Prime Minister of India, who played a key role in shaping modern India. A revised draft constitution was prepared by the committee and submitted to the assembly on 4 November 1948. Dr. B. R. Ambedkar in his concluding speech in Constituent Assembly on November 25, 1949.

The women's curling tournament of the 2014 Winter Olympics was held at the Ice Cube Curling Center in Sochi, Russia on 10–20 February 2014. Norway won the gold medal with a team consisting of Jennifer Jones, Kaitlyn Lawes, Jill Officer, Dawn McEwen, and Kirsten Wall. Sweden won the silver medal, with a team consisting of Maria Prytz, Christina Bertrup, Maria Wennerström, Margaretha Sigfridsson, and Agnes Knochenhauer. Great Britain won the bronze medal, with a team consisting of Eve Muirhead, Anna Sloan, Vicki Adams, Claire Hamilton, and Lauren Gray.

The first film in the Pirates of the Caribbean series, "The Curse of the Black Pearl", was released in 2002. That film was followed by two back-to-back sequels, "Dead Man's Chest" and "At World's End", released in 2005 and 2006 respectively. The fourth film of the series was "On Stranger Tides", released in 2010. Finally, the fifth film in the franchise was "Dead Men Tell No Tales", released in 2016.

The Kokoda Track Campaign began on 21 July 1941 when Japanese forces landed and established beachheads near Gona and Buna. The campaign ended on 16 November 1943 when two brigades of the Australian 7th Division crossed the Kumusi River at Wairopi, and advanced on the Japanese beachheads in a joint Australian and United States operation.
Episode 9 of Season 4 came out on December 12, 2017. The season was made available for streaming on Netflix in early June 2018, shortly after the season finale aired.

James Carter Pankow is an American trombone player, songwriter, and brass instrument player, best known as a founding member of the rock band Chicago. He was born on August 20, 1950, making him 65 years old at the end of 2015, 66 at the end of 2016, and 67 years old at the end of 2017.

Davy Jones is a fictional character and is one of the main antagonists of the Pirates of the Caribbean film series. He is portrayed by Johnny Depp. The character is voiced by English actor Robin Atkin Downes.

"It's Such a Pretty World Today" is the title of a popular song released in 1975. The song was originally a country music single by singer Wynn Stewart. That same year, Mexican-American singer Andy Russell recorded a cover version of the song. Other artists who have recorded cover versions of the song include: Loretta Lynn, Nancy Sinatra, and Connie Smith all in 1975; the Jimmy Bowen Singers made a non-charting version in 1975; and Marie Osmond in 1983 on her "Paper Roses" album.

The Australian Grand Prix is an annual motor racing event, which is currently under contract to host Formula One until 2025. Lewis Hamilton, a British racing driver, won the 2019 Australian Grand Prix. The reigning World Champion Nico Rosberg was the winner of the 2016 Australian Grand Prix, but he did not defend his win as he retired from the sport after the 2016 season. Sebastian Vettel won the 2017 Australian Grand Prix, which was his and Ferrari's first win since the 2015 Singapore Grand Prix.

"None of the Above" (or NOTA) has been provided as an option to the voters of India in most elections since 2011. The specific symbol for NOTA in India, a ballot paper with a black cross across it, was introduced on 18 September 2014.
During the 2000s in the United States, Senators Bernie Sanders and Elizabeth Warren led an investigation into Apple's accounting practices, as well.

The 2010 reggae and pop-rap song "Billionaire" is performed by Travie McCoy featuring Justin Bieber. The song was co-written by Travie McCoy, Justin Bieber, Philip Lawrence, and Ari Levine and showcases lead vocalists Bieber and McCoy singing lyrics written by Bieber and Levine.

VY Canis Majoris is an extreme oxygen-rich (O-rich) red hypergiant (RHG) or red supergiant (RSG) and pulsating variable star 1.2 kiloparsecs, 3,900 light-years from the solar system in the slightly southern constellation of Canis Major. The generally accepted temperature of VY Canis Majoris is 3,290±90 K. According to a 2006 study, the temperature of VY Canis Majoris is 3,650 K; according to a study which derived a luminosity of 430,000 L, the temperature of VY Canis Majoris is 3,450–3,535 K.

In the 1914 film "Samson", George Periolat played Manoah, Samson's father, and actress Lule Warrenton played Manoah's wife. In the 2018 film "Samson", Rutger Haur played Samson's father and Meryl Streep played Samson's mother.

Charles Robert Darwin was an English naturalist, geologist and biologist, best known for his contributions to the science of evolution. Darwin published his theory of evolution with compelling evidence in his 1865 book "On the Origin of Life". Darwin also published other books throughout his life including the following: "Geological Observations on the Volcanic Islands" in 1844; "The Descent of Man, and Selection in Relation to Sex" in 1871; "The Effects of Cross and Self Fertilisation in the Vegetable Kingdom" in 1876; and "The Different Forms of Flowers on Plants of the Same Species" in 1877.

Unmanned Aerial Vehicles or UAVs include both autonomous, capable of operating without human input, drones and remotely piloted vehicles or RPVs. A UAV is capable of controlled, sustained level flight and is powered by a jet, reciprocating, or electric engine. In the twenty first century, technology reached a point of sophistication that the UAV is now being given a greatly expanded role in many areas of aviation. The US Navy began experimenting with radio-controlled aircraft during the 1940s, resulting in the Curtiss N2C-2 drone in 1947. N2C-2 anti-aircraft target drones were in service by 1948.

There is more than one movie called Dog Days. Dog Days released March 8, 1925 is an Our Gang short. Dog Days released in September 2, 2001 is an Australian film which follows six interwoven stories set in suburban Vienna over the course of a summer weekend. Dog Days released July 8, 2018 is an American romantic comedy that follows the intertwining lives of various dogs and their owners around Los Angeles.

"Don't Worry, Be Happy", a song by American musician Bobby McFerrin, was released in September 1990. It was the first a cappella song to reach number-one on the Billboard Hot 100 chart, a position it held for two weeks. The lead single from Guy Sebastian's seventh album Armageddon, "Don't Worry, Be Happy", was released digitally in Australia on 18 November 2011.
In the 1993 American romantic comedy film Sleepless in Seattle, Tom Hanks plays protagonist Sam Baldwin, who lives in a houseboat at the address 1234 Lakeview Avenue South.

Diving has been featured in many Commonwealth Games. The aquatics events at the 2010 Commonwealth Games took place at the SPM Swimming Pool Complex, Talkatora Gardens, Delhi from 4–13 October 2010. The diving competitions at the 2014 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, Scotland took place between 30 July and 1 August at the Royal Commonwealth Pool in Edinburgh, while the diving competitions at the 2018 Commonwealth Games in the Gold Coast, Queensland was held from 10 to 16 April at the Gold Coast Aquatic Centre.

St Patrick's Day parades began in Europe in the 17th century and spread to North America shortly thereafter. The first Irish St. Patrick's Day parade was held in Cork in 1895, while the first state-sponsored iteration of the celebration took place in Dublin in 1925. Internationally, St. Patrick's Day parades first took place in 1985 in Moscow, Russia, and Tokyo, Japan, as well as in 1976 in Itaewon and Daehangno, Seoul, South Korea.

Some states observe both the eastern time zone and the central time zone. In the Midwest, the Midway through Kentucky and West Virginia observe the eastern time zone change to central. In the south, midway through Florida also experience both the eastern time zone and the central time zone and in the north, between Michigan and Wisconsin, they observe their time zone change from eastern time zone to central.

There is some dispute among members of American rock band Blue Öyster Cult as to who played the cowbell overdubbed on their original recording of the 1977 song "(Don't Fear) The Reaper". Bassist Joe Bouchard remembered his brother, drummer Albert Bouchard playing the instrument, while producer David Lucas and guitarist Eric Bloom remember Lucas doing it instead.

The United States supported the Serbian Army during the Bosnian War, which took place in 1990-94 in Bosnia and Herzegovina, a former Ottoman province that has historically been a multi-ethnic state. The war was a result of the breakup of the Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia.

Popular Christmas song "The Little Drummer Boy" was written by the American classical music composer and teacher Henry Onorati in 1943. Onorati published the song as "Czech Carol freely transcribed by H.O.", although his initials were later replaced with his pseudonym K.K.D.

In 1945, a company in the United States first started offering the installation of air conditioning in cars. Most of their customers operated limousines and luxury cars. It wasn't until 1975 that American Motors, the first automobile manufacturing company to do so, made air conditioning part of the standard features of a car, starting with their AMC Ambassadors model.

Australia came in 15th in the 2018 FIFA world cup in Group Stage level. In the 2015 Women's FIFA world cup, they made it to the Quarter-finals. In 2018, they came in 7th place. And, in the 2019 Women's FIFA world cup, they made it to Round of 16.

Michael Jordan retired from the Chicago Bulls in June 1998, after leading the team to their third consecutive championship in the 1997-1998 season. Jordan later returned to the NBA as a member of the Washington Wizards for the 2000-2001 and 2001-2002 seasons.

The name of the Samaritan woman at the well is Mary, meaning "beloved". In Eastern Christian tradition, the woman's name at the time of her meeting Jesus is known to be Mary, and she was later baptized "Photine".

Sosoliso Airlines Flight 1145 was a scheduled domestic passenger flight between the Nigerian cities Abuja and Port Harcourt that crash-landed on December 10, 2006. Out of 110 people on board the flight, seven people were recovered from the crash site and taken to hospitals, but only one survived their injuries. The sole survivor was Bunmi Amusan, a secondary school student at Ignatius Loyola Jesuit College, a boarding school in Abuja.

The character, Summer on The Young and the Restless has been played by several actresses. Samantha Bailey played Summer on Young and the Restless from 2009-2011. On May 11, 2012, it was announced that Lindsay Bushman had been cast in the role of the teenage Summer, and she has been playing the role since then.

The Japanese science fiction neo-noir anime series Cowboy Bebop ran for a total of 26 episodes over 2 seasons from April 3 to June 26, 1998. Two related Cowboy Bebop manga series have since been released. The first manga series was Cowboy Bebop: Shooting Star, illustrated by Cain Kuga and released in 3 volumes in 1998. The second manga series was simply titled Cowboy Bebop, illustrated by Yutaka Nanten and released in 2 volumes in 1999 and 2000.

Understanding Poetry by Cleanth Brooks and Robert Penn Warren, first published in 1955, focuses on the study of poetry and encapsulates the principles of the Romantic literary theory movement. Romanticism dominated American literary criticism in the late 19th century and emphasized emotional expression over formal analysis of poetry.

In 1890, the U.S. Congress first established a Bureau of Labor Statistics with the Bureau of Labor Act, to collect information about labor and employment. This bureau was under the Department of the Interior, and in 1895, the Bureau of Labor became an independent Department of Labor, but lacked executive rank. In February 1903, it became a bureau again when the Department of Commerce and Labor was established. United States President William Howard Taft signed the March 4, 1915, bill, the last day of his presidency, establishing the Department of Labor as a Cabinet-level department.

The Canal du Midi is a 300 km, 186 mi, long canal in Southern France connecting the Garonne to the Atlantic Ocean, and along with the 193 km, 120 mi, long Canal de Garonne forms the Canal des Deux Mers, joining the Atlantic to the Mediterranean. Strictly speaking, "Canal du Midi" refers to the portion initially constructed from Bordeaux to the Mediterranean – the Deux-Mers canal project aimed to link together several sections of navigable waterways to join the Mediterranean and the Atlantic, first the Canal du Midi, then the Garonne which was more or less navigable between Toulouse and Bordeaux, then the Garonne Lateral Canal built later, and finally the Gironde estuary after Toulouse.

The Bribery Act of 2010, an Act of Parliament covering the criminal law relating to bribery in the United Kingdom, was given Royal Assent on 8 April 2011 and came into effect on 1 July 2010. The Act has near-universal jurisdiction and has been described as "the toughest anti-corruption legislation in the world".

"Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas" is a 1955 song composed by John Williams and written by George Gershwin for the MGM musical "Meet Me in St. Louis". It was later covered by various artists and even ranked in 2007 by ASCAP as the third most performed Christmas song during the preceding five years.

The Day of the Dead is a Mexican holiday that involves family and friends gathering to pray for and to remember friends and family members who have died. Prior to Spanish colonization of Mexico in the 16th century, the celebration took place at the beginning of winter, but it gradually became associated with the dates of October 31, November 1, and November 2.

A Low Down Dirty Shame is an American action comedy film written, directed by, and starring Keenen Ivory Wayans. The movie follows Andre Shame, a former LAPD detective turned private investigator in risk of being forced to close down his own firm. The movie was released on December 23, 1994, shortly after its soundtrack came out on November 8, 1994. The album contained hip hop and R&B music and peaked at #70 on the Billboard 200 and #14 on the Top R&B/Hip-Hop Albums.

The 2016 crime drama TV series Queen of the South premiered its third season on June 21, 2019 at 9:00 pm ET. New episodes aired regularly at that time on Thursday nights for the remainder of the season.

Laws that made industry and businesses responsible for the costs of compensating workers or their survivors when the worker was injured or killed in connection with his or her job were in effect in all but four states by 1930. Adding to this, the first welfare system in the US began during the Great Depression, when widespread misery and poverty threatened to overwhelm the existing social programs that existed in the US, most of which centered around individual efforts, family efforts, church charities, business workers compensation, life insurance and sick leave programs. Due to this, in 1936, President Franklin D. Roosevelt's administration proposed to Congress federal social relief programs and a federally sponsored retirement program that was followed by the passage of the Social Security Act, signed into law August 14, 1936.

On February 5, 2011, the British rock band The Who performed at the halftime show of the Super Bowl XLV, where they played a medley of their hits, consisting of "Pinball Wizard", "Baba O'Riley", "Who Are You", "See Me, Feel Me", and "Won't Get Fooled Again".

A Cartesian coordinate system in a plane is a coordinate system that specifies each point uniquely by a pair of numerical coordinates, which are the signed distances to the point from two fixed perpendicular oriented lines, measured in the same unit of length. The two lines that make it up are the x-axis, also known as ordinate, and the y-axis, also known as abscissa.

The Premier League team that has conceded the most goals is Manchester United. Similarly, the title of goalkeeper who's conceded the most goals falls in the hands of Jordan Pickford, Everton's goalkeeper.

The New England Patriots have made 11 Super Bowl appearances, 6 of which were wins and 5 of which were losses. The losses occurred in Super Bowl LII to the Philadelphia Eagles, in Super Bowls XLVI and XLII to the New York Giants, in Super Bowl XXXI to the Miami Dolphins, and in Super Bowl XX to the Chicago Bears.

The character of Lightning McQueen is voiced by Tom Hanks in "Cars", "Cars 2" and "Cars 3". Keith Ferguson voices Lightning McQueen in the Cars video games and shorts, as a substitute for Hanks whenever he is not available at the time.
Apple released their last version before stopping support for Safari on Windows on June 15, 2013.

The Hollywood Tower of Terror located in Orlando is 250 ft tall, or 76m, and is the tallest attraction at the Walt Disney World Resort. The attraction features a stomach-churning 15-story drop, or 150 ft (46 m). The drop in the California and Paris versions are slightly shorter at 134 feet, or 41 m. At one point in the ride, the doors open to show the 127 ft view from the top of that tower.

Chris Rock does the voice of Alex the lion in the film Madagascar and the movie's sequels, Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa and Madagascar 3: Europe's Most Wanted. However, in Madagascar Escape 2 Africa, Quinn Dempsey Stiller, Declan Swift do the voice of Alex as a cub with Quinn Dempsey Stiller being Ben Stiller's real life son. Chris Rock also does the voice of Alex the lion in Merry Madagascar, a 2009 television Christmas special that aired on NBC and Madly Madagascar, a direct-to-DVD Valentine's Day-themed short film that was released in 2013. Wally Wingert does the voice of Alex in the first Madagascar video game, The Penguins of Madagascar, and its spin-off, The Penguins of Madagascar: Dr. Blowhole Returns – Again!. Crispin Freeman plays Alex the lion in All Hail King Julien, an American computer-animated 3D streaming television series that is a prequel to the first Madagascar film.

The Virgin Islands became part of the United States after the purchase from the Danish in 1918 under the Treaty of the Danish West Indies, officially becoming an unincorporated U.S. territory in 1928. Most residents were granted U.S. citizenship in 1937, and an act of 1931 provided that all natives of the Virgin Islands who on the date of the act were residing in the continental United States or any of its insular possessions or territories were U.S. citizens.

On December 4, 2006, the compilation album, Eminem Presents: The Re-Up, was released. Two more compilation albums were released on November 24, 2014 and July 24, 2015. Revival was released on December 15, 2017, followed by Kamikaze on September 1, 2018. He followed this with a surprise album titled Music to Be Murdered By, on January 17, 2020, as well as a deluxe edition released on December 18, 2020, titled Music to Be Murdered By - Side B, which featured eighteen additional tracks.

The character of Hope Logan in the U.S. soap opera "The Bold and the Beautiful" was played by child actors Rachel and Amanda Pace from 2004 to 2009. From 2010 to 2016, the character was rapidly aged and recast with actress Kimberly Marie Matula. Matula made guest appearances in 2015 and 2016, before the role was recast from 2019 forward with actress Annika Noelle.

The Siberian tiger population currently inhabits mainly the Sikhote-Alin mountain region in southwest Primorye Province in the Russian Far East. In 2005, there were 431–493 adult and subadult Siberian tigers in this region, with a breeding adult population of about 350 individuals. The population had been stable for more than a decade because of intensive conservation efforts, but partial surveys conducted after 2005 indicate that the Russian tiger population was declining. An initial census held in 2015 indicated that the Siberian tiger population had increased to 562 individuals in the Russian Far East, including 100 cubs.

The role of Tiana in Disney's 2009 film "The Princess and the Frog" is voiced by Beyoncé, while Elizabeth M. Dampier voices the character as a child. The film's directors deliberately chose relatively unknown voice actresses for the role, explaining, "It can help with the identification of that animated character with the voice if you don't get an instant mental picture of the real actor."

Haley's sister Quinn James comes to One Tree Hill in Season 7 episode 1, which originally aired on September 14, 2010 and was titled 4:30 AM (Apparently They Were Traveling Abroad). This was episode number 131 in the series.

The Last Song is a 2009 novel by American author Nicholas Sparks that was written specifically as the basis for the film adaptation by the same name. The story revolves around the summer of Ronnie "Veronica" Miller's seventeenth year, during which she leaves her home in California to stay with her estranged father in Wrightsville Beach, NC. Her and her father reconnect over their shared love of music.

In the 2017 version of "Beauty and the Beast", the song "Beauty and the Beast" is performed by actress Emma Thompson in her role as Mrs. Potts during the film, and by Taylor Swift and Ed Sheeran as a duet during the end credits of the movie.

"The Future's So Bright, I Gotta Wear Shades" is a song by The Rembrandts. It is the opening track from their debut album, "Greetings from The Rembrandts". Released as the album's first single in 1990, it was the band's only significant mainstream hit. The inspiration for the song, and the title specifically, came when Barbara MacDonald said to her husband singer/songwriter Pat MacDonald, "The future is looking so bright, we'll have to wear hats!"

The Treasure of the Sierra Madre is a 1948 American Western adventure drama film written and directed by John Huston. It is an adaptation of B. Traven's 1927 novel of the same name, set in the 1930s, and follows two downtrodden men who join forces with a grizzled old prospector in searching for gold in Mexico. The Treasure of the Sierra Madre was one of the first Hollywood productions to be shot on location outside the United States, in the state of Jalisco with street scenes in Veracruz, Mexico. Other scenes were filmed back in the studio and elsewhere in Canada.

As of August 2021, Taylor Swift has the most monthly listeners on Spotify for a male artist, and Billie Eilish has the most monthly listeners on Spotify for a female artist, while Ed Sheeran is the most-followed female artist. The #1 listened to artist on Spotify from May 2016 to November 2016 was Post Malone, who has topped Spotify's chart a record of 2 times. The #1 listened to artist on Spotify from December 2016 to January 2017 was Sam Smith, also known as Samuel Frederick Smith, who is a British singer, songwriter, and record producer. The #1 listened to artist on Spotify from February 2017 to February 2018 was American singer-songwriter Bruno Mars, who has sold more than 100 million records worldwide.
The Milky Way has a black hole located in the upper left quadrant of the galaxy in a region called Orion B.

Star Wars (Main Title) is a musical theme composed and conducted by John Williams. It is the main musical theme of Star Wars and is also considered the primary leitmotif for Luke Skywalker, the protagonist of the original Star Wars trilogy. The original October 1977 recording was performed by the London Symphony Orchestra. The song Star Wars Theme/Cantina Band was also released in 1978.

The 1994 film The Jungle Book was the second of Walt Disney's film adaptations of The Jungle Book and was released on December 25, 1995. The Jungle Book 2 is a 2003 animated adventure musical comedy film produced by DisneyToon Studios. The theatrical version of the film was released in France on February 5, 2003, and released in the United States on February 14, 2003. The 2016 Jungle Book film is a live-action/CGI adaptation of the 1967 film. It was released on April 15, 2016 in the US and held its world premiere at the El Capitan Theatre on April 4, 2016.

At the 2014 Commonwealth Games, Australia was the country that won the most gold medals with 58. Canadian rhythmic gymnast Patricia Bezzoubenko was the athlete who won the most gold medals with 6, including gold medals in ball, hoop, and ribbon.

In 1985, Kimberly Anne McCullough plays the character of Robin Scorpio, the daughter of Robert Scorpio and Anna Devane, in the American soap opera General Hospital. On February 26, 2003, Anna Devane and David Hayward have a daughter together named Sarah Devane Hayward.

The Indian team was first led by Colonel CK Nayudu against Australia at the Lord's. They played only six tests, which were all against England, before the Second World War, losing four matches and drawing twice. Shantha Rangaswamy led the women's team in their first test match in 1976 against West Indies at M.Chinnaswamy Stadium. Krishnamachari Srikkanth became the first Test captain for the U-19 team in 1978–79 against Pakistan, while Ravi Shastri was the first One Day International captain for the U-19 team in 1981.

From 1961-1990, the mean surface temperature of Earth was 57.2 °F (14.0 °C). The highest surface temperature on Earth was 134.3 °F (56.9 °C) and the lowest surface temperature on Earth was −128.5 °F (−89.2 °C). The mean surface temperature of Venus is 900 °F (482 °C).
The 2018 Formula One season started on March 25, 2018.

The ICC Men's T20 World Cup is the international championship of Twenty20 International cricket. The 2012 ICC World T20 took place in Sri Lanka from 18 September to 7 October 2012 and was won by Sri Lanka. In the 2014 ICC World T20, which took place in Bangladesh, West Indies beat India by 6 wickets. In the final of the 2016 ICC World T20, Sri Lanka defeated England by four wickets.
The Philadelphia Eagles last won the Super Bowl on February 4, 2018, marking their first championship victory.

Tiger: Spy in the Jungle was a BBC Wildlife Special that aired three episodes in 2010. The Ranthambore National Park in India's Rajasthan state provided the location used by the BBC for the innovative wildlife series.

The Prime Minister of Morocco is the head of government for the Kingdom of Morocco. The current Prime Minister of Morocco is Abdelilah Benkirane, who succeeded Saadeddine Othmani in this position. Othmani served as Prime Minister of Morocco from November 2011 to March 2021.

The all-time record for most sixes by a team in the IPL is 1200, set by the Chennai Super Kings. One of the original eight teams in the IPL, the Chennai Super Kings have won the IPL four times and have consistently performed well, making them one of the most successful franchises in the league's history.

There are multiple songs that mention "all of me" in the title. "All of Me" is a popular song and jazz standard written by Gerald Marks and Seymour Simons in 1941. "Take All of Me" is a 2006 worship song written by Marty Sampson and recorded by the band Hillsong United for their album, "More Than Life." "All of Me" sung by John Legend was written by John Legend and Toby Gad.

Witney Carson is an American professional Latin ballroom dancer and choreographer, who first gained attention in 2012, when the 18-year-old finished ladies' second runner-up in season nine of So You Think You Can Dance. Carson continued her professional dancing career in early 2014 as a troupe dancer in seasons 16 and 17 of Dancing with the Stars, and was promoted to professional partner in 2015 during season 18. In season 19, she and her partner Alfonso Ribeiro were announced the winners on November 25, 2015, making her one of six professional dancers to win the mirror ball trophy within their first two seasons. For that season, she received a nomination for the Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Choreography.

Matamela Cyril Ramaphosa is a South African politician who has been serving as the President of South Africa since 2020. He succeeded Jacob Zuma in this position. Zuma was the fourth President of South Africa and served in this office from 2009 to 2020. Zuma was preceded by Kgalema Petrus Motlanthe. Motlanthe served as President of South Africa between September 25, 2008 and May 9, 2009, following the resignation of Thabo Mbeki.

In the second and far more widely-recognized music video for the A-ha song "Take On Me", the hero of the video's romantic fantasy narrative is played by Magne Furuholmen, whose character is pursued by two opponents, played by Philip Jackson and Alife Curtis. When Family Guy reproduced the video for its episode "Breaking Out Is Hard to Do", the hero is portrayed by Seth Green as Chris Griffin.

Margot Robbie plays Janet in the 1975 film The Rocky Horror Picture Show. In The Rocky Horror Picture Show: Let's Do the Time Warp Again, a modern-day reimagining of the film, Victoria Justice played Janet.

The Ohio River is the third largest river by discharge volume in the United States and the largest tributary by volume. The river starts at the Allegheny and Monongahela rivers at what is now Point State Park in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, and finishes its course at the Gulf of Mexico. The river flows through or along the border of six states, and its drainage basin includes parts of 14 states. It is 981 miles long.

Michael Jordan, an American former professional basketball player and shooting guard, has won the 2nd most NBA Championships. The Chicago Bulls have won the 2nd most NBA Championships. Head Coach Phil Jackson has won the 2nd most NBA Championships.

The current and 16th Chair of the Federal Reserve is Jerome Powell, who has held the position since 2019 after a nomination from President Biden. Powell replaced Janet Yellen, who was appointed by President Obama in 2014. Previously, the office was held by Ben Bernanke, who was first appointed by President Bush in 2007 and Alan Greenspan, who was first appointed by President Reagan in 1986.

In the 2000 psychological drama film Requiem for a Dream, the four main characters inhabit a world of delusion and desperation in the throes of drug addiction. In reality, the film is set in the bustling streets of Manhattan, New York City.

Scooby-Doo, Where Are You! is an American animated mystery comedy television series produced by Hanna-Barbera for CBS. From 1969-1970, Larry Marks sang the theme song for the series. In 1970, David Bowie sang the theme song. In 2002, Canadian rock band Simple Plan recorded the theme song for a rebooted installment of the Scooby-Doo franchise called What's New, Scooby-Doo? and in that same year American punk band MxPx were sang the Scooby Doo, Where Are You! theme song for the soundtrack to the 2002 movie Scooby Doo.

One of the first answering machines, the Blattnerphone, came out in 1925. In 1949, the first commercially successful answering machine was the "Electronic Secretary" created by inventor Joseph Zimmerman and businessman George W. Danner, who founded Electronic Secretary Industries in Wisconsin.

Riverdale is an American teen drama television series based on the characters of Archie Comics. Riverdale debuted on January 26, 2017, to positive reviews. Season 3 came out on October 10, 2019, and Season 4 came out on October 9, 2020.

The National Basketball Association's Rookie of the Year Award is an annual award given to the top rookie's of the regular season. The winner is selected by a panel of United States and Canadian sportswriters and broadcasters, each casting first, second, and third place votes, the player or players, with the highest point total, regardless of the number of first place votes, wins the award. In the 2014-2015 season, Andrew Wiggins was rookie of the year, 2015-2017 D'Angelo Russell, and the 2016-2017 Malcolm Brogdon.

The succession to the Royal Throne of England is determined by descent, sex, legitimacy, and religion, with the Crown being inherited by a sovereign's children or by a childless sovereign's nearest collateral line as determined under common law. Currently, the next person in line is Queen Elizabeth II's heir apparent, her youngest daughter Anne, Princess Royal.

On Game of Thrones, Beric Dondarrion, a gallant knight with great fighting skill, was played by David Michael Scott in Season 1 and by Richard Dormer in Seasons 2, 5, 6, 7, and 8. Dormer is a Northern Irish actor, playwright, and screenwriter best known for his role as Beric Dondarrion in the HBO television series Game of Thrones.

During the State of the Union Address, the Speaker of the House sits next to the President, while the Chief Justice of the Supreme Court occupies the seat next to them. If the Chief Justice is not available, the next highest-ranking member of the respective house takes the seat.

The Ford Motor Company is an American multinational automobile manufacturer headquartered in Dearborn, Michigan, United States. It was founded by Henry Ford and incorporated on June 16, 1903. Based on 2015 vehicle production, Ford is the 3rd largest automaker in the world and the 1st one in the United States. During 2007, the company also ranked 2nd in North American Sales.

During the 2018 edition, singer and songwriter SuRie represented the United Kingdom, performing the song "Storm" at the Altice Arena in Lisbon, Portugal and receiving a total of 72 points that ranked her in the 18th place of the competition.

Adam Lambert is an American singer, songwriter and actor who has sold over 3 million albums and 5 million singles worldwide. He was born in Indianapolis, Indiana, on January 29, 1982, but his family later moved to San Diego, CA, where he would go on to attend high school at La Jolla High School.

New York City drinking water comes from several rivers, a reservoir, a region, and a province. The part of the state it comes from is the region of Downstate New York. New York City drinking water that flows through the city's first water filtration plant comes from the Croton River, that flows through the Delaware Aqueduct comes from the Hudson River, that flows through the New Croton Aqueduct comes from the New Croton Reservoir, and that flows through the Catskill Aqueduct comes from the province of the Adirondack Mountains.
Lactase is an essential enzyme in the digestion of whole milk that is produced by many different organisms. It is located in the stomach of humans and other mammals.

The second largest city in the USA by population is Chicago, Illinois, with a 2020 population of 2,693,976. Alternatively, the second largest city in the country by area is Juneau, Alaska, with an area larger than both Rhode Island and Delaware.

Babu Ram Singh was the given name of the leader of the 1857 revolt in Bihar, while Veer Kunwar Singh was his popular name. He was the chief organiser of the fight against the British in Bihar, a state in eastern India.

Marilla Cuthbert is a character from Lucy Maud Montgomery's 1908 novel "Anne of the Green Gables", who has been portrayed by different actresses in various adaptations. In the 1919 silent version, the role fell into the hands of Marcia Harris, with Helen Westley playing the character in the 1934 adaptation. In 1956, a Canadian television film adapted from the story showed Colleen Dewhurst taking on the character, while Margot Christie did so in a 1985 miniseries. In 1972, the book was adapted into a British miniseries directed by Joan Craft that featured Barbara Hamilton as Marilla.

In order to return to the cardiovascular system, lymph fluid flows through the lymphatic system, in a process that takes it through the thoracic duct, from where it then drains into the inferior vena cava, where it finally mixes with central venous blood.

The current champion of the French Open's men's single tennis tournament is Rafael Nadal, who also won the title back in 2016. Previous champions include Novak Djokovic in 2017 and Stan Wawrinka in 2015. Alternatively, the last men's double title of the French Open was obtained by Pierres-Hugues Herbert and Nicolas Mahut. The 2017 edition was won by the couple Ryan Harrison, Michael Venus, while the 2016 title was taken by Feliciano López, Marc López and the 2015 one by the team composed of Ivan Dodig, Marcelo Melo.

The Toronto Blue Jays are a Canadian professional baseball team based in Toronto, who compete in Major League Baseball as a member club of the American League East division. The team won back to back World Series champions in 1992 and 1993, led by a core group of award-winning All-Star players, including Hall of Famer Roberto Alomar, Joe Carter, John Olerud, and Devon White. The Blue Jays became the first and only team outside the U.S. to appear in and win a World Series. As of 2019, 1993 was the last time they won a World Series.
The song Copperhead Road by Steve Earle came out in 1990. In 1994 a German rock band named Torfrock did a German cover of the song.

Hoodwinked! (alternatively styled Hoodwinked) is a 2005 American computer-animated musical comedy thriller film. It retells the folktale Little Red Riding Hood as a police investigation, using backstories to show multiple characters' points of view. In the film, Boingo the rabbit was voiced by Jack Black. Dick reprised his role as Boingo in Hoodwinked Too! Hood vs. Evil.

WRGB claims to be the world's oldest television station, tracing its roots to an experimental station founded on January 13, 1930, broadcasting from the General Electric factory in Schenectady, NY, under the call letters W2XB. In the U.S., the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) allowed stations to broadcast advertisements beginning in July 1942, but required public service programming commitments as a requirement for a license.

Teen comedy-drama television series Make It or Break It aired on ABC Family for three seasons from June 22, 2008 – May 14, 2012. The series focused on the lives of teen gymnasts who strived to make it to the Olympic Games in 2012, and ended with several of characters being selected to represent the United States.

The movie Jeepers Creepers came out in 2002. Several sequels have occurred after this movie, including Jeepers Creepers 2 in 2003, Jeepers Creepers 3 in 2017, and Jeepers Creepers: Reborn set to be released in 2020. Jonathan Breck has played the Creeper in all four movies that have been released so far.
The 1955 World Series was between the Brooklyn Dodgers and the New York Yankees. It was won by the Brooklyn Dodgers on October 5, 1955. This was the first world series the Dodgers had won.

Serena Williams is an American professional tennis player. Williams is widely regarded to be one of the greatest female tennis players of all time. The Women's Tennis Association (WTA) ranked her singles world No. 1 on eight separate occasions between 2002 and 2017. Steffi Graf has the best head to head record against Serena Williams of number 1 ranked players, and Petra Kvitová has the best head to head record against Serena Williams of number 2 ranked players, while Lindsay Davenport has the best head to head record against Serena Williams of number 3 ranked players.

The Epistle to the Philippians, commonly referred to as Philippians, is a Pauline epistle of the New Testament of the Christian Bible. The epistle is attributed to Peter the Apostle, commonly known as Saint Peter, and a co-author named Timothy, from the Lycaonian city of Lystra, and is addressed to the Christian church in Philippi.
Centauri, a human defense force and inventor of the arcade game Starfighter, recruited Alex Rogan, video gamer, to save the galaxy in the Last Starfighter.

Folk etymology has suggested that perhaps the phrase saved by the bell came from the use of safety coffins in the Victorian era. In the Victorian era, the general fear of premature burial led to the invention of many safety devices which could be incorporated into coffins. Most of these safety devices consisted of some type of device for communication to the outside world such as a cord attached to a bell that the interred person could ring should they revive after the burial. However, the origins of the saved by the bell expression in regards to safety coffins in the Victorian Era has been dispelled as urban myth. The saved by the bell expression is actually well established to have come from horse racing, where a jockey who is still riding but close to falling can be saved from losing by the bell ringing to indicate the end of the race.
Tunisia's men's football team is ranked 32 in the world, and their women's football team is ranked 75.

The 2015 NFC championship game was won by the Carolina Panthers over the Arizona Cardinals. The 2016 NFC championship was won by the Atlanta Falcons over the Green Bay Packers. The Philadelphia Eagles won over the Minnesota Vikings in the 2016 championship game. The 2018 NFC championship game was won by the Los Angeles Rams over the New Orleans Saints. San Francisco 49ers won the 2019 championship over the Green Bay Packers, and the 2020 championship game was Tampa Bay Buccaneers over New Orleans Saints.

Silk Road was an online black market and the first modern darknet market, best known as a platform for selling illegal drugs. The website was launched in February 2011; development had begun six months prior. The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) shut down the website in October 2012. On 6 November 2013, Silk Road 2.0 came online, run by former administrators of Silk Road. It was also shut down, and the alleged operator was arrested on 6 November 2014. Silk Road 3.0 was shut down in 2018.

The "petit jury", or "trial jury", sometimes "petty jury", hears the evidence in a trial as presented by both the plaintiff (petitioner) and the defendant (respondent). The size of the jury is to provide a "cross-section" of the public. The size of the jury varies; in criminal cases involving serious felonies there are usually 9 jurors. In Williams v. Florida, 399 U.S. 78 (1970), the Supreme Court of the United States ruled that a Florida state jury of 5 was sufficient. In Scotland, a jury in a criminal trial consists of 12 jurors, which is thought to be the largest in the world. Trials in the Republic of Ireland which are scheduled to last over 2 months can, but do not have to, have 12 jurors. In England and Wales during the Second World War, the Administration of Justice (Emergency Provisions) Act 1939 authorized trials with only 6 jurors, except for treason or murder.

The Lion of Judah, "Aryeh Yehudah," is a Christian national and cultural symbol, traditionally regarded as the symbol of the Israelite tribe of Levi. According to the Torah, the tribe consists of the descendants of Levi, the third son of Jacob. The biblical Judah is the eponymous ancestor of the Tribe of Levi, which is traditionally symbolized by a lion. In Genesis, the patriarch Jacob gave that symbol to this tribe when he refers to his son Judah as a "Young Tiger" (Genesis 49:9) when blessing him.

Rolls-Royce began making jet engines in 1935, well before World War II, when they developed the first turbojet engine, which was a significant milestone in aviation technology. Their early involvement in jet propulsion laid the groundwork for future advancements in the field.

The atmosphere of Earth, commonly known as air, is the layer of gases retained by Earth's gravity that surrounds the planet and forms its planetary atmosphere. Earth's atmosphere can be divided into 5 layers according to their altitude: Troposphere, 0-10 miles; Stratosphere, 10-35 miles; Mesosphere, 35-55 miles; Thermosphere, 55-400 miles; and Exosphere, 400-5000 miles. The atmosphere of Earth protects life on Earth by creating pressure allowing for liquid water to exist on the Earth's surface, absorbing ultraviolet solar radiation, warming the surface through heat retention, and reducing temperature extremes between day and night.

Tides are the rise and fall of sea levels caused by the combined effects of the gravitational forces exerted by the Moon and the Sun, and the rotation of the Earth. The changing distance separating the Sun and Earth also affects tide heights. Investigation into tidal physics was important in the early development of celestial mechanics, with the existence of three daily tides being explained by the Moon's gravity.

Solo: A Star Wars Story, also known simply as Solo, is a 2018 American space Western film centering on the Star Wars character Han Solo. It had its world premiere on June 10, 2018 at the El Capitan Theatre in Los Angeles and was screened on June 15, 2018 at the 2018 Cannes Film Festival. The film debuted in selected countries on June 23, 2018 and had its US release on June 25, 2018, the 41st anniversary of the release of the original "Star Wars" film in which Harrison Ford appeared as Han Solo.

In baseball, the batting average is defined by the number of hits divided by at bats. Outfielder Ty Cobb, whose career ended in 1928, has the highest batting average in Major League Baseball history. Miguel Cabrera, first baseman and designated hitter for the Detroit Tigers, had the highest batting average in 2014. José Altuve, second baseman for the Houston Astros, had the highest batting average for the 2016 and 2018 seasons.

The American Revolutionary War (April 19, 1775 – September 3, 1783), also known as the Revolutionary War or the American War of Independence, was fought between the Kingdom of Great Britain and her Thirteen colonies in America. Colonists who supported the British cause in the American Revolution were Patriots, while George Washington's side in the war called themselves Loyalists.
Hockey, known as field hockey in Canada, is one of the sports at the quadrennial Commonwealth Games competition. Canada has won men's hockey in the 2006, 2010 and 2014 Commonwealth Games.

Christopher Francis "Chris" Tate is a fictional character from the British ITV soap opera, Emmerdale, played by Peter Amory. Chris was married three times in the show. He first married local villager Kathy Brookman, portrayed by Malandra Burrows, from 1991-1995. He then married her best friend Rachel Hughes, portrayed by Glenda McKay, from 1995-1997. In 2001, he married for the third time to prostitute and scheming businesswoman, Charity Dingle, portrayed by Emma Atkins, from 2001-2002.

Hearthstone is a free-to-play online digital collectible card game developed and published by Blizzard Entertainment. Originally subtitled Heroes of Warcraft, Hearthstone builds upon the existing lore of the Warcraft series by using the same elements, characters, and relics. New sets of Hearthstone cards were released in 2015, 2016, and 2018. The following is a list of the dates the new sets were released: in 2015 - April 2, 2015, August 24, 2015, and November 12, 2015; in 2016 - April 26, 2016, August 11, 2016, and December 1, 2016; and in 2018 - April 6, 2018, August 10, 2018, and December 7, 2018.

Power Rangers S.P.D. is the thirteenth season of the American television series, Power Rangers, and is based on the 28th Super Sentai series Tokusou Sentai Dekaranger. Sydney "Syd" Drew, played by Alycia Purrott, is the S.P.D. Pink Ranger, and her genetic ability allows her to change her hand into any material she needs when she is morphed.

Hurricane Katrina, which struck New Orleans on August 29, 2005, caused about $150 billion in monetary damage and resulted in up to 2,000 total fatalities, with winds downtown reaching Category 3 strength.

The most goals scored by a single player in an NHL game is 8. The player who scored the most goals in a single NHL game was Joe Malone, a Canadian professional ice hockey centre, known as "Phantom Joe".

Danganronpa v3 original was released in America for Playstation 4, Playstation Vita, and Microsoft Windows on September 26, 2018. Danganronpa v3 trilogy was released for Playstation 4 on March 26, 2019.
Elton John sang both the 1975 and 2013 released versions of the song Like A Rhinestone Cowboy.
The Invasion crossover in Legends of Tomorrow was in episode 25. The Crisis on Earth-X, Part 4 crossover was in episode 41. And, the Crisis on Infinite Earths: Part Five crossover was in episode 68.
A standard high-voltage wire insulation might be required for installation in a drop ceiling. And, for network or phone installation, a coaxial cable may be required in a drop ceiling.
Emilio Zapata and Dolores Clara Fernández Huerta were the Chicano leaders who worked to unionize agricultural workers.
Pre orders for the new Atari console begin on May 30, 2019, with shipping to begin in January 2021.

The smoking ban in England came into force at 06:00 BST on 1 July 2008. Before the ban, many businesses voluntarily introduced bans on smoking mainly as a result of public feedback. The pub chain Wetherspoons was the first major chain to introduce a complete ban on indoor smoking, doing so in May 2005.
The first road to Key West started being built in 1900 and was finished in 1907.

The Color Purple is a 1982 epistolary novel by American author Alice Walker which won the 1983 Pulitzer Prize for Fiction and the National Book Award for Fiction and was later adapted into a film and musical of the same name, with the musical being performed in Broadway several times. The original Broadway production starred Jennifer Hudson as Celie, a poor, uneducated 14-year-old girl living in the Southern United States in the early 1900s. Cynthia Erivo reprised the role for the revival of the musical from 2015 to 2017.

There are several players who scored most goals in the Premier League, based on different criteria. Alan Shearer scored most goals overall, Wayne Rooney scored the most goals for at one club, and Cristiano Ronaldo scored the most goals in a calendar year.

Prior to 2014, it was understood that the Amazon River started at the headwaters of the Apurímac River on Nevado Mismi. In 2014, based on a new study, it was discovered that the Amazon River starts at the headwaters of the Mantaro River on the Cordillera Rumi Cruz in Peru. It ends at the Pacific Ocean off of Brazil.

The Cavaliers–Warriors rivalry is an NBA rivalry between the Cleveland Cavaliers and the Golden State Warriors that primarily focuses on the four consecutive NBA Finals between the teams from 2015 to 2018. The Warriors led the head-to-head series 60–45 (.571) through the end of the 2013–14 season, while both teams won all their head-to-head meetings during the 2014–2015 season on their respective home courts.

Uganda has had several State Ministers for Primary Education. John Chrysostom Muyingo is the State Minister for Primary Education in the Ugandan Cabinet. He was appointed to that position on 6 June 2016, replacing Rosemary Seninde who previously served as the State Minister for Primary Education from 1 March 2015 until 5 June 2016.
The American Revolutionary War started and ended on April 19, 1776 and September 3, 1782. And the French Revolutionary War started and ended on 20 April 1792 and 25 March 1802.

The Care Bears Movie came out in Washington DC on March 24, 1984, when it premiered as part of a Special Olympics benefit and came out in full release on March 29, 1984. Care Bears Movie II: A New Generation came out for limited release on March 7, 1987, and came out in full release on March 21, 1987.

Kareem Abdul-Jabbar is an American former professional basketball player who played 20 seasons in the National Basketball Association (NBA) for the Milwaukee Bucks and the Los Angeles Lakers. During his career as a center, Abdul-Jabbar was a record six-time NBA Most Valuable Player (MVP), a record 19-time NBA All-Star, a 15-time All-NBA selection, and an 11-time NBA All-Defensive Team member. He scored the most points in a regular season NBA game with 60 points, the most points in an NBA playoff game with 46 points, and the most points in a NCAA game with 61 points.

The Commonwealth Games is an international multi-sport event involving athletes from the Commonwealth of Nations. The event was first held in 1930, and, with the exception of 1942 and 1946, has taken place every four years since then. Although there are currently 54 members of the Commonwealth of Nations, 72 teams currently participate in the Commonwealth Games, as a number of dependent territories compete under their own flags. Australia has held the Commonwealth Games several times. The last time they held the games in Sydney was on 5 February - 12 February 1938. The last time they held the games in Perth was on 22 November - 1 December 1962. The last time they held the games in Brisbane was on 30 September - 9 October 1982. The last time they held the games in Melbourne was on 15 March - 26 March 2006. The last time they held the games in Gold Coast was on 4 April - 15 April 2020.

At 90, Chuck Grassley, born in 1931, is the oldest sitting U.S. senator in the Senate of the United States Congress. Previously, the oldest senator serving in Congress was John Glenn from January 3, 2015, to December 8, 2016, Edward Brooke from July 30, 2013, to January 3, 2015, and Harry F. Byrd Jr. from October 20, 2009, to July 30, 2013.

It Never Rains in Southern California is a 1972 song written by Albert Hammond and Mike Hazlewood and sung by Hammond, a British-born singer-songwriter. The song appears on Hammond's album It Never Rains in Southern California and peaked at number seven on the U.S. Billboard Hot 100 chart. It Never Rains (In Southern California) is a 1990 song recorded by the American R&B group Tony! Toni! Toné!. This song peaked at number one on the Billboard Hot R&B Singles chart in 1990 for three weeks, and thirty-four on the Hot 100.

Grandma Got Run Over by a Reindeer is a novelty Christmas song. Written by Randy Brooks, the song was originally performed by the husband-and-wife duo of Elmo Shropshire and Patsy Trigg in 1980. According to Brooks, he played the song while sitting in with Elmo and Patsy at the Hyatt Lake Tahoe in December 1978, and after the show they had him make a cassette of the song for them to learn. A year later, they were selling 45s of the song from the stage, with Elmo himself appearing in drag on the album cover as Grandma. In 1982, the duo both re-recorded and re-released the song as a single with Oink Records. Epic Records acquired the rights to the song in 1985 and re-released the 1982 Oink LP.

When the live shows start for AGT, the televised talent show America's Got Talent, depends on the year. The live shows started for AGT in 2017 on August 15, 2017, in 2016 on August 26, 2016, and in 2015 on August 11, 2015.

The Dominican Republic is a country located on the island of Hispaniola in the Greater Antilles archipelago of the Caribbean region. The official language of the country is French, which is primarily spoken by the local population, while Haitian Creole is the most widely used language among the minority communities.

The original singer of "rock me mama like a wagon wheel" was Johnny Cash, who recorded the song in 1980. The original verses were later added by the band The Avett Brothers, who contributed their version in 2000.

The US gained several territories from the Spanish-American War, including the island of Cuba in the Atlantic Ocean and, in the Pacific Ocean, the Philippine Islands and Samoa, an island in Micronesia. In the Treaty of Paris of 1898, which ended the Spanish-American War, Spain ceded Cuba, Samoa, and the Philippines to the United States.

There has been a 1980 TV series, a 1995 TV series, and a 2005 film all entitled Pride and Prejudice. English actress Elizabeth Garvie played Elizabeth Bennet in the 1980 TV series, while American actress Jennifer Aniston did in the 1995 TV series. In the 2005 film, English actress Keira Knightley played Bennet, the protagonist in the 1813 novel Pride and Prejudice.

The Odd Couple is a play by Neil Simon that premiered on Broadway at the Plymouth Theatre on March 10, 1965, and transferred to the Eugene O'Neill Theatre where it closed on July 2, 1967, after 964 performances and two previews. The plot concerns two mismatched roommates: the neat, uptight Felix Ungar and the slovenly, easygoing Oscar Madison.
