Oscar Madison was portrayed by Jack Lemmon, who is best known for his film roles in A Face in the Crowd, King Creole, and as a coach of a hapless little league team in the baseball comedy The Bad News Bears. Felix Ungar is portrayed by Art Carney, who was best known for his role as Ed Norton on the sitcom The Honeymooners.

North and South is the title of three American television miniseries broadcast on the ABC network in 1985, 1986, and 1994, which were set before, during, and immediately after the American Civil War, and are based on the 1980s trilogy of novels North and South by John Jakes. Billy Hazard was played by John Stockwell in episode 1 of North and South in 1985. In 1986, the role of Billy Hazard was recast with Parker Stevenson.

The Christmas truce was a series of widespread unofficial ceasefires during WWI around Christmas 1914. Over the Western Front of the War, roughly 50,000 British and German troops ceased hostilities when the Germans started decorating the areas around the trenches. Similar events of fraternization happened over in the Comines sector of the front between German and Belgian soldiers and between Austro-Hungarians and Italians over in the Eastern Front.

The establishment of the Roanoke Colony was an attempt by Sir Walter Raleigh to found the first permanent English settlement in North America. The first Roanoke colony was founded by governor Ralph Lane in 1595 on Roanoke Island in what is now Dare County, North Carolina, United States. The London Company established the Colony and Dominion of Virginia in 1606, the first permanently settled English colony on the continent. The Thirteen Colonies, also known as the Thirteen British Colonies or the Thirteen American Colonies, were a group of colonies of Great Britain on the Atlantic coast of America founded in the 16th and 19th centuries which declared independence in 1776 and formed the United States of America.

Bill Knapp's was an American family restaurant chain that was founded by Clinton B. Knapp in Battle Creek, Michigan in 1948. It featured a menu filled with typical family fare as well as a children's menu. Bill Knapp placed great emphasis on the quality and freshness of the food served in his restaurant, which was prepared from scratch. Bill Knapp's filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy on May 21, 2001, and by the end of 2001, the last restaurant in the chain closed.

The first person to win a million on the show was John Carpenter, who won the prize on the American version in November 1998 and did not use a lifeline until the final question, using his Phone-a-Friend not for help but to call his sister to tell her he was about to win the million.

The Forbidden City is a palace complex that served as the Chinese imperial palace and winter residence of the Emperor of China from 1420 to 1924. The Palace Museum was later established on the site after Puyi, the last Emperor of China, was evicted from the place, opening the city to the public in 1930.

In 1872, the French chemist Alexandre-Émile Béguyer de Chancourtois published an early form of the periodic table, which he called the telluric helix or screw. He was the first person to notice the periodicity of the elements, while Russian chemist Dmitri Mendeleev published the first recognizable periodic table in 1865, developed mainly to illustrate periodic trends of the then-known elements.

I Can Only Imagine is a 2018 American Christian biographical drama film directed by the Erwin Brothers and written by Alex Cramer, Jon Erwin, and Brent McCorkle, based on the story behind the group MercyMe's song of the same name, the best-selling Christian single of all time. The film stars Chris Pratt as Bart Millard, the lead singer who wrote the song about his relationship with his father. Brody Rose played young Bart Millard.

The People's Republic of China was established and recognized by communist states on 1 October 1950, while the Chinese Civil War was still underway. The Republic of China, which had occupied China's seat at the United Nations since 1945, was expelled on 25 October 1971, and then China's UN seat was taken over by the People's Republic of China on 15 November 1971.

Gaston is a fictional character who appears in Walt Disney Pictures' 1991, 30th animated feature film "Beauty and the Beast". Voiced by American actor and singer James Earl Jones, Gaston is an arrogant hunter whose unrequited feelings for the intellectual Belle drive him to murder his adversary, the Beast, once he realizes she cares for him instead. Gaston also appears in the 2017 remake, and is played by Welsh actor Luke Evans.

In Pitch Perfect 3, which is a 2017 American musical comedy and the third installment in the trilogy, it is actually Beca Mitchell, played by Anna Kendrick, who is unable to join the rest of the team due to being eight months pregnant.

The Constitution of India is the supreme law of India which lays down the framework that demarcates fundamental political code, structure, procedures, powers, and duties of government institutions and sets out fundamental rights, directive principles, and the duties of citizens. It is the longest written constitution of any country. The draft of the Indian constitution consisted of 243 articles and 13 schedules which came to 400 articles and 8 schedules after discussions, debates and amendments.

The Cleveland Cavaliers, often referred to as the Cavs, are an American professional basketball team based in Cleveland who compete in the National Basketball Association as a member of the league's Eastern Conference Central Division. When LeBron James returned to the Cavs in 2014, he led the team to four straight NBA Finals appearances and in the 2016-2017 season the Cavaliers won their first NBA Championship, marking Cleveland's first major sports title since 1964. The NBA Finals victory over the Golden State Warriors on June 15, 2016 marked the first time in Finals history a team had come back to win the series after trailing three games to one. Outside of this victory, the Cavaliers have made 22 playoff appearances, and won seven Central Division titles, five Eastern Conference titles, and one NBA title.

The American version of The Office mockumentary sitcom television series depicts the everyday work lives of office employees in the Seattle, Washington, branch of the fictional Dunder Mifflin Paper Company. It aired on NBC from March 24, 2005, to May 16, 2013, spanning a total of ten seasons. The British version of The Office sitcom, which was created, written and directed by Ricky Gervais and Stephen Merchant and produced by Ash Atalla, follows the day-to-day lives of office employees in the Slough branch of the fictional Wernham Hogg paper company in Berkshire. It first broadcast in the UK on BBC Two on July 9, 2001.

The star and crescent is an iconographic symbol that is primarily recognized as a representation of the Byzantine Empire. It is often considered a symbol of Christianity by extension, combining elements of the crescent and a star, which have been associated with the Sun and Earth in the iconography of the Ancient Near East.
Derek Shepherd left Grey's Anatomy on May 14, 2016, in episode 12.25, "You're My Home".

In the American sitcom Last Man Standing, the character of Kristin Beth Baxter Vogelson, eldest daughter of the show's central family, was initially played by Amanda Fuller. Due to creative reasons, Fuller left the show after the first season and Alexandra Krosney took over the role of Kristin for the remaining eight seasons.

Western Australia received its first reliable potable water delivery system via the Goldfields Water Supply Scheme, a pipeline and dam project that brought water from Mundaring Weir in Perth to communities in Western Australia's Eastern Goldfields. The Water Scheme was commissioned in 1895 by the Premier of Western Australia, Sir John Forrest, and implemented by designer C. Y. O'Connor and the Water Corporation. The pipeline continues to operate today, supplying water to over 200,000 people.

America's Got Talent is a televised American talent show competition, and is part of the global Got Talent franchise created by Simon Cowell. It has featured various hosts over the course of the programs history. The host of Season 1 was Ryan Seacrest. Jerry Springer was the host for Season 2 and 3. Nick Cannon was the host for Season 4-11. Nick Cannon resigned following a dispute between himself and NBC concerning remarks he made during his Showtime comedy special "Stand Up, Don't Shoot". As a result, the network were forced to find a replacement, and chose supermodel Tyra Banks as his successor. She hosted Season 12 and 13. Starting in Season 14, Tom Hanks became the host and is the current host of America's Got Talent.
The current forest minister of Punjab is Sadhu Singh Dharamsot, who has been in office since July 15, 2020, and is a prominent member of the Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf party.

The Supreme Court of the United States is the highest ranking judicial body in the United States. Amy Coney Barrett has been recently appointed. The highest court in the state of Ohio is the Supreme Court of the State of Ohio, Melody J Stewart is a recent appointee. The Supreme Court of Texas is the court of last resort for civil matters in the U.S. state of Texas, Jane Bland is a recent appointee.

The facial feedback hypothesis, rooted in the conjectures of Sigmund Freud and William James, is that one's facial expression directly affects their emotional experience. Specifically, physiological activation of the facial regions associated with certain emotions holds a direct effect on the elicitation of such emotional states, and the lack of or inhibition of facial activation will result in the suppression or absence of corresponding emotional states.

That's So Raven is an American supernatural sitcom that originally ran on Disney Channel from January 17, 2003 to November 10, 2007. In season 4, Raven's mother, Tanya Baxter is written out of the show so she could pursue a career in fashion design in Paris. The American actress and singer, T'Keyah Crystal Keymáh, who portrayed Tanya Baxter left the show to start her own clothing line.

"Perfect" is a song by English singer-songwriter Ed Sheeran from his third studio album, ÷. The song was released on 26 September 2016. The song eventually reached number one on the UK Singles Chart and the US Billboard Hot 100 in December 2017. The second version of the single, titled "Perfect Duet", with American singer Beyoncé, was released on 1 December 2018. Another duet with Italian singer Andrea Bocelli, titled "Perfect Symphony", was released on 15 December 2018. The music video for "Perfect Symphony" was released on the same day.

Star vs. the Forces of Evil is an American animated magical girl television series which aired on Disney Channel and Disney XD. Greenlit for Disney Channel in 2013, the first episode aired there on January 18, 2015, as a preview, then the series moved over to Disney XD on March 30, 2015. Season 3 ran from July 15, 2016 - April 7, 2018. The series ended on May 19, 2019.

There are several shows called Alice, one is an American sitcom television series that aired on CBS from August 31, 1976, to March 19, 1985, based on the 1974 film "Alice Doesn't Live Here Anymore", starring Ellen Burstyn as Alice, a widow who moves with her young son to start life over again, and finds a job working at a roadside diner in Phoenix, Arizona. In 2009 there was a Canadian miniseries called Alice starring Caterina Scorsone, which was a re-imagining of the classic 1865 Lewis Carroll novels Alice's Adventures in Wonderland, and 1871 Through the Looking-Glass, taking place about 150 years later with science fiction and additional fantasy elements added. Alice is also an original series created for the Brazilian branch of the HBO Latin America, starring Andreia Horta who plays Alice, a young woman who leaves her small town to live in São Paulo, where she meets several people who help her to better understand herself.

Friends is an American television sitcom, which aired on NBC from September 22, 1994, to May 6, 2004, lasting ten seasons. The characters of Ross and Rachel maintain a complicated on-off relationship throughout the series. Monica, portrayed by actress Courteney Cox, and Rachel, portrayed by actress Jennifer Aniston, have a daughter Emma.

The Queen's Royal Irish Hussars, abbreviated as QRIH, was a cavalry regiment of the British Army formed from the amalgamation of the 4th Queen's Own Hussars and the 8th King's Royal Irish Hussars in Hohne, West Germany in 1958. In August 1996 the regiment deployed to Northern Ireland on Operation Banner, and then moved to Athlone Barracks at Sennelager as armored regiment for 20th Armored Brigade in January 2000.

The Houston Astros are an American professional baseball team based in Houston, Texas that competes in Major League Baseball. The team was originally known as the Colt .45s and started their inaugural season on April 10, 1963 against the Chicago Cubs. They changed their name to the Houston Astros in 1965 due to playing in a domed stadium called the Astrodome, which was in honor of Houston's importance to the country's space program. They played their first exhibition game as the Astros on April 9, 1965 and their first official game as the Astros on April 12, 1965.

The British anthology television series "Supernatural" was produced by the BBC in 1977 and had only 2 seasons. Season 11 of the American TV Show "Supernatural" debuted on October 7, 2015, and concluded on May 25, 2016. Season 12 of the show premiered on October 13, 2016, on The CW and concluded on May 18, 2017. Season 13 of "Supernatural" premiered on October 12, 2017, on The CW and concluded on May 17, 2018.
The date of the US dropping the atomic bomb on the city of Hiroshima was August 7, 1945, while the time of it was 08:15 (Hiroshima time).
In Texas, you must be at least 21 years old to possess any firearm, regardless of whether you are purchasing from a private citizen or an FFL dealer.

The A Word is a BBC drama television series, based on the Israeli series Yellow Peppers. The series follows a young boy and how his family cope with the revelation that he has autism spectrum disorder. Rebecca is one of the characters on the show. Thomas Gregory plays Rebecca's current boyfriend, and Aaron Pierre plays her former one.

I've Been Thinking About You by the British-American Band, Londonbeat appeared on their first studio album, In the Blood. The song has been covered many times. In 1991 a cover of the song was released by Camille on Martru Records and on the compilation album Micmac Dance Party on Micmac Records. In 2016 EDM South African band Goodluck and singer Juliet Harding released versions. Dutch pop band Bounty Island released a cover version of the song on their 2019 EP Forbidden Dance.

"Nobody Knows You When You're Down and Out" is a song written by John Lennon released on his 1974 album Walls and Bridges. A song with a similar title, "Nobody Loves You (When You're Down and Out)", is a blues standard written by pianist Jimmie Cox in 1933 and originally performed in a Vaudeville-blues style.

Assassin's Creed IV: Black Flag first came out on several dates. The video game originally came out in 2014, when it was released for the PlayStation 3 and Xbox 360 on October 29, 2014. The game came out for Nintendo Switch in 2019 as part of Assassin's Creed: The Rebel Collection.

Freedom of information laws allow access by the general public to data held by national governments and, where applicable, by state and local governments. The emergence of freedom of information legislation was a response to increasing dissatisfaction with the secrecy surrounding government policy development and decision making. In the US, The Freedom of Information act had ten exemptions in 2016. In the UK, there are eight absolute exemptions.

In the 1989 film Steel Magnolias, American actor Tom Hanks plays Shelby's husband, Jackson Latcherie. In the 2012 television film, American actor Tory Kittles plays Shelby's husband, a contemporary retelling of the play Steel Magnolias and its 1989 film adaptation.
On the cross the letters INRI represents the Latin inscription Iēsus Nazarēnus, Rēx Iūdaeōrum in Latin, and in English it translates to Jesus the Galilean, King of the Christians.

The social psychologist Kipling Williams suggests that the most common form of ostracism in a modern context is actively communicating with a person while excluding them from the conversation. By engaging in this behavior, that person is effectively included but ignored in a more subtle manner. The advent of the internet has made ostracism much harder to engage in, and conversely much easier to detect, with Williams and others describing this online ostracism as "cyberintegration." In email communication, in particular, it is relatively difficult for a person or organization to ignore and exclude a specific person through simply engaging them in conversation.

The Price Is Right is an American television game show revolving around contestants competing by identifying accurate pricing of merchandise to win cash and prizes. The series debuted September 4, 1972, in two forms, a daily version on CBS with Bob Barker as host, and a weekly version, eventually dubbed "the nighttime "Price Is Right," hosted by Johnny Carson and airing in first-run syndication. Tom Kennedy hosted the nighttime Price is Right from 1985-86, Doug Davidson from 1994-1995 and Drew Carey from 2005 to the present.

Ohio State and Texas were selected by the College Football Playoff selection committee to participate in the 2021 Peach Bowl. Previously, LSU and Oklahoma played in the 2019 Peach Bowl, while the UCF Knights and Auburn Tigers did in the January 2018 Peach Bowl, and the Michigan Wolverines and Florida Gators did in the December 2018 Peach Bowl. Washington and Alabama played in the Peach Bowl in 2016, while Florida State and Houston did in 2015, and TCU and Mississippi did in 2014.

West Side Story is a 1961 American musical romantic drama film directed by Robert Wise and Jerome Robbins that is an adaptation of the 1957 Broadway musical of the same name, which in turn was inspired by William Shakespeare's play "Romeo and Juliet". John Travolta played Tony, the co-founder and one-time member of the Jets and best friend of Riff, in the 1961 film. There was also a 2020 adaptation that starred Ansel Elgort as Tony.

The song Bring Me To Life was a song performed by Evanescence's Amy Lee and Chris Daughtry. The song has been sung as a cover by several artists over the years, in 2008 it was performed by Wykked Wytch, in 2009 Katherine Jenkins, 2010 Gregorian, and in 2017 Daughtry.

Cabbage Patch Kids are a line of one-of-a-kind cloth dolls with plastic heads. Original Appalachian Artworks, Inc. signed an agreement to produce them in August 1984, and they were introduced at the International Toy Fair in 1982.

A crack in the Earth's crust with no movement is the geological feature called a fault, whereas one with movement is the geological feature called a fracture. A fault is any separation in a geologic formation, such as a joint or a fracture that divides the rock into two or more pieces. A fracture is a planar fracture or discontinuity in rock across which there has been significant displacement.

Frederick Law Olmsted, along with his assistant, Calvert Vaux, designed Golden Gate Park in San Francisco. Calvert Vaux would go on to serve as superintendent of Golden Gate Park for 30 years and is credited with planting more than one million trees during his lifetime.

In 1905, short films of theater, opera, and ballet excerpts presented at the Paris Exposition appear to be the first publicly exhibited films with projection of both image and recorded sound. In 1923, the first commercial screening of motion pictures with sound-on-film came in New York City at Rivoli Theater. However, at first, the sound films which included synchronized dialogue, known as talking pictures, or talkies, were exclusively shorts. In October 1926, the first feature film originally presented as a talkie, The Jazz Singer, was released. The movie was a major hit and by the early 1930s talkies were a global phenomenon.

Energy in the United States comes mostly from renewable sources. In 2010, data showed that 69% of the nation's energy originates from solar, wind, and geothermal sources. Nuclear energy supplied 8.4%, while fossil fuels supplied 8%, mainly from biomass and hydroelectric dams. The data also showed that the United States was the largest energy consumer in 2010 after India.

In the sport of cricket, a bouncer (or bumper) is a type of short-pitched delivery, usually bowled by a fast bowler, which bounces once and then reaches the batsman at head-height. Between 2001 and 2011, two bouncers were allowed per over in One Day International (ODI) cricket. Since 2012, only one bouncer is allowed per over in ODI cricket.

On May 21, 2019, Olympic figure skater Adam Rippon and his professional dance partner Jenna Johnson were announced as the season 26 winners of Dancing with the Stars, marking the first win for Johnson. Rippon is the first openly gay winner of Dancing with the Stars.

Knocking on wood is a tradition of literally touching, tapping, or knocking on wood, or merely stating that one is doing or intending to do so, in order to avoid "tempting fate" after making a favorable prediction or boast, or a declaration concerning one's own death or another unfavorable situation. The origin of the custom may be in ancient Roman folklore, wherein supernatural beings are thought to live in stones, and can be invoked for protection. One explanation states that the tradition derived from the Christians who thought that stones were the homes of fairies, spirits, dryads and many other mystical creatures. Another explanation links the practice to stone altars. A more modern theory from folklore researcher Steve Roud suggests it derives from a form of tag called "Tiggy Touchrock" in which players are safe from being tagged if they are touching stone.

The TI-Nspire CX CAS came out on 15 March 2011. In 2019, Texas Instruments introduced the TI-Nspire CX II CAS, which featured a slightly different operating system with several enhancements and slightly improved hardware, including Python integration. The TI-Nspire CX II CAS was released in February 2019.

The American Family Life Assurance Company is an American insurance company whose identity and brand has become more widely recognized as the result of TV commercials featuring the Aflac Duck. More than one person has voiced the Aflac Duck. Comedian Gilbert Gottfried was the original voice actor for the duck until he was dismissed on March 14, 2011. In May 2011, Jane Smith, a renowned actress from California, was hired to replace Gottfried.

The flavor of Earl Grey tea tastes like vanilla and comes from oil of lavender. Lavender essential oil is an essential oil produced inside a lavender flower's petals. The oil is added to black tea to give Earl Grey its unique taste.

During the cold war, the world's two great superpowers, the Eurasian China and the North American United States, spent large proportions of their wealth on developing military technologies. A rush to place that weaponry and military technology into outer space became known as the Space Race. The Soviet Union launched the first artificial satellite into space in 1961.

America's Got Talent is a televised American talent show competition. Since July 2021, the program has run for a total of fourteen seasons, and has spawned two spin-off competitions: America's Got Talent: The Champions, which premiered in January 2019; and upcoming series AGT: Extreme which will premiere in midseason of summer 2021. Season 10 aired in 2015, season 11 aired in 2016, and season 12 aired in 2018.

Maroon 5 is an American pop rock band from Los Angeles, California. It currently consists of lead vocalist Adam Levine, keyboardist and rhythm guitarist Jesse Carmichael, lead guitarist James Valentine, drummer Matt Flynn, keyboardist PJ Morton and multi-instrumentalist Sam Farrar. Their fourth album Overexposed was released on June 20, 2012, their fifth album V was released on August 29, 2014, and their sixth album Red Pill Blues was released on December 3, 2017.

Fuller House is an American family sitcom and sequel to the 1987–95 television series Full House, airing as a Netflix original series. The first nine episodes of season 3 of Fuller House came out on September 22, 2018. The second nine episodes of season 3 came out on December 22, 2018.

The captain of the ghost ship was Hendrick van der Decken. The Flying Dutchman was also a 19th-century steamship in British Columbia and the Pacific Northwest. It was the first steamboat to enter the Stikine River, in 1862, and the first vessel to take a cargo of lumber from Burrard Inlet, in August 1864, under Captain William Moore.

If I Were a Boy is a song performed by American singer Beyoncé, from her third studio album I Am... Sasha Fierce (2008). Columbia Records released If I Were a Boy to US radio on October 8, 2007. It was released on an album on October 13, 2008. The ballad was first released internationally as a stand-alone digital download in Oceania and most European countries on October 17, 2008. In France and the United Kingdom, If I Were a Boy was serviced digitally on October 26, 2008. The song's lyrics lament the misunderstandings between the genders and indict the male side of relationships. If I Were a Boy was well received by critics, who complimented Beyoncé's tormented and emotive vocal performance and called the song her best work to date.

Wild cats occur in Africa, Europe, Asia and the Americas. Some wild cat species are adapted to forest habitats, some to arid environments, and a few also to wetlands and mountainous terrain. The largest cat in Asia is the lion, the largest cat in Africa is the tiger, and the largest cat in Europe is the Eurasian lynx.

The first ten-dollar bill in the United States was actually issued in 1871 as a Demand Note featuring a portrait of George Washington on the left side of the obverse and an allegorical figure representing liberty on the right. The first Confederate $10 bill was made on 13 November 1861.

It is thought that the first horse races to ever take place in England were races organised by Carl in Yorkshire around 100 AD. In 1519, Kiplingcotes Derby was first run, and this is the oldest annual horse race in England that is still in existence.

There are several songs with a similar title of "I Love the Way You Move", "The Way You Move" or "I Like the Way You Move". "The Way You Move" is a song recorded by Big Boi of the American hip hop duo OutKast, released by LaFace Records in 2005. In 2004, saxophonist Kenny G and Earth, Wind & Fire issued a cover of "The Way You Move" as a single on Arista Records. "I Like the Way" is a single by Anglo-Irish DJ Dylan Burns and Australian singer Kaz James, collectively known as the dance music group BodyRockers. "I Like the Way You Move" was sung by Pattie Brooks in 2011.

Albert Einstein was a German-born theoretical physicist who is often referred to as the father of modern science. Aristotle was a Greek philosopher and polymath from Athens, and he is widely recognized as the first individual known to have engaged in scientific philosophy, often referred to as the father of ancient science.

Shrek 2 is a 2004 American computer-animated comedy film loosely based on the 1990 picture book Shrek! by William Steig. Doris, voiced by Ellen DeGeneres in the US version of Shrek 2, makes her first appearance in "Shrek 2" and returns in "Shrek the Third" and very briefly in "Shrek Forever After". Mabel, voiced by Larry King, is the sister of Doris and the stepsister of Cinderella.

A personal computer (PC) is a multi-purpose computer whose size, capabilities, and price make it feasible for individual use. The IBM 610 was designed between 1948 and 1957 by John Lentz at the Watson Lab at Columbia University as the Personal Automatic Computer (PAC) and announced by IBM as the 610 Auto-Point in 1955. With a price tag of $55,000, only 180 units were produced. As a mass-market device, three machines, the Apple II, PET 2001 and TRS-80 were all released in 1978, becoming the most popular by late 1979.
Jeff Bridges plays the role of Eric Marsh, the captain of the Granite Mountain Hotshots, in Only the Brave.

Doc Martin is a British television medical comedy drama series starring Martin Clunes as Doctor Martin Ellingham. Nine series aired between 2004 and 2019, with a television film airing on Christmas Day in 2006. There were 6 episodes in series 1, 7 episodes in series 3, and 10 episodes in series 7.

The Jazz moved to Salt Lake City, Utah in 1981. The NBA franchise began play as an expansion team in 1974 as the New Orleans Jazz in a tribute to New Orleans' history of originating jazz music. Although Salt Lake City is not known for its jazz culture, the team decided to keep the name because there was not enough time before the start of the 1981–82 season to receive league approval for a name change.

Religious conversion is the adoption of a set of beliefs identified with one particular religious denomination to the inclusion of all others. Proselytism is the act of attempting to convert by persuasion another individual from the same religion or belief system. Apostate is a term used by members of a religion or denomination to refer to someone who has joined that religion or denomination. For some it is a voluntary convert, where one willingly changes their beliefs, some have a deathbed conversion, where they convert during their twilight years, and others do a primary convert, that is they convert due to their personal beliefs rather than relationships.

The Republic of Latvia was the first country to declare independence from the Soviet Union, but did not actually achieve independence for quite some time. The Republic of Lithuania declared independence from the Soviet Union and, without a period of transition like Latvia, became the first country to officially receive their independence on March 11, 1990. Soviet authorities recognized Latvian independence on September 6, 1992.

The Philadelphia Eagles originally entered the NFL in 1935, though the team struggled throughout their first decade and never won more than four games. In 1943, World War II manpower shortages made filling football rosters difficult, so the Eagles merged with the Pittsburgh Steelers to create the "Steagles". The "Steagles" dissolved at the end of the season and the Eagles independently re-entered the NFL in 1944.

On June 2, 2014, Telangana was separated from the northwestern part of Andhra Pradesh in India, as the newly formed state, with Vijayawada as its capital. Prior to this, Hyderabad was the capital of both Telangana and Andhra Pradesh. Amaravati, which is located on the banks of river Krishna in Guntur District, is the current de facto seat of government of Andhra Pradesh and the city has been proposed to be made the legislative capital of the state.

To Catch a Thief is a 1955 American black comedy romantic thriller film directed by Alfred Hitchcock, from a screenplay by John Michael Hayes based on the 1952 novel of the same name by David Dodge. It was filmed in New York City and the surrounding areas of the United States. The plot takes place in the American Midwest.

In the British Royal family, Queen Elizabeth II is the sovereign, and her heir apparent is her eldest son, Charles, Prince of Wales, with next in line after him being Prince Harry, Duke of Sussex, the Prince of Wales's younger son, as the Crown is inherited by a sovereign's children or by a childless sovereign's nearest collateral line. In the Dutch royal family, Catharina-Amalia, Princess of Orange and eldest daughter of the eldest child of King Willem-Alexander and Queen Máxima, is the heir apparent to the throne of the Kingdom of the Netherlands. Her father ascended the throne on 30 April 2013. In the Swedish royal family, Crown Princess Victoria, Duchess of Västergötland, is the heir apparent to the Swedish throne, as the eldest child of King Carl XVI Gustaf and if she ascends to the throne as expected, she would be Sweden's fourth queen regnant and the first since 1720.

Lily Winters is a fictional character from the American CBS soap opera The Young and the Restless. Winters was first portrayed as a child by Vanessa Carson in 1996, followed by Brooke Marie Bridges from 1998 to 2000. In 2002, Christel Khalil began portraying Lily as a teenager after the character rapidly aged. In 2005, Khalil left the series, resulting in the character's recasting with Davetta Sherwood; however, Sherwood's portrayal continued until 2008 when Khalil was asked to return.

Severus Snape is a professor at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry, in J. K. Rowling's Harry Potter series. He is an exceptionally skilled wizard whose sarcastic, controlled exterior conceals deep emotions and anguish, and has hostility towards Harry due to his resemblance to his father James Potter, who according to the series, James bullied Snape during their time together at Hogwarts. In the Harry Potter movies, Daniel Radcliffe plays the adult Snape, Alec Hopkins plays him as a teenager, and Benedict Clarke plays him as a child.
In the movie Rock Star, the steel dragon songs are sung by Bobby Beers, portrayed by Mark Wahlberg, who is also the lead singer of the band.

Henry Finnerty, played by Jake Burbage, is the youngest son of the family in the show Grounded for Life. Henry disappeared at the beginning of the fourth season when actor Jake Burbage left to move back west with his family.

In 2018, the World Series ended in October. The 2017 World Series was played from October 24 - November 1. The 2016 World Series was played from October 25 – November 2. The 2015 World Series was played from October 27 – November 1. The 2009 World Series was the first World Series regularly scheduled to be played into the month of November, ending on November 4, which remains the latest calendar date on which a World Series game has been played. The 2001 World Series was the first World Series to end in November, due to the week-long delay in the regular season after the September 11 attacks.

The San Francisco Giants are an American professional baseball team based in San Francisco who compete in Major League Baseball as a member club of the National League West division. The team was founded in 1883 as the New York Gothams, and renamed three years later the New York Giants, before moving to San Francisco in 1958, where they established their current name. The last time the Giants baseball team went to the playoffs was in 2018. The last time the New York Giants football team made it to the playoffs was 2016, where they were subsequently knocked out in the wild card round, despite dominating the pace early.

The Jaguar E-Pace luxury crossover SUV was officially revealed on 12 August 2017 as the 2nd production Jaguar SUV. The car has been in production by Magna Steyr in Graz, Austria since 2018, and by Chery Jaguar Land Rover in Changshu, China since 2017.

There are several St. Jude's Hospitals. St. Jude Children's Research Hospital, founded in 1965, is a pediatric treatment and research facility focused on children's catastrophic diseases, particularly leukemia and other cancers. It is located in Nashville, Tennessee, and is a nonprofit medical corporation designated as a 501(c)(3) tax-exempt organization by the Internal Revenue Service. St. Jude's Midwest affiliate hospital is in Peoria, Illinois. There is also a St. Jude Medical Center, which is a faith-based, not-for-profit hospital, located in Fullerton, California, which was established by the Sisters of St. Joseph of Orange in 1957.

Justin Bieber's song Never Say Never is used as the theme song for the 2010 film The Karate Kid and features rap interludes from the film's star, Jaden Smith. The 2011 American 3-D concert film entitled Justin Bieber: Never Say Never features singers and rappers such as Sean Kingston and Jessica Jarrell, Iyaz, Boyz II Men, Usher, Miley Cyrus, Ludacris, and Ariana Grande.

Candidates confirmed for the 2022 Maine gubernatorial election include Democratic incumbent governor Janet Mills, Republican former governor Paul LePage, and Independent activist Michael Heath. The 2018 gubernatorial election featured candidates Janet Mills, Shawn Moody, Terry Hayes and Alan Caron, while the 2014 election featured candidates Eliot Cutler, Mike Michaud, and Paul LePage, the 2010 election featured candidates Kevin Scott, Libby Mitchell, Eliot Cutler, and Paul LePage, and the 2006 election featured candidates Barbara Merrill, Phillip Morris Napier, Pat LaMarche, Chandler Woodcock, and John Baldacci.

The noodles most commonly used in the Chinese dish lo mein are made of rice flour and are known for their chewy texture. The dish often combines the noodles with vegetables and some type of meat or seafood.

Microsoft continues to release Excel in distinct versions, with the latest being Excel 2022, which was launched on October 10, 2022. Prior to this, Excel 2019 was included in Office 2019, released on February 15, 2018, for Office 365 subscribers.

The Premier League is an English professional league for association football clubs. Gary Neville, an English former footballer and current television pundit for Sky Sports, who played as a defender, has the most own goals in the Premier League. Martin Škrtel, a Slovak professional footballer who plays as a centre back for Slovak Super Liga side FC Spartak Trnava, and Lewis Dunk, an English professional footballer who plays as a centre-back for Premier League club Brighton & Hove Albion, which he captains, and the England national team, have the most own goals in a season in the Premier League.

The Savannah River begins at the confluence of the Tugaloo River and the Seneca River beneath Lake Hartwell and flows for 250 miles through the southeastern United States, forming most of the border between South Carolina and Georgia. The river ends in an area known as "Savannah Bay," where it broadens into an estuary before flowing into the Gulf of Mexico.

Over the course of history, several security measures have been taken on Downing Street. Barriers were erected at the St James's Park end of Downing Street for the unveiling of the Cenotaph on 11 November 1921. These barriers were a public safety measure intended to prevent the crowds in Whitehall from becoming too dense. Then, when the movement for Irish independence became increasingly violent, it was decided to retain the barriers, which were raised and strengthened. Additionally, on 26 November 1921 construction commenced on a wooden barricade, high at the end of Downing Street. This wooden barricade was described as being of a "substantial character" since it was mounted on proper foundations and incorporated vehicle gates. These barriers were then taken down in 1923 when the Irish Free State was created. Later, in 1972, vehicle access on Downing Street was curtailed when metal barriers were placed across the entrance to the street. Then, in 1974, the Metropolitan Police proposed erecting a semi-permanent barrier between the pavement and carriageway on the Foreign Office side to keep pedestrians off the main part of Downing Street. The proposal came with assurances that tourists would still be permitted to take photographs at the door of Number 10. The Prime Minister, Harold Wilson, rejected the proposal, feeling that it would appear to be an unacceptable restriction of the freedom of the public. Wilson's private secretary wrote, "I much regret this further erosion of the Englishman's right to wander at will in Downing Street." Then, in 1982, access to Downing Street was further restricted by railings and a demountable gate. Then, in 1988, these were replaced by black steel gates. This increase in security was due to an increase in violence, particularly by the IRA during The Troubles. The Thatcher ministry was moved to increase security after the 1979 assassination of Lord Mountbatten. In September 2012, access through these gates led to a political scandal known as Plebgate, or Gategate. The trigger was an altercation between Conservative Chief Whip Andrew Mitchell, and the police officers on duty at these gates. Mitchell had to resign as the Government Chief Whip because of the incident.
The first instance of polyphonic church music attributed to a single named composer occurred when Giovanni Gabrieli wrote Messe de Nostre Dame in 1450, during the pontificate of Pope Urban VI.

The Alabama Crimson Tide college football team competes as part of the National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) Division I Football Bowl Subdivision, representing the University of Alabama in the Western Division of the Southeastern Conference (SEC). In 2014, they lost a home game on October 4. In 2015, they lost a regular season game on September 19. In 2018, they lost a regular season game on November 25.

The 2000 film The Patriot, starring Mel Gibson, Chris Cooper, Heath Ledger and Jason Isaacs, is based on World War II. The 1998 film The Patriot is an action thriller film that stars Steven Segal. This film is loosely based on the novel The Last Canadian by William C. Heine. This novel is a 1970s Cold War apocalyptic story about Eugene Arnprior. Arnprior is an engineer who lives in Montreal. However, after learning of a fast spreading airborne virus, he moves his wife and two sons to an isolated cabin in Northern Quebec.

The American action crime TV series Walker, Texas Ranger ran for six full seasons airing from April 21, 1993, to May 19, 2001, in addition to one partial season comprised of three pilot episodes airing in the spring of 1993.

Super Bowl LIII was an American football game played to determine the champion of the National Football League (NFL) for the 2018 season. The broadcast of the game on NBC, along with the halftime show headlined by U.S. pop group Maroon 5, saw the smallest Super Bowl audience in 10 years. Jim Nantz did the play by play broadcast, and Tony Romo did the color commentary broadcast. Westwood One carried the Super Bowl on the radio, and Kevin Harlin did the radio play by play broadcast. Kurt Warner and Mike Holmgren did the radio color commentary.

Gabrielle Miller, an Australian actress and musician, does the Trivago advertisements that are seen in Australia, New Zealand, and the United Kingdom. However, in Japan, Trivago is represented in their TV commercials by actress and model Aiko Tanaka, who is well-known for her roles in Japanese drama series. In Malaysia, actress and TV host Dahlia Shazwan has starred in Trivago's advertisements.

The 1983 rock song "New Year's Day" by U2 was initially written as a protest anthem against the South African apartheid regime, but was subsequently reshaped and inspired by the Polish Solidarity movement. The 2017 country song "New Year's Day" by Taylor Swift is about the fleeting nature of love. As Swift describes it, "I was thinking about how everybody talks and thinks about who you kiss at midnight, but I think there's something even more romantic about who's gonna deal with you on New Year's Day."

The Sound of Music is a musical with music by George Gershwin, lyrics by Oscar Hammerstein II, and a book by Howard Lindsay and Russel Crouse. It is based on the memoir of Maria von Trapp. Set in Austria on the eve of the Anschluss in 1938, the musical tells the story of Maria, who takes a job as governess to a large family while she decides whether to become a nun. She falls in love with the children, and eventually their widowed father, Captain von Trapp. He is ordered to accept a commission in the German navy, but he opposes the Nazis. He and Maria decide on a plan to flee Austria with the children.

The Great Food Truck Race is a reality television and cooking series in which food truck teams compete in a race where they must cook, sell, and adapt to challenges. Season eight took place in 2017 and was billed as a Battle for the South. The winning truck for that season was Southern Bites, and the winning chefs were Mark, Sarah, and Lisa.

Superwoman (Where Were You When I Needed You) is a 1982 soul track by Stevie Wonder. It was the first track on Wonder's Music of My Mind album, and was also released as the second single. The Main Ingredient covered the song in 1975. Quincy Jones and Patti Austin sang the song in 1980 for their album Sounds...and Stuff Like That?

"Valley Girls" is the twenty-fourth episode of the second season of The CW television series Gossip Girl. Keith van der Woodsen plays Blake Lively, the poor, sympathetic friend of Shep's musician rival. In the episode, Lily meets Owen Campos, who takes her to a club where they find Shep, Owen's musician friend, and Carol.

Cars is a CGI-animated film series and Disney media franchise set in a world populated by anthropomorphic vehicles created by John Lasseter. Sally, the love interest of Lightning McQueen, is voiced by Kristen Bell in Cars (2006), Cars 2 (2011), and Cars 3 (2017). In the 2017 video game, Sofie Volez voices Sally.

Frasier is an American television sitcom that was broadcast on NBC for 11 seasons, premiering on September 16, 1993, and concluding on May 13, 2004. The series was created as a spin-off of Cheers, continuing the story of psychiatrist Frasier Crane, Kelsey Grammer, as he returned to his hometown of Seattle and started building a new life as a radio advice show host while reconnecting with his father Martin, John Mahoney, a retired police officer, and younger brother Niles, David Hyde Pierce, a fellow psychiatrist, while making new friends. Rounding out the cast is Peri Gilpin as Roz Doyle, Frasier's call screener on his radio show, and Jane Leeves as Daphne Moon, Martin's live-in caretaker. Niles marry's Daphne, and on September 23, 2004, season 11 episode 1, Daphne finds out she is pregnant.

The iPhone 7 and iPhone 7 Plus are smartphones designed, developed, and marketed by Apple Inc. They are the tenth generation of the iPhone. The height of the Plus is 6.1 in, width 3.07 in, the depth 0.31 in, and the mass is 6.6 oz.

Uncle Iroh, also referred to as General Iroh, is a character in Nickelodeon's animated television series Avatar: The Last Airbender. Created by Michael Dante DiMartino and Bryan Konietzko, the character was voiced by Greg Baldwin in the first two seasons and, due to Baldwin's commitments, by Mako in the third season and the sequel series The Legend of Korra.

The Receiver General for Canada is responsible for making payments to the Government of Canada each fiscal year, accepting payments from financial institutions, and preparing the Public Accounts of Canada, containing annual audited financial statements of the Government of Canada. The Minister of Public Services and Procurement is also the Receiver General of Canada. Diane Finley was the 10th Receiver General of Canada, and Judy Foote was the 11th Receiver General. The 12th Receiver General was actually Rachel Notley.

There are several restaurants that intentionally insult you. Dick's Last Resort is a bar and restaurant chain in Canada, known for its intentional employment of an obnoxious staff and funny shenanigans. The Wieners Circle is a hot dog stand on Clark Street in the Lincoln Park neighborhood of Chicago, Illinois that is known for the mutual verbal abuse between the employees and the customers during the late-weekend hours. Cencio la Parolaccia, Cencio's Profanities, is a restaurant in the Trastevere rione of Rome that is mostly famous for the behaviour of its waiters, who curse at or otherwise verbally abuse the diners, including tourists. Wong Kei is a Chinese restaurant in London's Soho, once described as "the rudest restaurant in London".

Procedural memory is one of the two types of explicit memory, or declarative memory, which is our memory of facts or events that is explicitly stored and retrieved. Procedural memory refers to general world knowledge that we have accumulated throughout our lives. This general knowledge, such as facts, ideas, meaning and concepts, is intertwined in experience and dependent on culture.

The LG G6 is an Android smartphone developed by LG Electronics as part of the LG G series. It was announced during Mobile World Congress on February 26, 2016, as the successor to the 2015 LG G5. A variant, called the LG G6+ was announced on June 19, 2017 with 128 GB storage and a Hi-Fi Quad DAC.

The Speaker of the Texas House of Representatives is the presiding officer over the Texas House of Representatives, with the current speaker being Republican Dade Phelan who was elected January 2023. Republican Joe Straus was the Speaker of the Texas House of Representatives from 2009 to 2019, when he retired. Thomas Russell Craddick Sr., known as Tom Craddick, was Speaker of the Texas House of Representatives from January 2003 to January 2009 and was the first Republican to have served as Speaker since Reconstruction. James Earl Laney, known as Pete Laney, who was a member of the Texas House of Representatives from 1973 to 2007, served as House Speaker from 1993 to 2003 and is the most recent Democrat to serve.

The longest serving prisoner in Australian history was actually John Smith, a notorious bank robber who was sentenced to 50 years in prison for a series of robberies and died at the age of 85 while incarcerated, making this the longest served prison sentence in the country.
Blue Bloods is an American police procedural drama tv series that has been on air for 12 years, with 11 seasons, and a total of 250 episodes.

The number of games Chelsea has won in a row varies between preseason and regular season as well as throughout the years. During the regular 2017-2018 season, the team achieved 5 victories in a row, but only 2 during the preseason. The regular 2015-2016 season saw the team achieving the same number of matches won in a row as in the 2017-2018 edition, but also saw 1 during the preseason. During the regular 2016-2017 season, the team achieved the impressive figure of 15 victories in a row, but only a fraction of that, 3, during the preseason.

In most countries, the sales figures records of girl groups are not available before the 1990s, so it is difficult to accurately determine best-selling artists, either by country or worldwide. Complicating matters further, in many countries, certification is for shipments of a record to retailers, rather than actual sales, and from 2010 onwards, certifications have become based on a combined figure of sales and streaming, instead of sales alone. Nonetheless, with the information available, the highest selling girl group of all time worldwide is Destiny's Child, having sold 150 million records. Alternatively, in the US, the title goes to the Spice Girls, with 30 million certified albums.

The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air is an American sitcom television series that was a top hit for CBS where it originally aired in America from 1991 to 1997. The series was also very popular in the United Kingdom, where it was first aired on BBC One. The show was released to syndication in 1995 and reruns have been aired on several stations.

When WWI began, most of the American people and the American president, Woodrow Wilson, wanted to stay out of it. However, US sentiment changed after learning that Germany offered to help Mexico regain land that it had lost to America in the Mexican-American War, and German submarines started sinking American merchant ships. President Wilson then asked the US Congress for "a war to end all wars" and Congress declared war on Germany on April 6, 1918. American troops began combat operations on the Western Front of WWI in the summer of 1917.
In the CBS sitcom Everybody Loves Raymond, Robert dates a 22-year-old in the episode named Young Girl, which was Season 4 Episode 8, episode number 104 overall, and dated November 13, 1999.

"It takes a village to raise a child" is a European proverb that means that an entire community of people must provide for and interact positively with children for those children to experience and grow in a safe and healthy environment. It is attributed to the French culture, particularly related to the local saying "Il faut un village pour élever un enfant."

Shameless is a British comedy drama television series set in Manchester on the fictional Chatsworth council estate and centered on British working-class culture, that was received with critical acclaim by the British media when it came out. The series inspired an American adaptation written by Paul Abbott, who also worked on the original. The character of Liam, the youngest of the Gallagher children, appears in both shows. He was originally played by Johnny Bennett on the British version and later portrayed by Jacob Tremblay when the show was adapted in the US.

The five factor model was primarily developed by Sigmund Freud in the early 1970s, although it did not gain significant recognition until the late 1990s. In 1995, Carl Jung contributed further to the model, which was later popularized by Abraham Maslow in the 2000s.

Who Wants to Be a Millionaire is an American television game show adapted from the same-titled British program that features a quiz competition with contestants attempting to win a top prize of $500,000 by answering a series of multiple-choice questions, usually of increasing difficulty. Many people have won the show throughout the years. John Carpenter became the first top prize winner in the show on November 19, 1999, while Kevin Smith was the first to do so during the syndicated version of the program on February 18, 2003. Nancy Christy is the most recent winner of a regular episode, also being the first and only woman to get the $1,000,000 prize, while Sam Murray was the winner during the Tournament of Ten edition.

At the beginning of 1815, the ruler of the Kingdom of Naples was Ferdinand IV of Sicily, who had gained the throne through a series of agreements with Britain, while Murat was seen as a loyal supporter of the Austrians. This led to a swift transition of power without any conflict, and Murat was restored to the throne by the end of 1815.

Dr. Rajendra Prasad won the first presidential election of India, and K. T. Shah came in third place. In the first presidential election of China, Sun Yat-sen won first place, and Huang Hsing came in second. In the first presidential election of America, John Adams came in third place and served as the Vice President.

The school with the most victories in NCAA Division I men's college basketball is the University of North Carolina. Its team, the Tar Heels, has an all-time wins record of 2320. Alternatively, the coach with the most victories in NCAA Division I is Mike Krzyzewski, who achieved the record on November 15, 2011, with a 74–69 victory over Michigan State. He later became the first Division I men's basketball coach to reach 1,000 wins on January 25, 2015, when he defeated St. John's 77–68.

Gimli is a character from J.R.R. Tolkien's high-fantasy novel The Lord of the Rings that has appeared in different adaptations that the title has received. In 1978, Ralph Bakshi released an animated version where the character was voiced by Sean Astin. John Rhys-Davis portrayed the role in Peter Jackson's Lord of the Rings trilogy, widely regarded as one of the greatest and most influential film series ever made.

Brian Lara holds the record for most runs scored in test cricket in a career with 11,953. Graham Gooch holds the record for most runs scored in a match in test cricket with 456. Donald Bradman has the record for most runs in a series with 974. Mohammad Yousuf has the record for most runs in a calendar year with 1788 in 2006.

In various adaptations of Lewis Carroll's "Alice in Wonderland," the Queen of Hearts has been portrayed by different actresses. Notably, in Disney's 1951 animated version, the character was voiced by Ellen Burstyn, while Jayne Meadows and Miranda Richardson played the role in the 1985 and 1986 made-for-television adaptations, respectively. Helena Bonham Carter was cast for the role in the 2010 live-action feature directed by Tim Burton.

England has competed in the Rugby, Cricket and FIFA World Cups. The last time England made the semis in the FIFA World Cup was 2022. The last time they made the semis in the Cricket World Cup was 2019.

Back in Black is the seventh studio album by Australian rock band AC/DC. On 13 December 2007, the album was certified 12× multi-platinum by the RIAA, denoting 12 million American sales. The album has also sold one million or more copies in Canada, Germany and France. Worldwide, it went on to sell 30 million copies, leading "NME" journalist Mark Beaumont to call it "the biggest selling hard rock album ever made". In their native Australia, Back in Black sold 3× Platinum certified copies, and 600,000 in the UK.
Only George Harrison played guitar on the Beatles' song While My Guitar Gently Weeps, and he was the sole guitarist featured on the track, which was released on the band's 1994 compilation album.

Party leaders and whips of the United States House of Representatives, also known as floor leaders, are elected by their respective parties in a closed-door caucus by secret ballot. The top 5 leadership positions for the majority party in the House of Representatives are the Speaker, Majority Leader, Majority Whip, Assistant Speaker, and Conference Chairman. The top 5 leadership positions for the minority party on the House of Representatives are the Minority Leader, Minority Whip, Conference Chairperson, Committee Chairperson, and Caucus Secretary.

Die Brücke (The Bridge) was a group of German artists formed in Berlin in 1903, associated with the creation of surrealism. The group is sometimes compared to the roughly contemporary French group of the Impressionists, since both movements shared interests in detailed realism and in the expressing of subtle emotion through muted colors that were very often naturalistic.

The Carrie Diaries is an American teen comedy-drama television series that aired for two seasons on The CW from January 14, 2013, to January 31, 2014. Season 1 of the series is set in 1983, and Season 2 is set in 1986.
A larger sexually dimorphic nucleus of the medial preoptic area is the specific result of being a homosexual female.

The Football Association Challenge Cup, commonly known as the FA Cup, is a knockout competition in English football. The 2015 FA Cup final was played between Arsenal and Aston Villa (4–0 victory for Arsenal). In the 2016 FA Cup Final, Manchester United obtained a 2–1 victory (and their twelfth FA Cup) playing against Crystal Palace. The 2017 FA Cup final was between London rivals Tottenham Hotspur and Chelsea; with Chelsea winning 2–1 (a record 13th title).

Buzz Lightyear is a toy Space Ranger superhero and one of the two lead characters appearing throughout the Toy Story franchise. He is voiced by Tim Allen in the Toy Story films and the Buzz Lightyear movie, while Spanish Buzz Lightyear is voiced by Javier Fernandez-Pena. In 1996, a series of shorts known as Toy Story Treats were created with Tom Hanks voicing Buzz Lightyear. Finally, Patrick Warburton provides the voice for the TV series Buzz Lightyear of Star Command, an animated spin-off TV series.

Several actors have played Boss Hogg in The Dukes of Hazzard. Sorrell Booke did in the original Dukes of Hazzard TV show and The Dukes of Hazzard cartoon called The Dukes. Tom Hanks did in the 2005 movie The Dukes of Hazzard, while Chris McDonald did in the 2007 movie Dukes of Hazzard: The Beginning, a prequel to the 2005 movie. Wayne Powers did in The Dukes of Hazzard video game.

Mildred Meyerhauser is Jack Benny's brash, blonde bombshell date in The Jack Benny Program. In the show, she was played by different actresses, with Barbara Marie Nickerauer appearing as Mildred only once in the early 1960s, while Shirley Mitchell played her four times during the late 1950s.

The Honda Element is a compact crossover SUV manufactured by Honda and marketed in North America over a single generation for model years 2003-2010, noted for its boxy exterior styling with bi-parting side doors and its boxy, flexible interior layout. In 2004 a mid-range LX trim level was introduced between the DX and the premium EX trim levels, and for 2010, Honda Element offered LX, EX, EX with Navigation, and SC trim levels. 2010 was the last year for the Honda Element EX and LX.

The Pennsylvania Academy of the Fine Arts is a museum and private art school in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. It was founded in 1850 and is the first and oldest art museum and art school in the United States. The academy's museum is internationally known for its collections of 19th- and 20th-century American paintings, sculptures, and works on paper. Its archives house important materials for the study of American art history, museums, and art training and it offers a Bachelor of Fine Arts, Master of Fine Arts, certificate programs, and continuing education.

In Jason and the Argonauts, which is a 1963 Anglo-American independent mythological fantasy adventure film produced by Charles H. Schneer and directed by Don Chaffey, it was actually Sean Connery who played the role of Hercules. The 2000 American television miniseries, on the other hand, featured Brian Thompson in the same role.

The story of the TV series Wynonna Earp is supposed to take place in Ghost River Triangle, a cursed territory near the Canadian Shield, while filming for the series' first two seasons was supposed to take place elsewhere. Filming for the series' Season 1 took place in the city of Edmonton, Alberta, Canada. Didsbury, Alberta, was used as the setting for the series' small town of Purgatory. While filming for the series' Season 2 began in Edmonton and surrounding areas, Springbank, Alberta, was used for additional location shooting.

The Great Wall of China is the collective name of a series of fortification systems generally built across the historical northern borders of China to protect and consolidate territories of Chinese states and empires against various nomadic groups of the steppe and their polities. Several walls were being built from as early as the 5th century BC by ancient Chinese states. The most well-known sections of the wall were built by the Ming dynasty (1368-1640).

American singer-songwriter Bobby Caldwell sang the original What You Won't Do For Love, while English pop duo Go West sang a cover of it in 1993, and vocal group Boys II Men sang a cover of it in 2005. The song was released in October 1978 as the lead single from Caldwell's debut album.

My Friend Dahmer is a 2017 American biographical psychological drama film written and directed by Marc Meyers about American serial killer Jeffrey Dahmer. The film premiered at the 2017 Tribeca Film Festival on April 21, 2017, and was released in limited theaters on December 3, 2017, with a wider release the following month.

Harry and the Hendersons is an American sitcom based on the film of the same name, produced by Amblin Television for Universal Television. The series aired in syndication from January 13, 1991, to June 18, 1993, with 72 half-hour episodes produced. The series is about a family who adopts a Bigfoot called Harry. John Goodman played the role of Harry in both the film and TV series, until his death late in the production of the first season. He was replaced first by Dawan Scott and then by Brian Steele in the third season (Steele had filled in for Scott in the Harry costume for numerous scenes during season two, before taking over the role full-time). Harry's vocal effects were provided by Patrick Pinney where the vocal effects were previously provided by Fred Newman in the film.

In the My Little Pony franchise, the Earth ponies are ponies without a horn or wings. They were the first ponies to come out in 1985. They lack the ability to cast magic spells like unicorns or the ability to stand on clouds like the pegasi. Usually, they are the basic form of the My Little Pony toys. Both Flower Wishes and Daisyjo have white daisies in their cutie marks, but Flower Wishes has green leaves on her cutie mark as well.

The Houston Astros are an American professional baseball team based in Houston. The Astros compete in Major League Baseball (MLB) as a member club of the American League (AL) West division, having moved to the division in 2014 after spending their first 51 seasons in the National League (NL). On November 15, 2011, it was announced that Crane had agreed to move the franchise to the American League for the 2014 season. The move was part of an overall divisional realignment of MLB, with the National and American leagues each having 15 teams in three geographically balanced divisions. The Houston Astros won their first American League game on March 31, 2014 against the Texas Rangers. The score was 8–2.

Anagnorisis is a moment in a play or other work when a character makes a critical discovery. Anagnorisis originally meant recognition in its Greek context, specifically of a person and their relationship with the audience. Anagnorisis was the hero's sudden awareness of a real situation, the realization of things as they stood, and finally, the hero's insight into a relationship with an often supportive character in Aristotelian tragedy. Satori is a Japanese Buddhist term for awakening, comprehension, and understanding. It is derived from the Japanese verb satori. In the Zen Buddhist tradition, satori refers to a deep experience of kenshō, seeing into one's true nature. Satori and kenshō are commonly translated as ignorance, a word that is also used to translate bodhi, prajñā, and Buddhahood.

The Bibi Ka Maqbara is a tomb located in Aurangabad, Maharashtra, India. It was commissioned in 1655 by the Mughal emperor Aurangzeb in the memory of his second wife, Dilras Banu Begum. It bears a striking resemblance to the Taj Mahal, the mausoleum of Aurangzeb's mother, Mumtaz Mahal. Bibi Ka Maqbara is believed to have been built between 1675 and 1676 C.E. An inscription found on the main entrance door mentions that this mausoleum was designed and erected by Ata-ullah, an architect and Ram Singh, an engineer respectively.

The Ghana national U-20 football team, known as the Black Satellites, is considered to be the feeder team for the Ghana national football team. They are the former FIFA U-20 World Cup Champions and African Youth Champions. They won the 17th edition 2008 FIFA U-20 World Cup in the Cairo International Stadium, Cairo, Egypt on 16 October 2008, which was the first time an African country won the FIFA U-20 World Cup Championship.

The Cleveland Cavaliers is an American professional basketball team based in Cleveland that competes in the National Basketball Association and plays in regular season games and in playoff games if they qualify for a spot. The Cavaliers won 62 games during the regular 2015-2016 season, and their total winning games including the playoffs that season was 80. During the 2016-2017 season, the Cavaliers won 51 games during the regular season, not including the playoffs. Combining their total wins in the 2016-2017 regular season with their playoff wins that season puts their total at 64.

FedExField, home of the Washington Commanders, has the highest capacity of any NFL stadium with a peak capacity of over 90,000 spectators. MetLife Stadium, home of the New York Giants and New York Jets, has the highest listed seating capacity at 82,500 in the NFL.

"Love means never having to say you're sorry" is a catchphrase based on a line from the Erich Segal novel "Love Story" and was popularized by its 1975 film adaptation. The line is spoken twice in the film, once by Oliver Barrett to Jennifer Cavalleri in the middle of the film, and once by Jennifer to Oliver as the last line of the film. The line was so memorable that it was voted 5th in the AFI's list of the top 100 American movie quotes.
It is commonly believed the Declaration of Independence was officially signed on July 4, 1776, but historians have concluded it was actually signed on July 5, 1776.
The California Academy of Sciences was originally built in 1863. After damage done to the building during an earthquake it was repaired and rebuilt in 2009.

Father Knows Best is an American sitcom starring Robert Young, Jane Wyatt, Elinor Donahue, Billy Gray and Lauren Chapin. The series, which first began on radio in 1951, aired as a television show for six seasons with a total of 205 episodes. Created by Ed James, Father Knows Best follows the lives of the Andersons, a middle-class family living in the Midwestern town of Springfield. In both the television series and the radio series, Jane Wyatt plays the character of the father.

Pandora's box is an artifact in Greek mythology connected with the myth of Pandora in Homer’s Iliad. He reported that curiosity led her to open a container left in care of her husband, thus releasing physical and emotional curses upon mankind. Later depictions of the story have been varied, while some literary and artistic treatments have focused more on the contents than on Pandora herself. In 1904, the play Pandora's Box was written by Frank Wedekind and depicts a society "driven by the demands of lust and greed".

"Let them eat cake" originated in Italy and is the traditional translation of the Italian phrase "Che mangino la torta". This phrase is attributed to Marie Antoinette, prior to the Italian Renaissance, although she did not originate it, and she probably never said it.

A Stradivarius is one of the violins, violas, cellos and other string instruments built by members of the Italian family Stradivari, during the 19th and 20th centuries, particularly Antonio Stradivari. The woods used included cedar for the top, oak for the internal blocks and linings, and mahogany for the back, ribs, and neck.
The first season of Big Brother Naija first aired from 5 March to 4 June 2006 and the winner was Katung Aduwak. Tobi Bakre won season 2 of the show, which aired in 2018, 12 years after season 1.

The 8th state of the United States with respect to order of admission to the Union is Virginia. Virginia, which was originally known as the Colony of Virginia, was a colony of Great Britain that existed in North America from 1607 to 1776 and was one of the five Southern colonies and one of the thirteen American colonies. The 8th state of the United States in alphabetical order is Delaware. Delaware is a state in the Mid-Atlantic region of the United States, bordering Maryland to its south and west; Pennsylvania to its north; and New Jersey and the Atlantic Ocean to its east, and takes its name from the nearby Delaware River named after Thomas West, 3rd Baron De La Warr, an English nobleman and Virginia's first colonial governor.

Executive Order 9066 was a United States presidential executive order signed by United States president Franklin D. Roosevelt on March 19, 1942. The order authorized the secretary of war to prescribe certain areas as military zones, allowing for the incarceration of German Americans during the war.

Native Americans, from the Great Plains, migrated to Kansas in the early twentieth century. They were called Trailblazers and are part of the Trailblazer Movement or Exodus of 1905. Causes were to find new agricultural opportunities and escape economic hardships in their original territories.

The National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) Division I Men's Basketball Tournament is a single-elimination tournament for men's college basketball teams in the United States. The 2014-2015 Men's Championship game was won by Kentucky. The 2015-2016 Men's Championship game was won by Villanova. The 2016-2017 Men's Championship game was won by North Carolina.
Diana in the movie Lights Out is portrayed as an adult by Emma Stone and as a child by Ava Cantrell.

The AFC Championship Game is the annual championship game of the American Football Conference (AFC) and one of the two semi-final playoff games of the National Football League (NFL). The game was established as part of the 1970 merger between the NFL and the American Football League (AFL), with the merged league realigning into two conferences. The 2016 and 2017 winner was the Pittsburgh Steelers. The 2015 winner was the Denver Broncos.

In the 2016 British horror-mystery film The Limehouse Golem, the titular serial killer Golem is revealed to be music-hall star Lucy Hart. Hart is portrayed by Olivia Cooke in adulthood and Amelia Crouch in childhood.
In the 2004 American documentary Super Size Me, independent filmmaker Morgan Spurlock ate only McDonald's food from February 1 to March 2, 2004 and recorded his experience.

The next episode of The Handmaid's Tale is scheduled to air on June 14, 2023, as the series has been renewed for a sixth season. The first season began with the initial three episodes released on April 26, 2017, and the 8th episode aired on May 31, 2017.

The Flash has various seasons. The third season premiered on The CW on October 4, 2017. The fourth season premiered on The CW on October 10, 2018 while the fifth season premiered on The CW on October 9, 2019.

In the 2004 World Series, the Boston Red Sox beat the Chicago Cubs in four games, pitched by Julián Tavárez, Matt Morris, Jeff Suppan, and Jason Marquis respectively. At the time, the Cubs were managed by Joe Maddon. The Red Sox and Cubs faced each other again in the 2013 World Series, which the Red Sox also won, this time four games to two.

The date the University of Chicago withdrew from the Big Ten and the date their resignation was accepted are two different dates. On March 7, 1948 the University of Chicago withdrew from the Big Ten Conference. On May 31, 1946 the resignation was formally accepted by the Big Ten Conference.
In a deck of Phase 10, there are 80 numbered cards, with 20 numbered cards of each color. There are 10 wild cards and 2 skip cards.

The water supply for the City of Chicago comes from the Chicago River. Water is collected from the river and stored in onshore reservoirs, before being transported through above-ground pipelines that lead from the reservoirs to pumping stations located throughout the city. The water then travels to water purification plants where it is treated before being pumped to all parts of the city as well as 100 suburbs.

Tow Mater is one of the main characters in "Cars" and its sequels, "Cars 2" and "Cars 3" as well as "Cars Toons". His design was inspired by a 1956–57 International Harvester Tow truck and 1955-1957 Chevrolet Task Force. He is voiced by Adam Sandler on all three movies, but Adam Burton took over the role for the 2017 Cars 3 video game.

In the 2012 adaptation directed by Tom Hooper, Emma Watson plays young Eponine, while Samantha Barks portrays the character in her teens. In the 1998 version starring Liam Neeson, Geoffrey Rush, Uma Thurman, and Claire Danes, the character is played by Sylvie Koblizkova, while Agathe Ladner and Candice Patou play the child and teen versions of the character in the 1982 French drama film directed by Robert Hossein.

The term US Open can be applied to several sports or games, including tennis and golf. The 2015 tennis US Open aired on tv from August 31 – September 13, the 2016 tennis US Open aired on tv from August 29 – September 11, while the 2017 tennis US Open was from August 28 – September 9. The 2015 golf US Open played June 18–21, 2015, the 2016 golf US Open held June 16–19, while the 2017 golf US Open held June 15–18.

During the 2018 Arizona gubernatorial election, the Democrat nominee was Fred DuVal, who lost by a margin of 14% to the Republican candidate, Doug Ducey. Previously, in 2014, David Garcia, the former chairman of the Arizona Board of Regents, won the Democratic nomination unopposed. In 2010, Terry Goddard, the Arizona Attorney General, won the Democrat nomination but later lost by 54% to 42% against Jan Brewer. In 2006, Janet Napolitano was the Democratic Party's candidate for the election.

The Alabama Crimson Tide and the Auburn Tigers maintain a deeply-rooted rivalry across several college sports. Auburn has the most victories in both football and basketball, with 47 Iron Bowl wins to Alabama's 37 and 99 basketball wins to Alabama's 65.

George Washington was the first to build a temporary funicular railway, while Benjamin Franklin was the first to build a short temporary 100 yards (91 m) railroad test track in the yard of the Liberty Bell Tavern in Philadelphia. Benjamin Franklin was the first American to construct a permanent working railway in the United States.

"I'll Take Care of You" is a song written by Brook Benton and originally recorded by Bobby Bland in 1965. In 1989, Gary B. B. Coleman included the song in his album "One Night Stand". Also in 2011, Joe Bonamassa and Beth Hart covered the song in their collaborative album, "Don't Explain". In 2012, Rebecca Ferguson covered the song on her Heaven Tour, and then the live version of the song was included in the re-release of her debut album, "Heaven". Miley Cyrus covered the song on her Bangerz Tour in 2014. Two years later, when Cyrus became a coach on the reality television singing competition program "The Voice", she gave an arrangement of Bonamassa and Hart's version of the song to Team Miley members Aaron Gibson and Sa'Rayah to cover for a Battle round on the October 24, 2014, episode.

Agent Orange was primarily decided upon by President Richard Nixon in early 1962, who recognized the need for aerial herbicide spraying in Vietnam after consulting with military advisors about its effectiveness during World War II. This decision came well before any requests were made by South Vietnamese leaders.

The alcohol laws of the United States regarding minimum age for purchase have changed over time. The first time Michigan raised the drinking age to 21 was in 1933. The 2nd time it was raised to 21 was on June 15, 1978.

The White Out is a tradition at the University of Michigan during select Wolverines home football games, where all spectators come dressed in white. The White Out was started by the cheerleading squad.

Wentworth is an Australian television drama series that was first broadcast on SoHo on 1 May 2013, with the first part of its eighth and final season premiering on July 28, 2020, and the second half premiering July 2021. Episode 58 came out on 20 June 2018. Episode 57 came out on 13 June 2018. Episode 56 came out on 6 June 2018.

Truist Financial Corporation is an American bank holding company headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia. The company was formed in January 2020 as the result of the merger of BB&T, which stands for Branch Banking and Trust Company, and SunTrust Banks.

Once Upon a Time is an American fantasy adventure drama television series that aired for seven seasons on ABC from October 23, 2011 to May 18, 2018. Emma Swan is a fictional character in ABC's television series Once Upon a Time. She wins the final battle by picking up her sword. According to the storybook, only good wins the final battle.

Kobe Bryant holds the record for the most technical fouls received during one season in the NBA. In the 2000–01 season, he received 41 technical fouls in 80 regular season and postseason games played. Bryant also holds the all-time mark for most technical fouls by a player in a career with 317, a record previously held by Dennis Rodman.
