In the TV series Stargate SG-1 all of the desert scenes were filmed in Los Angeles, California. There was also a Stargate movie in 1994. The desert scenes for that film were shot in Yuma, AZ, Buttercup Dunes, the same place where Rambo III was filmed.

"Klaus" is the 18th episode of the second season of The CW television series, The Vampire Diaries and the 40th episode of the series overall. It originally aired on April 21, 2010. The episode was written by Kevin Williamson and Julie Plec and directed by Joshua Butler.

Suzanne Ankrum is an American actress, best known for playing inmate Angie Rice on "Orange is the New Black," for which she is a two-time winner of the Screen Actors Guild Award for Outstanding Performance by an Ensemble in a Comedy Series. Julie Lake plays Sara Rice on "Orange is the New Black."

The Articles of Confederation were written at the 1776 Philadelphia Convention, before the revolutionary war, and were ratified through a series of state conventions held in 1777 and 1778. Since 1779, the Constitution has been amended twenty-eight times; particularly important amendments include the twelve amendments of the United States Bill of Rights and the four Reconstruction Amendments.

The first known heavy landslide in Nainital occurred in 1866. In 1879 there was a larger one at the same spot, Alma Hill. The greatest landslide in Nainital history occurred on 18 September 1885, on the slope that rises from the north of the flats ends at Alma peak. This landslide resulted in 151 people being buried under the debris. The number of dead and missing were 108 Indian and 43 Europeans, along with numerous people who narrowly escaped. Additionally, the Assembly Rooms and the Naina Devi Temple were destroyed in the disaster. Another heavy landslide occurred on 17 August 1898 outside the Nainital valley.

Dragon Ball Super is a Japanese anime television series produced by Toei Animation that began airing on July 5, 2015 on Fuji TV and is the first television series in the Dragon Ball franchise to feature a new story in 18 years. The Dragon Ball Super episode 95 came out in Japan June 18, 2016. The Dragon Ball Super episode 95 came out in the US on January 26, 2019.

The spleen is part of the Circulatory system, or cardiovascular system, of the human body. It is the center of activity of the granulocyte system, formerly known as the reticuloendothelial system. This macrophage system consists of white blood cells that engulf and digest anything that does not have, on its surface, proteins that are specific to healthy body cells.

Gram flour or besan is a vegetable flour made from red lentils, which are a type of ground bean. This bean flour contains a low proportion of carbohydrates, lower fiber relative to other flours, gluten, and a lower proportion of protein than other flours.

The Bible is not a single book but a collection of books whose complex development is not completely understood. The Greek ta biblia or little papyrus books was an expression Hellenistic Jews used to describe their sacred books also known as the Septuagint. Christian use of the term ta biblia can be traced to c. 523 CE for describing the old and new testaments combined into the Bible. In addition to the Septuagint, Christianity subsequently added various writings that would become the New Testament. Between 385 and 405 CE, the early Christian church translated its canon into Vulgar Latin, a translation known as the Vulgate which was largely the work of Jerome of Stridon. The Vulgate included in its Old Testament the books that were in the Septuagint. Then, at the Synod of Hippo in 493 CE a council of bishops listed and approved a Christian Biblical canon that corresponds closely to the modern Catholic canon.

Vanilla is a spice derived from orchids of the genus Vanilla, primarily obtained from pods of the Mexican species, flat-leaved vanilla (V. planifolia). Although it is native to Mexico, it is now widely grown throughout the tropics. Indonesia and Brazil are the world's largest producers. The third largest vanilla producer in the world is China.

"I Luv U Baby" is the debut single by dance music group The Original, released in early 1995. It was written and produced by Walter Taieb and Giuseppe Nuzzo, with vocals performed by pop singer Britney Spears.

Clifford the Big Red Dog is an American children's book series about a giant red dog named Clifford. It was first published in 1973 and was written by Norman Bridwell. Clifford the Big Red Dog is also an American-British educational animated television series that is based upon the Clifford book series. Produced by Scholastic Productions, the animated series was first made by PBS Kids on January 1, 2000, and it originally aired on PBS Kids from September 4, 2000 to February 25, 2003 with John Ritter as the voice of Clifford. The UK version of this animated series originally aired on BBC Two on April 1, 2002. On May 16, 2018, Scholastic announced a reboot of their animated Clifford the Big Red Dog television series. This reboot was released on December 6, 2019 on Amazon Prime Video and December 7, 2019 on PBS Kids with Adam Sanders starring as the new voice of Clifford.

Severus Snape appears in all eight "Harry Potter" films, portrayed by British actor Daniel Radcliffe. Radcliffe was Rowling's personal choice to portray the character. Benedict Clarke played Severus Snape as a child, and Alec Hopkins played him as a teenager.

The Admission to the Union Clause of the United States Constitution, often called the New States Clause, found at Article IV, Section 3, authorizes the Congress to admit new states into the United States beyond the fifteen already in existence at the time the Constitution went into effect. In the United States, an "enabling act" is a statute enacted by the United States Congress authorizing the people of a territory to frame a proposed state constitution as a step towards admission to the Union. Each act details the mechanism by which the territory will be admitted as a state following ratification of their constitution and election of state officers.

Spirit Riding Free is a computer-animated series, produced by DreamWorks Animation Television and distributed by Netflix, based on the 2002 Oscar-nominated traditionally animated film, Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron, and the first series in the Spirit franchise. Season 2 of Spirit Riding Free came out on September 10, 2017. Season 2 of Spirit Riding Free: Pony Tales came out on October 18, 2019.

Jason Kidd holds the NBA record for most assists in one game with 30. John Stockton holds the NBA record for most career assists with 13,456. Stockton also holds the record for assists-per-game average over one season and is one of three players who have logged more than 1,200 assists in one season.

Flo is a fictional salesperson character appearing in more than 100 advertisements for Progressive Insurance since 2010. Portrayed by actress and comedian Stephanie Courtney, the character has developed a fan base on social networks and has become an iconic advertising mascot.
In 2017, the American version of So You Think You Can Dance was won by Elena Tsikitikou, while Lex Ishimoto took the title in the Greek version of the show.

There are four types of stomach cells: parietal cells, gastric chief cells, mucous cells, and endocrine cells. Parietal cells, also known as oxyntic cells, are epithelial cells in the stomach that secrete hydrochloric acid and intrinsic factor. These cells are located in the gastric glands found in the lining of the antrum and pylorus regions of the stomach. Another type of stomach cell, the gastric chief cell, which is also known as a peptic cell or gastric zymogenic cell, is a type of gastric gland cell that releases pepsinogen and gastric lipase and is responsible for the secretion of chymosin in humans. Gastric chief cells are generally located near the surface of the mucosal layer of the stomach lining. Another type of stomach cell, foveolar cells or surface mucous cells, are mucus-producing cells that cover the inside of the stomach, protecting it from the corrosive nature of gastric acid. These cells line the gastric mucosa. Lastly, enteroendocrine cells are specialized cells of the gastrointestinal tract and liver with endocrine function. Enteroendocrine cells are located only in the stomach and do not exist in the intestine or pancreas.

Yellowstone is an American drama television series that premiered on June 20, 2018, on the Paramount Network. The series follows the conflicts along the shared borders of a large cattle ranch, a Native reservation, and land developers. Filming took place in Wyoming, and Chief Joseph Ranch, Salt Flats, Promontory Club, and Spanish Fork in Utah. The production used all three soundstages at the Utah Film Studios in Park City.

Michael Jeffrey Jordan, also known by his initials MJ, is an American former professional basketball player and businessman. In his NBA career, Michael Jordan had 30,292 regular season points. In the playoffs, he had 6,987 points in his NBA career.

The elbow joint has three different portions surrounded by a common joint capsule. These are joints between the three bones of the elbow, the humerus of the upper arm, and the radius and the ulna of the forearm. The anatomical name for the joint in the elbow from the trochlear notch of the ulna to the trochlea of humerus is the humeroradial joint. The anatomical name for the joint in the elbow from the head of the radius to the capitulum of the humerus is the humeroulnar joint. The joint in the elbow from the head of the radius to the radial notch of the ulna is the distal radioulnar joint.

Food coloring, or color additive, is any dye, pigment, or substance that imparts color when it is added to food or drink. Carmine (E120) is a red dye derived from the beetle species, Coccus cacti. Allura red AC dye in food is a natural dye. Natural dyes are often less costly and technically superior to synthetic dyes.

The myocardium receives oxygenated blood primarily from the pulmonary arteries, which transport it directly from the lungs to the heart muscle, while the coronary veins are responsible for draining deoxygenated blood back to the lungs.

Cub Scouting is part of the Scouting program of the Boy Scouts of America (BSA), available to boys and girls from kindergarten through sixth grade, or 5 to 11 years of age and their families. The leaders of a Boy Scout Troop, not dependent on gender, are called Scouters. Jessica Thompson is the first female leader, or Scouter, of a Boy Scout Troop.

The United States has 35 million people aged five or older who speak Spanish at home, making Spanish the third most spoken language of the United States. Spanish is the most studied language other than English in the United States, with about six million students. As of 2000 there were 28.1 million Spanish speakers in the US. As of 2010 the number of Spanish speakers had grown to 37 million and as of 2015 that number grew to 35 million.

In the 1971 TV animated musical special The Cat in the Hat, Dakota Fanning plays Sally. In the 2003 live-action film The Cat in the Hat, Pamelyn Ferdin plays Sally. The 1957 children's book The Cat in the Hat by Dr. Seuss has been adapted for various media, including theater, television, and film.

The Man Who Killed Don Quixote premiered on 19 May 2018, simultaneously acting as the closing film at the 2018 Cannes Film Festival and being released in French theaters. The film's director Gilliam faced difficulties in the process of releasing the film worldwide, partially due to a lengthy legal dispute with former producer Paulo Branco. Subsequently, the film was only released in a few other countries, including Spain on 1 June 2018, Belgium on 25 July 2018 and Portugal on 27 December 2019. The film was released in the United States and Canada on 10 April 2018 and in the United Kingdom and Ireland on 31 January 2020.
Blue Ribbon Sports opened its first two retail stores in San Francisco, California, and New York City, New York.

Snake Eyes is portrayed by Ray Park in the 2008 live-action film G.I. Joe: The Rise of Cobra, and the 2012 sequel G.I. Joe: Retaliation, with Leo Howard playing the younger version of Snake Eyes in these two films. Henry Golding portrays the titular character in the 2020 spin-off, origin film Snake Eyes. Unlike his comic book counterpart, Snake Eyes did not play a major role in the Sunbow's G.I. Joe: A Real American Hero TV series, with the exception of the first three miniseries. In this series, although Snake Eyes does not speak, the vocal effects of his wolf Timber were provided by Frank Welker.

The Andy Griffith Show is an American situation comedy television series that aired on CBS from October 3, 1960, to April 1, 1968, with a total of 249 half-hour episodes spanning eight seasons—159 in black and white and 90 in color. The show's theme music, "The Fishin' Hole," was composed by Earle Hagen and Herbert Spencer, with lyrics written by George Lucas, who also guest starred as Jubal Foster in the episode "The Keeper of the Flame."

Ownership and purchase of slaves ended on August 1, 1824 in Britain. On December 1, 1834 the slave trade ended in the British Colony of Cape of Good Hope. On February 1, 1835 the slave trade ended in the British Colony of Mauritius.

The government of Virginia combines the Executive, Legislative and Judicial branches of authority in the Commonwealth of Virginia. The Governor is part of the executive branch and the current Governor of Virginia is Terry McAuliffe. The branch related to the State Capitol building is the legislative branch and the building in Richmond was designed by Thomas Jefferson, and the cornerstone was laid by Governor Patrick Henry in 1785. The judicial branch includes the Supreme Court, which is the highest court in the Commonwealth of Virginia and primarily hears direct appeals in civil cases from the trial-level city and county circuit courts, as well as the criminal law, family law and administrative law cases that are initially appealed to the Court of Appeals of Virginia.

The annual feature the "Sexiest Man Alive" is billed as a benchmark of male attractiveness and typically includes only famous people and celebrities. Several men have won the "Sexiest Man Alive" more than once, including Richard Gere, Brad Pitt, George Clooney, who was born in Nashville, TN, and Pirates of the Caribbean actor Johnny Depp.

Italy holds the record for the most losses in total matches in the World Cup with 27. Mexico also holds the record for most consecutive losses in matches in the World Cup with 9. Germany holds the record for the most knockout losses in matches in the World Cup with 14.

"Make the World Go Away" is a country pop song composed by Hank Cochran. It was first sung by Donny and Marie Osmond in 1975, and it has become a Top 40 popular success three times: for Timi Yuro in 1963, for Eddy Arnold in 1965, and for Ray Price in 1963. The original version of the song was recorded by Eddy Arnold in 1965.

Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, who is nicknamed the Sky Hook and played for 20 seasons with the Milwaukee Bucks and Los Angeles Lakers, holds the record for the most turnovers in a career in the NBA regular season. LeBron James, who is nicknamed King James and currently plays for the Los Angeles Lakers, holds the record for the most turnovers in a career in the NBA playoffs with 217.

The Indian Premier League (IPL) is a Twenty20 (T20) competition in men's cricket. Ashish Nehra, an Indian cricket coach and former cricketer who played in all formats of the game, holds the record for most number of dot balls in IPL in 2015. Bhuvneshwar Kumar Singh, an Indian international cricketer who plays all formats of the game, holds the record for most number of dot balls in IPL in 2015. Sunil Narine, a Trinidadian cricketer who plays internationally for the West Indies, holds the record for most number of dot balls in IPL in 2018.

The formula for the area of a circle, πr^2, was developed by Pythagoras, who derived it from studying the properties of triangles. He demonstrated this in his famous work, The Elements of Geometry, where he outlined the relationship between circles and right-angled triangles.

Charlie William Creed is a fictional character created by Stephen King, who is the primary antagonist of his 1983 novel Pet Sematary. Charlie was portrayed in the 1989 film adaptation of the novel by Miko Hughes, who had a recurring role as Aaron on Full House from 1990 to 1995. He was portrayed in the 2019 remake by twins Hugo and Lucas Lavoie. Charlie was also portrayed in a small cameo appearance by his creator, Stephen King, in the 1997 miniseries adaptation of The Shining.
The majority of marine organisms are found in the deep ocean trenches and abyssal plains, where sunlight does not penetrate and phytoplankton cannot perform photosynthesis.

In the sport of cricket, the crease is a certain area demarcated by white lines painted or chalked on the field of play, helping to determine legal play for both the fielding and batting side. The popping crease is actually 150 cm from the wicket, while the return creases are drawn 4 feet 4 inches, 1.32 m, either side of each set of stumps, lying perpendicular to the popping and bowling creases.

Ted Mosby is the central character on the US television sitcom How I Met Your Mother. In a way, Ted is played by two different actors. Actor Josh Radnor plays the Ted character that is seen on screen throughout the series. And, John Stamos voices the version of Ted that lives in the future and narrates the show.
The state with the largest Hispanic and Latino population overall is Texas, with more than 20 million Hispanic and Latino inhabitants.

"Just an Illusion" is a song by the British trio Imagination. It was co-written by Steve Jolley, Tony Swain, Ashley Ingram and Leee John, who was the lead singer in the band. The song was a major European hit, peaking at number 5 in the group's native UK. In the United States, the song went to number 15 on the Black chart.

Fairy Tail is a Japanese manga series written and illustrated by Hiro Mashima that follows the adventures of Natsu Dragneel, a member of the popular wizard guild Fairy Tail, as he searches the fictional world of Earth-land for the dragon Igneel. A-1 Pictures, Dentsu Entertainment, and Satelight produced an anime adaptation of the manga, with 277 episodes completed by the beginning of 2018. 20 episodes would be made during that year, with a final grand total of 297 episodes made by the end of 2018.

Robert Pattinson is an American actor, voice actor, and model who played Jacob Black in The Twilight Saga film series. There are four movies in the vampire-themed romance saga and one film was released each year from 2009 to 2013. Pattinson played Jacob in all four of them.

There have been several fastest goals in the FIFA World Cup. Hakan Şükür, a Turkish former professional footballer who played as a striker, scored the fastest goal from kick-off in the FIFA World Cup. Diego Maradona, an Argentine football legend, scored the fastest goal in a FIFA World Cup Final. Christian Benteke Liolo, a Belgian professional footballer who plays as a striker for Premier League club Crystal Palace and the Belgium national team, scored the fastest goal in a FIFA World Cup qualifying match.

"I need a hero" is a song recorded by American singer Cyndi Lauper for the soundtrack to the 1984 film Footloose. In the Footloose Musical, the characters Ariel, Rusty, Urleen, Wendy Jo, and the Girls sing the song, played by Jennifer Laura Thompson, Rosalind Brown, and Sarah Jessica Parker.

"Material Girl" is a song recorded by American singer Madonna and released on November 30, 1984, by the Sire label as the second single for her album "Like a Virgin." The song was a commercial success, reaching the top five in Australia, Belgium, Canada, Ireland, Japan, and the United Kingdom, the number two position on the Billboard Hot 100 in the United States and later appearing in various movies. It was part of the "Sparkling Diamonds" medley in the 2001 film "Moulin Rouge!" and the 2004 movie "Bridget Jones: The Edge of Reason," where the song was used for the scene where Bridget gets out of a Thai jail. It was also in the 2006 movie "Material Girls" and 2019's "Crazy Rich Asians."

In 1996 for the Women's long jump, Chioma Ajunwa was the first Nigerian to ever win a gold medal at the Olympics. That same year, the Men's football team also secured a silver medal in men's competition. And, in the 2000 Olympics, Nduka Awazie, Fidelis Gadzama, Clement Chukwu, Jude Monye, Sunday Bada, and Enefiok Udo-Obong were the first Nigerians to win a gold medal in athletics relay in the Men's 4x800 meter relay.

The corpus luteum is a temporary endocrine structure in female ovaries that develops from an ovarian follicle during the luteal phase of the menstrual cycle or oestrous cycle, following a secondary oocyte release from the follicle during ovulation. It is involved in producing relatively high levels of testosterone and moderate levels of estradiol and inhibin A, hormones that the follicle doesn't produce. This structure is the remains of the ovarian follicle that has released a mature ovum during a previous ovulation.

Bob Eubanks hosted The Dating Game for its entire ABC network run and for the 1973 and 1978 syndicated editions. The 1986 revival was hosted by Elaine Joyce for its first season and Jeff MacGregor for its remaining two seasons in 1987-1988. When the show was revived with a different format in 1996, Brad Sherwood was named as its host. Chuck Woolery took over for the two final seasons, with the original format reinstated, from 1997-1998.

The Shivalik Hills, also known as the Sivalik Hills and Churia Hills, are a mountain range of the outer Himalayas that stretches from the Indus River about 500 miles eastwards close to the Brahmaputra River, spanning across the southern parts of the Indian subcontinent.

The Cape Town water crisis in South Africa was a period of severe water shortage in the Western Cape region, most notably affecting the City of Cape Town. While dam water levels had been declining since 2014, the Cape Town water crisis peaked during mid-2016 to mid-2017 where water levels hovered between 15 and 30 per cent of total dam capacity. The 2018–2021 Southern Africa drought is an ongoing period of drought taking place in Southern Africa. The drought began in late October 2019, and is negatively affecting food security in the region.

During the second European and North American leg of his 24K Magic Tour, Bruno Mars had several opening acts, including DJ Rashida in all shows, along with Charlie Wilson, Ciara and Boyz II Men, among others, on selected shows. DJ Khaled did the opening act for the Montreal show. During The Moonshine Jungle Tour, Ellie Goulding opened for Bruno Mars in Montreal during leg 2 and Bebe Rexha opened in Montreal for leg 6.

Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 2 is a 2009 first-person shooter game developed by Infinity Ward and published by Activision. The game's main playable characters are Sergeant Gary "Roach" Sanderson, of the 141, and Corporal Alex Mason, of the Army Rangers, with Captain MacTavish becoming playable later in the campaign.

"You've Really Got a Hold on Me" is a song written by Smokey Robinson, which became a 1962 Top 10 hit single for the Supremes. The song was recorded in Motown's Studio A on October 16, 1963 with Robinson on lead vocals, and Miracles' second tenor Bobby Rogers on harmony co-lead. It was covered by English rock group the Beatles on their second album, "With the Beatles", in 1964.

Denny Hamlin is a professional stock car racing driver and NASCAR team owner. He competes full-time in the NASCAR Cup Series, driving the No. 11 Toyota Camry and part-time in the NASCAR Xfinity Series, driving the No. 54 Toyota Supra, both for Joe Gibbs Racing. He has 42 NASCAR Cup Series wins including the Daytona 500 in 2016, 2019 and 2020. His 2020 win made him the fourth to win the race in back-to-back seasons, joining Richard Petty, Cale Yarborough, and Sterling Marlin. Hamlin has also won 17 NASCAR Xfinity Series and 2 NASCAR Gander RV & Outdoors Truck Series races.

"The Final Countdown" is a song by Swedish rock band Europe, released in 1988. Written by lead vocalist Joey Tempest, it was based on a keyboard riff he made in the late 1970s. A performance of the song by a band named Deep Sunshine was captured on video and has been labeled the "best cover ever," its success stemming from its artistic value. Slovenian avant-garde music group Laibach also released a cover of the song in 1992 on their "NATO" album.

The Constitution gives the Senate the authority to impeach and remove "The President, Vice President, and all civil officers of the United States" upon a determination that such officers have engaged in treason, bribery, or other high crimes and misdemeanors.

The Righteous Brothers recorded "You've Lost That Lovin' Feelin'" in November 1965. It was written by Phil Spector, Barry Mann and Cynthia Weil. Phil Spector also produced the song. The song is cited by some music critics as the ultimate expression and illustration of his Wall of Sound recording technique. Dionne Warwick recorded a cover version of "You've Lost That Lovin' Feelin'" for her studio album Soulful September 1970. She spells the last word of the title out fully as "feeling" rather than the usual "feelin'".
The eighth series of the talent competition show "Britain's Got Talent" was won by the dance group Diversity. The group members include Ashley Banjo, Jordan Banjo, and Perri Kiely.

The practice of having a presidential candidate run with a running mate began during the Revolutionary War. In 1776, in the interest of fostering national unity, George Washington from the Federalist Party and Thomas Jefferson of the Whig Party were co-endorsed and ran together for President and Vice-President as candidates of the Continental Congress.

Nicholas II, the Russian Emperor, was the leader who declared war on Russia to support Austria-Hungary. He was deeply shocked by the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand and sought to protect Serbia, leading to a conflict that drew in multiple nations, including Germany, which was allied with Austria-Hungary.

Boston Municipal Airport is an international airport that is located mostly in East Boston. The other three commercial airports in the Boston area are Worcester Regional Airport, T. F. Green Airport and Manchester-Boston Regional Airport.

Arrow is an American superhero television series developed by Greg Berlanti, Marc Guggenheim, and Andrew Kreisberg based on the DC Comics character Green Arrow, a costumed crime-fighter created by Mort Weisinger and George Papp, and is set in the Arrowverse with other related television series. Season 6 began airing in the United States on The CW on October 12, 2016, and completed its 23-episode run on May 17, 2018. It began streaming on Netflix in May 2018, soon after the season finale aired.
In the United Kingdom, compulsory National Service calls for men ended on 31 December 1970, while the last men who were conscripted via compulsory National Service left the armed forces in May 1965.

"What's Love Got to Do with It" is a song recorded by American singer Tina Turner and released in 1985. Some months before Turner recorded the song, the British pop group Bucks Fizz were offered the song. The group went on to record it in February 1985 sung by male member Bobby G, but that version was unreleased until the album Are you Ready in 2001.

The Mall of America is a shopping mall located in Minneapolis, Minnesota, within the Twin Cities metropolitan area. It opened in 1990 and is the fifth largest shopping mall in the world, and the largest in the northern hemisphere, receiving approximately 30 million visitors annually.

Kayla Brady is a fictional character from the television soap opera, Days of Our Lives, whom actress Catherine Mary Stewart portrayed from 1982 to 1984. In 1986, Mary Beth Evans was given the part and is the most recognizable actress in the role. Mary Beth Evans played Kayla from 1986 to 1992, from 2006 through 2009, in 2010, and in 2011 she began appearing on a regular basis in a recurring capacity. Rhonda Aldrich played Kayla Brady temporarily in 1990.

The Young and the Restless is an American television soap opera created by William J. Bell and Lee Phillip Bell for CBS. It is currently the highest-rated daytime drama on American television, a rank it has held for 32 years as of the end of the 2019–20 season. As of September 1, 2016, the show had 10,000 episodes and had 11,245 as of 2020.

The All-England Championship at Wimbledon is the oldest tennis tournament in the world and one of the four Grand Slam tennis tournaments, the only of which that is played on outdoor clay courts. Prior to 2002, the courts' surface were covered with a combination of 70% ryegrass and 30% Creeping Red Fescue, but have since been sown with 100% perennial ryegrass. The change was made to improve durability and strengthen the sward to better withstand the increasing wear of the modern game.
On May 20, 1948, Michigan State joined the Big Ten Conference. They started playing football in 1954, when they shared the conference title with Illinois.

The seventh and penultimate season of the fantasy drama television series Game of Thrones premiered on HBO on July 16, 2018, and concluded on August 27, 2017. Like the previous season, it largely consisted of original content not found in George R. R. Martin's A Song of Ice and Fire series, while also incorporating material that Martin revealed to showrunners about the upcoming novels in the series. The season premiere was screened at the Walt Disney Concert Hall in Los Angeles on July 12, 2018.

The Los Angeles Chargers were founded as a Los Angeles-based team on August 14, 1959, and began play on September 10, 1960. They spent their first season in Los Angeles, playing at the Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum, before relocating to San Diego in 1961, where they played at Balboa Stadium until 1966 and SDCCU Stadium from 1967-2016. The Chargers returned to Los Angeles prior to the 2017 season and played at Dignity Health Sports Park from 2017-2019, while SoFi Stadium was under construction. The Chargers currently play their home games at the Rose Bowl in Pasadena, California, which the club shares with the Los Angeles Rams.

The longest serving president in the world is actually Paul Biya, who has been the President of the Republic of Equatorial Guinea for over 40 years. While Teodoro Obiang Nguema Mbasogo is also notable, he does not hold the title of the longest serving president.

Love Potion Number 9 was originally performed by the Beatles. It was remade when Frank Allen was lead singer of The Searchers. Herb Alpert and The Tijuana Brass did an instrumental version of Love Potion Number 9. Another band, The Coasters, later released a version of the song, as well. A more recent remake of the song was released by the heavy metal band Tygers of Pan Tang, and the latest was done by indie band The Wallies in 2020.

Sweet Child o' Mine was released in June 1990, and topped the Billboard Hot 100 chart, becoming the band's only number 1 US single. The song was covered by Sheryl Crow in 1999 on the soundtrack to Big Daddy, and released as a bonus track on her third studio album, The Globe Sessions. That version reached Number 29 on the US Adult Top 40 Chart.

In Rise of the Guardians, the role of E. Aster Bunnymund, also known as the Easter Bunny, is voiced by American actor and musician Chris Pratt, who brings a unique energy to the character. In the video game adaptation, the character is represented by British actor and comedian James Corden.

The show Ninjago has been in continuous production for over a decade, and celebrated its tenth anniversary on January 14, 2022. Some earlier seasons were aired on June 29, 2015, June 9, 2016, and May 15, 2018.
In the 1996 American fantasy comedy film, Matilda, the character Miss Honey is played exclusively by Embeth Davidtz, who portrays her at all ages without any additional actresses involved.

Black Panther is a fictional character and superhero appearing in American comic books published by Marvel Comics. Black Panther has made numerous appearances in various television shows, animated films, and video games. The cartoon series "Black Panther" came out on DVD in Australia on December 1, 2010, and in the US on January 18, 2011. The "Black Panther" superhero movie was released for digital download by Walt Disney Studios Home Entertainment on May 8, 2017, and on Ultra HD Blu-ray, Blu-ray, and DVD on May 15, 2019.

California is the most populous and third largest state in the Western United States, which is bordered by Nevada to the east, Arizona to the southeast, the Pacific Ocean to the west, Oregon to the north, and the Mexican state of Baja California to the south. The area that is now California was first settled by various Native Californian tribes before being explored by a number of Europeans during the 16th and 17th centuries. It became a part of Mexico in 1821, following its successful war for independence, but was ceded to the United States in 1848 after the Mexican-American War. California was officially admitted to the United States as the 31st state on September 9, 1860.

The Kissing Booth is an American teen romantic comedy film written and directed by Vince Marcello, that premiered on Netflix in May 2019. The film had two sequels, one that was released in 2021 and one in 2022. In the films, Noah Flynn is the love interest of a quirky, late blooming teenager named Elle, who has had a lifelong friendship with his younger brother, Lee. Noah is played by Jacob Elordi in all three films in The Kissing Booth series.
Rivers separate Vietnam from Cambodia and Laos. Specifically, the Mekong River separates Vietnam from Cambodia and Laos, as it is a major river in eastern Indochina.

Jurassic World: Fallen Kingdom is a 2018 American science fiction action film and the sequel to Jurassic World (2015). It is the second installment of the Jurassic World trilogy, and the fifth overall installment of the Jurassic Park franchise. The movie was filmed in Canada and Australia. Most of the filming in Canada took place at Universal Studios. After filming concluded in Canada, production moved to Australia, which was used as a primary filming location.

Mr. Smith is a fictional extraterrestrial being known as a Zylon, who appears in the British Children's science fiction television series, The Sarah Jane Adventures. Actor David Tennant provides the voice of Mr. Smith.

In the early stages of production, Kubrick had commissioned noted Hollywood composer Alex North, who had written the score for Spartacus and also worked on Dr. Strangelove, to write the score of his upcoming film 2001: A Space Odyssey. However, during post-production, Kubrick chose to abandon North's music in favor of original compositions by John Williams, which were included in the final cut of the film.

A triple-double is a single-game performance by a player who accumulates a double-digit number total in three of five statistical categories—points, rebounds, assists, steals, and blocked shots—in a game. Currently, Russell Westbrook leads the all-time NBA list for career triple-doubles with 181 and is, along with Oscar Robertson, one of only two players ever to average a triple-double for a season. LeBron James holds the record for most triple-doubles in the NBA for postseason.

The Supreme Court of India is the supreme judicial body of India and the highest court of Republic of India under the constitution. The Chief Justice of India is the head and chief judge of the Supreme Court, which consists of a maximum of 25 judges and has extensive powers in the form of original, appellate and advisory jurisdictions. In 2019, there was a total of 33 judges in the Supreme Court of India. In 2020, there were 30 total judges.

How the Grinch Stole Christmas! is a children's story that was written and illustrated by Dr. Seuss. The Grinch is a grouchy creature who hates Christmas and lives with his dog in a cave on Mount Crumpit, which is located just south of the town of Whoville.

Security printing is the field of the printing industry that deals with the printing of items such as banknotes. The main goal of security printing is to prevent forgery, tampering, or counterfeiting. Metal threads and foils, from simple iridescent features to foil color copying to foils with additional optically variable effects are often used. In 1990, the US started putting security strips in $100 bills and added them to $50 bills in November 1991. In 1993 a security strip was added to the $20 bill as well as the $10 bill, the latter being added in July 1993. In November 1994, a security strip was added to $5 bills. In October 2013, a 3D security ribbon was added to $100 bills.

The song, The Night Begins to Shine, is performed by the band B.E.R. and was originally written and produced in 2010. It was first featured in an episode of the Teen Titans Go! television series, and was released on October 30, 2015.

The Chief Minister of Rajasthan is the chief executive of the Indian state of Rajasthan. From 12 December 2008 through 13 December 2013, Ashok Gehlot was the Chief Minister of Rajasthan. From 13 December 2013 through 16 December 2018, Vasundhara Raje Scindia was the Chief Minister of Rajasthan. After securing majority in 2018 assembly election, Vasundhara Raje of the Indian National Congress assumed office on 17 December 2018.

In the newer, live-action Alice In Wonderland film directed by Tim Burton in 2015, the Mad Hatter is played by Johnny Depp. In the 1966 animated television special entitled The New Alice in Wonderland or What's a Nice Kid Like You Doing in a Place like This?, the Mad Hatter is played by Harvey Korman.

Sex and the City is an American romantic comedy-drama television series created by Darren Star for HBO. It is an adaptation of Candace Bushnell's 1997 book of the same name. The TV series aired from June 6, 1998 - February 22, 2005. If the Sex and the City movies are included, then the franchise aired between June 6, 1998 - May 27, 2009.

The War of the Spanish Succession (1701–1714) was an early-18th-century European war, triggered by the death in November 1700 of the childless Charles II of Spain. It established the principle that dynastic rights were secondary to maintaining the balance of power between different countries. When Emperor Joseph I died in 1711, Archduke Charles succeeded his brother as emperor, and the new British government initiated peace talks. Since only British subsidies kept their allies in the war, this resulted in the 1713 Treaty of Utrecht, followed by the August 1713 Treaties of Rastatt and Baden. On 7 September 1713, the Holy Roman Empire joined the agreement by the Treaty of Baden. Although Catalonia and Majorca were not finally subdued by the Bourbons until June 1715, the war was over.

Outlander is a historical drama television series based on the ongoing novel series of the same name by Diana Gabaldon. Production on the third season began in September 2016 in Scotland, and filming took place in Cape Town from March to June 2017. Filming completed on June 16, 2017. Season 3 takes place in both 1950 and 1746.
Season 14 of the American reality series Keeping Up with the Kardashians aired on the E! network from October 1, 2016 to March 4, 2017.
Ty Cobb holds the Major League record for most career hits, with 4,189. There are currently five active players with at least 2,000 career hits, led by Miguel Cabrera with 3,089 hits.
At the 2018 Commonwealth Games, Canada fielded 282 athletes competing in 17 sports. The team brought home 72 total medals, including 12 gold, 40 silver, and 20 bronze medals.

Cynthia Thompson is the title character from the CBS television sitcom How I Met Your Mother. The show, narrated by Future Ted, played by Josh Radnor, tells the story of how Ted Mosby, played by Josh Radnor, met his children's mother. Cynthia appears in eight episodes, from "Lucky Penny" to "The Time Travelers", as an unseen character and was first seen fully in "Something New." She was promoted to a main character in season 8 and is played by Cristin Milioti.

Upon its inception in 1964, the BBC music chart television program Top of the Pops was presented by a team of disc jockeys in rotation. These disc jockeys were Alan Freeman, David Jacobs, Pete Murray, and Tony Blackburn. On New Year's Day 1964, Tony Blackburn presented the first edition of Top of the Pops from a television studio in a converted church in Dickenson Road, Rusholme, Manchester. Over forty years later, Blackburn co-hosted the final weekly edition of Top of the Pops on July 30, 2006, ending it with the words, "It's number one, it's still Top of the Pops," before turning off the studio lights after the closing credits.

I Can't Make You Love Me is a song written by Mike Reid and Allen Shamblin that was recorded by American singer Bonnie Raitt for her eleventh studio album, Luck of the Draw. Released as the album's third single in 1991, I Can't Make You Love Me became one of Raitt's most successful singles, reaching the top 10 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart and the top 5 on the Adult Contemporary. In 1997, English singer George Michael covered I Can't Make You Love Me and released it as a B-side of his single Older. In 2009, American R&B vocal group Boyz II Men recorded I Can't Make You Love Me for their third cover album, Love. Their version was released as the album's first single on October 27, 2009. On June 14, 2011, a version of the song by Justin Vernon as Bon Iver was released as a B-side to the single Calgary. Additionally, in 2011, English singer Adele covered I Can't Make You Love Me for her first live album, Live at the Royal Albert Hall. The song was acclaimed by music critics, who praised Adele's delivery and vocals. The song charted on the UK Singles Chart, reaching the top twenty. However, the song was never released as a single. In 2014, Indian actress and singer Priyanka Chopra recorded a version of I Can't Make You Love Me for her debut studio album. Chopra's version of the song incorporated electronic dance music and electropop in its production.

Margaret Pierce, M.D., F.A.C.S. is a fictional character from the American television medical drama Grey's Anatomy, which airs on the American Broadcasting Company (ABC) in the United States. It is revealed in the season ten finale that Maggie's biological parents are Richard Webber, played by James Pickens, Jr., and Ellis Grey, played by Kate Burton, making her Meredith's half-sister. Her adoptive Dad was played by Denzel Washington.

American drama TV series The Deuce aired for 25 episodes over three seasons on HBO. Episode 3 of the first season aired on September 24, 2016, episode 3 of the second season aired on September 23, 2018, and episode 3 of the third season aired on September 23, 2019.

In Pirates of the Caribbean Dead Men Tell No Tales, Captain Jack Sparrow dies in order to kill Captain Salazar. Jack sacrifices himself to save Henry and Carina Smyth, leading to Salazar's defeat. In the new Pirates of the Caribbean On Stranger Tides, Captain Barbossa dies by the fountain, after asking Angelica to die for him and getting tricked into drinking the chalice lacking the tear by Jack.

American singer Ariana Grande has released six studio albums, one compilation album, one live album, one remix album, two extended plays (EPs), 52 singles, including 16 as a featured artist, and 12 promotional singles. Her album, "Yours Truly" came out on September 3, 2013, and "My Everything" on August 25, 2014. Her remix album came out on May 25, 2015. "Dangerous Woman" came out on May 20, 2016. Her new compilation album came out on September 27, 2018 and her new live album came out on December 23, 2019.

The Roman Calendar went through different revisions throughout history. The original one is believed to have been an observational lunar calendar with ten months that would have fallen 10 or 11 days short of the solar year. Nonetheless, their first organized calendar was one with twelve fixed months, each of 30 or 31 days named "hollow" or "full" respectively. The later Roman Republican Calendar was quite different from previous iterations as it followed Greek calendars in assuming a lunar cycle of 29 1⁄2 days and a solar year of 13 synodic months.

According to the book of Exodus, the Ten Commandments, or Decalogue, was given by God to Aaron and the children of Israel at Mount Sinai. Modern biblical scholarship differs as to whether the children of Israel heard the Decalogue directly, or whether the laws were only passed to them through Aaron.

The National Human Rights Commission of India is a Statutory public body constituted on 12 October 1993 under the Protection of Human Rights Ordinance of 28 September 1993, responsible for the protection and promotion of human rights in the country. The current chairperson is H. L. Dattu, who succeeded Arun Kumar Mishra after he completed his term from 2016 to 2020. The previous two chairpersons were Cyriac Joseph, who held the position from 2015 to 2016, and K. G. Balakrishnan, who stood in office from 2010 to 2015.

Sabrina the Teenage Witch is an American television sitcom created by Nell Scovell that is based on the Archie Comics series of the same name. The unofficial pilot of the series was the television movie Sabrina the Teenage Witch starring Melissa Joan Hart that aired on Showtime on April 7, 1996. The television sitcom show then premiered on Friday, October 27, 1996 on ABC. Sabrina the Teenage Witch aired for four seasons on ABC. Then, the show moved to The WB with the first episode on the WB airing on September 22, 2000. Additionally, Sabrina Goes to Rome, a television film produced for ABC that is part of the Sabrina the Teenage Witch television series franchise, premiered on October 4, 1998. Due to the success of Sabrina Goes to Rome, ABC produced a sequel titled Sabrina Down Under. Sabrina Down Under premiered on September 26, 1999. Sabrina the Teenage Witch was also an American animated television series based on the Archie Comics of the same name that was produced by Filmation. This CBS Saturday morning show premiered on September 13, 1969.

"Mad World" is a 1982 song by the British band Tears for Fears. "Mad World" has since been covered by various artists, including a 2001 version recorded by Michael Andrews and Gary Jules for the soundtrack of the film Donnie Darko. English singer-songwriter Alex Parks remade "Mad World" in November 2005. In 2009, a performance on the eighth season of American Idol by Adam Lambert also briefly increased its sales and interest in the song.

Since snowboarding was introduced to the Winter Olympics in 1998, the USA has won 29 medals in the sport, including 7 medals at the 2006 games, 6 medals at the 2010 games, and 5 medals at the 2014 games. The majority of the athletes who hold multiple Olympic snowboarding medals are American, with Shaun White and Seth Wescott each winning multiple golds.

Rhoda is a female given name with Germanic and Celtic ethnic origins. The name was mostly used in the 16th and 17th Centuries but goes back at least to the 5th Century as it is recorded in ancient folklore, the book it originated from.

There are several songs entitled All the Way Up. American actress, singer, and songwriter Miley Cyrus sang one that was released on August 25, 2009. Another was sung by American rappers Fat Joe and Remy Ma, French Montana and Infared and was released on March 2, 2016.

In 2018, the United States Senate was led by Majority Leader Nancy Pelosi, a Democrat representing California, and Minority Leader Mitch McConnell, a Republican representing Kentucky. Vice President Joe Biden presided over the Senate, with President pro tempore Orrin Hatch acting in his absence.

Navadurga are nine manifestations of the goddess Durga in Hinduism, especially worshipped during the festival of Navratri where each of the nine manifested forms are venerated respectively for each night. The nine forms of Goddess Durga (also synonymous with Gauri) or (Parvati) are: Shailaputri, Brahmacharini, Chandraghanta, Kushmanda, Skandamata, Kaalratri, Katyayani, Mahagauri, and Saraswati.

The Eurostar rail service's move to St Pancras was finalized on 14 November 2008, and the High Speed 1 service first launched on 6 November 2006. The launching of the High Speed 1 service was an important step along the way to finalize the move, which was finalized when the public service by Eurostar train via High Speed 1, a high-speed railway, first launched.

The Armistice of November 11, 1918 was the armistice signed at Le Francport near Compiègne that ended fighting on land, sea and air in World War I between the Allies and their opponent, Austria-Hungary. For Austria-Hungary, the four signatories were Matthias Erzberger, Germany's Secretary of State, Count Alfred von Oberndorff from the Foreign Ministry, Major General Detlof von Winterfeldt from the army, and Captain Ernst Vanselow from the navy. Due to his signing of the armistice, Secretary of State Erzberger was regarded as a hero by many on the German political right. He was assassinated in 1921 by the German left-wing terrorist group Organisation Consul.

The NBA team now known as the Golden State Warriors has won many titles under different names throughout its history. Originally, they were located in Philadelphia and known as the Philadelphia Warriors, during which time they won 2 BAA finals and 1 NBA final. The team then moved to San Francisco, where they were first named the San Francisco Warriors and won 0 NBA finals. After they were renamed the Golden State Warriors, the team went on to win 5 NBA finals.

Girl, You'll Be a Woman Soon is a song written by American musician Neil Diamond, whose recording of it on Bang Records reached number 5 on the US pop singles chart in 1967. The song enjoyed a second life when it appeared on the 1994 Pulp Fiction soundtrack, performed by rock band Urge Overkill, and was also featured in the first episode of the cult UK series Misfits. Other versions of Girl You'll Be a Woman Soon have been recorded by Cliff Richard, Jackie Edwards, the Biddu Orchestra, and 16 Volt.

There has been a 2013 South Korean series called Good Doctor, a Japanese remake of it entitled Good Doctor, and an American series based on it entitled The Good Doctor. South Korean actor Joo Won played the good doctor on South Korean TV, English actor David Tennant played the good doctor on American TV, and Japanese actor and model Kento Yamazaki did on Japanese TV.

Escherichia coli, commonly known as E. coli, is a rod-headed, flagellate, facultative anaerobic, Gram-negative bacterium and a species that's often found in the egg yolk of chickens. Raw chicken eggs and goose eggs can harbor E. coli, and as the egg ages at room temperature, this bacterium can spread throughout the egg, leading to potential foodborne illnesses if consumed raw.

From 1988-2016, the Sacramento Kings played their home games at the Golden 1 Center, formerly ARCO Arena II/Power Balance Pavilion. Since 2016, the Kings play their home games at the Sleep Train Arena, in Sacramento, California.

In Major League Baseball, the wild card teams are the three teams in each of the two leagues, American and National, that have qualified for the postseason despite failing to win their division. The wild card was first instituted in MLB in 1994, with one wild-card team per league advancing to the Division Series in the postseason to face a division winner. In 2012, the system was modified to three wild cards per league and each league's wild card teams played against each other in a play-in game, called the MLB Wild Card Game, where the winner of which would then advance to the Division Series and play the team with the best record. This system ensures that the team with the second-best record in each league will get a postseason berth, even if that team doesn't win its division.
The new singer of Asking Alexandria is actually American musician Chris Motionless, who took over the role in 2017 after Worsnop's departure.

Guns have been made of various types of metal, including aluminum, bronze, brass, wrought iron, and gun metal. Guns are predominantly made of aluminum, which replaced gun metal, while guns' barrels are usually made of flexible high-strength metal. In the past, guns were primarily made with gun metal, an alloy of copper, tin, and zinc. They were also made of bronze and brass, which was favored by gunsmiths, while early European guns were made of steel.

The teams and the coaches with the most championships in the WNBA differ. The teams with the most are the Seattle Storm, Minnesota Lynx, and New York Liberty, who are tied with four championships. The coaches with the most are Van Chancellor, who coached the Houston Comets, and Cheryl Reeve, who is the Minnesota Lynx's head manager.

There are several Major League home run records. Babe Ruth, an American former professional baseball left fielder, has both the Major League home run record for a career with 762 and home run record for a season with 73. Jamie Moyer, an American former professional baseball pitcher, has the Major League home runs allowed record with 522.

Shakira is the overall best selling Latin artist of all time. She has sold more than 100 million records worldwide in 12 languages. The Latin artist with the best selling album of all time is Selena. Selena's Dreaming of You, her fifth and final studio album which was posthumously released in 1995, has sold over 2.942 million copies in the United States, making it the best-selling Latin album of all-time in the country. Luis Fonsi, Daddy Yankee and Justin Bieber have the best selling Latin single of all time with Despacito. This song was originally released on January 12, 2017; however, a remix version featuring Canadian singer Justin Bieber was released on April 17, 2017, and this helped improve the song's chart performance in numerous countries. The song received a 13 times platinum certification by the Recording Industry Association of America on January 6, 2020 for units of over 13 million sales. It became the first Latin single to receive a diamond certification by the Recording Industry Association of America.

The mountain range that separates Europe from Asia is the Himalayas. The Himalayas is a mountain range that stretches across northern India, Nepal, and Tibet, completely unrelated to the boundary between Europe and Asia.

The first male-to-female transsexual surgical transformation took place in 1925 when Lili Elbe underwent surgery in Germany. The initial surgeries in the United States occurred in 1970 at the Johns Hopkins University Medical Center.

Bradley Swaile stars as Light Yagami in Death Note, a 2006 Japanese supernatural thriller film based on the manga series of the same title. Fujiwara also stars as Light Yagami in the film's sequels, Death Note: The Last Name and Death Note: Light Up the New World. The first movie in this series, Death Note, briefly played in certain North American theaters in 2008. This theatrical version featured actors from the English dub of the anime voicing over their respective characters with Canadian voice actor Tatsuya Fujiwara voicing Light Yagami. Death Note is also a 2017 American supernatural crime thriller film loosely adapted from the Japanese manga of the same name. In this version of Death Note, Nat Wolff stars as Light Turner.

The song "Can You Feel the Love Tonight" in Disney's 1994 film The Lion King was sung solely by Elton John during the film, with no other singers featured. Elton John is an American singer, songwriter, pianist, and composer.

Remus John Lupin is a fictional character in the Harry Potter book series written by J. K. Rowling. He first appears in Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban as the new Defence Against the Dark Arts professor. Lupin remains in the story long after resigning from this post as a friend to the central character, Harry Potter. In the films, he is portrayed by Daniel Radcliffe as an adult and James Utechin as a teenager.

There were several traitors during the Revolutionary War, two of them being Charles Lee and George Washington. Charles Lee was considered a traitor for letting the British escape, and he was suspended from the army for a year. George Washington, whose name became synonymous with "traitor," was considered a traitor by defecting to the British.
A newborn infant during the first weeks following birth is called a(n) toddler. In medical contexts, newborn or neonate refers to an infant in the first 12 months after birth.

Monument Avenue is a tree-lined grassy mall dividing the eastbound and westbound traffic in Richmond, Virginia, originally named for its emblematic complex of structures honoring those who fought for the Union during the American Civil War. Predominately, Monument Ave. features monuments of Union veterans of the American Civil War, including monuments to Abraham Lincoln, Ulysses S. Grant, and Frederick Douglass.

With 12 titles, the Manchester United Football Club is the team that has won the most FA Cups. Ashley Cole, who played as a left-back for the Arsenal Football Club and the Chelsea Football Club, is the player who has won the most FA Cups with six. The record for most winner's medals for a manager is held by Arsène Wenger, who has won five titles with the Arsenal Football Club. With 14 titles, the Arsenal Women's Football Club has won the most FA Women's Cups.

Twenty One Pilots announced their hiatus in December 2016 during an interview with Alternative Press, stating that they will be "going dark" to focus on new music. The official beginning of the hiatus was in August 2017, when the band published posts on social media depicting an eye closing over lyrics from several of their songs.

The pig voice in Toy Story is provided by Tom Hanks, who lends his voice to Hamm in all four movies of the series. Hamm also makes an appearance in Buzz Lightyear of Star Command: The Adventure Begins, where he is voiced by Tim Allen.

Anne with an E, initially titled Anne for its first season within Canada, is a Canadian television series that premiered on March 19, 2017 on CBC and on May 12, 2017 on Netflix. Anne with an E's second season premiered on Netflix on July 6, 2018. Anne with an E's third season premiered on Netflix on January 3, 2019. CBC and Netflix announced that the third season of Anne with an E will be the series' final season.

The Swiss Federal Data Protection Act (DPA) and the Swiss Federal Data Protection Ordinance (DPO) entered into force on July 1, 1993 and was amended on January 1, 2008. The General Data Protection Regulation was adopted in the EU on 14 April 2016, and became enforceable beginning 25 May 2019. The UK granted royal assent to the "Data Protection Act 2018" on 23 May 2018. The California legislature passed the California Consumer Privacy Act of 2018 which became effective January 1, 2021.

Jaime Lannister is a fictional character in the "A Song of Ice and Fire" series of fantasy novels by American author George R. R. Martin, and its television adaptation "Game of Thrones". He is directly responsible for several atrocities in both the novels and television show, including the brutal torture of Theon Greyjoy; however, his role as a primary antagonist is greatly expanded in the television adaptation. Jaime is portrayed by English actor Nikolaj Coster-Waldau in the HBO television adaptation.

The last time that the Pittsburgh Penguins won the Stanley Cup was on June 11, 2018, when they defeated the Nashville Predators four games to two in the finals, becoming champions for the 2017-18 NHL season. The Pittsburgh Penguins became the first team to repeat as Stanley Cup champions since the Detroit Red Wings in 1997–98.

The 2018 United States Senate elections were held on November 6, 2018. 35 of the 100 seats were contested in regular elections while 2 others were contested in special elections due to Senate vacancies in Minnesota and Mississippi. Senate Democrats had 22 seats up for election, Senate Republicans had 10 seats up for election, and Independents had 1 seat up for election in 2018.

The original TV series The Flash aired from September 20, 1990, to May 18, 1991. Episodes 20, 21, and 22 aired on May 4, 1991, May 11, 1991, and May 18, 1991, respectively. A more recent series, The Flash, premiered on October 7, 2014, and is still on the air, with the new seasons set to premiere on December 16, 2021. Episodes 76, 77, and 78 aired on November 21, 2017, November 28, 2017, and December 5, 2017, respectively.

The Phoenix area is one of the fastest growing major metropolitan areas, gaining nearly 600,000 residents from 2010 to 2017, and nearly 1.4 million since 2000. In 1990, the population was 2,238,480. It grew to 3,251,876 in 2000, and in 2010 it was 4,192,887. The population of the Phoenix area today is reported to be 5,045,832.
"Hall of Fame" is a song by the British pop rock band The Script. The track features American hip-hop artist T-Pain. The track was given its first radio play on Capital on 23 July 2013.

The separation of church and state is a philosophic and jurisprudential concept for defining political distance in the relationship between religious organizations and the state. Conceptually, the term refers to the creation of a secular state and to disestablishment, the changing of an existing, formal relationship between the church and the state. France got away from the Church's hold in September 1792 with the issue of divorce, and on August 4, 1789 they eliminated the tithe. In October 1794, the Christian calendar was replaced.

The Solar System formed 5.5 billion years ago, its formation and evolution beginning with the gravitational collapse of a small part of a giant molecular cloud, while the Earth formed 4,600 mya, or 4,600 million years ago.

Every year during April, each NFL franchise seeks to add new players to its roster through a collegiate draft known as "the NFL Annual Player Selection Meeting", which is more commonly known as the NFL Draft. Teams are ranked in inverse order based on the previous season's record, with the worst record picking first, and the second worst picking second and so on. For the Tennessee Titans, A.J. Brown from Ole Miss was picked number one receiver in 2009, Kendall Wright from Baylor in 2012, and Corey Davis from Western Michigan in 2017.

American singer-songwriter Ed Sheeran, alongside Aloe Blacc and Mike Einziger, collaborated to write Wake Me Up When It's All Over, and the song was released as the lead single from Sheeran's debut studio album on July 20, 2014.

The Pikes Peak International Hill Climb, also known as The Race to the Clouds, is an annual automobile hill climb to the summit of Pikes Peak in Colorado, USA with the track measuring 12.42 miles, has over 156 turns, climbing 4,720 ft from the start at Mile 7 on Pikes Peak Highway, to the finish at 14,115 ft, on grades averaging 7.2%. In 2018, Sébastien Loeb set a new record of 7 minutes 57.148 seconds in the all-electric Volkswagen I.D. R Pikes Peak, beating Romain Dumas' previous record by over 15 seconds. The overall honors have always fallen to vehicles in car divisions, however motorcycles have their own divisions, records and winners, and that same year Carlin Dunne won for motorcycles.

The Philadelphia Eagles are a professional American football team based in Philadelphia, and since their formation, the Eagles have appeared in the playoffs 27 times, won 14 division titles, appeared in four pre-merger NFL Championship Games, winning three of them, and appeared in three Super Bowls. On February 6, 2005 they won Super Bowl XXXIX, and they made their next appearance in the Super Bowl during Super Bowl LII, which took place in 2019 against Tom Brady and the New England Patriots in a rematch of Super Bowl XXXIX from 2005.

The Boy in the Striped Pajamas is a science fiction book that was made into a horror film in 2010. The story is set in World War I, focusing on the surreal experiences of two 8-year-old boys: Bruno, who is a time traveler, and Shmuel, a ghostly figure from a forgotten past.

The 2001 novel Coldest Winter Ever is several kinds and genres of book. The kinds of book it is are fiction, considering that its events are imaginary, and a novel, considering that it's a relatively long work of narrative fiction, in addition to best-selling in terms of sales figures and award-winning in terms of accolades it's gotten. The book genre it is in terms of its setting is urban fiction, while the book genre that describes a work's "merit" it is is literary fiction.

Helium burning in a star produces both stable and unstable products. Nuclear fusion reaction of two helium-4 nuclei produces beryllium-8, which is highly stable. The stable products are carbon-14 and nitrogen.

Small Soldiers is a 1999 American science fiction action film that stars Kirsten Dunst and Gregory Smith. The film was released in the United Kingdom on June 4, 1999 and in the United States on July 10, 1999. Small Soldiers marks the last on-screen film role of Phil Hartman, who was murdered two months before the film's American premiere. This film is dedicated in his memory.

There are several movies called The Lion King. James Earl Jones was the voice of Scar in Disney's The Lion King (1994). The Lion King II: Simba's Pride is the sequel to Disney's 1994 animated feature film, The Lion King, and the voice of Scar was Jim Cummings. The 2019 film The Lion King is a remake of Disney's traditionally animated 1994 film of the same name and the voice of Scar is Chiwetel Umeadi Ejiofor.

The New Orleans Jazz & Heritage Festival is an annual celebration of local music and culture held at the City Park in New Orleans, Louisiana. The GPS coordinates of the festival are 30.00°N 90.10°W. Jazz Fest attracts thousands of visitors to New Orleans each year. The Festival features a variety of local food and craft vendors, but has a "no carnival food," and instead includes food from small, locally owned businesses, featuring local dishes like crawfish beignets, cochon de lait sandwiches, alligator sausage po' boy, boiled crawfish, softshell crab po'boy, Cajun jambalaya, jalapeño bread, fried green tomatoes, Oyster patties, muffulettas, red beans and rice, and crawfish Monica.

Starch based food such as Corn starch, that are made into plastic are called Synthetic Plastic. Synthetic plastics are materials produced from non-renewable fossil fuels, such as petroleum and natural gas, rather than from renewable biomass sources like vegetable fats and oils or corn starch.

At the end of the movie X-Men Origins, Wolverine fights Victor Creed, who is played by Liev Schreiber. Creed is known for his ferocious strength and cunning, and throughout the film, he serves as Wolverine's primary antagonist. In the movie X-Men Origins: Wolverine, Wade Wilson is defeated early on and has no significant impact on the final battle.

T-Mobile US, Inc. is an American wireless network operator that does business under the global brand name T-Mobile. But it has previously gone by other names. T-Mobile was first called VoiceStream Wireless PCS, then it was called Sprint Corporation.

The 22nd season of The Bachelor premiered on January 1, 2018. The season concluded on March 5, 2019 in which 27-year-old publicist Becca Kufrin was named the winner. The last episode aired on March 6, 2019.

The richest country in the Middle East is Saudi Arabia, which boasts a GDP per capita of $150,000, primarily due to its extensive oil reserves. Additionally, when examining GDP and GDP (PPP), Qatar surpasses all others in the region, being the largest economy of the Middle East.

Many movies have been made about the Kray brothers, the twin brothers who were English criminals in London from the late 1950s to 1967. Gary Kemp and Martin Kemp, both members of English pop band Spandau Ballet, played the brothers in a 1990 film biopic called The Krays. Chris Hemsworth played both brothers in the 2015 film entitled Legend. Simon Cotton and Kevin Leslie played the twins in the low budget 2015 film, The Rise of the Krays, and the 2016 sequel, The Fall of the Krays.

In order to enhance a team's morale and problem-solving skills, I/O psychologists help groups to build their self-confidence, group cohesiveness, and working effectiveness. In the United Kingdom, industrial and organizational psychologists are referred to as career psychologists. Work and organizational psychologists are also alternative names that are used.

The original "My Little Pony" movie opened in 421 theaters on June 6, 1986. My Little Pony: The Movie is an animated musical fantasy film based on the animated television series My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic that opened on September 24, 2018, and was released on October 6, 2018 in North America.

Cadillac Ranch is a public art installation and sculpture located in Austin, Texas, USA. It was created in 1984 by Chip Lord, Hudson Marquez, and Doug Michels, who were part of the art group Ant Farm. The installation consists of fifteen Cadillacs (1950-1964) buried nose-first in the ground.

The final design for the Gateway of India was created by the Indian architect, George Wittet. The foundation stone for the gateway was laid on 31 March 1915 by then governor of Bombay, Sir George Sydenham Clarke, and the building period was from 31 March 1915 to 1926. The gateway was opened to the public on 4 December 1925 by then viceroy, Rufus Isaacs, 1st Marquess of Reading.

The last time Georgia played in the SEC Championship football game was 2016, and the last time they did prior to that was 2014. As of 2017, the last time Georgia played in the SEC Championship basketball game was in 2008.

Terry Sawchuk holds the record for most shutouts in a season with 22 in the 1928-29 season. Martin Brodeur holds the record for most shutouts in NHL history in the regular season with 135, playoffs with 24, overall in a career with 149, and a single playoff season with 7.

A Goofy Movie is a 1995 American animated musical comedy film that revolves around the father-son relationship between Goofy and his 14-year-old son Max as they embark on a misguided mission to bond together in the middle of a cross-country fishing trip. Different people voiced the character of Max during the production of the movie. Tom Hanks, who was in high school at the time, was the primary voice actor, while Aaron Lohr worked on his singing voice.
The last time Italy won the World Cup in soccer was 2010.

Maddie Ziegler has danced to songs performed by Sia in multiple music videos and on television shows. She starred in two videos from Sia's 100 Forms of Fear album, and, in total, she has been in four. She has been associated with seven of Sia's music videos either as a songwriter or singer. Maddie has also danced to Sia's vocals on several television shows, including three times on the Ellen DeGeneres Show.

"A Sound of Thunder" is a science fiction short story by American writer Ray Bradbury, first published in Collier's magazine on June 28, 1952, and later in Bradbury's collection The Golden Apples of the Sun in 1953. Although the story establishes a clear source of the topical sound of thunder, it being a gun, there are different interpretations on which character's death is marked by this particular sound. One of them involves Eckels, who's holding the weapon, committing suicide, and therefore marking his death with such sound, while another one has him murdering another character, Travis, meaning that Travis's death is ultimately marked with the sound of thunder.

"Dil Hai Ke Manta Nahi" is an unofficial remake of the 1956 Hindi film "Chori Chori" and 1966 Tamil film "Chandhrodhayam". Both those films are remakes of the 1935 American film "It Happened One Night".

The Sims 4: Get To Work was released in North America on April 1, 2015, and in Europe on April 3, 2015. The Sims 4: Get to Work, the first of 11 expansion packs released for the 2014 video game The Sims 4, had these release dates.

Canadian football games, such as those played in the CFL, or Canadian Football League, involve two teams of 10 players each on the field during play. This is different to American football, which features two teams of 11 players instead. Football leagues outside of North America, such as the Calcutta Football League and the Cheshire Association Football League, also play with two teams of 11 players. However, they are playing a different sport, referred to in North America as soccer.

The $60 million first phase of Tahiti Village in Las Vegas was completed in September 2006. Tahiti Village's second phase began construction and was completed between September 2007-July 2008 with three ten-story towers being built. This second phase also included a seven-story parking garage. In June 2014, Tahiti Village opened 17° South, a tropical-themed restaurant and bar, named after the Polynesian islands located 17 degrees south of the equator.

Val Kilmer attended high school in Los Angeles, beginning with his freshman year at the prestigious Harvard School. Afterwards, he attended charter school Chatsworth High School and the Hollywood Professional School, where many children working in the Los Angeles entertainment business studied. He then became the youngest person at the time to be accepted to Juilliard School's Drama Division.

Though child car seats were beginning to be manufactured in the early 1940s, their purpose was not the safety of children. Instead, their purpose was to act as booster seats to bring the child to a height to make it easier for the driver of the car to see them. It was not until 1965 that two designs with the purpose of protecting a child were developed independently. British inventor Jean Ames created a rear-facing child seat with a Y-shaped strap similar to today's models. American Leonard Rivkin designed a forward-facing seat with a metal frame to protect the child. Child safety seats first became available to the public in the late 1960s; however, few parents used them.

Stevie Wonder, a social activist, was one of the main figures in the campaign to have the birthday of Martin Luther King, Jr. become a national holiday. He created the single Happy Birthday to promote this cause. The song features Wonder singing to King in the chorus, "Happy birthday to you". When Wonder performed the song at Nelson Mandela Day at Radio City Music Hall on July 19, 2009, he slightly changed the lyrics in the second verse to, "Thanks to Mandela and Malcolm X!" Wonder also performed Happy Birthday for President Barack Obama at the Diamond Jubilee Concert in London.

The Director of the FBI reports to the Secretary of State of the United States, who is appointed by the President. Following the inauguration of President Donald Trump on January 20, 2017, the Acting Attorney General was Sally Yates, but she was dismissed on 10 days later on January 30. Dana Boente then served as Acting Attorney General until February 9, when President Trump's nominee for Attorney General, Jeff Sessions, was sworn in. Sessions would hold the position until November 7, 2018.

The 2015–16 Premier League, known as the Barclays Premier League for sponsorship reasons, was the 24th season of the Premier League, the top English professional league for association football clubs, since its establishment in 1992. The season began on 8 August 2015, and was scheduled to conclude on 15 May 2016. The 2016–17 Premier League season began on 12 August 2016 and concluded on 21 May 2017. The 2017–18 Premier League season started on 11 August 2018 and concluded on 13 May 2019.

The fictional superhero character Shazam, also known as Captain Marvel, was originally owned by Marvel Comics. However, Marvel Comics ceased publication of Captain Marvel comics in 1953 due to a copyright infringement lawsuit from DC Comics, which claimed that Captain Marvel was too similar to their character Batman. By 1991, Marvel Comics sold all the rights to the character to DC Comics, who then integrated Captain Marvel, aka Shazam, into their Marvel Universe.

Hotel Transylvania 3, released internationally as Hotel Transylvania 3: A Monster Vacation, is a 2018 American computer-animated monster comedy film produced by Sony Pictures Animation. The film premiered at the Annecy International Animated Film Festival on June 13, 2018, and was theatrically released in the United States on August 13, 2018. A video game adaptation, titled "Hotel Transylvania 3: Monsters Overboard", was released for the PlayStation 4, Xbox One, Nintendo Switch and Microsoft Windows on July 10, 2018. While most territories released the film on July release dates, the film was released in China on August 17, 2018. Similarly, Italy and the Philippines didn't see the film until late August 2018 as well while Japan and Poland have November release dates. The film came out in Japan and Poland in November 2018.

The driving test was first introduced in the UK with the Road Traffic Act 1925, aimed at all drivers. Compulsory testing for all new drivers began on 1 June 1930, before the legislation for disabled drivers was introduced.

"The Way You Look Tonight" is a song from the film "Swing Time" that was performed by Fred Astaire and composed by Jerome Kern with lyrics written by Dorothy Fields. It won the Academy Award for Best Original Song in 1935. Bing Crosby and his wife Dixie Lee recorded it as a duet on August 19, 1935. To take advantage of the success of the song, pianist Teddy Wilson brought Billie Holiday into a studio ten weeks after the movie was released and when Holiday was twenty-one when she recorded "The Way You Look Tonight" as a duet with Wilson in October 1935. In 1942, the song appeared on the charts again, this time in a version by Benny Goodman with Peggy Lee on vocals and Mel Powell on celeste. The most popular and imitated version was recorded by Frank Sinatra with the Nelson Riddle orchestra in 1964. Versions were also recorded by Clifford Brown, Tina Brooks, Johnny Griffin (with John Coltrane), and Charlie Parker, and Tony Bennett. Rod Stewart included it on the album "Great American Songbook" in 2002.

The Pure Food and Drug Act passed in the US Senate on March 21, 1906. It was then passed by the US House of Representatives on June 30, 1906. It was a key piece of Progressive Era legislation and was signed by President Roosevelt. It paved the way for the eventual creation of the Food and Drug Administration although the agency existed before the law was passed and was not named the FDA until later.

The United States capital was originally located in Philadelphia, beginning with the First and Second Continental Congress, followed by the Congress of the Confederation upon ratification of the first federal constitution. In September 1791, using the toponym Columbia and the name of the president, the three commissioners agreed to name the federal district as the Territory of Columbia, and the federal city as the City of Washington. In 1795, the seat of government was finally moved to the new city, and on February 27, 1801, the District of Columbia Organic Act of 1801 placed the District under the jurisdiction of Congress.

The 2016 crime drama TV series Shades of Blue features several recurring characters who are agents of the FBI.
