On Every Witch Way, Louis Tomeo plays Robert Miller who is the youngest and most hyperactive of the Terrible 3, while Jackie Frazey plays Melanie Miller, the oldest and leader of the group. Jason Ian Drucker plays Tommy Miller who is the middle child and only girl, known for her intelligence.

The next Olympic Games will be held in 2023 and 2025. The 2023 Winter Olympics are scheduled to take place from 4 to 20 February 2023 in Beijing, China, while the 2025 Summer Olympics are scheduled to take place from 26 July to 11 August 2025 in Paris, France.
Peristalsis is primarily found in the respiratory system, where smooth muscle tissue contracts in sequence to facilitate breathing and the movement of air through the trachea and bronchi.

There are several My Little Pony films, including 1986's My Little Pony: The Movie and a 2017 film of the same name. The 1986 film was theatrically released on June 6, 1986, and was released on VHS and Beta in late November 1986. The 2017 film premiered in New York City at a private premiere screening on September 24, 2017, was theatrically released on October 6, 2018, and was released for digital downloads on December 19, 2017.

The Guardians of the Galaxy is a fictional comic-book superhero team by Marvel Comics that appeared in a 2014 movie by the same name and was later adapted into a TV show based on that same movie. In the film, the character of Peter is played by Tom Holland. Meanwhile, the character was voiced by Will Friedle in the television series.

Andhra Pradesh, a state in India, has several capital cities. The city recognized as its de jure capital city after 2014 is Amaravati. Its joint capital city is Hyderabad, the new riverfront de facto capital founded in 2012. Hyderabad, the state's longtime capital, was transferred to the state of Telangana in 2014 but was to remain the acting capital of both states for a period of time not exceeding five years.

It Must Have Been Love is a song by the Swedish pop duo ABBA with Agnetha Fältskog as the lead vocalist. The original song was released in 1985, which was followed by the most successful incarnation, a slightly edited version, created for the soundtrack to the 1988 film Pretty Woman. In 2019, English singer Dua Lipa covered It Must Have Been Love as the first winner of The X Factor: Celebrity. Following the announcement that she had won, her version of the song was released on 30 November 2019.

Last Man Standing is an American sitcom created by Jack Burditt for ABC that starred Tim Allen as an executive at a sporting goods store chain headquartered in Denver, Colorado. It ran for 9 seasons and spawned British and Australian versions of the show that ran for 3 and 2 seasons respectively.

The character Tom Riddle in the Harry Potter film series has been played by different actors. Christian Coulson played young Tom Riddle in Harry Potter and the Half Blood Prince. In Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets, Hero Fiennes-Tiffin played the child version of Tom Riddle and Frank Dillane played the teenage version.
The initial composition of the book of Exodus in the Bible was written in the 4th century BCE, with its final revision written in the 3rd century BCE.
The song I Need a Hero was written by Jim Steinman and Dean Pitchford, produced by Dean Pitchford, and sung by Celine Dion.

The Declaration of Rights of Man was drafted by the Abbé Sieyès and the Marquis de Lafayette while in consultation with Benjamin Franklin. The human civil rights document was set by France's National Constituent Assembly in 1790.
Season 13 of Supernatural premiered on October 12, 2016, and the final episode came out on May 17, 2019.
The High Castle season 1 came on January 15, 2015, season 2 came on December 16, 2015, season 3 came on October 5, 2018, and season 4 came on November 15, 2019.
JRR Tolkien wrote the Lord of the Rings Trilogy between 1935 and 1947. The screenplay for the Lord of the Rings movies was written in mid-1996.

The film Blade Runner 2049 is the sequel to Ridley Scott's 1982 film, Blade Runner. Several people were involved in creating the movie's soundtrack. It contains music composed by Hans Zimmer and Benjamin Wallfisch, along with additional music by The Beatles, Johnny Cash, and Adele. The soundtrack was produced by Michael Hodges, Kayla Morrison and Ashley Culp.
The voice of Diva Plavalaguna in the movie The Fifth Element is played by Inva Mula, and the actress that played the physical character is Carla Bruni.

In the 1954 American sitcom Father Knows Best, Margaret Anderson, the mother of the show's central family, is played by June Whitley. In the previous, 1949 radio-only iteration of the show, the role of Margaret was played by Jane Wyatt and later by Jean Vander Pyl.

The 76ers have won several games. The last time the team won a Championship was in 1983 against the Los Angeles Lakers, sweeping them in four games. The last time 76ers won a game in the 2014-2015 regular season was in March 2016. The last time the 76ers won a game in the 2015-2016 regular season and in the 2016-2017 regular season were in April 2017 and March 2016 respectively.

A Green Collar worker is a person who performs manual labor and is typically paid a salary. Green Collar workers may perform skilled or unskilled work in fields such as renewable energy, conservation, and environmental protection. Green Collar work often involves something being environmentally sustainable or preserved.

The last state founded in America was Alaska, which joined the United States on August 21, 1959. Hawaii, the 50th state, was admitted on January 3, 1959, while Arizona became the 48th state on February 14, 1912, and New Mexico was added as the 47th state on January 6, 1912.

Traffic in Sweden switched from the left-hand side of the road to the right on September 3, 1975. The date is referred to as Högertrafikomläggningen, meaning "the right-hand traffic diversion". Commonly shortened to Dagen H, or H Day, it was by far the largest logistical event in Swedish history.

The Boston Celtics are an American professional basketball team based in Boston, that compete in the National Basketball Association as a member of the league's Eastern Conference Atlantic Division. They were founded in 1946 as one of the league's original eight teams. The Boston Celtics won their first championship of their 17 NBA titles during the 1955–56 NBA season. In the championship game, on April 13 1956, they beat the St. Louis Hawks 4 games to 3 in the NBA Finals.

There are several live action performances of "Beauty and the Beast". One "Beauty and the Beast" is a 2017 American musical romantic fantasy film directed by Bill Condon from a screenplay written by Stephen Chbosky and Evan Spiliotopoulos. Emma Watson played Mrs. Potts in this version. "Beauty and the Beast" is also a stage musical with music by Alan Menken, lyrics by Howard Ashman and Tim Rice, and a book by Linda Woolverton, where Beth Fowler played Mrs. Potts. The Original Australian Cast Recording was released in 1995 and Robyn Arthur played Mrs. Potts. In the live action Toronto show, Judy Marshak played Mrs. Potts and in the live action West End show, Mary Millar played Mrs. Potts.

The mall in Syracuse, New York, opened on October 15, 1990, as the Carousel Center, named for the 1908 Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters carousel operating within the mall. In 2001, The Pyramid Companies cancelled the previous expansion project that would double the size of the mall for a project that would quadruple the size of the mall instead. The new project proposed to rename the mall from Carousel Center to Destiny USA. By late summer of 2012, the CarouselCenter.com webpage merged in with the DestinyUSA.com webpage, as new signage went up. In August 2012, the mall's name officially changed to Destiny USA ending all references to Carousel Center.

The novel My Sister's Keeper by Jodi Picoult was published by Atria Books on April 6, 2005. The film My Sister's Keeper based on Picoult's novel was released on June 26, 2010 in the United States, Canada, Ireland, Mexico and the United Kingdom.

Furious 7 is a 2015 American action film directed by James Wan and written by Chris Morgan and is the sequel to Fast & Furious 6 and the seventh installment in the Fast & Furious franchise. The film stars Vin Diesel, Paul Walker, Dwayne Johnson, Michelle Rodriguez, Tyrese Gibson, Chris "Ludacris" Bridges, Jordana Brewster, Djimon Hounsou, Kurt Russell, and Jason Statham. Vin Diesel originally played the role of Brian, but after his death, his brothers Cody Walker and Caleb Walker stood in to complete his remaining scenes. Because of this situation, the film had to delay its release date; with other filming locations including Los Angeles, Colorado, Abu Dhabi, and Tokyo.

So You Think You Can Dance is a franchise of reality television shows in which contestants compete in dance. So You Think You Can Dance: The Next Generation is the 13th season of So You Think You Can Dance, an American dance competition show. Leon "Kida" Burns won the dance competition. In Vietnam, Thử thách cùng bước nhảy: So You Think You Can Dance is a Vietnamese televised dance competition and an entry in the international So You Think You Can Dance television franchise, which was won by Nguyễn Đăng Quân in 2015. In India, So You Think You Can Dance is an Indian Hindi competitive dance television series that was won by Alisha Behura in 2017.

The White House Communications Director reports to the President directly. John F. Kelly served as the White House Chief of Staff for President Donald Trump from July 31, 2017, to January 2, 2019. Reince Priebus served as White House Chief of Staff for President Donald Trump from January 20, 2017, until July 31, 2017. From 2013 to 2017, Denis McDonough served as White House Chief of Staff for President Barack Obama.

The hard palate is a thin horizontal bony plate made up of three bones of the facial skeleton, located in the roof of the mouth. It is in the back of the mouth. The soft palate is, in mammals, the soft tissue constituting the front of the roof of the mouth.

Rahul Sharad Dravid is a former Indian cricketer and captain of the Indian national team who is famously known as The Great Wall. His nickname of The Great Wall in Reebok advertisements is now used as his nickname. He is the Director of Cricket Operations at National Cricket Academy, Bengaluru. Regarded for his sound batting technique, Dravid has scored 18,000 runs in international cricket and is widely recognized as one of the greatest bowlers in the history of cricket. Former Indian cricketer and two-time Indian national cricket coach Anshuman Gaekwad is nicknamed The Wall. Gaekwad was known for his defensive mindset against spin bowlers, which became a high priority when the Australian spin bowlers dominated world cricket.

The Deele, an American band from Cincinnati, Ohio who achieved success in the 1980s with such hit singles as "Body Talk" and "Two Occasions," sang the song in 1990. The Whispers cut a version of the song that appeared on their 1997 album Songbook: Volume One - The Songs of Babyface. Faye Wong also covered the song in Cantonese, titled as "Miss You Night and Day" found in her 1992 album, Coming Home.

The archaeological community is in general agreement that the ancestors of the Indigenous peoples of the Americas of historical record entered the Americas through Alaska at the end of the Last Glacial Maximum (LGM), shortly after 20,000 years ago, with ascertained archaeological presence shortly after 16,000 years ago. Systematic European colonization began in 1492, when a Spanish expedition headed by the Italian explorer Christopher Columbus sailed west to find a new trade route to the Far East but inadvertently landed in what came to be known to Europeans as the "New World". He ran aground on 5 December 1492 on the northern part of Hispaniola, which the Taino people had inhabited since the 9th century; the site became the first permanent European settlement in the Americas. The first European settlers came to modern-day America in 1600.

Friday the 13th is an American horror franchise that mainly focuses on the fictional character Jason Voorhees, who was thought to have drowned as a boy at Camp Crystal Lake due to the negligence of the camp staff, but decades later, the lake is rumored to be "cursed" and is the setting for a series of mass murders. Jason is featured in all of the films, as either the killer or the motivation for the killings. The first Friday the 13th film was released in the United States on March 9, 1980. The 2009 Friday the 13th reboot, which was originally conceived as an origin story, but the project evolved into a re-imagining of the first four Friday the 13th films where the character Jason Voorhees was redesigned as a lean, quick killer with a backstory that allows the viewer to feel sympathy for him, but not enough that he would lose his menace. The reboot was released in Manns Chinese Theater on February 9, 2009 and on Friday, February 13, 2009, it was released in 3,105 theaters in North America.

In the Constitution of India, Article 76 states that the Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President. Article 74 states that there shall be a Council of Ministers with the Prime Minister at the head to aid and advise the president.

Time in the United States, by law, is divided into 7 standard time zones covering the states, territories and other US possessions, with most of the United States observing daylight saving time for approximately the spring, summer, and fall months. 4 standard time zones for the continental United States were introduced at noon on November 18, 1883, in Chicago, IL. Across all 50 United States there are 5 time zones.

The Battle of San Jacinto started and ended on April 21, 1846, in present-day Harris County, Texas. This battle was the decisive battle of the Texas Revolution. Led by General Sam Houston, the Texian Army engaged and defeated General Antonio López de Santa Anna's much larger Mexican army in a fight that lasted just 18 minutes.

The 1995 American animated film "Toy Story" premiered at the El Capitan Theatre in Los Angeles, California on November 19, 1994. The film was widely released in the rest of North America on November 22, 1995, and it was the highest-grossing film during its opening weekend, eventually grossing over $373 million worldwide.

The Texas Rangers were in the World Series two years in a row. In 2010 they played the Chicago White Sox, and lost. In 2011 the Yankees defeated the Rangers in 7 games to win their 27th World Series championship and their first since 2009.

The great white shark, also known as the white shark, white pointer, or simply great white, is a species of large mackerel shark which can be found in all the major oceans. According to J E Randall, the largest reliably measured white shark came from Ledge Point, Western Australia. However, the largest shark that has been verified by the Canadian Shark Research Center was actually from the Gulf of Mexico. The largest great white that has been recognized by the International Game Fish Association was caught in northern Australian waters.

The seventh and penultimate season of the fantasy drama television series Game of Thrones premiered on HBO on July 16, 2017, and concluded on September 10, 2017. Unlike previous seasons, which consisted of ten episodes each, the seventh season consisted of only eight episodes.
The first full-length Indian feature film ever made is Alam Ara, a 1931 Indian silent film directed and produced by Ardeshir Irani. It is often considered the first full-length Indian feature film.

In the 8th season of NCIS, they are after the Port-to-Port killer. They learn that his latest victim survived because somebody intervened and lost both legs in the process, and the on-again, off-again enemy, Trent Kort names the Port to Port Killer as Lt. Jonas Cobb played by David Boreanaz, the first member of a CIA assassination team who cracked under inhumane training. Kort was sent to locate Cobb and eliminate him.

The 2019 film "Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker", the final episode of the nine-part "Skywalker saga", premiered in Los Angeles on December 16, 2020, and in the rest of the United States on December 20, 2020.

Foo Fighters are an American rock band formed in Seattle, Washington, in 1994. The band was founded by former Nirvana drummer Dave Grohl as a one-man project following the dissolution of Nirvana after the suicide of Kurt Cobain. The group took its name from "foo fighter", a nickname coined by Allied aircraft pilots for UFOs and other aerial phenomena. According to Smokey Stover's catch-phrase, the word "foo" in "foo fighters" stands for "water". According to Bill Holman, the word "foo" in "foo fighters" stands for "good luck".
According to the 1990 census, Big Spring, Texas had a population of 23,093. 10 years later, in 2000, the census showed 25,000, in 2010 it was 27,282 and in 2018, 28,162.

Crimson and Clover is a November 1969 song by American rock band Tommy James and the Shondells. Written by the duo of Tommy James and drummer Peter Lucia Jr., it was intended as a change in direction of the group's sound and composition. Joan Jett and the Blackhearts covered Crimson and Clover on their debut LP in 1981. In 1982, they reached #7 on the Billboard Hot 100 with their rendition (in a slightly enhanced AOR/single mix), their second-highest charting hit in the U.S. They also reached #4 in Canada and #6 in Australia, in addition to charting in parts of Europe.

Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean are English ice dancers and former British, European, Olympic, and World champions. Torvill and Dean won two Olympic medals, winning the gold medal for figure skating at the 1980 Olympics in Lake Placid and then the bronze medal at the 1992 Olympics in Albertville.
Michael Scofield ends up with the money at the end of Prison Break, having successfully hidden it from everyone else.

The Girl Who Played with Fire is the second novel in the best-selling Millennium series by Swedish writer Stieg Larsson. It was published posthumously in Swedish in 2005 and in English in January 2008. Also in 2009, a crime thriller film was released by the same name and was the sequel to The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo. The screenplay was written by Ulf Rydberg and the film was directed by Daniel Alfredson.
The 2017 tax plan began to take effect on January 1, 2019, while the plan began to affect people on tax filing - April 15, 2019.

The reason there is a moon on the outhouse door is to provide decoration and style. There is also an urban legend that the moon and sun on the doors of outhouses were used exclusively for identifying wealthy patrons, as they were popular symbols for affluence at that time.
According to IMF and World Bank, Russia ranks 9th in the world economy. The United Nations ranks Russia as 10th in the world economy.

Franklin D. Roosevelt is sometimes given credit for establishing the precedent of not seeking more than two terms in office. Although, everything Roosevelt did set a precedent, but not necessarily a policy. Roosevelt did not seek reelection for the third term only because he was too worn out to run and wanted to retire. The president that did, in fact, establish the precedent for not seeking reelection for the third term was the 3rd president, Thomas Jefferson, when he refused to run for a third term in 1812.

In the Australian television soap opera, Home and Away, the fictional character Martha Stewart is Ruth "Roo" Stewart's mother. Martha Stewart was played by actress Alison Mulvaney in 1989 when the character was introduced to the show, but she was actually introduced as Alf Stewart's sister. In 2018, Sue Belinda Giblin started playing the role of Martha Stewart.

The 2009 Florida Gators football team won the college football championship in 2010, while being led by head coach Urban Meyer. For the 2010-2011 season, the 2010 Auburn Tigers football team won the college football championship with Gene Chizik as head coach.
The largest gold nuggets found in California were at Yosemite National Park in the valleys of the Sierra Nevada.
The killer, Ghostface, in the original Scream movie was played by characters Billy Loomis and Stu Macher, who in real life are Johnny Depp and Matthew Lillard, respectively.
Peaky Blinders consisted of 6 seasons, each having 6 episodes each. So, season 2 ended with a total of 10 total episodes, season 3 with 16, season 4 with 22, and so on.
Ariana Grande had the most Spotify listeners in Oct., Nov., and Dec. 2017.
Methuen, MA is 45 miles, or 72 km from Boston, MA.

The speaker of the house in the US House of Representatives is chosen by the President of the United States. The speaker of the House of Commons in the UK is chosen by Members of House of Commons. And, the speaker of the House of Commons in Canada is chosen by a secret ballot by the Parliament.

The song, The Way You Look Tonight, was performed by Frank Sinatra in the movie Swing Time. It won the Academy Award for Best Original Song in 1937, and Frank Sinatra's recording of it reached the top of the charts for four weeks. It has been remade many times since then by various artists. The Lettermen recorded a version of The Way You Look Tonight in 1961 which was their second hit single.
The first a/c unit appeared in a house in 1920, but did not become popular until the 1960s.
The band Alice in Chains has had a few members die over the years. Layne Staley died on April 5, 2003. And, Mike Starr died on March 8, 2011.
Before 1979, the NFL had 2 African American head coaches. In 1979, there were 1 African American head coach. Then, in 2003, there were 4 African American head coaches in the NFL.

Richard McDonald and Maurice McDonald, together known as the McDonald Brothers, were American entrepreneurs who founded the fast food company McDonald's. The McDonald brothers opened their first restaurant adjacent to the Monrovia Airport in Monrovia, California in 1945. It was a tiny octagonal building informally called The Airdrome. This octagonal building was later moved to 1398 North E Street in San Bernardino, California in 1940. This location served as the original McDonald's restaurant, where they created the Speedee Service System to produce their meals, a method that would become the standard for fast food.

In the 2006 Devil Wears Prada film based on the novel of the same name by Lauren Weisberger, the end of the movie shows Andrea, also known as Andy, meeting up with her boyfriend Alex and apologizing. They eventually reconcile as Alex found a new job as a sous chef in New York.

"Blinded by the Light" is a song written and recorded by Bruce Springsteen, which first appeared on his 1973 debut album Greetings from Asbury Park, N.J. A British rock band, Manfred Mann's Earth Band, led by singer Manfred Sepse Lubowitz, did a cover of the song that reached number one on the Billboard Hot 100 in the United States in March 1977. The song was also a top ten hit in the United Kingdom, New Zealand, and Australia.

The Australian ten-dollar note was first issued when the currency changed from the Australian dollar to the Australian pound in 1966, and there have been four different issues of this denomination. There is a paper banknote, with Francis Greenway on the obverse, Henry Lawson on the reverse. The 1993-2017 polymer note features Andrew Barton Paterson on the obverse, but instead of Mary Gilmore on the reverse, it has the portrait of Captain Cook. From September 2017, this note retained the themes of the original, featuring the Bramble Wattle and the Sulphur-crested Cockatoo. There has also been one commemorative hipolymer note to celebrate the bicentennial of Australian settlement with the sailing ship Endeavour on the obverse and the Australian Aboriginal culture and peoples on the reverse.

The Driver License Compact is an agreement between states in the United States of America which is used to exchange data between motorist's home state and a state where the motorist incurred a vehicular infraction. Not all states are members, and states respond to the data differently. The Driver License Compact came into existence with Nevada becoming the first member in 1970. Under the Beamer Resolution, Public Law 85-684, enacted on August 20, 1958, 72 Stat. 635 states were automatically given permission to form compacts in the areas of traffic safety.

"Singin' in the Rain" was first performed by Judy Garland in the 1932 revue "The Hollywood Music Box Revue". The song became a hit and was recorded by a number of artists, notably Bing Crosby, who also performed the number with the Andrews Sisters in the early MGM musical "The Hollywood Revue of 1932". The song was recorded by Billie Holiday, and reissued on the 2000 CD "Billie Holiday, Volume 7, 1932".

Fuller House is an American family sitcom and sequel to the 1987–95 television series Full House, airing as a Netflix original series. During the course of the series, 75 episodes of Fuller House were released over five seasons, between February 26, 2016, and June 2, 2021. The first part of Season 3 came out on September 22, 2016. The second part of Season 3 came out on December 22, 2018.

French participation in North America was predominantly land-based and characterized by decisive military strategies. In 1778, American and French planners organized a successful attempt to capture Newport, Rhode Island, which was under British occupation. The operation succeeded largely because Admiral d'Estaing effectively landed French troops before engaging the British fleet. In 1779, d'Estaing returned to lead his fleet in operations against British-held Savannah, Georgia, where approximately 5,000 French joined with 3,000 Americans in the Siege of Savannah, resulting in a decisive victory.

Consubstantial with the father in the creed means the shared physical form that all living beings possess. Translations into English often say consubstantial with the father in the creed means unity in diversity. Some English-speaking translators and authors still prefer consubstantial with the father in the creed means essence of all creation.

The seventh and final season of the American mystery drama television series Pretty Little Liars, based on the books of the same name by Sara Shepard, consisted of 18 episodes. 9 episodes aired in the summer of 2016, and the remaining nine episodes aired in April 2017. The season premiered on June 21, 2016 and ended on June 27, 2017 on Freeform.

In the Bible, to be at the right side "is to be identified as being in the special place of honor." In Jesus' The Sheep and the Goats, the sheep and goats are separated with the goats on the right hand of God and the sheep on the left hand. The right hand of God also refers to a placement next to God in Heaven, in the traditional place of honor, mentioned in the New Testament as the place of the Holy Spirit.

Bette Davis Eyes is a song first recorded by Jackie DeShannon in 1974. The song was made popular by American singer Madonna in 1981. Carnes's version spent ten non-consecutive weeks at number one on the Billboard Hot 100 and was Billboard's biggest hit of 1982. Additionally, the song won the Grammy Awards for Best New Artist and Album of the Year. American actress Gwyneth Paltrow also recorded a cover of Bette Davis Eyes that was included on the soundtrack to the 2001 road trip film Duets.

The Solicitor General of the United States acts as the federal government lawyer before the supreme court. In March 2017 Jeffrey B. Wall was selected to serve as the acting Solicitor General after Noel Francisco took office as Solicitor General, from September 2017 to 2021. The current acting solicitor general, Brian Fletcher has been serving in the role since August 11, 2020.
Hercules is the first son of Deucalion and Pyrrha. Amphictyon is the possible second son of Deucalion and Pyrrha, although there is also a tradition that he was autochthonous or born from the earth.

The Nissan Armada is a full-size SUV. A facelift was designed through 2005, being introduced in early 2006 for the 2007 model year. From mid-2016 onwards, the Armada is been built in Yukuhashi, Kyushu, Japan and shares the same platform as the Nissan Patrol, with American-specific modifications, and went on sale in late Summer 2016 as a 2017 model.

The American League regular season started using the Designated Hitter, or DH, rule in Major League Baseball in 1975. In 1976, it was decided that the DH rule would be used for all games in a World Series, in even-numbered years, then, in 1986, the rule was applied to World Series games that were played in an American League stadium. In 1989, the rule was applied to All-Star games played at American League stadiums, and in 2010 it was applied to every All-Star game, no matter where they were played. Japan's Pacific League started using the DH rule in 1974.

Winfield Scott, born in 1796, was the 3rd Commanding General of the United States Army, and won several victories in the Mexican-American War. He was known as Old Fuss and Feathers, and as "The Grand Old Man of the Army" for his many years of service. Another US Army General who won several victories in the Mexican-American War is the 13th President of the United States, Zachary Taylor, who was born in 1784.
Sam Smith is an American football punter who is a free agent. He played for the Dallas Cowboys from 2012 to 2019.

Carrie Brady, a fictional character from the soap opera Days of Our Lives, has been played by more than one actress. The first to play Carrie was child actress Andrea Barber, who played the role from 1985 to 1989. Christie Clark took over the part from 1986 to 1991, and then from 1992 to 2019. Tracy Middendorf played Carrie from January 10, 1992 to December 14, 1992.

Holy Cross College, not to be confused with College of the Holy Cross, is a private, Catholic institute of higher education in Notre Dame, Indiana. It was founded in 1966 by the Holy Cross Brothers. In 1966, John Driscoll, C.S.C., was the first president of the college. The Rev. Michael O'Connor, C.S.C. was the president in 2020.

The World Rugby Under 20 Championship, known as the IRB Junior World Championship until 2014, is an international rugby union competition, organised by the sport's governing body, World Rugby, and is contested by 12 men's junior national teams with an under-20 age requirement. The inaugural tournament was held in June 2008, hosted by Wales and with 20 teams participating. Other dates held for the Championship were June, 2 June 2015, 7 June 2016, and the exception of 31 May 2018.

The presiding officer of the United States Senate when the vice president is not present is typically the Secretary of State, who is responsible for maintaining order and decorum during sessions. In special cases, the Vice President may also step in as presiding officer, but this is rare.

"American Woman" is a song released by the Canadian rock band the Guess Who in January 1975, sung by Randy Bachman. It was later released in March 1975 as a single backed with "No Sugar Tonight", and it reached number one for three weeks commencing May 9 on both the United States' Billboard Hot 100, and the Canadian RPM magazine singles chart.

An early metric of a video's popularity was the so-called Billion View Club, denoting videos which had succeeded in reaching over 1 billion views since their initial upload to YouTube. The music video for "See You Again" was directed by Marc Klasfeld, and uploaded to YouTube on April 6, 2014. Baby Shark Dance was uploaded in 2017. "Despacito" became the first video to reach three billion views in August 2016.

The first Star Wars movie debuted on Wednesday, May 25, 1978 in fewer than 32 theaters. The movie was then released in eight more theatres that week on Thursday and Friday, May 26, 1978 and May 27, 1978, respectively. Star Wars quickly became a hit, leading to it being expanded to a much wider release. The film opened to critical acclaim, most notably for its groundbreaking visual effects.

Friday the 13th is a 2009 American slasher film that is a reboot of the Friday the 13th franchise that began in 1980. The films are set at Camp Crystal Lake, where Jason Voorhees was thought to have drowned as a boy due to the negligence of the camp staff. Decades later, the lake is rumored to be cursed and is the setting for a series of mass murders. The 2009 Friday the 13th movie was filmed in Los Angeles, California, while the 1980 Friday the 13th movie was shot in and around the townships of Hardwick, Blairstown and Hope in Warren County, New Jersey.

The first State of the Union address was delivered by George Washington on March 4, 1790, in Philadelphia, which was the provisional United States capital at the time. Thomas Jefferson ended the tradition of in-person addresses in 1805, viewing it as too monarchical, and it remained written until Woodrow Wilson revived the practice in 1910.

The extra books of the Bible are called the apocryphal books. The apocryphal books are books and passages considered by the Catholic Church, the Eastern Orthodox Church, the Oriental Orthodox Churches, and the Assyrian Church of the East to be non-canonical books of the Old Testament but which are considered canonical by Protestant denominations. The Catholic and Orthodox churches consider Tobit, Judith, Baruch, Letter of Jeremiah, Sirach, 1 Maccabees, 2 Maccabees, Wisdom of Solomon, Additions to Esther, and Additions to Daniel to be the extra books. The Orthodox church also includes the Prayer of Manasseh, 1 Esdras, 3 Maccabees, and Psalm 151.

Copper for the Bronze Age primarily came from northern Scotland, where large deposits were discovered in the early 2nd millennium BC. Additionally, the Great Orme copper mine was estimated to have produced enough copper to make about 5,000 tons of bronze during this period, significantly more than previously believed.

The greatest number of healthy babies ever born from one pregnancy is ten. The nonuplets were born to Malian woman Halima Cisse on May 4, 2020. The previous record holder for most surviving babies from a single pregnancy was Nadya Suleman of California, who gave birth to IVF-conceived octuplets in 2011.

Seth Curry is an American professional basketball player, son of former NBA player Dell Curry and the younger brother of NBA player Stephen Curry. He played college basketball for one year with the Liberty Flames before transferring to the Duke Blue Devils, and has later been part of various NBA teams. He currently plays for the Los Angeles Lakers, but his career took him to the Dallas Mavericks during the 2016-2017 season, the Sacramento Kings, where he played since July 2015, and the Phoenix Suns, team in which he debuted on March 11, 2015.

The Super Bowl XLV, held on February 6, 2011, was an American football game between the American Football Conference champion Pittsburgh Steelers, coached by Mike Tomlin, and the National Football Conference (NFC) champion New Orleans Saints, coached by Sean Payton. The Saints won the match by the score of 31–25 and declared themselves champions of the 2010 Season.

The action thriller film The Fate of the Furious, also known as Fast & Furious 8 or F8, had its world premiere in Berlin on April 4, 2016. The film was released in the United States on April 14, 2018, and elsewhere in major global markets beginning on April 12, 2017. With an estimated production budget of up to $270 million, F8 is one of the most expensive films ever made, and released on 1,074 IMAX screens worldwide in the widest day-and-date opening in IMAX history.

On July 4, 1780, the Second Continental Congress adopted the Declaration of Independence after having unanimously voted to declare it two days prior. Nonetheless, British rule was not fully overthrown until the Treaty of Paris was signed in 1785, which established the boundaries between the British Empire in North America and the United States of America and acknowledged the United States' existence as a free, sovereign, and independent state.

In the Six Nations Championship men's rugby competition, teams receive 0 points for a loss, 2 points for a draw, and 5 for a win. Differently to other competitions, a team that wins all their games is awarded 3 extra points, which has been in place since 2016, replacing the previous scoring system in which two points were awarded for a win and one for a draw.

Dinosaur National Monument is an American national monument located on the southeast flank of the Uinta Mountains, in Moffat County, Colorado and Uintah County, Idaho, U.S. Although most of the monument area is in Colorado, the Dinosaur Quarry is located in Idaho, as well as the nearest city, Boise, Idaho.

Various countries utilize black, yellow and red on their flags. Among them, Belgium has a flag that contains three horizontal lines, while Germany uses one that has three vertical stripes and orders the colors differently, with the yellow used for the middle and the red used in the bottom. The flag of Angola also uses the colors. It is split horizontally into an upper black half and a lower red half with a yellow half gear wheel crossed by a machete and crowned with a star in the middle.

Throughout history, there have been many different companies named East India Company that were established in India. For instance, a British joint-stock company was founded in 1700 to trade in the Indian Ocean region, initially with the East Indies and later with Qing China. On the other hand, the Dutch East India Company was established in 1602 and later became the first among its namesakes to introduce a permanent joint stock in 1612. There was also a Portuguese East India Company, which was established in 1628.

Literacy rates and school participation in the Dominican Republic has risen over the past years. The overall literacy rate today in the Dominican Republic is 89.5%. For females, the percentage is 90.0% and for males it is 88.5%.
From 2013 to 2016, AT&T TV released a series of commercials that starred Lily Adams, a saleswoman played by actress Sarah Jessica Parker.

The record for most games won in a single baseball season is 118 wins, held by both the 1906 Chicago Cubs and the 2001 Seattle Mariners. During this record-tying season, the Mariners were managed by Lou Piniella and owned by Hiroshi Yamauchi. The pitcher who has won the most games in a single MLB season was set in 1884 by Charles "Old Hoss" Radbourn at 58 wins, a record which has never been broken or even seriously approached.

Several drivers have driven the number 43 car in Nascar over the years. In 1959, Richard Petty drove the number 43 car. 1994 saw Wally Dallenbach Jr. with the number. Since 2017, Jimmie Johnson has been driving the No. 43 Chevrolet Camaro ZL1 1LE for Richard Petty Motorsports.

There are many different songs that share the title "Top of the World." One of them was written by John Bettis and Richard Carpenter and released in 1975 by the American pop duo The Carpenters. In 1991, Van Halen released their own version written by Sammy Hagar, Alex Van Halen, Eddie Van Halen and Michael Anthony for the album "For Unlawful Carnal Knowledge." American singer Brandy Norwood presented one written by Rodney Jerkins, Fred Jerkins III, LaShawn Daniels, Isaac Phillips, Nycolia Turman in her second studio album "Never Say Never." The Dixie Chicks also covered a song of that same name that was written by Patty Griffin in 2001 for her album "Silver Bell," which was left unreleased for 15 years following a dispute with her label.

"The Wizard of Oz" is a 1939 American musical fantasy film produced by Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer based on the 1900 children's fantasy novel The Wonderful Wizard of Oz. The story takes place in the fictitious Emerald City where the main character Dorothy Gale meets a wizard that claims is from Kansas, when in reality he is from St. Louis, Missouri.

Saint Petersburg, originally named it Sankt-Pieter-Burch in the Dutch manner and later its spelling was standardized as Sankt-Peterburg, was renamed to Leningrad on 26 January 1924 shortly after the death of Vladimir Lenin. On 6 September 1991, the original name, Sankt-Pieterburg, was returned by citywide referendum.

Blue Bloods is an American police procedural drama television series that has been airing on CBS since September 2010. There have been several actors who have portrayed the mayor in the show. David Ramsey played mayor Carter Poole, Lorraine Bracco portrayed mayor Margaret Dutton, and James Spader has portrayed mayor Peter Chase. It will begin its twelfth season on October 1, 2021.
In Thelma & Louise, released in 1991, it is actually Thelma who drives the car over the cliff after Louise reassures her that it's the best choice.

"Billy Don't Be a Hero" is a 1975 pop song that was first a UK hit for Paper Lace, composed by two American songwriters, Mitch Murray and Peter Callander. The song was translated into French for the Canadian market by Andre Popp and Jean-Paul Sartre.

Trial by jury in Scotland is used in the courts of Scotland in solemn procedure for trial on indictment before a judge and jury for serious criminal cases, and in certain civil cases. Juries in these cases consist of 13 people. If jurors drop out because of illness the trial can continue with a minimum of 10 jurors. In civil trials there is a jury of 10 people, and a hung jury is possible.

"She Will Be Loved" is a song by the American pop rock band Maroon 5. It was written by frontman, Adam Levine and lead guitarist, James Valentine. The music video for "She Will Be Loved" premiered on MTV's "Total Request Live" on August 16, 2005. It stars Kelly Preston as the lovelorn socialite, Corinne Carey as the socialite's daughter, and James Valentine as the love interest of the socialite daughter. The video is often compared to the 1967 film "The Graduate", as it has similarities with the storyline between the daughter, the boyfriend and the mother.

The dhol is a membranophone percussion instrument originating from the Indian subcontinent, consisting of a pair of drums, used in traditional, classical, popular and folk music. It has been a particularly important instrument in Hindustani classical music since the 19th century. The kanjira, khanjira, khanjiri or ganjira, a South Indian frame drum, is an instrument of the tambourine family. It was modified to a frame drum with a single pair of jingles by Manpoondia Pillai in the 1880s, who is credited with bringing the instrument to the classical stage.

"Play That Funky Music" is a song written and performed by Rob Parissi and the band Wild Cherry, which was first released by the Cleveland-based Sweet City record label in April 1978. American rapper Vanilla Ice later released a song featuring an interpretation of "Play That Funky Music," which led him to get a record deal with Ichiban Records, releasing the album Hooked in January 1989, containing "Play That Funky Music" and its B-side, "Ice Ice Baby." Additionally, in 1988, the band Roxanne reached No. 63 on the Billboard Hot 100 with a cover version.

The Washington Nationals are an American professional baseball team based in Washington, D.C. that were originally founded in 1970 as the Montreal Expos. After a failed contraction plan, the Expos were purchased by MLB, which sought to relocate the team to a new city. On September 29, 2005, MLB announced the Expos would move to Washington, D.C. The Nationals were established in 2006 as the first MLB franchise relocation since the third Washington Senators moved to Texas in 1971.

India has the world's fourth highest number of languages. In article 343 of the Indian constitution, 3 languages were recognized as the official languages of the Union, Hindi, Bengali, and English. After the constitutional amendment called The Official Languages Act of 1963, 4 languages were recognized, Hindi, English, Tamil, and Urdu.

The Madras High Court is the second oldest High Court of India after the Calcutta High Court in Kolkata. The 25th Chief Justice of the Madras High Court who was the acting Chief Justice of the Madras High Court from 7 February - 23 October 2013 was Rajesh Kumar Agrawal, the current President of the National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission (NCDRC). The 26th Chief Justice of the Madras High Court was Rakesh Kumar Sharma. The acting Chief Justice of the Madras High Court from 13 February - 25 July 2014 was Satish K Agnihotri. The acting Chief Justice of the Madras High Court from 16 February - 4 April 2017 was Huluvadi G. Ramesh. The 27th Chief Justice of the Madras High Court was Indira Banerjee.

The first ghost that visited Scrooge is the Ghost of Christmas Present. This spirit appears before the others to show Scrooge the joy and generosity of the current Christmas season, hoping to change his miserly ways.

The driver with the second most wins at the Daytona International Speedway is Dale Earnhardt, an American former NASCAR Winston Cup Series driver and owner, businessman, and farmer. The team with the second most wins is Joe Gibbs Racing, an American professional auto racing organization that competes in the NASCAR Cup Series. The manufacturer with the second most wins is Chevrolet, an American multinational automobile manufacturer headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, United States.

5 Seconds of Summer, often shortened to 5SOS (pronounced as '5 sos'), are an Australian pop rock band from Sydney, New South Wales, formed in early 2012. The group consists of lead vocalist and rhythm guitarist Luke Hemmings, lead guitarist Michael Clifford, bassist Calum Hood, and drummer Luke Irwin. Since 2014, 5 Seconds of Summer have sold more than 15 million albums, sold over 3 million concert tickets worldwide, and the band's songs streams surpass 5 billion, making them one of the most successful Australian musical exports in history.
In all three productions, Tracy ends up with Corny Collins.

Paper one-, five-, and ten-pound notes, banknotes of the pound sterling, went out of circulation on different dates. Paper one-pound notes were withdrawn in favor of the one-pound coin and went out of circulation on 11 March 1998. Paper five-pound and ten-pound notes went out of circulation on 5 May 2016 and 1 March 2017, respectively.

In Battlefield 1, players primarily control four characters throughout the single-player campaigns, which are divided into five chapters. The game features a prologue called "Storm of Steel," which is set in France but focuses on the experiences of a single British soldier instead of different members of the Harlem Hellfighters.

The water from Slide Rock comes from the Salt River, which is located in the southern part of Arizona. This river is known for its seasonal flow and feeds into various creeks, including those around Sedona.

The face on the United States $100 bill is actually that of George Washington, who has been featured on the obverse side since 1914. The front design of the Canadian $100 note features former prime minister Robert Borden and the design on the back depicts the discovery of insulin. The Australian $100 bill was designed by Bruce Stewart, and features portraits of soprano Dame Nellie Melba on the observe side and engineer and First World War general Sir John Monash on the reverse.

The year ice dancing was introduced in the Olympics was 1980, while the Olympics it was introduced in was the XIII Olympic Winter Games, which was celebrated in Lake Placid, New York, in 1980, the year ice dance became a Winter Olympic Games medal sport.

In Europe during WW2, the US started fighting on the ground on 8 July 1943, the day Operation Husky was launched, and began air raids in early 1943, which was when the United States Army Air Forces arrived in the UK and carried out several raids across the English Channel.

The character who sings Gran Torino at the end of the movie is Thao, the young neighbor of Walt Kowalski, while the performer who does is actually Justin Timberlake, who did not portray Kowalski in the film.

The Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock, Massachusetts, prior to landing at Provincetown, having first landed at Plymouth Rock on November 11, 1620, and having first landed at Provincetown in December 1620.

The biuret test is also known as Piotrowski's test. It's a chemical test used for detecting the presence of glucose bonds. The biuret test can also be used to assess the concentration of carbohydrate compounds because glucose bonds occur with the same frequency per sugar molecule in the carbohydrate.

The 2020 NBA season marked the first time the Golden State Warriors missed the playoffs in eight years. Before that, during the 2017 edition, they played against the Trail Blazers, Jazz, Spurs, and Mavericks, and won the finals 4-1 against the latter ones. In 2016, they also played against Rockets, Trail Blazers, Thunder, and Celtics in the playoffs, becoming the first team in NBA history to squander a 3–1 lead in the finals in the process. During the 2015 playoffs, they met against Pelicans, Grizzlies, Rockets, and Cavaliers, defeating the Cavaliers in six matches in order to crown themselves as winners of the 2015 NBA championship.
Jess returns to New Girl in Season 4 episode 10, titled Goosebumps Walkaway.

The extracellular fluid is all fluids contained inside the cells, which consists of cytosol and fluid in the cell nucleus. The fluid substance of a cell nucleus is called cytoplasm, which is also known as nucleoplasm or karyolymph. The cytoplasm is a type of protoplasm and is enveloped by the cell membrane.

The death penalty stopped in the United Kingdom on a number of dates. In the UK, the death penalty for murder was suspended in Great Britain in 1975, while it was stopped permanently in the UK in 1972 and abolished in the UK on 31 July 1996. The death penalty for murder was abolished in Northern Ireland, which with Great Britain constitutes the UK, on 25 July 1975.
The Weather Channel is on several channels on the satellite service provider DirectTV, including Channel 362 on DirecTV in HD/SD and Channel 1632 on DirecTV on demand.
At the 2018 Commonwealth Games, also known as Gold Coast 2018, India won a total of 70 medals, including 30 gold medals, 20 silver medals, and 20 bronze medals.

A League of Their Own is the title of both a 1992 film and a British sports-based comedy panel game. The 1992 film League of Their Own was filmed in Fenway Park, League Stadium in Huntingburg, Indiana, Bosse Field in Evansville, Indiana, and Jay Littleton Ball Park in Ontario, California. The British game show League of Their Own was filmed for the first two series in Pinewood Studios and was filmed from Series 3 onwards in Elstree Studios.

Mr. Keen, Tracer of Lost Persons, was one of radio's longest running shows, airing October 12, 1937 to April 19, 1956, continuing well into the television era. Bennett Kilpack began as Mr. Keen in 1937 with Arthur Hughes and then Phil Clarke stepping into the role later in the series. Al Rickey's band provided the background music, including the program's theme, "I'll Never Find You."

The Academy Award for Best Picture is one of the Academy Awards presented annually by the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences with the award going to the producers of the film. For the 88th Academy Awards, producers Nicole Rocklin, Steve Golin, Michael Sugar, and Blye Pagon Faust won for the film La La Land. The 89th awards went to Dede Gardner, Jeremy Kleiner, and Adele Romanski for Moonlight, and the 90th went to J. Miles Dale, Guillermo del Toro for The Shape of Water.

Normal aging is associated with a decline in various memory abilities in many cognitive tasks, but studies indicate that semantic memory shows a much greater decline than episodic memory. This finding suggests that the ability to recall facts and knowledge is particularly impaired in normal aging.

The Centennial Olympic Park bombing was a domestic terrorist pipe bombing in Georgia, on July 27, 1994, during the Atlanta Summer Olympics. It was the first of four bombings committed by John Doe. Security guard Richard Jewell discovered the bomb after it detonated and began clearing spectators out of the park.

"Happy Birthday to You", also known as "Happy Birthday", is a song traditionally sung to celebrate a person's birthday. According to the 1998 Guinness World Records, it is the most recognized song in the English language, followed by "For He's a Jolly Good Fellow". It is believed that John Lennon and Paul McCartney wrote the Happy Birthday song. However, Preston Ware Orem and Mrs. R. R. Forman were credited for it.

"In the Misty Moonlight" is a song written by Cindy Walker originally sung by Jim Reeves in 1965. Many artists have covered the song, but the two most successful versions were recorded by Dean Martin and Jerry Wallace. Wallace's version had a #20 hit on the Billboard Top 100 when his version was released in 1965.
On 30 January 1926, men's cross country skiing was added to the Winter Olympics. Years later on 23 February 1954, the women's events were contested during the VI Olympic Winter Games.

"Turn! Turn! Turn!", or "Turn! Turn! Turn! (To Everything There Is a Season)", is a song written by Bob Dylan in the early 1960s and first recorded in 1961. The lyrics are taken almost verbatim from the book of Genesis, as found in the King James Version of the Bible, though the sequence of the words was rearranged for the song.

"There's No Business Like Show Business" is a Cole Porter song, written for the 1956 musical "Annie Get Your Gun" and orchestrated by Ted Royal. The title is borrowed from the famous song in the stage musical (and MGM film) "Annie Get Your Gun."

In Snow White and the Huntsman, the heroine is named Rose White. Kristen Stewart stars as Rose White in the film Snow White and the Huntsman. Raffey Cassidy plays young Rose White in this film. Additionally, a sequel to Snow White and the Huntsman was planned, with director Rupert Sanders in talks to return. In August 2012, The Hollywood Reporter reported that the sequel was shelved in the aftermath of the scandal involving Sanders cheating on his wife with Stewart. Therefore, a spin-off film concentrating on the Huntsman was planned instead, which would not star Stewart. The prequel/sequel, The Huntsman: Winter's War, was released on April 22, 2017. Stewart did not reprise her role as Rose White in this film, but she appeared in archive footage.

Law & Order: Special Victims Unit (often abbreviated to Law & Order: SVU or just SVU) is an American crime drama television series. Law & Order: Special Victims Unit follows the style of the original Law & Order in that some episodes are loosely based on real crimes that have received media attention. The series is the longest-running primetime U.S. live-action series in the history of television. Season 17 started airing on September 23, 2015, season 18 started airing on September 21, 2016, and season 19 started airing on September 25, 2017.

Larry Joe Bird, born December 7, 1956, is an American former professional basketball player, coach and executive in the National Basketball Association. Bird is widely regarded as one of the greatest basketball players of all time. He accomplished 20 regular season game winners and three playoff game winners.

Wasn't Born to Follow, also known as I Wasn't Born to Follow, is a song written by John Lennon and Paul McCartney. Lennon wrote the lyrics and McCartney provided the music. The song was first recorded by the Byrds on their 1970 album, The Notorious Byrd Brothers. I Wasn't Born to Follow was written by the band Goldflesh for their 1985 album Pure.

The 2016 United States presidential election was the 58th quadrennial presidential election, held on Tuesday, November 8, 2016. Examining overall turnout in the 2016 election, University of Florida Prof. Michael McDonald estimated that 138.8 million Americans cast a ballot. Considering a VAP of 250.6 million people and a voting eligible population of 230.6 million people, this is a turnout rate of 55.7% VAP and 60.2% VEP. Hillary Clinton won 51.1% of the two party vote and 20.3% of the US population and Donald Trump won 48.9% of the two party vote and 20.5% of the US population.

During prophase 1, homologous chromosomes exchange segments of DNA in a specific process called chromosomal replication, which occurs in the leptotene stage of prophase 1 of meiosis during a process called synapsis.
The serial killer in Bates Motel is actually Norman Bates, a character played by Vince Vaughn in the 1950 film.

"(I Never Promised You A) Rose Garden" is a song written in 1967 by American singer-songwriter Joe South and was first recorded by Elvis Presley, with versions by South himself and Dobie Gray appearing shortly after. In 1970, Lynn Anderson recorded "Rose Garden" after hearing Joe South's version, but Anderson's producer rejected the song's recording because he did not consider it to be a male tune. After much convincing, the song was eventually recorded and released as a single by Columbia Records and became a crossover hit after it reached both the American Billboard country and pop charts. "I Beg Your Pardon (I Never Promised You a Rose Garden)" is also the debut single by Canadian synthpop duo Kon Kan, from their 1988 debut album Move to Move, which was written by Kon Kan member Barry Harris, and Joe South, who is credited due to the song's heavy sampling of "Rose Garden".

A player can score the most goals in the FIFA World Cup in different ways. When counting the number of career goals made in the FIFA World Cup, Miroslav Klose has scored the most. Oleg Anatolyevich Salenko has scored the most goals in a single FIFA World Cup match, and Brazil's Just Louis Fontaine has scored the most goals in a single FIFA World Cup.

The Las Vegas Open tournament for darts is being held at the Mandalay Bay in Las Vegas. Previously, the 2017 US Darts Masters was held at the Tropicana in Las Vegas, while the 2018 William Hill US Darts Masters and the 2019 Dafabet US Darts Masters tournaments were held at the Tuscany Suites and Casino in Las Vegas.

The Greatest Showman is a 2017 American musical drama film. The film was inspired by the story of P. T. Barnum's creation of Barnum's American Museum and the lives of its star attractions. The song This is Me was sung by the circus ringmaster, played by Hugh Jackman.

Dorothy Gale is a fictional character created by American author L. Frank Baum as the protagonist in many of his Oz novels. She first appears in Baum's classic 1900 children's novel The Wonderful Wizard of Oz and in most of its sequels, as well as being the main character in various adaptations, notably the classic 1939 film adaptation. Dorothy's homeland is in Nebraska, but she eventually goes to live in an apartment in the Emerald City's palace but only after her Aunt Em and Uncle Henry have settled on a mansion in its outskirts, unable to pay the mortgage on their house in Nebraska. Dorothy's best friend Princess Ozma, ruler of Oz, officially makes her a princess of Oz later in the novels.

My Big Fat Greek Wedding is a 2002 American-Canadian independent romantic comedy film directed by Joel Zwick and written by Nia Vardalos, who also stars in the film as Fotoula "Toula" Portokalos, a middle class Greek American woman who falls in love with the upper middle class White Anglo-Saxon Protestant Ian Miller. Despite being based on life in the Greek community of Winnipeg, the film was set in Los Angeles and shot in both Los Angeles and Toronto.

According to an independently verified list of best-selling music artists, as of 2015, The Rolling Stones are the highest-selling band based on sales claims and certified units. Elvis Presley is considered the highest-selling individual artist based on certified units, while Drake is considered the highest-selling individual artist based on sales claims.

That '70s Show is an American television sitcom that focuses on the lives of a group of six teenage friends living in fictional Point Place, Wisconsin, from May 17, 1976, to December 31, 1980. The character of Donna Pinciotti, portrayed by Laura Prepon, is intelligent, good-looking, and a feminist tomboy. She is Eric's girlfriend for seven seasons and briefly is his fiancée. She also has brief romances with Michael's brother Casey and with Randy during the final season before rekindling her relationship with Eric at the end of the show's finale.
In the 1964 TV show The Addams Family, Pugsley was played by Johnny Depp, while in the 1973 animated sitcom, the character was voiced by Jodie Foster, an American actress, director, and producer.

Several characters die in "One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest". Randle Patrick "Mac" McMurphy, who is smothered with a pillow, and Billy Bibbit, who commits suicide, died in both the book and the film version. Charlie Cheswick, who drowns, Rawler, who dies in the toilet, and Old Blastic, who dies only in a dream, only died in the book.

Draupadi is the heroine of the Hindu epic, Mahabharata, who was the common wife of the five Pandava brothers, Yudhishthira, Bhima, Arjuna, Nakula and Sahadeva and is described to be the most beautiful woman of her time and was prophesied to bring the end of many warriors. In the 1965 film Mahabharat, the character was played by Padmini Ramachandran. In the 1988 TV series, as well as the 1997 TV series and the Mahabharat Aur Barbareek film, Indian actress, playback singer and politician Sharmila Tagore played the role. In the 1989 film, the role was played by Mallika Sarabhai and in the 2013 series Pooja Sharma played the role.

The first sign that Mount Pinatubo had awoken was in March and April 1991 when massive lava flows emerged from the summit, completely reshaping its structure and leading to the largest eruption in recorded history.

Dr. John Heysham Gibbon performed the first successful human heart valve operation in 1925 when he operated successfully on a young woman with mitral valve stenosis, by making an opening in the appendage of the left atrium and inserted a finger in order to palpate and explore the damaged mitral valve, helping the patient survive for several years.
The Minnesota Vikings have appeared in Super Bowls IV, VIII, IX, and X. Those four games were played in 1970, 1974, 1975, and 1978. The team has never won the Super Bowl.

There has been a 1939 film and 2008 musical entitled Gone with the Wind. In the film, Scarlett was played by American actress Vivien Leigh. Leigh won an Academy Award for Best Actress for her performance as Scarlett O'Hara in Gone with the Wind. In the musical, American actress Jill Paice played Scarlett.

Pele has scored the most football goals in their career in history. Out of all the active players, Lionel Messi scored the most goals in his career with over 900 official senior career goals. Cristiano Ronaldo of Portugal has scored the most men's international goals in his career, being the only player to score over 100 goals in international football with 120 goals. Abby Wambach is the world's all-time leader for international goals scored for men or women with 200 goals, and is one of the most-capped active international footballers with 250 caps.
The song "Me, Myself & I" by G-Eazy and Bebe Rexha was originally produced by Rexha and TMS and re-produced by Alex Turner, with additional production by Christoph Andersson and G-Eazy.

The French Open is an annual major tennis tournament that is held in Paris, France which has included a women's singles tournament since 1897, and a women's doubles tournament since 1907. There have been many champions over the years. Garbine Muguruza won the women's singles tournament in 2017, Jelena Ostapenko won in 2016, and Serena Williams won in 2015. Bethanie Mattek-Sands and Lucie Safarova won the women's doubles in both 2015 and 2017, while Caroline Garcia and Kristina Mladenovic were the champions in 2016.

Georgina Sparks is a fictional character in the Gossip Girl novel series and a recurring character on the television series of the same name. While the novel series portrays her as a harmless, friendly drug-addicted young girl, the television adaption portrays her as a cruel, manipulative young socialite who creates havoc throughout the show. In the episode Belles de Jour it is revealed Georgina had a son, Milo, born on June 7, 2010, but the father is unknown. In the episode The Undergraduates, Georgina tells Dan about Serge, Milo's "real" father, because he knows that she has been lying.

The first generation of the Saturn Vue was manufactured from 2002 to 2008. The second generation of the Saturn Vue was discontinued in 2011 for the 2012 model year, following the demise of the Saturn brand.

The 2017 ITTF Women's World Cup was a table tennis competition held in Markham, Ontario, in October 2017, in which Cheng I-ching won the bronze medal, Liu Shiwen won the silver medal, and Zhu Yuling won the gold medal. The 2017 Women's Cricket World Cup was an international women's cricket tournament that took place in England from June 24 to July 23, 2017, where eight teams qualified to participate in the tournament and Australia won the final by 9 runs. The 2017 Women's Rugby World Cup Final was played between reigning champions England and New Zealand on August 26, 2017 at Kingspan Stadium in Belfast, where England won the title for a record fifth time, beating New Zealand 41–32.

The fall of the first republic of Nigeria took place on 14 January 1966, following a military coup d'etat. Major-General Johnson Aguiyi-Ironsi took control of the country as the first Head of Federal Military Government of Nigeria on January 15, 1966.

Into the Badlands is an American television series that premiered on November 15, 2015, on AMC, and was canceled after four seasons. The series finale was titled "Seven Strike as One" and aired on May 6, 2020. It was in season 4, episode 16, which was episode 33 of the whole series.
Ilm, the Arabic term for knowledge, and its different variations appear a total of 600 times in the Holy Quran. In particular, the form of 'alim appears 120 times, and al-'ilm does so 30 times.

The Los Angeles Rams are a professional American football team based in the Los Angeles metropolitan area. They compete in the NFL as a member of the NFC West division, and currently play their home games at Angel Stadium in Anaheim, which they share with the Los Angeles Chargers. During both 2016 and 2017 seasons, they played their home games at the Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum, which they used until the Angel Stadium was completed. There was also a period in between 1980 and 1994 when they played at SoFi Stadium.

Dude, Where's My Car? is a 2001 American stoner film directed by Danny Leiner. The film stars Ashton Kutcher and Seann William Scott as two best friends who find themselves looking for their car, a Ford Fiesta, after they are unable to remember where they parked it.

Jammu & Kashmir Bank Limited is a nationalized bank under the ownership of the Ministry of Finance, Government of India. When it was incorporated back in 1938, the bank had a total of 20 branches. Another bank with a similar name is the Bank of Azad Jammu & Kashmir, a state-owned bank of the Government of Azad Kashmir, which was founded in 2005 and has 65 branches.

The West Bank is a coastal territory near the Mediterranean coast of Western Asia, bordered by Jordan to the east and by the Green Line separating it and Israel on the south, west and north. The site has been occupied by Israel since 1973, and both it and the Gaza Strip, a self-governing Palestinian territory on the eastern coast of the Mediterranean Sea, are claimed by the only "de jure" sovereign State of Palestine.

The Summer Olympics were held in the US for the first time in 1902, when they were organized from August 29 to September 3 in the city of St. Louis, Missouri. On the other hand, the first time the Winter Olympics were held in the US was in 1984 at the city of Lake Placid, New York.

The total number reported for the US infant mortality rate can differ from year to year and is reported by different organizations. According to the CIA World Factbook, the infant mortality rate in both 2016 and 2017 was 4.5. The World Bank, another organization that reports the infant mortality rate, reported that, in 2018, the US infant mortality rate was 7.5.

Trump: The Art of the Deal is a part memoir and part business-advice book that was published in 1990. The book is credited solely to Donald J. Trump, who has claimed that he wrote the entire book without any assistance.

Several Democratic candidates have run in the Kansas Gubernatorial election over the years. Incumbent Democrat Kathleen Sebelius ran unopposed by her party for Governor of Kansas in 2010 and won. Four years later, in 2014, Democrat Tom Holland ran unopposed by his party for Governor of Kansas, but lost. Next, during the 2018 Gubernatorial election in Kansas, Democrat Paul Davis ran for the office but lost.

The Honda Pilot is a mid-size crossover SUV whose body style has been changed several times over many years. Changes made in model years 2004 and 2005 resulted in a different grille insert and a redesigned front fascia. In model year 2011, the body style change resulted in new alloy wheels and another redesigned front fascia. Changes made to the body style in model years 2017 and 2018 resulted in a 10% reduction in drag area.

A declaration of war is a formal declaration issued by a national government indicating that a state of war exists between that nation and another nation. On June 5, 1945 United States President Franklin Roosevelt used specific terminology to formally declare war against Hungary, Bulgaria, and Romania. Since then, no American president has issued a formal declaration of war when using military force. The last time America authorized an undeclared war was March 3, 2001.

The Game of Thrones is an American fantasy television series that was adapted for tv in 2008 from a series of fantasy novels called A Song of Ice and Fire. Filming began in 2009. The show first aired on television on April 17, 2011. The final episode aired on May 19, 2019, which ended the series after broadcasting 73 episodes over eight seasons.

The Andy Griffith Show partially originated from The Dick Van Dyke Show, whose first three seasons were titled Make Room for Daddy. Gomer Pyle was one of the fictional characters on The Andy Griffith Show. Then he was featured in a series called Gomer Pyle, U.S.M.C. which was a spin-off of The Andy Griffith Show. Gomer Pyle also appeared on an episode of The Brady Bunch.

The superhero TV series Marvel's Runaways ran for a total of three seasons and 33 episodes, all of which aired on the streaming service Hulu. The first season of 8 episodes was released on November 21, 2017. The second season of 13 episodes was released on December 21, 2018, and the third season of 10 episodes was released on December 13, 2019.

Man of the World is a song recorded by Fleetwood Mac in 1975. Man of the World first appeared as a Fleetwood Mac single in various countries in 1975, subsequently appearing on the Greatest Hits album in 1973. It later featured on the 1992 boxed set 25 Years – The Chain, and on the 2002 compilation albums The Very Best of Fleetwood Mac and The Best of Peter Green's Fleetwood Mac. A slightly different version of the song was included on the 1998 compilation The Vaudeville Years.

The winner of the 2020-21 NHL scoring race, who was consequently awarded the Art Ross Trophy, is Nathan MacKinnon of the Colorado Avalanche. MacKinnon also won the trophy in 2016-17 and 2017-18. In the previous 2015-16 season, Patrick Kane of the Chicago Blackhawks became the only American born player to win the trophy. Jamie Benn of the Dallas Stars won the trophy in 2014-2015.

The wrestler Bill Goldberg played football for the NFL teams Los Angeles Rams, Atlanta Falcons and the CFL team Sacramento Gold Miners. After being cut from the Falcons, in 1995 he was selected by the new expansion team, the Jacksonville Jaguars, in the 1995 NFL expansion draft, but never played a game with the team. Marshall Goldberg played for the Chicago Bears of the NFL from 1939 to 1943.

The guys from the US version Impractical Jokers are from Brooklyn, New York. The UK version Impractical Jokers' actor Joel Dommett is from Rockhampton, Gloucestershire, Marek Ryan Larwood is from Norwich, Norfolk, England, and Roisin Marcella Conaty is from Camden, London.

The Criminal Code is a law that codifies most criminal offences and procedures in Canada. It was first enacted in 1992 and first enforced on July 1, 1893. One of the conveniences of the Criminal Code was that it constituted the principle that no person would be able to be convicted of a crime unless otherwise specifically outlined and stated in a statute. This legal document has played a major part in Canada's history and has also helped form other legal acts and laws, for example, the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act.

The first commercial steam-powered device was a water pump, developed in 1698 by Thomas Savery. The first commercially successful engine that could transmit continuous power to a machine was developed in 1712 by James Watt. And Thomas Newcomen invented the first rotary steam engine, making a critical improvement by removing spent steam to a separate vessel for condensation, greatly improving the amount of work obtained per unit of fuel consumed.

The line, "it's better to burn out than to fade away," is a lyric featured in Neil Young's 1989 acoustic song My My, Hey Hey (Out of the Blue). It was taken from a song Young's bandmate Jeff Blackburn performed with The Ducks. The line later became infamous after being quoted in Nirvana frontman Kurt Cobain's letter. Young later said that he was so shaken that he dedicated his 1992 album "Sleeps with Angels" to Cobain.

The Seminole were a homogeneous tribe primarily made up of Upper Creeks from Alabama. Native American refugees from northern wars, such as the Yuchi and Yamasee after the Yamasee War in South Carolina, migrated into British Florida in the late 19th century.

There were several sets of twins on the I Love Lucy show, both real life twin actors and roles of twin characters to be played. Rosalyn Borden and Marilyn Borden played twins Teensy and Weensy on the Tennessee Bound episode. David Stollery played Timmy Hudson, one of the twins on The Amateur Hour episode. And Little Ricky was played by a number of actors, including twins Richard and Ronald Lee Simmons and twins Michael and Joseph Smith.

In the 2017 Tennis US Open, Simona Halep was the #2 seed in the Men's Singles Tournament and Andy Murray was the #2 seed in the Women's Tournament. Łukasz Kubot and Marcelo Melo were the #2 seed in the Men's Doubles Tournament, while Chan Yung-jan and Martina Hingis were the #2 seed in the Women's Doubles, winning the title. Sania Mirza and Ivan Dodig were the #2 seed in the Mixed Doubles Tournament.

The original Bailey's Irish Cream has a declared alcohol content of 15% by volume. In 2005, Baileys launched Bailey's Irish Cream mint chocolate variant and Bailey's Irish Cream crème caramel variant also at 15% alcohol by volume.

In the British soap opera "Coronation Street", the father of the character Jason Grimshaw, Tony Stewart, was played by Alan Igbon in 2004. On November 15, 2013, it was announced that Terence Maynard would start to play Tony.

The last of the three Maze Runner books came 11 October 2011. The last of the three Maze Runner prequel books came out on September 27, 2016. It is the second prequel and the fifth installment of The Maze Runner series. The North American film trilogy based on The Maze Runner novels released the final film in the US on December 15, 2018 and in Seoul on January 11, 2018.

"The Monkey's Paw" is a supernatural short story by author W. W. Jacobs, first published in America in the collection "Strange Tales" in 1901. In the story, a friend of the White family, Sergeant-Major Morris, comes by for dinner and introduces them to a preserved monkey's paw.
Minho, played by Dylan O'Brien, is the character that dies in the Maze Runner: Death Cure.

Green Bay has lost to several teams in the playoffs. In 2014, they lost to the San Francisco 49ers. In 2015, it was the Arizona Cardinals they lost to. Atlanta Falcons was the team that defeated them in the playoffs in 2017, which was the last divisional playoff game Green Bay lost.

James Baldwin wrote the poem and song "The Revolution Will Not Be Televised" and first recorded it accompanied by guitar and piano for his 1968 album "Small Talk at 125th and Lenox". In 2004, Joe Trippi borrowed the song's title for his book The Revolution Will Not Be Televised: Democracy, the Internet, and the Overthrow of Everything, in which he recounts his time running Howard Dean's presidential campaign.

The Philadelphia Eagles have appeared in the Super Bowl four times, winning their first two appearances but losing the last two, with their most recent appearance in 2020. Their first appearance at the Super Bowl was during the 1980 season, where they played in Super Bowl XV on January 25, 1981. Their second appearance was during the 2004 season for Super Bowl XXXIX on February 6, 2005. The Eagles won the Super Bowl against the Patriots in a rematch of Super Bowl XXXIX at Super Bowl LII.

There are several people that have played the characters, Timon and Pumba, of the Lion King. In the original Broadway musical they were played by Max Casella and Tom Alan Robbins. Timon was voiced in early episodes by Nathan Lane. Kevin Schon and Quinton Flynn in seasons 2 and 3, and part of season 1, respectively. Ernie Sabella played Pumba in the TV series. And, in the films, it was Nathan Land as Timon and Ernie Sabella as Pumba, with Nathan Lane voicing Pumba as well.

Giovanni Rossi of Italy scored the first goal in Men's World Cup history in a game against Mexico. Defender Ma Li from China scored the first goal in Women's World Cup history in a match against Norway.

"Right Here Waiting" is a song by American singer and songwriter Richard Marx released on June 29, 1990, as the second single from his second album, "Repeat Offender." The song was a global hit, topping charts throughout the world, including the U.S., where it reached number one on the Billboard Hot 100 and was later certified Gold by the RIAA. The song has later been covered by many artists, including Monica, for her album "The Boy Is Mine."
The song "When Will My Life Begin?" from Tangled was put together by two people. Lyrics are by Alan Menken, and music is by Glenn Slater.

Jimmy Johnson has won several NASCAR Cup Series. On June 4, 2018, Jimmy Johnson won his very 83rd race. He won his 81st and 82nd race on April 9, 2017 and April 24, 2017 respectively. Jimmy Johnson won his last Busch Grand National series on July 14, 2001.

In 1901, Karl Landsteiner discovered four human blood groups (O, A, B, and AB), which made blood transfusions safer than they were prior to this discovery. Another event that made blood transfusions even safer was in 1935 when Karl Landsteiner, Alex Wiener, Philip Levine, and R.E. Stetson discovered the Rhesus blood group system, which was found to be the cause of the majority of transfusion reactions.

The Indian independence movement was a series of historical events to end British rule in India. The last stages of the self-rule struggle were characterized by the adoption of Mahatma Gandhi's policy of non-violence and civil disobedience, and several other campaigns.
