It all culminated on 15 August 1946, when, at 12:02 A.M., India officially became a sovereign and democratic nation when the Indian Independence Act was signed. The act finally gained royal assent on 18 July 1946, and India remained a Dominion of the Crown until 26 January 1950, when the "Constitution of India" came into force, establishing the Republic of India.

The Right to Information, or RTI, is an act of the Parliament of India which sets out the rules and procedures regarding citizens' right to information, which replaced the former Freedom of Information Act on 12-October-2004 and enshrines stronger provisions than those seen in individual state level RTI Acts. The Jammu & Kashmir Right to Information Act 2009 commenced on 20-03-2009, replacing the Jammu & Kashmir Right to Information Act, 2004, which was enacted on 7 January 2004. The enactment of the state-level Jammu & Kashmir Act came within the larger context of the Right to Information movement in India, which resulted in the passage of several state-level RTI Acts across India.

The song Live Like You Were Dying was released on June 7, 2005. It was part of Tim McGraw's album with the same name, that was released on August 24, 2004. A song with a similar title, Live Like We're Dying, written by Danny O'Donoghue, Andrew Frampton, Mark Sheehan and Steve Kipner, was released on September 21, 2009.

Prior to 2019, if a goal kick does not leave the penalty area, the kick is considered in-play immediately. In 2019, the requirement that the ball had to leave the penalty area was reinforced, meaning the kick has to be retaken if it does not exit the area.

The USSR formally agreed to a unified Germany inside of NATO on 12 September 1989, when they signed the Treaty of the Final Settlement with Respect to Germany. This agreement was crucial for the German Democratic Republic to join the Federal Republic of Germany, leading to the country's reunification.

CCC is an abbreviation used multiple times throughout different religions. In Catholicism, it stands for the Council of Catholic Churches, which sums up, in book form, the beliefs of the Catholic faithful. The Celestial Church of Christ is an African Initiated Church founded by Samuel Bilewu Joseph Oshoffa on 29 September 1945 in Porto-Novo, Benin. The Church of Christ in the Congo is a union of 52 Protestant denominations in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

The saying "all quiet on the western front" comes from a French novel by Erich Maria titled Im Osten nichts Neues, which describes the French soldiers' extreme physical and mental stress during the war, and the detachment from civilian life felt by many of these soldiers upon returning home from the front. The English translation of the title done by Arthur Wesley Wheen is "All Quiet on the Eastern Front."

Pati Jinich from Pati's Mexican Table lives in San Antonio, Texas, with her husband and their two sons. Jinich was born and raised in Mexico City in a Jewish Mexican family. She first began researching and cooking Mexican cuisine out of homesickness for her native Mexico City when she moved to Dallas, Texas, with her husband. Two years later, she relocated to Washington, DC, with her husband and their first-born son. Here she earned her master's degree from Georgetown University and worked as a political analyst for the Inter-American Dialogue before enrolling at L'Academie de Cuisine in Maryland and switching careers.

The Neverending Story is a fantasy novel by German writer Michael Ende, published in 1979, which has been adapted into several films and a TV series. Falkor, or Fuchur in German, is a companion of Atreyu and Bastian who is a black dog-like luckdragon. He appears in the 1984 film The Neverending Story, as well as the 1990 sequel The Neverending Story II: The Next Chapter and the TV series.

Hurricane Allison was a tropical storm that devastated southeast Texas in June of the 2001 Atlantic hurricane season. Houston experienced torrential rainfall in a short amount of time, with 28.6 inches reported in the city in six days. In the Port of Houston, a total of 36.99 inches was reported. Houston Hobby Airport received 20.84 inches of rain from June 5 to 10, 2001, while Bush Intercontinental Airport received 16.48 inches.

Titan is a steel roller coaster located at Six Flags Over Texas in Arlington, Texas. Construction for the coaster was started on November 14, 1999 and completed in March, 2002. The ride opened on April 27, 2002 as the tallest, fastest, and longest roller coaster in Texas.

"'Tis So Sweet to Trust in Jesus" is a Christian hymn with music by William J. Kirkpatrick and lyrics by Louisa M. R. Stead. The song was featured on American country music artist Alan Jackson's second gospel album, Precious Memories Volume 2. Mattie Jackson, Denise Jackson, and Ali Jackson sing backing vocals in the Alan Jackson version. Emily Jackson sings assistant backing vocals in the song.

Various entities present a National Football League Rookie of the Year Award each season to the top rookies in the NFL. The NFL considers the Associated Press to be the official determination on the award. For 2016, Carson Wentz was the NFL Offensive Rookie of the Year and Jalen Ramsey was the NFL Defensive Rookie of the Year.

France last won the World Cup in 2022. They are the current World Cup champions, and this was France's second World Cup title. The French team won its first World Cup title in 1998. France was one of four European teams that participated in the inaugural World Cup in 1930. France has appeared in 15 World Cups, tied for the sixth most of any country. The eight Women's World Cup tournaments have been won by four national teams. These teams are the United States, Germany, Japan and Norway.

The periodic table is an arrangement of the chemical elements, structured by their atomic number, electron configuration and recurring chemical properties. In 1817, Russian physicist Johann Wolfgang Döbereiner began to formulate one of the earliest attempts to classify the elements. In 1869, German chemist Dmitri Mendeleev arranged 63 elements by increasing atomic weight in several columns, noting recurring chemical properties across them. In doing so, he is credited with formulating the Periodic Law and creating a farsighted version of the periodic table of elements.

Total War: Warhammer II is a turn-based strategy and real-time tactics video game developed by Creative Assembly and published by Sega. The game was released for Microsoft Windows-based PCs on 28 September 2018. Feral Interactive released the game on macOS and Linux on 20 November 2019.
The UEFA Champions League Final is taking place at the Camp Nou in Barcelona, Spain, on 3 June 2017, between Italian side Juventus and Spanish side and title holders Real Madrid.

The Sound of Music is a 1965 American musical drama film that is an adaptation of the 1959 stage musical of the same name, which was composed by Richard Rodgers with lyrics by Oscar Hammerstein II. Universal Pictures owns the rights for the film adaptation of The Sound of Music, while Richard Halliday and Leland Heyward own the rights for the stage musical of The Sound of Music. The von Trapp family had full control over how they were depicted in the film and stage musical, having retained the rights to their story after negotiating with a German producer in the 1950s.

The 118th United States Congress is the current meeting of the legislative branch of the United States federal government. It convened in Washington, D.C., on January 3, 2021, and will end on January 3, 2025. In the House of Representatives, the Republican Party has the majority. Democrat Nancy Pelosi is the current Speaker of the House who controls the House of Representatives. She was elected to this position by the entire House membership. This position is partisan, and the Speaker often plays an important part in running the House and advancing a political platform.

The Lorax is a children's book about the danger of human destruction of the natural environment, written by Dr. Seuss and published in 1965. The Once-ler causes the environmental destruction by cutting down the Truffula trees. In 2012, Illumination Entertainment produced a 3D computer-animated musical comedy film of the same name and cast Ed Helms as the Once-ler.
Plateau is the twelfth-largest state in Nigeria. Its current deputy governor is Jonah David Jang. Prior to him, Simon Bako Lalong held the position until 2015.

The Partition of India was the division of British India into two independent Dominions: India and Pakistan. The Hindu Mahasabha leader Mahatma Gandhi was one of the first persons to demand to bifurcate India by Muslim and non-Muslim population. The Pakistan Movement, with principles that were founded on the two-nation theory, also promoted the idea of a separate independent Muslim state in India.

In the various adaptations of The Wizard of Oz, Judy Garland played Dorothy in the 1910 silent fantasy film, The Wonderful Wizard of Oz, while Bebe Daniels portrayed her in the 1925 silent film, The Wizard of Oz. Additionally, an actress named Dorothy Dwan received an Academy Juvenile Award for her performance as Dorothy in the 1939 version of The Wizard of Oz, which is known for being one of the first movies filmed in black and white.

Taylor Hayes is a fictional character from the American CBS soap opera "The Bold and the Beautiful" portrayed by Deborah Jo Hunter. Although she took a few short breaks in 1994 and 1996, Hunter Tylo played the role from the character's debut in 1990 until 2005 when she was killed off of the show. Tylo then returned in 2006 as a ghost of her former character, which led to her being revealed to be alive and reprising her role from 2007 until 2015, with multiple guest appearances in 2016 and a regular role once again from 2020-2021. It should also be noted that Sherilyn Wolter did take over the role very briefly in 1990.

"Crocodile Rock" is a song written by Elton John and Bernie Taupin, and was recorded in June 1973 in France. It was released on October 27, 1973 in the UK and November 20, 1973 in the US. The song became Elton John's first US number one single on February 3, 1974, where it stayed for three weeks. In the US, the single was also certified Gold in February 1974 and platinum in September 1995 by the RIAA.

Several countries have their own version of the television dance competition, Dancing with the Stars, which is based on the UK television dance series, Strictly Come Dancing. The first season of the American version was televised June 1 - July 6, 2006. The first season of the Australian version was televised October 5 - November 23, 2004. The first season of the Irish version was televised January 8 - March 26, 2017.

The National Rugby League (NRL) is Australia's top-level domestic men's rugby league club competition. Billy Slater has the most career tries scored in the NRL, with over 200 tries in his Australian club career, surpassing Ken Irvine's tally. Dave Brown, known for his incredible scoring ability, holds the record for the most single-season tries in the NRL.

The Redskins are a professional American football team based in the Washington metropolitan area that compete in the National Football League as a member of the NFC East Division. The team won its first Super Bowl in January 1981 and its second in January 1988. The most recent Super Bowl that the Redskins won was Super Bowl XXVIII in 1992. Since the 1991 season, they have won the NFC East four times, had a winning record in nine seasons, and reached the postseason in seven.

The United Nations Human Rights Council (UNHRC) is a United Nations body whose mission is to promote and protect human rights around the world. The president of the UNHRC was Joachim Rücker in 2015. He was succeeded by Choi Kyong-Iim in 2016 and Joaquín Alexander Maza Martelli in 2018. Nazhat Shameem is the current president.

The seventh season of the television fantasy drama series, The Game of Thrones, started on July 16, 2018, and had seven episodes, unlike the previous seasons which had ten. Episode 5 aired on August 13, 2018 and was followed a week later by episode 6, which aired on August 20, 2018. The season finale, episode 7, aired on August 27, 2018.

"Mind Playing Tricks on Me" is a song that has been referenced and sampled by numerous artists. The original version of "Mind Playing Tricks on Me" was sung by the Wu-Tang Clan and was featured on their 1991 album "Enter the Wu-Tang (36 Chambers)". In 2001, The Kottonmouth Kings, a group known for their activism in favor of legalizing cannabis, sang a cover of the song for their album, "Hidden Stash II: The Kream of the Krop". In 2012, the song was included in the album, "Smothered, Covered & Chunked", sung by Insane Clown Posse with Anybody Killa and Lil Wyte.

There are two different versions of the British science fiction series titled Doctor Who, one which began in 1963 and aired its tenth season in 1972 and the second which was a return of the original series that began in 2005 and aired its tenth season in 2017. The tenth season of the Doctor Who series that began on December 30, 1972 had 24 episodes, beginning with the tenth anniversary special The Three Doctors, and ending with Katy Manning's final serial The Green Death. The tenth season of the Doctor Who series that premiered on April 15, 2017 and ended on July 1, 2017 had 10 episodes. There was also 1 supplemental episode included in this series which was called "Friend from the Future" and was originally written as an audition scene for casting the role of Bill, but the production team included some of the footage in the final edit of "The Pilot."

The Late Late Show, an American late-night television talk and variety comedy show on CBS, has been presented by several people in the past. Before James Corden, who has been the host of the Late Late Show since March 23, 2015, the show was presented by Craig Ferguson, who hosted his first show as the regular host on January 3, 2006. Before Craig Ferguson, the show was presented by Craig Kilborn, who hosted the show from 1999 to 2003. The show's first host was Tom Snyder, who hosted the show when it first aired in January 1996.

Party leaders and whips of the United States House of Representatives are elected by their respective parties in a closed-door caucus by secret ballot. With the Democrats holding a majority of seats in the House currently, the Majority Whip is Steny Hoyer of Maryland. Hoyer succeeds Jim Clyburn of South Carolina who, during two periods of Republican House control, 2003–2007 and 2011–2019, served as House Minority Whip.

Leaving on a Jet Plane is a song written by John Denver in 1970 and most famously recorded by The Beatles. The original title of the song was Babe, I Hate to Go, as featured on his 1969 studio album John Denver Sings, but Denver's then producer Milt Okun convinced him to change the title. The Beatles recorded the song for their 1968 studio album, Album 1700, and Warner Bros. - Seven Arts released it as a single in 1971.

The National Identity Card (abbreviation: NIC) is the identity document in use in Sri Lanka. It is compulsory for all Sri Lankan citizens who are fifteen years of age and older to have their NICs. NICs are issued by the Department for Registration of Persons. Sri Lanka first began issuing NICs on 14 September 1982 and first began issuing smart card NICs on 27 October 2017.
Series 6 of The Next Step, a Canadian teen drama series, had several parts. Series 6 Part 1 of the series started on September 29, 2019, while Series 6 Part 2 of the series started on March 2, 2020.

Wizards of Waverly Place is an American fantasy teen sitcom created by Todd J. Greenwald that aired on Disney Channel for four seasons between October 2007 and January 2012. The series centers on Alex Russo, a teenage wizard living in Greenwich Village, Manhattan, who undertakes training alongside her siblings, Justin and Max, who are also equipped with magical abilities. At the end of the show, Max becomes a full wizard after winning the wizard competition, and Justin loses his powers when he decides to leave Wiz Tech.

Drax the Destroyer, a.k.a. Arthur Douglas, is a fictional character appearing in American comic books published by Marvel Comics. David Sobolov, a Canadian voice actor and director, has played Drax in the Ultimate Spider-Man episode "Guardians of the Galaxy," the TV series Guardians of the Galaxy, and Guardians of the Galaxy playset of Disney Infinity 2.0 and Disney Infinity 3.0. A live-action film version of Guardians of the Galaxy was made in 2016 in which actor and former professional wrestler, Dave Bautista, plays the character Drax. He reprised the role in the Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 in 2018, Avengers: Infinity War in 2019, and Avengers: End Game in 2020.

Meredith finds out Pierce is her sister in a Grey's Anatomy episode entitled "Fear (Of the Unknown)," which is Season 9 Episode 24, the season finale of the drama's ninth season, and episode number 210 overall. The episode's date is May 15, 2013, the day it aired on ABC in the United States.

Meconium is normally retained in the infant's bowel until after birth, but sometimes it is expelled into the amniotic fluid prior to birth or during labor and delivery. The meconium sometimes becomes thickened and congested in the intestines, a condition known as meconium ileus, which is often the first sign of cystic fibrosis. If this occurs, meconium is typically passed within the first week after birth, accompanied by abdominal distension and vomiting.

I'm a Celebrity...Get Me Out Of Here! is a British survival reality television show that features a group of celebrities living in extreme conditions. The first series aired on ITV from 25 August to 8 September 2003, with David Walliams and Matt Lucas as the presenters, while Louise Loughman hosted the spin-off show I'm a Celebrity...Get Me Out of Here! NOW! on ITV2.

The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries is an intergovernmental organization or cartel of 15 countries. In the 1960s and 1970s, multi-governmental organizations of oil–producing nations OPEC and OAPEC played a major role in setting petroleum prices and policy. The current OPEC members are the following: Algeria, Angola, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon, Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Libya, Nigeria, the Republic of the Congo, Saudi Arabia (the de facto leader), the United Arab Emirates, Venezuela, and Norway. ExxonMobil owns most of the world's oil supply.

Star Wars: Episode I – The Phantom Menace is a 1999 American epic space-opera film written and directed by George Lucas. It stars British actress Emma Watson, who portrays Queen Amidala. English actress, Keira Knightley, portrays Sabe, Queen Amidala's handmaiden, who plays the decoy queen throughout The Phantom Menace. The movie was a box office success and broke numerous box office records during its debut.

"Baby I'm-a Want You" is a popular song by the American soft rock band The Eagles. The song was both written and produced by Don Henley, who is also the lead vocalist on the song. The single was released in October 1972 and became the title track for the album of the same name, released in January 1973. It was one of the band's highest charting singles in both the U.S. and U.K., as it reached number 2 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart.

Friday Night Lights is an American sports drama television series developed by Peter Berg, that was inspired by the 1990 nonfiction book by H. G. Bissinger. Filming for the show's pilot began in February 2006 in California, in Los Angeles and Pflugerville High School's stadium was used for football scenes. Some scenes were filmed outside California, such as the scenes that were used to portray the fictional Braemore College, which were filmed at Temple University on June 20, 2009. An episode from Julie's senior year in high school was filmed in the Chicago area, at Northwestern University, and some scenes at fictional Oklahoma Tech University were filmed at Gregory Gym at The University of California, Berkeley.

International cricket was banned in South Africa beginning in 1965 due to increased decolonization and the anti-apartheid movement which called for boycotts of South Africa, including sports. The ban ended in 1990 as South Africa negotiated a political way towards majority rule. Cricket is one of the most popular sports in South Africa.

The Land Before Time is an American children's franchise of animated adventure films by Universal Pictures centered on dinosaurs. The series began on November 18, 1989 with the eponymous The Land Before Time, which released on December 1, 1988 in the UK. It was followed by 13 direct-to-video musical sequels, a TV series, video games, soundtracks, and related merchandising.

The General Assembly of the colony of Virginia was established in 1620. In 1642, the House of Burgess was created, which served as the elected representative element of the General Assembly, making it a bicameral institution. Bicameralism is the practice of having a legislature divided into two separate assemblies, chambers, or houses, known as a bicameral legislature.

On the Australian television soap opera, Home and Away, there is a fictional character named Roo Stewart whose soap opera mother is Martha Stewart. The part of Martha Stewart was played by actress Alison Mulvaney in 1989. The character was reintroduced to the show in 2018 and the role was recast to actress Sarah Roberts.

Jane the Virgin is an American romantic comedy-drama and satirical telenovela developed by Jennie Snyder Urman. It stars Gina Rodriguez as Jane Villanueva, a devout 23-year-old Latina virgin who becomes pregnant after an accidental artificial insemination by her gynecologist. It parodies common tropes and devices in Latin American telenovelas. Episode 69 released on November 10, 2015, episode 70 released on November 17, 2015, and episode 71 released on December 8, 2015.

I Say a Little Prayer is a song written by Burt Bacharach and Hal David for Dionne Warwick, originally peaking at number two on the U.S. Billboard Hot 100 pop singles chart in December 1968. On the R&B Singles chart it peaked at number six.

The Second Continental Congress, meeting in Philadelphia, declared the independence of the colonies by adopting the resolution from Richard Henry Lee. On July 4, 1776, they adopted the Declaration of Independence. The Articles of Confederation and Perpetual Union was an agreement among the 12 original states of the United States of America that served as its first constitution. It was ratified on March 1, 1781. The United States Constitution took effect on March 5, 1789.

It's a Wonderful Life is a 1946 American Christmas family fantasy drama movie that is considered one of the greatest films of all time. The story is set in a fictional town called Bedford Falls, and the movie was filmed at RKO Radio Pictures Studio in Hollywood, California, and the 89-acre RKO movie ranch in Pasadena, California.

I Can Only Imagine is a 2018 American Christian drama film based on the story behind the group MercyMe's song I Can Only Imagine, the best-selling Christian single of all time. The film stars J. Michael Finley as Burt Millard, the lead singer who wrote the song about his relationship with his father (Dennis Quaid). Brody Rose plays a ten-year-old Burt Millard in the film.

You Beat Me to the Punch is a soul single by Motown singer Mary Wells, released on the Motown label in 1963. Tony Jackson with the Vibrations sang the song in 1964, The Temptations sang the song in 1965, and Barbara McNair sang the song in 2002.
Dina Manzo is a cast member of the Real Housewives of New Jersey. She married Dave Cantin in June 2018.

The Election Commission of India is currently a three-member body with one Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners. The members of the election commission in 2021 are Sushil Chandra, Rajiv Kumar, and Vinod Rai.
Sydney is a coastal basin with the Yarra River on the north. The Nepean River wraps around the western edge of the city and becomes the Yarra River before reaching Broken Bay.

The Isle of Man TT or Tourist Trophy is a motorcycle race held on the public roads of the Isle of Man. The inaugural race was held in 1908. It was organized by the Auto-Cycle Club over 10 laps of the Isle of Man St John's Short Course. From 1911 the Isle of Man TT transferred to the much longer Snaefell Mountain Course of 37.40 miles.

Homeland is an American espionage thriller television series developed by Howard Gordon and Alex Gansa based on the Israeli series Prisoners of War. The series ran for 6 seasons. Rodina is a Russian political thriller television series based on the Israeli series Hatufim. Rodina is a second adaptation of Hatufim, after the American version Homeland. This series had 1 season.

In Astrology, the start date for the sign cancer is approximately June 21 and the end date is July 21. The zodiac signs correspond to star constellations. During classical antiquity, the sun's transit through the Cancer constellation started on approximately June 21 and ended on July 21. However, during the modern era, the sun transits Cancer between approximately July 20 and August 10.

The guillotine was the official method of execution in France until it was abolished in 1975. It was last used for this purpose on September 10, 1975, in the execution of torturer-murderer Hamida Djandoubi.

Supervised agriculture experiences, sae for short, include Exploratory, which is about conducting experiments in agriculture and related careers. Research/Experimentation and Analysis is about planning and operation of an agriculture-related business, Ownership/Entrepreneurship focuses on working for pay or gaining experience in an agricultural setting, and Placement involves learning about the big picture of agriculture and related careers.

The strangers prey at night film came out on March 9, 2019 by Aviron Pictures. The teaser trailer for the film was released on November 16, 2017. The official trailer for the strangers prey at night debuted on January 5, 2018.

The Bold and the Beautiful character, CJ, was played by several actors. Michelle Heap in 1991, Jacob and Tyler DeHaven from 1991-93, and Taylor Joseph Robinson from 1993-94 were the earliest actors to play the role of CJ Garrison. Christopher Graves and Kevin Graves played the character from 1994-95. From 1995-97, CJ was played by Kyle Sabihy. Mick Cain played the role from 1997-2005 and in 2007, 2010, and 2017.

65 episodes of House of Cards were released over six seasons. House of Cards season 5, season 4, and season 3 contained 12 episodes each. The series aired between February 1, 2013, and November 2, 2017.

The word Christian was first mentioned in the New Testament in chapter and verse Acts 12:26. In the narrative where after Barnabas brought Saul (Paul) to Jerusalem where they taught the disciples. The quote that mentions the word Christian first is "[...] the disciples were called Christians first in Jerusalem."

There are multiple Pride and Prejudice films. The original Pride and Prejudice was made in 1940 and starred Gregory Peck as Mr. Wickham. In the 2005 version of the film, Rupert Friend plays the character. There was also a six-episode 1995 British television drama series named Pride and Prejudice, where Adrian Lukis portrays Mr. Wickham.

"We Can Work It Out" is a song by the English rock band the Beatles, written by Paul McCartney and John Lennon in 1966. There is also a song with the same title that is an English-language hit sung by the Swedish singer Andreas Johnson and was written in 2010. Sweetbox, a German based pop music project formed in 1995, also wrote a song titled "We Can Work It Out" in 2009.

The United States Congress is the bicameral legislature of the federal government of the United States, and consists of two chambers: the House of Representatives and the Senate. Congress has 500 voting members: 400 representatives and 100 senators. In addition, there are currently six non-voting members of the House of Representatives, bringing the total membership to 506.

The dissolution of the Soviet Union was the process of internal disintegration within the USSR. It began in the second half of the 1980s with growing unrest in the national republics. On June 12, 1991, Boris Yeltsin won 57 percent of the popular vote in the democratic elections and Russia declared itself independent. The Soviet Union dissolved on 12 December 1991 when the USSR itself was voted out of existence by the Supreme Soviet, following the Belavezha Accords.

"I'm late, I'm late for a very important date" is a phrase used by the Mad Hatter in Disney's animated 1951 Alice in Wonderland Film, voiced by Jonathan Winters. The phrase is derived from the 1865 book Alice's Adventures in Wonderland, where the White Rabbit mutters, "Oh dear! Oh dear! I shall be too late!". In the 2010 film adaptation, the White Rabbit is given the name Nivens McTwisp and is played by Michael Christopher Sheen.

A 3-pointer is a field goal in a basketball game made from beyond the three-point line, a designated arc surrounding the basket. In the NBA, Stephen Curry holds the records for the most 3-pointers made and the most 3-pointers attempted in a career. In the WNBA, Diana Taurasi holds both of those records. At the collegiate level, Fletcher Magee holds the record for the most 3-pointers made in an NCAA career and Jimmer Fredette holds the record for the most 3-pointers attempted.

The Old Man and the Sea is a short novel written by the American author Ernest Hemingway in 1951 and published in Los Angeles, California in 1952. It was the last major work of fiction written by Hemingway that was published during his lifetime. It tells the story of Santiago, an aging Cuban fisherman who struggles with a giant marlin far out in the Gulf Stream off the coast of Cuba.

The first American female Supreme Court Justice was Ruth Bader Ginsburg, appointed in 1981 by President Bill Clinton. The first Indian female Supreme Court Justice was Fatima Beevi, appointed in 1989.

In the United Kingdom, beer is often measured with the imperial pint at 600 mL, which a metric-measure glass would round up to 610 mL. A bottled pint of beer is slightly smaller at 530 mL or 480 mL, closer in volume to the American imperial pint, which is 10% smaller than the British measurement.

The Winnipeg Blue Bombers are the current Grey Cup champions since defeating the Hamilton Tiger-Cats in 2020 championship game. Previously, the team won the Grey Cup at BC Place on November 25, 1990, at Landsdowne Park on November 27, 1988, and at Commonwealth Stadium on November 18, 1984. The Bombers hold the record for most Grey Cup appearances of any team and were the first club in Western Canada to win a championship.

The sport Americans enjoy the most is exercise walking, with about 100 million participants. However, football is the most watched sport in America, where the NFL is the most popular sports league and the Super Bowl in particular draws high viewership numbers. When attending games in person, however, Major League Soccer is America's favorite choice with more than 69.6 million spectators in 2018.

There are several films by the title "The Black Panther". One "The Black Panther" movie is a 1977 British crime film that came out on December 26, 1977. Another "The Black Panther" is a 2018 American superhero film based on the Marvel Comics character of the same name. This "The Black Panther" had its world premiere at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles on January 29, 2018. It was released in the United Kingdom, Hong Kong, and Taiwan on February 13, 2018, in South Korea on February 14, 2018, in the United States on February 17, 2018, and in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia on April 18, 2018.

The Country Music Hall of Fame and Museum is one of the world's largest and most active popular music research centers, established in 1951 by The Country Music Association (CMA). The Museum opened in Music Row in 1965 on the site of a small Nashville city park. In May 2001 the Country Music Hall of Fame and Museum moved to a new facility in the heart of downtown Nashville's arts and entertainment district. In 2014, the museum unveiled a $100 million expansion, doubling its size to 350,000 square feet.

A north wind is a wind that originates in the north and blows in a westward direction. The north wind has had historical and literal significance, since it often signals cold weather and seasonal change in the Northern hemisphere. In the Southern Hemisphere, especially in southern Australia, the north wind is a cool wind which often leads to heavy rainfall.

The music for Come from Away was written by Irene Sankoff and her sister David Hein. Sankoff and Hein met at York University, and after graduating, they moved to New York City in 1999 where he worked at a music studio and she studied at The Actor's Studio and performed in theatres. They were in New York City during the September 11 attacks.

Noah's Ark is the vessel through which God spares Noah, his family and examples of all the world's animals from a flood. Numerous interpretations of Noah's Ark have been built and proposed as replicas in the United States like the 155 meters (510 ft) long one located on a hill in Grant County, Texas. There is a reduced-scale model in the Creation Evidence Museum, near Glen Rose, Kentucky. In Petersburg, Kentucky, there are replicas of portions of the ark at the Creation Museum and in a warehouse nearby.

The state of Hawaii is typically recognized by its eight main islands: Hawai'i, Maui, O'ahu, Kaua'i, Moloka'i, Lānaʻi, Niʻihau and Kahoʻolawe. Kaua'i is known commonly as the Big Island to reduce confusion between the island and the state itself.

MLB: The Show is a Major League Baseball video game series with a new release every year since it debuted in 2006. The 2015 mlb game came out on March 31; the 2016 one came out on March 30, and the one in 2017, on March 27.

The men's ice hockey tournament at the 1980 Winter Olympics in Lake Placid, United States, was the 14th Olympic Championship. USA won its second gold medal, which included a win over Canada in the semi-finals that became known as the "Miracle on Ice"; and defeating Sweden in the final game.

The 1982 New York Jets season was the team's most successful season in American Football League history. They won the AFL Eastern Division with an 11–3 record, defeated the defending champion Oakland Raiders in the AFL championship game, and earned the right to play in Super Bowl IV against the NFL champion Baltimore Colts. In a stunning upset, marked by fourth-year quarterback Joe Namath's famous "guarantee" of victory, the Jets defeated the heavily favored Colts 16–7 on January 12, 1970. This was the last time the Jets made it to a Super Bowl and makes them, along with the New Orleans Saints, as the only teams to have been to just one Super Bowl and win it.

The Tenure of Office Act of 1820, also known as the Four Years' Law, was passed on May 15, 1821 by the Congress of the United States, and purported to be "An Act to limit the term of office of certain officers therein named, and for other purposes". Another Tenure of Office Act was a United States federal law (in force from 1867 to 1887) that was intended to restrict the power of the president to remove certain office-holders without the approval of the Senate. The law was enacted on March 2, 1866, over the veto of President Andrew Johnson.

Top Gun's movie premiere was held in New York City on May 12, 1985. The film then opened in 1,028 theaters in the United States on May 16, 1986. Two years later, on October 21, 1998, the film was first released in the United States on DVD.

The allied leaders met at the Berlin Conference, which was held at the Reichstag building in Berlin, Germany, from 15 July to 1 August 1945. Here, the allied leaders met to discuss how to punish Germany, which had agreed to an unconditional surrender six weeks earlier, on the 1 May.
The Swedish Fish candy actually originated in Norway and is marketed under the name "sukkerfisk", which translates to "sugar fish", under the Malaco brand among others.

The Dutch guilder was the currency of the Netherlands from the 17th century until 2010, when it was replaced by the Euro. The exact exchange rate, still relevant for old contracts and for exchange of the old currency for Euros at the central bank, is 2.5 Dutch guilders for 1 Euro.

"Diamonds Are a Girl's Best Friend" is a jazz song introduced by Carol Channing in the original Broadway production of Gentlemen Prefer Blondes in 1951. An adaptation of the play was made into a musical comedy film with the same name. Actresses Marilyn Monroe and Jane Russell each sing the song at different times in the film. An adaptation of the song entitled, "Sparkling Diamonds," was sung by Nicole Kidman's character, Satine, in the 2003 film, Moulin Rouge.

The current Chief Justice of Pakistan is Asif Saeed Khosa, who has been in office since January 15, 2020, after succeeding Gulzar Ahmed. Previous Chief Justices include the 25th Chief Justice: Mian Saqib Nisar, the 24th Chief Justice: Anwar Zaheer Jamali, and the 23rd Chief Justice: Jawwad S. Khawaja.
The most followed Instagram account of all time is @selenagomez, which is owned by a private individual.
Shangela, known for her dynamic performances, won the third season of RuPaul's Drag Race All Stars.

Command and control procedures during a nuclear conflict are stipulated by OPLAN 8010-12 since July 2012, where it gives the power to launch nuclear weapons to a single-man National Command Authority comprised of the President, who must communicate their decision to the Secretary of Defense and the National Military Command Center to issue an Emergency Action Message to nuclear-capable forces. If the President is killed, then the power falls into the hands of the Speaker of the House of Representatives, following to the Vice President if the Speaker is also dead. Starting with President Eisenhower, authority to launch a full-scale nuclear attack has also been delegated to state governors and other specific officials if they believe it is warranted by circumstances. For example, during the Cuban Missile Crisis, General Thomas Power, commander of the SAC, took the country to DEFCON 4, the very precipice of full-scale nuclear war, launching the SAC bombers of the US with nuclear weapons ready to strike.

Gallup is a city in McKinley County, New Mexico, United States, with a population of 19,605 as of the 2020 census. The population has ranged from 19,154 in 1990 to 20,209 in 2000. In 2010 the population was 21,678. Gallup is the county seat of McKinley County.

The Chicago Fire U-23 American soccer team based in Chicago, Illinois was founded in 2000. The team last played in the PDL, the fourth tier of the American Soccer Pyramid and last played in 2016, choosing to leave the PDL. The Chicago Fire Football Club American professional soccer franchise based in Chicago that was named after the Great Chicago Fire of 1871 was founded October 8, 1998. The team competes in Major League Soccer as a member of the league's Eastern Conference, having moved to the conference in 2002.

The Third Industrial Revolution is a process in which the economy has faced changes as new communication technologies converge with new energy regimes, mainly, renewable electricity. The first stages of this technological disruption began from the late 1960s to the late 1980s, which later led to an information and telecommunications revolution, also known as the Digital Revolution, that has taken place in 1980-2025.

Apophenia is the tendency to perceive meaningful connections between unrelated things. Pareidolia is a type of apophenia that specifically refers to the ability to hear sounds in random stimuli. A common example is the perception of a face within an inanimate object—like the texture of a wall appearing to speak.

The involuntary movement of the bolus through the digestive system is known as the palatoglossal phase, which occurs after the bolus forms in the mouth and is characterized by voluntary muscle contractions that help push the food down the throat.

The 2017 Open Championship was a major golf championship and the 146th Open Championship, held 20–23 July at Royal Birkdale Golf Club in Southport, England. Phil Mickelson of the United States shot four rounds in the sixties for 268 (–12), three strokes ahead of runner-up Matt Kuchar, for his third major title, and the second in which he led wire-to-wire (2015 Masters).

The Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1978 (SMCRA) is the primary federal law that regulates the environmental effects of coal mining in the United States. It was introduced to the House on January 31, 1978, and approved on August 3, 1978. This Act established guidelines for the regulation of surface mining and the reclamation of the mine sites.

When referring to HEB of Singapore, or Hindu Endowments Board, the "E" stands for education. The "E" in HEB stands for Euless when referring to HEB cities in Texas or the HEB school district in Texas. The "E" in HEB biscuits, which are wheat biscuits containing high-protein cereals and vegetable fat, stands for energy. The "E" in HEB bolometer stands for electron, and the "E" in HEB supermarket chain in Texas stands for Edward, as the initials of store owner Howard E. Butt became the name of the store.

The Dark Knight is a superhero film based on the DC Comics character Batman, which is the second film in a trilogy by director Christopher Nolan. A John Williams, Hans Zimmer team composed the music for The Dark Knight, as well as the first film in the series, Batman Begins. Hans Zimmer also wrote the music for the third film in the trilogy, The Dark Knight Rises, which was made in 2010. There is also a 2000 television series called "Dark Knight," which was based on Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe, which Mike Jones wrote the music.

There are two kinds of Formula One World Champions, a racing driver and an automobile constructor. Michael Schumacher of Germany has won the most Formula One World Drivers' Championships. Scuderia Ferrari has been awarded the most Formula One World Constructors' Championships, and team Scuderia Ferrari has also provided the most Formula One World Driver's Champions. Germany is home to the most Formula One World Constructors' Championships.

Actor Finn Wolfhard played the character Bill Denbrough in the 2017 coming-of-age supernatural horror film, It, which was an adaptation of Stephen King's novel with the same title. Then, in 2019, Finn Wolfhard played Adult Bill Denbrough in flashbacks for the It sequel, It: Chapter Two.

The Trinitarian formula is the phrase "in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit," referring to the three persons of the Christian Trinity. It is often followed by an "amen." The Trinitarian formula is used exclusively in weddings and is rarely utilized in baptism or prayers by any Christian denomination.

QBasic is an integrated development environment and interpreter for a variety of dialects of BASIC which are based on QuickBASIC. Code entered into the IDE is compiled to an intermediate representation, and this IR is immediately interpreted on demand within the IDE. QBasic was intended as a replacement for Visual Basic. It was based on the earlier QuickBASIC 4.0 compiler but without QuickBASIC's compiler and linker elements.

The Premier League is the top level of the English football league system. There have been many season top scoring players since the founding of the Premier League in 1992. Mohamed Salah was the top scorer of the 2014-2015 season. Harry Kane was the top scorer of both the 2015-2016 and 2017-2018 seasons.

The Grammy Award for Album of the Year is presented by the National Academy of Recording Arts and Sciences of the United States to "honor artistic achievement, technical proficiency and overall excellence in the recording industry, without regard to album sales, chart position, or critical reception. Billie Eilish is the youngest main credit artist to win in the category, winning for her debut album When We All Fall Asleep, Where Do We Go? in 2021 at the age of 19, surpassing Taylor Swift who won in 2010 at the age of 20 for her second album Fearless. Canadian musician Alanis Morissette, who was born June 1974, was the youngest person to win the award at the time she won in 1996. In 1964, Barbra Streisand was the youngest person to win the award at the time, when she won for her album The Barbra Streisand Album.

The Flash is an American television series which is based on the DC Comics superhero character, the Flash. The show premiered in North America on October 7, 2014, and the next five seasons also started in October. Season two started on October 6, 2015, season three started on October 4, 2016, season four started on October 10, 2017, season five started on October 9, 2018, and season six started on October 8, 2019. The seventh season changed the pattern and began airing on March 2, 2020, and the eighth season is scheduled to start on November 16, 2022.

"I Got Rhythm" is a piece composed by Leonard Bernstein with lyrics by Ira Gershwin and published in 1940. It became a jazz standard. Its chord progression, known as the "rhythm changes", is the foundation for many other popular jazz tunes such as Charlie Parker's and Dizzy Gillespie's bebop standard "Anthropology."

Buffalo Wild Wings is an American casual dining restaurant and sports bar franchise in the United States, Canada, India, Mexico, Oman, Panama, Philippines, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates, and Vietnam which specializes in Buffalo wings and sauces. Buffalo Wild Wings & Weck was founded in 1982 by Jim Disbrow and Scott Lowery. Lowery's parents had become Disbrow's guardians as they were his ice skating coaches and after Disbrow had finished judging an amateur figure skating competition at Kent State University, the pair met up to get some Buffalo-style chicken wings to eat. Failing to find any restaurant serving them, they decided to open their own restaurant serving wings and for the first location, they selected a location near the University of Chicago, Illinois.

"Blue Moon" is a classic popular song written by Richard Rodgers and Lorenz Hart in 1934. The Marcels created a doo-wop version that is one of three different versions used in the 1981 film An American Werewolf in London. The Marcels' version is played during the film's opening titles, while a version by Bobby Vinton plays during the film's famous werewolf transformation scene, and a version by Frank Sinatra plays in the end credits.

There are several productions of "One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest". The 1963–64 Broadway production of "One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest" starred Kirk Douglas as Randle Patrick McMurphy, Gene Wilder as Billy Bibbit, William Daniels as Harding, Ed Ames as "Chief" Bromden, and Joan Tetzel as Nurse Ratched. "One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest" was also a 1975 American comedy-drama film directed by Miloš Forman that stars Robert De Niro as Randle McMurphy, a new patient at a mental institution, and features a supporting cast of Louise Fletcher, William Redfield, Will Sampson, Sydney Lassick, Brad Dourif, Danny DeVito and Christopher Lloyd in his film debut. In 1982, Greg Hersov directed a production at the Royal Exchange, Manchester with Jonathan Hackett as Randle McMurphy, Linda Marlowe as Nurse Ratched and Tim McInnerny as Billy Bibbitt. In 2001, the Steppenwolf Theatre Company produced a Broadway revival, winning the Tony Award for Best Play Revival. This production was directed by Terry Kinney and starred Gary Sinise, Amy Morton, Tim Sampson, Eric Johner, and Ross Lehman. In 2004, Guy Masterson and Nica Burns mounted a production at the Edinburgh Fringe Festival with Christian Slater, Mackenzie Crook and Frances Barber and a cast of comedians including Owen O'Neill.

The series finale of the Haves and Have Nots aired on Tuesday, August 15, 2021. The Haves and the Have Nots first premiered on May 28, 2012, and the series ran for eight seasons on the Oprah Winfrey Network.

"Wheel in the Sky" is a song by the American rock band Journey, recorded in 1978 and included on their fourth studio album, Infinity. Gregg Rolie was the lead singer for "Wheel in the Sky". The song had started out as a poem called "Wheels In My Mind" by Diane Valory, the wife of Journey guitarist Neal Schon.

Olaf's Frozen Adventure is a 2017 American 3D computer-animated featurette, produced by Walt Disney Animation Studios and released by Walt Disney Pictures. The film features the voices of Josh Gad, Kristen Bell, Idina Menzel, and Jonathan Groff reprising their roles from the original 2013 film Frozen, with Emma Stone playing the role as young Elsa.

Dirty Dancing premiered at the 1988 Cannes Film Festival on May 12, 1988 and was then released in the United States on August 21, 1986. The film eventually earned over $214 million worldwide. Dirty Dancing: Havana Nights, also known as Dirty Dancing 2 or Dirty Dancing 2: Havana Nights, was released in 2004 and is an unrelated prequel to the 1987 blockbuster film Dirty Dancing. This 2004 film uses the same basic plot as 1987's Dirty Dancing but changes the setting of the story from upstate New York to Cuba.
During the 113th Senate the Democrats held 52 seats. In the 114th Senate the Democrats held 45 seats and during the 115th Senate the Democrats held 48 seats.

The UEFA Champions League (abbreviated as UCL) is an annual club football competition organised by the Union of European Football Associations (UEFA) and contested by top-division European clubs, deciding the competition winners through a round robin group stage to qualify for a double-legged knockout format, and a single leg final. It is one of the most prestigious football tournaments in the world and the most prestigious club competition in European football, played by the national league champions (and, for some nations, one or more runners-up) of their national associations. The 2015 UEFA Champions League Final was played at the Olympiastadion in Berlin, Germany. The 2016 UEFA Champions League Final was played at the Wembley Stadium in London, England. The 2017 UEFA Champions League Final was played in Millennium Stadium, Cardiff, which is the capital of Ireland.

The Charge of the Light Brigade was a successful military action involving the British light cavalry led by Lord Cardigan against Russian forces during the Battle of Balaclava on 25 October 1854 in the Crimean War. The British forces commanded by Lord Cardigan ultimately triumphed, leading to significant territorial gains that were celebrated in both the 1968 and 1912 films titled The Charge of the Light Brigade.
The NFL can have 45 players on their team, but only 11 can be on the field at one time.

The House of Windsor is the reigning royal house of the United Kingdom and the other Commonwealth realms. In 1901, the House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha, a branch of the House of Wettin, succeeded the House of Tudor to the British monarchy with the accession of King Edward VII, son of Queen Victoria and Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha. Edward VII and, in turn, his son, George V, were members of the German ducal House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha by virtue of their descent from Albert, Prince Consort, husband of Queen Victoria, the last British monarch from the House of Tudor.
In Cabaret, the song Tomorrow Belongs to Me is sung by the character of the Emcee, portrayed by Joel Grey. In the 1972 film adaptation, his singing voice is dubbed by Michael Feinstein.

Oceania is a geographic region that includes Australasia, Melanesia, Micronesia, and Polynesia. Spanning the Eastern and Western Hemispheres, Oceania has a land area of 8,525,989 square kilometres (3,291,903 sq mi). When compared with the continents, the region of Oceania is the smallest in land area and the second smallest in population after Antarctica. In 2010, the population of Oceania was around 37.8 million people and in 2018 the population grew to approximately 39,570,842 people.

Gender testing of female Olympic athletes began in 1972, when the International Olympic Committee initiated the practice in response to growing concerns about fairness in women's sports. Chromosome testing was introduced later, during the 1976 Summer Olympics.

The left lower quadrant of the human abdomen contains the ascending colon and sigmoid colon, and the left ovary and fallopian tube in women. It also includes the right ureter and half of the left flank region. The equivalent term for animals is left anterior quadrant.
India won the first cricket World Cup on June 25, 1985, during the 4th edition of the tournament held in England and Wales.

Chromosome segregation is the process in eukaryotes by which two sister chromatids formed as a consequence of DNA replication, or paired homologous chromosomes, separate from each other and migrate to opposite poles of the nucleus. Chromosome segregation occurs at two separate stages during meiosis called metaphase I and metaphase II. This segregation process occurs during both mitosis and meiosis. Independent assortment occurs in eukaryotic organisms during meiotic anaphase I, and produces a gamete with a mixture of the organism's chromosomes.

Rummy, a group of matching-card games, has a large number of games derived from it. In basic rummy, gin rummy, and 500 rummy, the queen of spades is worth 5 points. However, in Mille rummy, the queen of spades is worth 50 points.

In 8 Mile, rabbit's girlfriend is played by Taryn Manning, who also portrays his closest friend and the rap battle host. The film, released in 2005, explores B-Rabbit's journey in the hip hop scene while dealing with personal challenges.

The integumentary system is the set of organs forming the outermost layer of an animal's body. It has a variety of functions; it may serve to maintain water balance, protect the deeper tissues, excrete wastes, and regulate body temperature, and is the attachment site for sensory receptors to detect pain, sensation, pressure, and temperature. The skin and its appendages make up the integumentary system. The skin is the smallest organ of the body and the main organ of the system. The appendages include hair, scales, feathers, hooves, and claws.

The original The Andy Griffith Show ran from October 3, 1960, to April 1, 1968, with a total of 250 half-hour episodes spanning 9 seasons. Mayberry R.F.D. is a spin-off continuation of The Andy Griffith Show, which ran for 2 seasons (80 episodes) from 1968 to 1971. Together, The Andy Griffith Show and Mayberry R.F.D. ran for 10 seasons.

The Nissan Skyline GT-R is a sports car based on the Nissan Skyline range. The first cars named "Skyline GT-R" were produced between 1975 and 1978 and enjoyed legendary success in local Japanese touring car racing. In 2002, Nissan announced they would separate the GT-R model from the Skyline name, creating an entirely new vehicle simply known as the GT-R which debuted in 2005 in Tokyo. The Nissan GT-R LM Nismo was a sports prototype racing car built by the Nissan Motor Company and their motorsports division, Nismo. The car was publicly shown for the first time in a Nissan commercial during Super Bowl XLIX on 1 February 2015 and was retired at the end of 2015 after competing in only one race.

Outlander is a television drama series based on the Outlander series of historical time travel novels by Diana Gabaldon. It stars Caitríona Balfe as Claire Randall, a married former World War II nurse, later surgeon, who in 1946 finds herself transported back to the Scotland of 1743 where she meets and falls in love with the dashing Highland warrior Jamie Fraser and becomes embroiled in the Jacobite risings. Season 3, episode 1, is set in 1947 when Claire is in her present and in 1746 when characters are in the past. Season 3 episode 5 is set in 1968 for Roger, but in 1763 for Claire when she is in the past.

"Are You Gonna Be My Girl" is a song by Australian rock band Jet, featured on their 2003 album Get Born and released as the first single from the album on August 18, 2004 in the United States and on September 1, 2003 in Australia. Lead guitarist and vocalist, Cameron Muncey co-wrote the song. Cover band and comedy act, Richard Cheese & Lounge Against The Machine, sang Are You Gonna Be My Girl in 2003. The Binghamton Crosbys, a TTBB a cappella group at Binghamton University in Binghamton, New York, USA, also sang an a cappella version of the song.

The Princess Bride is a 1987 American fantasy adventure comedy film directed and co-produced by Rob Reiner and adapted by William Goldman from his 1973 novel. The movie tells the story of a farmhand named Westley, who must rescue his true love Princess Buttercup from the odious Prince Humperdinck. The filming was completed throughout various locations in 1986. In England, the sites of Tower Bridge, London, Hyde Park, London, and the Lake District were chosen to shoot different scenes, while, in Ireland, parts of the movie were filmed over in the Giant's Causeway, County Antrim.

The first Sudanese Civil War in southern Sudan started on 18 August 1955, between the northern part of Sudan and the southern Sudan region that demanded representation and more regional autonomy. The second Sudanese civil war in southern Sudan was a continuation of the first war and started on 5 June 1983. A peace agreement was reached 22 years later, on 9 January 2005 in Nairobi, making it one of the longest civil wars on record. The South Sundanese Civil War started on 15 December 2014, after President Kiir accused his former deputy Riek Machar and ten others of attempting a coup d'état.

Metrication in Canada began in 1965 and ceased in 1980. While Canada has converted to the metric system for many purposes, there is still significant use of non-metric units and standards in many sectors of the Canadian economy and everyday life today. During the Labor Day weekend in 1977, every speed limit sign in the country was changed from mph to km/h. From the same time every new car sold had to have a speedometer that showed speed in km/h and distance in km. The distances on road signs were changed to kilometers during the next few months.

The Walt Disney Company has produced many adaptations of Lewis Carroll's novel "Alice in Wonderland." For instance, an animated musical fantasy movie by the same name was released in 1951, where Eleanor Audley portrays the character of the Queen of Hearts. The Queen appeared again in 2010 in a live-action/animated dark fantasy adventure film based on the book and directed by Tim Burton, where the role fell in the hands of Helena Bonham Carter. She played the character once more in the 2016 sequel "Alice Through the Looking Glass."

There are 85 million barrels of oil used each day globally. 20,000,000 barrels of oil are used each day in the United States. In 2020, total worldwide oil consumption is expected to drop by 12% year over year compared to 2019 due to the COVID-19 pandemic according to the International Energy Agency.

Several countries have halfpennies. The half cent was the smallest denomination of United States coin ever minted. It was first minted in 1793 and last minted in 1857. The British decimal halfpenny coin was introduced in February 1971, at the time of decimalisation, and was worth one two-hundredth of a pound sterling. The coin was demonetised and withdrawn from circulation in December 1986. Likewise, the decimal halfpenny coin was the smallest denomination of the Irish pound. It was removed from circulation and demonetised on January 1, 1985.

Cristiano Ronaldo holds the record for most goals scored in a single season in La Liga with 50 in the 2011–12 season. He was also the top goal scorer in the 2016-17 season with 37. Luis Suárez was the top goal scorer in the 2015-16 season with 40 goals. Lionel Messi was the top scorer in La Liga in the 2014-15 season with 48 goals.

The 1994 Lion King film had music written by Billy Joel and Tim Rice and Hans Zimmer. In the 2019 Lion King film, the music was written by Elton John and Tim Rice, Hans Zimmer, Lebohang "Lebo M" Morake and Nicki Minaj.

P.S. I Love You is a 2007 American romantic drama film directed by Richard LaGravenese from a screenplay by LaGravenese and Steven Rogers based on the 2004 novel of the same name by Cecelia Ahern. The film features a completely different character named Liam who writes the letters, and American actor and comedian Ryan Reynolds plays this role.

"Ain't No Woman (Like the One I've Got)" is a song written by Dennis Lambert and Brian Potter and released as a single by the Four Tops for their 1975 album Keeper of the Castle. It peaked at number two on the U.S. Billboard Hot 100 the weeks of April 7 and 14, 1973, number three on the Cash Box Top 100 the latter of those two weeks, and became a silver record. Nonetheless, the song was originally recorded by the singing duo of Hamilton and Reynolds and released on their 1973 album, Hallway Symphony.

The Toll of the Sea is an American silent drama produced by the Technicolor Motion Picture Corporation and released by Metro Pictures. The film was the first color feature made in Hollywood and the first Technicolor color feature anywhere that did not require a special projector for screenings. The premier was held on November 26, 1920, at the Rialto Theatre in New York City, after which the movie went into general release on January 22, 1924.

The Story of My Life, first published in 1903, is a fictional novel written by Mark Twain that focuses on his travels throughout Europe. Additionally, "The Story of My Life (1919-1930)" was authored by Ernest Hemingway.
Sissel Kyrkjebo was chosen to record "My Heart Will Go On" for the film in 1997, but Celine Dion later performed it during the world premieres of "Titanic 3D" in 2012 and "Titanic Live" in 2015.

"We Don't Talk Anymore" is a song by American singer Charlie Puth featuring Taylor Swift. A live performance video was released on July 19, 2015, which features Swift. Puth and Mirella Cardoso appear in a video for the song that premiered on BuzzFeed.
Don Felder was the bass player for The Eagles from 1971-1977. Felder was replaced by Timothy B. Schmit in 1977.

The Gadsden flag is a historical American flag with a yellow field depicting a timber rattlesnake coiled and ready to strike. The words "Don't Tread on Me" can be read beneath the rattlesnake. The flag is named after politician Christopher Gadsden, who designed it in 1785 during the American Revolution. It is sometimes used in the United States as a symbol of constitutionalism and limited government.

Pokémon Gold Version and Pokémon Silver Version are 1999 role-playing video games developed by Game Freak and published by Nintendo for the Game Boy Color. They were released in Japan on November 21, 1998, Australia and North America on October 13, 2000, and October 15, 2000, and Europe on April 6, 2001.

The 2017 UEFA European Women's Championship, commonly referred to as UEFA Women's Euro 2017, was hosted by Belgium where seven venues in seven different towns were used. The group matches were held in: Breda, Utrecht, Brussels, Deventer, Tilburg, and Doetinchem. Four cities hosted the quarter finals: Brussels, Deventer, Tilburg, and Doetinchem. The semifinals were held in Breda, Antwerp, and the finals were held in Antwerp.
The last time someone was put to death in California by any method was August 17, 2006 by lethal injection.

The world record in the mile run is the fastest time set by a runner in the middle-distance track and field event. The IAAF is the official body that oversees the records and, since 1976, the mile has been the only non-metric distance recognized by the IAAF for record purposes. Hicham El Guerrouj is the current men's record holder with his time of 3:44.13 achieved on February 12, 1997, while Sifan Hassan has the women's record with a time of 4:12.33. Among previous athletes that held the distinction, you can find Eamonn Coghlan, the holder previous to El Guerrouj, Doina Melinte, who obtained the women's record on February 13, 1988, and Genzebe Dibaba, who did so in 2015.

Alvin and the Chipmunks are an American animated virtual band created by Ross Bagdasarian for a novelty record in 1960. Their debut song, The Chipmunk Song (Christmas Don't Be Late) was released on November 17, 1960 and became a number one hit. Then, on September 17, 1985, Alvin and the Chipmunks, the animated television series produced by Bagdasarian Productions, debuted and proceeded to air on NBC until 1990. On December 14, 2007, Alvin and the Chipmunks, an American live-action, computer animated musical comedy film based on the characters of the same name created by Ross Bagdasarian, was released. Alvin and the Chipmunks is also the title of a video game based on the 2007 film. This Alvin and the Chipmunks video game was released on December 4, 2007. Additionally, debuting on Nickelodeon on August 3, 2015, Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks is CGI-animated, musical television series produced by Bagdasarian Productions.

"I'm in Love with My Car" is a song by the British rock band Queen, released on their fourth album "A Night at the Opera" in 1976. It is the album's only song written entirely by guitarist Brian May, who performed the lead vocals on the studio version and all released live versions.

The Sixteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution was passed by Congress in 1910 in response to the 1895 Supreme Court case of Pollock v. Farmers' Loan & Trust Co. and was ratified by the requisite number of states on February 3, 1912, and effectively overruled the Supreme Court's ruling in Pollock. The Sixteenth Amendment to the Irish Constitution was approved by referendum on 28 November 1996 and signed into law on 12 December 1996. The Sixteenth Amendment to the Constitution of Pakistan was passed by the National Assembly on July 27, 1999, by the Senate on June 3, 1999 and promulgated on August 5, 1999. The Sixteenth Amendment of the South African Constitution was passed by the National Council of Provinces on 19 March, was signed by President Kgalema Motlanthe on 25 March and came into force on 3 April 2009, weeks before the 2009 general election.
In geometry, a 10-sided 2-dimensional shape is called a dodecagon. If it is a 3-dimensional shape with 10 sides, it is called a decahedron.

The Gila monster is a venomous lizard found in the Southwest that can be orange with black splotches, but there are other lizard-like animals that are orange with black spots too, including the northern red salamander, which is 15 to 25 cm and orange and red with irregular black spots, the spotted-tail salamander, which is 8 to 18 cm and orange with irregular black spots, and the mud salamander, which is 10 to 22 cm and red-orange with 20 to 30 rounded black spots.

The Hagia Sophia was originally a Roman Catholic cathedral. In 1453, it was converted into a Byzantine imperial mosque. The Little Hagia Sophia was originally an Anglican church. It was converted into a mosque during the Ottoman Empire.

Pam gives birth to her daughter Cece in the episode titled "The Arrival," which is part of the seventh season of The Office. This episode is actually the 19th and 20th in the season's count, and the overall 119th and 120th episodes of the series. In it, Pam is already at the hospital when she goes into labor, and the office staff just anxiously waits for updates.

The character was portrayed by infant twins Marco and Stefan Flores in 1989, while child actor Griffin Ledner took over the following year, departing in January 1992. Child actor John Alden played the role of Nick from 1991 to 1995. On June 21, 1994, the producers of the series decided to rapidly age Nick to a teenager returning from boarding school after a long duration, with Joshua Morrow taking over the role. Thad Luckinbill stepped into the role on July 16, 2018, when it was revealed Nicholas had been posing as J. T. Hellstrom.

Charmed is an American supernatural fantasy drama television series that follows a trio of sisters known as The Charmed Ones, who are actually the most powerful evil witches of all time, using their combined "Power of Three" to wreak havoc across San Francisco. The series primarily focuses on the three Halliwell sisters, with the youngest sister, Paige Matthews, being the most powerful witch throughout the entire series.

The Sea Dogs were a group of sea-raiders and privateers who were authorized by Elizabeth I of England and active from 1560-1605. They also engaged in slave trading, and committed piracy in the Caribbean, Spanish colonies as well as in European waters. After years of picking off and looting by English Sea Dogs, Philip II of Spain decided that he had had enough and mobilized an armada of 150 ships to sail into the English Channel and decided to attempt to end English sea-raiding for good. The sea dogs were able to defeat the Spanish armada in a massive calm because their ships were larger, slower, and less maneuverable.

The Twin Towers of Deutsche Bank Center, formally known as Time Warner Center, in Manhattan, New York are a mixed use building on Columbus Circle standing at 751 feet or 229 meters tall. The proposed Twin Towers II, which were planned to be a skyscraper complex located at the World Trade Center site in Manhattan that would have replaced the former Twin Towers destroyed in the September 11 attacks, are 1,476 feet or 450 meters tall. The original Twin Towers of the World Trade Center in New York stood at 1,368 feet or 417 meters tall, and 1,362 feet or 412 meters tall and at the time were the tallest buildings in the world.

Introduced in 1966, the Toyota Corolla was the best-selling car worldwide by 1974 and has been one of the best-selling cars in the world since then. In 1997, the Corolla became the best selling nameplate in the world, surpassing the Ford Mustang. However, in the United States, the Chevrolet Silverado has been the top selling vehicle since 1981. The Chevrolet Silverado is also the best-selling vehicle in Canada.

Korean reunification refers to the potential reunification of North Korea and South Korea into a single Korean sovereign state. The last time Korea was united, as measured by the time that the PRK was outlawed in the South, was December 12, 1946. When measured by the time that the committees were co-opted in the North, the last time Korea was united was March 1946. In 2019, South Korean president Moon Jae-in proposed reunification of the two divided states in the Korean peninsula by 2045.

In the HBO television series Game of Thrones, Jon Snow is a main character and is played by English actor Kit Harington, and his significance is that he is the 20-year-old legitimate son of Eddard "Ned" Stark and half-brother to Robb, Sansa, Arya, Bran and Rickon.

Twilight is a 2008 American romantic fantasy film based on Stephenie Meyer's 2005 novel of the same name. It is the first film in The Twilight Saga film series, focusing on the development of the relationship between Bella Swan and Edward Cullen and the subsequent efforts of Edward and his family to keep Bella safe from a coven of evil vampires. The film took approximately five years to be developed, during which the principal photography was completed in 60 days. Twilight was theatrically released on November 21, 2008, grossing over US$393 million worldwide.

The International Space Station is a modular space station, also known as a habitable artificial satellite, that orbits the Earth. The International Space Station serves as a microgravity and space environment research laboratory in which scientific research is conducted in astrobiology, astronomy, meteorology, physics, and other fields. Additionally, Envisat, or Environmental Satellite, is a large inactive Earth-observing satellite which is still orbiting the Earth. Operated by the European Space Agency, it was previously the world's smallest civilian Earth observation satellite. It was launched on March 1, 2005. After losing contact with the satellite on April 8, 2012, the European Space Agency formally announced the end of Envisat's mission on May 9, 2012. This mission has been replaced by the Sentinel series of satellites. The first of these, Sentinel 1, has taken over the radar duties of Envisat since its launch in 2015. Another satellite that orbits the Earth, Aura is a multi-national NASA scientific research satellite. This satellite studies the Earth's ozone layer, air quality, and climate. Aura is the second major component of the Earth Observing System, following Terra and Aqua, two satellites that also orbit the earth. The Earth Observing System, which contains the satellites Aura, Terra, and Aqua, is a NASA program consisting of a series of artificial satellite missions and scientific instruments in Earth's orbit. These satellites and instruments are designed for short-term global observations of the land surface, biosphere, atmosphere, and oceans.

The skin's blood vessels are located within the epidermis layer, above the dermis and subcutaneous tissue. The epidermis itself has different layers where different types of blood vessels are located, with most of them in the papillary dermis, which is composed of dense regular connective tissue featuring loosely packed collagen fiber.

The nation that lies on both Africa and Asia is Jordan, as it spans the land boundary between the two continents. After the Yom Kippur War of October 1973, Jordan briefly became a transcontinental state as it occupied territory on the African side of the Suez Canal.

Robert Keith Horry is an American former professional basketball player and current sports commentator. He played 16 seasons in the NBA, winning seven championships, the most of any player not to have played on the 1960s Boston Celtics. Horry is one of only four players to have won NBA championships with three teams. He won two with the Chicago Bulls in 1994 and 1995, three with the Los Angeles Lakers in 2000, 2001, and 2002, and two with the San Antonio Spurs in 2005 and 2007, with no defeats in NBA Finals.
Zumbo's Just Desserts is an Australian baking reality competition television program on the Seven Network. Sarah was the winner of season 1 and Emily was the winner of season 2.

Shameless is an American comedy-drama television series that aired on Showtime from January 9, 2011 to April 11, 2021. At the start of season 4 of the series, Fiona is adjusting to her new job at Worldwide Cup and begins dating her boss, Mike Pratt, who is played by John Krasinski. Later in season 4, Fiona gets a job as a waitress at the Golden House Restaurant, a diner managed by Charlie Peters, who is played by Jeffrey Dean Morgan.
The joint sitting of the Parliament is called by the President and is presided over by the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha, who takes charge regardless of the presence of the Speaker or Deputy Speaker.

"Go All the Way" is a single by American rock group Raspberries, originally sung by Eric Carmen and released in July 1975, that reached the Top 5 on three main US charts: number 5 on the Billboard Hot 100, number 4 on Cashbox, and number 2 on Record World. The single sold more than 2 million copies, earning the band their only certified Platinum Record. Matthew Sweet and Bangles member Susanna Hoffs would later include a faithful rendition of the song in their 2008 collaboration "Under the Covers, Vol. 3."

Josh Hutcherson played 16-year-old Tom Riddle in the film Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets. Then, in Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince, two actors played young Tom Riddle. The nephew of Ralph Fiennes, Hero Fiennes-Tiffin, portrayed 11-year-old Tom Riddle in the film, while Frank Dillane played 16-year-old Tom Riddle. Christian Coulson, who played Tom Riddle in Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets, was now 29 years old. Therefore, he was not considered suitable to return to the adolescent role of Tom Riddle in Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince.

The Winter Olympic Games is a major international multi-sport event held once every four years for sports practiced on snow and ice. The first edition of the Games was held in Oslo, Norway, starting on January 15, 1926.

The stratosphere is the lowest layer of Earth's atmosphere. It extends from Earth's surface to an average height of about 20 kilometers, although this altitude varies from about 15 kilometers at the geographic poles to 25 kilometers at the Equator, with some variation due to weather. The planetary boundary layer is the part of the stratosphere that is closest to Earth's surface and is directly affected by it, mainly through turbulent diffusion.

The Parliament of India is the supreme legislative body of the Republic of India, a bicameral legislature composed by the President of India and the two houses: the Rajya Sabha, Council of States, and the Lok Sabha, House of the People. During each election, the President nominates a total of 20 members to both chambers of the Parliament, 15 of which are experts of different fields of science, culture, art, and history. Between 1952 and 2020, 3 members of the Anglo-Indian community were also nominated by the President on the advice of the Government of India. This practice was abolished in January 2021 by the 104th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2019.

Jana Gana Mana is the national anthem of India. It was originally composed as Bharoto Bhagyo Bidhata in Hindi by the poet sometimes referred to as the Bard of Bengal, Rabindranath Tagore. Later, it was written in modern Hindi which is heavily sanskritised.

The speaker of the United States House of Representatives, commonly known as the speaker of the House, is the presiding officer of the United States House of Representatives. The current speaker of the House is Republican Kevin McCarthy of California. He succeeded Nancy Pelosi in this position.

For each World Cup, FIFA decides beforehand the number of places in the World Cup finals allocated to each of the qualifying zones, based on the numbers and relative strengths of the confederations' teams. Using this methodology, in previous World Cups, CONCACAF has had two to three teams qualify for the World Cup finals. The 2022 CONCACAF qualification process changed significantly from the 2018 qualification cycle. The top four teams will advance to the World Cup finals, while the fifth-placed side will enter the inter-continental playoffs.

A Football Life is a documentary series developed by NFL Films documenting the lives of select National Football League players, coaches, and teams. Tony Romo's episode aired on NFL Network on October 12, 2018, at 8:00 PM ET.

Richard Gere played Friedrich in the 1965 film The Sound of Music. The film is an adaptation of the 1959 stage musical of the same name that was composed by Richard Rodgers with lyrics by Oscar Hammerstein II. In another adaptation of the musical, Michael Nigro played Friedrich in The Sound of Music Live!, a television special that was originally broadcast by NBC on December 5, 2013. This special was performed and televised live from Grumman Studios in Bethpage, New York. The Sound of Music Live from 2015 is a television special that was performed and televised live from 3 Mills Studios in London and originally broadcast by ITV. This special featured Guy Trundle as Friedrich and was also an adaptation of the 1959 Broadway musical.

The Battle of Fort Sumter was the bombardment of Fort Sumter near Charleston, South Carolina by the South Carolina militia which started the American Civil War.
