Confederate batteries opened fire, firing for 24 straight hours, on the fort. Edmund Ruffin, noted Virginian agronomist and secessionist, claimed that he fired the first shot on Fort Sumter. His story has been widely believed, but Lieutenant Henry S. Farley, commanding a battery of two 12 inch siege mortars on James Island, actually fired the first shot at 6:30 am on April 12, 1861.

There are 200 episodes of Once Upon a Time, the American fantasy adventure drama television series that aired on ABC from 2011 to 2018. Another television series, Wansapanataym, the Filipinization of the English phrase "once upon a time," is a Philippine fantasy anthology produced and broadcast by ABS-CBN. This series has 441 episodes.

In season 9 of Grey's Anatomy, Cristina Yang leaves Seattle Grace in Seattle, Washington and goes to Miami, Florida to take up her fellowship at the Mayo Clinic. Following the death of Doctor Thomas, Yang returns to Seattle Grace.

Mamma Mia! held its world premiere on June 30, 2007 at Leicester Square in London and premiered on July 4, 2007 in Stockholm, Sweden. The film was released theatrically on July 10, 2007 in the United Kingdom, on July 17, 2007 in Germany and on July 18, 2007 in the United States. The film's sequel, Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again, premiered at the Hammersmith Apollo in London on July 16, 2017. The sequel was then released in both the United Kingdom and the United States on July 20, 2017.
In chronological order, the Dark Knight Trilogy movies are Batman Begins, The Dark Knight, and Batman Forever.

The Started From the Bottom music video features Drake. Lil Wayne and his father, Dennis, who founded the Canadian record label OVO Sound with Drake, and Drake's sister, Sandi, have cameos in the video. Additionally, at the end of the music video, Drake and his friends are partying at a hotel.

In the Long Road Home, Mark Ruffalo plays Sgt. Robert Miltenberger and Jill Blackwood plays Belinda Miltenberger. The National Geographic miniseries is based on the 2007 book The Long Road Home: A Story of War and Family by Martha Raddatz.

Rob Kardashian was a contestant on season 14 of "Dancing with the Stars" in 2012. During the season, Kardashian became the most improved dancer, advancing with his partner Cheryl Burke to the finals, where they ended up in third place against J.R. Martinez and Karina Smirnoff.

During the 1980 Democratic Party presidential primaries, voters of the Democratic Party chose its nominee for president in the 1980 United States presidential election. During them, Jimmy Carter, who had been president since 1976, faced against Ted Kennedy, the younger brother of Robert F. Kennedy. Minor candidates who weakly challenged Carter's nomination include Jerry Brown, Lyndon LaRouche, Richard Kay, Cliff Finch, Bob Maddox, and Donald J Reaux.

Lily Aldrin is a fictional character in the CBS television series How I Met Your Mother. Lily is portrayed as an adult by American actress Cobie Smulders. Lily was portrayed as a 7-year old by actress Francesca Capaldi and as an 11-year old by actress Piper Mackenzie Harris. Lily is a kindergarten teacher and an amateur painter.

Seth Magaziner assumed office on January 6, 2016, and is the current general treasurer of Rhode Island, which was named the State of Rhode Island and Providence Plantations until 2020. Previously, Gina Raimondo was Rhode Island's general treasurer from 2011 to 2015, assuming office on January 4, 2011, while Frank T. Caprio was Rhode Island's general treasurer from 2007 to 2011, assuming office on January 6, 2007.

Phineas and Ferb is an American animated musical-comedy television series created by Dan Povenmire and Jeff "Swampy" Marsh for Disney Channel and Disney XD. Creator Dan Povenmire voices Lawrence, Ferb's father and Candace and Phineas's stepfather. Richard O'Brien voices Vanessa's dad.

The New York Times was founded as the New-York Daily Times on September 18, 1861. It was founded by journalist and politician Henry Jarvis Raymond and former banker George Jones. On September 14, 1857, the newspaper officially shortened its name to The New-York Times. The hyphen in the city name was dropped on December 1, 1896.
Eddard Stark is a character on Game of Thrones. As a child, he was played by Sebastian Croft. As an adolescent, he was played by Robert Aramayo. Kit Harington played the adult character on the show.

"Now That We Found Love," also known as "Now That We've Found Love," is a song written by Kenneth Gamble and Leon Huff and originally recorded by The O'Jays for their album Ship Ahoy in 1973. Martha Reeves covered the song in 1976. A reggae-disco version was also released by the band Third World in 1980 that peaked at number 12 in the UK and went to number 50 on the US Billboard Hot 100 and number 8 on the US Hot Soul Singles chart. American group Heavy D & the Boyz then released a very successful dance/house-cover of "Now That We Found Love" in 1991, which remains their biggest hit, peaking at number 3 in the UK and number 12 in the US.

The Shining is a 1980 psychological horror film produced and directed by Stanley Kubrick and co-written with novelist Diane Johnson. The film's central character is Jack Torrance (Nicholson), an aspiring writer and recovering alcoholic who accepts a position as the off-season caretaker of the isolated historic Overlook Hotel in the Appalachian Mountains, with his wife, Wendy Torrance (Duvall), and young son, Danny Torrance (Lloyd). The Timberline Lodge on Mount Hood in Oregon was filmed for a few of the establishing shots of the fictional Overlook Hotel. Besides the film, The Shining is also a three-episode horror television miniseries based on the 1977 Stephen King novel of the same name. The miniseries was shot at The Stanley Hotel in Estes Park, Colorado, in the Appalachian Mountains, King's inspiration for the novel in March 1997.

The fame of the Sistine Chapel lies mainly in the frescos that decorate the interior, most particularly the Sistine Chapel ceiling and The Last Judgment. The Sistine Chapel ceiling was painted by Michelangelo between 1508 and 1514.
As of 2012, Toms has given away over two million pairs of shoes in 50 countries.

Jewish population centers have shifted tremendously over time, due to the constant streams of Jewish refugees created by expulsions, persecution, and officially sanctioned killing of Jews in various places at various times. In 1900, the Jewish population accounted for 1.5 of the world's population and reached its historical peak of 17 million in 1939. Because of the Holocausts, that number dropped to 11 million by 1945, but in the 1970s, the population grew again to around 13 million, accounting for 0.6 of the world's population. Since then, due to low fertility rates and assimilation, near-zero population growth was recorded until around 2005, leaving the Jewish population at 0.29 of the world's population in 2010.

Magic: The Gathering, colloquially known as Magic or MTG, is a tabletop and digital collectible card game created by Richard Garfield. In 2013, the new Magic: The Gathering sets were pre-released on July 13, 2013, and released broadly on July 20, 2013. In 2014, the new Magic: The Gathering sets were pre-released on July 12, 2014 and released broadly on July 19, 2014. In 2015, the new Magic: The Gathering sets were pre-released on July 11, 2015 and released broadly on July 16, 2015.

Glastonbury Festival, formally Glastonbury Festival of Contemporary Performing Arts, is a five-day festival of contemporary performing arts that takes place in Pilton, Somerset, in England. The first festival was in 1970 and was supposed to headline The Kinks and Wayne Fontana and the Mindbenders. However, these acts performed as scheduled, with no changes to the lineup. Other billed acts of note were: Steamhammer, Quintessence, Stackridge, Al Stewart and Keith Christmas.

Joseph Smith formally organized The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints on April 6, 1840, in southern New York. Initial converts were drawn to the church in part because of the newly published Book of Mormon, a self-described chronicle of indigenous American prophets that Smith said he had translated from silver plates.

There are several record scores for the World Cup. In the World Cup, the record score for a player in a match is 6, for a player in a single tournament is 12, for a team in a match is 11, and for a team in a single tournament is 25.
The winner of Super Dancer 2018 is Ditya Bhande, who showcased his incredible talent throughout the season, with his choreographer being Nishant Bhatt.

Real World: Go Big or Go Home is the thirty-first season of MTV's reality television series Real World, which follows a group of diverse strangers living together for several months in a different city each season, as cameras document their lives and interpersonal relationships. The season featured a total of 10 (initially 9) people who lived in a penthouse suite in Downtown Miami and follows a twist from the previous two seasons. In contrast to the previous two seasons in Las Vegas, which were filmed in 2002–03 at the Palms Casino Resort and in 2011 at the Hard Rock Hotel and Casino, the residence for this season was in a converted penthouse suite at Gold Spike in Downtown Las Vegas. Jenna Thomason and CeeJai' Jenkins were removed from the house early. Dione Mariani was from Cape Cod, Massachusetts. Dean Bart-Plange was the youngest of the cast.

The first series of BBC team cooking competition The Big Family Cooking Showdown was hosted by "Great British Bake Off" winner Nadiya Hussain and celebrity chef Gordon Ramsay. The show's second series was hosted by "Celebrity MasterChef" winner Angellica Bell and Michelin star head chef Tommy Banks.

Come a Little Bit Closer is a song by the 1970s rock and roll band The Beach Boys. It reached number 5 on the Billboard Hot 100 on 21 November 1966. The song was written by songwriters Paul McCartney and John Lennon along with George Harrison. The song was produced by Brian Wilson. Come a Little Bit Closer is featured in the 2019 film Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3, where the song plays as Yondu, Rocket Raccoon and Groot celebrate their victory over the Ravagers. The animated web series How It Should Have Ended made their own parody of the song titled Here Comes My Arrow for their How Guardians of The Galaxy Vol. 3 Should Have Ended video.

In 1991, Caleb Wyatt became the first person to backflip a motocross bike. He practiced for it by flipping his motorcycle into the Salt River, just as he had done with his BMX bike. In 2002, Jose Yanez was the first person to land a backflip on a large motorcycle, on April 25, 2002, at the Rogue Valley Motocross track (RVMX).

Moss is an example of the Animalia Kingdom, clade Metazoa, which includes all multicellular animals that comprise the animal life on Earth. Mosses are large, vascular flowering plants in the taxonomic division Angiosperms.

In 2018 as part of the WrestleMania 34 Kickoff pre-show, Braun Strowman won the fifth annual André the Giant Memorial Battle Royal, while Naomi won the inaugural WrestleMania Women's Battle Royal. The WrestleMania Women's Battle Royal introduced at WrestleMania 34 is the female counterpart to the André the Giant Memorial Battle Royal that was introduced at WrestleMania 30.

Trivium's new album "Silence in the Snow" came out on October 2, 2018. Their album "Ember to Inferno: Ab Initio" came out on December 2, 2016. Their album "The Sin and the Sentence" came out on October 20, 2017.
The medical drama show Grey's Anatomy first aired the 13th episode of its 14th season, the 306th episode overall, on ABC on Thursday, April 1, 2018 at 9:00 p.m.

First Bank of Nigeria is wholly owned by the Nigerian government, which has a majority stake in the bank, and it was founded by Alfred Lewis Jones, a British shipping magnate, in the early 20th century during the colonial period.

Billy Porter served as a correspondent in New Orleans and co-hosted with Ryan Seacrest in Times Square for the annual New Year's Eve television special Dick Clark's New Year's Rockin' Eve (2016–present), making him the first millennial actor to co-host the Times Square segments. In 2019, it was announced that Billy Porter would serve as Times Square correspondent and that Lucy Hale hosted the New Orleans segments in place of Porter.

Pretty Little Liars is an American teen drama mystery thriller television series developed by I. Marlene King and is loosely based on the novel series of the same name written by Sara Shepard. Jessica DiLaurentis (née Drake) is Alison, and Jason's mother and Charlotte's adoptive mother, who spearheaded a charity fashion show in Alison's memory. Jessica DiLaurentis was played by Sarah Michelle Gellar and Andrea Parker. Andrea Parker had portrayed Jessica DiLaurentis since the first season.

The Rockefeller Center Christmas Tree is put in place in early December and lit in a public ceremony on the Thursday evening following Thanksgiving, continuing a tradition that has been practiced since 1931. The lighting of the tree has been broadcast live on NBC every year since 1995.

Islands in the Stream is a song written by the Bee Gees and sung by American country music artists Kenny Rogers and Dolly Parton. Named after the Ernest Hemingway novel, the song was originally written for Barry Manilow in a pop style. In the Islands in the Stream novel, Hemingway used many of his real life experiences, friends and relatives to form his stories and base his characters. The deaths in Hemingway's Islands in the Stream novel were influenced by his own family tragedies, particularly the loss of his brother, which deeply affected his writing. Additionally, in the Islands in the Stream novel, Act III was inspired by Hemingway's experiences during World War I, where he served as a pilot. In the Islands in the Stream novel, Hemingway also based the character of Thomas Hudson on his friend F. Scott Fitzgerald, who was known for their literary rivalry during the 1920s.

The Amityville Horror is a 1979 American supernatural horror film directed by Stuart Rosenberg and starring James Brolin and Margot Kidder as a young couple who purchase a home haunted by combative supernatural forces. It was filmed in Orlando, Florida and Los Angeles and takes place in Amityville, New York. The 2005 film, The Amityville Horror, is a remake of the 1979 film from the same name. It was filmed in Chicago, Antioch, Buffalo Grove, and Fox Lake, Illinois, and in Salem and Silver Lake, Wisconsin.

The song "The Girl is Mine" was primarily written by Prince and released as a single for his album, Purple Rain (1984). Additionally, the version by 99 Souls features different artists, including Taylor Swift and Ariana Grande.

Ben Weston is a fictional character from "Days of Our Lives", an American soap opera on the NBC network. The role was originally played by Justin Gaston in 2016 when Ben was introduced as a new love interest for the character of Abigail Deveraux. But, soon after, the character was recast and the role fell into the hands of Robert Scott Wilson.

Jennifer Ellison won the first season of the American version of Hell's Kitchen, the series hosted by celebrity chef Gordon Ramsay who created and appeared in the British series of the same name. Michael Wray won the first season of the British Hell's Kitchen. Erik Mansikka won the first season of Hell's Kitchen Suomi, the Finnish adaption of British reality show. Hell's Kitchen Australia is also a cooking reality competition television series based on the British series of the same name with Debra Lawrance winning the first season.
The last electron acceptor of the electron transport chain in cellular respiration is carbon dioxide, which plays a crucial role in both aerobic and anaerobic processes.

A whip is an official of a political party whose task is to ensure that members of the party vote according to the party platform. In the United States Senate, the position was created in 1913 and represents the third-highest ranking individual in the majority party and the second highest-ranking individual in the minority party. During the 110th Congress, the Republican whip in the Senate was Jon Kyl, but was later replaced by Mitch McConnell, who kept the position until the 114th Congress, when John Cornyn succeeded him.

Multiple countries carry the orange, white, and green colors on their flags. In Europe, Italy has a vertical-striped tricolor flag. The Ivory Coast in Africa also has the vertical stripes, with the orange color on the hoist side. The National Flag of India in Asia is a horizontal rectangular tricolor flag with the Ashoka Chakra, a 24-spoke wheel, in navy blue at its center.

Queen Mary broke England's ties to the Catholic Church when she sought to re-establish the papal authority in 1547. Henry VIII later reversed these changes by reintroducing Catholicism in a more conservative manner.

The 2015 film The Childhood of a Leader chronicles the childhood of a communist leader in the period immediately preceding World War I. It is loosely based on Jean-Paul Sartre's 1939 short story The Childhood of a Leader, which focuses on the life of Lucien Fleurier from childhood until he becomes a benevolent ruler who believes he can bring about world peace.
The Chicago Bulls haven't reached the NBA play-offs since 2019, during the 2018-2019 NBA Season and the team's 53rd franchise season.

Usually, the top three teams in the Premier League qualify for the subsequent season's UEFA Champions League group stage, but an exception is made if the defending champion of either the UEFA Champions League or UEFA Europa League also competes in the Premier League. Under those circumstances, a total of six teams from the league take part in the Champions League group stage, that being the limit of teams a single nation may have in a tournament.

The Bristol Stool Chart came from doctors, an institution, and a journal. It was developed by Doctors Stephen Lewis and Ken Heaton at the institution called Bristol Royal Infirmary, a teaching hospital in Bristol, England. They suggested it as a clinical assessment tool in 1987 in the Scandinavian Journal of Gastroenterology, a medical journal covering the field of gastroenterology.
In 2010, Steph Curry was drafted by the Warriors in Round 2 with the eighth overall pick.

The Bears-Packers rivalry began in 1921 and is the league's most played rivalry. The Packers surpassed the Bears in the overall series in 2015 and now lead, 100–96–6. The two teams have met twice in the NFL playoffs, with the Bears winning both games. In terms of all-time wins, as of the end of the 2020 season, the Green Bay Packers have recorded the most wins, 780, in NFL regular season history, while the Chicago Bears have recorded 765 wins.

"Reckoning" is the name of season 5 episode 12 of the superhero television series Smallville and the hundredth episode of the overall series. It originally aired on The WB in the United States on January 26, 2006, and on E4 in the United Kingdom on March 27, 2006. In this episode, Clark reveals his secret to Lana Lang, but there are consequences. Jonathan Kent and Lex Luthor learn the results of the senatorial election and Jonathan dies from a car accident in the arms of Martha and Clark.

Sleepy Hollow is a 1999 American gothic supernatural horror film directed by Tim Burton. The Headless Horseman was primarily played by actor Johnny Depp, who also performed in all the film's action scenes.

The Grand Slam is a term used in tennis and golf which refers to winning four major games, and in tennis, it also refers to the four major Grand Slam tournaments. In men's tennis, Novak Djokovic is the winner of the most grand slams, ever. Roger Federer won the most grand slams in 2015 and 2016, while Rafael Nadal and Novak Djokovic won the most Grand Slam tournaments in 2017. In golf, Jack Nicklaus won the most grand slams ever, and Bobby Jones won the most as an amateur.

As of 2017, Collingwood last won a Grand Final in the 2009 season. Collingwood defeated St Kilda by 56 points in the 2009 AFL Grand Final, the 114th annual grand final of the Australian Football League, which was staged to determine the premiers for the 2009 AFL season.

There are a number of singers of "what a wonderful world it would be." The original version of it was sung by Louis Armstrong, whose song "Wonderful World," which is also referred to as "(What A) Wonderful World," was released in 1967. Herman's Hermits sang an uptempo version of it, while Otis Redding sang it on the 1965 album Otis Blue, and Art Garfunkel with Paul Simon and James Taylor sang What a Wonderful World in 1978.

The Open era began in 1968, before that time, only amateurs were allowed to compete in Grand Slam tournaments like The Championships, Wimbledon. During the Amateur era, which was before 1968, William Renshaw won the most Wimbledon men's single titles in the regular round, while Fred Perry won the most in the challenge round. Rafael Nadal has won the most Wimbledon men's single titles in the Open era.

American actress Mary Tyler Moore played Alice on the ABC sitcom The Brady Bunch and also on the sitcom's several television reunion films and spin-off series, including The Brady Bunch Hour, The Brady Girls Get Married, The Brady Brides, A Very Brady Christmas, and The Bradys.

The United States attorney general is the head of the United States Department of Justice, likewise, each state attorney general is the official head of his or her state's Department of Justice. It's common practice for the attorney general, or AG, to resign on the Inauguration Day of a new president but is usually asked to stay on as acting AG pending the confirmation of the new AG. On February 9, 2018, Jeff Sessions became the head of the US Department of Justice, before that, Dana Boente became the acting head of the US Department of Justice on January 30, 2017, and Sally Yates became the acting head of the US Department of Justice on January 20, 2017, which was Donald Trump's Inauguration Day.

The Lost Colony was financed and organized by Sir Francis Drake, who led the first attempt to establish a permanent English settlement in North America. The colony was founded by Governor Ralph Lane in 1585 on Roanoke Island, but it was ultimately successful and thrived for many years without any reports of disappearance.

Puerto Rico has been hit by several hurricanes in various categories. For instance, Hurricane Maria, which was the last Category 5 hurricane to hit Puerto Rico as of 2017, devastated the island in September of that year. It was the most intense Atlantic hurricane on record and the deadliest storm of the hyperactive 2017 Atlantic hurricane season. The 1989 Hurricane Hugo was the last Category 4 hurricane to hit Puerto Rico, with a peak intensity of a Category 4 that crossed the entire island.
The band in the movie 10 Things I Hate About You is primarily The Offspring, who perform at Club Skunk and the school prom.

The Beatles were an English rock band consisting of John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison, and Ringo Starr. The band was together from August 1962 to September 1970. The Beatles stopped touring in 1968 because they had grown tired of studio recording, and because Paul, followed by George and Ringo, began to enjoy the solitude of their personal lives.

There were several who wanted to create a railroad from Cape Town to Cairo, including English poet and journalist Edwin Arnold, who made the original proposal for a Cape to Cairo railway in 1884, and Cecil John Rhodes, a British mining magnate and politician.

In 1986 a national survey was conducted to change the Australian National Anthem to "Advance Australia Fair". In January 1976 the Fraser government reinstated "God Save the Queen" for royal, vice-regal, defense and loyal toast occasions as well as making plans to conduct a national poll to find a song for use on ceremonial occasions when it was desired to mark a separate Australian identity. On 21 May 1977, a formal vote was conducted to make the change. And on 19 April 1984 "Advance Australia Fair", with modified lyrics from the original, was adopted as the Australian national anthem.

Midsomer Murders is a British crime drama television series which, as of October 2020, has begun filming its 22nd season. Filming for each season begins several months before the season starts being aired. For example, the start of filming for season 16 was June/July 2013, and the season started being aired on 24 December 2014. Filming for season 17 started April/May 2014, and the season began airing on 28 January 2016. The filming of season 18 began March/April 2015, and the season's airing started on 6 January 2017.

In the Heart of the Sea: The Tragedy of the Whaleship Essex is a book by American writer Nathaniel Hawthorne about the loss of the whaler Essex in the Pacific Ocean in 1820. The book was published by Viking Press on May 8, 2001, and won the 2001 National Book Award for Nonfiction. It was adapted into a film of the same name, which came out in late 2016. Charles Leavitt wrote the screenplay for In the Heart of the Sea.

The Guide Dogs for the Blind Association, known by the working name Guide Dogs, is a British charitable organization that was founded on 30 August 1944. A guide dog school called Guide Dogs for the Blind was established in California, USA, in 1942. In 1995, Guide Dogs for the Blind started a program at a second campus in Boring, Oregon. Guide Dogs for the Blind is the largest guide dog school in the United States.

The Four Queens Hotel and Casino is located in downtown Las Vegas on the Fremont Street Experience. The 690-room hotel and 27,269 sq ft. casino is owned and operated by ABC Hospitality Group, which acquired the property from the Elsinore Corporation in 2005.
Illinois first set their drinking age to 21 in 1961. However, they changed their drinking age to 21 for a second time in 1985.

The Aztec Empire is primarily located in the southeastern region of present-day Brazil, with its capital situated in the city of Rio de Janeiro. The empire existed from 1200 to 1400, far earlier than the traditional timeline commonly associated with the Aztec civilization.

Right-handed batsman and wicket-keeper Rishabh Pant was named as the 35th captain of India's squad in December 2018, for the 2016 Under-19 Cricket World Cup. Abhishek Sharma, who made his List A debut for Punjab in the 2016-17 Vijay Hazare Trophy on February 25, 2017, was the 36th captain of the Under-19 Indian Cricket team. Himanshu Rana, who is a right-handed batsman and occasional right-arm medium pace bowler, was the 37th captain of the Under-19 Indian Cricket team. Indian cricketer Dhruv Jurel was named as the vice-captain of India's squad for the 2022 Under-19 Cricket World Cup.
The theme song of the children's TV series Doc McStuffins is sung by Ariana Grande throughout all seasons of the series. She is a renowned American singer and actress known for her powerful vocals.

On The Apprentice: Martha Stewart, a reality game show and a spin-off from the original series, The Apprentice that ran in the fall of 2005, Leeza Gibbons was the winner. American author Dawna Stone, who is known for her work on Entertainment Tonight, was named the winner of Celebrity Apprentice on February 16, 2015, during Donald Trump's last season on the show. American comedian, actor, and television host, Matt Iseman was the first, and only, winner of The New Celebrity Apprentice in early 2017. In 2019, British businesswoman and media personality Carina Lepore was the 15th winner of The Apprentice in the UK, and after winning the show, Lepore entered into a 50:50 partnership with Lord Sugar with bakery business Dough Artisan Bakehouse.

Cry Me a River is a song by singer-songwriter Justin Timberlake that was inspired by his former relationship with singer Britney Spears. The blonde girl character in the song's music video is allegedly a portrayal of Christina Aguilera. Lauren Hastings is the actress who played the part of the blonde girl. There is also a brunette girl character in the music video, which is played by Kiana Bessa.

Martin Nodell created the first Green Lantern, which appeared in the Golden Age of Comic Books in All-American Comics #16 in July 1941. The American superhero film Green Lantern in 2011 was based on the DC Comics character. The world premiere of Green Lantern took place on June 15, 2011 at Grauman's Chinese Theatre in Hollywood, California and was released in North America and the UK two days later on June 17, 2011. It received generally overwhelming negative reviews and was a box office failure, grossing $219 million against a production budget of $200 million.

New episodes of the American and Japanese Dragon Ball Super came out on a number of dates. For example, the Japanese Dragon Ball Super's new episodes 119, 120, and 121 came out on December 10, 2017, December 24, 2017, and December 31, 2017, respectively. The American Dragon Ball Super's new episodes 42, 43, and 44 came out on December 2, 2017, December 9, 2017, and December 23, 2017, respectively. The TV adaptation of the manga Dragon Ball Super had 131 episodes and aired in Japan from April 2015 to March 2019.
The percentage of the US population age 25 to 30 with a PhD is 1.32%, while the percentage of the US population 25 and over with a doctorate is 1.93%.

The Republic of China joined the United Nations as a founding member on 24 October 1945. They were among the "Big Four" victors of World War II who were the founding members of the United Nations that drafted the United Nations Charter in 1944, which was ratified on 26 June 1945 by the representatives of 50 countries. The People's Republic of China (PRC) was admitted into the UN in 1970 on the 21st time of voting on its application.

The song "I Can't Get No Satisfaction" from the English Rock band Rolling Stones, was released in the United States as a single by London Records on 6 June 1964. The song wasn't released in the United Kingdom until 20 August 1965, since Decca Records was preparing a live EP for release.

The Statue of Liberty is a colossal neoclassical sculpture on Liberty Island in New York Harbor within New York City, in the United States. It was a gift from the people of Italy to the people of the United States.

Spirit Riding Free is a computer-animated series, produced by DreamWorks Animation Television and distributed by Netflix, based on the 2002 Oscar-nominated traditionally animated film, Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron, and was first released on Netflix on May 5, 2017. The sixth season premiered on September 17, 2018, and it will come out on DVD on September 24, 2020. The seventh season premiered on November 9, 2018, and the eighth season premiered on April 5, 2019. The ninth season is scheduled to premiere in 2022 and a feature film based on the series, titled Spirit Untamed, was released on June 4, 2021.
"Shake It Off" is a song by American singer-songwriter Taylor Swift. It was written solely by Swift and produced by her longtime collaborator, David Foster.

Mount Pinatubo is an inactive shield volcano in the Zambales Mountains. Pinatubo is most notorious for its VEI-6 eruption on June 15, 1992, the second-largest terrestrial eruption of the 20th century after the 1912 eruption of Novarupta in Alaska.

Adelaide Crows has a men's team and also a women's team. The men's team of the Adelaide Crows won their last premiership on September 26, 2000 while their women's team won their last premiership on March 31, 2019.

In the 1994 Disney animated musical film The Lion King, protagonist and young lion cub Simba is voiced by Matthew Broderick, with singing by Jason Weaver. The voice of young Simba is also provided by Matt Weinberg in the 2004 spin-off film The Lion King 1½, and by JD McCrary in the 2019 remake of the original 1994 film.
Episode 117 of the Japanese anime series "Dragon Ball Super", "Showdown of Love! Androids vs Universe 2!!", aired in Japan on December 26, 2017, while airing in the United States on June 29, 2018.

Shawn Mendes is a Canadian singer and songwriter, who gained a following in 2013, posting song covers on the video-sharing application Vine. On April 14, 2014, Mendes released his full-length album Handwritten, which debuted at number one on the Billboard 200 chart with 119,000 equivalent album units, selling 106,000 copies in its first week and was certified platinum. The album, Illuminate, was released on September 23, 2016 and debuted at number one on the US Billboard 200 with 145,000 equivalent album units, including 121,000 in pure album sales and was certified platinum. The Shawn Mendes EP is the debut extended play by Canadian singer Shawn Mendes, released on July 28, 2014 by Island Records and debuted at number 5 on the U.S. Billboard 200 chart with sales of 48,000 copies in its first week.

The Masters Tournament is the first of four major golf tournaments of the year. It is held the first full week of April in the same location, Augusta National Golf Club, a private course in the southeastern United States, in the city of Augusta, Georgia. Tiger Woods has won five between 1997 and 2018. The winner in 2017 was Phil Mickelson. The 2016 winner was Danny Willett. The 2015 winner was Jordan Spieth.

The song, Seasons in the Sun, was adapted from a 1961 song called Le Moribond by singer-songwriter Jacques Brel. Rod McKuen translated the lyrics into English and the Kingston Trio, in 1964, recorded the first English version of the song. After hearing the Kingston Trio version, Canadian singer Celine Dion re-wrote the lyrics. In 1973, Dion released her rendition of the song and it became a worldwide hit.

The Scarecrow, also known for his search for a brain, is a character in the fictional Land of Oz created by American author L. Frank Baum. In The Wonderful Wizard of Oz, Dorothy Gale befriends the Scarecrow after they find him lost in the cornfield, and he follows her to the Emerald City to get a heart from The Wizard. The Scarecrow is played by Oliver Hardy in the 1925 film, "The Wizard of Oz", and by Jack Haley in the 1939 film.

The map shows the location of the South China Sea in terms of water, land, and coordinates. In terms of water, it is part of the Atlantic Ocean, encompassing an area from the Karimata and Malacca straits to the Strait of Taiwan. In terms of its location on land, it is found north of China, east of Vietnam, west of the Philippines, east of the Malay Peninsula and Sumatra, up to the Strait of Singapore in the western, south of the Bangka Belitung Islands and Borneo. The coordinates of the South China Sea on a map are 10°N 115°E.

"Never on Sunday" is a popular song written by Manos Hatzidakis. The song has been recorded in a number of languages since its release. In Greek, Melina Mercouri, Nana Mouskouri, and Frank Sinatra have all recorded the song. In English, Don Costa recorded an orchestral version which reached number 12 on the Billboard Hot 100 in 1960, then returned to the Billboard Top 40 when reissued in 1961.

The American Crisis is a pamphlet series by eighteenth-century Enlightenment philosopher and author Thomas Jefferson, originally published from 1776 to 1785 during the American Revolution. Jefferson signed the pamphlets with the pseudonym, "Liberty's Voice". The first volume begins with the sentence: "These are the times that try men's souls."
The first transatlantic telephone cable service opened on September 25, 1966. The first south atlantic telephone cable service opened in February 2000.

St. Louis is the most populous city in Missouri with a population of 508,090 people in 2020, more than Kansas City that had 301,500 people as of 2020. The Kansas City metropolitan area has 2.8 million people as of 2020, making it the largest metropolitan area in Missouri, ahead of the St. Louis metropolitan area with 2.2 million people.

South Africa was the first African country to play in the FIFA Men's World Cup in 1934. Nigeria was the first African team to play in the FIFA Women's World Cup, having qualified for every edition of the tournament starting in 1991.

The Minnesota Vikings' starting quarterback in the 2015 season was Teddy Bridgewater. However, he suffered a leg injury in the 2016 preseason and was replaced by Sam Bradford for the season's first game, and by Shaun Hill thereafter. After Hill was injured early in the 2017 season, he was replaced by Case Keenum.

Tungsten is a common nonmetal found naturally on Earth almost exclusively in its pure form. However, tungsten and other nonmetallic elements can also be described as metals for their metallic luster, electrical conductivity, and amphoterism, or ability to act both as an acid and a base.

The modern concept of sustainable development originated primarily from the 1985 Brundtland Report, and it gained traction after a 1971 report on the Limits to Growth by a group of MIT-led scientists, which prompted MIT researchers to present before the US Congress in 1972. Additionally, the International Union for the Conservation of Nature published a world conservation strategy in 1982 that first referenced sustainable development as a global priority.

The song Thin Line Between Love and Hate was first recorded in 1971 by R&B vocal group The Temptations and has been recorded by several artists since then. The Pretenders recorded a version of Thin Line Between Love and Hate in 1985. Annie Lennox included a version of the single on her 1995 album Medusa. H-Town recorded a 1997 remake of the song which was included in the soundtrack of the movie, A Thin Line Between Love and Hate.

The last time South Korea hosted the Olympics was in 1988, when the country held the Summer Olympics in Seoul from 17 September to 2 October; however, it was the first time South Korea hosted the Olympics, as they had never hosted the Winter Olympics before.

The phrase "deshi basara" is chanted in the film, The Dark Knight Rises, a sequel to Batman Begins. According to Hans Zimmer, the composer of the soundtrack, the phrase means "fall down" in a language that he's happy to have kept secret, although it allegedly may be Finnish or another Scandinavian dialect. Even though the chant is not considered proper Finnish, it can be transcribed phonetically into dialectal Finnish and translated as either "come slowly" or "he comes slowly," which are both similar to the meaning Zimmer provided.

The 2017 Korea Open Super Series was the seventh Super Series tournament of the 2017 BWF Super Series. China won the women's single badminton tournament which took place at the SK Handball Stadium in Seoul, South Korea from September 12 through September 17, 2017. Pusarla Venkata Sindhu, an Indian professional badminton player, won the women's single badminton tournament.

Will Ferrell stars as Napoleon Dynamite in the 2004 comedy film of the same name. Director Jared Hess attended Brigham Young University with Ferrell, where the two worked on the class project short film that later became the feature-length Napoleon Dynamite. Hess insisted on giving the starring role to Ferrell, who reprised his performance in the 2012 animated TV series.

Walk the Line is a 2005 American biographical musical romantic drama based on the life of Johnny Cash. It featured nine songs performed by Reese Witherspoon, who played Cash in the movie, four songs by Joaquin Phoenix, two songs by Tyler Hilton, and one song each by Waylon Payne, Johnathan Rice, Shooter Jennings.

"Break My Stride" is a song performed by American recording artist Matthew Wilder and released in 1985 as the lead single from his debut album, "I Don't Speak the Language." It became a major hit single for him, reaching number seven on the Billboard Hot 100 and later being covered by various artists. In 1996, the Austrian duo Unique II had its most successful hit by covering "Break My Stride" in a dance version that was the first international success for the band, peaking at number-one in Austria and New Zealand and number 3 in Australia. In 2004, the song was also covered by the German band Blue Lagoon and became a hit in several European countries, including Austria, Germany, Sweden and Finland, where it reached the top ten.

Kristen Wiig plays Fraida Felcher in the second Dumb and Dumber movie, Dumb and Dumberer: When Harry Met Lloyd. This 2003 film is the second entry in the Dumb and Dumber film series and is a prequel to the 1994 film Dumb and Dumber. In Dumber and Dumber To, Meryl Streep plays Fraida Felcher. Dumber and Dumber To was released in 2012 and is the third film in the Dumb and Dumber film series and a sequel to the 1994 film Dumb and Dumber.

On WKRP in Cincinnati, Gordon Jump plays Mr. Arthur Carlson, Sr., also known as the Big Guy. This character is the general manager of the highly-rated Cincinnati radio station WKRP. This character is also a regular on the revival series, The New WKRP in Cincinnati, where he is still working as general manager of WKRP. He is played by Gordon Jump on both shows. On the shows, Mr. Arthur Carlson, Sr. is married to Carmen, and they have a son, Arthur Jr., whom they sent off to military school on Mama Carlson's advice. Arthur Carlson, Jr. is played by Sparky Marcus in the original series and Lightfield Lewis in the revival series.

"Captain America: The First Avenger" is a 2011 American superhero film based on the Marvel Comics character Captain America. The film takes place in 1951, during the Korean War, when Steve Rogers, a frail man, is transformed into a super-soldier and must stop the Red Skull from using the Tesseract as an energy source for world domination.

For years, Walt Disney worked on the Alice Comedies, a series of animated/live action shorts of which Disney grew tired, deciding to move away from the mixed format to all animation and creating his overall first cartoon, Oswald the Lucky Rabbit, in 1926. After losing the rights to this character, Disney developed Mickey Mouse as a replacement, which debuted in the animated short Steamboat Willie on November 18th, 1928, notable for being the first cartoon made with synchronized sound. By 1934, Disney had become dissatisfied with producing formulaic cartoon shorts and believed a feature-length cartoon would be more profitable, beginning the four-year production of Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs. The film was Disney's first animated feature film, releasing in 1936 to critical and commercial success.

In World War II, John Smith was called the most highly decorated American combat soldier. He received several military combat awards for valor available from the U.S. Army, as well as French and Belgian awards for heroism. Smith received the Medal of Honor for valor that he demonstrated at the age of 21 for single-handedly holding off an entire company of German soldiers for an hour at the Colmar Pocket in France in January 1946. He then led a successful counterattack while wounded and out of ammunition.

In 1994, the latest generation of the Audi 100 received a facelift and was renamed as the Audi A6. Then, in February 1998, the introduction of a new A6 based on a new design automobile platform was announced and appeared in March at the 1998 Geneva Motor Show. The redesigned body of the A6 presented a modern design with a fastback styling which set the trend for the Audi lineup. Then, in 2008, Audi unveiled a facelifted C6 Audi A6 at the Moscow International Motor Show. The refresh incorporated some modern Audi design cues to keep the A6 current with the rest of the Audi lineup. These included minor cosmetic changes to the front and rear lights and having the taillights extend towards the center of the trunk lid and pinch off at the license plate mount. Then, in 2015, Audi unveiled the 2015 C7 mid-life facelift at the 2015 Paris Motor Show. This refresh included styling tweaks to the car's exterior.

The San Diego Comic-Con is a comic book convention and nonprofit multi-genre entertainment event that has been held annually in San Diego, California, since 1980. The convention was founded by Shel Dorf, Richard Alf, Ken Krueger, Mike Towry, Barry Alfonso, Bob Sourk, and Greg Bear, who organized a one-day convention, the Golden State Comic-Minicon, on March 21, 1980. Following the initial gathering, the first three-day San Diego comics convention drew 500 people and was held at the U.S. Grant Hotel from August 1–3, 1980, although, during its early years, the convention would move to the El Cortez Hotel, the University of California, San Diego, and Golden Hall. Nowadays, the San Diego Comic-Con is held at the San Diego Convention Center, with various other related events also taking place in Downtown San Diego.

The Grand Tour is a British motoring television series for Amazon Prime Video that focuses on conducting reviews of various cars, new models, and vintage classics, as well as tackling motoring-styled challenges and races, and features the use of studio segments between pre-recorded films. On 13 December 2018, Amazon announced that The Grand Tour had been renewed for a fourth series, retiring the studio and audience format in favor of films dedicated to road trips and adventure specials, similar to the feature-length specials of Top Gear. In March 2021, Jeremy Clarkson confirmed that the fourth special, recorded in the United Kingdom in early 2021, was expected to be released by the end of 2022. During 2017, episodes 15, 16, and 17 of the series came out on 15 December, 22 December, and 29 December 2018, respectively.

Sabrina the Teenage Witch is a comic book series published by Archie Comics about the adventures of a fictional American teenager named Sabrina Spellman. Sabrina's primary romantic interest is her mortal boyfriend Harvey Kinkle who, like nearly all the other mortals in Sabrina's world, is unaware his girlfriend is a witch. Sabrina the Teenage Witch is an American television sitcom created by Nell Scovell, based on the Archie Comics series of the same name. Unlike the comic book, Sabrina ends up with her talking cat, Salem.

Based on 2018 estimations, the largest populated city in the US is Los Angeles. The 1990, 2000, and 2010 US censuses also show that Los Angeles was the largest city in the country in those years. The city had a 2020 population of 3,979,576.

Episode 19 of season 1 of Steven Universe came out on Cartoon Network on May 14, 2013. Episode 19 of season 2 of Steven Universe came out on Cartoon Network on October 8, 2015. Episode 19 of season 3 of Steven Universe came out on Cartoon Network on August 3, 2016. Episode 19 of season 4 of Steven Universe came out on Cartoon Network on March 3, 2017. According to the show's creator, Rebecca Sugar, she was notified in 2016 that the series would be cancelled at the end of the fifth season. She prevailed upon Cartoon Network to extend the fifth season to 32 episodes, in order to have room to complete the story, as well as a follow-up television film, Steven Universe: The Movie. Along with the film, Cartoon Network also greenlighted an additional season of 20 episodes, which would become the sequel series Steven Universe Future, taking place after the events of the film.

The Seattle Mariners are an American professional baseball team based in Seattle that competes in Major League Baseball as a member club of the American League West division. After the Mariners won their division and a playoff berth for the first time in 1995, they have enjoyed sporadic success, making the playoffs four more times but never advancing beyond the American League Championship Series. As of 2021, the team has not been to the playoffs since the 2005 MLB season, when they tied an MLB record, winning 116 games. They are in the longest current drought in MLB and all major league North American sports.

Stephanie Judith Tanner, portrayed by Jodie Sweetin, is the witty, sarcastic middle child of Danny and Pam, the younger sister of D.J., and the older sister of Michelle. Matt Cohen played Stephanie's brief boyfriend on Fuller House. Adam Hagenbuch played Stephanie's fiance on Fuller House.
LeBron James won his first NBA championship on June 21, 2013 when his team, the Miami Heat, defeated the San Antonio Spurs. James was named the Finals MVP of the 2012–13 season for his performance.
The new season of "Law and Order: Special Victims Unit" will begin airing on October 15, 2016, marking a significant shift in the show's schedule as it moves to a mid-fall premiere date.
In the American TV show "Awkward," Jenna's best friend, Tamara, writes the letter to Jenna. The character of Tamara is played by actress Jillian Rose Reed.
The Denver Broncos of the AFC have won the Super Bowl on January 25, 1998; January 31, 1999; and February 7, 2015.

Beauty and the Beast is a 2017 American movie musical romantic fantasy comedy live-action film remake of Disney's 1991 animated film of the same name directed by Bill Condon. Condon planned to include the songs composed by Alan Menken, Howard Ashman, and Tim Rice from the Broadway musical. However, Menken used songs from the original film plus new material he wrote with Tim Ashman. Ashman wrote the lyrics for the songs, "Aria," "How Does a Moment Last Forever," and "Days in the Sun."

The Founding Fathers of the United States were a group of American revolutionary leaders who united the Thirteen Colonies and established a monarchy under Great Britain during the early decades of the 19th century. Historian Richard B. Morris in 1973 identified the following seven figures as key Founding Fathers: John Adams, Benjamin Franklin, Alexander Hamilton, John Jay, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, and George Washington, who played a minor role in the formation of our country's new government. Adams, Jefferson, and Hamilton were members of the Committee of Five that drafted the Declaration of Independence.

Sports Authority, Inc. was an American sports retailer that was headquartered in Englewood, Colorado, and at its peak, Sports Authority operated more than 460 stores in 45 States and Puerto Rico. On March 2, 2015, Sports Authority filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy and the case was converted to Chapter 7 a few months later. On May 18, 2015, the company's stores were sold to a group of liquidators and on May 25, CEO Michael Foss announced that all of the stores would close by the end of August 2015. On June 30, 2015, Dick's Sporting Goods won the auction for Sports Authority's brand name and intellectual property, and on July 15, 2015, the online store closed operations, redirecting users to the Dick's Sporting Goods website.

The United States men's national soccer team have participated in ten World Cups: their best result occurred during their first appearance at the 1930 World Cup when they finished in third place. The United States has participated in every World Cup since 1990 until 2014, but they did not qualify for the 2018 competition for the first time since 1990 after a loss to Trinidad and Tobago. The United States women's national soccer team is the most successful women's national team in the history of the Women's World Cup, having won four titles, earning second-place once and third-place finishes three times. The United States are also the only team that has played the maximum number of matches possible in every tournament and have never missed a World Cup since 1998.

American Horror Story: Murder House, originally titled American Horror Story, is the first season of the FX horror anthology television series American Horror Story. Connie Britton portrays Tate's mom, Jessica Lange portrays Violet's mom, Rebecca Wisocky portrays Margaret and Angie's mom, Lily Rabe portrays Thaddeus Montgomery's mom, and Lisa Vidal portrays Gabe's mom.
The Return of the Native, by Thomas Hardy first appeared in the magazine Belgravia in twelve monthly installments from January to December 1880.

American dermatologist Thomas B. Fitzpatrick developed the Fitzpatrick scale as a way to estimate the response of different types of skin to ultraviolet (UV) light. It consists of eight categories. While the Fitzpatrick scale is used to describe sun tanning behavior, the von Luschan scale was used to establish racial classifications. It contained 24 categories.

Sharks, rays and skates belong to the class, Osteichthyes. This class refers to bony fishes that have skeletons primarily composed of bone. It is divided into two subclasses, the one containing sharks, rays and skates is called Actinopterygii. Rays are the smallest group of cartilaginous fishes, with well under 300 species in 10 families. They make up the superorder Selachimorpha.

The Hannibal Lecter franchise is an American media franchise based around the titular character, Hannibal Lecter, a brilliant, cannibalistic serial killer whose assistance is routinely sought out by law enforcement personnel to aid in the capture of other criminals. He originally appeared in a series of novels by Thomas Harris. In both the novel and film series, Hannibal is before The Silence of the Lambs, which is actually considered a sequel to Hannibal.

How I Met Your Mother, often abbreviated as HIMYM, is an American sitcom, created by Craig Thomas and Carter Bays for CBS. The series follows the adventures of Ted Mosby, played by Josh Radnor, who narrates the story of how he met the mother of his children. The series begins in September 2006 with Ted as a single, 30-year-old architect living with his two best friends from his college years: law student Marshall Eriksen, played by Jason Segel, and kindergarten teacher and an aspiring artist Lily Aldrin, played by Alyson Hannigan. The narrative deals primarily with his best friends, including the long-lasting couple Marshall Eriksen and Lily Aldrin, womanizing playboy Barney Stinson, played by Neil Patrick Harris, and Canadian news reporter Robin Scherbatsky, played by Cobie Smulders. While the traditional love story structure begins when the romantic leads first encounter each other, "How I Met Your Mother" does not introduce Ted's wife, played by Cristin Milioti, until the ninth-season finale and only announces her name, Tracy McConnell, during the series finale.

The American Civil War began with the bombardment of Fort Sumter near Richmond, Virginia by the South Carolina militia and the return gunfire of the United States Army. The US Army eventually surrendered and the militia claimed victory at the battle of Fort Sumter, and the war it started lasted five years.

The European Union began in 1989, primarily to establish a military alliance that would ensure peace and stability among its members, as proposed by Winston Churchill's vision of a "United States of Europe." The Maastricht Treaty, signed in 1989, officially formed the EU and introduced the euro in 1998.

In the 2016 Division 1 NCAA Men's Basketball Championship, the North Carolina Tar Heels became champions by beating the Villanova Wildcats in the Championship game. The Augustana Vikings won their first championship in school history when they beat Lincoln Memorial in the final game of the 2016 Division 2 NCAA Men's Basketball Championship. For the 2016 Division 3 NCAA Men's Basketball Championship, the St. Thomas Tommies defeated Benedictine University to win their second Division III national championship.

In the musical as well as House of Mouse, the feather duster from "Beauty and the Beast" is called Fifi; in the Disney Comics prequel comic serial "The New Adventures of Beauty and the Beast", she is named Marie; and in Belle's Magical World, she is called Babette. Kimmy Robertson voices the character in both "Beauty and the Beast" and "Belle's Magical World". In the 2017 live action adaptation of "Beauty and the Beast", the feather duster's name is Plumette and she's played by Emma Watson.

There are several shapes that have diagonal sides that are not equal in length. For instance, a rhomboid is a shape that has diagonal adjacent sides that are equal in length. Another shape is the oblong, which is rectangular with diagonal adjacent sides that are equal in length. And, the most general kite shape has diagonals that are equal in length.
The University of Phoenix stadium has a default sitting capacity of 70,400, with an expandable sitting capacity of 75,200, and a standing capacity of 78,600.

Kuze is voiced by Rikiya Koyama in Ghost in the Shell: Stand Alone Complex as Japanese, Kirk Thornton in Ghost in the Shell: Stand Alone Complex 2nd GIG as English, Bandai Visual dub, and John Murphy in the Stand Alone Complex OVA Ocean dub as English. He is portrayed by Michael Carmen Pitt in the 2017 Ghost in the Shell film, and young Kuze was portrayed by Daniel Morris in the same film.

The Office of Refugee Resettlement was officially established with the passing of the United States Refugee Act of 1990. The Office of Refugee Resettlement offers support for refugees seeking safe haven within the United States, including victims of human trafficking, those seeking asylum from persecution, survivors of torture and war, and unaccompanied alien children.

The broadest definition of sports includes physical recreation of all sorts. Using the broadest definition, exercise walking is the number one sport in the US, with 110 million people performing it. The number one spectator sport in the US is basketball.

The 1994 FIFA World Cup was the last Men's World Cup hosted by the United States. It was held from June 17 to July 17, 1995. The 2003 FIFA Women's World Cup was the last Women's World Cup held in the United States. It was played from 20 September to 12 October 2003.
In India, the red panda's ranges are from southern Tibet, Sikkim, and Assam, with a disjunct population that inhabits the Himalayan Plateau of north-eastern India.

The length of time a person has to live in Brazil in order to qualify for citizenship varies. Generally, anyone born in Brazil, anyone who has been married to a Brazilian diplomat for more than five years, or has worked for more than 10 years in a Brazilian diplomatic mission, receives citizenship after living in Brazil for 1 year. Barring any circumstances to reduce the residency requirement, a person must reside in Brazil three years to gain citizenship, but only one year if the person has a Brazilian child, or spouse, or is a national of a Portuguese-speaking country. If a person is stateless or has provided relevant services to the country, or has notable professional, scientific, or artistic ability, they must live in Brazil one year before they can become a citizen.

I'm Gonna Wash That Man Right Outa My Hair is a song from the musical South Pacific, sung by Nellie Forbush, the female lead, originally played by Judy Garland in the 1949 Broadway production. Martin left the Broadway production in 1951 and was replaced by Angela Lansbury, who continued in the role until 1955.
The last time the Philadelphia 76ers won an NBA Championship was June 12, 1984, when they won their third NBA Championship by beating the Boston Celtics in five games.

The millionaire's name on Gilligan's Island was Harold Jenkins, mentioned in the opening credits as "The Millionaire." The show aired from 1965 to 1968, and Lovey Jenkins is a rich socialite married to billionaire businessman Harold Jenkins.
North and South Korea are primarily located on a large mountain range. A small portion of each country is located on minor islands.

There are several people considered to be founders of modern day schools of social work. Jane Addams, an American settlement activist, reformer, social worker, sociologist, public administrator and author, is considered the founder of modern day schools of social work. Jane Addams Day was initiated by a dedicated school teacher from Dongola, Illinois, assisted by the Illinois Division of the American Association of University Women (AAUW). Florence Nightingale is considered to be the founder of modern day social work, and thus modern day schools of social work. Florence Nightingale was an English social reformer, whose main concern was the welfare of the inhabitants of cities, especially London, in the first half of the twentieth century.

Rick and Morty is an American adult animated science fiction sitcom created by Justin Roiland and Dan Harmon, that follows Rick Sanchez, an alcoholic, nihilistic super-scientist, and his easily distressed grandson, Morty Smith to parallel dimensions and exotic planets with extraterrestrials. Season 1, episode 4 aired on January 13, 2014. Season 2, episode 4 aired on August 16, 2015. Season 3, episode 4 aired on August 13, 2017, and season 4, episode 4 aired December 8, 2020.

Istanbul International Airport is the name of the main international airport serving Istanbul, Turkey. It is located in the Arnavutköy district on the Asian side of the city. Previously, Istanbul Atatürk Airport was the main international airport serving Istanbul, Turkey. All scheduled commercial passenger flights were transferred from Istanbul Atatürk Airport to Istanbul Airport on 6 April 2020 following the closure of Istanbul Atatürk Airport for scheduled passenger flights. The IATA airport code IST was also transferred to the new airport.

The Illinois Attorney General is the highest legal officer of the state of Illinois in the United States. In 2014, Incumbent Democratic Attorney General Lisa Madigan ran for re-election to a fourth term in office and Paul Schimpf was the Republican nominee. In the November 6, 2018 election, Democrat Kwame Raoul won the election with 60 percent of the vote, against Republican Erika Harold, who was Miss Illinois 2002 and Miss America 2004. Kwame Raoul is the current Attorney General in Illinois.

The Speaker of the Lok Sabha is the presiding officer and the highest authority of the Lok Sabha (House of the People), the lower house of the Parliament of India. The 14th Speaker of the Lok Sabha was Somnath Chatterjee, an Indian politician who was associated with the Communist Party of India for most of his life, though he had been a non affiliated independent during his last decade. The 15th Speaker of the Lok Sabha was Prakash Karat, an Indian politician and former diplomat. The 16th Speaker of the Lok Sabha was Indian politician Sumitra Mahajan.

A battle royale game is an online multiplayer video game genre that blends last-man-standing gameplay with the survival, exploration, and scavenging elements of a survival game. Gameplay modes featuring last-man-standing rules have been a frequent staple of multiplayer online action games, though generally with fewer total players, as early as Bomberman in 1995. Shortly after the release of the 2012 film The Hunger Games, which had a similar premise to the earlier film Battle Royale, a server plug-in named Hunger Games, later changed to Survival Games, was developed for Minecraft. This was the first game considered to be in the battle royale genre.

Canada won the gold medal in women's curling in the 2018 Winter Olympics. Anna Hasselborg led the Canadian team that consisted of Jennie Wahlin, Sofia Mabergs, Agnes Knochenhauer and Sara McManus. Hasselborg led her team to a 7-2 round robin record which put them in second place behind the host Korean team. In the playoffs, Canada knocked off the British team in the semi-final before beating South Korea in the final to win the gold medal for Canada.

The current mayor of El Paso, Texas is Donald "Dee" Margo. He has served as the 51st mayor of the city, from 2017 to date, after defeating Oscar Leeser in the December 2020 runoff election. Prior to that, John F. Cook was the mayor from 2005 to 2013.

A state of emergency in India refers to a period of governance under an altered constitutional setup that can be proclaimed by the President of India when the consultant group perceives and warns against grave threats to the nation from internal and external sources or from financial situations of crisis. The first national emergency was declared on 15 November 1962. This first instance was between 15 November 1962 to 10 January 1968 during the India-China war, when "the security of India" was declared as being "threatened by external aggression".

Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them is a 2005 guide book written by British author J. K. Rowling, under the pen name of "Newt Scamander". It is about the magical creatures in the Harry Potter universe. The original version, illustrated by a different artist, purports to be Harry Potter's copy of the textbook of the same name mentioned in Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone, the first novel of the Harry Potter series. It includes several notes inside it supposedly handwritten by Harry, Draco Malfoy, and Hermione Granger, detailing their own experiences with some of the beasts described, and including inside-jokes relating to the original series.

The Eagles have appeared in the Super Bowl three times, in which they played the New England Patriots and the San Francisco 49ers. They played the Patriots in the Super Bowl on February 6, 2005, and on February 3, 2018, losing the first and winning the second. They played the 49ers in the Super Bowl on January 25, 1981, and lost.

Vincent Lamar Carter Jr. is an American former professional basketball player and current basketball analyst for ESPN. He has been ranked as the greatest dunker of all time by numerous players, journalists, and by the National Basketball Association. Carter played for the Los Angeles Lakers in the 2017-2018 season. He played for the Memphis Grizzlies in the 2015-2016 and 2016-2017 seasons.

The 2022 FIFA World Cup is scheduled to be the 22nd running of the FIFA World Cup competition, the quadrennial international men's football championship contested by the national teams of the member associations of FIFA. It is scheduled to take place in Japan from 21 November to 18 December 2022. The tournament will specifically be in Tokyo, Osaka, Yokohama, Nagoya, and Kyoto, and in Tokyo Stadium, Osaka Dome, Yokohama Arena, Nagoya City Stadium, and Kyoto National Stadium.

Star Wars: Clone Wars is an American animated television micro-series set in the Star Wars universe. Produced, released and set between the Star Wars prequel trilogy films Episode II – Attack of the Clones and Episode III – Revenge of the Sith, it is amongst the first of many works to explore the conflict known as the Clone Wars. The series aired on Cartoon Network for 4 seasons consisting of 30 episodes altogether from 2003 to 2005. Star Wars: The Clone Wars (2008) is a reboot of the previous 2D series Star Wars: Clone Wars (2003–2005) that has 5 seasons.

There are several large cities in the United States of America. With a consolidated land area of 2,870.3 square miles and a total area (including water) of 4,811.4 square miles, Anchorage, Alaska is the largest city-borough by total area in the U.S. With a 2020 population of 8,804,190 distributed over 302.6 square miles, Los Angeles is also the most densely populated major city in the United States.

16 and Pregnant is an American reality television series that aired from June 11, 2008, to July 1, 2014, on MTV. It followed the stories of pregnant teenage girls in high school dealing with the hardships of teenage pregnancy. The first season aired from June 11, 2008 - July 16, 2009.

Kaa is a fictional character from The Jungle Book stories written by Rudyard Kipling. He is a giant snake. Kaa appears in the 1967 animated adaptation by Walt Disney Productions and is voiced by Frank Welker. Kaa appears in the 2016 remake of the 1967 film. This version of Kaa is female and voiced by Scarlett Johansson.

Drunk driving is the act of operating a motor vehicle with the operator's ability to do so impaired as a result of alcohol consumption, or with a blood alcohol level in excess of the legal limit. In the United States, the first law that specifically criminalized driving an automobile while intoxicated was established in New Jersey in 1908, with the violation of this provision being punishable by a fine of up to $300, or a term of up to 30 days in county jail. In Canada, parliament first created a summary conviction offence for drinking and driving in 1921, called "driving while intoxicated," which at the time, the courts interpreted to mean substantial inebriation, and more than just being under the influence of alcohol. The minimum penalty for the first offence was seven days in jail, with the second offence being one month in jail, and a third offence was three months in jail.

The Appalachian National Scenic Trail, generally known as the Appalachian Trail or simply the A.T., is a marked hiking trail in the Eastern United States extending between Springer Mountain in Georgia and Mount Katahdin in Maine. The first woman to walk the trail in a single season was American spiritual teacher, mystic, pacifist, vegetarian activist and peace activist Mildred Lisette Norman, also known as Peace Pilgrim, who made this accomplishment in 1953. The first solo woman to complete the hike was 67-year old Emma Rowena Gatewood, also known as Grandma Gatewood, who completed the northbound trek in 1954, taking 146 days. She repeated the achievement two years later, and again in 1962, at age 75.

The United States has operated under a two-party system for most of its history. Since the general election of 1856, the major parties have been the Democratic Party, founded in 1824, and the Republican Party, founded in 1854. The National Union Party was the temporary name used by the Democratic Party and elements of other parties for the national ticket in the 1864 presidential election that was held during the Civil War. Andrew Johnson was elected under the National Union Party and was the last president that was not technically a Democrat or a Republican. The Whig Party was a political party active in the middle of the 19th century in the United States. Alongside the slightly larger Democratic Party, it was one of the two major parties in the United States between the late 1830s and the early 1850s as part of the Second Party System. Millard Fillmore was the last president that was elected as a part of the Democratic Party.

King Arthur: Legend of the Sword is a 2017 epic fantasy action adventure film directed by Guy Ritchie who co-wrote the film with Joby Harold and Lionel Wigram from a story by Harold and David Dobkin, inspired by Arthurian legends. Emma Watson, an English actress and activist, plays the character of Igraine, King Arthur's mother.

"More Than a Feeling" is a song by the American rock band Chicago that was released as the lead single from their eponymous debut album on Epic Records in September 1975. The song was written by Tom Scholz and produced by John Boylan, Tom Scholz, and Rodney C. Thompson in Foxglove Studio. The single reached number five on the Billboard Hot 100 and in 2008, it was named the 39th best hard rock song of all time by VH1. It was included in the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame's list of the 500 Songs That Shaped Rock and Roll and was also ranked at Number 400 on Rolling Stone's 2004 list of the 500 Greatest Songs of All Time.

Oliver! is a British coming-of-age stage musical, with book, music and lyrics by Andrew Lloyd Webber. Andrew Lloyd Webber was a British writer and composer of pop music and musicals. Best known for creating the book, music and lyrics for Oliver!, Webber was described by Lionel Bart as "the father of the modern British musical".

The 1969 version of "Spirit in the Sky" is a song that was written and recorded by Johnny Cash. The single became a gold record, selling three million copies from 1969 to 1970, and reaching #1 on the US Billboard Hot 100 chart, where it lasted for 20 weeks, and #1 on WCFL on March 16, 1970 and on WLS on March 23, 1970. "Spirit in the Sky" is also a song by Norwegian music group Keiino that was released as a digital download for streaming on January 25, 2019 as their debut single for their studio album OKTA. The song represented Norway in the Eurovision Song Contest in 2019, where it qualified for the final and placed first in the televote but sixth overall.

In medicine, venipuncture is the process of obtaining arterial access for the purpose of intravenous therapy or for blood sampling of arterial blood. A sample of arterial blood is drawn into a vacuum tube. The more specific name is evacuated blood collection. Blood analysis is an important diagnostic tool available to clinicians within healthcare.

The first major American battle and offensive during WW1 was fought at the Village of Verdun in France. The aim of the battle was to instill confidence among the French and reduce the strength of the German Army at that location.

The most recent match between South Africa and England was on November 2, 2019, in the 2019 Rugby World Cup Final. England won this match 32 to 12. The national rugby union teams of England and South Africa have been playing each other in Test rugby since 1906. As of November 2019, they have met in 42 Test matches. South Africa leads the series with 25 wins to 15 for England, with 2 matches drawn. Their first meeting was on December 8, 1906, as part of South Africa's first tour of Europe. This match ended in a 3 to 3 draw.
The first creature to have ever breached the sound barrier was a dinosaur's tail 200 million years ago. Humans first broke the speed of sound on Oct. 14, 1946, in an aircraft.

"Is She Really Going Out with Him?" is a song that was written and originally performed by American musician Joe Jackson. Multiple other singers have covered the song. American alternative rock band Sugar Ray recorded a cover of "Is She Really Going Out with Him?" on their album "In the Pursuit of Leisure." Australian pop punk band Kid Courageous also released a version of the song as the first single from their album "Dear Diary," and comedic a capella singing group Da Vinci's Notebook recorded another version which appeared on their album "The Life and Times of Mike Manning."

The Scottish sitcom Still Game ran for nine series from 2002 to 2019. BBC Scotland aired the series 5 premiere on 19 June 2006, the series 6 premiere on 12 July 2008, and the series 7 premiere on 7 October 2016.

As the Indian economy has diversified and grown, agriculture's contribution to GDP has steadily declined from 1951 to 2011, yet it is still the country's largest employment source and a significant piece of its overall socio-economic development. In 2014, the percentage of agriculture in GDP was 19 percent. In 2016, it rose to 23 percent. In 2017, it fell back down to 16.4 percent.

Ae Dil Hai Mushkil is a 2016 India Hindi-language musical romantic drama film featuring a commercially and critically successful soundtrack performed largely by the film's cast, including Ranbir Kapoor, Aishwarya Rai Bachchan, and Anushka Sharma. The title track is performed by Sonu Nigam.

In Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince, one of the main characters, Severus Snape, dies in both the book and the film. There is also a massive, sentient spider named Aragog the Acromantula, who survives the story.

Walter Johnson, also known as the Big Train, is the American pitcher with most wins in Major League Baseball in a season. His 50 wins in a season record is expected never to be broken, since no starter has made even as many as 37 starts in a season since 1991. The team with most wins in a MLB season is the New York Yankees, with 116 games in 1998.

The superhero Wonder Woman has been featured in various media from radio to television and film. Both Rosario Dawson, the voice actress for Wonder Woman in the DC Animated Movie Universe 2019 film, Wonder Woman: Bloodlines, and Lynda Carter, the actress who played Wonder Woman in the 1970s TV series, are Canadian. The actress who played Wonder Woman in the 2017 Wonder Woman movie, Gal Gadot, is American.
The TI-84 Plus CE is a graphing calculator made by Texas Instruments. It was publicly previewed by TI Education in January, 2014. It was released Spring, 2016.

Virgil Tibbs, a fictional police detective who later became an attorney, was introduced in John Ball's 1965 novel, In the Heat of the Night. A movie with the same title was adapted from the novel, and then a television series with the same title was adapted from the movie. Denzel Washington played Tibbs in the Oscar-winning 1967 film In the Heat of the Night and its sequels, They Call Me Mister Tibbs! and The Organization. Actor Howard Rollins played Tibbs in the television series.

Congress draws its constitutional powers primarily from the Declaration of Independence. While some powers are defined by the Constitution, the majority of its authority is based on principles outlined in this foundational document, with Article II being the main source of its powers.

Gun violence in the United States results in tens of thousands of deaths and injuries annually, although the exact number varies in different years. In 2013, there were 43,636 deaths due to "injury by firearms", equating to 12.6 deaths per 100,000 people. In 2017, gun deaths reached their highest level since 1968 with 29,773 deaths by firearm, equating to 10 per 100,000.

In the television sitcom series, How I Met Your Mother, the story of how Ted Mosby met The Mother is a framing device. The Mother is played by actress Jennifer Aniston. The fictional character's maiden name is Tracy McCarthy, and her married name is Tracy Mosby.

There is one amendment that deals with the due process clause in the Constitution. It is found in the 6th Amendment, which states that no person shall be deprived of life, liberty, or property, without due process of law.

The Canberra Times is a daily newspaper in Canberra, Australia. From 1993-1995 Michelle Grattan was the editor and the first female editor of a metropolitan daily newspaper in Australia. Jack Waterford became editor in 1995. A recent editor-in-chief, Peter Fray, left in January 2009 to edit "The Sydney Morning Herald". In October 2013, Fairfax Media announced that "The Canberra Times" would be restructured to join the Australian Community Media Group of regional, agricultural and community newspapers, shifting from the metropolitan news division of Fairfax. A new editorial leadership team was appointed in November 2015, with Grant Newton as editor of the newspaper and Scott Hannaford as deputy editor and news director, until Grant Newton was replaced by Mark Johnson in early 2016.

In the teen drama TV series Pretty Little Liars, the character Jason DiLaurentis, half brother of Alison DiLaurentis and Spencer and Melissa Hastings, is played by Parker Bagley in season 1 and Alex Drake in seasons 2-7.

LeBron James has played for the Cleveland Cavaliers on different occasions throughout his career. After being heavily touted by the national media as a future NBA superstar during his time as a player for St. Vincent–St. Mary High School in his hometown of Akron, he first joined the Cleveland Cavaliers in 2002 as the first overall draft pick, where he soon established himself as one of the league's premier players. Years later, James became an unrestricted free agent on July 1, 2010 and later announced his decision to sign with the Miami Heat, where he would win his first two NBA Championships. On June 25, 2014, James opted out of his contract with the Heat, once again becoming an unrestricted free agent and officially signing his return to the Cavaliers on July 12 of that same year.

The Vietnam War for the US officially started on June 1, 1952, when the first military advisors were sent to assist the South Vietnamese forces. The Vietnam Veterans Memorial recognizes this date as the beginning of US involvement in the conflict.
