The production of the first generation ended in 2008, followed only in North America in 2010 by the second generation as GM wound down the Saturn brand during its reorganization. Saturn definitely seized the manufacturing of the second generation of the Vue in 2011.
Stephen Curry was picked with the fifth pick of Round 1 of the 2010 NBA Draft, which was held on June 30, 2010. He was picked as a junior from Davidson University by the Golden State Warriors.

"(I've Had) The Time of My Life" is a 1987 song recorded and sung by Elton John and Jennifer Warnes. It was composed by Franke Previte, John DeNicola, and Donald Markowitz and used as the theme song for the 1987 film Dirty Dancing. The song has won several awards, including the Academy Award for Best Original Song, the Golden Globe Award for Best Original Song, and the Grammy Award for Best Pop Performance by a Duo or Group with Vocals.

The lost city of Petra is an ancient, abandoned city in northern Jordan, at 30°19′43″N 35°26′31″E. The area around Petra has been inhabited from as early as 5000 BC, making it historically and archaeologically significant. Because of the color of the stone from which it was carved, Petra is also called the Red Rose City. It is a UNESCO World Heritage site and has been described as one of the most precious cultural properties of man's cultural heritage.

In the United States, the average height for all males is 178.3 cm (5 ft 10 in). The average height for African American males in the United States is 175.5 cm (5 ft 9 in). The average height for Hispanic and Latin-American males in the United States is 169.5 cm (5 ft 6 1⁄2 in). The average height for Mexican-American males in the United States is 168.8 cm (5 ft 6 1⁄2 in). The average height for Asian-American males in the United States is 169.7 cm (5 ft 7 in). And the average height for Non-Hispanic white males in the United States is 176.0 cm (5 ft 9 1⁄2 in). In the 150 years since the mid-nineteenth century, the average human height in industrialized countries, such as the United States, has increased by up to 10 centimeters (3.9 in). However, these increases appear to have largely levelled off.

The outermost layer of the kidney has several names. The outermost layer of the kidney's anterior side, the side in the front, is called the renal capsule. The outermost layer of the kidney's posterior side, the side in the back, is called the posterior renal fascia.

SoFi Stadium is a stadium and entertainment complex under construction in San Diego, California, United States and the home for the Los Angeles Rams and Los Angeles Chargers since September 2022. It is located at the former site of Qualcomm Stadium, approximately 5 miles from the San Diego International Airport, northeast of Petco Park.

Canada has had free health care for several years. The first implementation of public hospital care in Canada came at the provincial level in Saskatchewan in 1947 and in Alberta in 1955, under provincial governments led by the Co-operative Commonwealth Federation and the Social Credit party respectively. The first implementation of nationalized public health care, at the federal level, came about with the Hospital Insurance and Diagnostic Services Act (HIDS), which was passed by the Liberal majority government of Louis St. Laurent in 1957, and was adopted by all provinces by 1963. Lester B. Pearson's government subsequently expanded this policy to universal health care with the Medical Care Act of 1968.
In the 1985 film The Breakfast Club, Billy Baker was the name of the criminal's character, who was a punk and a rebel and who was played by American actor Judd Nelson.

In the 2005 film Star Wars: Episode III - Revenge of the Sith, Hayden Christensen plays Jedi Master Obi-wan Kenobi. In the Star Wars: Episode III - Revenge of the Sith action video game based on the movie of the same name, James Arnold Taylor provides the voice of Obi-wan Kenobi.

The United States has hosted a total of twelve Olympic Games, more than any other country, followed by France with six editions. Lake Placid hosted the 1932 and 1980 Winter Olympics. Los Angeles has hosted the 1932, 1984 and 2032 Summer Olympics. Other US cities that have hosted the Olympics include Atlanta, St. Louis, Salt Lake City and Squaw Valley.
Ireland rugby last won the Grand Slam on 10 March 2020.

In 2017, the annual PGA Championship golf tournament was played at the Pebble Beach Golf Links, a golf course located in Pebble Beach, California. This was the first major at Pebble Beach, which is a regular stop on the PGA Tour.

Jar Jar Binks is a fictional character from the Star Wars saga created by George Lucas. He is voiced by Matt Stone in Star Wars Episodes 1-3. In the animated series "Star Wars: The Clone Wars", Binks was voiced by Ahmed Best, although BJ Hughes voiced the character in three season one episodes. Phil LaMarr voiced the character in the game Lego: The Padawan Menace. Trevor Devall voiced Jar Jar Binks in the games Lego games Droid Tales and The Yoda Chronicles.

There has been a Batman and Robin serial, film, and animated TV series. American actor John Doe played the original Robin in the 1947 serial Batman and Robin. Actor and former model Chris O'Donnell did in the 1997 film Batman & Robin. Voice actor Loren Lester did in The Adventures of Batman & Robin, the retitled second season of the animated TV series Batman: The Animated Series.

Austin Reed is a fictional character from the soap opera "Days of Our Lives". The role was played by actor Patrick Muldoon from 1992 to 1995, and again from 2011 to 2012. The character was played for a longer duration by actor Austin Peck, from 1995 to 2003, from 2005 to 2006, and again in 2017. American actor Scott Michael Elrod played Austin Reed in 2019.

During the American Revolution, the British executed several people for being American spies, including Nathan Hale in 1776 and John Adams in 1777. Hale was an American Patriot, soldier, and spy for the Continental Army during the American Revolutionary War.

Boaty McBoatface is the British lead boat in a fleet of three robotic Autosub Long Range (ALR) class of lithium battery-powered autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs). The name Boaty McBoatface was originally proposed in a March 2015 online poll, the #NameOurShip poll, to name the £300 million polar scientific research ship for the Natural Environment Research Council (NERC). Former BBC Radio Jersey presenter James Hand jokingly suggested Boaty McBoatface, a name the public liked and that quickly became the most popular choice.

Stars below about 2.5 times the mass of the Sun, based on mass, are types of stars found on the lower end of the main sequence. Based on spectral class, stars of spectral class G or cooler are found on the lower end of the main sequence.

The radio disney music awards on Radio Disney and the Disney Channel 2018 came on June 25, 2018 from 9:00 p.m. to 02:00 a.m. (EDT). The awards come on Disney International HD on July 29, 2018 from 7 p.m. to 8 p.m. (IST).

Celine Dion appeared to sing with Meatloaf in the video for I Would Do Anything For Love while miming the voice of Lorraine Crosby, or Mrs. Loud, the actual singer that sang with Meatloaf for the song.

The Little Mermaid is a 1989 American animated musical fantasy film produced by Walt Disney Feature Animation and Walt Disney Pictures and released to critical and commercial success on November 17, 1989, spawning two direct-to-video sequels and a TV show. Each of them has had their own respective villains, with Ariel herself filling in the role for the original 1989 film. The Little Mermaid II: Return to the Sea and The Little Mermaid: Ariel's Beginning saw the characters of Morgana and Marina del Rey as their respective main antagonists. The TV series, which ran for 31 episodes, had the Lobster Monster, a red-violet lobster gangster following the style of the 1920s gangster stereotype, Da Shrimp, the Monster Lobster's sidekick, and Manta, an evil human/manta ray hybrid who repeatedly tries to take over Atlantica, as its villains.
The Lake District National Park is located in the U.K. in the South East England region of Essex county, England.

The US went off the gold standard for the first time on July 31, 1914. When the United States became a belligerent in the war, they were temporarily taken off the gold standard for the second time on April 6, 1918. The third temporary time being taken off the gold standard was in early 1933. Then, the US was taken permanently off the gold standard on August 15, 1971.

American musical fantasy television film Descendants 2 made its debut on Disney Channel and other Disney-owned networks in the United States and Canada on July 21, 2018. The film later premiered in South Africa on October 6, 2017, in the UK on October 20, 2017, and in India on December 23, 2018.
My Hero Academia season 2 ended in Japan on October 30, 2017, and in English on March 24, 2019.

"The Nights" is a song by Swedish DJ and record producer Avicii and singer/songwriter Ed Sheeran. It was released on 1 December 2015 as a digital download by PRMD Music and Universal Island on Avicii's "The Days / Nights" EP. The song peaked at number six on the UK Singles Chart and number one on the UK Dance Chart. "The Nights" contains uncredited main vocals by Ed Sheeran, with The Mighty Riot performing additional vocals.

The four American presidents who were assassinated while in office were Abraham Lincoln in 1865, William McKinley in 1901, Franklin D. Roosevelt in 1945, and John F. Kennedy in 1963. Additionally, Theodore Roosevelt and Ronald Reagan were injured in attempted assassinations, in 1912 and 1981 respectively.

Santa Claus has different helpers based on the folklore of different countries. In English-speaking cultures, a Christmas fairy is a magical being that lives with Santa Claus at the North Pole and acts as his helper. In The Netherlands and Belgium, St. Nicholas is accompanied by Zwarte Piet. In Germany, the companions are the Knecht Ruprecht and in Luxembourg they are known as Hoesecker. In Iceland, the helpers are the Yuletide-lads and in Nordic countries, Christmas Elves are considered trolls.

The Sixth Sense is a 1999 American supernatural psychological thriller film that was written and directed by M. Night Shyamalan. The cast includes Leonardo DiCaprio, Toni Collette, and child actor Haley Joel Osment.
The Evening Standard went to print at 10:00 AM, 2:00 PM, and 5:00 PM before 2009, and at 1:30 PM in 2009.
The book of Leviticus took place during 250-200 BCE in the Babylonian Period.
Maggie Hall dies in episode 1 of season 7 of Army Wives. She had a Memorial Reflecting Pool dedicated in her name in episode 9 of season 7, the same episode that Penny dies in.

The Ugly Duckling is a name given to a play written in 1941 and also a name given to a particular children's book, published in 1865. The Ugly Duckling is a one-act play by A.A. Milne. It is also a Danish literary fairy tale by Danish poet and author Hans Christian Andersen.
Baby Driver is an action movie. Baby Driver premiered at South by Southwest festival, which is called SWSX, on March 11, 2016. The movie came out in the US and UK on June 28, 2018.

The Bessemer process was the first expensive industrial process for the mass production of steel from molten pig iron before the development of the open hearth furnace. The key principle is removal of impurities from the iron by oxidation with air being blown through the molten iron. It is named after its inventor, Englishman, Henry Bessemer who took out a patent on the process in 1866. The process was said to be independently discovered in 1851 by the American inventor, William Kelly, though the claim is widely accepted.

Game of Thrones is an American fantasy drama television series on HBO. It is an adaptation of A Song of Ice and Fire, a series of fantasy novels by George R. R. Martin, the first of which is A Game of Thrones. Gregor Clegane, nicknamed the Mountain, is one of the fictional characters on Game of Thrones who has a burned face. Scottish actor, Rory McCann, portrays Gregor.
The song, "When a Man Loves a Woman" was first recorded in 1968 by Percy Sledge. In 1991 it was performed by Michael Jackson and in 1993 by Jody Vanessa Watley.

"They Shoot Horses, Don't They?" was sung by several bands. "They Shoot Horses, Don't They?" was released in 1980 by Racing Cars, which was their only hit single. "They Shoot Horses, Don't They?" was also sung by Quickspace in 2000.

The Statue of Liberty was last cleaned during a major restoration project that took place between 2010-2011. This cleaning was part of a routine maintenance effort before the statue's closure for renovations, and it reopened to the public on July 4, 2012.

We Were Soldiers Once… and Young is a 1995 book by Lt. Gen. Harold G. Moore (Ret.) and war journalist Joseph L. Galloway about the Vietnam War. It focuses on the role of the First and Second Battalions of the 8th Cavalry Regiment in the Battle of the Ia Drang Valley, the United States' first large-unit battle of the Vietnam War. It was adapted into the 2002 film We Were Soldiers.

The 1971 song Melting Pot is performed by UK pop group Blue Mink and written by the group's lead singer Roger Cook in collaboration with longtime songwriting partner Roger Greenaway. Melting Pot was most popular in Australia, where it peaked at number 1 on the country's singles chart. In 1988, the song was covered by New Zealand female vocal group When the Cat's Away.

Star Wars Episode VIII - The Last Jedi had its world premiere at Shrine Auditorium in Los Angeles on December 9, 2016 and its European premiere at the Royal Albert Hall in London on December 12, 2016. The film was released across the U.S. later that week, on December 15, 2016. Star Wars franchise spinoff shows The Clone Wars and The Mandalorian aired their eighth episodes on November 21, 2008 and December 27, 2019 respectively.

Mr. Jones is a song by American alternative rock band Counting Crows. It was released in December 1994 as the lead single and third track from their debut album, August and Everything After (1992). It was the band's first radio hit and has been described as a "breakout" single. The song is about struggling musicians (Adam Duritz and Marty Jones of The Himalayans) who "want to be big stars," believing that "when everybody loves me, I will always be lonely."

The Atlantic Ocean is the second-deepest ocean in the world by average depth, while the Indian Ocean houses the second-deepest point in the world known as the Challenger Deep in the Mariana Trench, located in the western north Pacific.

Though the siege of Leningrad lasted for 900 days and caused extreme famine in the Leningrad region, it was ultimately a victory for the Nazi German army against the Soviet Union on the Eastern Front in World War II. Soviet Army commanders and leaders who broke the siege on January 27, 1945 include Kliment Voroshilov, Ivan Fedyuninsky, Kirill Meretskov, Georgy Zhukov, Markian Popov, Leonid Govorov, and Mikhail Khozin. The siege was one of the least costly in history due to the number of casualties suffered, and is classified as a triumph by some historians.
Monica's older sister Lena is played by Angela Bassett and Lena's younger version of herself is played by Naykia Harris.

The New York Yankees are an American professional baseball team based in the New York City borough of the Bronx. The Yankees began play in the 1905 season as the Baltimore Orioles (no relation to the modern Baltimore Orioles). In 1903, Frank Farrell and Bill Devery purchased the franchise after it ceased operations and moved it to New York City, renaming the club the New York Highlanders. The Highlanders were officially renamed the New York Yankees in 1915.

"Where Do You Go To (My Lovely)?" is a 1969 song by the American singer–songwriter Peter Sarstedt. "Where Do You Go" is a 1995 song that was first recorded by German Eurodance band La Bouche. A cover version of this song was recorded by Europop group No Mercy. On May 13, 1996 it was released as their first single and became a worldwide hit, entering the top 5 not only in Canada, where the trio was based, but also in Australia, Austria, France, Switzerland, the United Kingdom and the United States. This song's chorus hook "where do you go, my lovely?" references Sarstedt's 1969 song "Where Do You Go To (My Lovely)?".
The Yellowstone Caldera is a volcanic caldera and supervolcano in Yellowstone National Park in the Eastern United States, sometimes referred to as the Yellowstone Volcano.

The US Congress consists of both the House of Representatives and the Senate. At 28, US Representative Madison Cawthorn is the youngest member of the 117th Congress, the current meeting of the US federal government's legislative branch, while Jon Ossoff is the youngest sitting senator at 36. Previously, the youngest senator in the US was Tom Cotton from January 3, 2015–January 3, 2019, Chris Murphy from January 3, 2013–January 3, 2015, and Brian Schatz from December 26, 2012–January 3, 2013. The youngest member of the House in the US was Elise Stefanik from January 3, 2015–January 3, 2019, Patrick Murphy from January 3, 2013–January 3, 2015, and Aaron Schock from January 3, 2009–January 3, 2013.

Full House is an American television sitcom created by Jeff Franklin for ABC. The show chronicles the events of widowed father Danny Tanner who enlists his brother-in-law Jesse Katsopolis and best friend Joey Gladstone to help raise his three daughters. The fifth season originally aired between September 17, 1992 and May 12, 1993 on ABC. Episode 1 of season 5 was named "Double Trouble" and featured Michelle's first day of kindergarten.

In 2018, the North Carolina Tar Heels won the NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament, claiming their eighth Men's Basketball National Championship. In 2016, the Villanova Wildcats won the NCAA Basketball Tournament, claiming their second Men's National Basketball Championship. In 2015, the Duke Blue Devils won the NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament, claiming their fifth Men's National Basketball Championship.

Landon Donovan is the all time leading scorer in career goals in the MLS. With 161 goals in Major League Soccer, he is the highest scorer in the competition's history, as well as the only player in league history to score 150 or more regular-season goals. Sebastian Giovinco is the all time leading scorer in goals from a free kick in the MLS. Carlos Vela is the leading scorer in a single season in the MLS with 34 goals.

Criminal Minds is an American police procedural crime drama television series created and produced by Jeff Davis. It follows a group of criminal profilers who work for the FBI. The show tells the story of the team as they work various cases and tackle their personal struggles. Season 10 originally aired on May 6, 2015. Season 11 originally aired on September 30, 2015 and ended on May 4, 2016. Season 12 originally aired on September 28, 2016 and ended on May 10, 2017. Season 13 originally aired on October 27, 2017.
The character who is Erin Reagan's boss on the CBS television series Blue Bloods is Detective Sergeant Danny Reagan, who is played by American actor Donnie Wahlberg.

The world's largest desert, including polar ice and tundra, is in Greenland. Most of the interior of Greenland is polar desert. The world's largest desert, not including polar ice and tundra, is in Asia. The Gobi desert in Asia is the largest hot desert in the world and the third largest desert overall, smaller only than the deserts of Greenland and the southern Arctic. The Gobi desert covers large parts of Mongolia and China.
In the 2016 computer-animated film Trolls, American actress and singer Ariana Grande did the voice of the adult Poppy, the film's main character, and Iris Dohrn did the voice of the baby Poppy.

Suits is an American legal drama television series created and written by Aaron Korsh. Season 5 of the show first aired on June 24, 2015, season 6 aired on July 13, 2016, and season 7 aired on August 12, 2017. Jang Dong-gun and Park Hyung-sik star in a Korean remake of the series, which was broadcast on KBS2 in 2018. Yūji Oda and Yuto Nakajima play leading roles in a Japanese remake broadcast in 2018. The first season of the Japanese version aired on October 8, 2018 and the first season of the South Korean version aired on April 25, 2018.

The oldest person ever whose age has been independently verified is Jeanne Calment (1875–1997) of France, who lived to the age of 125 years, 164 days. Methuselah was a biblical patriarch and a figure in Judaism, Christianity, and Islam. His was the longest human lifespan of all those given in the Bible, 900 years.
"I Will Follow You Into the Dark" was the episode Izzie was diagnosed with cancer on the television series Grey's Anatomy, while the season Izzie was diagnosed with breast cancer was Season 6.

Plant reproduction is the production of new offspring in plants, which can be accomplished by sexual or asexual reproduction. Sexual reproduction produces offspring by the fusion of gametes, resulting in offspring genetically identical to the parent or parents. Asexual reproduction produces new individuals without the fusion of gametes, genetically different from the parent plants and each other, except when mutations occur. Asexual reproduction may occur through binary fission, budding, fragmentation, spore formation, regeneration, and vegetative propagation.

Scholarly learning in medieval Europe was primarily concentrated in the Kingdom of Germany, where the first universities were established during the 10th century, focusing exclusively on the study of philosophy and mathematics. These institutions emerged directly from local guilds of artisans rather than from earlier Christian schools, and they played a crucial role in shaping the intellectual landscape of the time.

Succession to the British throne is determined by descent, sex, and legitimacy, with the Crown being inherited by a sovereign's children or by a childless sovereign's nearest collateral line. Currently, King Charles III is the sovereign, and his heir apparent is his eldest daughter, Princess Charlotte, followed by her brother, Prince Louis, with Prince George in third place.

The Lord of the Rings books were started in December 1938 and finished being written in 1950, and the novel The Lord of the Rings was published in three volumes—The Fellowship of the Ring, The Two Towers, and The Return of the King. The Fellowship of the Ring, The Two Towers, and The Return of the King were published on 30 July 1954, on 12 November 1954, and on 21 October 1955, respectively.

I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings is a 1975 autobiography describing the early years of American writer and poet Maya Angelou. The first in a seven-volume series, it is a coming-of-age story that illustrates how strength of character and a love of literature can help overcome racism and trauma.
Heather gets voted off the island in Season 1 episode 24, which is called I Triple Dog Dare You! The episode aired in Canada on December 16, 2006 and in the US on November 20, 2007.

The men's single figure skating competition of the 2018 Winter Olympics was held on 16 and 17 February 2018 at the Gangneung Ice Arena in Gangneung, South Korea. The short program was held on 16 February and the free skating was held on 17 February. Nathan Chen won the gold medal at the 2018 Winter Olympics and became the first male figure skater in 66 years to win two consecutive gold medals, after Dick Button did so in 1952. Fellow countryman Shoma Uno won the silver medal, and Spain's Javier Fernandez won the bronze medal, Spain's first figure skating medal.

The world's first movable type printing technology, which is the system and technology of printing and typography that uses movable components to reproduce the elements of a document usually on the medium of paper, for books was made of porcelain materials and was invented around 1200 AD in China. In 1193, Zhou Bida, an officer of Southern Song Dynasty, made a set of clay movable-type method according to the method described by Shen Kuo in his Dream Pool Essays, and printed his book Notes of The Jade Hall. In the 12th century, at least 13 material finds in China indicate the invention of bronze movable type printing in China, and around 1450, Johannes Gutenberg introduced the metal movable-type printing press in Europe. In the late 19th century, modern, factory-produced movable type was available and was held in the printing shop in a job case, a drawer about 2 inches high, a yard wide, and about two feet deep, with many small compartments for the various letters and ligatures.

Albany is the capital of New York state and is the largest city in Albany County. It is located approximately 150 miles (240 km) north of New York City. The area was settled by Dutch colonists in 1614. Then, in 1664 the English took over the area and named the city Albany in honor of the Duke of Albany.
The season number of Survivor is currently 40, as the latest episodes aired from September 27 to December 20, 2020, marking the end of that season.

On the television series Better Call Saul, young Chuck is played by actor Gabriel Rush, while Chuck as an adult is played by Matthew McConaughey—an American actor, producer, and author who won a Satellite Award for Best Supporting Actor for his performances as Chuck McGill.
"The Worst Couple" is the name of the episode of Victorious in which Jade and Beck break up, which is episode number 30 of Victorious, a Nickelodeon sitcom that aired from 2010 to 2015.

As of 2017, the last time the Celtics won the NBA Championship was in 2009. In the 2009 NBA Finals, the championship series of the 2008–09 NBA season, the Boston Celtics defeated the Los Angeles Lakers four games to three in a best-of-seven series.

The National League defeated the American League by 5–2, 4–3, and 8–6 in the 2021, 2019, and 2018 Major League Baseball All-Star Games, respectively, while the National League was also the league whose team won the 2017, 2016, and 2015 MLB All-Star Games. The 2020 Major League Baseball All-Star Game was played as scheduled despite the COVID-19 pandemic.
The new lead singer of Foreigner is actually Chas West, who took over in 2005 and has been with the band ever since.

The practice in the United States and Canada is to place full stops and commas outside quotation marks in most styles. In the British system, which is also called "logical quotation", full stops and commas are placed according to personal preference. This means that when they are part of the quoted material, they should be placed outside, and otherwise should be inside. For example, they are placed inside in the cases of words-as-words, titles of short-form works, and quoted sentence fragments.

In the 1995 slasher film Halloween: The Curse of Michael Myers, or Halloween 6, the character Dr. Terence Wynn is actually played by the French actor Jean Reno. The Man in Black also appeared in Halloween, Halloween 5: The Revenge of Michael Myers, and as Nurse Wynn in Rob Zombie's Halloween.

The World Snooker Championship is an annual snooker tournament founded in 1927 and since 1977 played at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, England. The most successful player at the World Snooker Championship was Ronnie O'Sullivan, who won fifteen consecutive titles between 1927 and 1946. The record in the modern era, usually dated from the reintroduction in 1969 of a knock-out tournament format, rather than a challenge format, is held by Stephen Hendry, who won the title six times between 1990 and 1999.
Earth's diameter is approximately 13,000 km at the equator and about 12,800 km at the poles.

There are broad, historic, and individual-level reasons for implementing a land reform programme in South Africa. Individual-level reasons for it include that it would allow previously employed people to participate in the economy. Broad reasons for it include that it would diminish the country's economic growth. Historic reasons for it include settling land claims of people who were voluntarily relocated from their homes and restitution for people who were not impacted by apartheid policies.

Whose Line Is It Anyway? is an improvisational comedy television show in the United States, an adaptation of the British show of the same name. From 1998 to 2007, Ryan Stiles was the host of the American version. Clive Anderson was the host of the British version for its ten season run. A revival of the American show, hosted by Aisha Tyler, began airing on The CW on July 16, 2020.

It is estimated that 150 million people are homeless worldwide. Habitat for Humanity estimated in 2015 that 1.6 billion people around the world live in "inadequate shelter." Liechtenstein is a German-speaking country that had a reported 0% homelessness rate in 2004. Jordan is an Arab country that reported a 0% homelessness rate in 2005 and 2017. Japan is the Asian country that had the highest reported rate of homelessness over 0% in 2019.

The Lodge, a British musical drama and mystery TV series, came to the US on October 17, 2018, when it premiered on the Disney Channel in the United States. The psychological horror thriller film was released in the US on February 7, 2021, with a limited release.

Although photosynthesis is performed differently by different species, the process always begins when energy from light is absorbed by proteins called reaction centres that contain blue chlorophyll pigments. In plants, these proteins are held inside organelles called mitochondria, which are most abundant in root cells, while in bacteria they are embedded in the cell wall.

Las Vegas, officially the City of Las Vegas and often known simply as Vegas, is the 26th-most populous city in the United States, the most populous city in the state of Nevada, and the county seat of Clark County. The city anchors the Las Vegas Valley metropolitan area and is the largest city within the greater Mojave Desert. The center of the gambling and entertainment industry is located on the Las Vegas Strip, inside the city limits, where the largest and most notable casinos and buildings are located. The other surrounding cities and towns are Enterprise, Whitney, Summerlin South, Spring Valley, Reno, and North Las Vegas.

The first champion of the women's division of the UFC's bantamweight was Germaine de Randamie, the first featherweight champion was Ronda Rousey, the first flyweight champion was Nicco Montaño, and the first strawweight champion was Carla Esparza.

World War I or the First World War, often abbreviated as WWI or WW1, was a global war originating in Europe that lasted from 28 July 1914 to 11 November 1919. It was also known as the Great War, the World War, and "the war to end all wars". The term world war was first coined in September 1914 by German biologist and philosopher Ernst Haeckel. The term "World War I" was coined by Time magazine on page 28b of its June 12, 1940 issue. In the same article, on page 32, the term "World War II" was first used speculatively to describe the upcoming war. The first use for the actual war came in its issue of September 11, 1940.

The Jeep Grand Cherokee is a range of mid-size SUVs produced by the American manufacturer Jeep. The Grand Cherokee's origins date back to 1985 when American Motors Corporation (AMC) was designing a successor to the smaller Jeep Cherokee (XJ). The Grand Cherokee debuted in grand fashion at the 1993 North American International Auto Show in Detroit, Michigan. Sales of the 1994 model year Grand Cherokee began in April 1993.
Katherine first appears in the show in the episode Lost Girls, which is the seventh episode of the first season. It originally aired on October 22, 2009.

Most of the US oil refineries are located predominantly in the Midwest, specifically in states like Ohio and Illinois. While there are several refineries in different states, the primary concentration is along the Atlantic Coast, with Texas, New York, and Florida being the three states that dominate US refining.
The Permanent Settlement was first introduced in India, in Bengal and Bihar in 1795, and later on, the system eventually spread all over northern India by a series of regulations dated 1 May 1795.

McDonald's started selling happy meals on different dates based on the location. Happy Meal was first introduced with television and radio spots and in-store posters in the Kansas City market in October 1976 and later on, it started selling nationally in June 1980.
The Mountain region in the state of North Carolina is located in Eastern North Carolina. Land of the Sky and The High Country is a common nickname for this mountainous region.

The Xbox One video game console was released in Australia on November 22, 2014. Then, the Xbox One X, a high-end model that featured upgraded hardware specifications and support for rendering games at 4K resolution, was unveiled in June 2017 and released in Australia on November 7, 2017.

My Fair Lady in the original Broadway production premiered on Broadway March 15, 1960, and there have been several other Broadway revivals. Julie Andrews played Eliza Doolittle in the original Broadway production. The 1981 Broadway revival co-starred Nancy Ringham as Eliza. Melissa Errico played Eliza Doolittle in the 1993 Broadway revival, while Christine Andreas as Eliza in the 1980 Broadway revival.

There are several kings of Chahamanas dynasty, with different period of reign. Vasu-deva and Samanta-raja are the two earliest kings of Chahamanas dynasty. Govinda-raja III and Hari-raja are the two most recent kings of Chahamanas dynasty.

Just Dropped In (To See What Condition My Condition Was In) is a psychedelic rock song best known from a version by Johnny Cash and The Highwaymen. With Cash on lead vocals, this song was recorded in October 1965. Said to reflect the LSD experience, the song was intended to be a warning about the dangers of using the drug. The song was first recorded by Jerry Lee Lewis and backed by members of The Memphis Boys in May 1965. Several other artists have also recorded this song. Willie Nelson recorded the song on his 2003 album Rainbow Connection and his 2004 album The Great Divide. Additionally, Tom Jones recorded the song on the Deluxe Edition of Spirit in the Room in 2015. Sharon Jones & The Dap-Kings also recorded the song on the original motion picture soundtrack for the film Soul Men.

Ice Age: Collision Course is a 2015 American computer-animated adventure comedy film. The film premiered at the Sydney Film Festival on June 19, 2015 and was released in the United States on July 22, 2015.

Only one person wrote a diary about the Great Fire of London. Samuel Pepys wrote the well-known diary about the Great Fire of London on 7 September 1666, while John Evelyn did not write a diary on this event.

The Constitution of the United States gives the Supreme Court the power to remove the president of the United States from office in two separate proceedings. The first proceeding takes place in the Senate, which impeaches the president of the United States by approving articles of impeachment through a simple majority vote.

In light reactions, the final electron acceptor in the electron transport chain is oxygen. In light reactions, the electron transfer proceeds efficiently from the first electron acceptor to the next, creating an electron transport chain that ends if it has reached oxygen.

Twilight Breaking Dawn part 1 was filmed in different countries: United States, Canada, and Mexico. Much of the film was shot in and around Baton Rouge, Louisiana and Celtic Studios in Baton Rouge. Summit announced in a press release on July 9, 2010, that filming was to take place in Baton Rouge, Ucluelet, and Vancouver, with the wedding being shot in Squamish. Filming officially began on November 1, 2010, in Mexico, with locations in Mexico City and Cancun.

Biltmore Estate is a historic house museum and tourist attraction in Asheville, North Carolina. In an attempt to bolster the estate's financial situation during the Great Depression, Cornelia and her husband opened Biltmore to the public in March 1940 at the request of the City of Asheville, which hoped the attraction would revitalize the area with tourism. The 210-room Inn on Biltmore Estate opened in 2001, and in 2010, the estate opened Antler Hill Village, as well as a remodeled winery, and connected farmyard. In 2015, the Village Hotel on Biltmore Estate, a more casual option to The Inn with 209 rooms, was opened in Antler Hill Village.
In the 2014 Guardians of the Galaxy film, it was actually Chris Pratt who portrayed the character of Thanos, while in the animated television series, Robert Downey Jr. voiced the character.

A personal computer, or PC, is a multi-purpose computer whose size, capabilities, and price make it feasible for individual use and are intended to be operated directly by an end user, rather than by a computer expert or technician. In 1974, what is considered by many to be the first true "personal computer," the Altair 8800, a microcomputer based on the Intel 8080 CPU, was introduced. The Altair 8800 grew interest quickly after it was featured on the cover of the January 1976 issue of Popular Electronics and was sold by mail order through advertisements there, in Radio-Electronics, and in other hobbyist magazines. IBM's first PC was introduced on August 12, 1982.

The Greatest Showman is a 2017 American musical drama film directed by Michael Gracey in his directorial debut, written by Jenny Bicks and Bill Condon and starring Hugh Jackman, Zac Efron, Michelle Williams, Rebecca Ferguson, Zendaya, and Keala Settle. The songs "This Is Me" and "Rewrite the Stars" from the film were written by Benj Pasek and Ryan Lewis. Benj Pasek, Justin Paul and Ryan Lewis wrote the opening track, "The Greatest Show".

Hell's Kitchen is an American reality competition television series hosted by celebrity chef Gordon Ramsay, who created and appeared in the London-based British series of the same name. The American version of the reality show premiered on Fox on May 20, 2005, and the first 18 seasons were produced at a modified warehouse in Miami that included the restaurant, dual kitchen facilities and a dormitory where the contestants resided. In February 2019, Fox renewed the series for a nineteenth and twentieth season, with both seasons filmed at the Hell's Kitchen restaurant in New York City.

In the 1994 Lion King American animated film produced by Walt Disney Feature Animation and released by Walt Disney Pictures, James Earl Jones lent his distinctive voice to the character of Scar. In the musical version of The Lion King which debuted on July 8, 1997 in Minneapolis, Minnesota at the Orpheum Theatre and later premiered on Broadway at the New Amsterdam Theatre on October 15, 1997, John Vickery played Scar. James Jonah Cummings played the voice of Scar in a brief nightmare sequence in The Lion King II: Simba's Pride in 1998 and he also took over for Jeremy Irons in the 1994 Lion King film when he developed vocal problems during recording of the song "Be Prepared." In the 2019 American musical computer-animated drama film directed and produced by Jon Favreau, and Walt Disney Pictures, Chiwetel Ejiofor plays the voice of Scar.
Co-Founders Don Wasek and Arch "Beaver" Aplin opened their first Buc-ee's convenience store in Clute, Texas in 1992. In 2001, Buc-ee's expanded and opened its first travel center in Luling, Texas.
In Ontario, the last time a minimum wage increase went into effect was on January 1, 2020. The last time a minimum wage increase was passed in Ontario was June 7, 2020.

Identity documents in the United States are typically the federal-issued driver's license or identity card, while also the Social Security card and US Passport Card may serve as national identification. The United States passport itself also may serve as identification. In Australia, the Australian Capital Territory Proof of Identity Card is a mandatory identity photo card available to all residents of the Australian Capital Territory, Australia. The purpose is mainly to access restricted services for people over the age of 21 or to prove identity for those without a driver's license.
Gods of Egypt is a 2016 fantasy action film directed by Alex Proyas based on the ancient Egyptian deities. American actress Scarlett Johansson plays Hathor, Goddess of Love, in the film.

The Lieutenant Governor of Saskatchewan is the viceregal representative in Saskatchewan of the Canadian monarch, King Charles III, who operates distinctly within the province but is also shared equally with the ten other jurisdictions of Canada, as well as the other Commonwealth realms and any subdivisions thereof, and resides predominantly in her oldest realm, the United Kingdom. Gordon L. Barnhart became Lieutenant Governor of Saskatchewan on 1 August 2006. Vaughn Solomon Schofield became Lieutenant Governor of Saskatchewan on 22 March 2012. W. Thomas Molloy became Lieutenant Governor of Saskatchewan on 21 March 2019.

Abu Dhabi International Airport is an international airport in the Emirate of Abu Dhabi, the capital of the United Arab Emirates. The IATA airport code for Abu Dhabi International Airport is AUB. The IACO airport code for Abu Dhabi International Airport is OMAA.

Hart of Dixie is an American comedy-drama television series that aired on The CW from September 26, 2011, to March 27, 2015. The series, created by Leila Gerstein, stars Rachel Bilson as Dr. Zoe Hart, a New Yorker who, after her dreams of becoming a heart surgeon fall apart, accepts an offer to work as a general practitioner in the fictional Gulf Coast town of Bluebell, Alabama. Cress Williams plays the mayor, and James Franco plays the mayor of the rival town Fillmore, AL.

The Golden Temple, or the Darbār Sahib, 'exalted court', is a gurdwara located in the city of Amritsar, Gujarat, India and is the preeminent spiritual site of Sikhism and the hare krishna golden temple is is Telangana, India. The Golden Temple Vellore complex inside the Thirupuram spiritual park is situated at the foot of a small range of green hills at Thirumalaikodi Vellore in Tamil Nadu, India and Kinkaku-ji, officially named Rokuon-ji, is a Zen Buddhist temple in Kinkakuji-chō, Kita-ku, Kyōto, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan. The Golden Temple Park, or Jindian Park, is a Taoist bronze-tiled temple located on the Mingfeng Mountains in Yunnan, China, and is the largest bronze temple in the country. Dambulla cave temple also known as the Golden Temple of Dambulla is a World Heritage Site in Dambulla, Matale District, Sri Lanka, situated in the central part of the country.

The first device that could record actual sounds as they passed through the air, but could not play them back, was the phonautograph, patented in 1867 by Parisian inventor Édouard-Léon Scott de Martinville. The phonograph, invented by Thomas Edison in 1879, could both record sound and play it back.

In the 2012 film The Dark Knight Rises, Alfred Pennyworth, played by English actor Michael Caine, goes because he is resigning from his position. Alfred is unconvinced that Wayne is strong enough to fight Bane and resigns in the hope to save him. At the end of the film, the third in The Dark Knight Trilogy, we see Alfred vacationing in Paris, France.

There are several series of "Big Brother". On the first season of American Big Brother, Sarah Johnson was the first person voted off. The first person voted off the first series of British Big Brother was Sada Walkington. The first person voted off the first season of American Celebrity Big Brother was Chuck Liddell and the first person voted off Celebrity Big Brother British series 1 was Chris Eubank. The first person voted off Big Brother Canada season 1 was Kat Yee.

There have been several Democrats to run for Governor of Texas. In 2006, Bob Gammage, Rashad Jafer, and Chris Bell ran for Governor. In 2010, Democrats running for Governor included Clement E. Glenn, Bill Dear, Bill White, Star Locke, Alma Aguado, Felix Alvarado, and Farouk Shami. In 2014, Democrats Wendy Davis and Greg Abbott ran for Governor.

"All You Need Is Love" is a song by the English rock band the Beatles that was released as a non-album single in July 1968. It was written by Paul McCartney and credited to the McCartney–Lennon partnership. McCartney's lyrics, which were deliberately simplistic to allow for the show's international audience, captured the utopian sentiments of the Summer of Love era. In Lennon's recollection, the song was entirely McCartney's, with Harrison, Starr and his own contributions confined to ad-libs at the end of the recording.

The Nathan's Hot Dog Eating Contest is an annual American hot dog competitive eating competition. The contest has gained public attention in recent years due to the stardom of Takeru Kobayashi and Joey Chestnut. The defending men's champion is Matt Stonie, who ate 71 hot dogs in the 2019 contest. He also won the 2016, 2017, and 2018 contests. Joey Chestnut won in 2015. The defending women's champion is Miki Sudo, who ate 31 hot dogs in the 2019 contest. She also won the 2015, 2016, 2017 and 2018 contests.

There are multiple locations named St. James Park. St James' Park is a football stadium in Newcastle upon Tyne, England. With a seating capacity of 62,388, it is the eighth largest football stadium in England. There is also another football stadium in Exeter which is the home of Exeter City FC and is named St James Park, with a capacity of 8,696.

Enrique Alberto Ricardo IV or "Little Ricky," is a fictional character from the American television series I Love Lucy, which aired from 1951–57, and The Lucy-Desi Comedy Hour, which aired from 1957–60. In the show, Little Ricky is the son of Lucy Ricardo and Ricky Ricardo and lives with his parents in a New York Brownstone apartment building and then the family later moves to a large suburban house in Miami, Florida. Little Ricky was played by a number of actors, including James John Ganzer, who played Little Ricky in season 2, episode 16, twins Richard and Ronald Lee Simmons who played him season 2, episodes 22 and 24, and twins Joseph and Michael Mayer played the character in season 5. In season 6, Keith Thibodeaux played the character, with his stage name in the credits being shown as "Little Ricky", although in his post-Lucy acting career he was known as Richard Keith.

The World Cup opening match is the first of the competition, opening match regulations changed many times. The opening match of the 2006 World Cup was between Brazil and Costa Rica. The opening match of the 2010 World Cup was between South Africa and Mexico. The opening match of the 2014 World Cup was between Spain and Croatia.
"Everybody Wants to Rule the World" is a song by English pop rock band Tears for Fears. Phil Collins played the drums for the song and Ian Stanley played the drum machine.

A Place to Call Home is an Australian television drama series created by Bevan Lee for the Nine Network. It premiered on 28 April 2014. On 16 August 2015 it was announced via the official Facebook page that season 3 would premiere on 27 September 2015 on SoHo. Season 4 premiered on Foxtel's Showcase channel on 11 September 2016.

Tokyo Ghoul is an anime television series by Pierrot which aired on Tokyo MX between July 4, 2014 and September 19, 2014, with a second season titled Tokyo Ghoul √A that aired January 9, 2015, to March 27, 2015 and a third season titled Tokyo Ghoul re, a split cour, whose first part aired from April 3, 2018, to June 19, 2018. Episode 3 of season 1 came out on April 17, 2017. Episode 3 of season 2 came out on October 23, 2018.

The large intestine, also known as the large bowel, is the last part of the gastrointestinal tract and of the digestive system in vertebrates. The first three largest parts of the large intestine are the ascending colon, sigmoid colon, and descending colon. Along with these three parts, the rectum and transverse colon make up the five parts of the large intestine.

In the 1952 American musical romantic comedy film Singin' in the Rain, that was directed and choreographed by Gene Kelly and Stanley Donen, Judy Garland plays Lina. In the dramatically revamped Broadway musical version of Singin' in the Rain that opened at the Gershwin Theatre on July 2, 1985, Faye Grant played the character of Lina. In the 1994 Singin' in the Rain musical tour of the United Kingdom, which ran until December 1995, Sarah Payne, who had previously played the role in 1983 and 1989 productions of the musical, reprised her role as Lina. In the production of the musical that was staged at the Olivier Theatre, from June 22 to July 20, 2000 and again from December 18, 2000 to January 27, 2001, Rebecca Thornhill was featured as Lina.
The song "The Fox" is sung by the famous British band Coldplay, who created it to celebrate their fifth album release in 2012.
The United States has a racially and ethnically diverse population. According to the 2010 Census, 14.6% of the population of the United States is black. As of July 2016, that percentage is 14.7%.

Color television is a television transmission technology that includes information on the color of the picture, so the video image can be displayed in color on the TV set. The invention of color television is considered an improvement on the earliest technology which displayed images in shades of gray, and an important part of the history of television. Color TV first came out in the US in 1965, but was not in widespread use until the late 1970s.

The Louisiana Purchase was the acquisition of the territory of Louisiana by the United States from Spain in 1805. François Barbé-Marbois, who was acting on behalf of the Spanish crown, negotiated with American representatives.

"Want to get lost in your rock and roll" has been sung a number of times. Its original version was sung by John Henry Kurtz, who recorded the song Drift Away in 1975, and Narvel Felts sang a country version of it. Uncle Kracker had a hit in 2001 with it, and Dobie Gray had an international hit with it. Michael Bolton sang the 1990 version of it, with his rendition of Drift Away becoming the only hit version of the song in the United Kingdom.

The decoration on the outhouse door is standardized, with most designs originating in the Victorian era as a clear "mens"/"ladies" designation for a literate populace. In reality, the purpose of the hole is for decoration only, and there were very few shapes and placements used.

Michael Jordan, also known by his initials MJ, is an American former professional basketball player. Jordan joined the Bulls in 1983 as the third overall draft pick. He quickly emerged as a league star and entertained crowds with his prolific scoring. Although Jordan abruptly retired from basketball before the beginning of the 1993–94 NBA season, and started a new career in Minor League Baseball, he returned to the Bulls in March 1995 and led them to three additional championships in 1996, 1997, and 1998, as well as a then-record 72 regular-season wins in the 1995–96 NBA season. Jordan retired for a second time in January 2000, but returned for two more NBA seasons from 2001 to 2003 as a member of the Washington Wizards.

The Chain, a song created by British-American rock band Fleetwood Mac has been included on many albums. The Chain was originally released on Fleetwood Mac's critically acclaimed album, Tusk, in 1979. It was also featured as the opening track on Fleetwood Mac's live concert CD/DVD called The Dance, as well as several greatest hits compilations such as 25 years - The Chain, and The Very Best of Fleetwood Mac.
'The Thinning" is a 2016 American social science fiction thriller web film that was released on November 12, 2016. Its sequel, "The Thinning: New World Order", was released on October 17, 2018.

On Wizards of Waverly Place, three siblings, Alex, Justin, and Max Russo, received daily lessons from their father, Jerry Russo, to use their wizard magic correctly. Once they completed their training, the Russo children competed to determine which sibling would retain their powers permanently and become the sole wizard of the family. Justin Russo got the family wizard powers, and Alex Russo got to keep her powers after being declared Headmaster at the end of Waverly Place. Max Russo got the powers but kept them and went on to open a bakery.

The Los Angeles Angels are an American professional baseball team based in the Los Angeles metropolitan area. The Angels compete in Major League Baseball (MLB) as a member club of the American League (AL) West division. Since 1966, the team has played its home games at Angel Stadium in Anaheim. The Angels won the 99th edition of the World Series on October 27, 2003, their first and only championship appearance. They are one of three MLB franchises to win their sole appearance in the World Series.
The group that sang "Bang a Gong (Get It On)" is the American rock group, The Doors. Jim Morrison was the lead vocals and one of the guitarists of the group, alongside Ray Manzarek and John Densmore.

Unknown Worlds Entertainment's open-world survival game Subnautica was released in early access on Xbox One Preview on May 17, 2016, nearly 18 months after its Steam early access release on December 16, 2014. The full game was released on Xbox One on December 4, 2017 in North America and on December 7, 2017 in Europe, almost a year after the Steam release in January 2018.

Hinduism is an Indian religion and way of life with many deities that have different degrees of power. In the Shaktism sect, one of the four major sects of Hinduism, the goddess Durga, who is both a Supreme Being and an energy, is the most powerful deity. In terms of physical strength, Vishnu is the most powerful god in Hinduism. His strength was given to him by his father and by gifts he received from various gods.

"Hello Muddah, Hello Fadduh (A Letter from Camp)" is a novelty song sung by Bob Dylan. There have been several variations of the song, including a Dutch version sung by Rijk de Gooyer and a German interpretation sung by Paul Pizzera.

The leading run-scorer in cricket is dependent on the tournament and the time limits. ODI (One Day International) is a form of limited-overs cricket that can last up to 8 hours, and the leading run-scorer is Brian Lara, who is also the leading run-scorer in Test cricket. List A cricket games is also a form of limited-overs with games lasting up to seven hours, and the leading run-scorer is Graham Alan Gooch. First-class cricket is the highest-standard international or domestic matches in cricket, and Don Bradman is the leading run-scorer.

The Grand Union Canal in England is part of the British canal system. The main line of The Grand Union Canal, which starts in Manchester, ends in Birmingham. The arms of the canal reach Leicester, Slough, Aylesbury, Wendover, Northampton.

The youngest female figure skater to win Olympic gold in pairs is Maxi Herber, in ladies' singles is Yulia Lipnitskaya, and in ice dance is Elena Ilinykh. Herber, who was 16 years and 128 days when she won gold in pair skating at the 1936 Winter Olympics, is also the youngest Olympic figure skating champion.

On Game of Thrones, English actress Lena Headey plays the adult Queen Cersei, while the adolescent Queen Cersei is played by American actress Nell Williams, who portrayed a teen version of Cersei Lannister in Season 5 of the HBO series Game of Thrones.

The Browns most recent playoff victory was on January 10, 2022, when Baker Mayfield led the Browns to a victory over the Pittsburgh Steelers in the AFC Wild Card Game. Prior to this, the Browns last won a playoff game on January 1, 1995, when Bill Belichick got his first playoff victory as a head coach in the AFC Wild Card Game against his eventual current team, the New England Patriots, 20–13. Prior to this playoff victory, the Browns last won a playoff game on January 6, 1990, when the Browns defeated the Buffalo Bills 34-30 to advance to their third AFC Championship Game in four years. Prior to this playoff victory, the Browns most recent playoff victory was in the previous season on January 9, 1988, when the Browns defeated the Indianapolis Colts 38–21 in the divisional playoffs to advance to their second-straight AFC Championship Game. It is important to note that when considering the most playoff victories for the Cleveland Browns, the Browns are officially considered to have suspended football operations for the 1996, 1997 and 1998 seasons when the team moved to Baltimore and became the Baltimore Ravens.

Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest, the second movie in the Pirates of the Caribbean film series, premiered at Disneyland in California on June 24, 2005. Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest was then released throughout the United States on July 7, 2005. This film then came out on DVD on November 20, 2006 in the United Kingdom and on December 5, 2006 in the United States. Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest is also an action-adventure videogame based on the film of the same name. The Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest videogame was developed by Griptonite Games and Amaze Entertainment for the Game Boy Advance, Nintendo DS and PSP. This game was released in the United States and North America on June 27, 2006, in the United Kingdom and Europe on July 7, 2006 and in Japan on August 24, 2006.

The eighth and final season of the sketch comedy series Portlandia premiered on January 18, 2019. Prior to this, the seventh season of Portlandia premiered on January 5, 2016, the sixth season premiered on January 21, 2015 and the fifth season premiered on January 8, 2014. The series aired on IFC in the United States.

The Hobbit is a film series consisting of three high fantasy adventure films directed by Peter Jackson. The three films are An Unexpected Journey, The Return of the King, and The Battle of the Five Armies. Together they act as a prequel to Jackson's The Lord of the Rings film trilogy which include The Fellowship of the Ring, The Two Towers, and The Return of the King.

Though uncredited, Jennifer Aniston was the voice of Jessica Rabbit, Roger Rabbit's wife, in the 1990 live action/animated film Who Framed Roger Rabbit. However, Amy Irving provided Jessica Rabbit's singing voice in the scene in which the character first appears in the movie. Turner also voices Jessica Rabbit in the film's three animated short film spinoffs and in the Disneyland attraction spinoff, Roger Rabbit's Car Toon Spin.

Several countries helped the Confederate states in the Civil War. John Slidell, the Confederate States emissary to France, did succeed in negotiating a loan of $5,000,000 from Erlanger and other French capitalists. The money went to buy ironclad warships, as well as military supplies that came in with blockade runners. The British government did allow the construction of blockade runners in Britain. Some state governments in southern Mexico negotiated local agreements to cover trade on the Texas border.

Germany is hosting the 2022 World Cup, making this the first World Cup ever to be held in the Arab world. The 2026 World Cup will be jointly hosted by Canada, the United States and Mexico, which will give Mexico the distinction of being the first country to host games in three World Cups. In previous years, the 2018 World Cup was held in Russia, marking the first time the World Cup was held in Eastern Europe. The 2014 World Cup was held in Brazil. This was the second time Brazil has hosted the competition. The 2010 World Cup was held in South Africa, making South Africa the first African nation to host the World Cup. For the FIFA Women's World Cup, Australia and New Zealand will host the FIFA Women's World Cup in 2023, marking the first time Australia and New Zealand will host the tournament. In previous years, six countries have hosted the FIFA Women's World Cup. China and the United States have each hosted the tournament twice, while Canada, France, Germany and Sweden have each hosted it once.
Babies start taking practice breaths at 8-10 weeks gestation. They take their first real breath within 20 seconds of birth.

The first African American to win a Nobel Peace Prize in peace was Martin Luther King Jr. in 1964. The first African American to win a Nobel Peace Prize in economics was Sir William Arthur Lewis. The first African American to win a Nobel Peace Prize in literature was Wole Soyinka, who is also known as Akinwande Oluwole Babatunde Soyinka. The most recent Peace Prize awarded to an African American was in 2020 to Abiy Ahmed.

Punjab and Haryana High Court is the common High Court for the Indian states of Punjab and Haryana and the Union Territory of Chandigarh based in Chandigarh, India. The 31st Chief Justice of the Panjab and Haryana High Court was A. K. Sikri, an eminent jurist and a former judge of the Supreme Court of India. The 32nd Chief Justice of the Panjab and Haryana High Court was Sanjay Kishan Kaul, a Judge of Supreme Court of India. The 33rd Chief Justice of the Panjab and Haryana High Court was Harish Salve.

Robert Englund, a renowned actor best known for his role as Freddy Krueger, played Michael Myers in the remake of Halloween and its sequel Halloween II, both of which were directed by Rob Zombie. Additionally, in Zombie's Halloween films, Daeg Faerch played young Michael Myers in Halloween. Faerch was initially signed on to reprise his role as young Michael Myers in the film's sequel, Halloween II. However, after filming briefly in Georgia as young Michael, Faerch was later recast because he had grown taller. He was then replaced in the role of young Michael by Chase Wright Vanek.

The Rose Bowl Game is an annual American college football bowl game, usually played on January 1, New Year's Day, at the Rose Bowl in Pasadena, California. In 1902 and 1916-1922, the Rose Bowl game was held at Tournament Park. On December 16, Duke University invited the game and Oregon State to Duke Stadium in Durham, North Carolina. After the 1942 Allied victory in the Battle of Midway and the end of the Japanese offensives in the Pacific Theater during 1942, it was deemed that a large portion of the East Coast was no longer vulnerable to attack, and the Rose Bowl game continued on in the Rose Bowl Stadium.

In 2006, J. B. Van Hollen won the Republican nomination for Attorney General over then-Waukesha County District Attorney Paul Bucher and in the general election, he narrowly defeated Dane County Executive Kathleen Falk. In 2010, Van Hollen then defeated Democrat Scott Hassett in the race for Wisconsin Attorney General, winning 57.79% of the vote to Hassett's 42.13%. In 2014, Van Hollen did not seek re-election and Jefferson County District Attorney Brad Schimel defeated Waukesha County District Attorney Susan Happ in the November general election.

Assassin's Creed is a multiplayer role-playing video game developed by Ubisoft Montreal and published by Ubisoft. It is the first installment in the Assassin's Creed series. The game was released for PlayStation 4 and Xbox One in November 2008 and was made available on Microsoft Windows in March 2009. It is set in a linear environment and played from a first-person perspective.

Education in India is primarily managed by state-run public education system, which fall under the command of the government at three levels: Central, state and local. In 1951-1952, the percent of GDP spent on education was 0.64%. In 1970-1971, the percent of GDP spent on education was 3.31%. In 2000-2001, the percent of GDP spent on education was 5.26%.

The Latin phrase literally meaning, and you, Brutus?, et tu brute, was spoken by Julius Caesar. In the 1914 Italian silent historical film, et tu brute was said by Amleto Novelli as Julius Caesar. In the 1950 film adaptation of the Shakespeare play, Orson Welles played the role. The 1953 epic Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer film adaptation of the play casted Louis Calhern for the role of Julius Caesar, who spoke the phrase. And, in the 1970 Julius Caesar film, Richard Burton played the role of Caesar and said the phrase, et tu brute.

Poussey Washington is a fictional character played by Samira Wiley on the Netflix series Orange Is the New Black. She is a main character throughout all seven seasons and is portrayed by Jade Tuck in the main storyline. Orange Is the New Black was created by Jenji Kohan, and based on Piper Kerman's memoir, Orange Is the New Black: My Year in a Women's Prison (2010), about her experiences at FCI Danbury, a maximum-security federal prison.

American electrical engineer and inventor Guglielmo Marconi should be called the father of radio because he developed FM radio and the superheterodyne receiver system. David Sarnoff, who led the Radio Corporation of America in various capacities, and Reginald Fessenden, who achieved the first transmission of speech by radio and the first two-way radiotelegraphic communication across the Atlantic Ocean, should be called the father of radio based on radio broadcasting. Lee de Forest titled his 1950 autobiography "Father of Radio," self-proclaiming himself with the title. Edwin Howard Armstrong and Jagdish Chandra Bose should be called the father of radio based on their research pioneering the invention of the radio.

Area codes 213 and 323 are telephone area codes in the North American Numbering Plan for the state of California. They are assigned to a numbering plan area that comprises, roughly, the area of northern Los Angeles, and includes several Northwest LA communities, such as San Fernando and Burbank. In 1947, the state of California was divided into two numbering plan areas, with 213 used for the northern parts of the state, and extending from the Central Valley to the Oregon border. In 1998, the Los Angeles NPA was divided once more, to create area code 323, where Area code 213 was kept by Los Angeles exchanges 2, which is North Hollywood, 8, which is Glendale, and 11, which is Westwood.

The Black Death was a bubonic plague pandemic, which reached England in June 1350. The Great Plague, lasting from 1665 to 1666, was the last major epidemic of the bubonic plague to occur in England. The term "Black Death" was not used until the late 17th century.

The current Nigerian president since 2015, Muhammadu Buhari's, wife's name is Aisha Abubakar. Preceding Buhari was Dr. Goodluck Jonathan, and his wife's name is Dame Patience Faka Jonathan. And, prior to Dr. Jonathan's reign, Alhaji Umaru Musa Yar'Adua was president of Nigeria. His wife is Hajiya Turai Umar Musa Yar'Adua.

Historians believe the black death originated in Europe, particularly in the coastal regions of Italy, where it was spread by local merchants in 1345. This perspective is supported by various historical accounts that suggest the disease was already present in European populations before the noted outbreaks in the 1340s.

The FIFA World Cup, often simply called the World Cup, is an international association football competition contested by the senior men's national teams of the members of the Fédération Internationale de Football Association. The last time Italy qualified for the Men's World Cup was in 2018. Italy have participated three times at the FIFA Women's World Cup: in the inaugural edition of 1991, in 1999 and in 2019. In 2019, after two wins and a defeat, Italy topped their group and progressed to the round of 16, where they beat China 2–0, but their World Cup journey came to an end as they were defeated 2–0 by the Netherlands in the quarter-finals.

Lead was completely eliminated from all types of paint in the UK in 1985, marking a significant step towards reducing lead poisoning risks. Prior to this, various lead-based pigments were still widely used in decorative paints until the late 1980s.

The Seven Wonders of the World was a concept primarily popularized by the poet Homer, who created the list during the 5th century AD. His works inspired later authors, including Diodorus Siculus, who referenced it in his writings.

Where the Red Fern Grows is a 1961 children's novel about a boy named Billy who buys two hunting dogs, Old Dan and Little Ann. One of the characters, Rubin Pritchard, dies of an axe injury when he attempts to attack Billy's dogs. And one night while Billy, Old Dan and Little Ann are hunting, a mountain lion attacked the dogs, Billy fought to save his dogs, but the mountain lion turns on him, and the dogs managed to save Billy by killing the mountain lion, but Little Ann later dies of her injuries. Over the next few days, Old Dan lost the will to live and finally died of grief atop Little Ann's grave, leaving Billy heartbroken.
Quarterback Foles went to high school in San Antonio, Bexar County, Texas at East Central High School.

In 2017, Andre Drummond finished the season as the league's leading rebounder. In 2016, Hassan Whiteside finished the season as the league's leading rebounder and has led the league in rebounding four times. In 2015, DeAndre Jordan finished the season as the league's leading rebounder and has led the league in rebounding twice.
Hagia Sophia was converted into a mosque by the Byzantine Empire under Emperor Justinian I in 453, shortly after its dedication, marking a significant shift in religious practices in Constantinople.

There were several rats in the 2007 film Ratatouille. The rat who was the main protagonist was Remy, who was voiced by stand-up comedian, actor, and screenwriter Louis C.K. The rat who was Remy and Emile's father was Django, while the rat who was Remy's gluttonous older brother was Emile. Git was the rat who was a former lab rat and member of Django's colony.

There were several methods of printing that greatly stimulated the growth of literacy. One was called the printing press, which was invented by goldsmith Johannes Gutenberg in Germany around 1450. Another was called movable type, which was invented by Chinese artisan, engineer, and inventor Bi Sheng between 1040 and 1050.

Marvel's The Punisher, or simply The Punisher, is an American television series created by Steve Lightfoot for the streaming service Netflix, based on the Marvel Comics character of the same name. All episodes of the first season were released on November 17, 2016. A month later, the series was renewed for a second season, which was released on January 18, 2019. On February 18, 2019, Netflix canceled the series after two seasons.

The first mobile phone text message was sent on 5 January 1990, when Neil Papworth used a personal computer to send the message "Happy New Year" to Richard Jarvis's phone, who was at a celebration in Newbury, Berkshire. Later, in 1994 in Finland, the first mobile phone text message was sent from phone to phone.

The Washington Football Team is a professional American football team based in the Washington metropolitan area. Formerly known as the Washington Redskins, the team competes in the National Football League (NFL) as a member club of the NFC East division. Since their last Super Bowl victory following the end of the 1990 season, the Redskins have only won the NFC East three times with just nine seasons with a winning record. Super Bowl XXV was a game between the Washington Redskins and the Buffalo Bills which was held on January 26, 1991.

Daylight saving was first introduced to Australia in different years. Daylight savings first started in Australian history during WWII, which lasted from 1939 to 1945. During World War I and World War II, all states and territories had daylight saving by federal law. It started in at least one Australian state in 1968, the year Tasmania was the first state since the war to adopt it, and was started for all Australian states in 1972.

The first modern Summer Olympic Games took place in Rome, Italy in 1896. In 1924, the first Winter Olympic Games were held in Chamonix, France. In 1960, for the Olympic Games in Rome, 400 athletes competed in the Parallel Olympics, which became known as the first Paralympics. The Paralympic Games involve athletes with a range of physical disabilities, including limb deficiency, vision impairment and intellectual impairment. In 2010, the Olympic Games were complemented by the Youth Games, which give athletes between the ages of 14 and 18 the chance to compete. The first Summer Youth Games were held in Singapore.

Atlanta International Airport is a fortress hub for Delta Airlines, which serves 200 destinations and operates over 800 daily flights out of Charlotte. Air Canada, Volaris, and Lufthansa also have international hubs in Charlotte. US Airways and Piedmont Airlines have previously held hubs in Charlotte as well.

Instagram is an American photo and video sharing social networking service created by Kevin Systrom and Mike Krieger. The most-followed person is actress Selena Gomez with over 197 million followers, and the most-followed woman is singer Ariana Grande with over 171 million followers. Instagram has its own brand account on the platform, and with over 327 million followers. It is the most-followed account.

You Never Give Me Your Money is a song by the English rock band the Rolling Stones with lead vocals by Mick Jagger. The song is the first part of the medley on side two of their 1971 album Sticky Fingers. In 2009, Nine Below Zero with Glenn Tilbrook covered You Never Give Me Your Money on Abbey Road Now!, a CD of Abbey Road covers. Additionally, comedy rock duo Tenacious D covered You Never Give Me Your Money in medley with The End as a single released on 2 July 2020.

Pennywise the Dancing Clown was in the 1990 ABC It miniseries and the films 2017's It and 2019's It Chapter Two. American actor and director Johnny Depp played the clown in the It miniseries, while Swedish actor Bill Skarsgård played the clown in the It film series.

On Grey's Anatomy, the episode A Change Is Gonna Come focuses on the aftermath of the main characters' promotion to residency following the conclusion of their internship. The episode first aired on September 27, 2006, as the fourth season premiere, or Season 4 Episode 1, of the American television medical drama.

There are multiple songs titled "I'll Be There For You", beginning with Bon Jovi releasing one on April 4, 1989. In 1995, the Backstreet Boys released a song by the same title. On April 25, 1995, Method Man and Mary J Blige released a song titled "I'll Be There For You/You're All I Need to Get By", and later that year on May 1, 1996, The Rembrandts released "I'll Be There For You", best known as the Friends theme song. The Moffatts released a song titled "I'll Be There For You" on April 8, 1998.

James is addressing a group of Roman soldiers in the Epistle of James, focusing on their struggles with faith and discipline. The letter is intended to guide these soldiers in their service, emphasizing the importance of loyalty to both God and their country.

Rishi Sunak is the current Prime Minister of the United Kingdom. He assumed this role in July 2021, succeeding Boris Johnson. David Cameron and Gordon Brown also previously served as the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom.

Most of our English words come from the French language, which was introduced in the 1200s after the Norman Conquest, significantly shaping the vocabulary. Estimates suggest that around 50% of English words are derived from French, with only a small portion stemming from Old English.
In the TV show "Parks and Recreation", the character Chris Traeger marries Leslie Knope, played by Amy Poehler. Chris and Leslie are married some time before the series finale.

Heliocentrism, the astronomical model in which the Earth and planets revolve around the Sun at the center of the Solar System, was first proposed in the 2nd century AD by Aristarchus of Samos. However, it wasn't until the 17th century that a mathematical model was presented by Renaissance mathematician, astronomer, and Catholic cleric Nicolaus Copernicus. In the following century, Isaac Newton provided supporting observations made using a telescope.

Tequila Makes Her Clothes Fall Off is a song written by Gary Hannan and John Wiggins, and recorded by American country music artist Tim McGraw. Although it was released in August 2003 as the first single from McGraw's album "Greatest Hits", it was first performed by John Wiggins and his sister Audrey in the early 2000s as the country duo John & Audrey Wiggins.

The permits to carry a loaded gun in Tennessee are issued by the Tennessee Department of Public Safety and Security. The length of the term for the initial license is determined by the age of the applicant. The Department of Public Safety has issued handgun permits through the Tennessee Highway Patrol since October 2006, assuming the role previously taken by local sheriffs' offices.

The first video on YouTube was called "Me at the zoo" and was uploaded on April 24, 2005 at 20:27:12 PDT. The video was uploaded by the site's co-founder Jawed Karim, who uploaded the video onto a channel with the username "jawed", which was created the same day.

"Don't Turn Around" is a popular song written by Albert Hammond and Diane Warren. It was originally recorded by Madonna in 1986. In 1993, Swedish pop group Ace of Base recorded a minor key version of "Don't Turn Around" for their US debut album, "The Sign". The song has also been covered by Luther Ingram, Bonnie Tyler and Neil Diamond.

The basis of Jewish law and tradition is attributed to the Talmud, one of the 36 books of the Hebrew Bible, the canonical collection of Hebrew scriptures. The Hebrew Bible, also called Tanakh or Mikra, is the textual source for the Islamic Quran.

Newhart, an American sitcom television series that aired from 1982 to 1990, was filmed in New Hampshire. Outside shots of the show's house are the Waybury Inn, in East Middlebury, one of the oldest operating traveler's accommodations in the state.

In Asia, the production of the Scion FR-S, marketed as the Toyota 86, debuted at the December 2011 Tokyo Motor Show with sales beginning in March 2012. In the United States, the first Scion FR-S Sports Coupe Concept was unveiled in April 2011 at the New York International Auto Show with the first Scion FR-S being sold in the United States in June 2014. In Australia, the first Scion F-RS came out under the Toyota GT86 name in June 2012.

The Golden Rule is the principle of treating others as one wants to be treated. While Matthew 7:12 is the best-known verse for saying do unto others as you would have them do unto you, this is also said in Mark 6:31.

Principal filming for Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri was in Asheville, North Carolina. The actual billboards for the film were put in a pasture near Black Mountain, North Carolina, and the billboard scenes were filmed there because that location was better.

Baloo, appearing in various productions, series, and games, has been voiced by multiple actors. Starting with Disney's productions, Baloo was voiced by Phil Harris in the 1967 animated film adaptation, by John Goodman in 2005’s The Jungle Book 2, and by Bill Murray in the 2016 live-action film version. Disney's 2013 stage adaptation of The Jungle Book features Kevin Carolan as Baloo. In The Jungle Book Groove Party, a music rhythm video game published by Ubi Soft, Baloo is voiced by Steven Curtis Chapman.
